PT P
PN CN114381829-A
TI Preparing microporous carbon fiber material useful            for separating methane and nitrogen, separating methane            and carbon dioxide comprises stirring polyacrylonitrile            and N,N-dimethylformamide, adding metal salt activator,            stirring, performing electrospinning, and            high-temperature activation reaction.
AU LIAO N
   DU S
   HUANG J
   XIAO J
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 202260597V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microporous carbon fiber material                comprises (1) stirring polyacrylonitrile and                N,N-dimethylformamide evenly at 30-60&#176;C,                adding a metal salt activator, stirring evenly, and                performing electrospinning, after drying at                120-155&#176;C for 4-12 hours, removing                N,N-dimethylformamide to obtain polymer fibers, (2)                soaking the polymer fibers with an infiltration                solvent, dropping a cross-linking agent under                stirring conditions to carry out a cross-linking                reaction to obtain cross-linked fibers, and (3)                placing the cross-linked fibers obtained in step                (2) in an inert atmosphere at 600-900&#176;C                for high-temperature activation reaction to obtain                a microporous carbon fiber material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing microporous                carbon fiber material, which is useful for                separating methane and nitrogen, separating methane                and carbon dioxide, separating monofluoromethane                and carbon dioxide, separating nitrogen and oxygen,                separating xenon and krypton (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The microporous carbon fiber material: has                excellent performance in separating multiple small                molecular gas fiber; and has good industrial                application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                microporous carbon fiber material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  mass ratio of the polyacrylonitrile and the                  N,N-dimethylformamide is 1:9-1:12. In the step                  (1), the metal salt activator is copper chloride,                  zinc acetate, zinc chloride and/or zinc sulfate.                  In the step (1), the mass ratio of the metal salt                  activator to the polyacrylonitrile is 1:1-1:3. In                  the step (2), the soaking solvent is a kind of in                  anhydrous ethylene glycol, glycerol, propylene                  glycol. The crosslinking agent is                  ethylenediamine, 1,2-propylenediamine,                  1,6-hexanediamine, diethylenetriamine and/or                  triethylenetetramine. The mass ratio of the                  polymer fiber to the crosslinking agent is                  1:0.4-1:0.8.Preferred Method: In the step (1), the                  voltage of the electrospinning is set to 15-20                  KV, the injection volume of the solution is                  0.6-1.2 ml/hour, the distance between the needle                  and the collector is 10-20 cm, and the spinning                  time is 10-24 hours. In the step (2), the oil                  bath temperature of the cross-linking reaction is                  80-140&#176;C, the time of the cross-linking                  reaction is 2-5 hours. In the step (3), the                  heating rate of the high-temperature activation                  reaction is 5-10&#176;C/minute, and the time                  of the high-temperature activation reaction is                  1-4 hours.
DC A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); ; F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN216024779-U
TI Device for freezing and concentrating solution by using carbon dioxide hydrate generation and decomposition, comprises solution storage tank hydration reaction device, hydrate decomposition tank, and concentrated solution recycling tank.
AU WENG W
   FU X
   YIN H
   QIN G
   PENG K
AE UNIV DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDU-C)
GA 202242626Q
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for realizing solution freezing concentration by using carbon dioxide hydrate generation and decomposition, comprising: solution storage tank hydration reaction device, a hydrate decomposition tank, a concentrated solution recycling tank, the hydration reaction device comprises a reactor, a cooler and a high pressure gas supply bottle; said solution storage tank is connected with the upper part of the reactor, the hydrate decomposing tank is connected with the lower part of the reactor, the lower part of the hydrate decomposing tank is connected with the concentrated liquid recycling tank. The utility model firstly generates proper carbon dioxide hydrate in the original solution at high pressure and low temperature condition, then decomposing hydrate under the normal pressure heat preservation condition, using the characteristic of the hydrate decomposition of large heat absorption, the solution temperature is reduced to below freezing point, realizing the crystallization of water, the solution is concentrated; The utility model model does not need power device in the process of preparing ice slurry, and can fully recycle and utilize the cold energy contained in the hydrate itself, which can obviously reduce the device cost and operation cost of the freezing and concentrating technology.

   CHAE Y
   JUNG Y J
   PARK J H

   HUH J

   LEE J Y
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202228182G
AB    NOVELTY - The nuclear load response power generation system (100) has a thermal energy storage system (120) connected to a secondary system of a nuclear power plant through a thermal coupling method. The system converts surplus energy branched from the secondary system into thermal energy, and stores the converted thermal energy. Load response power is provided based on the carbon dioxide compressed energy and the thermal energy and provides load response power. A heat exchanger (121) is provided in the system, and a high-temperature tank (122) is provided with a heat transfer fluid i.e. hydrogenated tetra ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (HITEC) salt. A steam turbine (111) is connected to the system.
   USE - Nuclear load response power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The impact on the primary system of the nuclear power plant is minimized. The load-response capability of nuclear power generation is significantly improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the overall configuration diagram of a nuclear load response power generation system. (Drawing includes non-English Language texts).

AU SHEN Q

GA 202140358Q
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon emission reduction system has a flue gas processing module, a water vapour collection module, an oxygen extraction module, a carbon oxide collection and separation module, and a carbon oxide processing module. The flue gas processing module is used for the pre-processing of flue gas discharged from the boiler of the thermal power plant, and the water vapour collection module is used for the collection of residual water vapour in the internal steam turbine of the thermal power plant. The oxygen extraction module is used to hydrolyze the collected water vapour to obtain oxygen. The carbon oxide collection and separation module is used to separate and collect the main carbon oxide carbon dioxide in the flue gas. The carbon oxide processing module is used to process the carbon dioxide obtained from the carbon oxide collection and separation module.

PN CN112480059-A
TI Directly synthesizing cyclic carbonates from olefins catalyzed by metalloporphyrin, comprises e.g. adding olefin, metal porphyrin complex and co-catalyst into autoclave, and introducing carbon dioxide and oxygen/air.
AU ZHANG Z
   CHAN S
   YI M
   ZHENG X
   CHEN J
   YANG S
   BAO Z
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202130553J
AB    NOVELTY - Directly synthesizing cyclic carbonates from olefins catalyzed by metalloporphyrin, comprises adding olefin, metal porphyrin complex (I) and co-catalyst into autoclave, and introducing carbon dioxide and oxygen/air to obtain final product. The four-dentate coordination metalloporphyrin complex is on the benzene ring at the meso position; and co-catalyst is protic and aprotic ionic liquids based on pyridine or its derivatives and nitrogen-containing organic bases.
   USE - The method is useful for directly synthesizing cyclic carbonates from olefins catalyzed by metalloporphyrin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has mild reaction conditions, simple process, small amount of catalyst, and high conversion rate and high selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Directly synthesizing cyclic carbonates from olefins catalyzed by metalloporphyrin, comprises adding olefin, metal porphyrin complex of formula (I) and co-catalyst into autoclave, and introducing carbon dioxide and oxygen/air to obtain final product. The four-dentate coordination metalloporphyrin complex is on the benzene ring at the meso position; and co-catalyst is protic and aprotic ionic liquids based on pyridine or its derivatives and nitrogen-containing organic bases.
   R1, R2, and R3 = hydrogen, halo, aliphatic group, substituted aliphatic group, nitro group or amino group;
   X = halo, -NO3, CH3COO-, -CO, CF3COO-, ClO4, -CN, or -BF4; and
   metal ion = Fe, Mn, Ru, or Co.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the amount of the metal porphyrin complex to the substrate is 0.01-0.5%. The molar ratio of the amount of the co-catalyst to the amount of the metalloporphyrin complex is 0.1-5:1. The metalloporphyrin complex is specifically manganese tetraphenylporphyrin, and the co-catalyst is specifically (DMAPH)Br. The olefin is 1.2 g styrene, and the amount of the metalloporphyrin complex is 1 mg; amount of co-catalyst is 51 mg, the oxygen pressure is 0.1 MPa, carbon dioxide pressure is 1.2 MPa, the reaction time is 8 hours, and the reaction temperature is 100 degrees C. Preferred Method: The oxygen/air pressure is 0.1-5 MPa, the carbon dioxide pressure is 0.1-10 MPa, the reaction temperature in the autoclave is 25-150 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-10 hours.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN US2021008496-A1
TI Converting flue gas to combustible fuels by pyrolyzing biomass, gasifying biochar in presence of carbon dioxide, gasifying biochar in presence of natural gas, and combining carbon monoxide and hydrogen and inputting in hydrocarbon synthesis.
AU HAGO W
AE HAGO ENERGETICS INC (HAGO-Non-standard)
   HAGO W (HAGO-Individual)
GA 2021055128
AB    NOVELTY - Flue gas is converted to combustible fuels by (a) pyrolyzing biomass to produce biochar and volatile gas, (b) gasifying a portion of biochar in the presence of carbon dioxide obtained from flue gas to produce carbon monoxide and activated carbon in 1st gasification system, (c) gasifying a portion of biochar in the presence of natural gas to produce hydrogen and carbon black in a 2nd gasification system, and (d) combining the generated carbon monoxide and hydrogen from the 1st and 2nd gasification systems and inputting this combination in a hydrocarbon synthesis process to produce combustible fuels.
   USE - Conversion of flue gas to combustible fuels using biomass and natural gas as additional inputs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves conversion of carbon dioxide in flue gas to liquid fuels and valuable carbons in a carbon negative manner. The method incorporates the possibility of sequestering excess carbon as biochar, significantly reducing the carbon footprint of the entire project, possibly rendering it with a carbon footprint or lifecycle analysis that is carbon negative. The produced activated carbon and carbon black can also be considered sequesterable carbons and may qualify for carbon credits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: Activated carbon has surface area of greater than or equal to 300 m2/g. Carbon footprint is overall carbon negative. Preferred Components: A portion of biochar is suitable for agricultural applications. Carbon black is a powder that is removed with unreacted biochar. Synthesis gas ratio of carbon monoxide to hydrogen is adjustable, preferably 1.5-2.5. Preferred Processes: Activated carbon is produced with the aid of microwave energy. Carbon black is produced with the aid of microwave energy. Volatile gas is burned to produce energy useful for the 1st and 2nd gasification systems. Significant source of carbon dioxide escapes to the atmosphere.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Flue gas carbon dioxide, carbon in biomass, and carbon in natural gas are converted to biochar, activated carbon, carbon black, and carbon in combustible fuels. Hydrocarbon synthesis step is a Fischer Tropsch process. Hydrocarbon synthesis step is a methanol synthesis process. Carbon dioxide resulting from burning the volatile gas is fed back into the input flue gas. Preferred Parameter: Microwave energy uses frequencies of 915-2450 MHz. Preferred Components: Carbon black comprises carbon from fossil or biological sources. Activated carbon comprises carbon from fossil or biological sources. Carbon content of combustible fuel is approximately equally divided between fossil and biological carbons.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN211450324-U
TI Carbon dioxide storage tank useful for production of casting mold, comprises a tank body comprising an inner tank body and an outer tank body, where the inner tank body is installed in the outer tank body through a fixing plate.
AU LI S
AE NINGGUO ZHUFENG STEEL BALL CASTING CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2020907938
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide storage tank for producing casting mould, comprising a tank body, the tank body comprises an inner tank body and an outer tank body; the inner tank body is installed in the outer tank body through a fixing plate; the outer tank body is provided with an air inlet and an air outlet; the air inlet and the air outlet are connected with the inner tank body; the number of the air outlet is at least three; the air inlet is located at the left side of the tank body; the air outlet is provided with an air valve; the air valve is connected with the air pipe; the outer tank body is further provided with a pressure limiting valve; the pressure limiting valve is connected with the inner tank body; the outer tank body is further provided with a vent hole; The utility model overcomes the shortage of the existing technology, the carbon dioxide storage tank has a plurality of air outlet, a plurality of mould for solidifying operation, improves the working efficiency, and the tank has the function of using safety and air leakage automatic alarm.

PT P
PN US2020246405-A1; US11344591-B2
TI Obtaining extract enriched with terpenes and            extract enriched with cannabinoids from Cannabis, for,            e.g. treating epilepsy by contacting Cannabis twice            with supercritical fluid solvent containing carbon            dioxide, and removing solvent.
AU SPEIER G J
AE CURE PHARM HOLDING CORP (CURE-Non-standard)
GA 2020749702
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining an extract enriched with terpenes                and an extract enriched with cannabinoids from                Cannabis, comprises contacting the Cannabis with a                supercritical fluid solvent containing carbon                dioxide at 1000 plus minus 200 psi and 30 plus                minus 5 degrees C, for 1.5 plus minus 1 hours, to                provide an extract enriched with terpenes;                contacting the Cannabis with a supercritical fluid                solvent containing carbon dioxide at 2400 plus                minus 400 psi and 49 plus minus 5 degrees C, for 10                plus minus 4 hours, to provide an extract enriched                with cannabinoids; and removing the supercritical                fluid solvent from the extracts of the                Cannabis.
   USE - The method is used for obtaining an extract                enriched with terpenes and an extract enriched with                cannabinoids from Cannabis, used for treating                disease or disorder including neuroinflammation,                epilepsy, Alzheimer's disease, oxidative injury,                vomiting and nausea, anxiety, anorexia, arthritis,                schizophrenia, multiple sclerosis, joint                inflammation, joint pain, symptoms associated with                AIDS, chronic pain, acute pain, anxiety,                neuropathic pain and spasticity, inflammatory bowel                diseases, tumor neovascularization, tumor growth,                cancer, glaucoma, muscle spasms, severe pain,                severe nausea, cachexia or dramatic weight loss and                muscle atrophy (wasting syndrome), cancer cell                migration, cancer cell adhesion, cancer cell                invasion, and/or cancer cell metastasization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The supercritical fluid                  solvent comprises ethanol, hexanes or methanol.                  The extract enriched with cannabinoids comprises                  greater than or equal to 65 wt.% cannabinoids.                  The terpenes comprise beta -caryophyllene                  epoxide, mentha-1,8(9)-dien-5-ol, pulegone,                  limonene, limonene oxide, alpha -terpinene,                  terpinen-4-ol, carvacrol, carvone, 1,8-cineole,                  p-cymene, fenchone, pulegone-1,2 epoxide, and/or                  13-myrcene. Preferred Method: The supercritical                  fluid extraction is carried out at 1100 plus                  minus 100 psi and 38 plus minus 1 degrees C for                  1.5 plus minus 0.5 hours. The extract enriched                  with terpenes is further purified employing a                  technique from chromatography, adsorption,                  crystallization, distillation, liquid-liquid                  extraction, filtration, fractional distillation,                  precipitation, recrystallization, lyophilization                  (freeze-drying), and/or sublimation. The method                  further comprises contacting ethanol and the                  extract enriched with cannabinoids, such that the                  extract dissolves in the ethanol; cooling the                  solution; removing solids that precipitate from                  the solution; optionally repeating, contacting,                  cooling and removing steps; and removing the                  ethanol.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Neuroprotective;                Anticonvulsant; Nootropic; Antiemetic;                Tranquilizer; Anabolic; Eating-Disorders-Gen;                Antiarthritic; Neuroleptic; Analgesic; Muscle                Relaxant; Gastrointestinal-Gen; Ophthalmological.                No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); ; D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Separation of species e.g. carbon dioxide,            involves contacting mixture with sorbent comprising            metal organic framework containing secondary building            units containing repeating unit of one metal cation            connected to another metal cation.
AU QAZVINI O T
   TELFER S
AE UNIV MASSEY (UYMA-Non-standard)
   UNIV MASSEY (UYMA-Non-standard)
   UNIV MASSEY (UYMA-Non-standard)
   UNIV MASSEY (UYMA-Non-standard)
GA 202057194F
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of species (S1) from mixture                involves contacting the mixture with a sorbent                comprising a metal organic framework. The metal                organic framework comprises secondary building                units comprising a repeating unit of one metal                cation connected to another metal cation through                moiety (M1) of an organic linker, a layer of                connected adjacent secondary building units, in                which moiety (M2) of the linker in a secondary                building units (U1) is connected to a metal cation                of an adjacent secondary building units, and                adjacent layers are connected to each other through                linker-to-linker bonding interactions. The species                (S1) comprises carbon dioxide, carbon disulfide,                nitrous oxide, water, hydrogen sulfide, hydrogen                cyanide, and/or functionalized 1-3C                hydrocarbons.
   USE - Separation of species e.g. carbon dioxide,                carbon disulfide, nitrous oxide, water, hydrogen                sulfide, hydrogen cyanide, and functionalized 1-3C                hydrocarbons (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes metal organic framework to                selectively adsorb the species (S1) from a fluid or                a fluid flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system,                which consists of a chamber comprising an inlet                adapted to direct the mixture into the chamber, and                a sorbent comprising a metal organic framework                positioned within the chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The secondary building                  units is of formula: (ML2)n (III), where M is                  metal cation, L is organic linker and n is more                  than 0. PT P
PN CN110756214-A; CN110756214-B
TI Aluminum nitride-based catalyst comprises aluminum            nitride carrier, active metal component, and            alumina.
AU SUN Y
   ZHANG J
   LI S
   LIU S
   LU N
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   JUPENG BIO HK LTD (JUPE-Non-standard)
GA 202013894E
AB    NOVELTY - Aluminum nitride-based catalyst comprises                92-95 wt.% aluminum nitride carrier, 1-5 wt.%                active metal component, and 1-5 wt.% alumina.
   USE - The aluminum nitride-based catalyst is useful                in carbon dioxide methane reforming reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes nano-sized aluminum                hydroxide, which avoids the problem of aluminum                nitride oxidation during the removal of the organic                binder and thus improves the mechanical strength of                the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing aluminum nitride-based catalyst                comprising (i) baking the aluminum nitride powder                under an inert gas to obtain aluminum nitride                carrier, (ii) providing metal salt solution of                active metal components, adding the metal salt                solution dropwise into the aluminum nitride carrier                prepared in step (i) to obtain precursor A, (iii)                adding water into the precursor A prepared in step                (ii) and mixing to form aluminum hydroxide layer on                the surface of the precursor A, pressing, forming,                and drying to obtain precursor B, and (iv) firing                the precursor B prepared in step (iii) to obtain                aluminum nitride-based catalyst; and
   (2) use of aluminum nitride-based catalyst for                methane-carbon dioxide reforming reaction,                comprising catalyzing methane and carbon dioxide by                aluminum nitride-based catalyst at 750-950 degrees                C for 200-500 hours at a pressure of 1-5 bar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active metal                  component is nickel and cobalt. The amount of                  active metal component is 1-3 wt.% of the total                  catalyst mass. In step (i), the oxygen content in                  the aluminum nitride powder is less than 5%. In                  step (ii), the concentration of the active metal                  component in the soluble salt solution is 0.5-1.5                  mol/l. The metal salt solution of the active                  metal component is nickel(II) nitrate and/or                  cobalt(II) nitrate. The molar ratio between                  methane and carbon dioxide is 1:1-1:2. Preferred                  Method: In step (i), the baking temperature is                  900-1200 degrees C, and the baking time is 3-15                  hours. The step (ii) adopts an equal volume                  dipping method. In step (iii), the addition                  amount of water each time is 7-15% of the mass of                  the precursor A, and the reaction is performed                  for 3-10 hours and repeated 3-20 times. The                  pressure in the step (iii) during the press                  molding is 30-50 Mpa. In step (iv), the roasting                  temperature is 700-800 degrees C, and the                  roasting time is 3-6 hours.

PN CN110611240-A
TI Program-controllable carbon dioxide laser amplifying device, has electro-optical switch adjusting amplify range of laser beam, and thin film polarizer converting laser beam output by switch, where polarizer reflects and injects laser beam.
AU SUN J
   PAN Q
   GUO J
   CHEN F
   ZHANG K
   YU D
   ZHANG L
   HE Y
AE CHANGCHUN INST OPTICS FINE MECHANICS & P (CAIO-C)
GA 2020008372
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a seed laser for outputting or binding a polarizing element to output a linear polarization laser beam. A film polarizer outputs a laser beam output by the seed laser, where the film polarizer injects the laser beam into a ring amplifier after transmission. The ring amplifier amplifies the laser beam through an annular cavity axial flow laser amplifying structure. The ring amplifier injects amplified laser energy into a beam transformation system. An electro-optical switch adjusts amplification range of the laser beam by adjusting a driving voltage. A thin film polarizer converts the laser beam output by the electro-optic switch, where the polarizer reflects and injects the laser beam after transmission output.
   USE - Program-controllable carbon dioxide laser amplifying device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has compact structure, and can inhibit self-excited oscillation in an effective manner, and improves laser amplification efficiency, amplifies number of flexible control in the single laser amplifier to achieve high gain and satisfies injection seed light power requirement.

PN CN110236167-A
TI Salt and pepper seasoning having strong flavor comprises pepper oil resin, and edible salt.
AU ZHAO Z
   LIU F
   DAI D
   FAN C
AE SICHUAN YASHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201981925G
AB    NOVELTY - Salt and pepper seasoning having strong flavor comprises 2-4 pts. wt. pepper oil resin and 200-400 pts. wt. edible salt.
   USE - Used as salt and pepper seasoning having strong flavor.
   ADVANTAGE - The seasoning: has both volatile pepper oil and a large amount of non-volatile taste components; utilizes hydraulic micro-atomization process combined with the high-speed stirring of salt to achieve the combination of solid phase and oil phase; prepares product, which is more uniform and stable, and has long preservation time and excellent salt and pepper flavor and strong taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the salt and pepper seasoning, comprising (i) taking pepper powder, and sieving to obtain pepper powder, (ii) adding the pepper powder into an extraction kettle, sealing, heating, pumping carbon dioxide gas, increasing the pressure of the kettle, adjusting the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas, extracting pepper oil resin, performing carbon dioxide separation the fluid in which the pepper oleoresin is dissolved by using a separation kettle to obtain a pepper oil resin, (iii) performing atomization of the pepper oil resin by using a hydraulic pressure to form a fine mist droplet of the pepper oleoresin through a fine atomizing nozzle, and (iv) thoroughly stirring and uniformly mixing the salt by using a high-speed rotary flying cutter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The seasoning comprises 3 pts. wt. pepper oil resin and 300 pts. wt. edible salt. Preferred Components: The pepper is either: green pepper, red pepper or Zanthoxylum schinifolium. In the step (i), the mesh number of the pepper powder is 40-80 meshes. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the heating is performed at 25-45 degrees C, the extraction kettle pressure is 20-35 MPa, and the flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 20-30 l/hour. In the step (iii), the droplet size is 5 mu m. In the step (iv), the flying speed of the cutter is 4000-5000 revolutions/minute (rpm).

PN US10280102-B1
TI Treating source water to produce conditioned source water involves mixing trivalent cation with source water to produce acidified source water, where trivalent cation source comprises converting alkalinity in source water to carbon dioxide.
AU BADER M S
AE BADER M S (BADE-Individual)
GA 201937659B
AB    NOVELTY - Treating source water to produce conditioned source water involves mixing a trivalent cation source with source water to produce acidified source water, where trivalent cation source comprises converting a portion of alkalinity in source water to carbon dioxide, and feeding acidified source water to a set of hydrophobic membranes to separate carbon dioxide, oil, and combinations.
   USE - Method for treating source water to produce conditioned source water, where source water is selected from the group consisting of domestic wastewater, a reject stream from a domestic wastewater treatment plant, a reverse osmosis reject stream from a domestic wastewater treatment plant, a stream from a biological step of a domestic wastewater treatment plant, industrial wastewater, a reject stream from an industrial wastewater treatment plant, agricultural drainage water, brackish water, a reject stream from a brackish water treatment plant, seawater, a reject stream from a seawater treatment plant, formation water, flue gas de-sulphurization water, oil-fields produced water, methane-bed produced water, coal-bed produced water, and combinations (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat source water, which eliminate the need for the cost-prohibitive thermal de-salting methods which would significantly reduce the overall costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The trivalent cation source further comprises either aluminum chloride or iron chloride. The onditioned source water further comprises separating magnesium and sulfate from conditioned source water by mixing a hydroxide source with conditioned source water to form a precipitate comprising brucite, and either calcium sulfoaluminate or calcium sulfoferrate, filtering precipitate to produce de-brucited and de-sulfated source water, and stripping de-brucited and de-sulfated source water by another set of hydrophobic membranes. The de-brucited and de-sulfated source water is obtained by separating calcium from de-brucited and de-sulfated source water by reacting de-brucited and de-sulfated source water with carbon dioxide to form a precipitate comprising calcium carbonate, filtering precipitate comprising calcium carbonate to produce de-calcited source water, and stripping de-calcited source water by another set of hydrophobic membranes. The de-brucited and de-sulfated source water is further reacted with a hydroxide source and carbon dioxide. The hydroxide source is selected from the group consisting of hydrated lime, an amine solvent, and combinations. The amine solvent is selected from the group consisting of isopropylamine, propylamine, dipropylamine, diisopropylamine, ethylamine, diethylamine, methylamine, dimethylamine, and combinations. The filtering further comprises a vacuum filter or a filter press. The another set of hydrophobic membranes comprises separating oxygen, an amine solvent, and combinations.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))

PT P
PN CN108007812-A
TI Determination of methane adsorption capacity of coal, involves burning coal sample, calculating content of carbon, hydrogen, nitrogen, sulfur and oxygen, and evaluating correlation between hydrogen content and methane adsorption capacity.
AU CHAN Z
AE UNIV XIAN SHIYOU (UXSY-C)
GA 201837400A
AB    NOVELTY - Determination of methane adsorption capacity involves taking 0.2 g air-dried coal sample into the combustion tube, continuously burn, absorbing the generated moisture and carbon dioxide using moisture and carbon dioxide absorbers, calculating the carbon and hydrogen content from moisture and carbon dioxide absorbent, mixing 0.2 g air-dried coal with 2 g catalyst mixture and 5 ml 0.025 mol/L sulfuric acid solution, continuously heating, converting all nitrogen elements to ammonium hydrogen sulfate, adding excess sodium hydroxide solution, completely absorbing ammonia gas using boric acid solution, calculating nitrogen content based on the amount of sulfuric acid, supplying 1 g air-dried coal into a porcelain crucible, adding 2 g barium compound, converting sulfate ion into barium sulfate, calculating sulfur content based on barium sulfate precipitation, determining dry ash-free oxygen content, and evaluating the correlation between hydrogen content and methane adsorption capacity.
   USE - Determination of methane adsorption capacity of coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and efficient determination of methane adsorption capacity of coal with clear design idea.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The dry ash-free oxygen content in the coal is calculated based on the content of carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen and sulfur in coal.

GA 201824919Y

A condenser (500) cools the working fluid passing through one of the turbines and introduced into a re-cooperator (200). A water heater (600) heats heating water and cools the working fluid by branching a part of the working fluid introduced into the condenser and exchanging heat between the branched working fluid and the heating water supplied from a cogeneration system, where an ultra-critical carbon dioxide power generation system and the cogeneration system share the water heater.
   USE - Hybrid type power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves the ultra-critical carbon dioxide power generation system and the coal-fired power generation system and increases power generation efficiency.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation device comprises e.g. first-stage washing tower, a first-stage washing tower cooler, a second-stage washing tower, a second-stage washing tower cooler, a compressor, a gas-liquid separation tank, an ammonia refining tower, an ammonia refining tower top condenser, an ammonia refining tower reflux tank, an ammonia refining tower reboiler, a heat exchanger, an organic matter separation tower, an organic matter separation tower top condenser, an organic matter separation tower reflux tank, an organic matter separation tower reboiler, a carbonization kettle, and a filter, where the side of the first-stage washing tower is provided with an exhaust gas inlet to be treated, and the bottom of the first-stage washing tower is connected to the first-stage washing tower cooler and heat exchanger, respectively and both the first-stage washing tower and the first-stage washing tower cooler constitute a circulation loop. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for separating carbon dioxide and organic matter-containing ammonia, using the separation device, comprising (i) passing the tail gas A containing ammonia, carbon dioxide, polyol and cyclic carbonate and the washing liquid containing ammonia water from the washing tower to the first washing tower, where the carbon dioxide (CO2) in the tail gas A reacts with ammonia in the first scrubber to form ammonium bicarbonate, the polyol and cyclic carbonate organics in tail gas A are washed by the washing liquid to the tower kettle of the first-stage washing tower, where A part of the liquid produced in the tower kettle of the first-stage washing tower is cooled down and cooled by the first-stage washing tower cooler and returned to the first-stage washing tower, the other part is sent to the organic matter separation tower after being heat-exchanged by the heat exchanger and the ammonia gas, which is removed from carbon dioxide and organic matters that flows out from the top of the first-stage scrubbing tower and sent to the secondary washing tower; (ii) ensuring that the gas flowing from the top of the secondary scrubbing tower does not contain carbon dioxide and organic matter, ammonia water B is firstly passed into the secondary scrubbing tower, and then the gas flowing from the top of the primary scrubbing tower is passed into the secondary scrubbing tower, where the part of the washing liquid in the tower kettle of the first-stage washing tower is cooled by the second-stage washing tower cooler, and then returned to the second-stage washing tower, and the other part of the washing liquid is sent to the first-stage washing tower and the gas flowing out of the top of the second-stage washing tower is sent to the compressor; (iii) flowing gas from the top of the secondary washing tower i.e. sent to the compressor for pressurization, the pressurized gas phase material is sent to the ammonia refining tower and the pressurized liquid phase material is sent to a gas-liquid separation tank for gas-liquid separation and the liquid in the gas-liquid separation tank is sent to the secondary washing tower as washing liquid and the gas phase in the gas-liquid separation tank is sent to the ammonia refining tower; (iv) sending the gas phase materials after the compressor pressurization and the gas phase materials of the gas-liquid separation tank to the ammonia refining tower for ammonia refining, A part of the liquid produced in the reactor of the ammonia refining tower is heated by the reboiler of the ammonia refining tower and returned to the ammonia refining tower, and the other part is sent to the gas-liquid separation tank and the height of liquid level required during operation is controlled; (v) tower kettle washing liquid from the first-stage washing tower and the materials conveyed to the gas-liquid separation tank from the ammonia purification tower kettle are exchanged by the heat exchanger and sending to the organic matter separation tower for ammonia, water, and carbonic acid in the separation of ammonium hydroxide from polyols and cyclic carbonates, the gas phase at the top of the organic matter separation tower is condensed by the condenser at the top of the organic matter separation tower and sent to the reflux tank of the organic matter separation tower; The gaseous components CO2 and ammonia in the reflux tank of the organic matter separation tower are sent to the carbonization tank, the part of the liquid phase is refluxed, A part is sent to the carbonization kettle, tower kettle liquid E containing polyhydric alcohol, cyclic carbonate, and water in an organic matter separation tower, after the organic matter separation tower reboiler circulates to reboil, after testing for the absence of CO2and ammonia, and sent outside the boundary for further processing; (vi) taking the material entering the carbonization kettle containing CO2, ammonia and water, reacting in the carbonization kettle to form ammonium carbonate with crystal water; and (vii) sending the material in the carbonization kettle to the filter, the ammonium carbonate D containing crystal water after filtering is used as a by-product for sale and the filtrate is returned to the carbonization kettle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of separation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The separation device comprises first-stage washing tower, a first-stage washing tower cooler, a second-stage washing tower, a second-stage washing tower cooler, a compressor, a gas-liquid separation tank, an ammonia refining tower, an ammonia refining tower top condenser, an ammonia refining tower reflux tank, an ammonia refining tower reboiler, a heat exchanger, an organic matter separation tower, an organic matter separation tower top condenser, an organic matter separation tower reflux tank, an organic matter separation tower reboiler, a carbonization kettle, and a filter, where the side of the first-stage washing tower is provided with an exhaust gas inlet to be treated, and the bottom of the first-stage washing tower is connected to the first-stage washing tower cooler and heat exchanger, respectively and both the first-stage washing tower and the first-stage washing tower cooler constitute a circulation loop, and the top of first-stage washing tower is provided with a gas outlet to the second-stage first-stage washing tower, the outlet of the washing liquid of the second-stage washing tower is connected to the second-stage washing tower cooler and the first-stage washing tower respectively, and forms a circulation circuit with the second-stage washing tower cooler, and the gas outlet on the top of the second stage washing tower is connected to the compressor, the gas outlet of the compressor is connected to the ammonia refining tower, the liquid outlet of the compressor is connected with the gas-liquid separation tank for gas-liquid separation, the liquid outlet of the gas-liquid separation tank is connected with the second-stage washing tower, the gas outlet of the gas-liquid separation tank is connected with the ammonia refining tower, the gas outlet of the ammonia refining tower top condenser and ammonia refining tower return tank of ammonia of top of the refining tower is orderly connected to form a circulating loop and ammonia refining tower return tank in production of ammonia, ammonia purification tower bottom liquid outlet is divided into two parts, one part with the ammonia refining tower re-boiler connected to form a circulating loop, the other part is connected to a heat exchanger, a gas-liquid separation tank, a side inlet of organic matter separation tower is connected with the heat exchanger, the top outlet connecting organic separating tower top condenser and organic matter separation tower return tank, the bottom liquid outlet of the organic matter separation tower is divided into two parts, where one part is connected to organic matter separation tower reboiler and form a circulation loop, the other part after the detection is qualified and then sent to the next process, a gas outlet organic matter separation tower return tank connected to the carbonization kettle, the bottom liquid outlet are connected with the organic matter separation tower and carbonization kettle, the carbonization kettle bottom outlet is connected to a filter, and the filtrate outlet of the filter is connected to the carbonization kettle.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The exhaust gas A includes (i) ammonia, CO2, ethylene glycol or propylene glycol or glycerol produced in the process of preparing organic cyclic carbonates of ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate or glycerol carbonate by reacting urea with polyol substances, namely ethylene glycol or propylene glycol or glycerol polyol substance, and tail gas of organic cyclic carbonate of ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate or glycerol carbonate; (ii) mixed gas including ammonia, CO2 and organic matter with a boiling point higher than that of water; and (iii) ammonia, and CO2 mixed gas. Preferred Method: The operating conditions of the primary washing tower are: the temperature is 0-35 degrees C, the pressure is 0.1-1 MPa, the volume flow of liquid passing through the cooler of the first-stage washing tower is 2-80 times that of the liquid passing through the heat exchanger. The operating conditions of secondary washing tower are: the temperature is 0-35 degrees C, the pressure is 0.1-1 MPa, the volume flow of the liquid passing through the cooler of the secondary washing tower is 1-30 times the volume flow of the liquid sent to the primary washing tower, the ammonia water B is ammonia water having a concentration of 1-28 wt.%. In the method, when pressurizing, increasing the pressure of the gas flowing from the top of the scrubbing tower from 0.1-1.0 MPa to 1.4-2 MPa, the pressurization process can pass one or two or three or four or five stage or six stages of pressurization are completed, and the condensate produced in each stage of the pressurization process is sent to the gas-liquid separation tank. The operating conditions of the ammonia refining tower are: the temperature of the kettle is 65-90 degrees C, the temperature of the top of the tower is 25-55 degrees C, the pressure is 1.4-2 MPa, and the reflux ratio is 0.5-6:1. The operating conditions of the organic matter separation tower are: tower kettle is 80-200 degrees C, the top of the tower is 70-190 degrees C, the pressure is 0.03-0.3 MPa, reflow ratio is 0.5-3:1, organic matter separation tower, and decomposition of ammonium bicarbonate generates CO2 and ammonia. The operating conditions of the carbonization kettle are: the temperature is 5-40 degrees C, the pressure is 0.1-1 MPa, and the reaction time is 0.5-6 hours. The composition of the material E is extracted from the organic matter separation tower reactor except water, and the remaining depends on the type of polyols that undergo alcoholysis with urea and esters of the cyclic carbonates produced.

PT P
PN US2022195375-A1
TI Controlling bioreactor involves determining set point values for three parameters, such as nutrient, pH and oxygen level in bioreactor chamber, disposing first fluid in reservoir, growing cells in chamber, measuring actual values for parameters in chamber and flowing waste product out of chamber.
AU BERCOVICH N
   KARNIELI O
AE ADVA BIOTECHNOLOGY LTD (ADVA-Non-standard)
GA 202281686Q
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling a bioreactor involves determining set point values for at least three parameters in a bioreactor chamber, where three parameters are selected from a nutrient level in bioreactor chamber, a pH level in the bioreactor chamber, and an oxygen level in the bioreactor chamber, disposing a first fluid in a reservoir that is separate from the bioreactor chamber, where first fluid having a first nutrient value, a first pH value, and a first oxygen value, and disposing a second fluid in bioreactor chamber, where second fluid having a second nutrient value, a second pH value, and second oxygen value then second nutrient value is different than the first nutrient value, second pH value is different than the first pH value, and the second oxygen value is different than the first O2 value. The cells are grown in bioreactor chamber, and actual values for the at least three parameters are measured in bioreactor chamber.
   USE - Method for controlling a bioreactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls a bioreactor, which are directed towards growing cells or tissue while measuring various parameter, and adjusts fluid circuit automatically to adjust one to three parameters.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Controlling a bioreactor involves determining set point values for at least three parameters in a bioreactor chamber, where three parameters are selected from a nutrient level in bioreactor chamber, a pH level in the bioreactor chamber, and an oxygen level in the bioreactor chamber, disposing a first fluid in a reservoir that is separate from the bioreactor chamber, where first fluid having a first nutrient value, a first pH value, and a first oxygen value, and disposing a second fluid in bioreactor chamber, where second fluid having a second nutrient value, a second pH value, and second oxygen value then second nutrient value is different from the first nutrient value, second pH value is different from the first pH value, and the second oxygen value is different from the first oxygen value. The cells are grown in bioreactor chamber, actual values for the at least three parameters are measured in bioreactor chamber, and it is determined that the actual values are not equal to the set point values for each of the at least three parameters, a first liquid is flown from the bioreactor chamber into reservoir and second liquid is flown from the reservoir into the bioreactor chamber to adjust simultaneously each of actual values to approach corresponding set point value, and then waste product is flown out of the bioreactor chamber through an outlet after determining the set point values are not equal to the measured values.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating bioreactor by determining set point values for at least six parameters in a bioreactor chamber, where five parameters are selected from nutrient level in the bioreactor chamber, pH level in the bioreactor chamber, oxygen level in the bioreactor chamber, carbon dioxide level in the bioreactor chamber, and bicarbonate level in the bioreactor chamber; disposing a first fluid in a reservoir that is separate from the bioreactor chamber, where first fluid having a first nutrient value, a first pH value, a first oxygen value, a first carbon dioxide value, and a first bicarbonate value; disposing a second fluid in the bioreactor chamber, where second fluid has a second nutrient value, a second pH value, a second oxygen value, a second carbon dioxide value, and a second bicarbonate value, where second nutrient value is different from first nutrient value, second pH value is different from the first pH value, second oxygen value is different from the first oxygen value, second carbon dioxide value is different from the first carbon dioxide value, and the second bicarbonate value is different from the first bicarbonate value; growing cells in the bioreactor chamber; measuring actual values for the at least five parameters in the bioreactor chamber; determining that the actual values are not equal to the set point values for each of the at least five parameters; flowing a first liquid from bioreactor chamber into the reservoir and flowing a second liquid from the reservoir into the bioreactor chamber to adjust simultaneously each of actual values to approach the corresponding set point value; and flowing waste product out of the bioreactor chamber through an outlet after determining the set point values are not equal to the measured values.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The first liquid is flown from the bioreactor chamber into reservoir through a first fluid circuit, and second liquid is flown from the reservoir into the bioreactor chamber through a second circuit. The first fluid circuit is a different fluid circuit than the second fluid circuit. The first fluid circuit is fluidly isolated from the second fluid circuit. The set point values for at least three parameters in the bioreactor chamber are determined by using at least one sensor to measure nutrient level in bioreactor chamber, pH level in bioreactor chamber, and oxygen level in the bioreactor chamber. The set point values for the at least three parameters in the bioreactor chamber are determined by using a first sensor to measure the nutrient level in the bioreactor chamber, a second sensor to measure the pH level in the bioreactor chamber, and a third sensor to measure the oxygen level in the bioreactor chamber. The waste product is flown out of the bioreactor chamber by flowing solid and liquid waste through the outlet. The first liquid from bioreactor chamber is flown into the reservoir through a first fluid circuit, and second liquid is flown from the reservoir into the bioreactor chamber through a second circuit, where waste product is flown out of the bioreactor chamber through the outlet and waste is flown through a third fluid circuit, where first, second, and third fluid circuits are fluidly isolated from one another. The first fluid is disposed in reservoir through an inlet. The inlet is fluidly isolated from the outlet. The set point values for at least five parameters in the bioreactor chamber are determined by using at least one sensor to measure the nutrient level in the bioreactor chamber, pH level in the bioreactor chamber, oxygen level in the bioreactor chamber, carbon dioxide level in bioreactor chamber, and bicarbonate level in the bioreactor chamber. The set point values for at least five parameters in bioreactor chamber are determined by using a first sensor to measure nutrient level in bioreactor chamber, a second sensor to measure the pH level in bioreactor chamber, a third sensor to measure oxygen level in the bioreactor chamber, a fourth sensor to measure the carbon dioxide level in the bioreactor chamber, and a fifth sensor to measure the bicarbonate level in the bioreactor chamber.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN112791068-A; CN112791068-B
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide responsive            micro/nano drug delivery system involves dissolving            nanoparticles produced using modified polylactic            acid-polylysine in water, adding yeast microcapsules,            incubating, centrifuging and freeze-drying.
AU REN X
   WU C
   MA C
   DUAN Y
   ZANG C
   ZHANG X
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 2021591102
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide responsive                micro/nano drug delivery system involves dissolving                5-20 mg modified polylactic acid-polylysine and                4-16 mg budesonide in 5-20 mL dimethyl sulfoxide,                adding ultrapure water, stirring, dialyzing,                freeze-drying to produce polymer nanoparticles,                dispersing yeast cells in 30 mL 1 mol/L sodium                hydroxide solution, reacting at 90 degrees C for 1                hour, centrifuging, discharging supernatant to                obtain the precipitate, washing twice with 30 mL                ultrapure water, adding 30 mL hydrochloric acid                solution, reacting for 2 hours at 75 degrees C,                centrifuging at 3000-4000 rpm for 10-15 minutes to                obtain the precipitate, adding 20 mL ultrapure                water, washing, centrifuging, freeze-drying to                produce yeast microcapsule powder, dissolving                polymer nanoparticles in water, adding yeast                microcapsules such that mass ratio of nanoparticles                to yeast microcapsules (YGM) is 0.6-1, mixing,                incubating for 0.5-2 hours, centrifuging at 4000                rpm for 5-10 minutes, and freeze-drying.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide responsive                micro/nano drug delivery system for preparing drugs                used for treating asthma, pulmonary fibrosis,                chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, pneumonia                and lung injury, and preparation of inhalation                preparations for treatment of lung diseases (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide responsive micro/nano drug                delivery system can be prepared economically by                simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of carbon dioxide responsive                micro/nano drug delivery system involves dissolving                0.1-0.5 g polylactic acid having number average                molecular weight of 5000 in 5-25 mL chloroform,                adding 0.0116-0.0576 g N-hydroxysuccinimide,                0.0192-0.096 g                1-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-3-ethylcarbodiimide                hydrochloride, reacting with nitrogen at room                temperature for 4 hours, adding 64-316 mu L                ethylenediamine, reacting for 24-48 hours,                spin-evaporating the supernatant, adding 4-20 mL                anhydrous ether, centrifuging at 6000-8000 rpm for                10 minutes to obtain a precipitate, dialyzing with                ultrapure water for 3 days, freeze-drying to                produce amino-terminated polylactic acid,                dissolving 1-2 g N-benzyloxycarbonyl-L-Lysine in                14-27 mL tetrahydrofuran, reacting under nitrogen                protection at 50 degrees C for 10 minutes,                dissolving 0.26-1.05 g triphosgene in 7-13 mL                tetrahydrofuran to produce                N-benzyloxycarbonyl-L-Lysine solution, reacting for                1-2 hours, cooling, adding 50-110 mL n-hexane,                maintaining for 24 hours, precipitating at room                temperature, vacuum drying for 24 hours to obtain                benzyloxycarbonyl lysine anhydride, dissolving                0.02-0.05 g amino-terminated polylactic acid and                3-benzyloxycarbonyl lysine anhydride in 7 mL                N,N-dimethylformamide, reacting under nitrogen                protection at 40 degrees C for 72 hours, dialyzing                for 3 days, freeze-dried to obtain lactic                acid-poly(N-benzyloxycarbonyl-L-lysine), mixing                0.1-0.5 g obtained compound and 4-20 mL                trifluoroacetic acid, reacting for 30 minutes,                adding 0.3-1.5 mL acetic acid solution, reacting                for 1 hour, adding 10-50 mL anhydrous ether,                centrifuging, freeze-drying to produce polylactic                acid-polylysine polymer, dissolving 0.02-0.1 g                obtained polymer in dimethyl sulfoxide, adding 8-40                mu L N,N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal,                reacting at 60-70 degrees C for 3-4 hours,                dialyzing with ultrapure water for 3 days,                freeze-drying to obtain modified polylactic                acid-polylysine, dissolving 5-20 mg modified                polymer and 4-16 mg budesonide in 5-20 mL dimethyl                sulfoxide, adding ultrapure water, stirring,                dialyzing, freeze-drying to produce polymer                nanoparticles with a particle size of 200-250 nm,                dispersing yeast cells in 30 mL 1 mol/L sodium                hydroxide solution, reacting at 90 degrees C for 1                hour, centrifuging at 8000-12000 rpm for 10-15                minutes, discharging supernatant to obtain the                precipitate, washing twice with 30 mL ultrapure                water, adding 30 mL hydrochloric acid solution,                reacting for 2 hours at 75 degrees C, centrifuging                at 3000-4000 rpm for 10-15 minutes to obtain the                precipitate, adding 20 mL ultrapure water, washing,                centrifuging, freeze-drying to produce yeast                microcapsule powder, dissolving polymer                nanoparticles in ultrapure water, adding yeast                microcapsules such that mass ratio of nanoparticles                to yeast microcapsules (YGM) is 0.6-1, mixing,                incubating at room temperature for 0.5-2 hours,                centrifuging at 4000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, and                freeze-drying to produce drug delivery system with                a particle size of 3-6 mu m.
TF ACTIVITY - Antiasthmatic; Respiratory-Gen.;                Antiinflammatory.

TI Passaging and expanding working cells of public bank umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by preparing, observing cells, digesting, diluting, sampling, counting and viability rate, washing, sampling, culturing and recording information.
AU XIAO L
AE XIAO L (XIAO-Individual)
GA 202103978M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for passaging and expanding working cells of public bank umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells involves (a) preparing before operation, (b) taking out the non-polluting cells for observation, (c) digesting, (d) placing in a carbon dioxide incubator, letting to stand, taking out the culture flask, tapping, taking a culture flask, observing and terminating digestion, (e) transferring the cell suspension, washing the cell residues, combining the washed cell suspension, transferring to centrifuge tube and diluting, (f) sampling, counting, and viability rate, (g) centrifugally washing, (h) removing the supernatant and resuspending the cell suspension, (i) arranging the required T175 culture flasks in a row, marking and adding the culture medium, (j) sampling and seeding flasks, (k) capping, placing flat and shaking well, (l) placing into the carbon dioxide incubator for culture and (m) recording relevant information in time on the preparation file.
   USE - The method is useful for passaging and expanding working cells of public bank umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is conducive to establishment of standardized and standardized procedures for the whole process of cell passage and expansion of public bank umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, and improves the stability, safety and quality controllability of the public bank's umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell passage and expansion process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for passaging and expanding working cells of public bank umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells involves (a) preparing before operation, (b) taking out the non-polluting cells for observation by when the cell confluence is observed to reach 70-80%, then digesting and harvesting the cell, (c) digesting by adding 1-2 ml/flask pancreatin substitute to the culture flask, gently shaking the superimposed culture flask and infiltrating digestion solution of each culture flask through bottom of the culture flask, (d) placing in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, letting to stand for 1-2 minutes, taking out the culture flask, tapping gently, taking a culture flask, observing under an inverted microscope, and when the cells become rounded under the microscope, terminating digestion, (e) transferring the cell suspension in the culture flask after digestion to a 50 ml centrifuge tube, then washing the cell residues adhered to the culture flask with 10 ml sodium chloride injection, combining the washed cell suspension, transferring to a 50 ml centrifuge tube and diluting to 40-45 ml, (f) sampling, counting, and viability rate by pipetting and mixing the cell suspension after constant volume, taking not less than 0.5 ml cell suspension, and calculating the total number of harvested cells and cell viability, (g) centrifugally washing by balancing the centrifuge tube and placing into a centrifuge for centrifugation, (h) removing the supernatant and resuspending the cell suspension by removing the supernatant after centrifugation at 500 g, and resuspending the cell pellet after centrifugation with an appropriate amount of medium, (i) arranging the required T175 culture flasks in a row with respect to the number of T175 culture flasks for the final passage and the capacity of the biosafety cabinet to place T175 culture flasks at a time, placing upright in the biological safety cabinet, marking the cell code, cell generation number and culture time on the bottle wall, opening the lid and adding the culture medium with a 25 ml pipette, each of 21 ml, (j) sampling and seeding flasks by after the cells are blown evenly, adding 1 ml cell suspension to each T175 culture flask with 21 ml medium until the final volume of the cells and culture medium in each T175 flask is 22 ml, (k) capping, placing flat and shaking well by after adding the cell suspension, capping the T175 culture flask, tightening, placing the culture flask flat in a stack of 3, shaking at a constant speed for 10 seconds to make the cell suspension is added and evenly distributing the entire bottom surface of the culture bottle, (l) placing into the carbon dioxide incubator for culture by stacking the T175 culture flasks after the seed flasks are shaken in a stack of 3 into the carbon dioxide incubator and culturing until the cell fusion reaches 85-90%, and (m) recording relevant information in time on the preparation file.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves turning on each biological safety cabinet, each consumable cabinet and each section of the UV lamp in the Class C clean room in turn, irradiating for at least 30 minutes, then turning off the UV lamp in each section, turning on the space purification air conditioning fan for 30 minutes, turning off the UV of each consumable cabinet lights to make it in a running state, taking out the required consumables from the material storage cabinet of the C-level clean room, placing on the material cart, then pushing the material cart to the side of the biological safety cabinet, removing the culture medium from the 4 degrees C refrigerator in the material temporary storage room, placing on the material cart until it returns to room temperature, taking out the 0.9% sodium chloride injection, using a dust-free cloth sprayed with 75% ethanol to wipe it spirally from the bottle opening to the bottom of the bottle, removing the plastic cap of the bottle opening, placing in the biological safety cabinet, opening the stopper of the 0.9% sodium chloride injection bottle, fixing one side of the cotton ball at the junction of the rubber stopper and the glass bottle opening, wiping it around a circle, then wiping the top of the stopper with the other side of the cotton ball, discarding the ethanol cotton ball, letting to stand, after the ethanol at the opening of the bottle evaporates, making the opening of the bottle face 45 degrees horizontally, then clamping the edge of the rubber stopper with hemostatic forceps, avoiding touching from opening of the glass bottle with the hemostatic forceps, and pulling out the stopper in the direction of the bottle opening. The sodium chloride injection is used to gently rinse the cell culture flask 1-2 times before digestion in step (c). The calculation steps of the total number of cells and cell viability in step (f) involves taking 0.5 ml cell suspension, adding 0.5 ml 0.4% trypan blue staining solution, gently pipetting to mix, shaking the suspension evenly after staining for 2-3 minutes, pipetting a drop of the suspension onto a clean glass slide, adding a cover glass, under a low power microscope, randomly counting the total number of cells in the top, bottom, left, center and right five fields of view and the number of dead cells stained blue, and calculating the percentage of live cells to the total number of cells is the cell survival rate. In step (g), centrifugation is performed at 1200 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 8-10 minutes. In step (h), the final cell concentration is controlled at 0.87-1.05x 106 cells/ml. In step (l), the internal parameters of the carbon dioxide incubator are 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C temperature, 5 plus minus 0.2% carbon dioxide concentration and saturated humidity conditions. Preferred Medium: The medium is Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM-F12) added with 10% serum and 10 mg/ml epidermal growth factor (EGF).

PT P
PN WO2022168056-A1; US2022250832-A1
TI Moving bituminous material from location to            another location, comprises collecting bricks in            transport chamber at location, transporting into            another location by vehicle and environmentally            controlling transport chamber such that each brick            remains substantially solid during transport.
AU GIANNELIA P
AE PHILERGOS GROUP FOUND (PHIL-Non-standard)
   PHILERGOS GROUP FOUND (PHIL-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1542P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for moving bituminous material from a                first location to a second location, comprises (a)                collecting bricks in a transport chamber at the                first location, where each brick comprises                non-volatile bituminous material formed into an                irregular solid defined by non-planar surfaces                configured to reduce surface contact with adjacent                bricks, (b) transporting the transport chamber and                bricks into the second location by vehicle and (c)                environmentally controlling the transport chamber                such that each brick remains substantially solid                during transport.
   USE - The method is used for moving bituminous                material from a first location to a second location                and reducing carbon dioxide emissions when moving                bituminous material. The bituminous materials                includes bitumen, polymer modified bitumen, heavy                crude oil, extra heavy crude oil, asphalt, polymer                modified asphalt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares bituminous materials and                polymer modified bituminous materials for transport                in a manner that would increase buoyancy if spilled                into aquatic environments, making it easier to                clean up should any spill into lakes, rivers, or                oceans. The method maintains the desired                environment within the transport chamber holding                the bricks simply by the flow of air, water, or                other substance that naturally occurs as the                vehicle carrying the transport chamber moves, which                minimizes energy needs. The method further reduces                or eliminates harmful carbon dioxide emissions                using low- or zero-emissions vehicles to carry                transport containers with passive environmental                control systems in addition to the benefits that                result from transporting bituminous materials                without diluent and not having to heat bituminous                material to transport it by vehicle as a liquid as                is the current practice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for reducing carbon dioxide emissions when moving                bituminous material from the first location to                second location, comprising (a1) providing a                transport chamber comprising an environmental                control system, (b1) collecting bricks in the                transport chamber at the first location, where each                brick comprises non-volatile bituminous material                formed into an irregularly shaped solid defined by                faces configured to minimize surface contact with                adjacent bricks, (c1) transporting the transport                chamber and bricks collected therein to the second                location with a vehicle and (d1) with the                environmental control system, environmentally                controlling the transport chamber during transport                such that each brick remains substantially                solid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The transport chamber                  comprises an aerodynamic rail car. The transport                  chamber comprises an aerodynamic semi-trailer of                  a truck. The transport chamber comprises the                  cargo area of the ship. The transport chamber                  further comprises an air conditioning system                  positioned in the container, where the air                  conditioning system removably couples to the fuel                  cell.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The transport chamber is                  in fluid communication with a water distribution                  system and the step of environmentally                  controlling the transport chamber comprises                  distributing water from the water distribution                  system into the transport chamber. The transport                  chamber defines vents and the step of                  environmentally controlling the transport chamber                  comprises drawing ambient air into the transport                  chamber through the vents. The vehicle comprises                  a low-emissions ship powered by a fuel cell and                  is positioned in a body of water, the transport                  chamber comprises a cargo area of the ship, the                  environmental control system comprises a water                  distribution system in fluid communication with                  the cargo area of the ship and the water                  surrounding the ship and the step of                  environmentally controlling the transport chamber                  comprises sourcing water from the ship's                  surroundings and distributing it into the                  transport chamber. The vehicle comprises a                  low-emissions train powered by a fuel cell, the                  transport chamber comprises an aerodynamic rail                  car, the environmental control system comprises                  vents defined by the aerodynamic rail car, and                  the step of environmentally controlling the                  transport chamber comprises drawing air into the                  aerodynamic rail car through the vents. The                  vehicle comprises a low-emissions truck, the                  transport chamber comprises an aerodynamic                  semi-trailer, the environmental control system                  comprises vents defined by the aerodynamic                  semi-trailer, and the step of environmentally                  controlling the transport chamber comprises                  drawing air into the aerodynamic semi-trailer                  through the vents. Preferred Components: The each                  brick of the bricks is further configured with a                  skeleton distributed throughout the solidified                  bituminous materials. The each brick of the                  bricks is further configured with buoyant                  features positioned about the skeleton.
