TI Sugar-less jumping candy comprises isomalt, carbon dioxide, and ingredients including flavor and fragrance, cochineal natural pigment, vitamin C, and steviol glycosides.
AU HONG Y
AE GUANGDONG BAIDA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202219020C
AB    NOVELTY - Sugar-less jumping candy comprises 98.4-99.4% isomalt, 0.5-0.6% carbon dioxide, and 0.1-1% ingredients.
   USE - Used as sugar-less jumping candy.
   ADVANTAGE - The candy has auxiliary materials effect, covers bad after taste of high sweetness sweetener, and isomalt heat value is about half of sucrose, the plasma glucose and insulin level has no obvious effect, it can be eaten by the diabetes patient, children also can be eaten safely; it will not affect the health; at the same time, can prepare different tastes of jumping candy, which can meet the needs of different people, and improves interest of jumping candy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the sugar-free jumping candy, comprising (i) dispensing the raw material isomalt; (ii) adding isomalt and deionized water to the sugar-dissolving pot at a mass ratio of 7:1, heating, dissolving until the isomalt is completely dissolved to form a syrup state using a 40 mesh filter and filtering; (iii) transfering the filtered isomalt syrup to the sugar boiling equipment, keeping steam pressure of sugar boiling should at about 0.7-0.8 Mpa, where the temperature of sugar boiling should be set in the range of 150-155degreesC, boiling sugar needs to be vacuumed to 0.06 Mpa; (iv) stirring and aerating the isomalt syrup that has been boiled and evacuating into the stirring pot, and charging the carbon dioxide into the sugar body within the specified pressure, time and speed, and stirring to maintain a pressure of 4.0-4.5 Mpa inflate for about 7 minutes; (v) transporting the aerated syrup to the cooling pipe, forming under the pressure of 3-4 Mpa, and cooling the charging pipe by water circulation for about 4 hours; (vi) unloading the loading truck directly under the discharge port, and opening the valve to unload the material; (vii) sieving large particles, breaking particles and powder in the formed popping candy with a vibrating sieve to obtain popping candy with moderate particles; (viii) detecting the screened semi-finished products through a metal detector using a standard test piece for testing every hour or before switching on and off the machine; and (ix) passing sugar-free popping candy that through the metal detector will be sealed and packaging.

TI Processing method of plastic regeneration            particle, involves turning off flame and passing in            low-temperature carbon dioxide to cool recycled plastic            and drying and storing processed recycled plastic            particles in storage.
AU HONG J
AE JIANGSU HONGMEN IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C9363K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using a roller coating                machine to coat a surface of the plastic particles                with epoxy resin particles. The recycled plastic                particles are baked to melt the epoxy resin                particles on a csurface and form a resin film. The                baked recycled plastic particles are soaked in 95%                industrial ethanol for 30-45 minutes. The recycled                plastic particles are drained and laid flat on a                metal mesh. A surface of the recycled plastic is                flame treated from the bottom of the metal mesh                with a flame at 200degreesC. The flame is turned                OFF and pass in low-temperature carbon dioxide to                cool the recycled plastic. The processed recycled                plastic particles are dried and stored in storage.                A he epoxy resin raw material is soaked with an                alkaline solution to reduce the water absorption of                epoxy resin. The two exhaust pipes on a processing                box (2) are respectively connected with a carbon                dioxide supply pipe and a heating pipe.
   USE - Processing method of plastic regenerative                particle.
   ADVANTAGE - The flame-treats the particles to realize the                repair and filling of the pores on the surface of                the plastic particles. The mixed layer of resin and                plastic is formed on the surface of the plastic                particles to reduce the water absorption of the                plastic particles and prevent plastics. The epoxy                resin is prevented from adsorbing a large amount of                water by reducing the adsorptivity of the epoxy                resin. The mixed layer of resin and plastic is                formed on the surface of the plastic particles, to                reduce the water absorption of the plastic                particles and prevent the plastic particles from                being trapped. The rows of uniformly distributed                air ducts are fixedly connected to the processing                box, which quickly spreads the plastic recycled                particles on the metal mesh. The device is adjusted                according to the flame size, to ensure that the                flame outside the flame is in contact with the                metal mesh. The mixed layer of resin and plastic is                formed on the surface of the plastic particles to                reduce the water absorption of the plastic                particles and prevent the plastic particles from                being damp during storage.

TI Servo motor cooling device, has servo motor main body whose upper end is provided with semiconductor refrigerating sheet, silencing plates formed with multiple silencing holes and heat conducting sleeve filled with graphene powder.
AU LU C
   CHEN G
   FENG X
AE LU C (LUCC-Individual)
GA 202192652J
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a servo motor main body (1) whose upper end is provided with a semiconductor refrigerating sheet (2), where a lower end of the semiconductor refrigerating sheet is formed as a heat absorbing end and an upper end refrigerating sheet formed as a radiating end. An upper end of a radiating cover (3) is provided with a heat dissipation pore (14). An inner wall of a liquid storage tank is connected with a waterproof ventilating film. The liquid storage tank is filled with carbon dioxide solution. An end of a piston is connected with a telescopic air bag and a first magnet. An inner wall of an air extracting cylinder is connected with a second magnet. Two silencing plates are formed with multiple silencing holes and made of calcium silicate and glass wool materials. An inner wall of a heat conducting port is fixedly connected with a dustproof net that is made of stainless steel materials. A heat conducting sleeve is filled with graphene powder.
   USE - Servo motor cooling device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes working process of the servo motor main body, absorbs heat of the servo motor main body by the lower end of the semiconductor refrigerating sheet, radiates heat of the servo motor main body by the radiating end, conducts heat through the radiating holes and the graphene powder in the heat conducting sleeve, vibrates the servo motor body during use to overflow carbon dioxide gas in carbon dioxide aqueous solution, expands the telescopic air bag to push the piston to carry out phase separation movement for extracting hot air and discharging the hot air quickly and realizes movement of the piston to attract the first magnet and the second magnet so as to generate strong repulsion force, so that the piston is reversely pushed back by a spring, thus realizing continuous air exhaust process. The device passes noise generates by the servo motor main body through the silencing hole in the silencing plate to resonate and convert into sound energy and cooperates with water in the annular water bag to absorb the noise for reaching effect of reducing the noise.

TI Method for regenerating adsorption vessels used for hydrogen sulfide recovery from Claus exhaust gas, by feeding hydrogenated gas stream to quench tower to produce quenched gas stream by recovering liquid water via water condensate stream.
AU LITHOXOOS G
   WANG Y
   OTHMAN R M
   ALAMI I A
   OCONNELL J P
   DUVAL S A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202173538V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding an exhaust gas stream (120) to a hydrogenation reactor (104) to produce a hydrogenated gas stream (124) by converting sulfur-containing compounds to hydrogen sulfide. The exhaust gas stream includes a Claus exhaust gas comprising the sulfur-containing compounds. The hydrogenated gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide, water, and at least one of carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The hydrogenated gas stream is in the absence of the sulfur-containing compounds other than hydrogen sulfide. The hydrogenated gas stream is fed to a quench tower (106) to produce a quenched gas stream (126) by condensing and recovering liquid water via a first water condensate stream. The quenched gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide and at least one of: water, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen. The quenched gas stream is fed to a first stage adsorption vessel (150) of a first stage adsorption unit (110) to produce a first outlet gas stream (132) by adsorbing water.
   USE - Method for regenerating adsorption vessels used for hydrogen sulfide recovery from Claus exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - Higher sulfur removal and recovery efficiency can be achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The first outlet gas stream is in the absence of water. The first outlet gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide and at least one of: carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The first outlet gas stream is fed to a second stage adsorption vessel (152) of a second stage adsorption unit (112) to produce a second byproduct gas stream (138) by adsorbing hydrogen sulfide. The second byproduct gas stream is in the absence of hydrogen sulfide. The first stage adsorption vessel is regenerated to produce a first byproduct gas stream (136) by desorbing water. The first byproduct gas stream comprises water. The second stage adsorption vessel is regenerated to produce a second outlet gas stream (140) comprising hydrogen sulfide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a Claus exhaust gas treatment system for sulfur recovery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram of a process for Claus exhaust gas treatment.

PN CN112979594-A; CN112979594-B
TI Super critically extracting andrographolide comprises adding common Andrographis herb, cellulase, crushing, heating, introducing the supercritical extraction liquid, adding ethanol solution, extracting, concentrating and drying.
AU SHEN L
   WU J
AE NANTONG KEXIN SUPERCRITICAL EQUIP CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2021723362
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (i) taking common Andrographis herb in the crushing tank, adding cellulase, crushing by plant tissue crushing method to obtain primary extracting liquid, (ii) taking the primary extracting solution, using the heating device to heat the extractor to the supercritical temperature, ensuring the initial extracting solution and carbon dioxide reaches the temperature needed by supercritical extraction, obtaining the supercritical extraction liquid, (iii) introducing the supercritical extraction liquid obtained in (ii) into the separator, adding 75% ethanol solution into the separator for mixing and dissolving, and extracting to obtain the extracting mixed solution, (iv) overlapping multiple macroporous resin purifying layer up and down in the eluting device, (v) introducing the eluent into the liquid collecting cylinder, vacuum extracting and recycling ethanol, concentrating and drying to obtain the andrographolide extract.
   USE - The method is useful for super critically extracting andrographolide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is more beneficial for fully separating the extracting substance, so as to realize the prepared andrographolide yield is increased; improves purity; and obtains product with high yield and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Super critically extracting andrographolide comprises (i) taking common Andrographis herb in the crushing tank, adding cellulase, crushing by plant tissue crushing method to obtain primary extracting liquid, (ii) taking the primary extracting solution, introducing into an extractor, and shunting and pressurizing into the extractor carbon dioxide, using the heating device to heat the extractor to the supercritical temperature, ensuring the initial extracting solution and carbon dioxide reaches the temperature needed by supercritical extraction, obtaining the supercritical extraction liquid, (iii) introducing the supercritical extraction liquid obtained in (ii) into the separator, adding 75% ethanol solution into the separator for mixing and dissolving, and extracting to obtain the extracting mixed solution, (iv) preparing an eluting device and a liquid collecting cylinder, arranging the eluting device with multiple macroporous resin purifying layer, overlapping multiple macroporous resin purifying layer up and down in the eluting device, introducing extracted mixed liquid obtained in the (iii) into the eluting device by the liquid inlet pipe, using the ethanol liquid inlet pipe to synchronously introduce 45% of ethanol solution into the liquid inlet pipe, using multiple ultrasonic vibrators staggered distributed between the macroporous resin purification layer for ultrasonic mixing and eluting, (v) introducing the eluent into the liquid collecting cylinder through multiple discharging valve ports on the eluting device, vacuum extracting and recycling ethanol, concentrating and drying to obtain the andrographolide extract.

PN CN112937609-A
TI Obtaining approximate vacuum of closed system by chemical involves generating a closed system, requiring the container to withstand a negative pressure in the system and letting the hydrogen be fully combusted in system.
AU LU S
AE LU S (LUSS-Individual)
GA 202170759K
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining approximate vacuum of closed system by chemical involves generating a closed system, requiring the container to withstand a negative pressure of only 0.01 atmospheres in the system, letting the hydrogen be fully combusted in the system, removing the oxygen in the system, and then placing enough anhydrous calcium chloride inside to completely absorb the water vapor in the system, placing enough metal magnesium and ignite or discharge, letting the magnesium fully burn, and removing the nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the system, removing calcium chloride hexahydrate, using molecular sieve to absorb magnesium nitride, magnesium oxide, free carbon, and then removing the molecular sieve, subjecting to heat dehydration of calcium chloride hexahydrate and regenerating, adding water, magnesium nitrate and water to obtain magnesium hydroxide and ammonia.
   USE - Method for obtaining approximate vacuum of closed system by chemical.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing consumption of metal magnesium, removing nitrogen of air of a cubic meter, oxygen, carbon dioxide and other gas combustion magnesium about 2.51 kg, reducing 0.45 kg, generating steam more than 0.3375 kg and consuming anhydrous calcium chloride of 0.3469 kg.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Obtaining approximate vacuum of closed system by chemical involves generating a closed system, requiring the container to withstand a negative pressure of only 0.01 atmospheres in the system, letting the hydrogen be fully combusted in the system, removing the oxygen in the system, and then placing enough anhydrous calcium chloride inside to completely absorb the water vapor in the system, placing enough metal magnesium and ignite or discharge, letting the magnesium fully burn, and removing the nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the system, removing calcium chloride hexahydrate, using molecular sieve to absorb magnesium nitride, magnesium oxide, free carbon, and then removing the molecular sieve, subjecting to heat dehydration of calcium chloride hexahydrate and regenerating, adding water, magnesium nitrate and water to obtain magnesium hydroxide and ammonia, recovering ammonia, reacting magnesium hydroxide and hydrochloric acid to obtain magnesium chloride and water, washing with water to remove magnesium chloride, reacting magnesium oxide and hydrochloric acid to obtain magnesium chloride and water, washing with water to removing magnesium chloride, heating, drying and dehydrating until the free carbon particles are burned and recycling of metallic magnesium by electrolyzing magnesium chloride in the molten state to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The hydrogen combustion is carried out by reacting hydrogen and oxygen to water, and the equation for calcium chloride to absorb water includes reacting calcium chloride and water to obtain calcium chloride hexahydrate. The elemental magnesium and nitrogen includes treating magnesium and nitrogen to obtain magnesium nitride. The unit space of detection is 1 cubic meter, and 1 cubic meter of air needs to burn 3 kg of magnesium to remove nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide and other gases, 0.00025 kg anhydrous calcium chloride is required, and 1.1839 kg ammonia and collected important raw material for nitrogen fertilizer. The calcium chloride dehydration includes calcium chloride and water to obtain calcium chloride hexahydrate.

   (A) adding two antifreeze drip tanks before the feed gas enters the rectification separation tower, dividing into first antifreeze drip tank and second antifreeze drip tank, two antifreeze drip tanks are connecting in parallel with feed gas inlet pipeline, connecting middle of first antifreeze drip tank to raw material gas transmission pipe through pipe providing with first valve, and bottom is connecting to the raw material gas transmission pipe through pipe provided with third valve, middle portion of the second antifreeze drip tank is connecting to the raw material gas conveying pipe through pipe providing with second valve, connecting top of the first antifreeze drip tank with inhibitor addition pipeline with seventh valve and pressure relief pipeline with fifth valve, connecting top of the second antifreeze drip tank with inhibitor addition pipeline with eighth valve and pressure relief pipeline with sixth valve, closing first valve, third valve, fifth valve, opening seventh valve, filling tube from seventh valve with inhibitor, then closing seventh valve and opening first valve, filling drip tank with pressure, after pressure is balanced, opening third valve to make inhibitor drip into the pipeline and mixing with raw material gas, so that raw material gas and inhibitor are mixing, mixed raw material entering rectification separation tower, operating second antifreeze drip tank, opening fourth valve of second antifreeze drip tank for infusion, and closing third valve; and
   (B) opening fifth valve for pressure relief, opening seventh valve to re-add inhibitor.

TI Device for degrading phenol in chemical wastewater, has infusion structure that is installed above gas pipe, observation window that is inlaid on outer surface of degradation box, and timer that is installed above observation window.
AU YAN C

GA 202013778N
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a degradation tank (1) and an infusion structure (10). A box cover (2) is installed at the top of the degradation box. A water inlet pipe (3) is provided inside of the box cover. A stirring shaft (4) is installed inside the degradation box. A stirring driving structure (5) is provided at the lower right of the stirring shaft. A drain pipe (6) is installed at the lower left end of the degradation tank. A water stop valve (7) is installed in the middle position of the drain pipe. An air delivery pipe (8) is arranged above the drainage pipe. A single sealed valve structure (9) is installed inside the gas pipe. The infusion structure is installed above the gas pipe. A flow sensor (11) is installed in the middle position of the infusion structure. An observation window is inlaid on the outer surface of the degradation box. A timer is installed above the observation window.
   USE - Device for degrading phenol in chemical wastewater such as process waste water, cooling water, waste washing water, equipment and site washing water discharged in chemical production.
   ADVANTAGE - The device conveniently stirs and mixes the concentrated wastewater in the degradation tank through a stirring shaft so that the added sodium hydroxide solution and carbon dioxide gas fully react with the phenol in the concentrated wastewater, thus, the degradation rate of the phenol in the wastewater is increased. The outflow of waste water is avoided to ensure the tightness when carbon dioxide is added. The convenience of adding carbon dioxide is ensured through the setting of gas pipelines and single sealed valve structure. The flow sensor conveniently measures the amount of sodium hydroxide solution added, which easily ensures the adequacy of the amount of sodium hydroxide solution added and ensures the safety when using sodium hydroxide solution, which greatly improves the practicability of device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for degrading phenol in chemical wastewater.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the structure of the device for degrading phenol in chemical wastewater.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The pump structure works to extract the sodium hydroxide solution in the storage tank through the liquid guide tube. The sealed ball valve is separated from the gasket, such that the carbon dioxide gas is flushed into the degradation tank, and phenol and sodium bicarbonate solution is obtained through the reaction.

PN CN110076430-A; CN110076430-B
TI Gas shielded welding method of steel plate by welding mechanical engineering steel substrate under carbon dioxide and argon-rich protective gas atmosphere, depositing solid wire, placing to holding furnace, and cooling to room temperature.
AU FANG Y
   HUANG Z
   MU W
   CHEN F
   HU Y
AE WUHAN STEEL CO LTD (WSGC-C)
GA 2019692771
AB    NOVELTY - A gas shielded welding method of 1000 MPa greater than or equal to 40 mm thick steel plate includes welding mechanical engineering steel substrate under carbon dioxide and argon-rich protective gas atmosphere, depositing solid wire by preheating substrate, performing filling and cap gas shielded welding, subjecting to multi-layer and multi-pass continuous welding, and controlling interlayer temperature; and placing to holding furnace, cooling to room temperature or immediately covering in joint area within 300 mm of both sides of weld, and cooling to room temperature.
   USE - The method is for gas shielded welding of 1000 MPa greater than or equal to 40 mm thick steel plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The welded joint has excellent strength and toughness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A gas shielded welding method of 1000 MPa greater than or equal to 40 mm thick steel plate comprises welding not less than 40 mm thick mechanical engineering steel substrate under 20 vol.% carbon dioxide and 80 vol.% argon-rich protective gas atmosphere, depositing 1.2 mm diameter solid wire by preheating substrate at 90-160 degrees C, performing filling and cap gas shielded welding, subjecting to multi-layer and multi-pass continuous welding at 13-16 kJ/cm, current of 250-280 A, voltage of 27-29 V and speed of 28-32 cm/minute until the weld is filled, and controlling interlayer temperature at 10-200 degrees C; and placing to holding furnace, cooling to room temperature or immediately covering in joint area within 300 mm of both sides of weld, and cooling to room temperature when heated to 200 degrees C. The substrate has tensile strength of greater than or equal to 1000 MPa, yield strength of greater than or equal to 890 MPa, elongation rate of greater than or equal to 12%, and - 20 degrees C CKV2 impact work of greater than or equal to 42 J. The solid wire has tensile strength of greater than or equal to 1000 MPa, elongation rate of greater than or equal to 15%, section shrinkage of greater than or equal to 60% and - 20 degrees C CKV2 impact work of greater than or equal to 56 J.

PN CN108576755-A
TI Preparing betel nut smoky flavor natural spice comprises drying waste areca fruit seed, pulverizing, extracting, centrifuging, and mixing supercritical extract and the solvent ultrasonic extraction component.
AU YI Z
   LIANG J
   SHAO C
   ZHAN L
AE GUANGDONG MINGKANG FLAVORS & FRAGRANCES (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201879866G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing betel nut smoky flavor natural spice comprises (i) taking waste areca fruit seed, and drying by microwave, (ii) pulverizing the areca seed, (iii) adding the pulverized betel nut seed powder into the barrel of the supercritical extraction kettle, and adding two pieces of filter paper at one end of the barrel for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (iv) collecting the betel nut seed extract in the separation tank I and the separation kettle II, (v) centrifuging the collected betel nut seed extract, and preparing the obtained betel nut seed supercritical extract, (vi) subjecting the residue to solvent ultrasonic extraction, concentrating liquid under reduced pressure, and (vii) mixing supercritical extract and the solvent ultrasonic extraction component.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing betel nut smoky flavor natural spice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing betel nut smoky flavor natural spice comprises (i) taking waste areca fruit seed, drying by microwave, where the microwave drying power is 100-300 w, temperature is 45-60 degrees C, and drying time is 1-5 hours, (ii) pulverizing the areca seed by super micron mill, (iii) adding the pulverized betel nut seed powder into the barrel of the supercritical extraction kettle, and adding two pieces of filter paper at one end of the barrel for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, the conditions of supercritical extraction are: extraction pressure is 30-40 MPa, extraction temperature is 35-55 degrees C, separation tank I pressure is 5-7 MPa, separation kettle II pressure is 6-8 MPa, separation kettle I temperature is 30-40 degrees C, separation kettle II temperature is 30-40 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate is 25-45 l/hour, and the extraction time is 30-90 minutes to obtain betel nut smoky flavor natural spice, (iv) collecting the betel nut seed extract in the separation tank I and the separation kettle II, (v) centrifuging the collected betel nut seed extract to collect the lower layer liquid, and preparing the obtained betel nut seed supercritical extract, (vi) subjecting the residue to solvent ultrasonic extraction, extracting the residue raw material with 1 times of water and 1 times of edible alcohol, ultrasonically extracting at 0-10 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, extracting 1-2 times, concentrating liquid under reduced pressure to a density of 1.3-1.5 mg/ml, and (vii) mixing supercritical extract and the solvent ultrasonic extraction component.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the effect of the ultrafine pulverization of betel nut seeds is 1 kg after the betel nut seed is pulverized, and the sieving rate after passing through the 80 meshes sieve is 99.5%. In the step (iii), the carbon dioxide gas has a purity of more than 99.5%. Preferred Method: In the step (v), the rotation speed is 6000-8000 rotations/minute and the time is 20-30 minutes.

TI Waste and carbide slag comprehensive treatment system comprises carbide slag treatment unit, mixed molding unit and high temperature firing unit, where the carbide slag process unit comprises carbide slag inlet and gas shortage entrance.
AU LIU W
   DING L
   MA Z
   WU D
AE SHENWU TECHNOLOGY GROUP CORP CO LTD (BJSW-C)
GA 201803786Y
AB    NOVELTY - Waste and carbide slag comprehensive treatment system comprises carbide slag treatment unit, mixed molding unit, pellet pyrolysis unit and high temperature firing unit. The carbide slag processing unit comprises a carbide slag inlet, gas shortage entrance, syngas exports and exports of calcium carbide slag after drying. The hybrid molding unit includes a dry carbide slag inlet, waste entrance and mixed pellet exports. The dried carbide slag inlet and the dried carbide slag outlet are connected. The mixing pellet inlet is connected to the mixing pellet outlet.
   USE - Used as waste and carbide slag comprehensive treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system produces high quality synthesis gas and has high performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Waste and carbide slag comprehensive treatment system comprises carbide slag treatment unit, mixed molding unit, pellet pyrolysis unit and high temperature firing unit. The carbide slag processing unit comprises a carbide slag inlet, gas shortage entrance, syngas exports and exports of calcium carbide slag after drying. The hybrid molding unit includes a dry carbide slag inlet, waste entrance and mixed pellet exports. The dried carbide slag inlet and the dried carbide slag outlet are connected. The pellet pyrolysis unit includes a mixing pellet inlet, flue gas exports, shortage of gas exports and pyrolysis pellets exports. The mixing pellet inlet is connected to the mixing pellet outlet. The shortage gas outlet is connected to the shortage gas inlet. The high temperature firing unit includes a pyrolysis pellet inlet, carbon dioxide-rich gas exports and roasted ceramsite exports. The pyrolysis pellet inlet is connected to the pyrolysis pellet outlet. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for waste and carbide slag comprehensive treatment, comprising (i) carbide slag treatment: adding the carbide slag into the carbide slag treatment unit, generating pyrolytic waste by the direct contact with the dry gas, obtaining dry carbide residue, adsorbing the carbide slag the tar in the shortage gas, dust and carbon dioxide and syngas; (ii) mixing the dried carbide slag with the waste material in a mixing and molding unit and pressing to form a mixed pellet; (iii) adding the mixed pellets into the pellet pyrolysis unit, pyrolyzing by the shortage of gas and obtaining pyrolysis pellets; and (iv) adding the pyrolysis pellets directly into the high temperature firing unit, calcining to produce high concentrations of carbon dioxide, hardening heavy metals by minerals and coker and obtaining building ceramsite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further includes a waste preprocessing unit. The waste pretreatment unit is composed of a bag breaking mechanism, a rolling sieve mechanism, a sorting mechanism and a crushing mechanism which are sequentially connected. The crushing mechanism is connected to the hybrid molding unit. The pellet pyrolysis unit is a device used by the preheating zone, a pyrolysis zone and pyrolysis of the two zones of the oxygen-generating pyrolysis device. The mixing pellet inlet and the mixing pellet outlet are connected by a conveying device. The conveying device is provided with a flue gas inlet connected with the flue gas outlet near the inlet of the mixing pellet. The carbide slag processing unit comprises an air drying bed and a gas-solid separation device connected to each other. The carbide slag inlet and the shortage of gas inlet is provided in the air drying bed. The synthesis gas outlet and the dried carbide outlet are provided in the gas-solid separation device. The hybrid molding unit comprises a mixing device and a molding device which are connected with each other. The dried carbide slag inlet and the waste inlet are provided in the mixing device. The mixing pellet outlet is provided in the forming device. The size of the waste material is controlled at greater than or equal to 15 mm. In the step (ii), the mass ratio of the rubbish material and the dried carbide slag is 0.8-1.2:1. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), adding the pellets are fed into the pellet pyrolysis unit, subjecting the pellets are sequentially into preheating zone, pyrolysis zone and pyrolysis zone to complete pyrolysis, the preheating is carried out at 350-500 degrees C, the pyrolysis zone temperature is 700-800 degrees C and the pyrolysis zone two temperature is 750-850 degrees C.

PN US2018003067-A1; WO2018005846-A1; IN201947001661-A; CN109642677-A; EP3478994-A1; JP2019521277-W; US10626743-B2; JP6799088-B2; EP3478994-B1; CN109642677-B
TI Turbomachine used in large-scale carbon dioxide            (CO2) production system, compressor, aircraft engine,            has first segmented seal ring which is slidably coupled            to stator and defines face seal clearance between rotor            and seal bearing faces.
AU BIDKAR R A
   SEVINCER E
   THATTE A M
   WANG J
   ZHENG X
   BIKA R A
   SAVENSE E
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC CO (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC CO (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC CO (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC CO (GENE-C)
GA 201801234X
AB    NOVELTY - The turbomachine has a stator. A rotor                comprises rotor bearing face. A face seal assembly                (102) comprises a first/second segmented seal ring                (114,116) comprising multiple joints and a                first/second flat-contact surface (160,166). The                second segmented seal ring is coupled to first                segmented seal ring comprises a seal bearing face                (132) such that second flat-contact surface is in                contact with first flat-contact surface. The                segment ends is offset from joints. The first                segmented seal ring is slidably coupled to stator                and defines a face seal clearance between rotor and                seal bearing faces.
   USE - Turbomachine such as gas turbine, steam                turbine, supercritical carbon dioxide turbine, and                hydro turbine used in large-scale carbon dioxide                (CO2) production system, compressor, aircraft                engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The turbomachine minimizes the axial step or                discontinuity at the seal bearing face and                consecutively improves the rigidity of the first                segmented seal ring of the face seal assembly, thus                reducing deformation of the first segmented seal                ring. Choosing dissimilar materials for the first                and second segmented seal rings allow the seal                cross-section to deform differently under thermal                loads, thus allowing the designer/user/operator to                control or optimize deformation of the seal bearing                face. The method includes a step of coupling each                joint of mulitiple joints using mulitiple coupling                devices to increase rigidity of the face seal                assembly and reduce deformation of the first                segmented seal ring. The machining of segmented                seal rings in a separate machining process and then                assembling the segmented seal rings on a relatively                large sized rotor without the need to perform                machining of the segmented seal rings in the rotor,                minimizes the axial step on the bearing face. The                mulitiple joints is coupled to each other using                mulitiple coupling units and mulitiple clamps to                reinforce the joining ends of each of mulitiple                joints, thus increasing the rigidity of the face                seal assembly and reducing deformation of the first                segmented seal ring.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of assembling a face seal assembly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded perspective                view of a face seal assembly and a stator                adaptor.
   Face seal assembly (102)
   First segmented seal ring (114)
   Second segmented seal ring (116)
   Seal bearing face (132)
   First flat-contact surface (160)
   Second flat-contact surface (166)

PT P
PN WO2022212473-A1
TI Controlled gasification of carbonaceous feedstock,            by pyrolyzing feedstock to produce gas product and            solid product, incomplete combustion step of resultant            carbon producing carbon monoxide, and controlling            resultant carbon step using feedback and providing            carbon monoxide gas product.
AU TUCKER R D
AE TUCKER R D (TUCK-Individual)
GA 2022C7663M
AB    NOVELTY - Process for the controlled gasification of a                carbonaceous feedstock, involves pyrolyzing                feedstock to produce a gas product and a solid                product, where the gas product comprises organic                gases and tars/oils and the solid product comprises                carbon, an incomplete combustion step of the                resultant carbon producing carbon monoxide, and                controlling the incomplete combustion of resultant                carbon step using feedback related to the carbon                monoxide bill value of the gas product, and                providing a predictable and stable carbon monoxide                gas product.
   USE - The process is used for the controlled                gasification of a carbonaceous feedstock from                decomposing materials (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process has efficient processes for the                gasification of carbonaceous feed stocks by a                pyrolysis step augmented with an incomplete                combustion step with no emissions into the                atmosphere, eliminates landfills, waste organic and                synthetic materials, and animal waste with no                emissions such as carbon dioxide into the                atmosphere while driving ancillary systems such as                engine/ generators, produces hydrogen from the                wastes contaminating our environment to fuel                vehicles and business processes in our society                producing only water protecting our environment,                improves overall system efficiency via the use of                ancillary systems waste heat utilization, matching                the heat transfer rate and dwell timing of die                pyrolysis process to that of the particular                feedstock specific heat complex function and                keeping the product gas above any contained very                complex organic compounds vapor phase temperature                during transfer to an ancillary sub-system,                produces a stable and predictable gas product from                a variable or unknown feedstock, and utilizes the                full pyrolysis step product gas energy density                results in numerous energy and system cost                efficiencies and eliminates carbon dioxide emission                to the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                advanced gasification system comprising a high                temperature pyrolysis step unit for generating                above tar/oil vapor phase pyrolysis product gas and                carbon dioxide, where the high temperature                pyrolysis step unit comprises at least one                independent heating chamber, each independent                heating chamber comprises at least one burner, at                least one exhaust port, an incomplete combustion                retort connected to the high temperature pyrolysis                step unit, a conveyor for transporting materials to                be decomposed through the high temperature                pyrolysis step unit, and at least one sequestration                agent injector for injecting the materials to be                decomposed with a sequestration agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises injecting steam into or about the                  feedstock prior to or concurrent with the                  pyrolysis step for additional hydrogen atoms                  needed for organic gas production within the                  pyrolysis step, where the steam injecting step is                  performed responsive to and controlled based on                  feedback from the constituents and British                  thermal unit value of the pyrolysis step organic                  gas product.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN CN114671910-A
TI Synthesis of cidofovir involves reacting chiral            benzoylcytosine epoxy compound with ionic liquid            catalyst in presence of carbon dioxide, and reacting            obtained compound with trityl chloride in presence of            dimethylamino pyridine followed by reacting with            trimethylsilyl bromide and ammonium hydroxide.
AU JIA X
   MA X
   LI X
AE UNIV HENAN NORMAL (UYNR-C)
GA 202288505A
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of cidofovir (VII) involves                uniformly mixing chiral N4-benzoylcytosine epoxy                compound (I), water, and ionic liquid catalyst,                adding the mixture into a high-pressure reactor,                introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting the gas at                0-140&#176;C, separating product and ionic                liquid catalyst, subjecting product to column                chromatography to obtain chiral N4-benzoylcytosine                diol compound (II), reacting obtained diol compound                (II) with trityl chloride in presence of                dimethylamino pyridine to obtain hydroxy-protected                trityl compound (III), reacting compound (III) with                triethyl phosphite in presence of sodium hydride                and dimethylformamide at 0C to obtain phosphite                ester compound (IV), reacting compound (IV) with                hydrochloric acid in dichloromethane at 0C to                obtain hydroxy-deprotected compound (V), reacting                compound (V) with trimethylsilyl bromide in                dichloromethane at room temperature to obtain                phosphonic acid compound (VI), and reacting                compound (VI) with ammonium hydroxide.
   USE - Synthesis of cidofovir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides cidofovir with high yield                and high enantiomeric excess value, without using                catalyst such as metal, inorganic base, and large                amount of solvent, under mild reaction conditions,                by simple and environmentally-friendly                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of cidofovir of formula (VII)                involves uniformly mixing chiral N4-benzoylcytosine                epoxy compound of formula (I), water, and ionic                liquid catalyst, adding the mixture into a                high-pressure reactor, introducing carbon dioxide                gas, reacting the gas at 0-140&#176;C,                separating a product and an ionic liquid catalyst,                subjecting product to column chromatography to                obtain chiral N4-benzoylcytosine diol compound of                formula (II), and recycling separated ionic liquid                catalyst having structure of formula (i), reacting                obtained diol compound (II) with trityl chloride in                presence of dimethylamino pyridine to obtain                hydroxy-protected trityl compound of formula (III),                reacting compound (III) with triethyl phosphite in                presence of sodium hydride and dimethylformamide at                0&#176;C to obtain phosphite ester compound of                formula (IV), reacting compound (IV) with                hydrochloric acid in dichloromethane at                0&#176;C to obtain hydroxy-deprotected compound                of formula (V), reacting compound (V) with                trimethylsilyl bromide in dichloromethane at room                temperature to obtain phosphonic acid compound of                formula (VI), and reacting compound (VI) with                ammonium hydroxide. m,n=1-16; and X=F, Cl, Br, I,                or OH.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure of carbon                  dioxide gas in the reactor is 0.1-10 MPa,                  preferably 1-4 MPa. The temperature of the                  reaction is 60-100&#176;C.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  chiral N4-benzoylcytosine epoxy compound, water,                  and ionic liquid catalyst is 1:10:1-1:45:1.

PN CN114588934-A
TI Silicon-modified indium-based oxide-molecular            sieve composite material useful in catalytic carbon            dioxide hydrogenation for preparing butane, comprises            e.g. silicon-modified indium-based oxide comprising            silicon dioxide coated on surface of indium-based            oxide, and molecular sieve.
AU WANG J
   DONG M
   QIN Z
   WANG H
   FAN W
   WANG S
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202279851N
AB    NOVELTY - Silicon-modified indium-based oxide-molecular                sieve composite material comprises a                silicon-modified indium-based oxide and a molecular                sieve, where the silicon-modified indium-based                oxide comprises indium-based oxide and silicon                dioxide coated on the surface of the indium-based                oxide, and the indium-based oxide comprises                Indium(III) oxide or InaMebOc, where Me is                zirconium or chromium, where the molecular sieve is                an acidic H-BEA molecular sieve.
   USE - The composite material is useful in catalytic                carbon dioxide hydrogenation for preparing butane                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: uses silicon-modified                indium-based oxide and acidic H-BEA molecular sieve                as co-catalysts; improves the selectivity of butane                when used in the reaction of carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to butane; reduces the generation of                carbon monoxide by-products; and improves the                catalytic stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the composite material, comprising (i)                mixing water-soluble metal salt and water to obtain                metal ion solution, where the water-soluble metal                salt is a water-soluble indium salt or a                water-soluble indium salt and a water-soluble Me                salt, where the water-soluble Me salt is a                water-soluble zirconium salt or a water-soluble                chromium salt, (ii) using the metal ion solution as                a raw material to prepare an indium-based oxide                solid precursor, (iii) roasting the indium-based                oxide solid precursor to obtain an indium-based                oxide, (iv) performing a silicon modification                treatment on the indium-based oxide by a chemical                liquid deposition method to obtain a                silicon-modified indium-based oxide, and (v) mixing                the silicon-modified indium-based oxide and the                acidic H-BEA molecular sieve to obtain the finished                product, where the step (ii) comprises (iia)                dripping metal ion solution and the precipitant                solution to water for carrying out a precipitation                reaction to obtain an indium-based oxide solid                precursor, (iib) mixing the metal ion solution and                complexing agent, and performing a complexation                reaction to obtain an indium-based oxide solid                precursor, or (iic) mixing the metal ion solution                and the hydrothermal synthesis reagent, and                performing a hydrothermal reaction to obtain an                indium-based oxide solid precursor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  silicon-modified indium-based oxide and molecular                  sieve is 0.1-10:1. The mass of the silicon                  dioxide is 0.1-20 wt.% of the indium-based oxide.                  The ratio of a and b in the InaMebOc is 0.1-5.0:                  0.1-1. The silicon-alumina ratio of acidic H-BEA                  molecular sieve is 10-100:1. The molar ratio of                  the total metal ions in the metal ion solution                  and precipitant is 1:0.5-5. The molar ratio of                  the total metal ions in the metal ion solution                  and complexing agent is 1:0.5-5. The molar ratio                  of the total metal ions in the metal ion solution                  and hydrothermal synthesis reagent is                  1:0.5-5.Preferred Method: The step of precipitation                  reaction is carried out at 60-150&#176;C for                  3-20 hours. The step of complexation reaction is                  carried out at 60-150&#176;C for 3-20 hours.                  The step of hydrothermal reaction is carried out                  at 60-150&#176;C for 3-20 hours. The step of                  roasting treatment is carried out at                  350-700&#176;C for 2-10 hours.

PT P
PN CN114562876-A
TI Electric heating cement clinker sintering system            comprises electric heating circulating fluidized bed            decomposing furnace, raw material pre-heater, electric            heating kiln and clinker cooling device.
AU YANG S
   ZHANG S
   TAO J
   SUN Z
   REN T
   XIAO Y
   HU X
AE SINOMA ENERGY CONSERVATION CO LTD (CHNB-C)
GA 202277464B
AB    NOVELTY - Electric heating cement clinker sintering                system comprises electric heating circulating                fluidized bed decomposing furnace (1), raw material                preheater (3), electric heating kiln (5) and                clinker cooling device (6). The discharging port of                the raw material pretreatment furnace is connected                with the feeding port of an electric heating                circulation fluidization bed decomposition furnace.                The exhaust port of a side wall of the electric                heating circulation fluidization furnace is                connected to the air inlet at the bottom of the                material inlet. The discharge port at the top of                the electrification circulating fluidization                conveyor furnace is in communication with the feed                port of electric heating rotary kiln, and the                discharge port in the electric heat kiln is                communicated with the inlet of the clinker.
   USE - Electric heating cement clinker sintering                system for use in a building engineering                application. Uses include but are not limited to a                residential building, a commercial building, an                office building and a factory building.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves the concentration of                carbon dioxide gas in the cement clinker sintering                process, avoids the mixing of air, and reduces the                generation of nitrogen oxide gas. The electric                heating circulating fluidized bed decomposing                furnace and electric heating kiln are matched with                subsequent carbon dioxide capture recovery system,                so as to effectively reduce the carbon discharge.                The system saves the use of fossil fuel, and does                not generate nitrogen oxides gas, without setting                denitration system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for sintering cement clinker heating, which                involves:
   1. opening the electric heating circulating                fluidized bed decomposing furnace and the electric                heating kiln;
   2. putting the cement raw material from the                top of the raw material pre-heater, the external                carbon dioxide gas is connected to the bottom of                the electric heating circulating fluidized bed                decomposing furnace, the external cooling air is                connected to the bottom of the grog cooling                device;
   3. directly transmitting the clinker cooled in                the clinker cooling device to the clinker                storehouse.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic view                of electric heating cement clinker sintering                system. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   Electric-heating circulating fluidized bed                decomposing furnace (1)
   Electro heating device (2)
   Raw material preheater (3)
   Kiln tail waste heat boiler (4)
   Electro heating rotary kiln (5)
   Clinker cooling device (6)
   Kiln head waste heat boiler (7)

PN CN114478530-A
TI Extracting high-content theobromine from cocoa            beans comprises crushing cocoa bean raw material,            extracting raw material by carbon dioxide supercritical            extraction method, adding quicklime, decolorizing and            removing impurities, preliminarily concentrating,            collecting filter residue, and drying.
AU FAN Y
   WEI B
   DANG B
   ZHANG Y
   WEI Y
   JI Y
AE SHAANXI JIAHERB PHARM CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202269588E
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting high-content theobromine from cocoa                beans comprises (i) crushing cocoa bean raw                material, extracting raw material by carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction method to remove                fat-soluble impurities, and collecting extraction                residual, (ii) adding quicklime into the extraction                residue, uniformly stirring, adding ethanol                solution for multiple extractions, filtering,                merging and preliminarily concentrating the                filtrate, adding a composite adsorbent into the                preliminary concentrated filtrate, stirring,                decolorizing and removing impurities, and                collecting the mother liquor after filtration,                (iii) preliminarily concentrating the mother liquor                collected, adding chitosan, and stirring uniformly                at the set temperature, filtering, collecting the                filter residue, and drying to obtain theobromine                crude product, and (iv) adding ethanol solution and                aqueous ammonia into the theobromine crude product                and heating to dissolve, adding activated carbon to                decolorize, and filtering.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting                high-content theobromine from cocoa bean.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has recovery rate of as high as                more than 85%, and the purity can reach more than                98%, does not require the participation of strong                acids and alkalis, the amount of organic solvent is                small, the process is simple, safe and                environmentally friendly, and the requirements for                technology and equipment are low, and industrial                production is easy to be realized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting high-content theobromine from cocoa                beans comprises (i) crushing cocoa bean raw                material, extracting raw material by carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction method to remove                fat-soluble impurities, and collecting extraction                residual, (ii) adding quicklime into the extraction                residue, uniformly stirring, adding ethanol                solution for multiple extractions, filtering,                merging and preliminarily concentrating the                filtrate, adding a composite adsorbent into the                preliminary concentrated filtrate, stirring,                decolorizing and removing impurities, and                collecting the mother liquor after filtration,                (iii) preliminarily concentrating the mother liquor                collected, adding chitosan, and stirring uniformly                at the set temperature, allowing the solution to                stand for settling time at the set temperature,                filtering the solution, collecting the filter                residue, and drying to obtain theobromine crude                product, and (iv) adding ethanol solution and                aqueous ammonia into the theobromine crude product                and heating to dissolve, adding activated carbon to                decolorize, filtering, collecting the filtrate, and                concentrating, allowing to stand at a low                temperature for a set time and filtering, rinsing                the filter residue with hot ethanol and purified                water in turn, collecting the filter residue and                drying to obtain theobromine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  condition of carbon dioxide supercritical                  extraction method is: the extraction pressure is                  25-30 MPa, the extraction temperature is                  45-50degrees Celsius, the extraction time is 3-4                  hours, the pressure of the first-level separation                  kettle is 5-8 MPa, the temperature is                  40-45degrees Celsius, the pressure of the                  second-level separation kettle is 3-5 MPa, and                  the temperature is 35-40degrees Celsius. In the                  step (ii), the stirring and extracting is carried                  out at 65plus minus5degrees Celsius for 3 times,                  2 hours each time, and after combining and                  preliminarily concentrating the filtrate, the                  concentration of ethanol in the filtrate is 50-60                  vol.%; and after stirring to decolorize and                  remove impurities, filtering while hot. In the                  step (iii), during preliminary concentration, the                  mother liquor collected is concentrated to 1-3                  times the volume of the cocoa bean raw material                  at 65degrees Celsius, and the added amount of                  chitosan is 5-12%, preferably 9% of the volume of                  the mother liquor after preliminary                  concentration; the set temperature is 20degrees                  Celsius, and the set time is 48 hours. The step                  (iv) comprises heating to 70degrees Celsius to                  dissolve the crude theobromine; and the filtrate                  is concentrated and allowed to stand for 24 hours                  at 0-5degrees Celsius.Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the                  amount of quicklime is 10-20% of the quality of                  the cocoa bean raw material; the concentration of                  the ethanol solution is 80-90 vol.%, preferably                  85 vol.%, and the consumption of the ethanol                  solution is 8-12 times the mass of the cocoa bean                  raw material during each extraction; the                  composite adsorbent is a mixture of 70-80%,                  preferably 75% activated carbon and 20-30%,                  preferably 25% activated clay, the amount of the                  composite adsorbent is 10-20%, preferably 15% of                  the mass of the cocoa bean raw material. In the                  step (iv), the concentration of ethanol in the                  ethanol solution is 70 vol.%, and the quality is                  20-30 times the quality of the theobromine crude                  product, and the quality of the aqueous ammonia                  is 3-5% of the quality of the crude theobromine;                  the consumption of activated carbon is 3-8% of                  the quality of the crude theobromine.

   USE - The clinker is useful in a cement industry for                a system i.e. microwave calcining system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes energy saving and                consumption reduction, takes account of carbon                dioxide capture and purification, and reduces                greenhouse gas emission. The method uses the system                waste heat generate electricity and solar energy to                provide electric energy, converting the electric                energy into microwave energy to replace the fossil                energy used by the original kiln tail decomposing                furnace, realizing energy saving, and collecting                and purifying carbon dioxide, reducing the                greenhouse gas discharge effect. The microwave                calcining is composed of inner and outer heating,                the heating is uniform, the temperature gradient is                small, heating speed is fast, the heat efficiency                is high, the clinker quality is ensured. Using the                roller granulator to granulate and form the                material, changing the suspended calcining mode                into stacking state calcining, reduces the blocking                effect of the gas in the microwave heating energy                transmission process, ensuring the

PN CN113441009-A; CN113441009-B
TI Producing bipolar membrane acidification of            vitamin C comprises e.g. introducing vitamin C-sodium            salt aqueous solution into first-stage acid chamber,            promoting hydrolyzed hydroxide ion and sodium ion            migrated through cation membrane, and neutralizing            chamber conversion solution with carbon dioxide.
AU YING J
   WANG Y
   CHEN B
   ZHAO D
   ZHENG P
AE ZHEJIANG NHU CO LTD (CNHU-C)
   HEILONGJIANG XINHECHENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 2021B54978
AB    NOVELTY - Producing bipolar membrane acidification of                vitamin C comprises (1) introducing vitamin                C-sodium salt (VCNa) aqueous solution into                first-stage acid chamber of multi-stage                series-connected bipolar membrane electrodialysis                membrane stack, passing obtained first-stage acid                chamber conversion liquid into next-stage acid                chamber to continue conversion, and obtaining                product liquid rich in vitamin C, and (2)                introducing water or dilute sodium bicarbonate                solution into first alkali chamber corresponding to                first acid chamber, promoting hydrolyzed hydroxide                ions and sodium ions migrated through cation                membrane to converge into aqueous sodium hydroxide                solution in alkali chamber by bipolar membrane                under action of direct current electric field,                neutralizing obtained first-stage alkali chamber                conversion solution with carbon dioxide, and                obtaining aqueous sodium bicarbonate solution                containing small amount of VCNa after last-stage                conversion.
   USE - The method is useful for producing bipolar                membrane acidification of vitamin C.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces production costs and                environmental protection pressure; and at the same                time, significantly improves the deterioration loss                of vitamin C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing bipolar membrane acidification of                vitamin C comprises (1) introducing vitamin                C-sodium salt (VCNa) aqueous solution into                first-stage acid chamber of multi-stage                series-connected bipolar membrane electrodialysis                membrane stack, promoting hydrolysis of hydrogen                ions to combine with remaining vitamin C negative                ions to form vitamin C by bipolar membrane under                action of direct current electric field, passing                obtained first-stage acid chamber conversion liquid                into next-stage acid chamber to continue                conversion, proceeding to last stage of acid                chamber in turn, and obtaining product liquid rich                in vitamin C after last stage of transformation,                and (2) introducing water or dilute sodium                bicarbonate solution into first alkali chamber                corresponding to first acid chamber, promoting                hydrolyzed hydroxide ions and sodium ions migrated                through cation membrane to converge into aqueous                sodium hydroxide solution in alkali chamber by                bipolar membrane under action of direct current                electric field, neutralizing obtained first-stage                alkali chamber conversion solution with carbon                dioxide and passing into next-stage alkali chamber                to continue conversion, proceeding to last-stage                alkali chamber in turn, and obtaining aqueous                sodium bicarbonate solution containing small amount                of VCNa after last-stage conversion.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                bipolar membrane acidification production device of                vitamin C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The number of series                  of bipolar membrane electrodialysis membrane                  stacks is 2-6. The mass percentage concentration                  of the VCNa aqueous solution passed into the                  first-stage acid chamber is 10-35 wt.%. The                  concentration of the sodium bicarbonate solution                  passed into the first-stage alkali chamber is                  0.1-2 wt.%. The voltage used in the bipolar                  membrane electrodialysis membrane stack is 20-25                  V, and the current density is in the range of                  40-100 mA/cm2, preferably 40-80 mA/cm2.Preferred Method: The acid room and alkali                  room solutions of each stage are                  self-circulating, the alkali room conversion                  solution and carbon dioxide will enter the next                  stage after neutralization when the sodium                  hydroxide concentration in the alkali room                  reaches 2-3 wt.%, and the acid room conversion                  solution will directly enter the next stage. The                  conversion is stopped when the conductivity of                  the last-stage acid chamber solution drops to 5                  mS/cm, and ion exchange resin is used for deep                  acidification treatment. The production method                  further comprises transesterification process of                  methyl gulonic acid, undergoing                  transesterification of methyl gulonic acid with                  sodium bicarbonate to generate VCNa and carbon                  dioxide, entering the first-stage acid chamber of                  the step (1) for conversion after the generated                  VCNa is dissolved, and using the generated carbon                  dioxide in the neutralization reaction of the                  step (2). The sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution                  containing small amount of VCNa obtained in the                  step (2) is evaporated and crystallized, and                  applied to the transesterification process.

PT P
PN CN112838289-A; CN112838289-B
TI Recovery method of lithium and ferrous phosphate from lithium iron phosphate cathode material by dissolving lithium iron phosphate cathode material in acid solution, separating, adding ferrous sulfate to filtrate, and adding carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG Y
   SANG Z
   HAN P
   CHEN Z
   HU Y
   GE C
AE WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
   WUHAN DELIAN CHEM CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 202160547Y
AB    NOVELTY - A recovery method of lithium and ferrous phosphate from lithium iron phosphate cathode material includes dissolving lithium iron phosphate cathode material in acid solution, and subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain filter residue and filtrate having lithium element; adding ferrous sulfate to filtrate, continuously adding carbon dioxide, controlling temperature of filtrate, and adding alkaline magnesium compound to adjust pH to obtain ferrous phosphate hydrate and filtrate; adding alkaline magnesium compound to filtrate, subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain filter residue and filtrate, and using filter residue to replace or partially replace the alkaline magnesium compound; and adding magnesium precipitation agent to filtrate to fully precipitate magnesium ions, subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain filter residue and purified lithium liquid, and using purified lithium liquid to prepare lithium products.
   USE - The method is for recovery of lithium and ferrous phosphate from lithium iron phosphate cathode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material uses alkaline magnesium compound as precipitant which is easy to filter. The whole process consumes less water and the product obtained has high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A recovery method of lithium and ferrous phosphate from lithium iron phosphate cathode material comprises:
   (A) dissolving lithium iron phosphate cathode material in acid solution at 40-100 degrees C to remove metal copper and aluminum impurities, and subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain filter residue and filtrate having lithium element concentration of 0.1-3 mol/L;
   (B) adding ferrous sulfate to filtrate, continuously adding carbon dioxide, controlling temperature of filtrate to 40-100 degrees C, and adding alkaline magnesium compound to adjust pH to 3-5 to obtain ferrous phosphate hydrate and filtrate;
   (C) adding alkaline magnesium compound to filtrate until pH is greater than 6, subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain filter residue and filtrate, and using filter residue to replace or partially replace the alkaline magnesium compound when the dry basis content of aluminum in filter residue is less than 2%; and
   (D) adding magnesium precipitation agent to filtrate to fully precipitate magnesium ions, subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain filter residue and purified lithium liquid, using filter residue to completely replace or partially replace the alkaline magnesium compound in step (B) or replacing all or portion of alkaline magnesium compound in step (C), and using purified lithium liquid to prepare lithium products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium product is lithium carbonate, lithium hydroxide, lithium sulfate and/or lithium chloride. The molar ratio of acid solution and lithium iron phosphate is 1-1.5:1. The molar ratio of ferrous sulfate and lithium iron phosphate is 0.4-0.7:1. The alkaline magnesium compound is magnesium hydroxide, alkaline magnesium carbonate, magnesium carbonate and/or magnesium bicarbonate. The magnesium precipitation agent is ammonia, organic amine, lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate and/or ammonium carbonate.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN CN112145816-A; CN112145816-B
TI PVC pipe heating anti-icing and cracking device            has diaphragm that is arranged between distilled water,            where edible gelatin is set to bottom end of distilled            water, and whose outer end wall is in contact with            outer wall of water pipe.
AU ZHANG S
AE ZHANG S (ZHAN-Individual)
   CHONGQING WEISHIDUN IND CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 202103257K
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a liquid storage bag that is                fixedly connected between an inner walls of a card                slot. An aqueous solution is injected into the                liquid storage bag. A storage bag is provided on a                lower side of the aqueous solution. An interior of                the storage bag is filled with distilled water and                sodium hydroxide particles. A diaphragm is arranged                between the distilled water and the sodium                hydroxide particle. The distilled water is located                on the upper side of the sodium hydroxide                particles. An edible gelatin is fixedly connected                to the bottom end of the distilled water. An outer                end wall of the edible gelatin is in contact with                the outer wall of a PVC water pipe (1). A storage                cavity is filled with carbon dioxide gas. A                material of an insulation layer is polyurethane                foam. The material of a connecting pipe (2) is                formed with aluminum alloy.
   USE - PVC pipe heating anti-icing and cracking                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The water-swelling rubber strips quickly swell                after contacting water to reduce the loss of                distilled water and sodium hydroxide particles. The                top teeth at the bottom end of the top plate                puncture the storage bag and the diaphragm allowing                the distilled water and sodium hydroxide particles                to mix quickly and evenly. The heating efficiency                is improved and the probability of freezing inside                the PVC water pipe is reduced. The mixture of                distilled water and sodium hydroxide particles is                distributed on the inner wall of the connecting                pipe when the temperature inside the PVC water pipe                increases. The high temperature heats the carbon                dioxide gas in the latex anti-leak ring which                causes the latex anti-leak ring to expand and                isolates the external air by carbon dioxide. The                temperature difference is reduced and the internal                heating effect is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of using PVC pipe heating anti-icing and cracking                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                PVC pipe heating anti-icing and cracking device.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   PVC water pipe (1)
   Connecting pipe (2)
   Fixing ring (3)
   Main water inlet hole (4)
