PT P
PN CN111422891-A
TI Utilization method of magnesite involves heating and decomposing solid ammonium sulfate to produce ammonium hydrogen sulfate, then dissolving in water, and adding magnesite fine ore to obtained ammonium bisulfate solution, and reacting.
AU WU Y
   LI L
   LIU N
   LIU J
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 202069429J
AB    NOVELTY - Utilization method of magnesite involves: (1) grinding magnesite to a 100-mesh sieve to make magnesite fine ore; (2) heating and decomposing solid ammonium sulfate to produce ammonium hydrogen sulfate and ammonia gas, and dissolving ammonium hydrogen sulfate in water to prepare an ammonium hydrogen sulfate solution; (3) adding the magnesite fine ore obtained in step (1) to the ammonium bisulfate solution obtained in step (2), and uniformly mixing to prepare an ore slurry; (4) heating the ore slurry under stirring conditions to produce carbon dioxide gas, adding oxalic acid to the solution, stirring until the reaction solution is uniform, and performing primary solid-liquid separation to obtain crude liquid and leaching residue; and (5) passing air and portion of the ammonia produced in step (2) into the obtained crude liquid, and then performing secondary solid-liquid separation to obtain pure magnesium ion-containing solution and aluminum-iron-containing solid residue.
   USE - Utilization method of magnesite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Utilization method of magnesite involves: (1) grinding magnesite to a 100-mesh sieve to make magnesite fine ore; (2) heating and decomposing solid ammonium sulfate to produce ammonium hydrogen sulfate and ammonia gas, and dissolving ammonium hydrogen sulfate in water to prepare an ammonium hydrogen sulfate solution; (3) adding the magnesite fine ore obtained in step (1) to the ammonium bisulfate solution obtained in step (2), and uniformly mixing to prepare an ore slurry; (4) heating the ore slurry under stirring conditions to produce carbon dioxide gas, after the reaction is completed, adding oxalic acid to the solution, stirring until the reaction solution is uniform, and performing primary solid-liquid separation to obtain crude liquid and leaching residue; (5) sequentially passing air and portion of the ammonia produced in step (2) into the obtained crude liquid, and then performing secondary solid-liquid separation to obtain pure magnesium ion-containing solution and aluminum-iron-containing solid residue; and (6) simultaneously introducing the magnesium ion-containing solution obtained in step (5) into the ammonia gas produced in step (2) and the carbon dioxide gas produced in step (4) under stirring conditions, reacting and carrying out tertiary solid-liquid separation to obtain basic magnesium carbonate and ammonium sulfate solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves dissolving aluminum-iron-containing solid residue obtained in step (5) in a sufficient amount of 15-40% sodium hydroxide solution at 80-140 degrees C for 10-60 minutes, then separating to obtain iron hydroxide solution and sodium aluminate solution, and calcining at 600-100 degrees C to obtain ferric oxide having a purity of 94-97%. The ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas in step (6) are introduced simultaneously, and the pH of the solution after the reaction is 7.5-9. After the step (6) is completed, the ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas are continuously stirred for 60-360 minutes. The method involves washing the leaching residue obtained in step (4) 3-5 times with hot distilled water at greater than or equal to 80 degrees C, then drying, evenly mixing 20-30 %mass dried leaching residue, 10-30 %mass sawdust, 10-20 %mass magnesium oxide, 15-30 %mass magnesium chloride, 5-10 %mass water, 1-2 %mass liquid silicone, 0.5-2 %mass polyvinyl acetate and 1-3 %mass plasticizer, and curing at room temperature for 3 days to obtain magnesite products. The performance indicators of magnesite products are a density of 1300-1500 kg/m2, a 7-day compressive strength of 5-9 MPa, a softening coefficient of 0.53-0.59 and a thermal conductivity of 0.2-0.27. Preferred Conditions: The slurry stirring speed in step (4) is 80-200 rpm, the reaction time is 30-240 minutes, and the reaction temperature is 60-150 degrees C. Preferred Components: The liquid-solid ratio of ammonium bisulfate solution and the fine magnesite ore in the slurry in step (3) is 2.5-6. The molar ratio of ammonium bisulfate to magnesium in magnesite is 2-4.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PN CN111165272-A
TI Cultivating white needle mushroom in bottles by            filling cultivation material into bottle, sterilizing,            inoculating strain, controlling temperature and air            humidity, increasing carbon dioxide concentration,            harvesting and packing mushroom.
AU WANG Y
AE WANG Y (WANG-Individual)
GA 2020450355
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating white needle mushroom                in bottles involves uniformly mixing cultivation                mixed dry material with water to obtain cultivation                material, where the cultivation mixed dry material                comprises corn cob, rice bran, wood chips, corn                flour and shell powder, filling the cultivation                material into cultivation bottle, sterilizing in a                sterilization pot, inoculating the white needle                mushroom strains in cultivation bottle in sterile                room, transferring the cultivation bottles into the                cultivation room, controlling the temperature while                performing ventilation every day, then cultivating                until the mycelium matures, using high-pressure air                flow to blow off the old strains, then performing                bud treatment, controlling the temperature and air                humidity during the reproductive stage, terminating                illumination, gradually increasing the carbon                dioxide concentration, harvesting and packing the                mushroom.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating white                needle mushroom in bottles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and cultivates white                needle mushroom which has good and stable quality                and high yield, hence reduces production cost of                white needle mushroom, and is suitable for                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cultivating white needle mushroom                in bottles involves (a) uniformly mixing                cultivation mixed dry material with water to obtain                cultivation material with a water content of 65                wt.%, where the cultivation mixed dry material                comprises 45-50 pts. wt. corn cob, 15-18 pts. wt.                rice bran, 15-18 pts. wt. wood chips, 3 pts. wt.                corn flour and 1.5 pts. wt. shell powder, (b)                filling the cultivation material into cultivation                bottle to the shoulder of the bottle and                sterilizing in a sterilization pot, (c) inoculating                the white needle mushroom strains in cultivation                bottle in sterile room, where the quality of the                strains and the cultivation material is 1:30-1:35,                (d) transferring the cultivation bottles into the                cultivation room and controlling the temperature at                15-18 degrees C and air humidity at 80-85% for 2                days while performing ventilation every day and                then cultivating at 20-22 degrees C until the                mycelium matures, (e) using high-pressure air flow                to blow off the old strains and then performing bud                treatment and (f) controlling the temperature at                10-12 degrees C and air humidity at 78% during the                reproductive stage, terminating illumination,                gradually increasing the carbon dioxide                concentration to 5500-5800 ppm, harvesting and                packing the mushroom.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves                  sterilizing under normal pressure at 105 degrees                  C for 60 minutes and then sterilizing under high                  temperature and high pressure at greater than or                  equal to 0.15 MPa and 122 degrees C for 60                  minutes and to ensure the quality of mushrooms                  and naturally cooling the sterilization                  room.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN110923483-A
TI Preparing high-strength wear-resistant aluminum alloy comprises mixing waste residue with hydrochloric acid, filtering, vacuuming, adding carbon dioxide, sieving, refining, removing residue, casting into mold preheated, and demolding.
AU ZHANG W
AE QUANZHOU PAITENG NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLOG (QUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020258790
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-strength wear-resistant aluminum alloy comprises (i) mixing waste residue with hydrochloric acid, allowing to stand, filtering, and collecting the filtrate, and taking aluminum powder, mixed mineral powder, filtrate, dispersant, additive, refining agent, and flux; (ii) placing aluminum powder, mixed mineral powder, filtrate and dispersant into a melting furnace, vacuuming, adding carbon dioxide into inside, pressurizing, and heating; (iii) adding additives, refining agent and flux, heating to 860-950 degrees C, maintaining the temperature, pouring into the mold and allowing to stand, cooling, demolding, placing the demolding material into the extruder for squeezing, crushing, sieving, and collecting sieving particles; and (iv) adding the sieved particles, maintaining the temperature, and placing into a melting furnace, melting, transferring to intermediate frequency induction heating furnace, refining, removing the residue, casting into the mold preheated, and demolding.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-strength wear-resistant aluminum alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the brittleness of the alloy, and improves the strength of the aluminum alloy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-strength wear-resistant aluminum alloy comprises (i) mixing waste residue with hydrochloric acid according to the mass ratio of 3: 6-8, allowing to stand, filtering, and collecting the filtrate, and taking 200-220 pts. wt. aluminum powder, 30-33 pts. wt. mixed mineral powder, 22-26 pts. wt. filtrate, 10-13 pts. wt. dispersant, 6-12 pts. wt. additive, 3-5 pts. wt. refining agent, and 1-3 pts. wt. flux; (ii) placing aluminum powder, mixed mineral powder, filtrate and dispersant into a melting furnace, vacuuming to 0.05-0.08 MPa, adding carbon dioxide into inside, pressurizing to 1.3-1.5 MPa, heating to 530-550 degrees C, and heating to 720-760 degrees C; (iii) adding additives, refining agent and flux, heating to 860-950 degrees C, maintaining the temperature, pouring into the mold and allowing to stand, cooling, demolding, placing the demolding material into the extruder for squeezing, crushing, sieving, and collecting sieving particles; and (iv) adding the sieved particles to 190-210 degrees C, maintaining the temperature, and placing into a melting furnace, melting at 890-930 degrees C for 1 hours, transferring to intermediate frequency induction heating furnace, refining at 1200-1250 degrees C, removing the residue, casting into the mold preheated to 350 degrees C, and demolding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the waste slag is a mixture of blast furnace slag, ferrochromium slag and surfactant at a mass ratio of 5-8: 2-3:0.1, and placing into a ball mill for ball milling, and collecting the ball mill. The mixed ore powder is obtained by crushing feldspar, talc and manganese ore in a mass ratio of 5:2-4:3 to obtain mixed ore powder. The additive is prepared by mixing bone powder and boron oxide in a mass ratio of 6:10-13. Preferred Components: The surfactant is a mixture of perfluorooctyl quaternary amine iodide and dodecylfluoroheptyl methacrylate in a mass ratio of 6:1. The dispersant is sodium stearate or magnesium stearate. The refining agent is sodium chloride or magnesium chloride. The flux is lithium carbonate or lithium chloride.
DC M26 (Non-ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).)
MC M26-A01; M26-A01A; M26-A02; M26-A02A; M26-B09

PN CN110760548-A
TI System useful for producing ethanol from sugarcane, comprises pressing unit, filtering unit, fermentation unit, and separation unit.
AU WANG S
AE WANG S (WANG-Individual)
GA 202013752M
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises pressing unit, filtering unit, fermentation unit, and separation unit.
   USE - The system is useful for producing ethanol from sugarcane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures residual sugar content lowest in water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for producing ethanol from sugarcane, which involves using dumper to unload the sugarcane to the loader after the sugarcane is weighed and sampled, adding sugarcane into pelletizer after washing, entering the crusher after two-stage pelleting, after 5 stages of compression, adding sugarcane juice and bagasse, where the content of bagasse is 40-50% to power plant, pumping sugarcane juice into filter by rotating oblique axis orbit filter during rotation of the filter, passing sugarcane juice through filter, adding bagasse of the filter through threaded track in the filter, spraying blocked channels with high-pressure water to dredge, filtering bagasse from filter by belt filter, pumping filtered sugarcane juice into fermenter for fermentation, where the fermentation practice is 24-36 hours, measuring ethanol content in the mature fermentation broth to control the fermentation end point, adding dry yeast into yeast preparation tank, using fresh fermentation broth as nutrition, and ammonium sulfate as nitrogen source after 12 hours of yeast cell growth, adding mature yeast liquid, pumping the matured liquid after fermentation to a centrifuge, separating fermented mature ethanol solution and the unfermented sugar solution by centrifugation, adding sugar solution once by adding sugar invertase, completely converting fermented sugars into easily fermentable sugars, returning to the fermenter for repeated fermentation, pumping ethanol solution to the separation unit, where the separation unit comprises three towers, and the material enters the de-lighting tower, the operating pressure control range of the de-lighting tower is 180-260 kPag, and the operating temperature control range is 50-140 degrees C, the azeotrope of carbon dioxide and ethanol/water dissolved in material is discharged at the top of the tower, the bottom material is water, which is recycled into water collection tank, extracting large amount of ethanol aqueous solution from side line of the tower, separating carbon dioxide from ethanol aqueous solution, discharging carbon dioxide, after ethanol aqueous solution is mixed with the sideline mined materials, where tower operating pressure control range is 600-1200 kPag, and operating temperature control range is 120-180 degrees C, and producing qualified ethanol product at top of the rectification tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The sugarcane ethanol production technology industrially produce qualified ethanol products. The sugarcane ethanol production system combines characteristics of the sugar industry and the chemical industry. The original sugarcane juice is directly filtered, fermented, and separated to obtain ethanol products. The process is simple, and the investment and operating costs are low. After the sugarcane is fed in the squeezing unit, from washing to squeezing out residue, all are completed using closed device to avoid sugar loss. The sugarcane juice in the pressing unit is continuously compressed and counter-currently washed to reduce the amount of washing water, thus reducing the amount of fermentation broth. The method involves adopting rotating oblique axis orbit filter, which simply filter the solid matter in the sugarcane juice and send it to the filter to transfer. The sugar liquid in the mature fermentation broth and the ethanol aqueous solution are separated by a centrifuge, and the sugar liquid is returned to fermentation after treated with an enzyme. The tank effectively improve ethanol fermentation conversion rate. The steam at the top of the crude mash tower is exchanged with the reboiler of the rectification tower, and the light tower and rectification tower top of the tower is cooled by air coolers, which saves energy and reduces consumption to the greatest extent.
DC D17 (Sugar and starch industry (C07H, C13).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)

PN CN110040732-A; CN110040732-B
TI Preparing alkali metal-modified porous carbon            material useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide in flue            gas comprises e.g. performing Friedel-Craft alkylation            reaction to synthesize super-crosslinked microporous            polymer, and performing oxidation.
AU SUN N
   LUO X
   ZHAO H
   YU W
   WEI W
   SUN Y
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 201966042M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alkali metal-modified porous carbon                material comprises e.g. (i) performing                Friedel-Craft alkylation reaction to synthesize a                super-crosslinked microporous polymer, comprising                reacting a monomer and a crosslinking agent in a                solvent under the action of a catalyst to obtain a                super-crosslinked microporous polymer, where the                monomer is benzene, pyrrole, toluene and/or                chlorobenzene, the crosslinking agent is dimethanol                formal, diethylene glycol dimethyl ether and/or                cyanuric chloride, and the solvent is                dichloromethane, 1,2-dichloroethane and/or ethylene                oxide, (ii) performing oxidation of the                super-crosslinked microporous polymer, comprising                oxidizing the super-crosslinked microporous polymer                by using a strong acid mixed solution to obtain a                super-crosslinked microporous polymer incorporating                an oxygen-containing functional group, and (iii)                introducing alkali metal ion by mixing an alkali                metal ion-containing alkali solution with the                super-crosslinked microporous polymer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing alkali                metal-modified porous carbon material for adsorbing                carbon dioxide in flue gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; utilizes economical raw                materials which are easy to obtain; and produces                product that has excellent carbon dioxide                adsorption performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing alkali metal-modified porous carbon                material comprises (i) performing Friedel-Craft                alkylation reaction to synthesize a                super-crosslinked microporous polymer, comprising                reacting a monomer and a crosslinking agent in a                solvent under the action of a catalyst to obtain a                super-crosslinked microporous polymer, where the                monomer is benzene, pyrrole, toluene and/or                chlorobenzene, the crosslinking agent is dimethanol                formal, diethylene glycol dimethyl ether and/or                cyanuric chloride, and the solvent is                dichloromethane, 1,2-dichloroethane and/or ethylene                oxide, (ii) performing oxidation of the                super-crosslinked microporous polymer, comprising                oxidizing the super-crosslinked microporous polymer                by using a strong acid mixed solution to obtain a                super-crosslinked microporous polymer incorporating                an oxygen-containing functional group, (iii)                introducing an alkali metal ion by ion exchange by                mixing an alkali metal ion-containing alkali                solution with the oxygen-containing functional                group-containing super-crosslinked microporous                polymer to obtain an alkali metal ion-doped                super-crosslinked microporous polymer, and (iv)                performing in-situ activation of alkali metal                ion-doped supercrosslinked microporous polymer at                500-1000&#176; C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  catalyst is anhydrous ferric chloride. In the                  step (i), the molar ratio of the monomer and                  crosslinker is 0.5-6:1. In the step (i), the                  relative unit mole of monomer and solvent used is                  in an amount of 1000-5000 ml. In the step (ii),                  the mass-to-volume ratio of the super-crosslinked                  microporous polymer and the strong acid mixed                  solution is 1 g:10-100 ml. In the step (ii), the                  mixed solution of the strong acid is a mixed                  solution of concentrated nitric acid and                  concentrated sulfuric acid, where the volume                  ratio of concentrated nitric acid and                  concentrated sulfuric acid is 0.5-6:1. In the                  step (iii), the concentration of the alkali                  solution is 0.5-6 mol/l.In the step (iii), the                  mass-to-volume ratio of the oxygen-containing                  functional group-containing supercrosslinked                  microporous polymer and the alkali solution is 1                  g: 50-500 ml. Preferred Method: In the step (i),                  the reaction is carried out under the protection                  of an inert gas at a temperature &#8815;                  100&#176; C for &#8814; 2 hours. In the                  step (i), the reaction product is subjected to                  post-treatment to obtain the super-crosslinked                  microporous polymer, and the post-treatment                  comprises washing and drying the solid product                  produced by the reaction. In the step (ii), the                  reaction is carried out at a temperature                  &#8815; 100&#176; C. In the step (ii),                  the oxygen-containing functional group-containing                  super-crosslinked microporous polymer is obtained                  after post-treatment by the end of the oxidation                  reaction, and the post-treatment comprises                  filtration, washing and drying. In the step (ii),                  the strong acid mixed droplets are added to the                  super-crosslinked microporous polymer, and                  oxidized. In the step (iv), the activation is                  carried out for &#8814; 1 hour.
DC A91 (Ion-exchange resins, polyelectrolytes.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN109775682-A; CN109775682-B
TI Preparing lithium cobalt phosphate involves adding phosphoric acid to dissolve cobalt powder, adjusting pH of solution, and then precisely filtering to obtain cobalt solution, slurrying lithium carbonate, then inletting carbon dioxide.
AU BAO J
AE BAO J (BAOJ-Individual)
GA 201948289K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium cobalt phosphate involves adding phosphoric acid to dissolve cobalt powder, adjusting pH of solution to 3.5-4.5, and then precisely filtering to obtain cobalt solution, slurrying the lithium carbonate, then inletting carbon dioxide into lithium carbonate slurry to obtain lithium solution, preparing polyethylene glycol solution, using polyethylene glycol solution as base solution. The cobalt solution and the lithium solution are added to the bottom liquid under stirring, and reacted for 30-60 minutes. The material after the reaction is spray-dried to obtain spray-dried material. The spray material is dried into roller furnace, nitrogen is inlet. The oxygen content in the furnace is lower than 100 parts per million. The material is passed through the heating section, insulating section and cooling section. The heating speed of the heating section is 80-100 degrees C per hour.
   USE - Method for preparing lithium cobalt phosphate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare lithium cobalt phosphate that has good capacity and high compacted density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lithium cobalt phosphate involves adding phosphoric acid to dissolve cobalt powder, adjusting pH of solution to 3.5-4.5, and then precisely filtering to obtain cobalt solution, slurrying the lithium carbonate, then inletting carbon dioxide into lithium carbonate slurry to obtain lithium solution, preparing polyethylene glycol solution, using polyethylene glycol solution as base solution. The cobalt solution and the lithium solution are added to the bottom liquid under stirring, and reacted for 30-60 minutes. The material after the reaction is spray-dried to obtain spray-dried material. The spray material is dried into roller furnace, nitrogen is inlet. The oxygen content in the furnace is lower than 100 parts per million. The material is passed through the heating section, insulating section and cooling section. The heating speed of the heating section is 80-100 degrees C per hour. The heating temperature is 720-740 degrees C for 5-7 hours, then cooled to temperature lower than 80 degrees C, and discharged to obtain calcined material. The calcined material is passed through the pipeline conveying, conveyed to an airflow crusher, after air flow crushing, iron is removed by electromagnetic screening, sieved with 200-250-mesh sieve, then vacuum packaged to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The ratio of cobalt powder, phosphoric acid and lithium carbonate is 1:1.01-1.02:0.501-0.505. The concentration of the phosphoric acid is 5-6 mole/L. The solid content of the lithium carbonate slurry is 40-50 %. The concentration of glycol solution is 0.3-0.5 mole/L. The polymerization degree of polyethylene glycol is 2000, 4000 or 6000. The volume of lithium cobalt solution and the total volume of the solution is 1/10-1/5. The feeding time is 60-120 minutes. The temperature of the feeding process and the reaction process is 50-65 degrees C. The drying inlet temperature is 200-250 degrees C. The discharging temperature is less than 100 degrees C. The spray drying particle material is 3-6 micrometer and water content is less than 0.5%. The volume of nitrogen is 300-600 times of volume of the spray dried material. The pressure roller in the furnace is 50-80 pascal. The dew point of the gas in the furnace is (-)50 degrees C or less. The magnetic induction intensity is 10000-15000 gauss. The concentration of the dilute sulfuric acid is 0.1-0.3 mole/L. The ratio of removing iron residue and dilute sulfuric acid is 1:3-4. Preferred Components: The electromagnetic iron remover is dry dust remover.

PN CN208207630-U; CN109308083-A
TI Automatic planting system for intelligent green building, has remote monitoring station which is connected to proximal control main bearing unit and carbon dioxide gas source that accelerates photosynthesis in automatic planting module.
AU WU Z

AB    (CN208207630-U)   NOVELTY - An automatic planting system for intelligent green building, comprising several automatic planting modules which are set up in each household of green building community and internally planted with several RFID-labeled plants, several planting environment parameter sensing, plant identification and type RFID identification and several plant environment control functions, at least a remote monitoring station, a landscape ecological pool, a culturing water monitoring station and a carbon dioxide supply, the automatic planting module can automatically monitor and control the environmental factors such as soil, temperature, humidity and sunlight corresponding to the plant types, each automatic planting module is connected with the remote monitoring station, which are remotely monitored and controlled by the remote monitoring station, and the remote monitoring station can assign professionals to the site to assist in the planting according to the planting state in each automatic planting module or the assist information sent by the user, the landscape ecological pool provides cultivation water source with controllable quality and cyclic utilization to each automatic planting module by culturing water monitoring station.

A planting module monitoring station for filling cultivation water in a cultivating water monitoring station to provide cultivating water source. The planting module is connected with a plant drip irrigation or irrigation water source. The planting module is connected with a carbon dioxide gas source.

PN CN108842162-A
TI Preparation of stannic oxide nanosheet gas diffusion electrode for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide, involves reacting electrode substrate with ethanol, dilute hydrochloric acid, urea, tin(II) chloride and thioglycolic acid.
AU YE D
   CHEN G
   CHEN R
   LI J
   ZHANG B
   ZHU X
   LIAO Q
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 201894313G
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of stannic oxide nanosheet gas diffusion electrode involves (A) placing electrode substrate (I) in acetone, ultrasonically cleaning with ethanol and dilute hydrochloric acid and removing impurities of electrode substrate (I), (B) adding urea, thioglycolic acid and concentrated hydrochloric acid to deionized water and mixing to obtain clear solution, adding tin(II) chloride to mixture and stirring, (C) putting prepared catalyst solution into reaction kettle, and placing the cleaned electrode substrate (I), reacting, cooling, removing, washing and drying, (D) taking electrode substrate (II) and using PTFE for hydrophobic treatment and drying, placing the treated electrode substrate in tube furnace, sintering, (E) spraying toner on electrode substrate, (F) sintering treated electrode substrate to obtain gas diffusion electrode, (G) bonding stannic oxide nanosheet catalytic layer electrode to carbon powder-coated side of gas diffusion electrode and hot pressing.
   USE - Preparation of stannic oxide nanosheet gas diffusion electrode for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of stannic oxide nanosheet gas diffusion electrode ensuring enhanced transmission of electrons and improved conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of stannic oxide nanosheet gas diffusion electrode for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide, involves (A) placing electrode substrate (I) in acetone, ultrasonically cleaning with ethanol, removing grease from the surface, putting into dilute hydrochloric acid solution for ultrasonic cleaning and removing impurities of the electrode substrate (I), (B) adding urea, thioglycolic acid and concentrated hydrochloric acid to deionized water and mixing to obtain clear solution, adding tin(II) chloride to the mixture and stirring to mix the solution thoroughly, (C) putting prepared catalyst solution into reaction kettle, and placing the cleaned electrode substrate (I) in reaction kettle, controlling reaction at 120 plus minus 20 degrees C, after the reaction is completed, cooling, removing, washing with deionized water and drying, (D) taking electrode substrate (II) and using PTFE for hydrophobic treatment, and drying for use, placing the treated electrode substrate in tube furnace, sintering at 340 plus minus 20 degrees C, (E) spraying toner on the side of the electrode substrate, (F) placing the treated electrode substrate in tube furnace and sintering at 340 plus minus 20 degrees C to obtain gas diffusion electrode, (G) bonding stannic oxide nanosheet catalytic layer electrode to the carbon powder-coated side of gas diffusion electrode and hot pressing at high temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrode substrate is carbon paper, carbon cloth or carbon felt. The gas diffusion electrode has hydrophobic layer, and one side of the gas diffusion electrode is sprayed with mixed liquid slurry of carbon powder and PTFE. The gas diffusion electrode is coated with a mixture of carbon powder and PTFE, and the stannic oxide nanosheet catalytic electrode is bonded to the side.

PN CN108821526-A
TI Reinforcing river channel bottom mud by microbial grouting comprises e.g. intercepting river channels of construction section and draining river water in construction section and draining drainage board into river silt.
AU YU X
   YU C
   QIAN C
   CAI X
   GAO Y
AE UNIV WENZHOU (UNWZ-C)
GA 201891921G
AB    NOVELTY - Reinforcing river channel bottom mud by microbial grouting comprises e.g. intercepting river channels of construction section and draining river water in construction section, draining drainage board into river silt, inserting pumping head into bottom of the river channel, connecting drainage board and pumping head to main pipeline, where main pipe appearance is arranged with pumping device and conveying device, and transporting calcium source to river silt through main pipe and drainage plate through conveying device, adding mixed solution of Bacillus subtilis and silicate bacteria and carbon dioxide, adding surface of river sludge into mixed solution of Bacillus subtilis and silicate bacteria, flocculating after river silt and forming layer of sediment and upper clear water, using pumping device to simultaneously link pump head and drain plate to extract water in upper clear water and sludge and connecting drainage board to conveying device and calcium source.
   USE - The method is useful for reinforcing river channel bottom mud by microbial grouting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can process river silt in large area, surface of sludge after biological flocculation and water in the soil are pumped away, utilizes original extraction channel to transport mineralized bacteria to the soil so the soil is fully consolidated and hardened from inside to the surface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reinforcing river channel bottom mud by microbial grouting comprises (a) intercepting river channels of construction section and draining river water in construction section; (b) draining the drainage board into river silt, inserting pumping head into bottom of the river channel, connecting drainage board and pumping head to main pipeline, where main pipe appearance is arranged with pumping device and conveying device, and transporting calcium source to river silt through main pipe and drainage plate through conveying device, adding mixed solution of Bacillus subtilis and silicate bacteria and carbon dioxide, adding surface of river sludge into mixed solution of Bacillus subtilis and silicate bacteria; (c) fully flocculating after river silt and forming layer of sediment and upper clear water, using pumping device to simultaneously link pump head and drain plate to extract water in the upper clear water and sludge; and (d) connecting drainage board to conveying device and calcium source, transporting silicate bacteria solution and carbon dioxide to soil through drainage board and allowing to coagulate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method after step (d) further comprises removing drainage board and main pipeline, spraying silicate bacteria mixed solution on the surface of river channel by spraying device every other day in rainless weather and spraying more than three times continuously. The method after step (d) comprises spraying silicate bacterial mixed solution on the surface of river by spraying device every other day in a rainless weather and spraying four times continuously.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN108675929-A
TI Production of ethyl acetate involves mixing acetic acid and ethanol, introducing into pretreatment tube added with phosphorus molybdenum vanadium heteropoly acid, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into pretreatment tube.
AU WU W
   ZHANG S
   XUE D
AE GUANGXI JINMAO BIOLOGICAL CHEM CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018842854
AB    NOVELTY - The production of ethyl acetate involves mixing acetic acid and ethanol in molar ratio of (1-1.5):1, introducing into pretreatment tube added with phosphorus molybdenum vanadium heteropoly acid, introducing carbon dioxide gas into pretreatment tube to obtain pretreatment substance, introducing pretreatment substance into esterification reaction column, introducing ethanol from lower stage of esterification reaction column, adding concentrated sulfuric acid in the esterification reaction tower, reacting for producing ethyl acetate and water, distilling off azeotropic formed by ethanol from esterification reaction column, returning remaining acetic acid of reaction from the bottom of the esterification tower, condensing azeotrope distilled from the top of the column, dehydrating to obtain crude ester, refluxing portion of crude ester to esterification reaction column with water, and refining of certain amount of crude ester into refining column.
   USE - Production of ethyl acetate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of ethyl acetate with high yield, effectively shortens the residence time of the raw materials in esterification reaction tower, and improves the conversion rate of acetic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The production of ethyl acetate involves mixing acetic acid and ethanol in molar ratio of (1-1.5):1, introducing into pretreatment tube added with phosphorus molybdenum vanadium heteropoly acid, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the pretreatment tube at pressure of 5-7 MPa for 30 minutes when the pretreatment tube is evacuated to -1 to -2 MPa to obtain a pretreatment substance, controlling reaction temperature at 60-65 degrees C for 25 minutes, and controlling reaction temperature to 100-110 degrees C for 5 minutes, introducing pretreatment substance from the upper middle stage of esterification reaction column into esterification reaction column, introducing ethanol from the lower stage of esterification reaction column so that the molar ratio of acetic acid to ethanol reaches 1:(2-2.5.), adding concentrated sulfuric acid at 95-100 degrees C for 40-50 minutes under atmospheric pressure in the esterification reaction tower, reacting for producing ethyl acetate and water, distilling off azeotropic formed by ethanol from the top of esterification reaction column, returning remaining acetic acid of reaction from the bottom of the esterification tower to continue the reaction, condensing azeotrope distilled from the top of the column, dehydrating to obtain crude ester, refluxing portion of crude ester to esterification reaction column with water, and refining of certain amount of crude ester into a refining column.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The addition amount of phosphorus molybdenum vanadium heteropoly acid is 2-3% of mass of acetic acid. The purity of carbon dioxide is 99.5% or more. The ethanol is introduced into the esterification reaction column three times. The first import volume is 3/5 of total import amount. The addition amount of concentrated sulfuric acid is 0.5-0.8% of mass of acetic acid. The reflux ratio of esterification reaction column is 1.5-2.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN WO2018127562-A1
TI Solar concentration apparatus or catalytic reactor            for thermochemical process, has catalyst which is            transparent or semi-transparent, deposited on support            chosen from structured and non-structured transparent            or semi-transparent supports.
AU MANENTI F
   PIROLA C
   BIANCHI C
AE POLITECNICO DI MILANO (MIPT-C)
   UNIV MILANO (UMIL-C)
GA 201855583W
AB    NOVELTY - A solar concentration apparatus or catalytic                reactor (1) has a solar radiation concentrator (2),                a solar radiation receiver, or transparent or                semi-transparent reactor (3) having at least one                module, a supported catalyst placed inside the                reactor, and a support for the apparatus. The                catalyst is transparent or semi-transparent, and is                deposited on a support chosen from structured                transparent or semi-transparent support (a),                non-structured transparent or semi-transparent                support (b) which is being held inside the reactor                by at least one mesh or grid, and support (c) which                is an inner surface of the transparent                reactor.
   USE - Solar concentration apparatus or catalytic                reactor is used for thermochemical process e.g.                treating acidic gases which occurs in processing                chosen from extraction and refinement of petroleum,                extraction of natural gas, extraction of shale                oil/shale gas, gasification of coal containing                sulfur, extraction of heavy crude oil, treatment of                biomass, and treatment in biogas plants and in                geothermal plants, and for reduction of carbon                dioxide, and photoreforming (all claimed) for                producing hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The solar concentration apparatus or catalytic                reactor has not only the receiver of the solar                radiation or reactor, which is transparent or                semi-transparent but also the catalyst arranged                inside the reactor is transparent or                semi-transparent and is in turn deposited stably on                transparent or semi-transparent material. The solar                concentration apparatus or catalytic reactor                provides necessary energy contribution to carry out                endothermal reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) treatment of acidic gases which occurs in                processing, which involves using the solar                concentration apparatus or catalytic reactor. The                processing is chosen from extraction and refinement                of petroleum, extraction of natural gas, extraction                of shale oil/shale gas, gasification of coal                containing sulfur, extraction of heavy crude oil,                treatment of biomass, and treatment in biogas                plants and in geothermal plants;
   (2) reduction of carbon dioxide in presence of                a reducer, which involves using the solar                concentration apparatus or catalytic reactor. The                reducer is chosen from hydrogen sulfide, methane,                methanol, coal, hydrocarbon, ammonia, hydrogen and                any potentially reducing agent for carbon dioxide;                and
   (3) method of photoreforming, which involves                using the solar concentration apparatus or                catalytic reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                solar concentration apparatus or catalytic                reactor.
   Solar concentration apparatus or catalytic                reactor (1)
   Solar radiation concentrator (2)
   Reactor (3)
   Robotized device (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The concentrator is                  linear-parabolic, Fresnel, parabola or oriented                  plane type, preferably linear parabolic. The                  reactor or receiver is arranged in an axial or                  radial direction with respect to the                  concentrator, preferably in the axial direction.                  The reactor (3) is a star type, an internally                  and/or externally finned spiral or cylindrical                  type, cylindrical type arranged along the axis of                  the concentrator, or tubular or batch type,                  preferably tubular. The catalyst support (a) has                  a honeycomb, foamed lamellar or pinned structure.                  The catalyst support (b) consists of Raschig                  rings, pellets, balls, or saddles. When the                  reactor (3) is tubular, the support (b) is held                  by at least two grids arranged close to the                  entrance and exit of the reactor. When the                  reactor (3) is batch type, the support (b) is                  held by a grid. When the support is of a type (a)                  or (b), it occupies at least 80 vol.%, preferably                  at least 95 vol.% of the reactor. When the                  support is of type (c), it occupies at least 80%,                  preferably at least 95% of the inner area of the                  receiver or reactor. The apparatus has many                  modules arranged in series or in parallel. The                  entire reactor (3) is provided with a robotized                  device (4) cleaning periodically the external                  surface of the reactor from atmospheric agents                  and dust.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: When the reducing agent                  is hydrogen sulfide, the reduction process is                  carried out by reacting 2 mol of hydrogen sulfide                  with carbon dioxide at more than 250 degrees C,                  preferably more than 650 degrees C to give                  hydrogen, carbon monoxide, disulfur, and water.                  Preferred Components: The catalyst used in the                  reduction process is chosen from alumina                  magnesium oxide and/or magnesium aluminate,                  preferably gamma -alumina, or a catalyst                  supported on gamma -alumina.

PN CN108002369-A; CN108002369-B
TI Continuously preparing highly dispersed nano-cobalt/reduced graphene oxide composite material comprises e.g. mixing soluble divalent cobalt salt CoX, graphene oxide, hydrazine hydrate and/or ascorbic acid graphene oxide and centrifuging.
AU YANG M
   CHEN G
   TAO S
   CHEN H
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201837516R
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously preparing highly dispersed nano-cobalt/reduced graphene oxide composite material comprises (i) dissolving soluble divalent cobalt salt CoX and graphene oxide in aqueous solution A, (ii) mixing sodium borohydride, hydrazine hydrate and/or ascorbic acid graphene oxide to obtain aqueous solution B, (iii) entering inert gas phase to the micro-reactor, separating aqueous solutions A and B into inert liquids, and mixing and reacting aqueous solutions A and B to obtain precipitate, and (iv) washing the precipitate, centrifuging, and drying.
   USE - Used as continuously preparing highly dispersed nano-cobalt/reduced graphene oxide composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high dispersion of cobalt nano-particles and narrow particle size.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuously preparing highly dispersed nano-cobalt/reduced graphene oxide composite material comprises (i) dissolving soluble divalent cobalt salt CoX and graphene oxide in aqueous solution A, where the X is chloride, nitrate, acetate and/or sulfate, the concentration of carbon dioxide in the aqueous solution A is 0.0001-0.1 mol/l, and the mass ratio of carbon dioxide to graphene oxide is 1:100-1:5, (ii) mixing sodium borohydride, hydrazine hydrate and/or ascorbic acid graphene oxide to obtain aqueous solution B, where the ratio of the total molarity of the sodium borohydride, hydrazine hydrate and/or ascorbic acid in aqueous solution B to the molar concentration of carbon dioxide in aqueous solution A is 2:1-50:1, (iii) entering the aqueous solutions A and B to a micro-reactor at the same flow rate from the two inlet channels, entering inert gas phase to the micro-reactor from the third inlet channel at a flow rate of 1-10 times the total flow rate of the aqueous solutions A and B, separating aqueous solutions A and B into inert liquids by the inert gas phase in the reactor internal reaction channel, contacting aqueous solutions A and B each other, mixing and reacting, and flowing the reaction out of the outlet channel of the reactor to obtain precipitate, (iv) washing the precipitate, centrifuging, and drying, where the reaction process comprises two liquid inlet one is the micro-reactor of gas inlet and the gas outlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The micro-reactor comprises two sealing plates and one micro-channel plate. The micro-channel plate is engraved with four micro-channels, which are three inlet channels, and one reaction channel respectively connected to the inlet channel. The inlet channels are connected with three inlets respectively. The reaction channel is connected to the outlet.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert gas inlet channel is between the aqueous solution A and B inlet channels, and the two inlet channels comprise equal angles. The angle is 20-80?, which is directly connected with the reaction pipe. The aqueous solution A and B inlet channels are the same size. The hydraulic diameter is 0.2-1 mm. The length is 0.5-2 mm. The inert gas inlet channel comprises a hydraulic diameter of 0.1-0.3 mm, and the length is 0.5-2 mm. The reaction channel hydraulic diameter is 0.2-1 mm, and the length is 5-100 cm. The aqueous solutions A and B enter the micro-reactor at the same flow rate of 0.06-5 ml/minutes. The inert gas phase is air, nitrogen and/or argon.

PN WO2018064395-A1; US2020029583-A1; US11464236-B2
TI Separation of lean meat from lean meat-containing            material e.g. beef pieces involves reducing chilled            lean meat-containing material into lean meat and fat            particles, combining particles with microbiocidal            fluids, and collecting particles.
AU GARWOOD A J M
AE GARWOOD A J M (GARW-Individual)
   GARWOOD A J M (GARW-Individual)
   GARWOOD J (GARW-Individual)
GA 2018260205
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of lean meat from lean                meat-containing material involves chilling a lean                meat-containing material having lean meat and fat,                reducing the material into particles having a                majority of lean meat and smaller particles having                a majority of fat, combining the particles with a                fluid comprising nanobubbles and microbiocidal                agents chosen from e.g. hydrochloric acid, bromine,                fluorine, halogen, chlorine, sulfuric acid, lactic                acid, citric acid, acetic acid, carbonic acid,                carbon dioxide, chlorine, and chlorine compound,                generating the nanobubbles in a tower, and                collecting particles.
   USE - Separation of lean meat from lean                meat-containing material e.g. beef pieces.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently separates lean meat and                fat particles from lean meat-containing material                and eliminates the sources of contamination by                using the microbiocidal fluids with suspended                nanobubbles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of lean meat from lean                meat-containing material involves providing a lean                meat-containing material having lean meat and fat,                chilling the material such that the fat is rigidly                frozen and friable but lean meat remains flexible,                reducing the material into particles, which                comprises particles having a majority of lean meat                and generally smaller particles having a majority                of fat, combining the particles with a fluid                comprising nanobubbles, water and one or more                microbiocidal agents chosen from hypochlorous acid,                hydrochloric acid, bromine, fluorine, halogen,                chlorine, sulfuric acid, lactic acid, citric acid,                acetic acid, ozone, carbonic acid, carbon dioxide,                chlorine dioxide, chlorine, acidified sodium                chlorite, chlorine compound, chlorine compound and                water, aqueous alkaline solution of sodium                hydroxide or calcium hydroxide or any other                suitable alkaline solution or acid, and water with                carbon dioxide, generating the nanobubbles in a                tower having semispherical baffles arranged along a                length of the tower, and collecting particles that                float in the fluid or collecting particles that                sink in the fluid. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included                for the following: (i) separation of fat particles                from lean particles; and (ii) reduction of pathogen                populations e.g. E. coli (0157:H7), Shiga                toxin-producing E. coli, and Salmonella present on                surface of meat pieces and beef carcass.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                chilled beef rapid separation system.
   Stream of beef particles (3)
   Cryogenic freezing tunnel (4)
   Conveyor (8)
   Steel rollers (20,22)
   Cone shaped vessel (24)
DC D12 (Butchering, meat treatment, processing poultry or fish (A22).)

PN US2018038644-A1; WO2018027152-A1; CA3033075-A1; US10281203-B2
TI Liquefaction of industrial gas e.g. natural gas,            involves removing carbon dioxide and water from natural            gas stream, warming and sending it to methanol            production facility, and expanding resulting gas stream            and liquefying industrial gas.
AU GUILLARD A
   TURNEY M A
   ROESCH A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   GUILLARD A (GUIL-Individual)
   TURNEY M A (TURN-Individual)
   ROESCH A (ROES-Individual)
GA 201813593A
AB    NOVELTY - Liquefaction of an industrial gas involves                withdrawing (a) a pressurized natural gas stream                from a pipeline, removing (b) carbon dioxide and                water, expanding (c) the pressurized natural gas                stream (12) to form an expanded natural gas stream                and warming, sending (d) the warmed natural gas                stream to a methanol production facility, expanding                (e) the resulting purge gas and warming, sending                (f) the warmed gas stream to the methanol                production facility for use as fuel, and expanding                (g) a pressurized air gas stream from/derived from                an air separation unit and liquefying the                industrial gas.
   USE - Liquefaction of industrial gas such as natural                gas, nitrogen, and hydrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, efficient and                economical liquefaction of industrial gas under                reduced pressure energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Liquefaction of an industrial gas involves                withdrawing (a) a pressurized natural gas stream                from a natural gas pipeline, removing (b) carbon                dioxide and water from the pressurized natural gas                stream, expanding (c) the pressurized natural gas                stream to form an expanded natural gas stream and                warming the expanded natural gas stream in a                portion (32) of a heat exchanger against the                industrial gas to form a warmed natural gas stream,                sending (d) the warmed natural gas stream to a                methanol production facility under conditions                effective for producing a methanol stream, a                purified hydrogen stream, and a purge gas rich in                hydrogen, expanding (e) the purge gas rich in                hydrogen to form an expanded purge gas (62) and                warming the expanded purge gas in a portion (p2) of                the heat exchanger against the industrial gas to                form a warmed purge gas stream, sending (f) the                warmed purge gas stream to the methanol production                facility for use as fuel, and expanding (g) a                pressurized air gas stream from/derived from an air                separation unit (ASU) to form an expanded air gas                stream and warming the expanded air gas stream in a                portion (p3) of the heat exchanger against the                industrial gas to form a warmed air gas stream. The                industrial gas is liquefied during step (g).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the liquefaction of industrial gas.
   Natural gas (2)
   Pressurized natural gas stream (12)
   Main air compressor (20)
   Portion (32)
   Expanded purge gas (62)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The air gas from/                  derived from an ASU is a gas stream chosen from a                  compressed and purified air from a main air                  compressor (20), and a nitrogen stream from a                  medium pressure column. The industrial gas                  further comprises trace components having a                  freezing point temperature warmer than that of                  methane. The pressurized natural gas stream                  comprises methane, and trace components having                  freezing point temperatures warmer than that of                  methane, preferably warmer than -140 degrees C.                  The pressurized air gas stream further comprises                  nitrogen gas from a nitrogen pipeline. Preferred                  Process: The portion (32) of the heat exchanger                  and the portion (p2) of the heat exchanger are                  kept at temperatures warmer than the freezing                  point temperature of the trace components. The                  trace components are removed from the industrial                  gas prior to the industrial gas being cooled in                  the portion (p3) of the heat exchanger. Preferred                  Condition: The portion (p3) of cooling is                  provided at colder temperatures than that of the                  other two portions. In step (f), the portion (p3)                  of the pressurized natural gas stream is cooled                  to a temperature warmer than -140 degrees C. In                  step (g), the portion (p3) of the pressurized                  natural gas stream is cooled to -140 to -165                  degrees C. Preferred Property: The air gas from                  the ASU has a pressure of 4-40 bara.

PN CN113680325-A
TI Preparation of diamine-modified metal organic framework/graphene oxide olefin composite adsorbent for absorbing carbon dioxide, involves dispersing graphene oxide, adding aminoterephthalic acid, activating obtained complex, and drying.
AU XU Q
   SONG F
   CAO Y
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2021E0995V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of diamine-modified metal organic framework (MOF)/graphene oxide olefin composite adsorbent, involves dispersing graphene oxide in aqueous solution, obtaining graphene oxide olefin and stripped graphene oxide, removing insoluble matter by centrifugation, and centrifuging remaining solution to obtain graphene oxide olefin, freeze-drying, using chemical cutting method to prepare graphene oxide olefin, adding obtained product to N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol co-solvent and mixing, adding zirconium tetrachloride and aminoterephthalic acid, synthesizing graphene oxide olefin/ammonia-UiO-66 complex in-situ by microwave-assisted solvothermal method, activating graphene oxide olefin/ammonia-UiO-66 complex and removing solvent by supercritical carbon dioxide, adding obtained product to anhydrous hexane suspension, adding urea, filtering after refluxing under nitrogen atmosphere for 18 hours, washing with hexane, and drying at 50degrees Celsius for 24 hours under vacuum conditions.
   USE - Preparation of diamine-modified MOF/graphene oxide olefin composite adsorbent used for absorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of diamine-modified MOF/graphene oxide olefin composite adsorbent, involves using graphene oxide as raw material, dispersing graphene oxide in aqueous solution and ultrasonically peeling off, where the ultrasonic frequency and time are 300 W and 2 hours, respectively, obtaining graphene oxide olefin and incompletely stripped graphene oxide, removing insoluble matter by centrifugation, and centrifuging remaining solution to obtain graphene oxide olefin of primary sieve, freeze-drying, using chemical cutting method to prepare graphene oxide olefin with 1-3 layers and surface size of 60-150 nm, adding obtained product to N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol co-solvent and mixing, adding zirconium tetrachloride and aminoterephthalic acid, where the mass ratio of zirconium tetrachloride, aminoterephthalic acid and graphene oxide olefin is 1.4:1:0.05, synthesizing graphene oxide olefin/ammonia-UiO-66 complex in-situ by microwave-assisted solvothermal method, activating graphene oxide olefin/ammonia-UiO-66 complex and removing solvent by supercritical carbon dioxide, adding obtained product to anhydrous hexane suspension, adding urea, where the mass ratio of urea to graphene oxide olefin/ammonia-UiO-66 complex is (0.05-0.15):1, filtering after refluxing under nitrogen atmosphere for 18 hours, washing with hexane, and drying at 50degrees Celsius for 24 hours under vacuum conditions.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for absorbing carbon dioxide, which has an air inlet device, an air outlet device and a filter channel provided with an adsorbent packing layer, where the adsorbent packing layer is attached with the diamine modified MOF/graphene oxide olefin composite adsorbent.

TI Flue gas carbon dioxide recovery and resource utilization device includes heat recovery system, circulation channel, flue gas purification system, decarbonization tower, bipolar membrane electrodialysis system, and product recovery system.
AU JIN X
   TONG M
   QIU M
   XUE Y
   ZHANG X
   LI S
   LI J
   ZHANG J
AE BAOWU WATER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BAOW-Non-standard)
GA 202196816J
AB    NOVELTY - A flue gas carbon dioxide recovery and resource utilization device includes heat recovery system, flue gas purification system, decarbonization tower, circulation channel, bipolar membrane electrodialysis system, and product recovery system. The flue gas purification system is used for purifying flue gas. The heat recovery system is used to recover the heat in purified flue gas. The bipolar membrane electrodialysis system is used to receive the concentrated brine formed in zero-discharge treatment process of industrial wastewater and prepare sodium hydroxide solution. The circulation channel is provided with second liquid inlet and second liquid outlet. The decarburization tower is provided with first liquid inlet, air inlet and first liquid outlet. The product recovery system used to separate the sodium bicarbonate crystals to obtain sodium bicarbonate filter cake, and uses recovered heat to dry the sodium bicarbonate filter cake to obtain baking soda product.
   USE - Flue gas carbon dioxide recovery and resource utilization device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device achieves combined treatment of zero discharge of industrial wastewater and flue gas decarbonization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A flue gas carbon dioxide recovery and resource utilization device comprises flue gas purification system, heat recovery system, bipolar membrane electrodialysis system, decarbonization tower, circulation channel and product recovery system. The flue gas purification system, heat recovery system and decarbonization tower are connected. The flue gas purification system is used for purifying flue gas. The heat recovery system is used to recover the heat in purified flue gas. The bipolar membrane electrodialysis system is used to receive the concentrated brine formed in zero-discharge treatment process of industrial wastewater and prepare sodium hydroxide solution. The decarburization tower is provided with first liquid inlet, air inlet and first liquid outlet. The circulation channel is provided with second liquid inlet and second liquid outlet. The product recovery system used to separate the sodium bicarbonate crystals to obtain sodium bicarbonate filter cake, and uses recovered heat to dry the sodium bicarbonate filter cake to obtain baking soda product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for recovery and resource utilization of flue gas carbon dioxide comprising purifying flue gas and recovering heat in purified flue gas, collecting concentrated brine formed during the zero-discharge treatment of industrial wastewater, processing through bipolar membrane electrodialysis system to obtain sodium hydroxide solution, and contacting with the flue gas to carry out chemical reaction and obtain mixed solution of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate; and continuously contacting with flue gas until sodium bicarbonate crystals are precipitated, separating crystals to obtain sodium bicarbonate filter cake, and drying using recovered heat to obtain baking soda product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction of flue gas and sodium hydroxide solution is done for 4-20 seconds.

TI System for pretreating fly ash water washing            wastewater using carbon dioxide in exhaust gas of            cement kiln, includes intake pipe is connected to            multi-stage spray absorption bed and multi-stage spray            absorption bed connected to exhaust pipe.
AU SU W
   XING Y
   CAI C
   LIANG B
   MA H
   SONG C
   WANG L
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 202051903Y
AB    NOVELTY - System includes induced draft fan (2),                multi-stage spray absorption bed (3), first flow                meter (4), wastewater pump (5), second flow meter                (6), sedimentation tank (7) and circulation pump                (8). The induced draft fan and the second flow                meter are arranged on the intake pipe, the intake                pipe is connected to the multi-stage spray                absorption bed. The multi-stage spray absorption                bed is simultaneously connected to the exhaust                pipe. The water inlet pipe and the water outlet                pipe are provided with a first flow meter, a waste                water pump and a circulation pump, and the water                outlet pipe is connected to the sedimentation                tank.
   USE - System for pretreating fly ash water washing                wastewater using carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas                of cement kiln (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively solves the problems of                excessively high cost of reagents used in the                treatment of fly ash water washing wastewater and                excessive sodium content of the wastewater after                treatment, and reduces the introduction of sodium                ions and reduces the salt content in the water, and                simultaneously reduces carbon dioxide emissions and                effectively reduces the concentration of alkaline                earth metals and heavy metal ions in fly ash water                washing wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of using the carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas of a                cement kiln to pretreat fly ash water washing                wastewater, which involves:
   (A) separating portion of the exhaust gas from                the cement kiln exhaust pipe, and passing portion                of the exhaust gas into the multi-stage spray                absorption bed through the induced draft fan;
   (B) pumping the water-washed fly ash                wastewater into the multi-stage spray absorption                bed on the outlet side through the wastewater pump,                and using the circulating pump to pumps the                supernatant in the absorption bed into the                multi-stage spray absorption bed on the intake                side;
   (C) washing fly ash wastewater and mixing                exhaust gas in a multi-stage spray absorption bed,                and slurry after the final absorption treatment                flows into the sedimentation tank and forms a                precipitate; and
   (D) making the supernatant meet the                requirements of the lower-level process, recycling,                and absorbing the treated gas and discharging                through the exhaust pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of                system for pretreating fly ash water washing                wastewater using carbon dioxide in exhaust gas of                cement kiln.

   Sedimentation tank (7)
   Circulation pump (8)

The water-cement ratio of the                  water-washed fly ash wastewater is                  8:1-12:1.

PT P
PN CN111068743-A; CN111068743-B
TI Production of low-carbon olefins involves            contacting methane with steam, mixing with dry weight            integrated gas, contacting obtained Fischer-Tropsch            synthesis reaction feed with catalyst, and separating            low-carbon olefins and methane.
AU JIN C
   WU Y
   ZHANG R
   HOU C
   SUN X
   YAN Z
   XIA G
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 202038123F
AB    NOVELTY - Production of low-carbon olefins involves                contacting methane with steam to perform steam                reforming reaction to obtain steam to be reformed                into gas, contacting methane and carbon dioxide to                perform a dry reforming reaction to obtain dry                weight integrated gas, recombining at least portion                of the steam into gas and mixing at least portion                of the dry weight integrated gas into gas to                prepare a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed,                reducing Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst                precursor and activating to obtain a                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst, contacting the                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed with                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst to perform a                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction to obtain a                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream, and                separating low-carbon olefins, methane and carbon                dioxide from the product stream. The catalyst                precursor comprises a carrier and an active metal                oxide supported on the carrier. The carrier is a                manganese oxide molecular sieve.
   USE - Production of low-carbon olefins.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of increasing                utilization rate of two greenhouse gases, carbon                dioxide and methane, improving overall process                resources and energy utilization, and reducing                emission of greenhouse gas and system energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of low-carbon olefins involves                contacting methane with steam to perform steam                reforming reaction to obtain steam to be reformed                into gas, contacting methane and carbon dioxide to                perform a dry reforming reaction to obtain dry                weight integrated gas, recombining at least portion                of the steam into gas and mixing at least portion                of the dry weight integrated gas into gas to                prepare a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed,                reducing Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst                precursor and activating to obtain a                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst, contacting the                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed with                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst to perform a                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction to obtain a                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream, and                separating low-carbon olefins, methane and carbon                dioxide from the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product                stream. The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst                precursor comprises a carrier and an active metal                oxide supported on the carrier. The carrier is a                manganese oxide molecular sieve. The active metal                in the active metal oxide is a group VIII metal.                The reduction activation step involves pre-reducing                the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst precursor in                a gas (g1) to obtain a pre-reduction catalyst, and                reducing the pre-reduction catalyst in a second gas                (g2) to obtain Fischer-Tropsch synthesis                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The active metal is                  at least one chosen from iron, ruthenium,                  platinum, cobalt, rhenium, palladium and iridium,                  preferably iron. The catalyst precursor comprises                  0.2-40 wt.%, preferably 0.3-30 wt.% active metal                  oxide. The gas (g1) is hydrogen or a mixture of                  hydrogen and an inert gas. In the Fischer-Tropsch                  synthesis reaction feed, the molar ratio of                  hydrogen with respect to carbon monoxide is                  0.4-3:1, preferably 1.5-2.2:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The catalyst precursor is                  prepared by doping or supporting the active metal                  on the carrier, drying at 80-350 degrees C,                  preferably 100-300 degrees C for 1-24 hours,                  preferably 2-12 hours, and calcining at 250-900                  degrees C, preferably 350-800 degrees C for                  0.5-12 hours, preferably 2-6 hours to obtain the                  supported catalyst precursor. The pre-reduction                  step is carried out at 200-600 degrees C,                  preferably 350-550 degrees C and a pressure of                  0-2.5 MPa, preferably 0.1-2 MPa for 1-20 hours,                  preferably 5-8 hours. The steam reforming                  reaction is carried out in a fixed bed reactor at                  a reaction temperature of 700-950 degrees C,                  preferably 800-900 and a reaction pressure of                  0.1-5 MPa, preferably 1-3 MPa. The dry reforming                  reaction is carried out in a fixed bed reactor at                  a reaction temperature of 600-800 degrees CC,                  preferably 650-750 degrees C and a reaction                  pressure of 0.1-5 MPa, preferably 1-3 MPa. The                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction is carried out                  in a fluidized bed reactor and/or a fixed bed                  reactor at a reaction temperature of 200-400                  degrees C, preferably, 300-380 degrees C, a                  reaction pressure of 0.5-3 MPa, preferably 1-2.5                  MPa, and a gas hourly volumetric space velocity                  of feed of 5000-50000 hour-1, preferably                  10000-30000 hour-1. The method further involves                  recycling the separated methane for steam                  reforming reaction and/or dry reforming reaction,                  and recycling the separated carbon dioxide for                  dry reforming reaction. Preferred Composition:                  The gas (g2) is a gaseous hydrocarbon or a mixed                  gas of a gaseous hydrocarbon and an inert gas.                  The molar ratio of inert gas with respect to                  gaseous hydrocarbon is 1-200:1, preferably                  15-30:1. The gaseous hydrocarbons are gaseous                  alkanes and/or gaseous olefins, preferably at                  least one chosen from methane, ethane, ethylene,                  propylene, propane, butane, and butene.

TI Method for processing Agrocybe aegerita, involves calculating mass of substrate, calculating carbon dioxide, removing matrix followed by formulating gas environment for slow growth of mushrooms and introducing gas into sealed box.
AU ZHANG Z
   GUO Q
   LIU J
