   FAN X
AE CHENGDU AGRIC & FORESTRY ACAD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201972883B
AB    NOVELTY - An Agrocybe aegerita which is suitable for transportation at different distances processing method involves calculating the mass of the substrate, calculating the carbon dioxide, removing the matrix according to the calculated cut off mushroom substrate, weighing the standard matrix weight and cut off the substrate which does not grow one end of mushroom from the standard substrate, that is substrate having the weight grown with mushroom body is obtained by performing color protection and fresh-keeping treatment by placing mushroom in the composite color protection fresh-keeping solution for 30-50 minutes, formulating a gas environment for slow growth of mushrooms by preparing gas composition in the closed box to be consistent with the original growth environment of mushroom, and introducing calculated carbon dioxide, and configuring new gas according to the gas composition and ratio, placing cut mushroom in a closed box, and introducing gas into the sealed box.
   USE - Method for processing Agrocybe aegerita which is suitable for transportation at different distances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An Agrocybe aegerita which is suitable for transportation at different distances processing method involves calculating the mass of the substrate to be excised represented by the expression as given in the specification, calculating the carbon dioxide that is required to pass through the transportation process of the same quality mushroom, removing the matrix according to the calculated cut off the mushroom substrate, weighing the standard matrix weight and cut off the substrate which does not grow one end of mushroom from the standard substrate, that is substrate having the weight grown with mushroom body is obtained by performing color protection and fresh-keeping treatment by placing mushroom in the composite color protection fresh-keeping solution for 30-50 minutes, where composite color protection fresh-keeping liquid is a sodium citrate-citric acid buffer solution system, the concentration of citric acid is 1-2%, and the pH value is 4.5-5.5, followed by formulating a gas environment for slow growth of tea tree mushrooms by preparing gas composition in the closed box to be consistent with the original growth environment of the tea tree mushroom, and introducing calculated carbon dioxide to form a new gas environment, and configuring new gas according to the gas composition and ratio, placing cut mushroom in a closed box together with the substrate, and introducing gas into the sealed box at the same time period as C2 according to the transportation route during transportation, where temperature in the closed box is 1-7 degrees C.

PT P
PN US2018078895-A1; US10369513-B2
TI Purification of methane-containing stream used as            fuel, involves adding raw methane stream comprising            methane and contaminants, and water stream, into mixing            zone, homogenizing to disperse contaminants within            water stream which is chilled.
AU PEASE J R
   BLATNICK J F
AE ALCHEM ENVIRONMENTAL IP LLC (ALCH-Non-standard)
GA 201822368V
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of methane-containing stream                involves introducing a raw methane stream                comprising methane and contaminants e.g. carbon                dioxide, ammonia and hydrogen sulfide, into a                mixing zone, introducing a water stream into the                mixing zone such that the water stream is                commingled with the methane stream, homogenizing                the streams such that the contaminants are                dispersed within the water stream, and forming a                contaminant isolation stream comprising the                contaminants and a purified methane stream. The                water stream is chilled to below ambient                temperature to increase dissolution of the                contaminants.
   USE - Purification of methane-containing stream e.g.                biogas stream used as fuel in internal combustion                engine and burner (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently purifies                methane-containing stream. The reactions carried                out in the method yield value added and                environmentally neutral products and                compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of methane-containing stream                involves introducing a raw methane stream                comprising methane and contaminants selected from                carbon dioxide, ammonia and/or hydrogen sulfide,                into a mixing zone through an inlet, introducing a                water stream into the mixing zone through a                separate water inlet such that the water stream is                commingled with the raw methane stream upon both                streams entering the mixing zone, passing the                commingled streams through a Venturi (8) to                homogenize the streams such that one or more                contaminants within the raw methane stream are                dispersed within the water stream, and forming a                contaminant isolation stream comprising the                contaminants and a purified methane stream. The                water stream is chilled to below ambient                temperature to increase dissolution of the                contaminants. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                purification of biogas stream containing methane                using a polyphasic pressurized homogenizer (PPH),                which involves introducing the raw biogas stream                comprising methane, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen                sulfide into the mixing zone through an inlet of                the PPH, introducing a chilled water stream into                the mixing zone through the separate water inlet                such that the water stream is commingled with the                raw biogas stream upon both streams entering the                mixing zone, passing the commingled streams through                a Venturi of the PPH such that to homogenize the                streams such that the carbon dioxide and hydrogen                sulfide are dissolved into the chilled water stream                and removed from the raw biogas stream, forming a                purified methane stream and an aqueous contaminant                isolation stream. The water stream is chilled to                below ambient temperature to increase dissolution                of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a central, longitudinal                section of a homogenizer.
   Water/solvent jet (1)
   Ante-mixing chamber (3)
   Mixing chamber (4)
   Venturi (8)
   Housing (9)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The chilled                  oxygenated water stream comprises 8-12 ppm                  oxygen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen sulfide of methane stream dissolve into                  the chilled water stream such that to remove                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide from the raw                  methane stream. The dissolved hydrogen sulfide                  forms hydrogen gas and precipitated elemental                  sulfur. The raw methane stream is introduced into                  an antechamber before introduction into the                  mixing zone. The antechamber comprises a packing                  material for retarding flow of the raw methane                  stream through the antechamber. A retention                  chamber is provided downstream from an outlet of                  the Venturi so as to extend a residence time of                  the contaminant isolation stream and the purified                  methane stream. The inlets, Venturi, and                  retention chamber are insulated. Purification of                  methane-containing stream further involves                  capturing carbon dioxide and conveying it one or                  more storage tanks for use as a plant metabolite,                  collecting the precipitated sulfur which is used                  as value added product. Chilled water is                  oxygenated, and the raw biogas stream further                  comprises ammonia. The hydrogen sulfide is                  converted to water and elemental sulfur in the                  presence of chilled oxygenated water, and the                  carbon dioxide and ammonia are converted to                  ammonium carbonate in the presence of the chilled                  oxygenated water. Purification of biogas stream                  further involves introducing oxygen into the                  chilled water stream to produce the chilled                  oxygenated water stream prior to its introduction                  into the mixing zone. Oxygen is introduced into                  the chilled water stream within an upstream PPH.                  Oxygen is dissolved within the chilled oxygenated                  water stream at a concentration, which is above                  the concentration provided by sparging. The                  dissolved hydrogen reacts with oxygen in chilled                  oxygenated water to produce water and elemental                  sulfur. The carbon dioxide is converted to                  carbonic acid in chilled oxygenated water. The                  ammonia reacts with the carbonic acid in chilled                  oxygenated water to produce ammonium carbonate.                  The raw biogas stream further comprises water                  vapor and particulate matter. The water vapor                  condenses to form liquid water that is removed                  from the raw biogas stream, and which enters the                  aqueous contaminant isolation stream. The                  particulate matter settles with elemental sulfur                  in the aqueous contaminant isolation stream.                  Preferred Condition: The chilled water stream is                  chilled to a temperature of 60-75 degrees                  F.

TI Modular drainage filter system for treatment of            agricultural drainage water (ADW), has inlet,            bioelectrochemical reactor module, outlet for expelling            partly denitrified ADW, inlet sensor and processing            means for determining optimal energy.
AU KJARGAARD C
AE NOVADRAIN APS (NOVA-Non-standard)
GA 2022D2232M
AB    NOVELTY - Modular drainage filter system (1) comprises                an inlet for receiving agricultural drainage water                (ADW), at least one bioelectrochemical reactor                (BER) module arranged to denitrify ADW and at least                one outlet for expelling at least partly                denitrified ADW, where the system further comprises                at least one inlet sensor arranged upstream the BER                and processing means for determining the optimal                energy provided to the BER based on the data from                the inlet sensors.
   USE - The system is useful for treatment of ADW                (claimed) e.g. residual streams derived from                agricultural activities.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: is arranged to provide reports to                authorities and/or users; is effectively                denitrifies ADW cost effectively and preferably                carbon dioxide-equivalent effectively and even                carbon dioxide-equivalent neutral; provides                continuous and reliable cleaning of incoming ADW                even through periods with large change in flow                rates and/or level of pollution; may take up 10.5                m2/100 hectare field versus e.g. 1120 m2/100                hectare field for a wood chip based biofilter; and                requires significantly less space than known                solutions to achieve the required degree of                denitrification.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates schematic view of a                drained field and power sources in relation to a                system.

AU ZHANG Y
   XU F
   GU Y
AE WUXI YONGXIAN INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 2022C9022E
AB    NOVELTY - The early warning system comprises a sensor                node for monitoring the concentration change of the                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane                and volatile organic gas, and monitoring the                temperature and humidity of the gas. The data of                the sensor node is collected through the data                collecting box. The collected data to the edge                computing machine is expanded and uploaded. The                temperature of the battery cabinet is detected                through the thermal imaging instrument, and sending                the temperature change to the edge computing                machine. The data of the sensor node collected by                the data collecting box is accessed according to                different addresses and collecting the temperature                change of the thermal imaging device through the                edge computing machine according to different                addresses. The judging result of the edge computing                machine is received through the fire main                machine.
   USE - Early warning system for an electrochemical                energy storage power station.
   ADVANTAGE - The fault position and early warning can be                located. The fire-fighting host adopts a certain                measures to avoid or reduce the occurrence of the                disaster. PN CN115006589-A
TI Preparing carbon nanotube modified mineralized            collagen material involves e.g. mixing carbon nano-tube            with strong oxidant, crushing, sieving, adding into            collagen acid solution, dropping calcium salt solution,            allowing to stand, washing, filtering, freezing drying            and crushing.
AU HU G
   WU J
   QIU Z
   HU Y
   HE Z
   CUI Y
   SONG T
   ZHU J
AE BEIJING AOJING HEALTH TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   ALLGENS MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ALLG-Non-standard)
GA 2022B99598
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon nanotube modified mineralized                collagen material involves (a) mixing carbon                nano-tube with strong oxidant, ultrasonically                sequentially, heating, filtering, washing and                vacuum drying to obtain modified carbon nano tube,                (b) crushing the modified carbon tube and sieving                to obtain a modified carbon dioxide powder, (c)                adding the obtained modified carbon oxide powder to                the collagen acid solution, continuously stirring                to form a first blend, (d) slowly dropping calcium                salt solution into the first blend and continue to                mix uniformly, slowly adding phosphate solution,                and continuing to continue to stir, and (e) slowly                dripping sodium hydroxide solution to the second                blend until the pH is 6-8, and continuously                stirring until there is white suspended matter,                allowing to stand to obtain precipitate, washing,                filtering and freezing and drying, crushing and                sieveing to obtain the carbon dioxide-modified                mineralized gelatin material.
   USE - The method for preparing carbon nano-tube                modified mineralized collagen material which is                useful in bone defect repair (claimed), of the                bearing part.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon nano-tube modified mineralized                collagen material has excellent mechanical property                and biological property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                carbon nanotube modified mineralized collagen                material, obtained by the method as mentioned                above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (3), the                  calcium salt is calcium chloride and/or calcium                  nitrate; and the soluble phosphate is sodium                  phosphate, sodium monophosphate and potassium                  phosphate.

TI Method for simultaneously detecting twelve            elements in plant sample, involves obtaining detection            result according to standard curve, converting to            obtain content of elements in sample, and comparing            detection result with standard curve.

   LI R
   LI M
   CHANG Q
AE GEOLOGICAL SURVEY JIANGSU PROVINCE (GEOL-Non-standard)
GA 2022A20973
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves weighing a sample in a                microwave digestion tank. A deionized water washing                digestion tank inner wall is washed. A reverse wang                aqueous solution is added. A microwave digestion                device is placed in the microwave digestion chamber                for digestion. A cover is screwed in a ventilation                cabinet. The digestion liquid is transferred to a                plastic colorimetric tube. A standard solution and                an internal standard solution of each element are                prepared. The concentration of the element to be                measured is taken as an abscissa. The ratio of the                to-be-measured element and a response signal value                of a selected internal standard element is selected                as an ordinate. A detection result is obtained                according to the standard curve. The content of                multiple elements in the plant sample is obtained.                Low temperature heating is performed to remove                ethanol or carbon dioxide when the sample contains                ethanol or the carbon dioxide.

AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202271280R
AB    NOVELTY - Full-inorganic halide perovskite composite                material, is claimed. The composite material is                silver/silver (AgX)/cesium lead tribromide                composite material. The silver/AgX are                nanoparticles, and the silver nanoparticles are                uniformly distributed on the surface of AgX, and                cesium lead tribromide is supporting material,                which is combined with AgX through the exchange of                halogen atoms. The AgX is silver chloride or silver                bromide.
   USE - The composite material is useful as                photothermal catalyst in environmental pollution                control, preferably methane dry reforming reaction                or carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material: can separate                photogenerated electron holes of the prepared                silver/silver bromide/cesium lead tribromide                composites; shows good photothermal catalytic CRM                performance; greatly improves the light absorption                efficiency when used as photothermal catalyst for                CRM; is beneficial to improve the utilization rate                of solar energy; reduces the reaction temperature;                and improves the stability of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   preparing the composite material, comprising                preparing silver/AgX/cesium lead tribromide                composites by in-situ ion exchange reaction between                silver/AgX composites and cesium lead tribromide in                organic solvents; and
   photothermal catalyst, comprising carrier and                catalyst supported on the carrier, where the                catalyst is the full-inorganic halide perovskite                composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the                  silver/AgX/cesium lead tribromide composite                  material, the mass ratio of silver, AgX and                  cesium lead tribromide is 0.5-1.5:5-10:90-100.                  The particle size range of silver/AgX                  nanoparticles is 200-500 nm. The particle size of                  the silver nanoparticles is in the range of 10-30                  nm. In the ethanolic solution of sodium chloride,                  the volume ratio of the saturated sodium chloride                  solution to absolute ethanol is 1:20-500,                  preferably 1:80-120. The organic solvent of the                  reaction system of silver/AgX complex and cesium                  lead tribromide is acetonitrile, isopropanol,                  cyclohexane, n-propanol, acetylene, toluene or                  acetone, preferably absolute ethanol. The                  reaction time of the silver/AgX composite and                  cesium lead tribromide is 0.5-3 hours, preferably                  1-2 hours.Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  silver/AgX composite comprises dispersing silver                  nitrate and polyvinylpyrrolidone in absolute                  ethanol to obtain mixed solution, pouring the                  saturated sodium halide solution into absolute                  ethanol to obtain ethanolic solution of sodium                  halide, adding the mixed solution dropwise to the                  ethanol solution of sodium halide, stirring the                  reaction to obtain uniform AgX/sodium (NaX)                  mixed-phase structure, photo reducing the AgX/NaX                  mixed-phase structure to generate uniform silver                  nanoparticles on the surface of AgX to obtain                  silver/AgX/NaX, removing the NaX core in the                  Ag/AgX/NaX mixed phase structure by washing to                  obtain Ag/AgX nanoparticles. The sodium halide is                  sodium chloride or sodium bromide, the lamp used                  in the illumination reduction process is xenon                  lamp, the illumination power is 300 mW/cm2, and                  the illumination time is 20-60 minutes,                  preferably 30 minutes, and alternately washing                  with ethanol and using water to remove the sodium                  chloride core in the silver/AgX/sodium chloride                  mixed phase structure. The preparation method of                  cesium lead tribromide is mixing and grinding                  cesium bromide and lead (II) bromide in                  proportion, and obtaining cesium lead tribromide                  after sintering. The mass ratio of cesium bromide                  and lead (II) bromide is 0.8-1.2:1. The sintering                  temperature is 300-450&#8451;, preferably                  400&#8451;, and the sintering time is 1-4                  hours, preferably 2 hours. The preparation method                  further comprises washing and drying the prepared                  silver/AgX/cesium lead tribromide composite                  material. The washing is 2-3 times with                  acetonitrile and ethanol. The drying temperature                  is 50-120&#8451;, preferably                  70-100&#8451;, and the drying time is 10-28                  hours, preferably 10-15 hours.

GA 2022378295
AB    NOVELTY - producing high-purity lithium carbonate from crude lithium carbonate lime caustic carbonization, which comprises washing crude lithium carbonate with water to remove soluble salts, stirring the separated solid with water and quicklime to undergo causticization reaction, and then performing solid-liquid separation to obtain a lithium hydroxide solution. The lithium hydroxide solution is concentrated, and mixed with an impurity remover under stirring to obtain a lithium hydroxide refined solution. The lithium hydroxide refined solution is filtered after absorbing carbon dioxide and carbonizing to obtain a lithium bicarbonate solution. The lithium bicarbonate solution is mixed and reacted with another portion of the refined lithium hydroxide solution to obtain the high-purity lithium carbonate product.
   USE - Method for producing high-purity lithium carbonate used in battery, medicine, war industry and aerospace fields.

TI Producing latex foam bra pad by adding natural            latex, foam stabilizer, sulfur, zinc oxide, accelerator            Mz to foaming chamber of foaming machine, preparing            foaming material by stirring and mixing, injecting into            mold, vacuumizing, placing mold into plate press,            washing and drying.
AU HUANG J
AE HUANG J (HUAN-Individual)
GA 202202785M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a latex foam bra pad                involves adding 100 pts. wt. natural latex, 1-2                pts. wt. foam stabilizer, 2-3 pts. wt. sulfur, 2-4                pts. wt. zinc oxide, 1-2 pts. wt. accelerator Mz to                the foaming chamber of the foaming machine,                pressing the air of 3-8 times the volume of the                natural latex, and preparing the foaming material                by stirring and mixing, injecting the foamed                material into the mold, and vacuumizing, so that                the foamed material loses pressure and expands to                fill the mold, placing the mold into the                refrigerating chamber and freezing for 10-20                minutes, feeding carbon dioxide gas for 3-5                minutes, and the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas                introduced is 0.1-0.5 MPa, placing the mold into                the plate press for heating and vulcanization,                taking out the bra pad from the mold, washing and                drying to obtain a foamed bra pad.
   USE - The method is used for producing a latex foam                bra pad.
   ADVANTAGE - The latex foam bra pad is lighter in weight                and cost-effective, and has excellent elasticity                and air permeability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method involves drying                  3 hours at 70degrees Celsius of temperature in                  baking oven, dehumidification while drying. TI Preparing fresh coriander seasoning oil comprises cleaning fresh coriander, draining, freeze-drying, crushing, subjecting crushed coriander to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, refining, mixing coriander extract essential oil with e.g. small ground sesame oil and soybean oil.
AU XING X
   SUN K
   HE X
   DENG D
AE ZHENGZHOU XUEMAILONG FOOD SPICES CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C8422Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fresh coriander seasoning oil comprises (a) taking fresh coriander, cleaning, draining, cutting into 3-5 cm sections, (b) placing the coriander pretreated in step (a) into a low-temperature freeze-drying machine for low-temperature freeze-drying treatment, (c) crushing the freeze-dried coriander in step (b) with a crusher, subjecting the crushed coriander to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, collecting the extract, (d) refining the coriander extract in step (c) by molecular distillation to obtain coriander extract essential oil and (e) uniformly mixing the coriander extract essential oil obtained in step (d) with small ground sesame oil, garlic oil, ginger oil and soybean oil to obtain fresh coriander seasoning oil

AU KAEAERIAEINEN T

   MANNINEN A
   GENOUD G
AE VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a solid or liquid carbon-containing material exhibiting a carbon isotope ratio. The oxygen gas or gas mixture (14) comprising oxygen gas are provided. The gas or gas mixture exhibits an oxygen isotope ratio. The solid carbon-containing material is brings in contact with the oxygen gas or the gas mixture comprising oxygen gas, in a high temperature to oxidize a portion of the solid carbon-containing material to carbon dioxide to obtain the gaseous carbon or oxygen isotope reference in the form of carbon dioxide. The carbon isotope ratio in the solid carbon-containing material is determined and determines oxygen isotope ratio in the oxygen gas or the gas mixture comprising oxygen.
   USE - Method for preparing gaseous isotope reference.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a new gaseous standard for isotope ratio measurements that will not require large volumes of standard gases to be transported to the analysis laboratory, particularly if several standards exhibiting different isotope ratios are desired.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: In the method (M1), the                  step of inserting further comprises placing less                  than 10 g into the housing, placing 4 plus minus                  1 g of the nitrous oxide into the housing, and                  placing 4 plus minus 1 g of at least one of the                  argon and the nitrogen into the housing, where at                  least one of the nitrous oxide, the argon, and                  the nitrogen comprises a liquid form. The method                  further comprises: pressurizing a secondary                  container containing cream by passing from the                  sealed container via the delivery port at least a                  portion of the nitrous oxide and at least one of                  the argon and the nitrogen; heating the sealed                  container by at least 10 degrees C, which changes                  a state of the nitrous oxide in the sealed                  container from a liquid phase to a gas phase;                  heating the sealed container by at least 100                  degrees C, which changes a state of the argon, in                  the sealed container, from a liquid phase argon                  to a gas phase argon; pressurizing a secondary                  container containing a cream with at least 30                  mass.% of the nitrous oxide and at least 30                  mass.% of at least one of the argon and the                  molecular nitrogen passing from the sealed                  container through the delivery port to the                  secondary container; The                  method (M2) further comprises the step of                  inserting, further comprising: placing a liquid                  form of the nitrous oxide into the housing, and                  heating the pressurized cartridge by at least 10                  degrees C, the step of heating resulting in a                  phase change of the nitrous oxide from a liquid                  phase into a gas phase within the pressurized                  cartridge; placing a liquid into the housing, the                  liquid comprising at least one of the argon and                  the molecular nitrogen, and heating the                  pressurized cartridge by at least 100 degrees C,                  the step of heating resulting in a phase change                  of the liquid into a gas within the pressurized                  cartridge; and the step of placing a solid into                  the housing, the solid comprising at least one of                  the nitrous oxide, the argon, and the molecular                  nitrogen, and heating the pressurized cartridge                  by at least 100 degrees C, the step of heating                  resulting in a phase change of the solid into a                  gas within the pressurized cartridge. The step of                  capping further comprises: cold welding the                  housing to the cap; and welding a first flange of                  the housing to a second flange of the cap.

PN WO2020197612-A1; CA3134911-A1; US2022012757-A1; EP3942507-A1; IN202127048103-A
TI Method for automatically measuring carbon offsets            for devices consuming electrical power executed by            computer, involves calculating carbon offset amount by            converting final reduction value from kilowatt-hours to            mass of carbon dioxide saved.
AU WALKER C
   MARTIN J
AE WALKER C (WALK-Individual)
   MARTIN J (MART-Individual)
   MARTIN J (MART-Individual)
   WALKER C (WALK-Individual)
   WALKER C (WALK-Individual)
   MARTIN J (MART-Individual)
GA 202096065F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining power usage data                in kilowatt-hours from the electrically- powered                devices. An initial power reduction value is                calculated based upon the power usage data. A final                reduction value is calculated by applying a line                loss multiplier to the initial reduction value. The                line loss multiplier accounts for power loss over a                distance between a power generator and the devices.                A carbon offset amount is calculated by converting                the final reduction value from kilowatt-hours to a                mass of carbon dioxide saved based upon a fuel type                used to generate the electrical power. The carbon                offset amount is associated to the user.
   USE - Method for automatically measuring carbon                offsets for devices consuming electrical power                executed by computer.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon credit redeeming entities relies                upon the accuracy of the carbon credit value, as                power generator fuel type and line losses are taken                into account.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for automatically measuring carbon offsets for the                devices consuming electrical power.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows block diagram of a database                stored in a computer-based automated carbon                reduction aggregation system.
   Carbon reduction aggregation system                (101)
   Processor (201)
   Memory (203)
   Consumer data (207)
   Control logic (251)

PT P
PN CN111691182-A
TI Surface treating plant fiber, by cleaning and drying plant fiber, soaking dried plant fiber in alkali liquid, washing, introducing nitrogen, sulfur dioxide and atomized water into mixed gas and irradiating carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN X
   ZHANG W
AE ZHANG W (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 202095343D
AB    NOVELTY - Process for surface treating plant fiber, involves (a) using clean water to clean and drying the plant fiber, (b) soaking the dried plant fiber in alkali liquid for 1-3 hours, (c) washing with water and drying, after taking the plant fiber out of the alkali liquid, (d) introducing nitrogen, sulfur dioxide and atomized water into mixed gas of nitrogen, sulfur dioxide and atomized water at normal pressure, and heating at 50-80 degrees C and preserving heat for 0.5-1 hour, mixing nano silicon dioxide and an antibacterial agent, adding the mixture to the surface of the plant fiber, keeping the temperature for 0.5-1 hour, and introducing nitrogen for replacement to the room temperature, and (e) irradiating the carbon dioxide to 30-60 kGy using cobalt-60 under a nitrogen atmosphere, to produce a surface-grafted plant fiber.
   USE - The process is used for surface treating plant fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The process effectively removes the non-reinforcing components hemicellulose and pectin in the fiber through the plant fiber after the alkali solution treatment so that the fiber swells and is more uniform, improves the tensile strength, increases the number of micropores in the fiber, reduces the water absorption of the fiber and enhances the hardness of the fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the alkali liquid is 15-50%. The content of sulfur trioxide in the mixed gas is 0.1-1 g/l. The volume ratio of the nitrogen, the sulfur dioxide and the atomized water is 5:3:1. The mass ratio of the nano silicon dioxide to the antibacterial agent is 100:1-10:1. Preferred Components: The alkali liquid is a sodium hydroxide solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antibacterial agents are quaternary ammonium salt active antibacterial monomers and oxazolidinone active antibacterial monomers. The quaternary ammonium salt active antibacterial monomer is one or two or more of a combination of acryloxyethyl-benzyl-dimethylammonium chloride, methacryloxyethyl-benzyl-dimethylammonium chloride, methacryloxyethyl-trimethylammonium chloride, methacryloxyethyl-benzyldiethylammonium chloride, acryloxyethyl-trimethylammonium chloride or diacryloxyethyl-benzyl-methyl ammonium chloride. The oxazolidinone active antibacterial monomer is two or more or a combination of 4-methacryloxymethyl-4-ethyl-1,3-oxazolidin-2-one, 4-acryloxymethyl-4-one ethyl-1,3-oxazolidin-2-one or 1-propenyl-hydantoin. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the quaternary ammonium salt active antibacterial monomer and the oxazolidinone active antibacterial monomer is 2:1.

PN EP3705549-A1; JP2020143280-A; US2020283666-A1; DE102019105676-A1; KR2020108245-A; CN111662685-A; SG10202001995-A1; DE102019105676-B4
TI Refrigerant for cooling device, is mixture            containing carbon dioxide, difluoromethane and other            component chosen from trifluoriodomethane,            fluoromethane, ethane, 1,1-difluoroethene, ethene,            ethane, propane, propene and fluoroethane.
AU AYDIN M
   HAACK C
   MURAT A
   CHRISTIAN H
AE WEISS UMWELTTECHNIK GMBH (WEIS-Non-standard)
   VICE UMBERTOTECHNIC GMBH (VICE-Non-standard)
   WEISS UMWELTTECHNIK GMBH (WEIS-Non-standard)
   WEISS UMWELTTECHNIK GMBH (WEIS-Non-standard)
GA 202090162X
AB    NOVELTY - A refrigerant is provided in a cooling device                (10) with a cooling circuit (11) having at least                one heat exchanger (12) in which the refrigerant                undergoes a phase change. The refrigerant is a                refrigerant mixture containing 5-65 %mass carbon                dioxide, 68 %mass or less difluoromethane and 55                %mass or less other component (C) chosen from                trifluoriodomethane, fluoromethane, ethane,                1,1-difluoroethene, ethene, fluoroethene, ethane,                propane, propene and fluoroethane.
   USE - Refrigerant for cooling device (claimed)                installed in buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigerant is environmentally-friendly                and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) test chamber for conditioning air, which                comprises a temperature-insulated test room for                receiving test material, which can be closed off                from the environment, and a temperature control                device for controlling the temperature of the test                room. The temperature control device is used to set                a temperature of -70 degrees C to 180 degrees C,                and has the cooling device with cooling circuit                having the refrigerant, heat exchanger, compressor                (13), a condenser (14) and an expansion element                (15); and
   (2) use of refrigerant for conditioning air in                test chamber, which can be closed and is                temperature-insulated from the environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                cooling device.
   Cooling device (10)
   Cooling circuit (11)
   Heat exchanger (12)
   Compressor (13)
   Condenser (14)
   Expansion element (15)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The refrigerant                  comprises 20-50 %mass, preferably 30-40 %mass                  carbon dioxide, 30 %mass or less, preferably 10                  %mass or less component (C). The refrigerant                  contains 40 %mass or less, preferably 3-13 %mass                  or less pentafluoroethane, 62 %mass or less,                  preferably 27-37 %mass difluoromethane and 55                  %mass or less, preferably 20-30 %mass                  trifluoromethane. Alternately, the refrigerant                  contains 8-68 %mass, preferably 33-43 %mass                  difluoromethane and 55 %mass or less, preferably                  20-30 %mass trifluoriodomethane, or mixture of 60                  %mass or less, preferably 27.5-37.5 %mass                  difluoromethane and 60 %mass or less, preferably                  27.5-37.5 %mass pentafluoroethane. Preferred                  Properties: The refrigerant has temperature glide                  of 5.6-22.1 K, and relative carbon dioxide                  equivalent of less than 2337. The refrigerant is                  not flammable.

PN CN111638288-A
TI Detecting hydrogen and oxygen in chlorine gas comprises carrying out agilent gas chromatograph with calibration gas of hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide, flowing carrier gas, argon and carrier gas, closing carrier gas valve.
AU ZHANG G
   JIA Q
   KONG T
   SHAN W
   XU F
   LU H
AE SHANDONG XINFA CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202088062S
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting hydrogen and oxygen in chlorine gas comprises carrying out agilent 7820 A gas chromatograph with calibration gas of 0.0478% hydrogen, 0.762% oxygen, 0.0882% nitrogen and remaining amount of carbon dioxide, flowing carrier gas, argon and carrier gas, making up gas flow rate of nitrogen 5 ml/minutes, where injection port temperature is 150 degrees C, chromatographic column I is activated carbon packed column, chromatographic column II is 5 A molecular sieve packed column, oven temperature of 80 degrees C, endo current of 100 mA and thermal conductivity detector at 200 degrees C, injecting sample via 10-port valve comprising (a) opening main argon valve and adjusting partial pressure valve, (b) turning on gas chromatograph, setting carrier gas flow rate, (c) preparing for sample injection analysis after baseline is stable, passing standard gas, detecting, (d) passing gas, detecting and (e) allowing carrier gas flow to cool instrument and closing carrier gas valve and completing test.
   USE - The method is useful for Detecting hydrogen and oxygen in chlorine gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method detects of content of trace gas contained in chlorine; avoids using dangerous detection method of explosion method; has good reproducibility and stable and reliable test results, fast detection speed, simple operation and has convenient and fast analysis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carrier gas flow rate is measured by Simple Object Access Protocol film flow meter. In the step (c), the detection time is set to 6 minutes. In the step (d), the detection time is set to 6 minutes. Preferred Components: The injection volume of the standard gas is 1 ml. In the step (d) the injection volume of the gas to be measured is 1 ml.

PT P
PN CN111575727-A; CN111575727-B
TI Preparing multilayer nanoporous silver-based film            gas diffusion electrode in carbon dioxide reduction            reaction and oxygen evolution reaction, involves            cutting flexible film, washing with acetone, ethanol            and deionized water, and drying.
AU LIN B
   XIAO Y
   QIAN Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAITECH (USKJ-C)
GA 202084751G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multilayer nanoporous metal-based                flexible film gas diffusion electrode involves                cutting flexible film, washing with acetone,                ethanol and deionized water, and drying. The                physical vacuum method is used to deposit metal                film on one side of flexible film. The lattice                expansion and contraction strategies are used to                build layered nanoporous structure. The metal X                film is anodized in solution, and transformed into                metal salt or metal oxide micro-domains, which                causes volume of metal lattice to expand. The                subsequent cathode reduction removes negative ions                or oxygen atoms from metal salts or metal oxides,                reduced to elemental metal to shrink lattice in                situ, and resulted in formation of domain boundary                channels/gaps at domain boundaries. The small holes                in domain are caused by shrinkage of crystal                lattice and loss of negative ions or oxygen atoms,                i.e. multi-level nanoporous metal-based flexible                film gas diffusion electrode.
   USE - Method for preparing multilayer nanoporous                silver-based film gas diffusion electrode used in                carbon dioxide reduction reaction (CO2RR), oxygen                evolution reaction (OER), and reducing CO2 to                carbon monooxide, alcohols, acids, alkanes or                alkenes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare multilayer                nanoporous metal-based flexible film gas diffusion                electrode that is cheap, simple, efficient and                favorable for industrial application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal comprises                  silver, gold, copper, tin, bismuth and nickel.                  The flexible film is porous polypropylene film.                  The physical vacuum method comprises electron                  beam evaporation method, thermal evaporation                  method and magnetron sputtering method. Preferred                  Process: The physical vacuum method is used to                  deposit silver (Ag) film on one side of                  polypropylene film. The Ag film is anodized in                  aqueous hydrochloric acid solution, and Ag                  nanoparticles are transformed into silver                  chloride (AgCl) micro-domains. The subsequent                  cathode reduction removes chlorine atoms from                  AgCl lattice, and resulted in formation of domain                  boundary channels/gaps at domain boundaries, as                  well as intradomain pores due to lattice                  contraction and loss of chloride ions. The silver                  film is formed by densely packing irregular                  silver nanoparticles. Preferred Conditions: The                  pore size of nanoporous polypropylene membrane is                  15-800 nanometer. The thickness of silver film is                  600-1800 nanometer. The particle diameter of                  silver nanoparticles is 10-1000 nanometer. The                  size of AgCl micro-domain is 0.1-2.5 micrometer.                  The size of domain boundary channel/gap is                  10-1200 nanometer. The average size of pores in                  domain is 10-200 nanometer.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).); A85 (Electrical applications.); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PN CN111530496-A
TI Preparing aromatic hydrocarbons used for producing            para-xylene, by converting product to gaseous            hydrocarbon and liquid aromatic hydrocarbon, doping            carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide and staggering            nano-metal cluster and molecular sieve.
AU FAN S
   WANG D
   LV J
   ZHAO T
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2020807878
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing aromatic hydrocarbon,                involves (a) using hydrocarbon compound, methanol                or both as raw material and converting the product                to two extreme transitions of a 1-3C gaseous                hydrocarbon and a liquid aromatic hydrocarbon under                a fixed bed reaction condition through a catalyst                effect, (b) doping carbon dioxide and carbon                monoxide in the raw material atmosphere, where the                catalyst is a molecular sieve modified by a metal                additive and a non-metal assistant, and (c)                staggering a nan-metal cluster, a non-metal same                metal and a molecular sieve between the metal and                the molecular sieve, and the aromatic hydrocarbon                prepared by the method does not need to be                separated.
   USE - The method is used for preparing aromatic                hydrocarbons, which is used for producing                para-xylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective and achieves                high-yield aromatics of more than 90 wt.%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                hydrocarbon compound, where the hydrocarbon                compound refers to a 1-9C hydrocarbon, and adopts a                pure chemical raw material, a petrochemical                industry and a hydrocarbon raw material produced in                the coal chemical industry process, and comprises                various hydrocarbon raw materials such as alkanes                and olefins, and is a mixture of pure raw materials                or hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction conditions                  comprise the mass space velocity of the raw                  materials is 0.1-10 hour-1, the reaction pressure                  is 0.1-5 MPa, and the reaction temperature is                  350-700 degrees C. Preferred Components: The                  catalyst is of formula: AxByZz. The molecular                  sieve has an MFI structure. The molecular sieve                  of silicon dioxide and aluminum oxide is 20-80,                  the mass content of each modifier is 1-10 wt.%                  based on oxide, and the molar ratio A/B = 0.01-2.                  The front end of the fixed bed raw material feed                  is provided with a serrated baffle.A = non-metal, preferably B, P, Si and other                  elements;B = metal, preferably Zn, Cr, Ga, Zr, Ni,                  La, Mg and other elements;andZ = molecular sieve, where x, y, and z are                  mass percentages respectively.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN111484473-A
TI Extracting apigenin involves grinding celery root into powder, passing through sieve, and placing in extraction kettle, using carbon dioxide supercritical fluid as extractant and ethanol as entrainer.
AU WANG X
   CHEN F
   REN Y
   WANG G
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 202075966J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting apigenin involves grinding celery root into powder, passing through a 40-mesh sieve, and placing in an extraction kettle, using carbon dioxide supercritical fluid as the extractant and ethanol as the entrainer. The apigenin in the celery root powder is extracted under the conditions of 30 Megapascals (MPa) and 40 degrees C in the extraction kettle for 2 hpurs. The extract is collected, rotary evaporated until no alcohol taste to obtain a concentrated solution, freeze-dried concentrated solution to obtain a pale yellow powder of apigenin.
   USE - Method for extracting apigenin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract apigenin, which has high yield, less pollution, and not easy to damage the structure of the extract and has potential market value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a apigenin buccal tablets made of apigenin extracted by an apigenin extraction, which comprises 20-40 pts. wt. apigenin, 15-44 pts. wt. additives, 25-34 pts. wt. sweetener, 5-10 pts. wt. citric acid, 1 pts. wt. magnesium stearate; and
   (2) a method for preparing apigenin buccal tablets, which involves adding apigenin, adjuvants, sweeteners, citric acid, drying in 60 degrees C oven, passing through 40 mesh sieve, adding magnesium stearate, mix well, and pressing to obtain apigenin buccal tablets.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of the carbon dioxide (CO2) supercritical fluid is 50 g/minute, and the flow rate of ethanol is 3.2 ml/minute, mass concentration of ethanol is 95-99.5%, and mass ratio of celery root powder to ethanol is 1:3. The auxiliary agent is dextrin, microcrystalline cellulose or starch, and the sweetener is sorbitol or sucralose. Preferred Composition: The buccal tablets comprises 20 pts. wt. apigenin, 44 pts. wt. dextrin, 30 pts. wt. sorbitol, 5 pts. wt. citric acid, and 1 pts. wt. magnesium stearate.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. TI Method of making recycled aggregate green concrete, involves soaking reclaimed coarse aggregate with lime water, taking out and spreading out, and then contacting reacted coarse aggregate with carbon dioxide gas.

GA 2020477338

The concrete test block is made of pure ordinary coarse aggregate, and the compressive strength of the concrete test block is detected. The reclaimed coarse aggregate is soaked with lime water for 10 to 15 minutes, taken out and spread it out, and then contact the reacted coarse aggregate with carbon dioxide gas to obtain calcified reclaimed coarse aggregate. PN WO2020035564-A1
TI Reducing carbon monoxide content in sulfur-containing feeds for steam reformers in hydrogen production plant involves e.g. treating process gas in sour shift step, removing e.g. carbon dioxide and passing gas from amine wash to hydrogenator.
AU TRANE-RESTRUP R
   VANNBY R
   CHRISTENSEN S S
   WIX C
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202015065B
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of the content of carbon monoxide in sulfur-containing feeds for steam reformers in hydrogen production plant comprises: treating process gas in sour shift step; removing carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide from shifted gas by an amine wash; passing the gas from the amine wash to hydrogenator fed with natural gas and with hydrogen; adsorbing the hydrogen sulfide, in subsequent absorber; pre-reforming the treated gas; splitting the pre-reformed gas into two sub streams, one of which being passed to heat exchange reformer and the other being subjected to steam reforming in fired steam reformer and further steam reforming in heat exchange reformer together with pre-reformed sub-stream; shifting the effluent from the heat exchange reformer in water gas shift unit; separating the shifted gas in membrane unit to obtain hydrogen product; and combining the hydrogen product with hydrogen from methanation of the hydrogen-containing purge gas from the hydrotreater purge gas loop.
   USE - The process is useful for reduction of the content of carbon monoxide in sulfur-containing feeds for steam reformers in hydrogen production plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides an efficient and cost-effective means of elimination of carbon monoxide from the gas stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrogen is separated from a hydrotreater purge gas loop. The process gas is a permeate gas from a biomass gasifier. The membrane unit is a pressure swing adsorption unit.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110745839-A; CN110745839-B
TI Performing activation process of non-defective            DD3R molecular sieve membrane for carbon            dioxide/methane separation, involves preparing a DD3R            molecular sieve membrane by hydrothermal synthesis            method.
AU GU X
   DU P
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202011711L
AB    NOVELTY - Performing activation process of a                non-defective DD3R molecular sieve membrane                involves preparing a DD3R molecular sieve membrane                by a hydrothermal synthesis method, and then                removing temperature to remove the moisture                absorbed in the pores. The DD3R molecular sieve                membrane is quickly heated to the first temperature                for high temperature treatment. The molecular sieve                membrane is rapidy cooled after the high                temperature treatment to the second temperature.                The molecular sieve membrane is calcined and                activated at a third temperature.
   USE - Method for performing activation process of a                non-defective DD3R molecular sieve membrane used                for carbon dioxide/methane (CO2/CH4) separation                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform activation                process of a non-defective DD3R molecular sieve                membrane, that can effectively avoid the formation                of intergranular defects in the film and ensure the                denseness of the film layer, and compared with the                low-temperature ozone-activated detemplate method,                the preparation efficiency is significantly                improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for using rapid heating equipment in the                preparation of DD3R molecular sieve membrane, which                is used to shrink the particle size of the                molecular sieve, remove the amantadine template                agent, avoid the defects of the molecular sieve                membrane, or improve the separation coefficient of                the molecular sieve membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The thickness of the                  molecular sieve membrane after hydrothermal                  synthesis is 0.3-30 micrometer, and the molecular                  sieve membrane is supported on a carrier. The                  high-temperature treatment time is 0.5-30                  minutes. The relative humidity of the environment                  in which the membrane is located is less than                  20%. The temperature of the activation                  calcination is 450-600 degrees C, and the                  treatment time is 10-50 hours. The temperature of                  the carbon dioxide/methane (CO2/CH4) separation                  is 10-80 degrees C, the pressure difference                  between the two sides of the membrane is 0.05-2.0                  megapascal, and the volume ratio of CO2/CH4 is                  1:9-9:1. Preferred Components: The amantadine is                  used as a template agent in the hydrothermal                  synthesis process. The carrier material is                  ceramic such as porous alumina, mullite,                  cordierite, or stainless steel, and the carrier                  form is sheet, tube, or hollow fiber. The                  atmosphere of the molecular sieve membrane is an                  air, nitrogen, argon, or oxygen atmosphere. The                  rapid cooling method is air cooling, air cooling                  or water cooling.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN EP3578247-A1; DE102018113502-A1; CN110560058-A
TI Honeycomb catalyst for oxidation of carbon monoxide and organic air pollutants in exhaust gas stream, is formed from material containing oxides of tungsten and titanium, which are doped with noble metal, and has preset honeycomb density.
AU KLOSE F
   BUETTNER O
AE CLARIANT INT LTD (CLRN-C)
   CLARIANT INT LTD (CLRN-C)
GA 2019A26724
AB    NOVELTY - A honeycomb catalyst is formed from a material containing oxides of tungsten and titanium, which are doped with a noble metal, and has honeycomb density of less than 20 cpsi.
   USE - Honeycomb catalyst for oxidation of carbon monoxide and organic air pollutants including soot particles, olefins, oxygenates, aldehydes and/or alcohols in exhaust gas stream (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The honeycomb catalyst has excellent catalytic activity, and effectively enables oxidation of organic air pollutants under high dust load. The deactivation of the catalyst at high temperature can be prevented. The adhesion of particles contained in exhaust gas stream to the catalyst can be prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for oxidation of carbon monoxide in an exhaust gas stream to carbon dioxide, which involves contacting the honeycomb catalyst with the exhaust gas stream and air at 350 degrees C or more;
   (2) method for oxidation of soot particles, olefins, oxygenates, aldehydes and/or alcohols in an exhaust gas stream, which involves contacting the honeycomb catalyst with the exhaust gas stream and air at 350 degrees C or more; and
   (3) use of honeycomb catalyst for oxidation of carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst has a base material formed from the oxides of tungsten and titanium, and is doped with platinum or palladium. The catalyst further contains silicon dioxide, glass fibers, aluminum oxide or calcium oxide for stabilization. The dust particles are a mixture of soot particles and particles of basic cement dust. Preferred Properties: The base material is a porous material having BET specific surface area of more than 30 m2/g. The exhaust gas flow rate is more than 1 g/m3, preferably 40 g/m3. The oxidation is performed at 350-450 degrees C for 1000 hours or more, preferably 2000 hours or more. Preferred Composition: The ratio of titanium and tungsten is more than 8. The doping amount of noble metal is 5 g/l or less, preferably 0.7-0.9 g/l. Dust particles in amount of more than 1 g/m2, preferably 40 g/m3 are present during oxidation.

PT P
PN CN110317121-A
TI Composite catalyst useful for preparing ethylene comprises methanol synthesis catalyst, graphite and modified hydrogen mordenite molecular sieve obtained by treating with acid solution and pre-adsorbing with organic alkali.
AU LIU S
   ZHU W
   LIU Z
   NI Y
   LIU H
   LIU Y
   MA X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201987334F
AB    NOVELTY - Composite catalyst comprises a methanol synthesis catalyst, a modified hydrogen mordenite (H-MOR) molecular sieve and graphite, where the modified H-MOR molecular sieve is obtained by treating the H-MOR molecular sieve with an acid solution and then subjecting to pre-adsorption organic alkali treatment.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst is used in synthesizing LPG synthesis gas by one-step preparation of ethylene, which breaks the hydrocarbon Anderson-Schulz-Flory (ASF) distribution rule in Fischer-Tropsch (F-T) synthesis with ethylene selectivity reaching 86%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the composite catalyst comprising (a) obtaining the methanol synthesis catalyst, (b) obtaining the modified H-MOR molecular sieve and (c) adding graphite to a mixture containing the methanol synthesis catalyst and the modified H-MOR molecular sieve and molding to obtain the composite catalyst; and
   (2) preparing ethylene comprising passing a raw material gas containing synthesis gas through a reactor equipped with the composite catalyst to obtain ethylene, where the raw material gas is carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide and the carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide satisfying a molar ratio of 1:0.2-4:0-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pre-adsorption with organic alkali comprises at least contacting the acid solution-treated H-MOR molecular sieve with a gas containing an organic alkali for pre-adsorption organic alkali treatment, preferably the H-MOR molecular sieve has a silicon to aluminum atomic ratio of 5-60 and activating the acid solution-treated H-MOR molecular sieve in an inactive atmosphere before being contacted with the gas containing the organic alkali, where the activation temperature is 300-500 degrees C for 3-5 hours and the step of pre-adsorbing with organic alkali comprises at least activating the acid solution treated H-MOR molecular sieve in inactive atmosphere, adjusting the temperature to the pre-adsorption organic alkali treatment temperature, contacting the organic alkali-containing gas to carry out pre-adsorption organic alkali treatment and alternately carrying out the mixture to obtain a modified H-MOR molecular sieve. The pre-adsorption organic alkali treatment is carried out at 150-350 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours. The treatment condition of the acid solution comprises treating at 30-100 degrees C, preferably 40-90 degrees C for 1-10, preferably 2-5 hours, drying, and roasting at 500-600 degrees C for 1-10 hours. The modified H-MOR molecular sieve is obtained by (a) treating the H-MOR molecular sieve with acid solution at 30-100 degrees C for 1-10 hours, drying and calcining at 550 degrees C for 1-10 hours in gas atmosphere and (b) subjecting the obtained sample to pre-adsorption organic alkali treatment to obtain the modified H-MOR molecular sieve. The methanol synthesis catalyst is obtained by a coprecipitation method or an impregnating method. The coprecipitation method comprises mixing a salt solution containing zinc, metal element and aluminum element with a solution containing a precipitant in a cocurrent manner under stirring, controlling the pH of the system to 7-9, finishing the precipitation, aging for 2-4 hours, performing solid-liquid separation, washing, drying and roasting the solid phase at 400-600 degrees C for 1-6 hours to obtain the methanol synthesis catalyst or the impregnating method comprises mixing the alumina powder in a salt solution containing zinc element and metal element or immersing an oxide of the alumina and the metal element in a salt solution containing the zinc element for 1-6 hours, removing the solvent, drying at 60-200 degrees C for 1-10 hours and calcining at 400-600 degrees C for 1-6 hours to obtain the methanol synthesis catalyst, where metal element is zirconium, chromium, and/or cerium, preferably, the zinc element, the metal element and the aluminum in the solution are independently derived from a nitrate, a hydrochloride, an acetate, an acetylacetonate or a sulfate of zinc element, the metal element and the aluminum. The step (c) specifically comprises thoroughly mixing the methanol synthesis catalyst and the modified H-MOR molecular sieve by ball milling, adding graphite to the mixture and forming into the composite catalyst. The ethylene preparation comprises the reaction temperature of 250-380 degrees C, the pressure of 1.0-8.0MPa and the raw material gas mass space velocity is 300-10000 ml.g-1.h-1. Preferred Components: The mass space velocity of the organic alkali-containing gas is 300-6000 ml.g-1.h-1. The organic alkali-containing gas comprises a carrier gas and an organic alkali. The carrier gas is nitrogen, helium, carbondioxide, argon and/or hydrogen. The organic alkali is trimethylamine, diethylamine, triethylamine, pyridine, pyridazine, pyrimidine and/or pyrazine. The volume fraction of the organic alkali in the organic alkali-containing gas is 0.1-10%. The concentration of the acid solution is 0.05-1.0 mol/l. The acid solution is hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, sulfuric acid, acetic acid, oxalic acid and/or citric acid. The mass ratio of the methanol synthesis catalyst, the modified H-MOR molecular sieve and the graphite in the composite catalyst is 10-90:7-90:1-5, preferably 65.2:32.6:2.2. The methanol synthesis catalyst is zinc oxide-aluminum dioxide complex ((ZnO)(a)Mb(Al2O3)1-a-b). The raw material gas further comprises an inactive gas. The inactive gas is nitrogen, argon, helium or methane. The volume content of the inactive gas in the mixed gas is greater than or equal to 10%. The reactor is a fixed bed reactor, a fluidized bed reactor or a moving bed reactor.M = Zr, Cr and/or Ce;a = 0.1-0.9; andb = 0.02-0.8.

The processor transmits environmental information detected by second sensor to external system through communication unit. The communication unit communicates with external system through wireless fidelity (WiFi), long range (LoRa) and narrow band-Internet of Things (NB-IoT) communication methods.

PN CN109718787-A; CN109718787-B
TI Preparation of cerium/yttrium stabilized zirconia carrier used in catalyst preparation, involves adding organic ammonium salt and organic zirconium precursor to glacial acetic acid and formic acid, adding soluble salt of cerium/yttrium.
AU GUO C
   SHEN Z
   LING F
   WANG S
   YANG W
   JI H
   WANG L
   ZHANG H
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 201945688X
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of cerium/yttrium stabilized zirconia carrier involves adding organic ammonium salt and organic zirconium precursor to a mixed solution of glacial acetic acid and formic acid, adding aqueous solution of soluble salt of cerium and/or yttrium, transferring above mixed solution to closed reaction kettle, carrying out constant temperature reaction at 160-220 degrees C for 12-48 hours, cooling, separating, washing and drying, and calcining the solid product.
   USE - Preparation of cerium/yttrium stabilized zirconia carrier used in catalyst preparation for catalyzing reaction of methane and carbon dioxide reforming to synthesis gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carrier and the catalyst stabilizes the zirconia by cerium/yttrium to prevent grain growth. The constituent unit is 5-10 nm zirconia nanocrystals, and has mesopores, which improves the specific surface area and has high carrier stability. The nickel and cobalt are prepared as catalysts to catalyze the reaction of methane and carbon dioxide to synthesis gas, with high conversion rate, less carbon deposition and stable performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of catalyst, which involves loading active metal ions of nickel and cobalt by impregnation method with cerium/yttrium-stabilized zirconia support, followed by drying and calcination.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble salts of cerium and/or yttrium are cerium nitrate and cerium nitrate, and the solution concentration is 0.01-2 mol/L, based on mole of nitrate. The nickel salt used for loading is nickel nitrate or nickel chloride, and the cobalt salt used is cobalt nitrate or cobalt chloride. Preferred Composition: The amount of nitrate solution added to the reaction system is calculated by converting the amount of the cerium oxide and the yttrium oxide into 0.1-10% of total amount of oxide in carrier. The nickel salt and the cobalt salt are added in an amount of 1-20% based on total loading of nickel and cobalt, and both nickel and cobalt is in any ratio.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic ammonium salt is oleylamine. The organic zirconium precursor is zirconium isopropoxide, zirconium n-butoxide and/or zirconium tert-butoxide. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of glacial acetic acid and formic acid is (0.1-10):1. The zirconium precursor is added in an amount of (4-20):1 in terms of molar ratio of acetic acid and formic acid to zirconium. The organic ammonium salt is added in a ratio of (2-10):1 in terms of a molar ratio of the organic ammonium salt to zirconium.

PN CN109401277-A
TI Preparing expanded thermoplastic polyurethane composite material involves preparing composite foamed particles, which involves mixing the the thermoplastic polyurethane particles and the antibacterial agent.
AU QIN L
   SHI Z
   LIANG Y
   MA W
   ZHANG C
AE NINGBO GMF NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201923126V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing expanded thermoplastic polyurethane (E-TPU) composite material involves preparing composite foamed particles, which involves mixing the the thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) particles and the antibacterial agent and granulating to obtain a composite particle to be foamed, then placing the composite particles to be foamed in a high pressure reaction vessel, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into the high pressure reactor to adjusting the pressure and temperature of the high pressure reactor. The carbon dioxide is in a supercritical state, the pressure is maintained, the pressure is released, the foam is heated, and the composite foamed particles are obtained.
   USE - Method for preparing expanded thermoplastic polyurethane (E-TPU) composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare expanded thermoplastic polyurethane (E-TPU) composite material can effectively inhibit the growth of bacteria (the antibacterial rate against bacteria and fungi is more than 90%), and the silver ion component in the antibacterial agent can repeatedly act, and its waterproof and high temperature resistance also guarantees that it will not be invalidated by the customer's cleaning, and giving customers a better experience.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing expanded thermoplastic polyurethane (E-TPU) composite material with antibacterial function, which involves preparing composite foamed particles and subjecting them to steam compression molding to obtain an E-TPU composite material having an antibacterial function.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The antibacterial agent is a nano silver antibacterial powder. Preferred Conditions- The ratio of the TPU particles to the antibacterial agent is from 100:5-100:1 by weight. The mixed synthetic granulation adopts a twin-screw underwater granulating unit and the temperature of the twin-screw extruder in the twin-screw underwater granulating unit is 140-250 degrees C. The pressure is 7.38-30 megapascal, the temperature is 31-80 degrees C, and the permeation time is 1.5-6 in particle foaming. The foaming temperature is from 90-160 degrees C, and the foaming time is from 15-90 seconds. The foaming ratio of the composite expanded beads is 4-8 times. The pressure at the time of steam molding is 0.1-0.5 megapascal, and the time is 2-6 minutes.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).)

PT P
PN CN109174918-A; CN109174918-B
TI Microwave activation of blast furnace slag to            obtain filtrate useful in mineralization of carbon            dioxide, by mixing blast furnace slag, ammonium salt            and catalyst, followed by heating and heat preserving,            adding water and filtering.
AU WANG Y
   HAN Z
   CHEN H
   YUAN X
   YANG L
   ZHONG Y
   XU D
   WANG X
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201909476D
AB    NOVELTY - Microwave activation of blast furnace slag                involves: (a) mixing the blast furnace slag, an                ammonium salt and a catalyst to obtain a mixture,                where the catalyst is one of barium titanate and                lead titanate, and the ammonium salt is one of                ammonium sulfate or ammonium bisulfate; (b) heating                the mixture in a microwave at 250-500 degrees C,                preferably 250-350 degrees C and heat preserving                for 5-10 minutes; and (c) adding water to the                material after heat preserving in the step (2),                dissolving and filtering to obtain a filtrate (I)                and a filter residue (I), adding water to the                filter residue (I), dissolving and filtering to                obtain a filtrate (II) and a filter residue                (II).
   USE - The method is useful for the microwave                activation of blast furnace slag to obtain a                filtrate, which is useful in the mineralization of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts microwave technology to                cooperate with specific catalyst; realizes the                microwave strengthening and catalysis in the                activation process of the blast furnace slag; and                activates calcium and magnesium ions in the blast                furnace slag in a short processing time, thus                greatly reducing the purification energy                consumption and improving the efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the                  mixture is placed in a crucible or a porcelain                  boat, and the material of the crucible is                  preferably graphite, corundum or quartz. The step                  (b) further involves cooling method, after the                  heat preservation reaction, which involves closed                  cooling or quenching cooling. The dissolving step                  in the step (c) involves stirring at 50-80                  degrees C for 1 hour or more. The filter residue                  (I) or the filter residue (II) as catalyst in the                  step (c) is returned to the step (a) for                  recycling. Preferred Components: The mass ratio                  of the blast furnace slag to the ammonium sulfate                  salt in the step (a) is 1:(3-6), preferably                  1:(3-5). The ammonium salt is ammonium sulfate.                  The mass ratio of the catalyst to the blast                  furnace slag in the step (a) is 1:(4-10). The                  water in the step (c) is deionized water,                  purified water or distilled water. Preferred                  Properties: The blast furnace slag has a particle                  size of 150-500 mu m.
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC P43-E05; P43-U18
IP B09B-003/00; C01B-032/55
