PN CN108785140-A
TI Carbonate cleansing foam comprises rheological agent, bacteriostatic agent, amino acid surfactant, gas component, oil phase component, fragrance and aqueous phase component.
AU WANG Y
   WANG L
AE SHANGHAI CAL QIDONG DAILY CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018933614
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonate cleansing foam comprises 0.1-1.8% rheological agent, 0.1-4% bacteriostatic agent, 7.5-17% amino acid surfactant, 1.5-17% gas component, 0.5-14% oil phase component, 0.1-1% fragrance and rest is aqueous phase component, where the gas component iss liquefied petroleum gas and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as carbonate cleansing foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam is mild, non-irritating, more glossy texture and dense, long-lasting; and increases blood circulation of the face and promotes absorption of other active ingredients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbonate cleansing foam, comprising (i) taking all raw materials, (ii) taking one-half of the weighed glycerin, adding the rheological component into the glycerin, stirring and dispersing, (iii) heating the oil phase component to 75-80 degrees C, and completely dissolving, (iv) mixing water, dipropylene glycol with the remaining one-half of glycerin, adding the rheological agent for pre-mixing, heating to 75-80 degrees C, stirring uniformly, adding the oil phase components, homogenizing, stirring uniformly, cooling to 50 degrees C, adding an amino acid surfactant and continuously cooling to 40 degrees C, adding bacteriostatic agent moisturizer and a fragrance, filtering and discharging to obtain a stock solution, and (v) filling the liquefied petroleum gas and carbon dioxide, subjecting to a water bath, blowing, adding nozzle, and extruding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rheological agent is xanthan gum and hydroxyethyl cellulose and/or polyquaternium. The bacteriostatic agent is octanoyl hydroxamic acid, caprylyl glycol, ethylhexyl glycerol and/or 1,3-propanediol. The amino acid surfactant is coconut oleoyl glutamic acid sodium and potassium lauryl phosphate. The oil phase component is behenyl alcohol, myristic acid, macadamia seed oil, magnesium stearate, PEG-10 stearate and/or argan tree seed oil. The aqueous phase component is glycerol, dipropylene glycol and water. The liquefied petroleum gas is a combination of propane and isobutane. The raw material comprises 0.1% xanthan gum, 0.1-0.5%cellulose, 0.1-0.3% polyquaternium-70, 1-5% behenyl alcohol, 0.5-2.5% myristic acid, 0.1-1% macadamia seed oil, 0.5-2% stearic acid, 0.5-2.5% PEG-10 stearate, 0.1-1% argan tree seed oil, 0.1-1% octanoyl hydroxamic acid, 0.1-1% caprylyl glycol, 0.01-1% ethylhexyl glycerol, 0.1-1% 1,3-propanediol, 2-8% isobutene, 0.1-1% propane, 0.5-1.5% carbon dioxide, 7-15% cocoyl glutamic acid disodium, 0.5-2% potassium lauryl phosphate, 5-15% glycerin, 1-5% dipropylene glycol and the rest is water.
DC D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PN WO2018185235-A1; AU2018247919-A1; EP3606353-A1; BR112019018719-A2; US2021100260-A1; RU2019135307-A
TI Biodegradable film used for forming package and flexible container for cheese ripening or preservation, has preset oxygen transmission rate, moisture vapor transmission rate and carbon dioxide transmission rate.
AU STAFYLA M E
   SPIGAROLI R
   GARAVAGLIA M D
   PICARIELLO M M
   STAFYLA E
   GARAVAGLIA D
   PICARIELLO M
   SPIGAROLI M R
AE CRYOVAC INC (CRYV-C)
   STAFYLA M E (STAF-Individual)
   SPIGAROLI R (SPIG-Individual)
   GARAVAGLIA M D (GARA-Individual)
   PICARIELLO M M (PICA-Individual)
   CRYOVAC INC (CRYV-C)
   CRYOVAC LLC (CRYV-C)
   CRYOVAC LLC (CRYV-C)
GA 201878163J
AB    NOVELTY - A biodegradable film has oxygen transmission rate of 200-450 cc/m2.day.atm measured based on ASTM F1927 at 25 degrees C and relative humidity of 85-95%, moisture vapor transmission rate of 500-900 g/m2.day.atm measured based on ASTM F1249 at 38 degrees C and relative humidity of 0-98% and carbon dioxide transmission rate of 2000-6000 cc/m2.day.atm measured based on ASTM F2476 at 23 degrees C and relative humidity of 0-23%.
   USE - Biodegradable film is used for forming package and flexible container e.g. bag and pouch for ripening or preservation of cheese e.g. Percorino cheese, Taleggio cheese, Cascaval cheese, Manchego cheese, Raclette cheese, Rigatino cheese, Gouda cheese, Edam cheese, Tilsit cheese, Gruyere cheese, Cheddar cheese, Emmental cheese and Maasdam cheese (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biodegradable film has excellent flexibility and enables ripening and/or preservation of cheese with reduced loss of water and suppressed generation of mold and yeast growth, without color and flavor deviations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) flexible container/package, which is formed using the biodegradable film; and
   (2) method for cheese ripening and/or preservation, which involves introducing cheese into the flexible container, closing preferably under vacuum condition, and ripening and/or preserving the packaged cheese.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The biodegradable film is a biaxially oriented film or a thermal-shrinkable film having free shrink rate in longitudinal direction and/or transverse direction of 20% based on ASTM D2732 at 85 degrees C. The biodegradable film comprises 30%, 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, 80%, 90%, 95%, 99% or 100% biodegradable polymers. The biodegradable polymer is chosen from biodegradable polysaccharide and biodegradable copolyester, preferably chosen from starch, polyhydroxybutyrate, polycaprolactone, polylactate and copolyester of 1,4-butanediol, adipic acid and terephthalic acid. The biodegradable film comprises 3 layers, in which outer layers comprise the biodegradable copolyester and the core layer comprises the starch. The film comprises 85 wt.% or more starch. Preferred Properties: The biodegradable polymer has melting point of 110-120 degrees C, melt-flow rate of 2.7-4.9 g/10 minutes measured at 190 degrees C under load of 2.16 kg, Vicat softening point of 91 degrees C and density of 1.25-1.27 g/cc. The film has elastic modulus based on ASTM D882 of 2000-4000 kg/cm2, tensile strength modulus based on ASTM D882 of 400-700 kg/cm2, elongation at break based on ASTM D882 of 200-350%, preferably 200-300%, in both longitudinal direction and transverse direction, and gloss based on ASTM D2457 of 60 degrees C.
DC A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); ; Q32 (Container/closure types, special packaging features and transit packaging (B65D)); Q33 (Packaging container/closure material (B65D)); Q34 (Types of goods packaged/bottled/bound/labelled/unpacked (B65B, C, D))

TI Crosslinking rubber composition for forming seal,            comprises carboxyl group-containing nitrile rubber and            carboxyl group-containing acrylic rubber, in preset            weight ratio, and polyamine type crosslinking            agent.
AU MOSAKI S
AE ZEON CORP (JAPG-C)
   ZEON CORP (JAPG-C)
   ZEON CORP (JAPG-C)
   ZEON CORP (JAPG-C)
GA 2018776179
AB    NOVELTY - A crosslinking rubber composition comprises                carboxyl group-containing nitrile rubber (A),                carboxyl group-containing acrylic rubber (B) and                polyamine type crosslinking agent (C). The                components (A) and (B) are contained in the weight                ratio of5:95-95:5. The component (A) comprises 5-60                wt.% ( alpha ),( beta )-ethylenically unsaturated                monocarboxylic-acid ester monomer unit (a1) and                5-60 wt.% ( alpha ),( beta )-ethylenically                unsaturated nitrile monomer unit (a2), and has                iodine number of 120 or less.

PT P
PN US2018207546-A1; US11369895-B2
TI Nanoporous starch aerogel impregnated with a bioactive for use as bioactive carriers, fillers in food preparations, and solid supports for biocatalysts is a wheat starch aerogel impregnated with phytosterol as the bioactive.
AU CIFTCI O N
AE NUTECH VENTURES (NUTE-Non-standard)
GA 201857999G
AB    NOVELTY - A nanoporous starch aerogel impregnated with a bioactive is a wheat starch aerogel impregnated with phytosterol as the bioactive.
   USE - A nanoporous starch aerogel impregnated with a bioactive for use as bioactive carriers, fillers in food preparations, and solid supports for biocatalysts.
   ADVANTAGE - The production method is a simple and clean approach to decrease the size and crystallinity of the phytosterols to improve their bioaccessibility, and in turn bioavailability. This approach uses nanoporous food grade starch as a nanoporous and high surface area mold and supercritical carbon dioxide to impregnate the phytosterols into the nanopores and to form colloidal phytosterols with decreased crystallinity. The nanomanufacturing method produces dry phytosterol nanoparticle formulations without the use of any emulsifiers or toxic organic solvents. The dry formulation makes the handling, storage, and transportation more convenient. Impregnating crystalline phytosterol into nanoporous starch aerogels decreases the crystallinity of the phytosterol and changes the crystal morphology, improving the water solubility of the phytosterol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for formation of starch aerogel impregnated with a bioactive which involves forming nanoporous starch aerogel and cooling-controlled impregnating supercritical carbon dioxide-bioactive solvato-complex into the nanoporous starch aerogel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the main steps involved in the production of aerogels.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Phytosterol is selected from beta -sitosterol, campesterol, and/or stigmasterol. Starch hydrogel comprises approximately 10% starch. Starch comprises wheat starch. Preferred Processes: Formation of nanoporous starch aerogel includes forming starch hydrogel; exchanging water in the starch hydrogel with ethanol to form an alcogel; and drying the alcogel with supercritical carbon dioxide. Formation of starch hydrogel includes gelatinizing starch at 80-140 degrees C to form a three-dimensional starch hydrogel. Exchanging water in the starch hydrogel with ethanol to form an alcogel comprises immersing the starch hydrogel in an ethanol solution comprising 30-100 vol.% ethanol. Starch hydrogel is immersed in a 1st ethanol solution comprising approximately 30 vol.% ethanol for a 1st time period of approximately 1 hour; starch hydrogel is immersed in a 2nd ethanol solution comprising approximately 50 vol.% ethanol for a 2nd time period of approximately 1 hour; starch hydrogel is immersed in a 3rd ethanol solution comprising approximately 70 vol.% ethanol for a 3rd time period of approximately 1 hour; starch hydrogel is immersed in a 4th ethanol solution comprising 100 vol.% ethanol for a 4th time period of approximately 1 hour; and starch hydrogel is immersed in a 5th ethanol solution comprising 100 vol.% ethanol for a 5th time period of approximately 24 hours. Supercritical carbon dioxide drying of the alcogel to form the aerogel comprises supercritical carbon dioxide extraction. Impregnating of supercritical carbon dioxide-bioactive solvato-complex into the nanoporous starch aerogel comprises supercritical carbon dioxide dissolving the bioactive at approximately 70 degrees C and a pressure of approximately 45 MPa to form supercritical carbon dioxide-bioactive solvato-complex; and cooling supercritical carbon dioxide-bioactive solvato-complex to room temperature to precipitate the bioactive from the supercritical carbon dioxide into the nanoporous starch aerogel. The method further includes grinding the nanoporous starch aerogel into a nanoporous starch aerogel powder.

PN CN108097331-A; CN108097331-B
TI Catalyst useful for synthesizing propylene carbonate from carbon dioxide and propylene oxide, comprises activated carbon carrier, polymerization active center and zirconium oxychloride auxiliary agent.
AU FANG X
   ZHANG Z
   ZHANG X
   SUN X
   YANG C
   LU J
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC FUSHUN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEMICA (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC FUSHUN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM (SNPC-C)
GA 201846965E
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises activated carbon carrier, polymerization active center (I) and zirconium oxychloride auxiliary agent.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for synthesizing propylene carbonate from carbon dioxide and propylene oxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has long service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst comprises activated carbon carrier, polymerization active center i.e. C-(OC2H4)n(XkY(1-k)/3C2H4(C4H4N2)p)m of formula (I) and zirconium oxychloride auxiliary agent.
   X = bromide ion;
   Y = phosphate;
   k = 0.5-0.7;
   p = 0.6-0.9;
   n = 160-340;
   m = 160-340; and
   G = active center.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing catalyst comprising (i) performing steam treatment of activated carbon, (ii) impregnating treated activated carbon obtained in step (i) with zirconium oxychloride ethanol solution and drying to obtain catalyst precursor A, (iii) adding carbon tetrachloride solution of phosphorus tribromide into solution of polyvinyl alcohol in carbon tetrachloride, stirring at 40-60 degrees C for 2-8 hours and then concentrating to one-fifth to four-fifths of the original volume, (iv) impregnating catalyst precursor A with solution prepared in step (iii) and drying to obtain catalyst precursor B, and (v) impregnating catalyst precursor B with pyrazine toluene solution, refluxing at 40-60 degrees C, cooling, filtering, washing with toluene for many times and drying to obtain catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises 4-9 wt.% nitrogen, 4-10 wt.% oxygen, 9-18 wt.% halogen, 0.6-1.5 wt.% phosphorus, 4-7 wt.% zirconium, carbon and hydrogen (balance amount). (I) is 1,4-bis(6-methoxyoctan-3-yl)pyrazine-1,4-diium phosphoric acid bromide of formula (II). The particle size of activated carbon is 20-40 mesh, specific surface area is 1400-2300 m2/g, and oxygen content of activated carbon is 7-13%. The activated carbon is coconut shell type activated carbon. The concentration of zirconium oxychloride solution is 5-10 g/100 ml. The concentration of phosphorus trichloride solution in carbon tetrachloride solution is 3-27 g/100 ml; and concentration of polyvinyl alcohol solution in carbon tetrachloride is 7-12 g/100 ml. In step (iii), volume ratio between carbon tetrachloride solution of phosphorus tribromide and carbon tetrachloride solution of polyvinyl alcohol is 1:1 In step (v), concentration of pyrazine toluene solution is 30-60 g/100 ml. The solid-liquid volume ratio of the impregnation process is 1 g/5-10 ml. Preferred Method: In step (i), steam treatment temperature is 650-800 degrees C, steam treatment pressure is 0.4-0.8 MPa, and steam treatment time is 2-8 hours. In step (ii), impregnation is carried out by equal volume impregnation, supersaturation impregnation and spray impregnation. In step (i), drying temperature is 50-2000 degrees C, and drying time is 24-48 hours. In step (iv), impregnation is carried out in an equal volume. The drying temperature is 20-180 degrees C, and drying time is 24-48 hours. In step (v), reaction time is 12 hours. The toluene washing operation includes 2-4 operations, and then each washing operation includes washing and subsequent drying operations.

PT P
PN BR102020022432-A2
TI Formulation used for forming biodegradable compound for forming product, comprises base composition comprising vegetable starch from cassava or potato maize, glycerol, carbonate and polybutylene.
AU FAJARDO F H
AE MAXXIMA ASSESSORIA EM COMERCIO EXTERIOR (MAXX-Non-standard)
GA 202285089B
AB    NOVELTY - A biodegradable compound formulation comprises a base composition comprising vegetable starch from cassava or potato maize, glycerol, carbonate and polybutylene.
   USE - Formulation used for forming biodegradable compound for forming product (all claimed) used as raw material in industries producing disposable articles such as cutlery, lids, plates, trays and straws.
   ADVANTAGE - The formulation provides the biodegradable compound that biodegrades totally in the environment or in composters in average period of one year through anaerobic digestion or composting in period of six months, leaving as residue only water, carbon dioxide, minerals (carbonate) and starch (vegetable material). Since starch plasticizing process is carried out in the inner portion of the extruder in the process of obtaining biodegradable compound, under controlled temperature, it contributes to save time and cost with respect to the plastification separately.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of biodegradable compound, which involves altering the physical and chemical structure of the vegetable starch to couple the resin based on polybutylene succinate or polybutylene adipate-co-terephthalate; and
   (2) product, which comprises the granular biodegradable compound supplied to industries for the production of various disposables which can be completely biodegraded in the environment in an average period of one year through anaerobic digestion or composting in a period of six months, leaving residues such as water, carbon dioxide, minerals (carbonate) and starch (vegetable material).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The composition is formed by vegetable starch, glycerol, carbonate and polybutylene adipate-co-terephthalate resin. The formation comprises a first portion comprising 50% base resin, 40% vegetable starch, 4% glycerol and 6% carbonate and a second portion comprising 70% base resin, 22% vegetable starch, 2% glycerol and 6% carbonate. The manufacture of biodegradable compound involves pre-preparing a compound by mixing evenly the glycerol and the vegetable starch through a mixer, until there is no sign of segregation between both items, after mixing, adding calcium carbonate, cold mixing, charging polybutylene succinate or polybutylene adipate-co-terephthalate, when the compound is prepared, and extruding using a thermoplastic extruder with twin screw and a side feeder, in which the extruder heats the polybutylene succinate or polybutylene adipate-co-terephthalate resin, to make liquid, through friction and heat through the gun at 120 degrees C, in the course of the gun, mixing the liquid base resin by a lateral feeder in the form of a doser, mixing with prepared compound and mixing through the twin screw provided in the barrel, homogenizing at 120 degrees C sufficient to activate the chemical reaction of glycerol and starch, and plasticizing and in which after a few seconds of extrusion, the compound is homogeneous and the starch is plasticized, cooling through contact with water, hardening and granulating.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.)

PT P
PN CN113864162-A
TI Method for realizing energy-saving transformation of high pressure 4M40-186 type carbon dioxide compressor in chemical industry, involves multi-level cylinder body air valve whose air flue clearance is changed from specific values.
AU WU X
AE SHANGHAI LONGHONG COMPRESSOR MFG CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202214722U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves changing inner diameter of a first-level cylinder body from 1160 millimeter to 1162 to 1163 millimeters. A piston is changed from a split type to an integral type. Outer diameter of the first-level cylinder body is changed from 1151 millimeter into 1153 to 1155 millimeters. Outer diameter of a primary air valve is changed from 345 millimeter into 347 to 349 millimeters. Outer diameter of a secondary air valve is changed from 320 millimeter into 322 to 324 millimeters. Outer diameter of a first-stage valve sheet is changed from 315 millimeter to 319 to 321 millimeters. An air flue clearance of a 1-2 level cylinder body air valve is changed from 25 millimeter to 10 to 13 millimeters.
   USE - Method for realizing energy-saving transformation of a high pressure 4M40-186 type carbon dioxide compressor in a chemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables increasing beating amount greater than or equal to 30 % by a compressor, which breaks through original design requirement of the compressor, reducing operation power and energy consumption to increase compressor yield by an enterprise, and saving energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of compressor. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC Q55 (Positive displacement fluid machines/pumps/compressors (i.e. for driving fluid) (F04B,C))
MC Q55-G; Q55-A

PN CN113652706-A
TI Preparing composite electro-catalyst, comprises            e.g. adding polymer, zinc metal organic framework            material and metal salt into organic solvent, and            performing electrostatic spinning to mixed solution to            obtain precursor fiber composite.
AU HU Q
   YANG H
   WANG S
   HE C
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 2021D5910K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite electrocatalyst comprises                providing polymer, zinc metal organic framework                material and metal salt, where the metal salt in                the metal boiling point is higher than the boiling                point of the zinc, adding the polymer, the zinc                metal organosheet material and the metal salts to                the organic solvent, stirring to obtain a mixed                solution, performing electrostatic spinning on the                mixed solution to obtain precursor fiber composite,                pre-oxidizing the precursor fiber compound,                carbonizing and purifying, and loading metal single                atoms on the porous carbon nanofiber. The mass                ratio of said polymer, zinc metal organic framework                material and metal salt is                (120-180):(40-80):(1-3).
   USE - Preparation method of composite                electro-catalyst used for electrochemical reduction                of carbon dioxide (all claimed). Uses include but                are not limited to metal, metal oxide, metal                complex, transition metal phosphide or carbon-based                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare composite                electro-catalyst, which improves the utilization                efficiency of the metal single atom active site,                and realizes the high-efficiency carbon dioxide                electro catalytic reduction. The carbon nano-fiber                has rich nano-scale porous structure, and is good                for the transmission of the charge.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                composite electro-catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The polymer is                  selected from polyacrylonitrile and                  polyvinylpyrrolidone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc metal organic                  framework material is selected from ZIF-8,                  and/or, the metal salt is selected from palladium                  salt , copper salt, gold salt, and iron salt.                  Preferred Conditions: The conditions of the                  pre-oxidation treatment are as follows: In an air                  atmosphere, the air flow rate is 60-100                  mL/minute, the temperature is raised to                  100-400?oC. The temperature is kept for 1-2 hours                  at a temperature-rising rate of 1-5?oC/minute. In                  an inert gas atmosphere, the gas flow rate is                  60-100 mL/minute, the temperature is raised to                  800-1100?oC at a heating rate of 3-7?oC/minute,                  and the temperature is kept for 1-2 hours. The                  conditions of the pickling are: the concentration                  of the acid used in the pickling is 2-5 mol/L,                  and the time of the pickling is 8-14 hours. The                  drying conditions are: the temperature is                  80-120?oC, and the time is 15-30 hours.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)
MC A10-E05B; A12-W11K; A12-W14; A11-B15C; A10-E11; E31-N05C; E11-N; E11-Q02B; E05-U05B; J03-B; N07-G

TI System useful for producing hydrogen by            electrolyzing seawater in thermal power plant,            comprises electrolytic tank connected with thermal            power generating unit and seawater evaporating and            concentrating component to obtain hydrogen, chlorine            and sodium hydroxide solution.
AU HUANG Y
   PENG S
   ZHANG S
   YANG Y
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2021C43442
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a seawater purifying                component connected with a thermal power generating                unit for supplying flue gas generated by power                generation to the seawater purifying component, so                as to use carbon dioxide in flue gas to purify raw                material i.e. seawater. A seawater evaporating and                concentrating component is connected with the                seawater purifying component for receiving purified                seawater from the seawater purifying component and                concentrating purified seawater. An electrolytic                tank is connected with the thermal power generating                unit and the seawater evaporating and concentrating                component for receiving concentrated seawater from                the seawater evaporating and concentrating                component and electrolyzing concentrated seawater                to obtain hydrogen, chlorine and sodium hydroxide                solution, so as to use sodium hydroxide solution to                prepare caustic soda.
   USE - The system is useful for producing hydrogen by                electrolyzing seawater in thermal power plant                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Flue gas generated by the thermal power                generating unit is purified by desulfurization and                primary carbon reduction. The obtained carbon                dioxide in purified flue gas can react with calcium                and magnesium ions in seawater to precipitate, so                that calcium and magnesium ions in seawater are                removed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing hydrogen by electrolyzing seawater in                thermal power plant.

PN IN202021001421-A
TI Method for production of bio-CNG from biogas, involves pre-conditioning of feed biogas at near atmospheric pressure and temperature to remove moisture droplets and particulate matter from feed biogas, and removing water from sweet gas.
AU PATIL A G
   MENON S
AE PATIL A G (PATI-Individual)
   MENON S (MENO-Individual)
GA 202187100T
AB    NOVELTY - The method (100) involves pre-conditioning of a feed biogas at near atmospheric pressure and temperature to remove moisture droplets and particulate matter from the feed biogas. The pre-conditioned biogas is treated with a formulated amine at a predefined pressure and a predefined temperature to obtain sweet wet gas and to remove hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide. The sweet wet gas is polished at a predefined temperature and a predefined pressure for hydrogen sulfide removal. The water is removed from the polished sweet gas to obtain final bio-CNG.
   USE - Method for production of bio-compressed natural gas (CNG) from a biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides multi-stage filtration at low differential pressure (DP) for removal of liquid droplets and particulate matter. The process is suitable for low-pressure end-to-end treatment of the biogas. The process facilitates simultaneous removal of carbon dioxide and high quantity of sulfur compounds, such as H2S, carbonyl sulfide, carbon disulfide, and like using methyl diethanolamine (MDEA) and appropriate additives, such as monoethanolamine (MEA), piperazine and phosphoric acid in the absorber column.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method for production of bio-compressed natural gas from a biogas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions - The treatment of the biogas with the formulated amine is carried out at pressure from 1 bar to 4 bar and at temperature from 40 to 130 degrees C. The formulated amine includes any one of ethanolamine, methy diethanolamine, triethanolamine, and chemicals, such as phosphoric acid and piperazine in water. The polishing of the sweet wet gas is carried out at pressure from 1 bar to 1.5 bar and at the temperature from 40 to 50 degrees C. The sweet wet gas is passed through iron turnings for crude polishing and thereafter through activated carbon for fine polishing to reduce final hydrogen sulfide (H2S) concentration to less than 10 ppm. The water in the polished sweet gas is first reduced in a refrigeration unit and removed from the sweet gas using any one of molecular sieves and glycol dehydration. The glycol dehydration is carried out using any one of triethelyene glycol, diethylene glycol, ethylene glycol and tetraethylene glycol.

PN CN113041999-A
TI Preparation of atmospheric pressure carbon dioxide adsorbent from nut shell by taking nut shell in furnace, introducing argon gas in furnace to discharge air in furnace, carbonizing, and cooling to obtain initial carbon material.
AU LIN R
   CHENG Z
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202178819P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of atmospheric pressure carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorbent from nut shell comprises taking the nut shell in a furnace, introducing argon gas in the furnace to discharge the air in the furnace, carbonizing, and cooling to obtain initial carbon material; stirring the initial carbon material in water bath for 30-60 minutes, and filtering after stirring to obtain cleaned initial carbon material; adding the cleaned carbon material into a solution containing active agent, standing for 12-18 hours, after standing, taking argon gas as protective gas, activating, taking out after cooling, and using deionized water to wash to neutral and obtain activated carbon; and mixing the activated carbon obtained with modifier, putting in water bath, stirring and ultrasonically processing, putting in 120 degrees C oven to dry, and obtaining the modified activated carbon adsorbent.
   USE - The method is for preparation of atmospheric pressure CO2 adsorbent from nut shell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes recycling of nut shell waste and improves the value and adsorption effect of waste. The modified carbon has CO2 maximum adsorption amount after desorption at room temperature and atmospheric pressure of 7% according to thermogravimetry desorption adsorption performance test, low material cost, and highly-efficient and controllable preparation flow.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first step comprises introducing argon gas for 30-60 minutes at 0.3-0.6 L/minute, and carbonizing at 0.01-0.2 L/minute argon gas and 600-800 degrees C for 60-120 minutes. In the third step, the activation time is 30-120 minutes, and the activation temperature is 700-800 degrees C. The active agent is dipotassium carbonate (K2CO3), disodium carbonate (Na2CO3), potassium hydroxide (KOH) and/or sodium hydroxide (NaOH). The modifier is magnesium chloride (MgCl2), sodium chloride (NaCl), calcium chloride (CaCl2) or potassium chloride (KCl). The mass ratio of activated carbon to the modifier is 0.1-5:1. The stirring speed in the fourth step is 200-400 revolutions/minute, and the ultrasonic time is 30-60 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The nut shell is melon seed shell, peanut shell, walnut shell, or broad bean shell. In the third step, the mass ratio of cleaned carbon material and active agent is 0.1-5:1.

   KUANG H

   GUANGXI BEIBU GULF NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021455212
AB    NOVELTY - Smelting high manganese stainless steel comprises (i) adding molten iron in AOD furnace, and adding the first batch of lime, and use top lance and side lance to blow oxygen, desiliconizing, decarburizing, adding high-carbon ferro-chromium and high-chromium raw materials to molten iron to form high-chromium molten iron; continuing to use top guns and side guns to blow oxygen and decarburizing and changing the side lance to carbon-dioxide and oxygen mixed blowing; (ii) removing the slag, adding lime and high magnesia content slagging agent, adding high-carbon ferromanganese to form high-carbon chromium-manganese hot metal; (iii) stopping oxygen supply from the top gun, mixing and blowing; and (iv) strictly controlling the slag state; (v) utilizing high-silicon silicomanganese, silicomanganese, and ferrosilicon reducing agents to reduce the manganese oxide and chromium oxide in the slag; and (vi) adding lime and fluorite to adjust the slag and increasing the yield.

PN CN111841237-A; CN111841237-B
TI Method for inhibiting crystallization of straight-run gasoline flue gas comprises e.g. pre-absorbing active carbon dioxide nitrogen, absorbing active carbon marked by pollutant as active carbon II and performing sulfur resource recovery.
AU YANG B
   WEI J
   LI J
   LIANG M
AE ZHONGYE CHANGTIAN INT ENG CO LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 2020A94417
AB    NOVELTY - Method for inhibiting crystallization of straight-run gasoline (SRG) flue gas comprises (i) pre-absorbing the activated carbon dioxide nitrogen and obtaining activated carbon I; (ii) absorbing the activated carbon marked by the pollutant as active carbon II and mixing the active carbon II with the activated carbon I obtained in the step (i), where the pollutants are derived from flue gas, preferably from sintering flue gas, preferably, the flue gas is a mixed flue gas containing at least one of nitrogen oxides, sulfur dioxide, dust, volatile organic compounds, heavy metals, and halogen; (iii) introducing the mixture of activated carbon II and activated carbon I obtained in the step (ii) through a regeneration process to obtain SRG gas; and (iv) performing sulfur resource recovery on the SRG gas obtained in the step (iii), where the sulfur resource recovery for SRG gas is sulfuric acid production processing for SRG gas and the SRG gas does not contain or contains very little ammonia.
   USE - The method is useful for inhibiting crystallization of SRG flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents pipeline blockage and reduce the loss of sulfur resources.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the activated carbon flue gas purification process (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN111568809-A
TI Preparing aqueous safe plant antibacterial agent for isinfectants and hand sanitizers, involves using supercritical extraction method, using carbon dioxide fluid as the extractant, and pure water as the entrainer and extract essential oils.
AU ZHANG H
AE ZHANG H (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 202084904J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aqueous safe plant antibacterial agent involves using supercritical extraction method, using carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid as the extractant, and pure water as the entrainer, extract essential oils from linal leaves, citronella leaves, lavender leaves, eucalyptus leaves, lemongrass leaves, and jasmine leaves. The linalole leaf essential oil, citronella leaf essential oil, lavender leaf essential oil, eucalyptus leaf essential oil, lemongrass leaf essential oil and jasmine leaf essential oil extracted are fully mixed in certain ratio to obtain mixed essential oil. The surfactant, solubilizer and co-solvent are added into a stirring tank and stirred and mixed thoroughly. The mixed essential oil is added to a stirred tank containing the modifier in ratio of 1:2-1:10, and stirred to obtain aqueous essential oil liquid mother liquor. The mother liquor of aqueous essential oil liquid is passed into reactor, and fully reacted to prepare the final aqueous plant antibacterial agent.
   USE - Method for preparing aqueous safe plant antibacterial agent used in disinfectants, hand sanitizers, humidifiers, air purifiers or fragrances (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepared aqueous safe plant antibacterial agent, that has good antibacterial and antibacterial properties, and has the characteristics of low irritation, safe ingredients and mild odor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mixing ratio of linalole essential oil, citronella leaf essential oil, lavender leaf essential oil, eucalyptus leaf essential oil, lemongrass leaf essential oil, and jasmine leaf essential oil is 40:20:10:10:10:10. The supercritical extraction parameters of linalool leaves includes extraction temperature is 45 degrees C, extraction pressure is 20 megapascal, CO2 fluid flow rate is 14 L/minute, static extraction time is 70 minutes, and dynamic extraction time is 70 minutes. The supercritical extraction parameters of citronella leaves includes extraction temperature is 45 degrees C, extraction pressure is 15 megapascal, CO2 fluid flow rate is 35 L/minute, static extraction time is 45 minutes, and dynamic extraction time is 45 minutes. The supercritical extraction parameters of lavender leaves includes extraction temperature is 45 degrees C, extraction pressure is 21 megapascal, CO2 fluid flow rate is 20 L/minute, static extraction time is 50 minutes, dynamic extraction time is 70 minutes. The supercritical extraction parameters of eucalyptus leaves includes extraction temperature is 35 degrees C, extraction pressure is 30 megapascal, CO2 fluid flow rate is 25 L/minutes, static extraction time is 60 minutes, dynamic extraction time is 60 minutes. Preferred Components: The surfactant is Tween (RTM: non-ionic surfactant)-40 and Tween-80, the solubilizer is glycerin, hydrogenated castor oil-40, polyethylene glycol (PEG-200) and hydrogenated castor oil-60, and the cosolvent is triethanolamine.

PN CN110156038-A; CN110156038-B
TI Microporous-mesoporous-macroporous multistage pore            SBA-15 molecular sieve useful for converting catalytic            gas, hydroconversion of carbon dioxide or detoxifying            biomass hydrolyzate.
AU CHEN X
   GUO H
   ZHANG H
   LI Q
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 2019771688
AB    NOVELTY - Microporous-mesoporous-macroporous multistage                pore SBA-15 molecular sieve, is claimed. The total                specific surface area of the                microporous-mesopore-macroporous multistage pore                SBA-15 molecular sieve is 792.4-970.5 m2/g,                specific pore surface area is 92.8-203.6 m2/g, the                mesopore specific surface area is 472.8-571.3 m2/g,                the macropore specific surface area is 97.0-294.1                m2/g and the average pore diameter is 6.5-14.7                nm.
   USE - The sieve is useful for converting catalytic                gas, hydroconversion of carbon dioxide or                detoxifying biomass hydrolyzate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sieve retains micropore adsorption sites                of molecular sieve and improves the flow diffusion                property of the molecular sieve to the large-sized                guest molecules.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the                microporous-mesoporous-macroporous multistage pore                SBA-15 molecular sieve comprising (i) applying soft                template agent P123, deionized water, mixing                hydrochloric acid, stirring uniformly, adding hard                template, stirring, then adding ethyl orthosilicate                and continuing stirring to obtain mixed solution,                and (ii) transferring the mixed solution obtained                in the step (i) into a reaction vessel for                hydrothermal reaction to obtain molecular sieve                material, filtering the molecular sieve material,                washing to neutrality, drying, and calcining;                and
   (2) microporous-mesoporous-macroporous                multistage pore SBA-15 molecular sieve prepared by                the preparation method as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the                  hard templating agent to the soft templating                  agent P123 is 1:1-5. The hard templating agent                  composed of three-dimensional ordered macroporous                  polymethyl methacrylate mixed with styrene-based                  macroporous adsorption resin or acrylate-modified                  styrene-based macroporous adsorption resin. The                  mass ratio of the three-dimensional ordered                  macroporous polymethyl methacrylate to the                  styrene-based macroporous adsorption resin or the                  acrylate-modified styrene-based macroporous                  adsorption resin is 1:0.5-1. The acrylate is                  methyl methacrylate, methyl acrylate, ethyl                  acrylate, butyl acrylate, hydroxyethyl acrylate                  and glycidyl acrylate. Preferred Method: The                  method comprises (i) mixing the soft template                  agent P123, deionized water and hydrochloric acid                  according to a molar ratio of                  1:9500-10000:350-1000, then stirring at 35-40                  degrees C for 3-6 hours and adding the hard                  template for 10-60 minutes, continuing dropwise                  addition of tetraethyl orthosilicate for 20-30                  hours to obtain mixed solution and the molar                  ratio of the ethyl orthosilicate to the soft                  template is 55-65:1; and (ii) transferring the                  mixed solution obtained in the step (i) into a                  reaction vessel to obtain molecular sieve                  material by hydrothermal reaction at a                  temperature of 100-150 degrees C for 24-48 hours,                  the molecular sieve material is filtered, washed                  to neutrality, dried, and calcined. The                  three-dimensional ordered macroporous polymethyl                  methacrylate is prepared by centrifugal                  self-assembly method and the three-dimensional                  ordered macroporous polymethyl methacrylate has a                  particle diameter of 200-800 nm and the                  styrene-based macroporous adsorption resin is                  prepared by a suspension polymerization method                  and the styrene-based macroporous adsorption                  resin has a pore diameter of 50-200 nm.

AB    NOVELTY - A bimetal oxide nano-sheet comprises an oxide of two transition metal elements and has a porous layer structure, where the metal element is a divalent metal cation and a trivalent metal cation, and the molar ratio of the divalent metal cation to the trivalent cation metal ion is 1:2-5:1.
   USE - Bimetal oxide nano-sheet for a lithium carbon dioxide battery as battery positive electrode catalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-sheet has better controllability and consistency, and can be prepared in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing a bimetal oxide nano-sheet, which involves dissolving a divalent metal salt and a trivalent metal salt in deionized water to obtain a metal salt solution, adding a pH adjuster to the metal salt solution, and adjusting the pH to 2-6 times the mass of the total metal salt to obtain a mixed solution after stirring, heating the mixed solution to 80-160 degrees C, hydrothermal reaction for 5-24 hours, and naturally cooling, centrifuging the obtained product, washing with deionized water, and drying to obtain a layered double metal hydroxide, calcining the layered double hydroxide at 400-800 degrees C for 1-12 hours to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The divalent metal cation is magnesium ion, copper ion, nickel ion, cobalt ion or zinc ion and the trivalent metal cation is aluminum ion, manganese ion, ferric ion, vanadium ion or indium ion. The divalent metal salt and the trivalent metal salt are sulfates, nitrates, chloride salts or carbonates.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pH adjuster is urea, ammonia or hexamethylenetetramine.

TI Enzyme solution composition for making breast cancer primary cells comprises hyaluronidase, collagenase and tripin, which is more simple and applicable.
AU WIDOWATI W
   HERIADY Y
   LAKSMITAWATI D R
   JASAPUTRA D K
   FARIED A
   WARGASETIA T L
   AMALIA A
   PERDANA F S
   QODARIAH R L
   ARLISYAH A
   KHOIRIYAH Z
   RIZAL
AE UNIV MARANATHA CHRISTIAN (UYMA-Non-standard)
GA 201981983B
AB    NOVELTY - Enzyme solution composition for making breast cancer primary cells comprises lmg/ml hyaluronidase, 4mg/ml collagenase and tripin is 0.1 wt.% of the total solution.
   USE - Enzyme solution composition for making breast cancer primary cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The enzyme solution composition for making breast cancer primary cells is more simple and applicable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for making breast cancer primary cells, which involves
   (A) cleansing tumor tissue in a petri dish containing 1x phosphate buffer saline (PBS), enumerating the tumor tissue using a surgical scalpel until it becomes a small fragment x 1mm3;
   (B) washing the sample fragments again with lx PBS, inserting the fragment into a 50 ml falcon tube containing an enzyme solution consisting of 1mg/ml hyaluronidase, 4mg/ml collagenase and trypsin;
   (C) incubating for 16 hours using a 300 revolutions per minute shaker at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide, filtering the sample using a 100pm cell strainer, centrifuging the samples at 1600 revolutions per minute for 10 minutes;
   (D) removing the supernatant formed, then washing the pellet with PBSlx and centrifuging at 1600 revolutions per minute for 5 minutes, washing is done for 2 times;
   (E) discarding the supernatant again, resuspending the pellets with complete growth medium, cell suspension is grown in T Flask 25; and
   (F) incubating the sample in an incubator at 37 degrees c with 5% carbon dioxide.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109025937-A; WO2019242191-A1; AU2018428500-A1; CN109025937-B; RU2735711-C1; US2021148205-A1; AU2018428500-B2; US11131172-B2
TI Method for extracting multi-stage combustion shock            wave induced cracking coal gas in hydraulic cutting            seam, involves injecting nitrogen or carbon dioxide in            drill hole, and forming fractures network around coal            borehole by coal body.
AU LIN B
   ZHAO Y
   KONG J
   SONG H
   LIU T
   LI Q
   ZHENG Y
   TAO Q
   YUAN C
   YANG W
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY XUZHOU (UYMT-C)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   XUZHOU BOAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   XUZHOU BOAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   XUZHOU BOAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   XUZHOU BOAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018A5057P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying out a                construction impacting and drilling processing in                coal by a high-pressure water jet slitting device                for forming a slot around percussive drilling. A                porous cylinder is connected with a piston in                impact drilling. An end of an injection tube is                connected to the porous cylinder through the                piston. Another end of the injection tube is                extended to an outer side of the percussive                drilling. Combustible gas i.e. methane gas and                auxiliary gas i.e. dry air are ignited in a                combustion chamber. Nitrogen or carbon dioxide is                injected in a drill hole by the piston for closing                the injection tube. A fractures network is formed                around a coal borehole by a coal body.
   USE - Method for extracting multi-stage combustion                shock wave induced cracking coal gas in hydraulic                cutting seam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables forming the fractures                network around the coal borehole by the coal body                so as to improve strength of drilling efficiency in                gas extraction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating a method for extracting multi-stage                combustion shock wave induced cracking coal gas in                hydraulic cutting seam. '(Drawing includes                non-English language text)'

AE ZHENJIANG GUMANYUAN ECOLOGICAL AGRIC CO (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201898041L
AB    NOVELTY - A culture material comprises 28-32 pts. wt.                broadleaf wood chips, 28-32 pts. wt. grape                branches, 18-22 pts. wt. pea seedling roots, 16-20                pts. wt. bran, 0.8-1.2 pts. wt. white sugar and                0.8-1.2 pts. wt. gypsum powder.
   USE - Culture material for cultivating Hericium                erinaceus.
   ADVANTAGE - The culture material has rich in nutrition,                fresh and tender, delicious taste and enhanced                quality and yield, in rational design.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for cultivating Hericium erinaceus, which                involves preparing a culture material in                proportion, adding water to the culture material,                stirring and mixing, stacking the fermentation,                inoculating the strain and the culture material,                the inoculum is 10-15% at a temperature of 6-33                degrees C, air relative humidity 55-65%, air carbon                dioxide content of 0.3-1% in the mycelium culture                chamber, until the mycelium in the bag is full,                moving the bacteria bag to the mushroom culture                room, keeping the material temperature at 12-24                degrees C, keep the carbon dioxide content in the                air not more than 0.1%, spraying water to make                indoor relative humidity of the air is 85-90% and                harvesting, the surface is cleaned after                harvesting, stop spraying and ventilate for 3-4                days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gypsum powder is                  phosphogypsum powder.

PN KR1896689-B1
TI Recovering waste concrete from recycled aggregate involves separating waste water into fine particles of sludge containing fine particles and water, mixing fine particles separated from sludge with water to form suspension.
AU HEE Y S
   SANGWOO J
   JONG C Y
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 2018714168
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering waste concrete from recycled aggregate involves separating waste water into fine particles of a sludge containing fine particles and water, mixing fine particles separated from the sludge with water to form a suspension, injecting carbon dioxide into the suspension to cause calcium ions dissolved in the water to react with carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate and precipitate on the surface of the fine particles in the suspension, separating liquid and the solid from each other in the suspension. The upper and lower surfaces are opened, and the overflow product includes particles having a relatively small particle size is discharged to the upper portion, and the underflow product includes particles having a relatively large particle size is discharged to the lower portion using multistage cyclone apparatus in which cyclone units performing particle size separation are continuously arranged.
   USE - Method for recovering waste concrete from recycled aggregate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recovere waste concrete from recycled aggregate is environment-friendly processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The multistage cyclone apparatus continuously performs particle size separation using cyclone units on the overflow product and. The fine particles contained in the overflow product of the cyclone unit disposed last in the multistage cyclone device have a particle size passing through 400 sieve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The particles contained in an overflow product of the cyclone unit disposed last in the multistage cyclone apparatus are used as the fine particles. The multi-stage cyclone apparatus continuously performs particle size separation using cyclone units on an overflow product, and the flow product is used as the suspension. The dehydration is performed on the underflow product of each cyclone unit of the cyclone device, and the separated dehydrated filtrate is mixed with the overflow product of the cyclone unit disposed at the end of the cyclone device and forming suspension. The concentration of the calcium ion dissolved in the suspension is in the range of 400-1000 mg/L. The carbon dioxide is collected in an exhaust gas of a power plant. The carbon dioxide is supplied in the form of micro-sized bubbles. The carbon dioxide is injected in the coating step so that the suspension is formed in a pH range of 8.3-8.5.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108355719-A; CN108355719-B
TI Composite photocatalytic material useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by visible light, is prepared by supporting monoatomic palladium on covalent triazine organic polymer by UV photoreduction.
AU BI J
   HUANG H
   LI L
   WU L
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 201862962C
AB    NOVELTY - A composite photocatalytic material is prepared by supporting monoatomic palladium on a covalent triazine organic polymer by UV photoreduction. The content of monoatomic palladium in the composite photocatalytic material is 0.01-0.05 %mass.
   USE - Composite photocatalytic material is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by visible light (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite photocatalytic material is simple and convenient to prepare.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for preparing the composite photocatalytic material, which involves (a) adding 0.1-0.5 g covalent triazine organic polymer to a reactor, adding 30 ml ethylene glycol, stirring for 30-90 minutes, placing the reactor in an oven at 150 degrees C for 2-4 hours, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging the mixture with ultrapure water and ethanol and vacuum-drying to obtain an intermediate product (CTF-EG), (b) weighing 0.1-0.5 g CTF-EG in 30 ml ultrapure water, adding 20-50 mu l of 5 mmol/l tetrachloropyridinic acid solution under stirring condition, carrying out UV reduction for 30-40 minutes under nitrogen atmosphere, adding ultrapure water, oscillating for 1 day, removing supernatant, adding ultrapure water oscillating for 1 day, repeating the operation several times and drying the precipitate at room temperature to obtain the composite photocatalytic material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In the step (a), the stirring step is carried out at 800-1000 rpm and the vacuum-drying step is carried out at 60 degrees C for 12-24 hours. In the step (b), the stirring step is carried out at 800-1000 rpm.

TI Synthesizing 2-bromo-3-fluorobenzoic acid by deprotonating o-bromofluorobenzene with lithium diisopropylamide, and then reacting with trimethylchlorosilane in presence of organic solvent to form (3-bromo-2-fluorophenyl)(trimethyl)silane.
AU GAO F
   CENG S
   JIANG J
   ZHANG H
   TONG J
   ZENG S
   ZHANG X
AE SHANGHAI JINLU PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU YIXIANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2018367587
AB    NOVELTY - The synthesizing method of 2-bromo-3-fluorobenzoic acid involves: (1) deprotonating o-bromofluorobenzene with lithium diisopropylamide, and then reacting with trimethylchlorosilane in the presence of organic solvent (A) to form (3-bromo-2-fluorophenyl)(trimethyl)silane; and (2) forming an intermediate 4-trimethylsilyl-3-fluoro-2-bromobenzene lithium salt using (3-bromo-2-fluorophenyl)(trimethyl)silane under the action of lithium diisopropylamide, and then reacting with carbon dioxide to generate 2-bromo-3-fluoro-4-(trimethylsilyl)benzoic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing 2-bromo-3-fluorobenzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of simply synthesizing the 2-bromo-3-fluorobenzoic acid with high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The synthesizing method of 2-bromo-3-fluorobenzoic acid involves: (1) deprotonating o-bromofluorobenzene with lithium diisopropylamide, and then reacting with trimethylchlorosilane in the presence of organic solvent (A) to form (3-bromo-2-fluorophenyl)(trimethyl)silane; (2) forming an intermediate 4-trimethylsilyl-3-fluoro-2-bromobenzene lithium salt using (3-bromo-2-fluorophenyl)(trimethyl)silane under the action of lithium diisopropylamide, and then reacting with carbon dioxide to generate 2-bromo-3-fluoro-4-(trimethylsilyl)benzoic acid; and (3) reacting 2-bromo-3-fluoro-4-(trimethylsilyl)benzoic acid with fluorine-containing reagent in the presence of organic solvent (B) to obtain the target product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (1), the organic solvent (A) is chosen from tetrahydrofuran, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran, cyclopentyl methyl ether and/or diethoxymethane. The molar ratio of o-bromofluorobenzene, lithium diisopropylamide and trimethylchlorosilane is 1:(1-1.2):(1-4). The molar ratio of (3-bromo-2-fluorophenyl)(trimethyl)silane and lithium diisopropylamide is 1:(1-1.2). In step (3), the organic solvent (B) is chosen from dichloromethane and/or tetrahydrofuran. The fluorine-containing reagent is chosen from trifluoroacetic acid and/or quaternary ammonium fluoride. The molar ratio of 2-bromo-3-fluoro-4-(trimethylsilyl)benzoic acid and fluorine-containing reagent is 1:(2-6).

PN CN107540696-A
TI Production of biapenem side chain product useful for inhibiting e.g. Haemophilus, involves adding sodium bicarbonate solution into reactor in vacuum, adding pyrazolotriazole compound and propionamide and reacting.
AU HU T
   PEI H
   ZHENG X
AE NINGXIA HAICHENG ELECTRONIC INFORMATION (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201803953S
AB    NOVELTY - Production of biapenem side chain product comprises adding 25% sodium bicarbonate solution into a reactor in a vacuum, adding a pyrazolotriazole compound (IX) and propionamide and then reacting at 20 degrees C for 1 hour, adding ethanol and ammonia to the mixture and then stirring at 15-25 degrees C, adjusting the pH to 5 using 30% hydrochloric acid and sodium bicarbonate, adding toluene to the mixture and then extracting, crystallizing the extract and then decompressing, reacting using ammonia and carbon dioxide, and processing.
   USE - Used for production of biapenem which is useful for inhibiting methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus, Streptococcus pneumoniae, S. pyogenes, gram-positive bacteria, Enterobacteriaceae, Haemophilus, Moraxella catarrhalis, Neisseria meningitidis, Enterococcus, Pseudomonas aeruginosa and Acinetobacter.
   ADVANTAGE - The product enables production of biapenem with excellent antibacterial properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of biapenem side chain product comprises adding 25% sodium bicarbonate solution into a reactor in a vacuum, adding a pyrazolotriazole compound of formula (IX) and propionamide and then reacting at 20 degrees C for 1 hour, adding ethanol and ammonia to the mixture and then stirring at 15-25 degrees C, adjusting the pH to 5 using 30% hydrochloric acid and sodium bicarbonate, adding toluene to the mixture and then extracting, crystallizing the extract and then decompressing, reacting using ammonia and carbon dioxide, and processing.
TF ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

PN WO2020191117-A1
TI Producing methanol and olefins by feeding a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture to CPO reaction zone, feeding a cracking zone reactant mixture to a cracking zone and cooling the CPO reaction zone effluent.
AU CHINTA S
   PANT A
   NARAYANASWAMY R
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   CHINTA S (CHIN-Individual)
   PANT A (PANT-Individual)
   NARAYANASWAMY R (NARA-Individual)
GA 202093784A
AB    NOVELTY - Methanol and olefins are produced by (a) feeding a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture to CPO reaction zone (100) where CPO reactant mixture comprises oxygen, 1st hydrocarbons, and optionally steam, where greater than or equal to 1 portion of CPO reactant mixture reacts, via an exothermic CPO reaction, in CPO reaction zone to produce a CPO reaction zone effluent, where CPO reaction zone comprises a CPO catalyst, where the CPO reaction zone effluent comprises hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), water, and unreacted 1st hydrocarbons, and where the CPO reaction zone effluent is characterized by a CPO effluent temperature; (b) feeding a cracking zone reactant mixture to a cracking zone; (c) cooling the CPO reaction zone effluent by heating the 2nd hydrocarbons to cracking zone temperature while cooling the CPO reaction zone effluent by heat transfer between CPO reaction zone effluent and 2nd hydrocarbons; and (d) removing greater than or equal to 1 portion of water from combined effluent.
   USE - Production of methanol and olefins.
   ADVANTAGE - The process uses syngas separated from combined effluent having an increased hydrogen content relative to hydrogen content of CPO reaction zone effluent for downstream methanol synthesis, generates less CO2 through fuel burning, and enables operating the CPO reaction zone at relatively low carbonn/oxygen ratios.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Methanol and olefins are produced by (a) feeding a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture to CPO reaction zone (100) where CPO reactant mixture comprises oxygen, 1st hydrocarbons, and optionally steam, where greater than or equal to 1 portion of CPO reactant mixture reacts, via an exothermic CPO reaction, in CPO reaction zone to produce a CPO reaction zone effluent, where CPO reaction zone comprises a CPO catalyst, where the CPO reaction zone effluent comprises hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), water, and unreacted 1st hydrocarbons, and where the CPO reaction zone effluent is characterized by a CPO effluent temperature; (b) feeding a cracking zone reactant mixture to a cracking zone (600), where the cracking zone reactant mixture comprises greater than or equal to 1 portion of CPO reaction zone effluent, 2nd hydrocarbons, and optionally steam, where a portion of cracking zone reactant mixture reacts, via an endothermic cracking reaction, in cracking zone to produce a combined effluent, where the cracking zone optionally comprises a cracking catalyst, where the cracking zone is characterized by a cracking zone temperature, where the CPO effluent temperature is greater than cracking zone temperature, where the 1st hydrocarbons and 2nd hydrocarbons are the same or different, and where combined effluent comprises H2, CO, CO2, water, olefins, unreacted 1st hydrocarbons, and unreacted 2nd hydrocarbons; (c) cooling the CPO reaction zone effluent by heating the 2nd hydrocarbons to cracking zone temperature while cooling the CPO reaction zone effluent by heat transfer between CPO reaction zone effluent and 2nd hydrocarbons; (d) removing greater than or equal to 1 portion of water from combined effluent to produce a dehydrated combined effluent; (e) separating greater than or equal to 1 portion of dehydrated combined effluent into syngas and greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons stream, where the syngas comprises H2, CO, CO2, and methane (CH4), and where greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons stream comprises greater than or equal to 2C olefins and greater than or equal to 2C alkanes; and (f) introducing greater than or equal to 1 portion of syngas to a methanol reactor to produce methanol. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) producing methanol and olefins which involves (g) separating greater than or equal to 1 portion of methanol reactor effluent stream into a crude methanol stream and a vapor stream, where crude methanol stream comprises methanol and water, and where vapor stream comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, and optionally 1-2 carbon-containing (1-2C) alcohols; (h) separating greater than or equal to 1 portion of vapor stream into a hydrogen stream and a residual gas stream, where the hydrogen stream comprises greater than or equal to 1 portion of hydrogen of vapor stream, and where the residual gas stream comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, and optionally 1-2C alcohols; (i) recycling greater than or equal to 1 portion of the hydrogen stream to the methanol reactor; and (j) optionally recycling greater than or equal to 1 portion of residual gas stream and/or greater than or equal to 1 portion of alkanes stream to cracking zone in step (b) and/or to CPO reaction zone in step (a); and
   (2) system (1000) for producing methanol and olefins comprising (a) catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reaction zone comprising a CPO catalyst and is operable to react greater than or equal to 1 portion of CPO reactant mixture; (b) a cracking zone operable to convert greater than or equal to 1 portion of cracking zone mixture to a combined effluent; (c) separation apparatus operable to remove greater than or equal to 1 portion of water from combined effluent to produce a dehydrated combined effluent, and separate greater than or equal to 1 portion of dehydrated combined effluent into syngas stream; and (d) methanol reactor (200) configured for producing methanol from greater than or equal to 1 portion of syngas stream, where the CPO reaction zone and cracking zone are configured such that direct heat transfer between CPO reaction zone effluent and second hydrocarbons provides for cooling of CPO reaction zone effluent and heating of second hydrocarbons to the cracking zone temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for methanol production process.
   CPO reaction zone (100)
   Methanol reactor (200)
   Gas-liquid separator (300)
   Cracking zone (600)
   System (1000)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Devices: The common housing comprises both the CPO reaction zone and cracking zone. The system further comprises (i) a line connecting the separation apparatus with CPO reaction zone, (ii) a line connecting the separation apparatus with cracking zone, and/or (iii) line fluidly connecting the separation apparatus with a furnace. Preferred Components: The cracking zone reactant mixture comprises an amount of 2nd hydrocarbons effective to provide for cooling CPO reaction zone effluent to cracking zone temperature while heating 2nd hydrocarbons to the cracking zone temperature. The cracking zone reactant mixture comprises an amount of 2nd hydrocarbons effective to provide for a combined effluent temperature variation within less than or equal to 10% of target combined effluent temperature. The 1st hydrocarbons and/or 2nd hydrocarbons comprise methane, ethane, propane, butanes, naphtha, natural gas, natural gas liquids, associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas, paraffins, shale gas, shale liquids, fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) off gas, refinery process gases, stack gases, and/or fuel gas from fuel gas header. Preferred Process: Heating the 2nd hydrocarbons to cracking zone temperature comprises (i) introducing liquid 2nd hydrocarbons to cracking zone; (ii) heating the liquid 2nd hydrocarbons to vaporize greater than or equal to 1 portion and to form gaseous 2nd hydrocarbons; and (iii) heating the gaseous 2nd hydrocarbons to cracking zone temperature. The 2nd hydrocarbons are introduced to cracking zone via a nozzle, spray nozzle, atomization nozzle, injector, spray injector, atomization injector, steam atomization injector, quill, distributor, and/or distributor plate. Step (e) comprises (el) introducing greater than or equal to 1 portion of dehydrated combined effluent and oil to an oil scrubber to yield a syngas-containing stream and a scrubbed oil stream comprising greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons and oil, and where amount of greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons in scrubbed oil stream is greater than the amount of greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons in syngas-containing stream; (e2) introducing greater than or equal to 1 portion of scrubbed oil stream to an oil regenerator to yield a recovered oil stream and 1st greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons stream; (e3) optionally recycling greater than or equal to 1 portion of recovered oil stream to oil scrubber; (e4) feeding greater than or equal to 1 portion of syngas-containing stream to a size-exclusion column to yield syngas and a 2nd greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons stream; and (e5) optionally introducing greater than or equal to 1 portion of syngas-containing stream to methanol reactor in step (f). The process further comprises (1) separating greater than or equal to 1 portion of greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons stream into olefins stream and alkanes stream; and (2) optionally recycling greater than or equal to 1 portion of greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons stream and/or greater than or equal to 1 portion of alkanes stream to cracking zone and/or to CPO reaction zone. Step (f) comprises (fl) introducing greater than or equal to 1 portion of syngas to methanol reactor to produce a methanol reactor effluent stream; (f2) separating greater than or equal to 1 portion of methanol reactor effluent stream into crude methanol stream and a vapor stream, where crude methanol stream comprises methanol and water; (f3) separating greater than or equal to 1 portion of vapor stream into a hydrogen stream and residual gas stream, where the hydrogen stream comprises greater than or equal to 1 portion of hydrogen of vapor stream; (f4) recycling greater than or equal to 1 portion of hydrogen stream to the methanol reactor; and (f5) optionally recycling greater than or equal to 1 portion of residual gas stream to cracking zone in step (b) and/or to CPO reaction zone in step (a). Preferred Parameters: The syngas has an M ratio of greater than or equal to 1.8 and molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide is greater than or equal to 2. The CPO reaction zone has greater than or equal to 1 CPO operational parameter selected from CPO feed temperature of 25-600 degrees C; CPO pressure of 1-90 barg; a CPO contact time of 0.001 ms-5 s; a carbon to oxygen (C/O) molar ratio in CPO reactant mixture of 0.5:1-3:1, where the C/O molar ratio refers to total moles of carbon (C) of hydrocarbons in reactant mixture divided by total moles of oxygen (O2) in the reactant mixture; a steam to carbon (S/C) molar ratio in CPO reactant mixture of 0.01:1-2.4: 1, where the S/C molar ratio refers to total moles of water in the reactant mixture divided by the total moles of carbon (C) of hydrocarbons in the reactant mixture. CPO effluent temperature is 500-1600 degrees C; and cracking zone temperature is 400-1200 degrees C.
