PT P
PN CN109809440-A; CN109809440-B
TI Preparation of high purity lithium chloride for            preparing e.g. lithium formate, by mixing crude lithium            chloride with organic solvent, heating, passing ammonia            into obtained suspension, stirring, filtering, and            adding purified water.
AU TAN Y
   REN Q
   GONG L
   ZHONG Q
AE SHENZHEN MAXCHEM TECH CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2019503252
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high purity lithium chloride                involves: (S1) mixing crude lithium chloride with                an organic solvent to obtain a mixed solution,                heating the mixed solution until boiling and                removing water in the reflux liquid, refluxing an                organic solvent until the water content in the                reflux liquid and the mixed solution is less than                or equal to 0.5 wt.%, stopping heating and cooling                to room temperature to obtain a suspension; (S2)                passing ammonia into the suspension, stirring for a                period of time, dissolving all the lithium chloride                suspended in the organic solvent, and filtering to                remove insoluble impurities to obtain a filtrate;                and (S3) adding purified water to the filtrate                obtained in the step (S2), evenly stirring, adding                and refining pre-dispensing solution, thoroughly                mixing, filtering, again adding oxalic acid to the                filtrate, stirring, cooling, and filtering to                remove insoluble matter in the solution to obtain                an organic solution of refined lithium                chloride.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lithium                chloride, which is useful for preparing lithium                formate and lithium carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of preparing lithium                chloride with high purity of greater than or equal                to 99.9 wt.%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high purity lithium chloride                involves: (S1) mixing crude lithium chloride with                an organic solvent having a boiling point of less                than 100 degrees C to obtain a mixed solution,                heating the mixed solution until boiling and                removing water in the reflux liquid, refluxing an                organic solvent until the water content in the                reflux liquid and the mixed solution is less than                or equal to 0.5 wt.%, stopping heating and cooling                to room temperature to obtain a suspension; (S2)                passing ammonia into the suspension under stirring,                where the mass fraction of ammonia in organic                solvents is 10-15%, maintaining stirring for a                period of time, dissolving all the lithium chloride                suspended in the organic solvent, and filtering to                remove insoluble impurities to obtain a filtrate;                and (S3) adding purified water to the filtrate                obtained in the step (S2), where the mass                percentage of purified water in the filtrate is                0.5-5%, evenly stirring, adding and refining                pre-dispensing solution, thoroughly mixing,                filtering, again adding oxalic acid to the                filtrate, stirring for a period of time, cooling to                below -5 degrees C, and filtering to remove                insoluble matter in the solution to obtain an                organic solution of refined lithium chloride.                INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing a high purity                lithium formate, which involves (S1a) mixing the                crude lithium chloride with an organic solvent                having a boiling point of less than 100 degrees C                to obtain a mixed solution, heating the mixed                solution until boiling and removing moisture from                the reflux liquid, refluxing an organic solvent                until the water content in the reflux liquid and                the mixed solution is less than or equal to 0.5                wt.%, stopping heating and cooling to room                temperature to obtain a suspension, and adding an                equimolar amount of ammonium formate to lithium                chloride, (S2a) passing ammonia into the suspension                under stirring, stirring for a period of time,                dissolving all lithium chloride and ammonium                formate suspended in the organic solvent, followed                by carrying out metathesis reaction to form lithium                formate and ammonium chloride, and filtering to                remove insoluble impurities to obtain a filtrate,                and (S3a) adding purified water to the filtrate                obtained in the step (S2a), evenly stirring, adding                the refined pre-dispensing solution, thoroughly                stirring, filtering, then adding oxalic acid to the                filtrate, stirring for a period of time, cooling to                below -5 degrees C and removing the insoluble                matter in the solution by filtration to obtain an                organic solution of refined lithium formate, where                the purity of lithium formate (dry basis) is                greater than or equal to 99.9 wt.%; and
   (2) a method for preparing a high purity                lithium carbonate, which involves (1) taking the                organic solution of the refined lithium formate as                a raw material and/or the organic solution of the                purified lithium chloride as a raw material, adding                a saturated aqueous solution of an equal molar                ratio of ammonium bicarbonate to carry out                metathesis reaction at 0-30 degrees C to obtain a                mixture of lithium bicarbonate and lithium                carbonate, or introducing carbon dioxide into the                organic solution of the refined lithium formate                and/or the organic solution of the purified lithium                chloride, evenly stirring, so that the carbon                dioxide is fully absorbed by the solution, after                the absorption is completed, pressurizing a closed                stirring tank to 0.1-1 MPa with an inert gas,                heating to room temperature, adding purified water                to carbon dioxide, continuously stirring for 2-4                hours until the reaction is completed, the pH value                drops to 6-7 to obtain a mixture of lithium                bicarbonate and lithium carbonate, and (2)                filtering the mixture of lithium bicarbonate and                lithium carbonate obtained in the step (1),                dissolving the filter cake in deionized water and                introducing excess carbon dioxide for full                carbonization, filtering and removing impurities to                obtain a purified solution of lithium bicarbonate,                heating the purified solution at 80-100 degrees C                for negative pressure decarburization to obtain a                lithium carbonate particle suspension, cooling the                resulting suspension to room temperature, filtering                to obtain a filter cake, and repeatedly washing the                filter cake with hot water to obtain a lithium                carbonate having a purity (dry basis) of greater                than or equal to 99.5 wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method                  further involves (S4) carrying out evaporation on                  the organic solution of the purified lithium                  chloride obtained in the step (S3) to remove the                  organic solvent and volatile matter to obtain a                  high-purity anhydrous lithium chloride. The                  filtration step in the step (S2) is carried out                  using a precision filter element having a                  filtration accuracy of 0.05-1 mu m. Preferred                  Components: In the step (S1), if the crude                  lithium chloride raw material used is a solid                  powder, the ratio of the crude lithium chloride                  solid powder to the organic solvent is 100-450                  kg/M3, and if the crude lithium chloride raw                  material used is a liquid, the amount of the                  organic solvent to be added is 0.5-5 liters per                  liter of the crude lithium chloride liquid. In                  the crude lithium chloride in the step (S1), the                  mass ratio of divalent magnesium ion to                  monovalent lithium ion is 1-10%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent in                  the step (S1) is methanol, ethanol, n-propanol,                  isopropanol, acetone, dichloromethane, and/or                  benzene.

PN CN107964467-A
TI Preparing organic grape wine involves selectecting grape plants with anthelmintic and insecticidal effects that are interplanted in orchards and harvesting ripen, fresh, healthy, disease-free and rotten-free grape fruits from orchard.
AU LIANG H
   WANG Y
AE YANTAI ZHIQI CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YANT-Non-standard)
GA 2018364253
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing organic grape wine involves selecting grape plants with anthelmintic and insecticidal effects that are interplanted in orchards, and managing orchards according to the requirements of organic cultivation. The ripen, fresh, healthy, disease-free and rotten-free grape fruits with acidity of 6.0-6.5 g/L, and sugar content 180-200g/L from the orchard are harvested and stored at 0-20 degrees C or 5-20 degrees C for wine brewing. The harvested cold-impregnated grapes are crushed and mixed with water in the wine in the weight ratio of 1-75:25-99 to obtain grape pulp juice, which is heated to 50-65 degrees C for 20-60 minutes to inactivate the oxidase in the pulp, and the temperature is reduced to 15-25 degrees C and added into the fermenter. The fermenter is added with 20-50mg/L oxidase enzyme, at a temperature of 5-8 degrees C for 12-96 hours and then added with clarifier to obtain sediment for after 24-48 hours of mixing to obtain solution with 150-250 NTU turbidity.
   USE - Method for preparing organic grape wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare organic grape wine, which is rich in nutrition, healthy, safe, and free of organic sulfur dioxide and has no pesticide residues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The pulp juice is obtained is separated from sediment and the nitrogen or carbon dioxide is also during oxygen removal. The pulp juice is stored at 0-5 degrees C, then covered with nitrogen or carbon dioxide for preventing oxidation. The grape juice is warmed to 10-12 degrees C, then inoculated with 106 cfu/ml Saccharomyces cerevisiae and fermented at a temperature of 10-12 degrees C for 24-72 hours to obtain primary fermented solution. The primary fermented solution is subjected to alcohol Fermentation at 15-25 degrees C and 16-20 degrees C for 24-72 hours to obtain alcohol fermented solution. The alcohol fermented solution is specific gravity is reduced to 0.993, and temperature is reduced to 5-10 degrees C to obtain wine. The wine obtained is inoculated with 20-60 mg/L lactic acid bacteria and subjected to is malic acid-lactic acid fermentation at 20-25 degrees C for 24-480 hours to obtain malic acid-lactic fermentation wine, which is stored at a temperature of 10-15 degrees C for 3-24 months. The obtained malic acid-lactic fermentation wine is clarified with clarifying agent to obtain grape base wine. The grape base wine is added with 2-8 wt.% alcohol, 50-80 g/L sugar, and 17g / L dry extract and controlling acidity to 5-6 g/ L to obtain mixture. The mixture is frozen at a temperature of -5 to 0 degrees C for 5-15 days, then filtered, clarified, and filled with nitrogen or carbon dioxide to remove oxygen to obtain cold-stabilized grape wine. The obtained cold-stabilized grape wine is sterilized by 0.45 and 0.20 micron membrane equipment and dissolved into carbon dioxide with pressure of 0.3-0.5 megapascal (MPa). The cold-stabilized grape wine is further tested and qualified. The enzyme used is selectecd from pectinase, cellulase, papain, alginase or amylase. The clarifying agent is selected from gelatin, bentonite, Polyvinylpolypyrolidone (PVPP) and egg white. The fertilizer used in the organic cultivation is organic fertilizers or bio-organic fertilizer. The organic manure, organic-inorganic fertilizer and bio-organic fertilizer are added with an ecological algae biostimulating hormone or seaweed residue. The ecological seaweed biostimulating hormone is prepared by washing and crushing seaweed to obtain crushed seaweed product. The seaweed crushed material, water and enzyme are added into a stirred tank in the weight ratio of 5-30:70-95:0.02-1, sterilized at 60-120 degrees C for 5-40 minutes, then cooled to 27-45 degrees C, and enzyme is enzymatically digested for 30-60 minutes and the algae is hydrolyzed to obtain crude enzymolysed seaweed. The crude enzymolysed seaweed is filtered to obtain filtrate and seaweed residue. The seaweed residue, urea, magnesium sulfate, sodium chloride, potassium dihydrogen phosphate and glucose are added to the fermenter according to a weight ratio of 95.5:1:0.5:1:1:1, and mixed with water. The moisture content is adjusted to 50-70%, sterilized at 70-150 degrees C for 5-40 minutes, and cooled to 27-36 degrees C to obtain mixed culture medium. The Rhodotorula glutinis are inoculated into culture media in the weight ratio of 0.1 to 2:98 to 99.9 at a temperature of 27-36 degrees C, where oxygen concentration in the fermentor is maintained to 8-21%, fermentation is performed for 6-144 hours, and sterilization is performed at a temperature of 70-150 degrees C for 20-40 minutes to obtain seaweed fermented material. The seaweed filtrate is added to the seaweed fermented material mixed under stirring to obtain mixture and inoculated with Corynebacterium ammoniagenes culture solution, then subjected to thawing, and it is fermented for 1-6 days at 25-35 degrees C temperature with 1-8 mg/L of oxygen to obtain ecological seaweed liquid biostimulating hormone and seaweed residue. The obtained ecological seaweed liquid biostimulating hormone and seaweed residue is concentrated and spray-dried, where seaweed is Enteromorpha, kelp, gulfweed, or Undaria pinnatifida.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The pesticides used in the orchard are selected from lime sulfur, Bordeaux mixture, mineral oil, harzianum, quinolinone suspension, ethanol, soda, berberine, rotenone, pyrethrum or matrine. Preferred Conditions: The beef extract is prepared by washing and chopping 550 grams beef and soaked in 1375 ml water for overnight, then filtered and sterilized after 0.6kg/cm2 for 40 minutes. The enzyme is a composition of lysozyme and pectinase in a weight ratio of 5:1. The separation of pulp juice from sediment is performed by applying pressure filtration, filtration and centrifugation. The mixed culture medium is adjusted to a moisture content of 60%, and added with Rhodotorula zygosis in a weight ratio of 0.5:99.5 at a temperature of 30 degrees C, where oxygen concentration is maintained to 12%, and fermentation is performed for 120 hours at a temperature of 120 degrees C and sterilized for 20 minutes. The Corynebacterium ammoniagenes culture solution is mixed with mixture in the weight ratio of 98:2 and subjected to thawing and fermentation is carried out for 5 days at a temperature of 28 degrees C under oxygen content of 3 mg/L. The Rhodotorula glutinis and Corynebacterium ammoniagenes are preaerved in China Industrial Microbiological Culture Collection Management Center (CICC) under preservation number (CICC NOs: 31192 and 10168) respectively. The homogenization is performed for twice, where homogenization temperature is maintained to 40 degrees C and homogenization is performed for 15 minutes, where first homogenization pressure is maintained to 25 MPa, and second homogenization pressure to 4 MPa. The eco-algae biostimulating substances are added to fertilizers and mixed for spraying fertilizer particles to the surface. Preferred Process: The Corynebacterium ammoniagenes culture solution is prepared by using Corynebacterium ammoniagenes cells in the logarithmic growth phase and inoculated in a liquid medium at 10% inoculation, and cultured at 30 degrees C on a shaker at stirring speed of 160 revolutions/minute for 48 hours and centrifuged at 8000 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes and expanded and re-cultivated strains are obtained. The expanded re-cultivation strains are placed in a refrigerator at -20 degrees C for 4 hours, and taken out at room temperature and cultured by adding into liquid medium. The liquid medium has a pH of 7.2-7.4. The lysozyme is obtained by adding 5 times water to egg white or whole egg liquid, then adding into homogenizer and homogenizing at a temperature of 30-60 degrees C, with homogeneity pressure of 2-40 MPa for 10-40 minutes and filtered to obtain filtrate, which is diluted to obtain diluted solution.The diluted solution obtained is ultrafiltered using an ultrafiltration membrane having a molecular weight cut-off of 15,000 to 30000 Dalton to obtain diluted solution and concentrated solution. The diluted solution is added with resin in the weight ratio of 1:3 to 8 for adsorption to obtain the adsorbed resin. The adsorbed resin is eluted with 0.001 to 0.5 mol/L aqueous sodium chloride solution to obtain eluent. The eluate obtained is ultrafiltered by ultrafiltration to desalt to obtain ultrafiltrate having a molecular weight cut-off of 2000 to 10000 Dalton. The ultrafiltrate obtained is dried to obtain lysozyme and the resin used is a cation exchange resin or macroporous adsorption resin. The type of cation exchange resin is selected from 001x 7, 732, Amberlite IR-120, Dowex-50, Lewatit-100, Diaion SK-1, AllassionCS, Duolite C-20, SDB-3, macroporous adsorption The resin is one or more of AB-8, D101, D3520, X-5, NKA-II, NKA-9, S-8, XDA-1, H-20, H-30, or H-40. Preferred Composition: The liquid medium used for preparing Corynebacterium ammoniagenes culture solution comprises 1.0L beef extract, 10.0g peptone, 5.0g sodium chloride and 20.0-25.0g agar.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media.    USE - The gas fermentation culture medium composition is useful for performing fermentation for enhancing gas consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for performing fermentation, which involves preparing gas fermentation culture medium composition, and supplying a gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide or hydrogen to the medium composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 0.5-30 g glutamic acid or its salt with respect to 1 l medium composition. PT P
PN US2021261873-A1; US11434441-B2; WO2022235285-A1
TI Blended gasoline composition comprises aromatic            amine, ethanol, non-amine aromatic hydrocarbons,            olefins, and paraffins.
AU BURGER J
AE BURGER J (BURG-Individual)
   BURGER J (BURG-Individual)
GA 202198707Y
AB    NOVELTY - Blended gasoline composition comprises an                aromatic amine in concentrations ranging from 0.1-5                vol.%, ethanol in concentrations up to about 20                vol.%, non-amine aromatic hydrocarbons in                concentrations up to about 15 vol.%, olefins in                concentrations up to about 8 vol.%, and paraffins                in concentrations up to about 90 vol.%.
   USE - Blended gasoline composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The blended gasoline composition is used to                provide lower carbon dioxide emission upon                combustion would significantly improve the                environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aromatic amine is                  selected from the group consisting of                  m-toluidine, p-toluidine, o-toluidine and aniline                  as well as mixtures of the identified compounds.                  Preferred Compositions: The one pound of said                  blended gasoline composition produces 0.00004                  pound of carbon per BTU or less when combusted in                  an internal combustion engine. The blended                  gasoline composition has an anti-knock index                  value of at least 87. The olefin concentration is                  5.00% or less. The olefin concentration is 3.00%                  or less. The olefin concentration is 1.00% or                  less. The olefin concentration is 0.00%. The                  aromatic concentration is 10% or less. The                  aromatic concentration is 5% or less. The ethanol                  is present in concentrations of 10-20%. The                  ethanol is present in concentrations of 10-15%.                  The blended gasoline composition has a paraffin,                  olefin, naphthene and aromatic distribution by                  volume comprises 70-90% paraffin, 0-8% olefins,                  0-10% naphthenes and 0-15 wt.% aromatics. The                  blended gasoline composition has hydrocarbons                  with a carbon chain length distribution comprises                  hydrocarbons having four carbon atoms from about                  0-10% by volume, hydrocarbons having five carbon                  atoms and hydrocarbons having six carbon atoms                  from 25-40 vol.%, hydrocarbons having seven                  carbon atoms and hydrocarbons having eight carbon                  atoms from 30-50 vol.%, hydrocarbons having nine                  carbon atoms and hydrocarbons having ten carbon                  atoms from 3-30 vol.%, hydrocarbons having eleven                  carbon atoms and hydrocarbons having twelve                  carbon atoms from 0-25 vol.% and, hydrocarbons                  having at least thirteen carbon atoms from 0-5                  vol.%. The aromatic amine is m-toluidine and said                  m-toluidine is present in an amount of about 4%                  by volume of said blended gasoline composition.                  The aromatic amine is m-toluidine and m-toluidine                  said m-toluidine has a blended octane value of at                  least 500.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PT P
PN FR3086942-A1
TI Mixture of raw materials used for preparing molten glass, comprises silica, alkali hydroxide and alkaline earth hydroxide.
AU DI PIERRO S
AE SAINT-GOBAIN GLASS FRANCE (COMP-C)
GA 2020279951
AB    NOVELTY - Mixture of raw materials comprises silica, at least one alkali hydroxide and at least one alkaline earth hydroxide.
   USE - The mixture of raw materials is useful for preparing molten glass (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixture: is free of any carbonate and pulverulent; and emits little carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for manufacturing glass comprising heating and melting glass into the mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixture comprises: 5-25 wt.% alkali hydroxide; and 5-25 wt.% alkaline earth hydroxide. The sum of the weight of alkali carbonate and alkaline earth carbonate is less than 30%, preferably less than 1 wt.% or even is zero. The raw materials of the mixture are chosen to lead by fusion to a glass comprising 30-75 wt.% silica, 5-20 wt.% sodium oxide, 0-10 wt.% potassium oxide, 5-40 wt.% calcium oxide, 0-10 wt.% magnesium oxide, 0-30 wt.% alumina, and iron oxide. Preferred Method: The maximum heating temperature of the molten glass is 1200-1700 degrees C. The heating and the melting of glass are carried out in an oven, preferably an electric electrode oven, or an aerial burner oven e.g. transcersal burner oven or a loop oven and bake submerged burners. Heating and melting in glass, the mixture of raw materials, if necessary humidified, if necessary pulverulent, is introduced into an oven with cullet. The mixing of raw materials does not conform by mechanical pressure.
   EXAMPLE - Typical mixture of raw materials comprised of: 73 wt.% silica; 14 wt.% calcium oxide; and 13 wt.% sodium oxide.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. Chemical aspects of optical fibres (C03).)
MC L01-B01; L01-C02

PN CN109734717-A; CN109734717-B
TI New pteroic acid crystal form, prepared by dissolving carboxyl-containing compound in alkali solution, passing carbon dioxide gas and filtering.
AU XU L
   CHEN S
   XIAO P
   SHEN S
AE SUZHOU HUAJIAN RUIDA PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201945174J
AB    NOVELTY - A pteroic acid crystal form exhibiting an X-Ray powder diffraction pattern with peaks given in the specification, is new.
   USE - Pteroic acid crystal form.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has cheap raw materials, simple device and small environment pollution. The product improves solubility in organic solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the pteroic acid crystal form.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali in alkali solution is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and/or potassium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The pteroic acid crystal form is prepared by dissolving carboxyl-containing compound of formula (I) in alkali solution, passing carbon dioxide gas, and filtering. Preferred Method: The method involves dissolving compound (I) in alkali solution, passing carbon dioxide gas, reacting at 35-65 degrees C, and filtering. Preferred Components: The solvent in alkali solution is methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, tetrahydrofuran, acetone, acetonitrile and/or water or water.

PN CN110687287-A
TI Analyzing deer fetal oligopeptide for macrophage            immune regulation by crushing deer fetus raw product,            obtaining deer fetal polypeptides, obtaining cell            proliferation and phagocytosis indexes and obtaining            flow cytometry test results.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG K
   WU N
   DU Y
   LI Z
   LI J
   BIAN X
   LV J
   LAN M
   GAO X
AE JILIN XINSHUI TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 202007345X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for analyzing deer fetal oligopeptide                for macrophage immune regulation involves crushing                deer fetus raw product, centrifuging, filtering the                supernatant, obtaining deer fetal polypeptide                DFP-1, DFP-2 with molecular weight of greater than                10 KDa, DFP-3 with molecular weight of 3-10 KDa,                DFP-4 with molecular weight of 1-3 KDa and DFP-5                with molecular weight of less than 1 KDa,                determining protein content, measuring absorbance                at 595 nm between 5 minutes and 20 minutes,                obtaining cell proliferation index, obtaining cell                phagocytosis index, measuring secretion of nitric                oxide, interleukin (IL)-1 beta , IL-6 and TNF-                alpha , and obtaining flow cytometry test                results.
   USE - The method is useful for analyzing deer fetal                oligopeptide for macrophage immune                regulation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for analyzing deer fetal oligopeptide                for macrophage immune regulation involves (a)                taking 40 g deer fetus raw product, crushing to a                particle size of 60 mesh, adding 10-30 times                distilled water, stirring for 1-3 times at 4                degrees C for 6-8 hours for each time, centrifuging                at 3600 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 10-30                minutes and recovering supernatant, (b) filtering                the supernatant through double-layer oil filter                paper and then 0.45 mu m filter membrane,                subjecting to ultrafiltration using 1 KDa, 3 KDa                and 10 KDa ultrafiltration membrane, obtaining deer                fetal polypeptide DFP-1, DFP-2 with molecular                weight of greater than 10 KDa, DFP-3 with molecular                weight of 3-10 KDa, DFP-4 with molecular weight of                1-3 KDa and DFP-5 with molecular weight of less                than 1 KDa and lyophilizing, (c) accurately                weighing 1 mg bovine serum albumin (BSA) in a 10 ml                volumetric flask, dissolving and making up to                volume with distilled water, obtaining BSA standard                solution with a concentration of 0.1 mg/ml,                pipetting 0 ml, 0.1 ml, 0.2 ml, 0.4 ml, 0.6 ml, 0.8                ml and 1 ml BSA standard solution, making up to 1                ml with distilled water, zeroing with a blank tube,                adding 5 ml Coomassie brilliant blue reagent to                each tube, vortexing, reacting at room temperature                for 5 minutes, measuring absorbance at 595 nm                between 5 minutes and 20 minutes, taking protein                concentration of bovine serum albumin standard                solution in each tube as abscissa x and measured                absorbance value as ordinate Y for linear                regression and obtaining a standard curve                regression equation for determining protein                content, (d) taking 1 mg lyophilized powder, adding                1 ml distilled water to obtain 1 mg/ml sample                solution, pipetting 1 ml, adding 5 ml Coomassie                brilliant blue reagent, vortexing, reacting at room                temperature for 5 minutes and measuring absorbance                at 595 nm between 5 minutes and 20 minutes, (e)                taking logarithmic growth phase RAW264.7 cells at                105 cells/well in a 96-well cell culture plate,                incubating in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37                degrees C for 24 hours, removing supernatant,                adding the lyophilized powder obtained in step (b)                to prepare 150 mu l 100 mu g/ml solution, where                blank control group is 150 mu l Dulbecco's modified                Eagle medium (DMEM) high glucose medium containing                10% fetal calf serum and positive control group is                150 mu l 10 mu g/ml lipopolysaccharide (LPS),                incubating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide                for 12 hours, 24 hours and 48 hours, removing                supernatant, adding 10 mu l 5 mg/ml thiazolyl blue                tetrazolium bromide (MTT) solution to each well,                after 4 hours, adding 150 mu l dimethyl sulfoxide                (DMSO), shaking for 5 minutes, measuring                transmittance at 490 nm and obtaining cell                proliferation index, (f) separately configuring                lyophilized powder of DFP-5 with a molecular weight                of less than 1 KDa into 150 mu l 6.25 mu g/ml, 12.5                mu g/ml, 25 mu g/ml, 50 mu g/ml, 100 mu g/ml, 200                mu g/ml and 400 mu g/ml solution, the blank control                group is 150 mu l DMEM high glucose medium                containing 10% fetal calf serum and the positive                control group is 150 mu l 10 mu g/ml LPS,                incubating under 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours,                removing culture supernatant, adding 10 mu l 5                mg/ml MTT solution to each well, after 4 hours,                adding 150 mu l DMSO, shaking for 5 minutes,                measuring transmittance OD at 490 nm and obtaining                a cell proliferation index, (g) taking logarithmic                growth phase RAW264.7 cells at 105 cells/well in a                96-well cell culture plate, incubating in 5% carbon                dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 24 hours,                removing supernatant, adding the lyophilized powder                obtained in step (b) to prepare 150 mu l 100 mu                g/ml solution, where blank control group is 150 mu                l DMEM high glucose medium containing 10% fetal                calf serum and positive control group is 150 mu l                10 mu g/ml LPS, incubating at 37 degrees C and 5%                carbon dioxide for 12 hours, 24 hours and 48 hours,                removing supernatant, adding 10 mu l 5 mg/ml MTT                solution to each well, after 4 hours, adding 150 mu                l DMSO, shaking for 5 minutes, measuring                transmittance at 490 nm and obtaining a cell                proliferation index, stimulating DFP-5 by 12.5 mu                g/ml, 25 mu g/ml, 50 mu g/ml, 100 mu g/ml and 200                mu g/ml, setting LPS group and blank group at same                time, incubating for 24 hours, aspirating                supernatant, washing twice with polybutylene                succinate in phosphate buffered saline (PBS),                adding 200 mu l cell culture solution and 20 mu l                neutral red dye solution, incubating for 2 hours,                removing cell culture medium containing neutral red                staining solution, washing once with polybutylene                succinate in PBS, adding 200 mu l neutral red                detection lysate to each well, lysing on a shaker                at room temperature for 10 minutes and measuring                transmittance OD value at 540 nm to obtain cell                phagocytosis index, (h) seeding logarithmic growth                phase RAW264.7 cells in 96-well culture plates,                incubating at 37 degrees C for 24 hours, removing                supernatant, adding 150 mu l DFP-5 sample solution                of 12.5 mu g/ml, 25 mu g/ml, 50 mu g/ml, 100 mu                g/ml and 200 mu g/ml, setting blank group and LPS                group, incubating for 24 hours, taking 150 mu l                supernatant, and measuring secretion of nitric                oxide, interleukin (IL)-1 beta , IL-6 and TNF-                alpha according to instructions of each kit, and                (i) inoculating 2 ml per well in a 6-well plate,                aspirating culture medium after 24 hours, adding                deer fetal oligopeptide DFP-5, setting LPS group                and blank group, incubating, collecting sample                cells, washing twice with cold polybutylene                succinate in PBS, blowing the cells into a 15 ml                centrifuge tube, centrifuging, removing                supernatant, resuspending cells in 500 mu l                polybutylene succinate in PBS, dropwise adding 3 ml                75% cold ethanol, placing in -20 degrees C for 1                hour, centrifuging, adding 500 mu l cold                polybutylene succinate in PBS, resuspending cells,                adding 20 mu l RnaseA endonuclease solution,                incubating in water bath at 37 degrees C for 30                minutes, centrifuging for 10 minutes, removing                supernatant, adding 400 mu l fluorescent dye PI                staining solution, after mixing, incubating under                dark conditions at 4 degrees C for 30 minutes, and                obtaining flow cytometry test results.

containing neutral                  red staining solution, washing once with                  polybutylene succinate in PBS, adding 200 mu l                  neutral red detection lysate to each well, instructions of each kit, and (i) inoculating 2                  ml per well in a 6-well plate, aspirating culture                  medium after 24 hours, adding deer fetal                  oligopeptide DFP-5 with a mass concentration of                  12.5 mu g/ml, 25 mu g/ml, 50 mu g/ml, 100 mu g/ml                  and 200 mu g/ml, setting LPS group and blank                  group, cold ethanol, placing in -20                  degrees C for 1 hour, centrifuging, adding 500 mu                  l cold polybutylene succinate in PBS,                  resuspending cells, adding 20 mu l RnaseA                  endonuclease solution, incubating in water bath                  at 37 degrees C for 30 minutes, centrifuging for                  10 minutes, removing supernatant, adding 400 mu l                  fluorescent dye PI staining solution, after                  mixing, incubating under dark conditions at 4                  degrees C for 30 minutes, and obtaining flow                  cytometry test results, where x represents mean                  and s represents standard deviation, using                  SPSS19.0 statistical software for paired t test                  and ANOVA, where results are statistically                  different between p less than 0.01 and p less                  than 0.05 and p is significance. Preferred                  Method: The Coomassie Brilliant Blue reagent is                  prepared by weighing 50 mg Coomassie brilliant                  blue G-250, dissolving in 25 ml of 95% ethanol,                  adding another 50 ml 85% phosphoric acid, shaking                  uniformly, and making up to 50 ml with distilled                  water in brown bottle.

TI Performing carbon neutral cell-free conversion of            formaldehyde or methanol to diacids by non oxidative            glycolysis, reverse beta-oxidation or omega oxidation            involves contacting a non-naturally occurring cell with            formaldehyde or methanol.
AU HIMMEL M E
   MALLINSON S J B
   ALAHUHTA P M
   BOMBLE Y J
AE ALLIANCE SUSTAINABLE ENERGY LLC (ALLU-C)
GA 2022C77536
AB    NOVELTY - Performing carbon neutral cell-free conversion                of formaldehyde or methanol to diacids by non                oxidative glycolysis, reverse beta-oxidation or                omega oxidation involves contacting a non-naturally                occurring cell with formaldehyde or methanol.
   USE - Method for performing carbon neutral cell-free                conversion of formaldehyde or methanol to diacids                by non oxidative glycolysis, reverse beta-oxidation                or omega oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method performs carbon neutral cell-free                conversion of formaldehyde or methanol to diacids                by non oxidative glycolysis, reverse beta-oxidation                or omega oxidation, which balances cofactor, uses                cell-free biocatalysis pathway to make diacids from                formaldehyde or methanol that does not use ATP and                does not produces carbon dioxide, conserves carbon                and efficient formaldehyde condensation to enable                conversion of formaldehyde to diacids to further                increase carbon efficiency, significantly reduces                emission during the production of biochemicals due                to large market share of key precursor, uses                ATP-independent conversion of carbon dioxide                derived methanol to balance cofactors and produces                key metabolic intermediate, acetyl-CoA, stabilizes                and encapsulates key metabolic enzyme to shield                chemical toxicity and enables longer operating                lifetime and process scaling, achieves carbon                efficient biochemical production from electricity                and carbon dioxide, simultaneously fixes carbon                dioxide into a versatile and high energy-density                bioproduct and enables greater industrial adoption                of renewable biochemicals by valorizing electricity                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   a method for performing a carbon negative                cell-free conversion of formaldehyde or methanol to                diacids by non oxidative glycolysis, reverse                beta-oxidation or omega oxidation by contacting a                non-naturally occurring cell with formaldehyde or                methanol;
   a non-naturally occurring organism capable of                carbon neutral cell-free conversion of formaldehyde                or methanol to diacids by non oxidative glycolysis,                reverse beta-oxidation or omega oxidation;                and
   a non-naturally occurring organism capable of                the carbon negative cell-free conversion of                formaldehyde or methanol to diacids by non                oxidative glycolysis, reverse beta-oxidation or                omega oxidation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide is                  not produced. The conversion is co-factor                  balanced. The adenosine triphosphate is not                  required for cell-free conversion. The net                  negative carbon dioxide is produced. The                  conversion is co-factor balanced. Preferred                  Components: The co-factor is selected from a                  group consist of nicotinamide adenine                  dinucleotide (NAD) + hydrogen (NADH) and                  NAD+.

A reduction reaction occurs with the Nexmo call control object reaction layer in the purification reactor, thus reducing harmful gases into water and carbon dioxide. The water can be used by the atomizing mechanism, and the carbon dioxide is discharged from the air outlet, thus realizes the decomposition and purification of harmful gases in indoor air.

PN CN113750820-A
TI Preparing mixed matrix composite film based on            polyethyleneimine modified porous montmorillonite in            carbon dioxide separation and purification, involves            using montmorillite, sodium hydroxide and            polyethyleneimine as raw materials.
AU LI J
   ZHANG X
   WANG Y
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2022041020
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mixed matrix composite film based on                polyethyleneimine modified porous montmorillonite,                involves using montmorillite, sodium hydroxide and                polyethyleneimine as raw materials to prepare                porous montmorillonite. The porous montorillonite                is modified by polyethyleneimine with                interconnected two-dimensional porous structure by                electrostatic interaction of polyethyleneimine and                porous montorrillonite. The                polyethyleneimine-modified porous montmorillonite                is added into a polyvinylamine solution to prepare                a uniform membrane casting solution, and coated a                layer of nano-scale membrane on the surface of a                microporous filter membrane, where wet coating                thickness of the film is 10-600 microns.
   USE - Method for preparing mixed matrix composite                film based on polyethyleneimine modified porous                montmorillonite used in carbon dioxide separation                and purification (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to promote CO2 transfer and                improves the CO2 permeation rate and CO2/N2                selectivity of mixed matrix composite film due to                reversible reaction of large number of amino                carriers with CO2 in pores, simple to process and                easy to operate, and also has high carbon dioxide                permeability and selectivity
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for using mixed matrix composite film in carbon                dioxide separation and purification, which                involves: fixing film in a circular film pool,                where effective film area is 1-12.56 cm2, gas test                pressure range on the feed side is 0.1-1.5 MPa, the                pressure on the permeate side is 0.1 MPa, and the                test temperature is 10-80degrees Celsius, and                controlled the flow rate of the feed gas to 40-60                sccm. The downstream side of the membrane cavity is                purged with H2 as the purge gas, the purge gas flow                rate is controlled at 20-40 standard cubic                centimeters per minute (SCCM), and the downstream                side components are detected by gas chromatography.                The humidity of the upstream gas is regulated by a                humidifier, and maintained at 40-100%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The montmorillonite and                  sodium hydroxide solid are ground and mixed                  uniformly, and put the mixture in a tubular                  furnace under the protection of nitrogen at 1-10                  degrees Celsius per minute-1, increased the                  temperature to 200-500degrees Celsius for 0.5-8                  hours to obtain product A. The product A is                  naturally cooled, washed with deionized water for                  3-7 times to remove unreacted sodium hydroxide                  and impurities, and then dried in a drying oven                  at 60-100degrees Celsius for 8-48 hours to obtain                  a dry solid, namely porous montmorillonite as a                  product B. The product B is dispersed in                  deionized water, and carried out ultrasonic                  treatment for 30-240 minutes by using an                  ultrasonic dispersion machine within the                  ultrasonic frequency of 30-60 kHz and the                  ultrasonic temperature of 15-60degrees Celsius to                  form a stable dispersion as solution C. The                  polyethyleneimine is added into deionized water,                  and magnetically stirred for 30-480 minutes with                  rotating speed of 50-400 revolution per minute at                  15-80degrees Celsius to uniformly mix the                  solution to obtain solution D. The solution D is                  added into the solution C for 3-6 times,                  magnetically stirred for 30-480 minutes with                  rotating speed of 50-400 revolution per minute at                  15-80degrees Celsius to obtain a solution E. The                  ultrasonic treatment is carried out for 30-240                  minutes by adopting an ultrasonic dispersion                  machine in the ultrasonic frequency range of                  30-60 kHz and the ultrasonic temperature range of                  15-60degrees Celsius, and continuously reacted                  for 2-24 hours under the magnetic stirring at                  20-80degrees Celsius and the rotating speed of                  50-400 revolution per minute to obtain a crude                  product of the polyethyleneimine modified porous                  montmorillonite as a product F. The product F is                  washed with deionized water for 2-5 times to                  remove unreacted polyethyleneimine and                  impurities, and finally dried in a vacuum oven at                  50-100degrees Celsius for 10-24 hours to obtain                  polyethyleneimine-modified porous montmorillonite                  as a product G. The product G is added into 1-10                  wt.% polyvinylamine solution, magnetically                  stirred for 30-720 min at the rotation speed of                  50-400 revolution per minute at 15-80degrees                  Celsius to prepare a uniform membrane casting                  solution. The membrane casting solution is coated                  on the surface of a microporous filter membrane                  by a coating method. The thickness of a wet                  membrane is controlled to 30-200 microns, and                  placed in a blast drying box with the relative                  humidity of 40-100% at 30-60degrees Celsius for                  drying for 8-24 hours. Preferred Components: The                  montmorillonite is sodium montmorillonite which                  is purchased commercially. The material is                  polysulfone, polyethersulfone, polyvinylidene                  fluoride, polytetrafluoroethylene and cellulose                  acetate. Preferred Conditions: The molecular                  weight of polyethyleneimine is 1000-2000. The                  mass ratio of montmorillonite and sodium                  hydroxide solid is 1:(0.1-5) and a nitrogen flow                  rate of 10-200 mL/min-1. The mass ratio of the                  product B and deionized water in the solution C                  is (0.1-10):(25-250), the mass ratio of the                  polyethyleneimine and the deionized water in the                  solution D is (0.1-5):25-250, and the mass ratio                  of the product B and the polyethyleneimine in the                  solution E is (0.1-5):1. The mass ratio of the                  product G and polyvinylamine is (0.1-10):100. The                  microporous filter membrane is a support                  membrane, the molecular weight cutoff is                  5000-50000.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Gas-making apparatus for gas-making system has source gas supply unit, reaction unit with reactor which accommodates reducing body, and concentration adjustment unit which adjusts concentration of oxygen between supply unit and reactor.
AU NAKAMA Y
   DASANAYAKE A R
AE SEKISUI CHEM IND CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 202135222Q
AB    NOVELTY - A gas-making apparatus (1) comprises source gas supply unit which supplies source gas; reaction unit (4) provided with greater than or equal to 1 reactor (4a, 4b) which is connected to source gas supply unit and accommodates reducing body; and concentration adjustment unit (5) which adjusts concentration of oxygen provided between source gas supply unit and reactor, and is contained in the source gas, where concentration of oxygen contained in the source gas is adjusted to less than or equal to 1 vol.%.
   USE - A gas-making apparatus for gas-making system for manufacturing generated gas containing carbon monoxide by contacting source gas containing carbon dioxide and reducing body for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Generated gas containing carbon monoxide can be efficiently manufactured from source gas containing carbon dioxide. Since concentration adjustment unit is provided, the gas composition of source gas can be made to be able to deviate from range of explosion, and ignition of source gas can be prevented beforehand.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) gas-making system comprising the gas-making apparatus connected to the source gas production unit through the source gas supply unit; and
   (2) gas-making method of generated gas which contains carbon monoxide which involves preparing greater than or equal to 1 reactor which accommodates reducing body and source gas, making source gas and reducing body contact in the reactor, converting carbon dioxide into the carbon monoxide, and recovering mixed gas as generated gas containing monoxide or purifying mixed gas to produce generated gas containing carbon monoxide, where before supplying the reactor, concentration of oxygen contained in the source gas is adjusted to less than or equal to 1 vol.%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the gas-making system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Gas-making apparatus (1)
   Connection unit (2)
   Reaction unit (4)
   Reactors (4a, 4b)
   Concentration adjustment unit (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: Concentration adjustment unit has deoxidation apparatus from which oxygen contained in the source gas is removed. Reactor accommodates reducing body that produces carbon monoxide by reductive reaction of carbon dioxide that arises by contact with source gas, and greater than or equal to 1 portion of oxygen atom separated from carbon dioxide is reduced. Reactor contains multiple reactors which accommodate the reducing agent containing a metal oxide which reduces carbon dioxide as reducing body, and apparatus further has reducing-gas supply unit which supplies reducing gas containing reducing substance which reduces further the reducing agent oxidized by the contact with carbon dioxide, where each of the reactor is connected to source gas supply unit and/or reducing-gas supply unit, and switching of source gas and reducing gas supplied to each of reactor is possible or reducing agent can be moved among the reactors. Apparatus further has gas merging unit which makes gas after passing the reactor merge to produce mixed gas. Apparatus further has gas line connected to each of the reactor and mutually independent of the gas line. Metal oxide is selected from metallic element which belongs to groups 3-12. Source gas production unit which produces source gas containing carbon dioxide. Preferred Process: Gas-making method includes processing gas after passing each reactor mutually independently.

TI Synthesizing functionalized            metalloporphyrin/quaternary phosphonium salt            double-catalytic system-based cyclic carbonate,            comprises e.g. performing cycloaddition reaction of            epoxide and carbon dioxide with functionalized            metalloporphyrin.
AU XIONG Y
   WANG K
   LIU Y
   DAI Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG SCI-TECH (UZST-C)
GA 202131829U
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing functionalized                metalloporphyrin/quaternary phosphonium salt                double-catalytic system-based cyclic carbonate,                comprises carrying out cycloaddition reaction using                epoxide and carbon dioxide as reaction substrate,                functionalized metalloporphyrin as main catalyst                and quaternary phosphonium salt as co-catalyst at                25-80 degrees C for 4-24 hours to obtain final                product, where the functionalized metalloporphyrin                is (I), and the quaternary phosphonium salt is                (II).
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing                functionalized metalloporphyrin/quaternary                phosphonium salt double-catalytic system-based                cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: combines functionalized                metalloporphyrin and quaternary phosphonium salt as                dual catalytic system to achieve the purpose of                synergistically catalyzing the cycloaddition                reaction of epoxide and carbon dioxide to                synthesize cyclic carbonate under mild conditions;                utilizes different main catalyst functionalized                metal porphyrin (MTRPP) and quaternary phosphonium                salt synergistic catalysis, which have excellent                catalytic activity under mild conditions; and                utilizes functionalized metalloporphyrin (CoClTPP),                which has highest catalytic activity because of its                metal center of cobalt(III) ion with strongest                Lewis acidity and metal frame of 5, 10, 15,                20-tetrakis(tetraphenylporphyrin) (TPP) with least                steric hindrance, functionalized metalloporphyrin                (CoClTPP)/hexyl quaternary phosphonium salt                (Ph3HexPBr) dual catalytic system, which exhibits                excellent catalytic activity in the cycloaddition                reaction of epichlorohydrin to cyclocarbonate under                normal temperature and pressure conditions, with                high conversion rate and turnover frequency value,                and has excellent catalytic activity for other                epoxides under mild conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing functionalized                metalloporphyrin/quaternary phosphonium salt                double-catalytic system-based cyclic carbonate,                comprises carrying out cycloaddition reaction using                epoxide and carbon dioxide as reaction substrate,                functionalized metalloporphyrin as main catalyst                and quaternary phosphonium salt as co-catalyst at                25-80 degrees C for 4-24 hours to obtain final                product, where the functionalized metalloporphyrin                is of formula (I), and the quaternary phosphonium                salt is of formula (II).
   R = H, COOH, OCH3 or CH=CH2;
   M = Zn, Co or Ni;
   X = Cl;
   Ra = phenyl or butyl;
   R1 = H, CH3, or OH;
   X1 = Cl, Br or I; and
   n = 1-6.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio                  between main catalyst functionalized                  metalloporphyrin and epoxide is 1:650-1:6600. The                  molar ratio between co-catalyst quaternary                  phosphonium salt and epoxide is 1:66-1:132. The                  epoxide includes epichlorohydrin, styrene oxide,                  butylene oxide, allyl glycidyl ether, butyl                  glycidyl ether, phenyl glycidyl ether, or                  3-glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane. The                  functionalized porphyrin includes                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis(tetraphenylporphyrin),                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis(tetracarboxyphenylporphyrin),                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis(tetravinylphenylporphyrin)                  (sic) or                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis(tetramethoxyphenylporphyrin).                  The metal salt is cobalt chloride hexahydrate,                  zinc acetate dihydrate or nickel acetate                  tetrahydrate. The halogenated alkane includes                  bromoethane, 2-bromoethanol, bromobutane,                  chlorobutane, iodobutane, bromohexane,                  bromooctane, bromododecane, 1,2-dibromohexane, or                  benzyl bromide. Preferred Method: The pressure of                  carbon dioxide in cycloaddition reaction is 1                  atm. The preparation method of functionalized                  metalloporphyrin comprises reacting using                  functionalized porphyrin and metal salt as                  reaction substrates, dimethylformamide as solvent                  at 140-150 degrees C for 4-12 hours under nirogen                  atmosphere, adding hydrochloric acid to acidify,                  then vacuum distilling, suction filtering,                  washing and drying to obtain functionalized                  metalloporphyrin, where the molar ratio between                  functionalized porphyrin and metal salt is                  1:5-1:10. The preparation method of quaternary                  phosphonium salt comprises dissolving                  triphenylphosphine or tributylphosphine and                  halogenated alkanes in toluene, stirring and                  refluxing at 90-110 degrees C for 12-24 hours                  under nitrogen atmosphere, then rotary                  evaporating to remove the solvent, then adding                  into diethyl ether dropwise to precipitate white                  solid, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain                  quaternary phosphonium salt, where the molar                  ratio between triphenylphosphine or                  tributylphosphine and halogenated alkane is                  0.8:1-2.5:1.

PT P
PN US10876057-B1; WO2021077123-A1; AU2020366533-A1; CA3154818-A1; CN114901784-A; BR112022007135-A2
TI Process for obtaining energy from waste, involves            drying and carrying out anaerobic pyrolysis of waste to            produce syngas, char and bio-oil, and anaerobic            pyrolysis of syngas to produce gas with increased            hydrogen content and oil.
AU GEINOZ F I
   CUENI M
   YAKOB K
   GEINOZ F
   JACOB K
AE MED ENERGY INC (MEDE-Non-standard)
   MED ENERGY INC (MEDE-Non-standard)
   MED ENERGY INC (MEDE-Non-standard)
GA 202100605X
AB    NOVELTY - Process for obtaining energy from waste                involves drying and carrying out anaerobic                pyrolysis of the waste at 300-600 degrees C to                produce syngas, char, and bio-oil. Anaerobic                pyrolysis of the syngas is carried out in the                presence of a portion of the bio-oil, char,                additional carbon dioxide, and water at 600-900                degrees C to produce a gas with increased hydrogen                content and a product oil. The anaerobic pyrolysis                of the produced gas and product oil is carried out                in the presence of additional water and additional                carbon monoxide at 800-1200 degrees C and about 20                atmospheric pressure to further increase the                hydrogen content of the gas. Hydrogen, carbon                dioxide and carbon monoxide are separated from the                produced gas. The separated carbon monoxide is used                as the additional carbon monoxide in pyrolysis of                the syngas. The separated carbon dioxide is used as                the additional carbon dioxide in pyrolysis of the                produced gas.
   USE - Process for obtaining energy e.g. bio-oil used                as liquid fuel or feedstock from waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The process produces energy with high                efficiency and minimal emissions because a                combination of processes is provided and a variety                of inputs such as mixed municipal waste, sewage,                used tires and oils, and agricultural and                food-processing wastes are used. The use of                induction heating of the reactors is rapid,                efficient, and enables high temperatures with                accurate control. The inefficiencies and                environmental disadvantages of the existing carbon                combustion methods are avoided because this process                relies on the oxidation of hydrogen rather than                carbon. The process avoids the capital investment                and maintenance costs of the fuel cell, and                contributes to land reclamation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                medium and high temperature pyrolysis stages of the                process.
   Medium-pressure pyrolyzer (21)
   Port (25)
   Hydrogen rich syngas (28)
   Compressor (29)
   High pressure pyrolyzer (30)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: A fuel cell e.g. solid                  oxide fuel cell (SOFC) is fuelled with the                  produced gas.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

The second desulfurization tower (7) is fixedly installed on the top of the second base, and the right side of the second desulfurization tower is fixedly installed with the first desulfurization tower. The mixture of limestone slurry and calcium carbide slag slurry entering from the lime slurry pipe is then combined with The flue gas undergoes a secondary reaction to achieve a sufficient desulfurization effect. This structure effectively improves the desulfurization efficiency. The protective cover facilitates the filtration of the mixture of limestone slurry and calcium carbide slag slurry, avoids transported to the inside of the slurry guide pipe by the screw conveyor shaft, and overcomes problem of inconvenient discharge.

PN CN111393746-A
TI Polypropylene/polyolefin elastomer supercritical foam composition material comprises polypropylene, polyolefin elastomer, maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene, maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene modifier and nucleating agent.
AU DING Y
   YANG W
   DING X
   LV F
   LIN Q
AE FUJIAN ANDAFU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202066697S
AB    NOVELTY - Polypropylene/polyolefin elastomer supercritical foam composition material comprises 140-165 pts. wt. polypropylene, 35-42 pts. wt. polyolefin elastomer, 10-14 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene, 10-14 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene modifier, 2-3 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 8-12 pts. wt. mesoporous silica, 1.5-2 pts. wt. dispersant, and 0.8-1.2 pts. wt. crosslinking agent.
   USE - Used as polypropylene/polyolefin elastomer supercritical foam composition material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition material: can optimizes the content of each raw material, can play to their respective advantages, and complement each other, mutual improvement, improves product quality, high tensile strength, good mechanical properties, low compression set rate, has good flexibility, high expansion ratio, uniform pores, moderate size, dense, which can complete the cell structure, good sealing effect, and good foaming effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the supercritical foamed composition material, comprising (i) adding polypropylene, polyolefin elastomer, maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene, maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene modifier, nucleating agent, mesoporous silica, dispersant and crosslinking agent; (ii) mixing polypropylene, polyolefin elastomer, maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene, maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene modifier, mesoporous silica, and dispersant, sending to the internal mixer, carrying out banbury blending at a temperature of 120-125 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, then adding crosslinking agent and nucleating agent at a temperature of 125-130 degrees C for banburying and blending until the components are uniformly mixed to obtain a banbury blend; (iii) using a tablet press to press the banbury blend into a plate, and cutting; and (iv) placing the plate prepared in step (iii) into an oven and preheating to 120-125 degrees C, and then placing the preheated foaming mother board into the high temperature mold, and introducing carbon dioxide gas, when the carbon dioxide gas is saturated in the sheet, rapidly releasing the carbon dioxide gas in the mold, where the temperature of the high-temperature mold is 130-136 degrees C, and the pressure of carbon dioxide gas in the mold is 15-17 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition material comprises 153 pts. wt. polypropylene, 38.5 pts. wt. polyolefin elastomer, 12 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene, 12 pts. wt. maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene modifier, 2.5 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 10 pts. wt. mesoporous silica, 1.8 pts. wt. dispersant, and 01 pts. wt. crosslinking agent. Preferred Components: The polyolefin elastomer is ethylene-butene copolymer and/or ethylene-octene copolymer. The polyolefin elastomer is a mixture of ethylene-butene copolymer and ethylene-octene copolymer at the mass ratio of 1:(0.4-0.5). The maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene modifier is polypropylene grafted copolymer with silicon group and prepared by reaction of epoxy-containing polysiloxane and maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene at a mass ratio of 1:(2.4-3.2). The mesoporous silica has a pore diameter of 5-10 nm and a specific surface area of 500-640 m2/g. The nucleating agent is nano calcium carbonate and the dispersing agent is dispersing agent HT-5020. The crosslinking agent is an organic peroxide crosslinking agent. The organic peroxide crosslinking agent is at least one of tert-butyl hydroperoxide, dicumyl peroxide, and benzoyl peroxide.

PN US2020003366-A1
TI Carbon-dioxide (CO2) safety interlock fill system for automatically leak checking of fill manifold, has controller that receives signal from pressure transducer during predetermined time of leak check or pressurization of fill manifold.
AU SONG X
   CECULA S M
AE SONG X (SONG-Individual)
   CECULA S M (CECU-Individual)
GA 202001555S
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has an onsite CO2 source. The onsite CO2 source comprises a source vessel containing liquefied CO2 and vaporized CO2 in a headspace of the source vessel. A controller receives signals corresponding to real-time pressure measurement from the pressure transducer during the predetermined time period of the leak check and/or the pressurization of the fill manifold. The controller allows the subsequent filling operation when the real-time pressure measurement changes in pressure by an amount that is less than the first set point of the unallowable reduction in pressure of the vaporized CO2 in the manifold, and the real-time pressure measurement is equal to or above the predetermined lower pressure at which dry ice forms, and the real-time pressure measurement is equal to or lower than the predetermined upper pressure at which reversible flow of CO2 vapor occurs from the container into the fill manifold.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide (CO2) safety interlock fill system for performing pre-fill integrity checks for automatically leak checking of fill manifold and pressurizing fill manifold. Van also be used for delivery of carbon dioxide from multiple containers to an-end-user or customer point of use for variety of applications e.g. beverage dispensing applications, such as carbonated beverage dispenser.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces the amount of carbon dioxide vented to the atmosphere due to increase of temperature, and thus resulting in less CO2 product waste, less cost to both the customer or end-user and less potential hazards.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for performing pre-fill integrity checks for automatically leak checking a fill manifold and pressurizing the fill manifold.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of two cylinder systems for dispensing CO2 vapor to an end-user or customer.

PN CN110396700-A
TI Application of tin oxide catalyst prepared by mixing zinc chloride and citric acid monohydrate, dissolving tin chloride pentahydrate in ethanol, mixing and calcining used in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid.
AU LIU L
   ZHANG X
   CHEN Z
   MU K
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 201992710N
AB    NOVELTY - Application of tin oxide catalyst prepared by mixing zinc chloride and citric acid monohydrate in deionized water to form solution (A), dissolving tin chloride pentahydrate and citric acid monohydrate in ethanol to form solution (B), mixing solution (A) and solution (B), adding to alkaline solution to obtain white precipitate, centrifuging, drying to obtain zinc hydroxystannate precursor, calcining zinc hydroxystannate precursor to convert into zinc stannate/stannic oxide, cooling, etching in aqueous solution of nitric acid, centrifugal washing, drying to obtain three-dimensional stannic oxide cube or placing zinc hydroxystannate precursor, thioacetamide and EDTA in beaker containing deionized water, stirring to completely dissolve solid powder, transferring to PTFE lined stainless steel reactor, placing in oven for hydrothermal reaction, centrifuging, drying to obtain yellow tin sulfide powder and calcining used in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid is claimed.
   USE - Application of tin oxide catalyst in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Application of tin oxide catalyst prepared by mixing zinc chloride and citric acid monohydrate in molar ratio of 1:1 in deionized water to form solution (A), dissolving tin chloride pentahydrate and citric acid monohydrate in ethanol in molar ratio of 1:1 to form solution (B), mixing solution (A) and solution (B), magnetically stirring, adding to alkaline solution, stirring to obtain white precipitate, centrifuging, drying to obtain zinc hydroxystannate precursor, calcining 50-100 mg zinc hydroxystannate precursor to convert it into zinc stannate/stannic oxide, cooling to room temperature, etching obtained zinc stannate/stannic oxide in aqueous solution of nitric acid, centrifugal washing, drying to obtain three-dimensional stannic oxide cube with microporous structure modification or placing zinc hydroxystannate precursor, thioacetamide and EDTA in beaker containing deionized water, stirring to completely dissolve solid powder, transferring to PTFE lined stainless steel reactor, placing in oven for hydrothermal reaction, centrifuging mixture, drying to obtain yellow tin sulfide powder and calcining used in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid is claimed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of 4 mmol zinc chloride and 4 mmol citric acid monohydrate is 1:1. The volume of deionized water used is 100-200 mL, preferably 140mL. The mass of tin chloride pentahydrate is 4 mmol. The mass of zinc stannate/stannic oxide is 50-100 mg, preferably 50 mg. The etching solution is 5-8 M, preferably 8M 5 mL aqueous solution of nitric acid. The volume of deionized water used is 30-40 mL, preferably 35 mL. The molar ratio of zinc hydroxystannate, EDTA and thioacetamide is (0.3-0.5):(1.5-2):(0.5-1), preferably 0.3:1.8:0.8. The amount of the electrically reduced carbon dioxide catalytic material used is 0.1-3 mg/cm2, preferably 1 mg/cm2. The ratio of electroreduction carbon dioxide catalytic material to isopropanol is (4-10) mg:(0.3-0.5) mL, preferably 6 mg:0.35 mL. The electrolyte is 0.5 M 25 mL potassium hydrogen carbonate. Preferred Condition: The calcination is carried out at 500-850 degrees C, preferably 500 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, preferably 2 hours. The etching time is 30 minutes to 12 hours, preferably 2 hours. The stirring treatment time is 1-3 hours. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 180-220 degrees C, preferably 220 degrees C for 1-3 hours, preferably 3 hours. The calcination is carried out at 500-850 degrees C, preferably 500 degrees C for 30 minutes to 2 hours, preferably 2 hours. The molar concentration of reaction raw material carbon dioxide is 99.99%, the aeration flow rate is 30 cm3/minute, the aeration time is 10-30 minutes, preferably 30 minutes, and the reaction temperature is room temperature of 25 degrees C. Preferred Process: The preparation preferably involves adding EDTA, stirring at room temperature for 0.5-1 hour to fully dissolve and adding thioacetamide. The electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide involves loading gas diffusion electrode with a catalytic material tin oxide catalyst. The preparation method of gas diffusion electrode involves placing synthetic electrochemically reduced carbon dioxide catalytic material in a mortar, grinding, ultrasonically dispersing in an isopropyl alcohol solution, adding 5 wt.% solution of perfluorosulfonic acid resin Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) to obtain a mixed solution, applying the obtained mixed solution to the gas diffusion electrode and drying. The electrochemical reduction reaction involves placing tin oxide catalyst in a solution containing isopropyl alcohol and a perfluorosulfonic acid resin Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer), ultrasonically dispersing, evenly spreading on cut carbon cloth, naturally drying, dried carbon cloth is used as a working electrode, silver chloride is placed as a reference electrode in the chamber (A) of the H-type electrolytic cell, platinum sheet is placed in the chamber (B) as a counter electrode and chambers (A) and (B) contain potassium hydrogen carbonate electrolyte, introducing chamber (A) with carbon dioxide, reacting for 30 minutes to form formic acid from carbon dioxide by electrocatalytic reduction and monitoring the formic acid concentration and reaction tail gas composition in chamber (A) electrolyte. Preferred Component: The gas diffusion electrode is carbon paper, carbon cloth or carbon felt, preferably carbon cloth.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume of ethanol used is 15-30 mL, preferably 20 mL. The volume ratio of the 5 wt.% perfluorosulfonic acid resin Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution to isopropanol is (1:5)-(1:10), preferably 1:5, more preferably 0.07 mL:0.35 mL.

   USE - Energy-saving type carbon dioxide trapping                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is simple in structure, and reduces                the regenerative energy consumption, the size of                the regeneration tower, the consumption of                demineralized water and the cost of equipment                manufacturing and system maintenance, transmits                part of the condensate of the reflux tank in the                upper stage of the regeneration tower to the                absorption tower washing device for reuse and                prevents the diffusion of the absorbent                vapor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                energy-saving type carbon dioxide trapping                method.
