PT P
PN CN109734128-A
TI Preparing zirconium tetrachloride by-product from chlorination of zircon sand comprises e.g. dry grinding and crushing zircon sand, drying, adding dried zircon sand to a boiling chlorination furnace, and adding carbon tetrachloride.
AU ZHENG X
AE JINZHOU XURI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JINZ-Non-standard)
GA 201944639J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing zirconium tetrachloride by-product from chlorination of zircon sand comprises e.g. (i) dry grinding and crushing zircon sand, (ii) drying the crushed zircon sand, (iii) adding dried zircon sand to a boiling chlorination furnace and adding carbon tetrachloride to the bottom of the furnace for chlorination, (iv) reacting to generate zirconium(IV) chloride and silicon tetrachloride gases in a fluidized bed, performing gas-solid separation by chlorination furnace top dust collector, spraying the separated gas by molten salt spray, further condensing into the zirconium tetrachloride condenser to obtain solid zirconium(IV) chloride non-condensable gas through the gas-solid separation device, spraying the silicon tetrachloride liquid, and neutralizing the remaining gas by the solid base tower to obtain liquid carbon dioxide by carbon dioxide adsorption, desorption, compression and condensation, and (v) discharging the exhaust gas after being washed by the elution tower.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing zirconium tetrachloride by-product from chlorination of zircon sand.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing zirconium tetrachloride by-product from chlorination of zircon sand comprises (i) dry grinding and crushing zircon sand, (ii) drying the crushed zircon sand, (iii) adding dried zircon sand to a boiling chlorination furnace and adding carbon tetrachloride to the bottom of the furnace for chlorination, (iv) reacting to generate zirconium(IV) chloride and silicon tetrachloride gases in a fluidized bed, performing gas-solid separation by chlorination furnace top dust collector, where the collected dust returns to the furnace to continue the reaction, spraying the separated gas by molten salt spray, further condensing into the zirconium tetrachloride condenser to obtain solid zirconium(IV) chloride non-condensable gas through the gas-solid separation device, spraying the silicon tetrachloride liquid, where silicon tetrachloride is absorbed, and neutralizing the remaining gas by the solid base tower to obtain liquid carbon dioxide by carbon dioxide adsorption, desorption, compression and condensation, and (v) discharging the exhaust gas after being washed by the elution tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zircon sand has a particle size of -80 to 200 mesh after crushing. The inner diameter of the reaction zone of the chlorination furnace is 300-1300 mm. The molten salt is sodium chloride, potassium chloride, and magnesium chloride in the weight ratio of 1:1:0.2-0.5. Preferred Method: When the temperature of the reaction zone of the boiling chlorination furnace reaches 750-999 degrees C, zircon sand is added, where the reaction temperature of the boiling chlorination furnace reaction section is 750-999 degrees C. The zircon sand drying temperature is 120-150 degrees C. The zircon sand is fed into the chlorination furnace at a feed rate of 125-450 kg/hour. The feed rate of the carbon tetrachloride to the chlorination furnace is 108-388 kg/hour. The boiling fluidized bed pressure is 0.0015-0.008 MPa.

PN WO2019046430-A1; US2020182095-A1; US10954825-B2; US2021270152-A1; US11448101-B2
TI Method for producing work for power generation in            industrial applications, involves increasing pressure            of working fluid, where output fluid is refrigerated,            which receives from outlet of ejector to condense vapor            phase portion.
AU CIRUCCI J F
   LACKNER K S
AE UNIV ARIZONA STATE (UYAS-C)
   UNIV ARIZONA STATE (UYAS-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
GA 2019229441
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves increasing the pressure of                a working fluid (102) with a pressurization device                (104). A first pressure (106) is equal to a triple                point pressure of the working fluid. A second                pressure (108) is above the triple point pressure                of the working fluid, which is higher than the                first pressure. The working fluid with a first heat                exchanger (110) is heated until it is substantially                one of a vapor and a supercritical fluid. A third                pressure (120) is below the triple point pressure                of the working fluid, which is extracted mechanical                work (114) by expanding a first portion (118) of                the heated working fluid. The fourth pressure (134)                is equal to the triple point pressure of the                working fluid, which increases the pressure of the                expanded working fluid (132). A output fluid (136)                is refrigerated, which is received from an outlet                (138) of the ejector (126) to condense a vapor                phase portion of the output fluid into a liquid                phase.
   USE - Method for producing mechanical work using                carbon dioxide for power generation in industrial                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-pressure and low-pressure end of the                cycle can comfortably straddle the triple point                pressure due to the use of ejectors. Improves                expansion energy output or enable the capture and                compression of low-pressure carbon dioxide for                carbon capture, use and storage. Carbon dioxide                requires only a fraction of the energy needed to                pressurize carbon dioxide as a vapor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for pressurizing a source                fluid;
   (2) a system for producing work; and
   (3) a system for pressurizing a source                fluid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic views of a                system.
   Working fluid (102)
   Pressurization device (104)
   First pressure (106)
   Second pressure (108)
   First heat exchanger (110)
   Extracted mechanical work (114)
   First portion (118)
   Third pressure (120)
   Ejector (126)
   Expanded working fluid (132)
   Fourth pressure (134)
   Output fluid (136)
   Outlet (138)

TI Carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant low alloy steel material useful for preparing carbon capture/sealed product including marine hose, comprises carbon, silicon, manganese, chromium, molybdenum, aluminum, phosphorus, sulfur and iron.
AU LIU Z
   GU Z
   WANG J
   WANG Y
AE UNIV JIANGSU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJG-C)
GA 201919175H
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant low alloy steel material comprises 0.03-0.15 wt.% carbon, 0.15- 0.6 wt.% silicon, 0.6-1.3 wt.% manganese, 0.7-2 wt.% chromium, 0.2-0.9 wt.% molybdenum, 0.01- 0.04 wt.% aluminum, less than or equal to 0.01 wt.% phosphorus, less than or equal to 0.005 wt.% sulfur and iron (balance amount), where microstructure is tempered martensite.
   USE - The alloy steel material is useful for preparing carbon capture/sealed product including marine hose (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The alloy steel material: has low cost and simple preparation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant low alloy steel material comprising (i) smelting and continuous casting of raw materials to obtain primary blank, (ii) heating the blank at 1000-1200 degrees C for 40-70 minutes, and ensuring that the temperature difference between the inside and outside of the blank is less than 40 degrees C, (iii) subjected the blank to high-speed wire hot rolling production, where starting rolling temperature is 1050-1130 degrees C, final rolling temperature is 880-980 degrees C, then accelerating the water to 820-860 degrees C with accelerated cooling, and air cooling to 200-400 degrees C at a rate of 1-5 degrees C/second to obtain wire having a circular cross section, and size is phi 8-12 mm, (iv) removing the iron oxide from the wire, and cold forming to obtain profiled profile steel, and (v) heat-treating the profiled profile steel at 850-910 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, quenching, and tempering at 550-700 degrees C for 30-60 minutes to obtain product; and
   (2) use of carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant low alloy steel material for preparing carbon capture/sealed product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide corrosion-resistant low alloy steel material comprises 0.07 wt.% carbon, 0.24 wt.% silicon, 0.84 wt.% manganese, 1.1 wt.% chromium, 0.27 wt.% molybdenum, 0.02 wt.% aluminum, 0.002 wt.% phosphorus, 0.001 wt.% sulfur and iron (balance amount). The corrosive environment is water-saturated carbon dioxide environment or carbon dioxide saturated water environment.

PN US2018171421-A1; KR2018073895-A; KR2018087202-A; KR1946200-B1; KR1953443-B1; US10526669-B2
TI Recovery of carbonate from steel slag generated during steel making processes, involves mixing steel slag and acidic solvent, mixing ligands with mixture to form complex compound mixture, and reacting complex compound with carbon dioxide.
AU YOU J Y
   KOO J H
   SHIN K H
   KIM Y S
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
   DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 2018486029
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of carbonate from steel slag involves mixing steel slag and an acidic solvent to produce a mixture (M1) comprising metal ions, separating the mixture (M1) into a mixture (M2) comprising the metal ions and residues by allowing the mixture (M1) to pass through a filter (F1), inserting and mixing ligands into and with the mixture (M2) to produce a mixture (M3) comprising a complex compound produced by reacting metal ions with the ligands, separating the complex compound from the mixture (M3) by electrochemical method, reacting the separated complex compound with carbon dioxide to produce carbonate, and separating the produced carbonate.
   USE - Recovery of carbonate e.g. calcium carbonate from steel slag (claimed) generated during steel making processes in industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases carbonate extraction efficiency by reusing unreacted metal ions and acidic solvent and reduces generation of wastes, and can efficiently recycle steel slag and use carbon dioxide gas without emission to atmosphere. The capacitive deionizer comprises carbon fiber electrodes with excellent chemical resistance and adsorbability, and high heat resistance and shock resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system, which consists of a metal ion extraction unit configured to mix steel slag and acidic solvent to produce the mixture (M1), a separation unit configured to separate the mixture (M1) into the mixture (M2) and residues, a complex compound production unit configured to insert and mix ligands with the mixture (M2) to produce the complex compound, a complex compound separation unit configured to separate the complex compound, a carbonation process unit configured to react the complex compound with carbon dioxide to produce carbonate, and a carbonate separation unit configured to separate the carbonate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the capacitive deionizer used for electrochemical process.
   Cathode (910)
   Anode (920)
   Cation exchange membrane (930)
   Anion exchange membrane (940)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The complex compound separation unit comprises a recovery unit (U1) configured to recover the unreacted charged ligands and metal ions from the single capacitive deionizer or multistage capacitive deionizer and to supply the recovered unreacted charged ligands and metal ions to the metal ion extraction unit, and a recovery unit (U2) configured to recover the unreacted charged ligands from the multistage capacitive deionizer and to supply the recovered unreacted charged ligands to the complex compound production unit. The separation unit comprises the filter (F1) having pore diameter of 30-50 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The complex compound with no charge is separated from unreacted charged ligands and unreacted charged metal ions by electrochemical method using a single capacitive deionizer or multistage capacitive deionizer, and the unreacted charged ligands and the unreacted charged metal ions are recovered and supplied to the mixing processes. The unreacted charged metal ions and ligands are bonded to a pair of electrodes of the single capacitive deionizer and the acidic solvent flows to the deionizer so that the unreacted charged ligands and metal ions move to the acidic solvent by adjusting potentials of the electrodes and the acidic solvent is supplied to the step of mixing steel slag and acidic solvent, or the unreacted charged ligands are bonded to an anode of the multistage capacitive deionizer and the acidic solvent flows to the multistage capacitive deionizer so that the unreacted charged ligands move to the acidic solvent by changing the anode (920) to a cathode (910), and the acidic solvent comprising the recovered unreacted charged ligands is supplied to the step of mixing ligands with the mixture (M2), or the unreacted charged metal ions are bonded to a cathode of the multistage capacitive deionizer and the acidic solvent flows to the multistage capacitive deionizer so that the unreacted charged metal ions move to the acidic solvent by changing the cathode to an anode and the acidic solvent comprising the recovered unreacted charged metal ions is supplied to the step of mixing steel slag and acidic solvent. Preferred Components: The acidic solvent is one or more chosen from nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrobromide acid, hydroiodide acid, perchloric acid, and phosphoric acid. The metal ions of the mixture (M1) are calcium ions.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ligands are one or more chosen from acetic acid of formula: CH3COOH (I), carboxyglutamic acid of formula: C6H9NO6 (II), nitrobenzene of formula: C6H5NO2 (III), iminodiacetic acid of formula: HN(CH2CO2H)2 (IV), citrate3+, glutamate+, and oxalic acid of formula: C2H2O4 (V).

The zirconium component is supported on the catalyst carrier by drying or baking process and drying process, and the cobalt component is then supported on the catalyst carrier by reduction or baking process and reduction process. The hydrocarbons are produced by liquid phase reaction using a slurry bed.

AE RELIANCE IND LTD (RELN-C)
GA 2018121487
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing biodegradable polycarbonate polymer involves (i) performing epoxidation of at least one vegetable oil to obtain an epoxide of vegetable oil and reacting epoxide with carbon dioxide (CO2) in presence of catalyst to form cyclic carbonate, and (ii) performing ring opening polymerization (ROP) on cyclic carbonate to obtain mixture containing crude polymer and precipitating biodegradable polycarbonate polymer from mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing biodegradable polycarbonate polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize biodegradable polycarbonate polymer, which is environment-friendly, non-toxic to human, and has high functionality, high polarity and flowability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The vegetable oil is castor oil. The epoxide is reacted with carbon dioxide at a temperature of 90-140 degrees C for 2-8 hours with pressure of 30-50 bars and in presence of predetermined amount of at least one polar solvent. The polar solvent is tetrahydrofuran. The catalyst is chosen from zinc, nickel and cobalt based salen complex. The ROP is performed in presence of at least one polar solvent in predetermined amounts and stannous octoate catalyst at 60-120 degrees C for 3-10 hours. The stannous octoate catalyst is tin octanoate.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The eutectic solvent                  (DES) is a mixture of a hydrogen bond acceptor                  (HBA) and a hydrogen bond donor (HBD) in a molar                  ratio of 1:3-10. The composition further                  comprises a cosolvent. The cosolvent is added in                  an amount of 5-20% of the total molar ratio of                  the eutectic solvent (DES). The cosolvent is an                  alcohol including an aliphatic primary alcohol,                  aromatic alcohol, secondary alcohol, or tertiary                  alcohol including ethanol, ketones including                  acetone, methyl ethyl ketone, methyl isobutyl                  ketone or cyclohexanone, aldehydes including                  formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, and/or                  glutaraldehyde.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN WO2021239955-A1
TI Synthesis of urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide by stripping process involves forming urea in high-pressure section comprising reaction environment, stripping environment and condensation environment, to obtain reaction effluent which is subjected to stripping process in stripping environment.
AU MARRONE L
   BENEDETTI A
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2021D9373B
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide by stripping process involves forming urea in a high-pressure section comprising a reaction environment, a stripping environment and a condensation environment, to obtain a reaction effluent which is subjected to a stripping process in the stripping environment to obtain a urea-containing solution (4) and a gaseous phase (8) containing unconverted gaseous ammonia and carbon dioxide removed from the solution. The gaseous phase obtained in the stripping process is subjected to condensation in the condensation environment to obtain a condensate (9) which is sent to the reaction environment. The urea-containing solution is processed in a recovery section where a urea solution (5) and a recovery solution (6) containing ammonium carbamate are produced, and the recovery solution is sent back to the high-pressure section where it is processed for recovering the unconverted matter contained.
   USE - Synthesis of urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide by stripping process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates or reduces the drawbacks caused by the undesirable introduction of water in the synthesis section, due to the recycle of the aqueous solution of carbamate from a recovery section, and increases capacity and/or reduces the energy consumption of urea stripping process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide by stripping process involves forming urea in a high-pressure section comprising a reaction environment, a stripping environment and a condensation environment, to obtain a reaction effluent which is subjected to a stripping process in the stripping environment to obtain a urea-containing solution and a gaseous phase (8) containing unconverted gaseous ammonia and carbon dioxide removed from the solution. The gaseous phase obtained in the stripping process is subjected to condensation in the condensation environment to obtain a condensate (9) which is sent to the reaction environment. The urea-containing solution is processed in a recovery section where a urea solution (5) and a recovery solution (6) containing ammonium carbamate are produced, and the recovery solution is sent back to the high-pressure section where it is processed for recovering the unconverted matter contained. The processing of the recovery solution in the high-pressure section involves converting ammonium carbamate to urea in a carbamate conversion environment, which is separated from reaction environment, stripping environment and condensation environment. The urea-containing solution (7) obtained from carbamate conversion environment is subjected to the stripping process. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a plant for the synthesis of urea, which has a high-pressure synthesis section comprising a urea synthesis reactor, a stripping section and a condenser which operate at a urea synthesis pressure, at least one recovery section arranged to operate at a pressure lower than the synthesis section and arranged to process a urea containing solution obtained in the stripping section to obtain a urea solution and a recovery solution containing ammonium carbamate. The high-pressure synthesis section comprises a separate carbamate conversion reactor arranged to convert ammonium carbamate contained in the recovery solution to urea, and a line arranged to feed the urea-containing effluent of separate carbamate conversion reactor to the stripping section; and
   (2) revamping of plant for the synthesis of urea, which involves installing carbamate conversion reactor in the high-pressure section, and the reactor which is arranged to convert ammonium carbamate contained in the recovery solution to urea, and installing line which is arranged to feed the urea-containing effluent of the carbamate conversion reactor to stripping section.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the self-stripping process.
   Fresh ammonia feed (1)
   Carbon dioxide feed (2)
   Reaction effluent (3)
   Stripped urea solution (4)
   Purified urea solution (5)
   Carbamate-containing recovery solution (6)
   Urea-containing solution (7)
   Overhead stripper vapor phase (8)
   Condensate recycled to the primary reactor (9)
   Carbamate conversion reactor (CR)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbamate conversion environment is hosted in a carbamate conversion reactor (CR) with a pressure vessel separate from other pressure vessels hosting the reaction environment, the stripping environment and the condensation environment. The urea-containing solution obtained in the carbamate conversion environment is subjected to stripping together with reaction effluent (3) or separately. The processing of the recovery solution in the high-pressure section involves scrubbing a gaseous phase extracted from the synthesis section, prior to the conversion of ammonium carbamate into urea, using the recovery solution as a scrubbing medium. The carbamate conversion environment is heated indirectly with a heating medium which does not mix with the carbamate-containing recovery solution. The carbamate conversion environment is heated directly by vapors taken from the reaction environment and the vapors come into contact with the carbamate-containing recovery solution and are absorbed in the liquid phase in the carbamate conversion environment. The condensation process in the condensation environment is performed in the presence of a portion of a fresh carbon dioxide feed. A portion of a fresh ammonia feed (1) is sent to the carbamate conversion environment, preferably after a preheating of portion of ammonia feed. The reaction environment is hosted in at least one primary reactor, the stripping environment is hosted in at least one high-pressure stripper, the condensation environment is hosted in at least one high-pressure condenser, and the carbamate conversion environment is hosted in at least one secondary reactor. The full amount of recovery solution is sent to the carbamate conversion environment. The revamping of the stripping section in order to process the solution coming from the carbamate conversion reactor, optionally by the addition of a new stripper dedicated to the stripping of the solution from the carbamate conversion reactor. Preferred Conditions: The conditions in the carbamate conversion environment comprise one or more of molar ratio between ammonia and carbon dioxide of 3 or more, a residence time of 25 minutes or more, and a temperature of 175 degrees C or more. The synthesis of urea in the reaction environment, the stripping process in the stripping environment, the condensation in the condensation environment and the conversion of carbamate in the carbamate conversion environment are performed at a pressure of 100-200 bar abs, preferably 140-160 bar abs.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

   FANG Z
   WANG Z

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a vehicle heat exchanger (2)                whose input pipeline (11) or output pipeline (12)                is provided with a refrigerating sleeve pipe (8)                that is formed with a heat exchanging cavity (13).                Secondary refrigerant liquid outlet end of a water                pump (9) is fixed with the heat exchanging cavity.                Secondary refrigerant section inlet end of an                external sleeve heat exchanger (1) is fixed with                the heat exchanging cavity. Secondary refrigerant                section outlet end of the external sleeve heat                exchanger is connected with secondary refrigerant                liquid inlet end of the water pump, where the                vehicle heat exchanger is a fin type heat                exchanger.
   USE - Carbon dioxide heat pump defrosting device for                use in a refrigeration system of a vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is simple in structure, and                defrosts the external heat exchanger without                changing direction of refrigerant inlet and outlet                heat exchangers.

PN WO2022207621-A1; EP4067514-A1
TI Recovering platinum group metals from feed            containing precursor compounds of platinum group metal            ions, comprises e.g. supplying carbon dioxide            containing gas to cathode compartment of            electrochemical cell equipped with cathode.
AU FRANSAER J
   MARTINEZ-MORA O
   DOMINGUEZ X
AE VITO NV (VLAA-C)
   UNIV KATHOLIEKE LEUVEN (UYLN-C)
   VITO NV (VLAA-C)
   UNIV KATHOLIEKE LEUVEN (UYLN-C)
GA 2022C7702H
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering platinum group metals from feed                containing at least one precursor compounds of at                least one platinum group metal ions, comprises (i)                supplying to cathode compartment of an                electrochemical cell equipped with a cathode                comprising a gas diffusion electrode with a porous                electrochemically active material, the feed                containing the one or more precursor compounds to                form a liquid phase in the cathode compartment,                (ii) supplying a carbon dioxide containing gas to                the cathode compartment, (iii) applying a potential                to the cathode which is so as to cause                electrochemical reduction of the carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide, (iv) and recovering from the                liquid phase precipitated particles of the at least                one platinum group metal in elemental form. The                solvents comprises e.g. water, dimethylsulfoxide,                dimethylformamide, acetone, an ionic liquid, and                deep eutectic solvent, or alcohol, ammonia, an                amine, an amide. The alcohol comprises methanol,                ethanol, n-propanol and/or isopropylalcohol.
   USE - The process is useful for recovering platinum                group metals from a feed containing precursor                compounds of platinum group metal ions which is                useful in proton exchange membrane fuel cells or                direct alcohol fuel cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables to recover particles of                platinum group metals in their elemental form from                complex feed streams which contain precursor                compounds of one or more platinum group metal                ions.

TI Propulsion system for aircraft, has control system            that adapts fractions of total fuel energy flow rate to            engine system represented by hydrocarbon and hydrogen            fuel flow rates to reduce climate warming impact caused            by carbon dioxide.
AU SWANN P
   HOLT J
   HOLT J E
AE ROLLS-ROYCE PLC (RORO-C)
   ROLLS-ROYCE PLC (RORO-C)
GA 2022C74772
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has an engine system                producing thrust by combusting hydrocarbon fuel                and/or combusting or oxidizing hydrogen fuel. A                conveying system conveys the hydrocarbon and                hydrogen fuels from fuel stores to the engine                system. A control system controls the respective                flow rates of the fuels. The control system adapts                the fractions of the total fuel energy flow rate to                reduce climate warming impact caused by one of                carbon dioxide, water vapor and condensation trails                produced by the system and to increase climate                cooling impact produced by condensation trail                produced by propulsion system.
   USE - Propulsion system for aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The control system adapts the fractions of the                total fuel energy flow rate to the engine system to                reduce climate warming impact caused by carbon                dioxide, water vapor and condensation trails                produced by the aircraft propulsion system and/or                to increase climate cooling impact due to contrails                produced by contrails, thus reducing net climate                warming caused by an aircraft, and hence increasing                the range of the aircraft beyond that achievable                using hydrogen fuel alone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an method                for operating aircraft propulsion system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of                propulsion system for aircraft.

   USE - Method for producing electricity in a molten carbonate fuel cell comprising a lithium-containing electrolyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is efficient, reduces system complexity and components while maintaining advantageous operating conditions. The method allows for additional power generation, reduces or minimizes the overall carbon dioxide emissions that result from power generation. The molten carbonate fuel cell is operated at a transference of 0.95 or less. The electrolyte comprises a non-eutectic mixture, and the lithium carbonate content of the electrolyte is greater than a corresponding eutectic composition by 10 wt.% or more. The current density is 150 mA/cm2 or more. The molten carbonate fuel cell is operated for a cumulative time of 50 hours or more. The target cathode electrolyte fill level comprises 85-140 vol.% of the cathode pore volume. Preferred Components: The electrolyte comprises a lithium carbonate content that is greater than a corresponding lithium content in a corresponding eutectic mixture by 10 wt.% or more.

PN KR2021151536-A
TI Synthetic natural gas manufacturing method involves cooling effluent flowing out from first methane synthesis reactor, and removing water and carbondioxide from effluent by passing cooled effluent through separator contactor.
AU JEONG C W
   JUN-WOO K
AE IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY RES INST (POSC-C)
GA 2021E3577P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves synthesizing methane by supplying a reactant containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen to the first methane synthesis reactor. The effluent flowing out from the first methane synthesis reactor is cooled. The water and carbondioxide are removed from the effluent by passing the cooled effluent through a separator contactor, and the effluent is recovered.
   USE - Synthetic natural gas manufacturing method.
   ADVANTAGE - By using a separator contactor, a large amount of carbon dioxide can be removed together with water, so that the yield of methane can be improved, and the purity of the finally obtained synthetic natural gas can be improved. The removed carbon dioxide can be separately recovered, and by using it, an additional methanation reaction can be promoted, so that the purity of the natural gas is improved. The recovered carbon dioxide can be used as a raw material in another chemical synthesis process, or it can be used as a stabilizer for adjusting the pH of the solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a synthetic natural gas production apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view schematically showing a process for producing natural synthesis gas.

PN CN113713608-A
TI Catalyst combination method for removal of carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide, involves passing flue gas into reactor to oxidize carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide to nitrogen dioxide, injecting ammonia to undergo selective catalytic reduction and converting into nitrogen.
AU HU X
   TANG X
AE UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 2021E6936F
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst combination method for simultaneous removal of carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide involves connecting reactor (A) and reactor (B) together, filling reactor (A) with carbon monoxide oxidation catalyst, filling reactor (B) with nitrogen oxide catalyst, passing flue gas into reactor (A) to completely oxidize carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide and oxidizing a portion of nitrogen oxide to nitrogen dioxide under the action of the carbon monoxide oxidation catalyst and heat released by the carbon monoxide reaction, injecting ammonia, so that ammonia, remaining nitrogen oxide, nitrogen dioxide formed by oxidation and carbon dioxide enter the reactor (B) of ammonia selective catalytic reduction of nitrogen oxide and ammonia, remaining nitrogen oxide, oxidized nitrogen dioxide undergo a rapid selective catalytic reduction reaction under the action of ammonia selective catalytic reduction catalyst, reducing and converting into nitrogen and water.
   USE - Catalyst combination method for simultaneous removal of carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and uses catalysts with high selectivity of 100% to simultaneously remove carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide with removal rate of up to more than 99% at low temperature condition and does not generate toxic by-product such as nitric oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The carbon monoxide (CO) oxidation catalyst and ammonia selective catalytic reduction (NH3-SCR) catalyst must be spatially separated, and the position of ammonia injection is between the two catalysts. The CO oxidation catalyst is prepared by grinding method or hydrothermal method. The NH3-SCR catalyst is prepared by impregnation method.Preferred Components: The CO oxidation catalyst is oxidation catalyst with CO oxidation activity, preferably metal oxide of one or more of copper, manganese, iron, cobalt and nickel. The NH3-SCR catalyst is metal catalyst (AB'/C') with NH3-SCR activity.A = V, Ce, Mn, Fe or Co; B' = W, Mo, Nb or Ta; and C' = TiO2, Al2O3, or MgO.Preferred Process: The grinding preparation method of CO oxidation catalyst involves adding potassium permanganate and one or more of manganese sulfate, copper sulfate, ferric sulfate, cobalt sulfate and nickel sulfate to mortar, adding few drops of deionized water, quickly grinding until the solid is basically dry, adding few drops of water to continue grinding, repeating the process until all the solids in the mortar turn brown-black, suction filtering with deionized water until the filtrate is colorless, drying in oven for 2-12 hours to obtain sample, and calcining in muffle furnace at 300-600degrees Celsius for 3-12 hours. The hydrothermal preparation method of CO oxidation catalyst involves adding one or more of ammonium sulfate, ammonium persulfate, manganese sulfate, copper sulfate, ferric sulfate, cobalt sulfate and nickel sulfate to beaker, adding deionized water, stirring with glass rod, dividing the solution into PTFE-lined several stainless-steel autoclaves, placing in homogeneous reactor, reacting at 120-200degrees Celsius for 12-36 hours, natural cooling, washing with deionized water, suction filtering, placing washed sample in oven, drying for 2-12 hours, and calcining in muffle furnace at 300-600degrees Celsius for 3-12 hours. The impregnation preparation method of NH3-SCR catalyst involves adding one or more of ammonium metatungstate, ammonium molybdate, niobium oxide hydrate (H2Nb2O6), and tantalum hydroxide (Ta(OH)5) to beaker, adding deionized water, stirring with glass rod, adding one or more of titanium dioxide, aluminum oxide and magnesium oxide under stirring condition, stirring turbid solution in water bath at 60-80degrees Celsius, drying prepared sample in oven for 2-12 hours, calcining at 300-700degrees Celsius for 3-12 hours metal composite (A/C') catalyst and grinding, adding one or more of ammonium vanadate, cerium nitrate, manganese nitrate, ferric nitrate and cobalt nitrate to beaker, adding deionized water, stirring with glass rod, adding ground metal composite catalyst under stirring condition, stirring turbid solution in water bath at 60-80degrees Celsius to dryness, drying the dried sample in oven for 2-12 hours and calcining in muffle furnace at 300-600degrees Celsius for 3-12 hours.

AU HU J
   DENG D
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2021C0549V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprising (i) dispersing or dissolving the metal salt precursor in a solvent and place it in an autoclave, and adding a sulfur source to obtain a precursor mixture, where metal salt precursor is tungsten salt precursors, molybdenum salt precursors, vanadium salt precursors, and tantalum salt precursors; (ii) adding reducing agent to high-pressure reaction vessel, and sealing reaction vessel; (iii) heating autoclave for reaction at 250-500degrees Celsius, under 5-30 MPa; and (iv) cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature, taking out crude product and washing it to obtain final product when the reaction is complete, where thickness of thin two-dimensional metal sulfide layer is 0.5-20 nm, where defects are edge defects and/or lateral plane defects. The catalyst is supported or unsupported catalyst, the supported catalyst carrier is oxides, molecular sieves, and/or carbon materials, and the carrier is added and dispersed in the solvent during the synthesis process.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce methanol, carbon dioxide hydrogenation, fixed bed reactor, fluidized bed reactor, moving bed reactor, slurry bed reactor, and/or kettle type reactor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can obtain high activity and selectivity, and good stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt precursor is ammonium tungstate, sodium tungstate, tungsten oxide, phosphotungstic acid, sodium phosphotungstate, tungsten chloride, ammonium tetrathiotungstate, ammonium dithiotungstate, ammonium molybdate, sodium molybdate , molybdenum oxide, phosphomolybdic acid, sodium phosphomolybdate, molybdenum chloride, ammonium tetrathiomolybdate, ammonium dithiomolybdate, vanadium chloride, vanadium sulfate, vanadyl oxalate, vanadyl acetylacetonate, vanadyl sulfate , vanadyl chloride, sodium vanadite, metavanadate, pyrovanadate, orthovanadate, polyvanadate, decavanadate, tantalum oxalate, tantalum ethoxide, tantalum acetylacetonate, chloride tantalum and/or tantalite, sulfur sources are sulfur, hydrogen sulfide, sodium sulfide, potassium sulfide, thiourea, carbon disulfide, thioacetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, sulfur dioxide, cysteine, methionine, thiol, thiophenol, and/or thioether and in the input amount of the raw material sulfur source and the metal salt precursor, the molar ratio of S to the metal element is 2-500:1. The solvent is water, methanol, ethanol, acetonitrile, hexamethylene diamine, N, N-dimethyl formamide or dimethyl sulfoxide. The reducing agent is hydrogen, ammonia, hydrogen sulfide, carbon disulfide, hydrazine hydrate, sodium borohydride, and/or thiourea. In step (i) or step (ii), a carrier is added, and the carrier is oxides, molecular sieves, and/or carbon materials.Preferred Method: In the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol reaction, comprises the pretreatment gas containing hydrogen is used for pretreatment at 200-600degrees Celsius for 1-6 hours before the catalyst is used in the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN113385055-A; CN113385055-B
TI Preparation of mixed matrix membrane involves preparing composite material using e.g. halloysite nanotube, aqueous solution of dopamine hydrochloride and glacial acetic acid, mixing with poly ether-block-amide matrix solution, obtaining casting liquid, and casting into membrane.
AU XIAO W
   GUO F
   LI B
   DING R
   LI D
   JIANG X
   HE G
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021A8692U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of mixed matrix membrane involves (1) adding halloysite nanotube (HNT) to aqueous solution of dopamine hydrochloride, adjusting the pH, polymerizing dopamine hydrochloride and wrapping on the surface of HNT, obtaining polydopamine-encapsulated HNT (PHNT), adding PHNT to N,N-dimethylformamide solution containing zirconium chloride and terephthalic acid, adding glacial acetic acid as regulator, growing metal organic framework material UiO-66 on the surface of PHNT at high temperature to replace the polydopamine on the surface of PHNT, obtaining composite material by solvothermal method, (2) adding the composite material to Pebax-1657 (RTM: poly ether-block-amide) matrix solution, ultrasonically mixing, making the composite material evenly dispersed into the matrix solution, obtaining casting liquid, pouring the casting liquid into the petri dish, and casting into membrane in oven at 40-50 degrees C.
   USE - Preparation of mixed matrix membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses composite material as a filler. The HNT itself can construct a gas transport channel in the membrane, reduce mass transfer resistance, and increase gas permeation rate. The affinity of UiO66 for carbon dioxide accelerates the passage of carbon dioxide molecules, which increases the permeability of the membrane to carbon dioxide, and improves the selectivity of the mixed matrix membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The concentration of the aqueous solution of dopamine hydrochloride is 1-2 g/L. The concentration of the Pebax-1657 (RTM: poly ether-block-amide) matrix solution is 3-10 wt.%. Preferred Composition: The ratio of aqueous solution of dopamine hydrochloride to HNT is 10-20 mL:1 g. The ratio of zirconium chloride to terephthalic acid is 1:1.2-1.4. The ratio of the PHNT, zirconium chloride, terephthalic acid and glacial acetic acid is 0.5 g:5 mmol:5 mmol:3 mL.

   CHEN M
   JIANG D
   MENG S
   XU Q
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202184183J
AB    NOVELTY - Cadmium sulfide-based composite photocatalytic material comprises a cobalt(II) hydroxide/cadmium sulfide composite nano photocatalytic material, where the non-precious metal promoter cobalt(II) hydroxide is uniformly dispersed on the surface of the cadmium sulfide nanowires, and the particle size is 2-5 nm.
   USE - The material is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material provides water oxidation half-reaction sites, accelerates kinetics of oxygen generation, inhibits recombination of photogenerated electron-hole pairs, and improves reduction of carbon dioxide reaction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the material involving weighing the cadmium sulfide nanowires, dispersing in deionized water under ultrasonic treatment, stirring to obtain a cadmium sulfide dispersion, adjusting the pH value of the dispersion to greater than 10 with a sodium carbonate solution to obtain a cadmium sulfide suspension, dissolving cobalt nitric acid hexahydrate in deionized water to obtain a cobalt nitrate solution, dripping the cobalt nitrate solution to the stirred cadmium sulfide suspension, continuing stirring to obtain a mixed solution, centrifuging the mixed solution to obtain a precipitate, washing the precipitate with deionized water and absolute ethanol for 3 times, and drying in an oven to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of cobalt(II) hydroxide is 0.2-1%, preferably 0.5%. The concentration of the sodium carbonate solution is 0.25 mol/l. Preferred Method: The step of continuing stirring is carried out for 1 hour.

PT P
PN CN112791698-A
TI Preparing natural organic acid hydrophobically modified alkaline hydroxylated biochar in supercritical carbon dioxide medium involves adding acetone to the biochar, mixing, sonicating, filtering, and then washing with ethanol.
AU WANG X
   HUANG H
   YE G
   CHENG H
   ZHU W
   YAO F
   WANG Y
   JIAO Y
   YE D
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2021566170
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing natural organic acid hydrophobically modified alkaline hydroxylated biochar in supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) medium involves adding acetone to the biochar, mixing, sonicating, filtering, and then washing with ethanol, isopropanol, and deionizing water to obtain clean biochar. The clean biochar is stirred with hydrochloric acid, filtered and rinsed with deionized water, and dried in an inert gas atmosphere to obtain dry biochar. The dried biochar is added to alkali solution, heated and stirred, filtered and dried in an inert gas atmosphere to obtain hydroxylated biochar. The hydroxylated biochar is added to a solution in which natural macromolecular organic acids, anhydrous ethanol, and dehydrating agent are dissolved, and reacted by heating in a supercritical CO2 medium to obtain a reacted mixed liquid. The filtered precipitate of the reaction mixture is washed with ethanol and deionized water, and dried in an inert gas atmosphere at 50-100 degrees C to obtain modified biochar.
   USE - Method for preparing natural organic acid hydrophobically modified alkaline hydroxylated biochar in supercritical carbon dioxide medium for adsorbing volatile organic compounds (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in operation, low in price, environmentally friendly, has little influence on the pore structure of the carbon material, and has good adsorption performance for volatile organic compounds in a high-humidity environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline solution is a mixed solution of sodium hydroxide (NaOH), sodium phosphate dodecahydrate (Na3PO4.12H2O) and deionized water.

TI Corrosion inhibitor for oilfields for inhibiting            corrosion of carbon steel in coexistence system of            carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, is prepared by add            imidazoline, organic aldehyde, thiourea and organic            solvent to reaction vessel.
AU LU Y
   ZHAO J
   ZHANG G
   HU T
   WANG W
   WANG L
   GUO H
   LI Y
AE CNOOC TIANJIN OILFIELD CHEM CO LTD (CNOO-C)
   CNOOC ENERGY TECHNOLOGY & SERVICES LTD (CNOO-C)
   CHINA NAT OFFSHORE OIL CORP (CNOO-C)
GA 2021434538
AB    NOVELTY - Corrosion inhibitor is prepared by add                imidazoline, organic aldehyde, thiourea and organic                solvent to a reaction vessel, using concentrated                hydrochloric acid as a catalyst to carry out                reaction at 65-95 degrees C under stirring                conditions to obtain product. The aldehyde is                formaldehyde or paraformaldehyde. The organic                aldehydes and alcohol compounds containing double                bonds or triple bonds are added to the reaction                vessel at 75-95 C under stirring conditions to                obtain another product. The alcohol compound                containing double or triple bond is allyl alcohol,                propynol or butynediol. Quinoline quaternary                ammonium salt, non-ionic surfactant, long-chain                alkyl gemini quaternary ammonium salt, thiocyanate                and water are added to the reaction vessel in                sequence under stirring to obtain a corrosion                inhibitor. The non-ionic surfactant is nonylphenol                polyoxyethylene ether, octylphenol polyoxyethylene                ether, fatty amine polyoxyethylene ether or fatty                alcohol polyoxyethylene ether.
   USE - Corrosion inhibitor for oilfields for                inhibiting corrosion of carbon steel in coexistence                system of carbon dioxide and hydrogen                sulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The corrosion inhibitor can effectively                protect metal equipment and pipelines corroded by                carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing corrosion inhibitor, which involves                adding imidazoline and organic aldehyde, thiourea,                organic solvents and concentrated hydrochloric acid                as catalyst to the reaction vessel under stirring                conditions to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The imidazoline is                  oleic imidazoline or lauric imidazoline. The                  organic solvent is ethanol, isopropanol, ethylene                  glycol, N,N-dimethylformamide or                  N,N-dimethylacetamide. The quinoline quaternary                  ammonium salt is cetylquine phloline quaternary                  ammonium salt, tetradecyl quinoline quaternary                  ammonium salt, dodecyl quinoline quaternary                  ammonium salt, cetyl hydroxyquinoline quaternary                  ammonium salt or dodecyl methyl quinoline                  quaternary ammonium salt. The long chain alkyl                  diquaternary ammonium salt is dodecyl gemini                  quaternary ammonium salt, tetradecyl gemini                  quaternary ammonium salt or cetyl gemini                  quaternary ammonium salt. The thiocyanate is                  sodium thiocyanate, potassium thiocyanate or                  sulfur ammonium cyanate.

PT P
PN CN110279620-A; CN110279620-B
TI Preparing North American Epilobium latifolium            extract useful for human hair scalp or pet hair fur            care products comprises e.g. pre-processing North            American Epilobium latifolium and using surface carbon            dioxide for dedusting treatment.
AU XIE J
AE SHANGHAI YAOXIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201984558X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing North American Epilobium latifolium                extract comprises (i) separating flowers, leaves                and stems of the North American Epilobium                latifolium and using surface carbon dioxide for                surface dedusting treatment, carrying out                pulverization treatment and carrying out composite                enzymatic treatment; (ii) performing pretreatment                to obtain ethanol and leaves in North American                Epilobium latifolium, adding ethanol as an                cosolvent, placing in supercritical carbon dioxide                extractor for extraction with a pressure of 12 MPa                at 36-40 degrees C and collecting extract; (iii)                pre-processing to obtain samples of North American                Epilobium latifolium, adding to subcritical                extractor for butane extraction and collecting                extracts; (iv) centrifuging leaves and stem                extracts separately, collecting supernatant,                concentrating and collecting two concentrated                liquids; and (v) mixing two concentrates.
   USE - The North American Epilobium latifolium                extract is useful for human hair scalp or pet hair                fur care products (claimed) and human scalp                microenvironment conditioning liquid using for                inhibiting 5 alpha reductase, diminishing                inflammation and improving scalp environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: optimizes and yields many active                ingredients as possible.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (I)                North American Epilobium latifolium extract,                prepared by above mentioned method; and (II) human                scalp microenvironment conditioning liquid,                comprising North American Epilobium latifolium                extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The pre-processing step                  comprises screening North American Epilobium                  latifolium samples, cleaning and separating                  flowers, leaves and stems, surface dedusting                  treatment of flowers, leaves and stem samples                  with carbon dioxide gas, adding ethanol to the                  sample after dust removal for ultrasonic                  vibration, adding ethanol 3 times the weight of                  the sample, adding complex enzyme and digesting                  enzyme for 12 hours under normal pressure at                  35-55 degrees C at a pH of 3-7. The composite                  enzyme comprises 8-12 pts. wt. cellulase, 8-12                  pts. wt. pectinase, 0.8-1 pts. wt. amylase, and                  0.7-1 pts. wt. protease. The complex enzyme is                  added in an amount of 12-9% by weight of the                  sample. The method comprises subjecting                  pretreated samples for subcritical butane                  extraction with a pressure of -0.1 MPa, and                  single immersion time is 8-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The human scalp                  micro-environment conditioning liquid comprises                  3% butanediol, 0.6% lecithin, 0.1% xanthan gum,                  0.05% EDTA disodium, 0.05% tocopherol, 1-5% North                  American willow herb extracting solution, 0.08%                  isothiozolone, 0.3% phenoxyethanol and de-ionized                  water (remaining amount).
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A10; B04-B01B; B04-C02F; B05-B01P; B10-B01B; B10-E04B; B10-E04C; B11-A02C2; B11-B03C; B12-M07; B14-C03; B14-R02; B14-S18; B15-E00; D05-A02; D08-C

PT P
PN CN108586243-A; CN108586243-B
TI Indirectly synthesizing organic carbonate involves adding an organic amine A into synthesis tower, uniformly introducing excess carbon dioxide gas into organic amine A hours and carrying out gas-liquid separation.
AU ZHAO W
   XU Z
   CHEN Y
   CHAI M
   CHEN L
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
   UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 201879547F
AB    NOVELTY - Indirectly synthesizing an organic carbonate involves adding an organic amine A into a synthesis tower at 140-220 degrees C and 12-20MPa, uniformly introducing excess carbon dioxide gas into the organic amine A for 2-6 hours and carrying out gas-liquid separation to obtain carbonic acid solution. The carbonic acid solution is dehydrated and concentrated to obtain the carbonic acid amide. The remaining carbon dioxide gas is returned to the synthesis tower for synthesis. Carboxamide, alcohol or phenol and catalyst are added into the reaction vessel for carrying out a reaction at 120-180 degrees C and 25-35 degrees C for 1-4 hours to obtain organic carbonate and organic amine B. The catalyst is a Lewis acid catalyst, a metal composite oxide catalyst or a supported catalyst. The alcohol or phenol gas is refluxed to the reaction kettle. The organic carbonate, alcohol Or phenol liquid, organic amine B and catalyst are centrifuged to obtain crude liquid A.
   USE - Method for indirectly synthesizing an organic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method synthesizes an organic carbonate with high yield in cost-effective and eco-friendly manner on industrial scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The crude liquid A is rectified to obtain fraction organic amine B and crude liquid B.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Lewis acid catalyst is copper(II) chloride, aluminum chloride, zinc chloride, lead oxide, zinc oxide-lead oxide, zinc oxide-lanthanum oxide or zinc oxide-lead(II,IV) oxide metal composite oxide catalyst. The supported catalyst is copper(II) chloride/4A molecular sieve, copper oxide/activated carbon, copper-nickel/carbon nano-tube.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine is methylamine, ethylamine, propylamine, butylamine, aniline or phenylenediamine. The alcohol is methanol, ethanol, propanol or butanol. The phenol is phenol or catechol.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)
MC E10-A11B1; E11-F06; E11-F07A; E11-W; N01-A; N01-B; N01-C02; N02-A01; N02-B01; N02-C01; N02-D01; N02-E; N02-F03; N02-F05; N03; N04-D01; N06-F; N07-D07

PT P
PN US2018229180-A1; US11185813-B2
TI Increasing selectivity of absorption process for hydrogen sulfide absorption from gas mixture which also comprises carbon dioxide by reducing the pH of absorbent solution comprising amines used in the absorption process.
AU PEREIRA C S
   SISKIN M
   INGRAM T
   VORBERG G
   ERNST M
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   BASF AG (BADI-C)
GA 201863442G
AB    NOVELTY - The selectivity of an absorption process for hydrogen sulfide (H2S) absorption from a gas mixture which also comprises carbon dioxide (CO2) is increased by reducing the pH of absorbent solution comprising greater than or equal to 1 amines used in the absorption process.
   USE - A method for increasing the selectivity of an absorption process for hydrogen sulfide (H2S) absorption from a gas mixture which also comprises carbon dioxide (CO2).
   ADVANTAGE - Decreasing the weight percentage of hindered amine/alkanolamine absorbent in solution effectively reduces the amount of carbonate that can be formed, which increases the amount of free amine available for acid gas recovery as bicarbonate and hydrosulfide. Because absorption of HS is kinetically faster, the selectivity for HS- formation is favored over HCO3- formation. Additional advantages of the method include reduced chemical costs (directly related to reduced hindered amine/alkanolamine usage), reduced viscosity resulting in reduced circulation energy, reduced corrosivity of the absorption system, and reduced energy required to regenerate the higher loaded (1:1 stoichiometry), but reduced volume of hindered amine/alkanolamine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for selective separation of H2S from a gas mixture which also comprises CO2 which involves contacting a stream of gas mixture with an absorbent solution comprising greater than or equal to 1 amines, where the absorbent solution has a reduced pH.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The pH reducing step comprises diluting the absorbent solution to less than or equal to 36 wt.%, preferably less than or equal to 30 wt.%. The pH reducing step comprises adding an acid selected from phosphoric acid and sulfuric acid to the absorbent solution. Preferred Properties: H2S/CO2 selectivity of absorbent solution is greater than or equal to 5 for an acid gas loading (mol(CO2+H2S)/mol(amine)) range of 0.2-0.55. H2S/CO2; greater than or equal to 6 for an acid gas loading range of 0.2-0.55; and greater than or equal to 7 for an acid gas loading of 0.2-0.55.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Amines are selected from amines, alkanolamines, and/or sterically hindered akanolamines. Sterically hindered alkanolamine is a capped alkanolamine. Amine is selected from methoxyethoxyethoxyethanol-tert.-butylamine (M3ETB), ethoxyethanol-tert.-butylamine (EETB), and methyl diethanolamine (MDEA).
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN107714651-A
TI Preparation of febantel liposome by dissolving polyethylene glycol-modified lipid derivative in ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing febantel in ethanol, mixing and treating with supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU SUN J
   CHEN H
   CUI Z
   CHANG L
   LIANG X
   FU X
AE HENAN SOAR VETERINARY PHARM CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
   HENAN MUXIANG VETERINARY PHARM CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2018168145
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of febantel liposome comprises weighing febantel and polyethylene glycol-modified lipid derivative, adding ethanol to polyethylene glycol-modified lipid derivative, dissolving, adding citric acid-sodium citrate buffer solution as medium to obtain polyethylene glycol-modified lipid derivative suspension with final concentration of 50-100 mg/ml, ultrasonically dispersing febantel in ethanol to prepare febantel dispersion with final concentration of 5-10 mg/ml, mixing suspension and dispersion, and treating with supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for preparing febantel liposome.
   ADVANTAGE - The liposome improves non-bioavailability of febantel, and targeting and sustained-release property, and prolongs in-vivo.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of febantel liposome comprises weighing febantel and polyethylene glycol-modified lipid derivative at weight ratio of 1: 30-40, adding ethanol to polyethylene glycol-modified lipid derivative, dissolving to pH of 2.7-5.2, adding 200-400 mmol/l citric acid-sodium citrate buffer solution as hydrating medium to obtain polyethylene glycol-modified lipid derivative suspension with final concentration of 50-100 mg/ml, ultrasonically dispersing febantel compound in ethanol to prepare febantel dispersion with final concentration of 5-10 mg/ml, mixing suspension and dispersion, and treating with supercritical carbon dioxide at 50-55 degrees C and 10-15 MPa for 5-10 minutes. The polyethylene glycol-modified lipid derivative is polyethylene glycol-distearoylphosphatidylethanolamine or polyethylene glycol-distearoylphosphatidylglycerol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyethylene glycol has molecular weight of 2000-5000 Da.Preferred Method: The preparation of suspension further comprises performing film dispersion, injection, reverse evaporation or spray-drying.

PN CN107540170-A; CN107540170-B
TI Printing and dyeing wastewater treatment method, by concentrating waste water, neutralizing with carbon dioxide, reducing suspended solids, pumping into e.g. pre-acidified tank, biochemically treating and desulfurizing methane.
AU LE D
   YUE D
AE ZHEJIANG MIZUDA SPINNING PRINTING & DYEING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   ZHEJIANG MIZUDA SPINNING PRINTING & DYEI (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018049576
AB    NOVELTY - Printing and dyeing wastewater treatment method, by (a) concentrating the waste water that each process produces to collect concentrate, (b) neutralizing concentrated wastewater with carbon dioxide, (c) reducing suspended solids (SS) in neutralized concentrated waste water by ion flotation, (d) pumping SS reduced concentrated waste water into pre-acidified tank, (e) pumping pre-acidified waste water into internal circulator (IC), and biochemically treating to produce methane, and (f) desulfurizing methane, drying and collecting or combusting methane.
   USE - As printing and dyeing wastewater treatment method (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The printing and dyeing wastewater method is environmentally-friendly, and which does not cause any harm to health of workers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Printing and dyeing wastewater treatment method, involves (a) concentrating the waste water that each process produces to collect concentrate, (b) neutralizing concentrated wastewater with carbon dioxide to reduce the pH of concentrated wastewater, (c) reducing suspended solids (SS) in neutralized concentrated waste water by ion flotation, (d) pumping SS reduced concentrated waste water into pre-acidified tank for 24 hours, (e) pumping pre-acidified waste water into biological anaerobic reactor internal circulator (IC), and biochemically treating the granular sludge pre-acidified waste water to produce methane, and (f) desulfurizing methane, drying and collecting or combusting methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The effluent from IC flows into the aerobic section through aeration pipeline for aeration treatment. The step (b) involves adding 400 mg/kg sulfuric acid to concentrated waste water generated in each process. In step (b), a sewage pump is concentrated wastewater and carbon dioxide through the pipeline mixer to fully mix for reaction, the carbon dioxide is mixed with concentrated wastewater through a gas flow meter, through the pH meter feedback signal to automatically control the flow rate of carbon dioxide, and concentrated waste water after carbon dioxide neutralization has a pH of 7.5-8.5. In step (c), after the first concentrated wastewater through the IC to reduce the water temperature to 36-39 degrees C. The concentrated SS wastewater has concentration of less than 400 mg/kg. In step (d), the concentration of anaerobic activated sludge in the pre-acidified tank is about 10%, and the treated concentrated wastewater has a pH of 6.8-7. In the step (e), the pre-acidified concentrated waste water first enters the circulation tank of the IC, the wastewater and the circulation tank are mixed with water circulated and discharged from the IC and then enter the IC for reaction. The diameter of the IC is 11 m and height is 24 m, granular sludge and umbrella cloth water are used to arrange the three-phase separator, first layer is 15 m from the bottom of the IC and second layer is 19 m from the bottom of the IC. In step (a), each process comprises a printing and dyeing pretreatment process, a dyeing material process, a printing material, and a mesh washing process.

PN CN114807098-A
TI Producing extracellular cellulose degradation            enzyme system by adopting Clostridium thermocellum            involves inoculating Clostridium thermocellum strain            into seed culture medium under anaerobic condition, and            culturing to middle logarithmic growth phase.
AU CUI Q
   ZHANG Y
   WAN W
   CHEN C
   SONG X
   LIU Y
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 2022A2260Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing extracellular cellulose degradation                enzyme system by adopting Clostridium                thermocelluminvolves inoculating the Clostridium                thermocellumstrain into seed culture medium under                the anaerobic condition, and culturing to the                middle logarithmic growth phase under the                temperature condition of 50-60&#8451; and                rotating speed condition of 150-250                revolutions/minute (rpm) to obtain seed solution,                seed culture medium comprises 5 gram/liter (g/L)                carbon source, 2.9 g/L dipotassium phosphate, 1.5                g/L potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 1.4 g/L                ammonium bicarbonate, 0.1 g/L calcium chloride, 0.5                g/L magnesium chloride, 0.5 mg/L ferrous sulfate,                0.5 g/L sodium sulfide, 6 g/L yeast extract or 100                ml/L corn steep liquor with pH of 7.5.5.
   USE - Method for producing extracellular cellulose                degradation enzyme system by adopting Clostridium                thermocellum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to add components such as                carbon dioxide and ammonium bicarbonate in a                culture system, optimize a culture medium by                reducing sulfides, induce the robustness of a                strain, improve the growth activity of cells,                enable the cells to grow rapidly, and increase the                synthesis level of a fibrosome. The Clostridium                thermocellumfermentation liquor obtained by the                method is used for saccharifying lignocellulose,                saccharification is completed within 3-4 days, and                the sugar yield exceeds 90%, so that the                saccharification period is greatly shortened, the                time cost is greatly reduced, and the method has                important significance for actual production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing extracellular cellulose degradation                enzyme system by adopting Clostridium thermocellum                involves inoculating the Clostridium thermocellum                strain into seed culture medium under the anaerobic                condition, and culturing to the middle logarithmic                growth phase under the temperature condition of                50-60&#8451; and rotating speed condition of                150-250 revolutions/minute (rpm) to obtain seed                solution, seed culture medium comprises 5                gram/liter (g/L) carbon source, 2.9 g/L dipotassium                phosphate, 1.5 g/L potassium dihydrogen phosphate,                1.4 g/L ammonium bicarbonate, 0.1 g/L calcium                chloride, 0.5 g/L magnesium chloride, 0.5 mg/L                ferrous sulfate, 0.5 g/L sodium sulfide, 6 g/L                yeast extract or 100 ml/L corn steep liquor with pH                of 7.5.5. The prepared seed solution is inoculated                into an activation culture medium in the                inoculation amount of 0.1-1% (v/v) under the                anaerobic condition, and cultured to the middle                logarithmic growth phase under the temperature                condition of 50-60&#8451; and rotation speed                condition of 200-250 rpm to obtain activation                solution, where activation culture medium comprises                5 g/L carbon source, 2.9 g/L dipotassium phosphate,                1.5 g/L potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 2.8 g/L                ammonium bicarbonate, 0.1 g/L calcium chloride, 0.5                g/L magnesium chloride, 0.5 mg/L ferrous sulfate,                0.2 g/L sodium sulfide and 50 ml/L corn steep                liquor with pH of 7.5.5. An enzyme production                culture medium is prepared, mixed gas of nitrogen                and carbon dioxide is blown into system gas layer                to replace air, sterilized at high temperature and                high pressure, inoculated in the inoculation amount                of 0.1-1% (v/v), inoculated into the obtained                activation solution, cultured at the temperature of                55-65&#8451; and rotation speed of 200-250 rpm                until the protein content is not increased further,                and fermentation is completed to obtain Clostridium                thermocellum fermentation liquor, i.e.                extracellular cellulose degradation enzyme system,                where enzyme production culture medium comprises                1-5 g/L carbon source, 0.6 g/L dipotassium hydrogen                phosphate, 0.3 g/L potassium dihydrogen phosphate,                2.8 g/L ammonium bicarbonate, 0.1 g/L calcium                chloride, 0.5 g/L magnesium chloride, 0.5 mg/L                ferrous sulfate, 0.2 g/L sodium sulfide and 25 ml/L                corn steep liquor with pH of 8.0.0.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The seed culture                  medium adopts a carbon source which is a                  pretreated lignocellulose raw material or                  microcrystalline cellulose. The carbon source                  adopted by the activation medium is pretreated                  lignocellulose raw material or microcrystalline                  cellulose. The carbon source is adopted as a                  pretreated lignocellulose raw material or                  microcrystalline cellulose in the enzyme                  production culture medium. The lignocellulose raw                  materials are selected from corn straws, wheat                  straws, switchgrass, pasture and xylose                  residues.Preferred Conditions: The pretreatment                  method is selected from alkaline method, an                  ammonia water method or a sulfonation method. The                  content of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is                  0.1-10% (v/v). The preparation of the activating                  solution is repeated for one time. The                  high-temperature high-pressure sterilization is                  sterilization at 121&#8451; for 60                  minutes.Preferred Process: The step of performing                  enzymatic fermentation specifically involves                  adopting fed-batch fermentation method, where pH                  is controlled at 7.0, carbon source is                  supplemented every 12 hours at the initial                  concentration, and carbon dioxide concentration                  in the gas layer is controlled at the initial                  level or 2% (v/v) by gas displacement, i.e.,                  initial level is maintained when the initial                  content of carbon dioxide is 0.1-2% (v/v), and 2%                  (v/v) is maintained when the initial content of                  carbon dioxide is greater than 2% (v/v) until the                  protein content no longer increased, and                  fermentation is ended.

TI Apparatus for processing i.e. sulfur oxide contained in combustion exhaust gases emitted from factories, using microbubbles, has gas injection nozzle for injecting processing target gas completely filled in lower space to upper space.
AU JUNG J O
