   JEONG H J
   JEONG Y J
AE JUNG J O (JUNG-Individual)
   KIM M S (KIMM-Individual)
   JEONG H J (JEON-Individual)
   JEONG Y J (JEON-Individual)
GA 202280361M
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a bottom plate (113a) dividing an inner space (110a) into a lower space (1211a) and an upper space (1122a). A gas injection nozzle (115a) injects gas to be processed from the lower space to the upper space. A processing object is placed in the upper space and injected from the gas injection nozzle and a collision plate (116a) to collide gas and inject the gas to be treated from the gas injection nozzle, collides with the collision plate to form microbubbles in treatment liquid directly supplied to the upper space. A processing target component is processed by reacting with the processing component included in processing liquid. The gas injection nozzle injects the processing target gas completely filled in the lower space to the upper space.
   USE - Apparatus for processing gas i.e. sulfur oxide contained in combustion exhaust gases emitted from factories, thermal power plants, and incinerators, using microbubbles.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus reduces installation space of the reactor and overall installation cost when the gas processing unit is vertically disposed in the reactor. The apparatus includes a gas flow fan that uses enough power to flow the gas to be treated in the reactor, so that overall power consumption can be reduced. The apparatus improves operation performance of the exhaust gas purification device by spraying water or compressed air into a nozzle that forms microbubbles by spraying the gas to be treated in the reactor to prevent clogging of the nozzle by solids generated during the reaction. The nozzle forms the microbubbles by injecting the gas to be treated into the treatment liquid and the solid content generated and deposited during reaction in the space in which the treatment liquid is accommodated is discharged by the solid content discharging unit, so that operating performance of the exhaust gas purification device is improved. The apparatus provides iron chloride that does not react with hydrogen sulfide with carbon dioxide or ferric chloride as a hydrogen sulfide removal agent so as to reduce amount of chemical using sodium hydroxide reacting with carbon dioxide as a hydrogen sulfide removal agent. The apparatus changes pH of the oxidizing agent aqueous solution by changing the amount of the oxidizing agent included in the oxidizing agent aqueous solution according to the amount of nitrogen monoxide included in the gas to be treated, measures the pH of the oxidizing agent aqueous solution by predicting increase or decrease of nitrogen monoxide according to the pH and increase or decrease input amount of the oxidizing agent, so that oxidation reaction efficiency can be improved. The apparatus reduces overall power consumption when a gas flow fan uses enough power to flow the gas to be treated in the nitrogen monoxide reaction module and the nitrogen dioxide reaction module. The apparatus removes dust i.e. fine dust, included in the gas to be treated from the treatment liquid by contact with the treatment liquid such i.e. oxidizing agent aqueous solution. The apparatus reacts nitrogen monoxide contained in the exhaust gas to be treated with the oxidizing agent to be oxidized to nitrogen dioxide in the oxidation treatment tank, flows the oxidation treatment gas discharged from the oxidation treatment tank into the gas treatment tank and rises in the reaction space of the gas treatment tank so as to perform process of passing through the treatment unit contact with treated water in form of microbubbles, so that reduction treatment with a reducing agent and a neutralization absorption treatment with a neutralizing agent is performed, and dedusting and desulfurization treatment can be efficiently processed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the configuration of a reactor and a processing liquid supply unit in the gas processing apparatus.

AU ZHANG Y
   GU C
   YANG G
AE ANHUI DINGYANG BIO-BASED MATERIAL CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021B57539
AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally-friendly degradable kitchen garbage bag comprises corn starch, polyvinyl alcohol, glycerol, ethylene glycol, urea, montmorillonite, and anti-mouse microcapsule. The preparation of anti-mouse microcapsule comprises e.g. adding resorcinol, sodium bicarbonate, de-ionized water into a reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide, performing reflux reaction, cooling to room temperature and filtering, crystallizing and washing to obtain intermediate 1, adding m-methyl phenol, intermediate 1, zinc chloride, phosphorus oxychloride and sulfolane into the reaction kettle, and reacting to obtain intermediate 2; and adding intermediate 2, nitrogen-bromosuccinimide, benzoyl peroxide, carbon tetrachloride into the reaction kettle, reacting to obtain intermediate 3, adding the intermediate 3, potassium carbonate, de-ionized water, tetraethylammonium bromide into reaction kettle, performing reflux reaction to obtain intermediate 4.
   USE - Environmentally-friendly degradable kitchen garbage bag for preventing rat damage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has microencapsulation process makes the capsaicin in the garbage bag longer than the time, and does not make the capsaicin exposed directly outside, avoids the direct and the contact, and the micro-capsule surface contains chelate ring, after absorbing the ultraviolet light, hydrogen bond fracture generating molecular isomer, intramolecular structure heat vibration; hydrogen bond damage; ring chelate, intramolecular structure is changed, so that the harmful ultraviolet light into harmless heat can be discharged, so as to avoid the damage of the microcapsule increases the use life of the anti-rat and environment-friendly degradable kitchen waste wet garbage bag; and the garbage bag uses starch as raw material can be quickly degraded.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Environmentally-friendly degradable kitchen garbage bag for preventing rat damage, comprises 60-70 pts. wt. corn starch, 50-80 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol, 15-20 pts. wt. glycerol, 8-10 pts. wt. ethylene glycol, 5-8 pts. wt. urea, 5-8 pts. wt. montmorillonite, and 8-10 pts. wt. anti-mouse microcapsule. The preparation of anti-mouse microcapsule comprises (i) adding resorcinol, sodium bicarbonate, de-ionized water into a reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide, performing reflux reaction, cooling to room temperature and filtering, crystallizing and washing to obtain intermediate 1, adding m-methyl phenol, intermediate 1, zinc chloride, phosphorus oxychloride and sulfolane into the reaction kettle, and reacting to obtain intermediate 2; (ii) adding the intermediate 2, nitrogen-bromosuccinimide, benzoyl peroxide, carbon tetrachloride into the reaction kettle, reacting to obtain intermediate 3, adding the intermediate 3, potassium carbonate, de-ionized water, tetraethylammonium bromide into reaction kettle, performing reflux reaction to obtain intermediate 4, adding p-iodotoluene, dimethyl malonate, copper iodide, hexamethyl phosphoric acid amide into the reaction kettle, reacting to obtain intermediate 5, adding the intermediate 4, intermediate 5, toluene, lithium amide into the reaction kettle, introducing nitrogen to protect, reacting to obtain intermediate 6; (iii) adding intermediate 6, dimethyl sulfate, potassium carbonate, and acetone into the reaction kettle, and performing reflux reaction to obtain Intermediate 7, adding Intermediate 7, potassium permanganate, and benzene into the reaction kettle, refluxing to obtain Intermediate 8, and adding intermediate 8, ethylenediamine and 1-hydroxybenzotriazole, reacting, Intermediate 9, ethanol and trifluoroacetic acid to the reaction kettle and reacting to obtain intermediate 10; and (iv) adding intermediate 10, potassium carbonate and acetone into the reaction kettle, refluxing and adding chloromethyltriethoxysilane to obtain intermediate 11, adding intermediate 11, ethanol, deionized water, and ethyl orthosilicate in the reactor, filtering and drying to obtain modified silica, adding capsaicin and gelatin liquid into the reactor, stirring until the mixture is uniform, adding deionized water, stirring, adding glutaraldehyde and modified silica, removing filtrate by filtration, and drying the filter cake.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the degradable kitchen garbage bag, comprising (i) adding the corn starch into a stirrer at 50-60degreesC under the rotating speed of 800-1000 revolutions/minute for 10-15 minutes, adding polyvinyl alcohol, continuously stirring for 10-15 minutes to obtain mixed material; and (ii) mixing the mixed material, glycerol, glycol, urea, montmorillonite, anti-mouse microcapsule into the kneading machine under the condition of the temperature is 85-90degreesC, mixing and extruding to obtain the plastic master batch.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (i), the dosage ratio of resorcinol, sodium bicarbonate, and deionized water is 3 g:10 g:30 ml, and the dosage ratio of resorcinol, intermediate 1, zinc chloride, phosphorus oxychloride, and sulfolane is 0.15 mol:0.1 mol:0.15 mol:0.2 mol:20 ml. PN CN113072177-A
TI Device useful for synchronously treating sludge digestive juice and biogas by using in situ algae comprises biogas purifying column, whose top set to gas collecting device one, and top of photobioreactor set to gas collecting device two.
AU GE S
   YU Z
   QIU S
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 202187066J
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises hydrolysis acidification tank, feeding tank, photobioreactor, biogas evolution column, gas collecting device 1 and gas collecting device 2 in sequence. A top of biogas purifying column is connected to gas collecting device 1. The top of photobioreactor is connected to gas collecting device 2. A hydrolysis acidification tank is equipped with water inlet pipe and between hydrolysis acidification tank and feeding tank. A water outlet of biogas evolution column is connected to photobioreactor through connecting pipe, and between feeding tank and photobioreactor. The photobioreactor and biogas evolution column are connected by connecting pipe equipped with peristaltic pump. The photobioreactor is equipped with water outlet, dissolved oxygen (DO) sensor and pH sensor. The DO sensor and pH sensor are connected with DO and pH meter. An illumination plate is provided on one side of photobioreactor, and gas collecting device 2 is provided with gas sampling valve.
   USE - The device is useful for synchronously treating sludge digestive juice and biogas by using in situ algae (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: utilizes sludge digestive juice to cultivate in-situ in situ algae; improves the adaptability to high ammonia nitrogen sewage environment; realizes the bioremediation of sludge digestive juice; produces the microalgae biomass energy, which has potential commercial value; upgrades and purifies the biogas components obtained in the anaerobic digestion process through microalgae carbon fixation; mixes the sludge digestive juice and natural snow water as a microalgae culture medium; provides a source of carbon dioxide by biogas; simultaneously realizes the bioremediation of sewage and the purification of biogas components.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synchronously treating sludge digestive juice and biogas by using in situ algae, comprising (i) separating in-situ microalgae from sludge digestive juice, using the diluted digestive juice obtained by mixing the sludge digestive juice and tap water for culturing in-situ microalgae, and gradually increasing the volume ratio of digestive juice from 50-80% in 20 cycles to obtain in situ in-situ algae, (ii) hydrolyzing and acidifying including entering the sludge digestive juice into a hydrolysis acidification tank through an inlet pipe, and performing anaerobic operation under hydraulic retention, where the suspended solids concentration of the mixed solution is 6000 mg/l, (iii) mixing sewage including directly flowing the effluent from the hydrolysis acidification tank into the feeding tank, and further entering the natural snow water into feed tank to mix with it, where the volume ratio of the effluent of the hydrolysis acidification tank to the natural snow water is 7:3, and forming a diluted sludge digestive juice, and (iv) culturing microalgae cultivation and optimizing biogas including inoculating the in-situ algae in situ in the photobioreactor, entering the diluted sludge digestive juice to the open photobioreactor through the peristaltic pump, running in semi-continuous flow mode at room temperature under light conditions, measuring the specific composition of a gas sampling valve set in the gas collecting device 2, discharging the generated gas, connecting it to the biogas purifying column through a peristaltic pump while a reactor is running, circulating the algae liquid, completely connecting with the biogas, filling the column with 70% nitrogen, 25% carbon dioxide, 1.8% oxygen, and 3.2% water as synthetic biogas, entering the exhaust gas from the biogas purifying column into the gas collecting device 1, transmitting the acquired digital information to the DO meter and pH meter by pH and DO sensors in real time during operation, realizing online monitoring of parameters, and discharging the effluent of the photobioreactor through the water outlet pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device for synchronously treating sludge digestive juice and biogas by using in situ algae (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the hydraulic retention time is 3 days. In the step (iv), the illumination condition comprises the illumination intensity is 4000 lux, and illumination and the dark period is 14 hours:10 hours. The filling rate of in in-situ algae is 50%, the continuous water intake time of the diluted sludge digestive juice is 24 hours, and the hydraulic retention time is 6 days.

PT P
PN CN112250896-A; CN112250896-B
TI Preparation of pressing color-changing light            emitting sensor film formed based on carbon dot and            hydrotalcite by dissolving divalent soluble metal salt            and trivalent soluble metal salt in carbon dioxide and            deionized water.
AU SHI W
   XING X
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2021111468
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of pressing color-changing light                emitting sensor film formed based on carbon dot and                hydrotalcite includes preparing nitrate                hydrotalcite by hydrothermal method comprising                dissolving divalent soluble metal salt and                trivalent soluble metal salt in carbon dioxide                (CO2) and deionized water to obtain mixed salt                solution, where the molar ratio of divalent metal                ion and trivalent metal ion is 1-3, and the                concentration of divalent metal is 0.01-1.6 M,                slowly dropping 1-4 M sodium hydroxide (NaOH)                solution into three-mouth flask until the pH is                6.5-8.7, pouring the obtained slurry into a high                pressure reaction kettle, crystallizing for 15-20                hours at 100-120 degrees C, cooling the reaction                kettle to room temperature, centrifuging the                obtained colloidal material and washing the                precipitates with water for 4-6 times, and vacuum                drying at 50-70 degrees C to obtain nitrate                hydrotalcite; and preparation of intercalated                hydrotalcite.
   USE - The method is for preparation of pressing                color-changing light emitting sensor film formed                based on carbon dot and hydrotalcite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides film, which has double                emission (fluorescence and phosphorescence) action                of carbon dots. When performing pressure sensing                test, the film is not only in the aspect of                fluorescence and phosphorescence, but also in                linear relationship with the pressure. After the                film is pressed, it can be heated to the original                state in a short time. After the film is irradiated                for 1 hour under UV light, the fluorescence                intensity and the phosphorescence intensity of the                film are only reduced to 80% of the original value,                and the film exhibits good sensitivity to external                pressure, reversibility and light (fluorescence,                phosphorescence) stability. The film with double                emitting performance greatly improves the                sensitivity of the sensor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of pressing color-changing light                emitting sensor film formed based on carbon dot and                hydrotalcite comprises preparing nitrate                hydrotalcite by hydrothermal method comprising                dissolving divalent soluble metal salt and                trivalent soluble metal salt in 90-150 ml CO2 and                deionized water to obtain mixed salt solution,                where the molar ratio of divalent metal ion and                trivalent metal ion is 1-3, and the concentration                of divalent metal is 0.01-1.6 M, slowly dropping                1-4 M NaOH solution into three-mouth flask until                the pH is 6.5-8.7, pouring the obtained slurry into                a high pressure reaction kettle, crystallizing for                15-20 hours at 100-120 degrees C, cooling the                reaction kettle to room temperature, centrifuging                the obtained colloidal material and washing the                precipitates with water for 4-6 times, and vacuum                drying at 50-70 degrees C to obtain nitrate                hydrotalcite; preparation of intercalated                hydrotalcite by dissolving 0.012-0.03 mol                3-aminobenzenesulfonic acid in 100-150 ml water,                adding 1.0-2.0 g nitrate hydrotalcite, and stirring                at 60-80 degrees C for 70-80 hours under the                protection of nitrogen, washing the intercalated                hydrotalcite by centrifuging the product for 4-6                times with water, and finally dispersing in water;                preparation of carbon dots/hydrotalcite by pouring                the intercalated hydrotalcite dispersion liquid                into a high pressure reaction kettle, reacting for                10-12 hours at 200-220 degrees C, cooling the                reaction kettle to room temperature, centrifuging                the product, washing the obtained carbon                dots/hydrotalcite with water for 4-6 times, and                finally dispersing in water; and preparing carbon                dots/hydrotalcite-polyvinyl alcohol composite film                by adding polyvinyl alcohol into water, stirring                and dissolving at 85-95 degrees C to obtain 3-10                mass% polyvinyl alcohol solution, mixing the carbon                dots/hydrotalcite dispersion liquid with the                polyvinyl alcohol solution, stirring for 3-3.5                hours at 55-65 degrees C, ultrasonically processing                for 30-40 minutes, pouring the mixed solution into                surface dish, and drying at 50-60 degrees C to                obtain carbon dots/hydrotalcite-polyvinylalcohol                composite film i.e. pressing color-changing light                emitting sensor film formed based on carbon dots                and hydrotalcite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The divalent metal ion is                  selected from magnesium ion (Mg2+), zinc ion                  (Zn2+) and/or calcium ion (Ca2+). The trivalent                  metal ion is selected from iron ion (Fe3+) and/or                  aluminum ion (Al3+).

PN CN111449698-A
TI Preparing scar-removing autologous skin tissue cell and platelet-rich plasma polymer used in dot, linear and small-area platelet scarring, by selecting normal skin around wound surface, infiltrating anaesthesia region and forming polymer.
AU YOU L
AE YOU L (YOUL-Individual)
GA 202072828A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a scar-removing autologous skin tissue cell and a platelet-rich plasma (PRP) polymer, involves (a) skin supplying region generally selecting the normal skin around the wound surface, exposing the operation region, and configuring the local anesthetic and the swelling liquid, (b) infiltrating the anaesthesia region, shearing to remove scar surface tissue, or using a super-pulse carbon-dioxide lattice laser to gasify scar surface tissue until wound surface dense dot bleeding, and normal saline gauze covering, (c) estimating the pickup amount from the wound volume, cutting the skin tissue from the skin surface by taking the skin tissue and soaking in normal saline, preliminarily cleaning and filtering the collected crushed particles, shearing the fine scissors into polar fine blocks, soaking the fine cutting blocks in brine again, filtering adipose tissue, and leaving the skin tissue cells, and (d) adding autologous PRP to form a viscous polymer.
   USE - The method is used for preparing a scar-removing autologous skin tissue cell and a platelet-rich plasma polymer, which is used in dot-like, linear and small-area platelet scarring (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is non-invasive and painless, has no infection, is high safety coefficient, provides the risk probability of the proliferative scar healing to form a new wound surface is zero, reaches 98% recovery effect without infection and scarring after treatment, and improves the skin texture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a scar-removing autologous skin tissue cell and a platelet-rich plasma (PRP) polymer, involves (a) skin supplying region generally selecting the normal skin of 10-15 cm around the wound surface after scars are removed, exposing the operation region and the conventional disinfecting laying towel, and configuring the local anesthetic and the swelling liquid, (b) locally infiltrating the anaesthesia region, shearing to remove scar surface tissue by 1-2 mm, or using a super-pulse carbon-dioxide lattice laser to gasify scar surface tissue until wound surface dense dot bleeding, normal saline gauze covering, (c) estimating the pickup amount from the wound volume, cutting the skin tissue at a distance of 5 mm from the skin surface by taking the skin tissue, and soaking in normal saline at the same time, preliminarily cleaning and filtering the collected crushed particles, repeatedly shearing the fine scissors into polar fine blocks, soaking the fine cutting blocks in brine again, filtering adipose tissue, and leaving the skin tissue cells, and (d) adding an appropriate amount of autologous PRP to form a viscous polymer, preferably autologous skin tissue cells and PRP polymers. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of applying the scar-removing autologous skin tissue cell and PRP polymer, comprising (A) extracting autologous small unit skin cells from normal skin tissues at the periphery of the facial scars of the facial scars by using a pickup sharp needle with the diameter of 1.0-1.2 mm, and performing separation, purification and screening treatment, (B) adding a proper amount of autologous platelet-rich plasma to prepare autologous skin tissue cells and PRP polymers, and applying the autologous skin cells and PRP polymers prepared by the method to dot-like, linear and small-area platelet scarring, (C) flatly laying the prepared autologous skin tissue cells and the PRP polymer on the wound surface after the scar tissue is removed, where the thickness is slightly higher than that of the skin surrounding the wound surface, the combined action of the autologous living cells and the high-energy biological factor keratinocyte growth factor, the Vaseline (RTM: Petroleum jelly) gauze and the sterile dressing are wrapped and fixed, and (D) carefully removing the dressing from 10-12 days post-operative, where the planted skin tissue cells are substantially all viable, nearly completely near normal skin cell separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The separation in step (A) is specifically a density gradient principle, and the cells, preferably autologous cells are separated by different densities. The method specifically involves preparing the cell suspension by taking a separated autologous cell culture solution, respectively putting the cell suspension into a micro-culture tray by using a straw, performing culture at 0.05 ml, saturation humidity and 37 degrees C for each hole, observing by an inverted microscope every day, selecting a hole with only one colony to grow, cloning a large amount of propagation, moving to a tissue culture bottle with a feeding layer, and transferring 2-4 generations to obtain a clone strain. The screening in step (A) specifically involves flowing a solution of a stable cell line in a channel, classifying the types of cells in the solution, and performing detection, and pushing cells in a solution flowing in the channel to flow into an adjacent flow channel through communication path for screening. The local anesthetic is configured as a 2% lidocaine and 1% epinephrine 3 drops are added. The swelling solution is configured to add 100 ml 0.9% sodium chloride, 2 ml lidocaine, 0.1 ml 1% epinephrine, and 1 ml 5% sodium bicarbonate.
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary.

PN CN111357582-A
TI Cultivating strawberries in greenhouse comprises e.g. taking flat and fertile fields that have not been planted in past two years as strawberry planting fields, spraying sterilizing liquid, digging planting pits and covering animal feces.
AU HE R
   GUO X
AE HE R (HERR-Individual)
GA 202063850H
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating strawberries in greenhouse comprises (i) planting field and selecting plant comprising taking flat and fertile fields that have not been planted in the past two years as strawberry planting fields, and taking seedling varieties that are robust and resistant to insects as planting plants, (ii) preparing site and constructing greenhouse comprising plowing planting field selected in (i) 15-20 days before strawberry planting, spraying sterilizing liquid for sterilizing, and building planting greenhouse on planting field by the operator, and (iii) planting pit digging comprises digging planting pits 5-7 days before strawberry planting, where planting pit depth is 30-40 cm, controlling length and width at 20-30 cm, horizontal spacing of planting pits at 40-50 cm, and longitudinal spacing at 10-25 cm, excavating the planting pit, burying a layer of 5-8 cm thick animal feces at the bottom of the planting pit, and covering animal feces with a layer of 20-25 cm thick fertile soil.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating strawberries in greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes preset planting pits containing sufficient nutrients to grow strawberries; improves survival rate of strawberry plants; provides sufficient nutrients to the strawberry plants, so that strawberries grow stably; makes the fruits grow sweet and big; improves photosynthesis by applying carbon dioxide; makes strawberry harvest in advance; improves the quality of strawberry fruit, and yield by more than 10%; and achieves the effect of increasing yield and income.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating strawberries in greenhouse comprises (i) planting field and selecting plant comprising taking flat and fertile fields that have not been planted in the past two years as strawberry planting fields, and taking seedling varieties that are robust and resistant to insects as planting plants, (ii) preparing site and constructing greenhouse comprising plowing the planting field selected in (i) 15-20 days before strawberry planting, spraying sterilizing liquid for sterilizing, and building planting greenhouse on the planting field by the operator, (iii) planting pit digging comprises digging planting pits 5-7 days before strawberry planting, where planting pit depth is 30-40 cm, controlling length and width at 20-30 cm, horizontal spacing of planting pits at 40-50 cm, and longitudinal spacing at 10-25 cm, excavating the planting pit, burying a layer of 5-8 cm thick animal feces at the bottom of the planting pit, covering the animal feces with a layer of 20-25 cm thick fertile soil, and burying a layer of covering material on the fertile soil, (iv) watering the planting pit that has been filled up the day before planting, cultivating the plants, cultivating two plants in each planting pit, stabilizing the plants by watering immediately after planting, and the plants will survive about 7-8 days later, (v) early management comprising after the plants survive, spraying a special nutrient solution for every 3-5 days, after the plants grow leaves, removing the dead leaves in time, thinning buds and flowers during budding and flowering, mainly leaving 1, 2 and 3 flowers, and first 4 and 5 flower buds, removing frozen flowers and the severely deformed fruits and top inflorescence of each strawberry leaves 6-7 fruits, controlling total amount of flowers per plant at 10-12, using artificial pollination at the same time, during the preliminary management, controlling the temperature in the greenhouse at 22-27 degrees C during the day and 15-20 degrees C at night, and (vi) later management comprising after the plants grow fruits, controlling the temperature in the greenhouse at 15-25 degrees C during the day and 10-15 degrees C at night, applying a special complex fertilizer for every 15-20 days, watering for once every 3-5 days, cleaning old and dead leaves regularly, applying carbon dioxide in the greenhouse, and picking strawberry fruit picked after the strawberry fruit coverage is greater than 80%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), installing a lighting lamp and a temperature monitoring module in the planting greenhouse. In the step (vi), the method for producing carbon dioxide is mixing sodium bicarbonate into an aqueous solution, then adding an equal amount of potassium dihydrogen phosphate, reacting sodium bicarbonate and potassium dihydrogen phosphate in the aqueous solution, and preparing carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the covering material is a mixture of plant ash and cake fertilizer in a weight ratio of 3:1. The cake fertilizer is bean cake, rape seed cake, cotton seed cake and/or peanut cake. In the step (v), the specially prepared nutrient solution comprises 20-30 pts. wt. calcium nitrate, 10-15 pts. wt. folic acid, 11-16 pts. wt. sucrose, 1-3 pts. wt. potassium nitrate, 2-7 pts. wt. vitamins, 3-8 pts. wt. amino acids, and 60-68 pts. wt. pure water. The amount of the special nutrient solution sprayed on each planting pit is 50-70 g. In the step (vi), the special complex fertilizer comprises 10-15 pts. wt. potassium chloride, 15-25 pts. wt. straw scraps, 20-25 pts. wt. livestock and poultry dry manure, 32-50 pts. wt. plant ash, 3-5 pts. wt. monoammonium phosphate, and 3-5 pts. wt. nitrogen fertilizer. The amount of the complex fertilizer sprayed on each planting pit is 60-80 g.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

AB    (CN210862210-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a cement sintering system based on pure oxygen combustion, the system comprises a pure oxygen producing device, an oxygen conveying pipe, rotary kiln, tertiary air pipe, a decomposing furnace, kiln tail pre-heater, a high-temperature fan, a tail gas processing device, a CO2 capture device, cooler, fan, cooling machine wind pipe, a cooling machine residual air pipe, a waste heat recycling device and a heat exchanger. way of this utility model adopts heat exchange for heating and improve the burning efficiency to pure oxygen burning of the involved, pure oxygen at high temperature at the same time, this utility model changes the generated divided into three paths into the combustion system, increase the burning temperature, reduce smoke, so as to reduce the cement burning heat consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cement sintering system based on pure oxygen combustion includes pure oxygen preparation device for producing high-concentration oxygen, connected to oxygen conveying pipe; rotary kiln for calcining hot raw material exiting decomposition furnace, connected to oxygen conveying pipe for conveying high-temperature pure oxygen into rotary kiln; kiln head burner connected with oxygen conveying pipe for delivering high-temperature pure oxygen into kiln head burner; tertiary air pipe connecting with oxygen conveying pipe; decomposition furnace connected to tertiary air pipe for transporting high-temperature pure oxygen into decomposition furnace for fuel combustion; kiln tail preheater connected to decomposition furnace, for preheating raw meal; high-temperature fan connected to kiln tail preheater through pipeline, for traction and transportation of flue gas generated after combustion; exhaust gas treatment device connected to high-temperature fan, for performing dust removal, desulfurization and denitrification treatment on flue gas that is transported; carbon dioxide (CO2) capture device connected to exhaust gas treatment device, for CO2 capture of treated flue gas; cooling machine connected to rotary kiln, for cooling clinker output by rotary kiln; blower arranged on side of cooling machine, for providing sufficient cooling air into cooling machine; cooler hot air pipe connected to cooler, for transporting hot air generated in first half of cooler; cooler residual air pipe connected to cooler, for transporting hot air generated in second half of cooler; waste heat recovery device connected to end of cooler hot air pipe and cooler waste air pipe, for recovering waste heat of conveyed hot air; and heat exchanger connected with oxygen conveying pipe and hot air pipe of cooler, for exchanging heat between pure oxygen and high-temperature waste heat air, and preheating pure oxygen into high temperature pure oxygen. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cement sintering method based on pure oxygen combustion.

   USE - Cement sintering system based on pure oxygen combustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can effectively improve the combustion efficiency, improve the combustion temperature, reduce the smoke amount, so as to reduce the cement burning heat consumption.

PN US2020148960-A1; WO2020096741-A1; US10703984-B2
TI Performing fluidized coking comprises e.g. exposing feedstock to first portion of fluidized bed having solid particles in coking zone of reactor under thermal cracking conditions and introducing oxygen-containing stream into reactor.
AU HARANDI M N
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202039889D
AB    NOVELTY - Performing fluidized coking, comprises: exposing a feedstock comprising a T10 distillation point of greater than or equal to 343 degrees C to a first portion of a fluidized bed comprising solid particles in a coking zone of a reactor under thermal cracking conditions to form a 343 degrees C liquid product; introducing an oxygen-containing stream into a stripping zone of the reactor; passing at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke from the reactor to at least one of a gasifier and a burner; exposing the a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke to oxidation conditions in the at least one of a gasifier and a burner to form partially oxidized solid particles and a gas phase product comprising carbon oxide and carbon dioxide; removing at least a first portion of the partially oxidized solid particles from the a gasifier and a burner; and passing at least a second portion of the partially oxidized solid particles into the coking zone of the reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for performing fluidized coking.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: allows the temperature of the stripping zone that to be increased relative to the temperature of the coking zone of a fluidized coking system; utilizes flow of oxygen can be controlled that achieve a desirable temperature in the stripper while the reactor temperature is independently set by preheating of the feed and/or hot coke circulation to the reaction zone; utilizes reaction of oxygen with coke and/or other hydrocarbons present in the stripping section that is sufficiently fast and sufficiently exothermic to allow for a significant temperature rise in the stripping zone, this can allow the temperature of the stripping zone to be increased relative to the temperature of the coking zone of a fluidized coking system; utilizes flow of oxygen can be controlled that achieve a desirable temperature in the stripper while the reactor temperature is independently set by preheating of the feed and/or hot coke circulation to the reaction zone; utilizes oxygen-containing gas as the stripping gas that improved yield and reduced equipment complexity; and operates the stripping zone at a temperature above the temperature of the coking zone that result in improved yield of liquid products, e.g. C5+ liquid product yield increase of greater than or equal to 1 wt.% relative to the weight of the input feed to the coker.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing fluidized coking, comprises: exposing a feedstock comprising a T10 distillation point of greater than or equal to 343 degrees C to a first portion of a fluidized bed comprising solid particles in a coking zone of a reactor under thermal cracking conditions to form a 343 degrees C liquid product, where the thermal cracking conditions is effective for depositing coke on the solid particles; introducing an oxygen-containing stream into a stripping zone of the reactor, where the stripping zone comprises a second portion of the fluidized bed, under stripping conditions to oxidize at least a first portion of the deposited coke, a stripping temperature of the stripping conditions are greater than a cracking temperature of the thermal cracking conditions; passing at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke from the reactor to at least one of a gasifier and a burner; exposing the a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke to oxidation conditions in the at least one of a gasifier and a burner to form partially oxidized solid particles and a gas phase product comprising carbon oxide and carbon dioxide; removing at least a first portion of the partially oxidized solid particles from the a gasifier and a burner; and passing at least a second portion of the partially oxidized solid particles into the coking zone of the reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The molar flow rate of the oxygen in the oxygen-containing stream comprises 0.1-10% of a molar flow rate of the feedstock. The stripping temperature is greater than the cracking temperature by 3-14 degrees C, preferably 15-60 degrees C or 5-22 degrees C. The stripping conditions further comprise oxidizing hydrocarbons in the second portion of the fluidized bed. The second portion of the partially oxidized solid particles is passed into the coking zone of the reactor without externally passing partially oxidized solid particles into the stripping zone of the reactor. The oxygen-containing gas is passed into the stripping zone at many locations. The portion of the solid particles comprises deposited coke are passed into a gasifier, where the oxidation conditions comprise gasification conditions, and the gas phase product further comprises hydrogen gas. The method further comprises separating carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide from the gas phase product to form at least a synthesis gas stream. The passing at least a portion of the solid particles comprises deposited coke from the reactor to the gasifier comprises passing the at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke to a heater, and passing the at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke from the heater to the gasifier. The passing at least a second portion of the partially oxidized coke particles from the gasifier to the coking zone of the reactor comprises passing the at least a second portion of partially oxidized coke particles to a heater, and passing the at least a second portion of the partially oxidized coke particles from the heater to the coking zone of the reactor. The thermal cracking conditions and/or the stripping conditions comprise greater than or equal to 10 wt.% conversion of the feedstock relative to 343 degrees C. The stripping gas comprises air, at least a portion of the air for the stripping gas and air for the gasifier and/or burner is originated from a common compressor. The stripping gas comprises air and steam, the air and steam are supplied to the stripper at least in part through a common piping and distribution system. The reactor does not include a baffle between the coking zone and the stripping zone. Preferred Components: The oxygen-containing stream comprises greater than or equal to 50 vol.% of air. The oxygen-containing stream is substantially free of added steam. The oxygen-containing stream comprises less than or equal to 49 vol.% of added steam. The solid particles comprise coke particles.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

density at about 1x                106/ml, amplifying and culturing for 10-12                days.

PN CN110172444-A
TI Preparing human cartilage stem cell comprises cutting cartilage tissue, adding e.g. P-type collagenase, digesting, centrifuging, resuspending in saline, microscopically observing, staining by antibodies, and transferring to flow cytometry.
AU WANG J
   XIE Z
   PAN C
   DING X
   YIN J
   LIU D
AE JIANGSU SAIERSHIDAI HEALTH IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201975555U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing human cartilage stem cell comprises diluting laminin with a sterile Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline, dispensing into multiple sub-packages, diluting the portion of dispensing solution with sterile Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline, taking the cartilage tissue from the fascia and perichondrium of human articular cartilage, placing the cartilage tissue in the centrifuge tube in culture dish, washing with physiological saline, fixing the cartilage tissue on both sides with a gum, cutting the cartilage tissue, transferring the tissue into centrifuge tube, adding P-type collagenase and dispase enzyme, digesting, diluting the filtered liquid with physiological saline, centrifuging, collecting the bottom precipitate, resuspending in physiological saline, counting, placing in carbon dioxide incubator, microscopically observing the cells, taking P3-P5 generation stem cells, digesting, centrifuging, staining with antibodies, and transferring to flow cytometry.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing human cartilage stem cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can obtain a large amount of uniformity by using a small amount of cartilage tissue; is simple and easy to operate; and has strong repeatability. The cell: has short growth period.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing human cartilage stem cell comprises (a) diluting laminin to 100 mu g/ml with a sterile Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline having a pH of 7.6-8.2, dispensing into multiple sub-packages, diluting the portion of dispensing solution with sterile Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline to 10-20 mu g/ml, coating 35 mm culture dish i.e. 2 ml each per culture dishes, and placing at 4 degrees C overnight or 37 degrees C for 1 hour, (b) taking the cartilage tissue from the fascia and perichondrium of human articular cartilage, then placing in 5 ml centrifuge tube containing normal saline, storing at 4 degrees C, and separating within 12 hours, (c) placing the cartilage tissue in the centrifuge tube in 90 cm culture dish, washing with physiological saline for 2-3 times, fixing the cartilage tissue on both sides with a gum, cutting the cartilage tissue into a thickness of more than 1 mm2 with a sterile scalpel, transferring the tissue into a 50 ml centrifuge tube, cutting the tissue block of 0.5-1 mm3 size, then adding 2 ml P-type collagenase and 2 ml dispase enzyme, digesting for 1-2 hours to obtain cell suspension, then diluting the cell for 2 times with the medium, then passing through 100 mesh screen, diluting the filtered liquid for 2 times with physiological saline, then centrifuging at a rate of 1000-1500 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes, collecting the bottom precipitate, resuspending in physiological saline, and centrifuging for 2 times, (d) resuspending the cell pellet finally centrifuged in serum-free medium, counting, inoculating in a density of 0.5-0.8x 103/ml into a petri dish coated with laminin in advance, then placing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, removing the unadhered cells by changing the medium within 30-40 minutes, then adding new medium, and changing the solution in whole amount for every 3 days, (e) microscopically observing the cells after initial screening, when the single cell forms a clonal cluster greater than or equal to 30 cells then marking it with a marker pen, when the monoclonal is up to 90% confluence then picking up a single clone cell cluster with a cloning ring, inoculating into a new culture flask at a density of 1-2x 103/ml for culturing, changing the medium for every 3 days, and passing the cells when the cells grow to 80% confluence, and (f) taking P3-P5 generation stem cells, digesting, centrifuging, staining with antibodies, and transferring to flow cytometry for detecting the expression of cluster of differentiation-44 (CD44), cluster of differentiation-90 (CD90), cluster of differentiation-166 (CD166), cluster of differentiation-34 (CD34) and cluster of differentiation-45 (CD45).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mass concentration of P-type collagenase, and dispase enzyme, respectively is 0.2-0.5%, and 0.1-0.3%. Preferred Method: The step (e) specifically comprises adding the culture medium in the flask, washing the surface of the cells for 2 times by using 0.9% saline, adding 2 ml mild digestive enzyme (0.125%), digesting at 37 degrees C for 1-2 minutes, then diluting with 2 times volume of saline, pipetting the cells with pipette, completely detaching the cells, then transferring the detached cells to a centrifuge tube, centrifuging at a rate of 1000-1500 revolutions/minute for 6-10 minutes, resuspending the serum-free medium, inoculating in a culture flask in a density of 1-2x 105/ml for subculturing, then adding 15 ml medium to each flask, allowing for 2-3 days to reach a cell up to 85% confluence, and then cryopreserving and continuously propagating the cells at the same time. The step (f) specifically comprises washing the cells for 2 times with Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline, taking 0.8-1x 106 cell suspension, placing 100 mu l cell suspension in an Eppendorf tube, adding 15 mu l primary antibody without adding blank tube, incubating at 4 degrees C for 30 minutes in the dark, then adding 400 mu l Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline, centrifuging at a rate of 1500 revolutions/minute for 6 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding 10 mu l secondary antibody, incubating at 4 degrees C for 30 minutes in the dark, then adding 400 mu l Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline, centrifuging at a rate of 1500 revolutions/minute for 6 minutes, discarding the supernatant, again adding 500 mu l Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline, mixing and transfering to the flow tube, and detecting.

PT P
PN US2019239526-A1; US10932475-B2
TI Preparing phosphorus-reduced skim milk by heating or cooling skim milk, bubbling carbon dioxide gas into skim milk, coagulating casein component in skim milk, filtering coagulated skim milk, and adding calcium chloride to permeate.
AU CLARK G H
   CLARK M A
AE CLARK G H (CLAR-Individual)
   CLARK M A (CLAR-Individual)
GA 2019684002
AB    NOVELTY - Phosphorus-reduced skim milk is prepared by providing skim milk; heating or cooling the skim milk to 65 degrees F; bubbling carbon dioxide (CO2) gas into the skim milk until a pH of 4.54.6 is reached; coagulating a casein component in the skim milk to form coagulated skim milk; filtering the coagulated skim milk by passing it through a filter having pores, where each pore has a size of 40 mu to separate the coagulated casein as retentate and liquid serum as permeate; retaining the coagulated casein as coagulated retentate; adding calcium chloride to the permeate; adjusting the pH of the permeate to 6.5-7.0 with a base such as sodium hydroxide to form a white crystalline precipitate of calcium phosphate in the permeate; filtering the permeate in an ultra-Filtration unit to separate the calcium phosphate from the permeate to form a phosphorus-reduced serum; and mixing the coagulated retentate with the phosphorus-reduced serum to form phosphorus-reduced skim milk.
   USE - The methods are useful for preparing phosphorus-reduced skim milk, and lactose-free and phosphorus-reduced milk (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process eliminates the conventional use of lactase enzyme to hydrolyze lactose into its simple sugar components.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing lactose-free and phosphorus-reduced milk which involves providing skim milk; pasteurizing the skim milk; pre-cooling the skim milk to between about 5 degrees C to about 9 degrees C; pre-filtering the skim milk with a filter having pore sizes from about 30 to about 40 microns to remove any suspended solids to form pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk; cooling the pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk to about 4 degrees C; separating solid and liquid phases of the pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk with ultra-centrifugation to produce a solid phase comprising casein micelles, fat globules and globular proteins and a liquid phase comprising water, dissolved sugars, minerals and lipoprotein particles; collecting and storing the solid phase in a stainless-steel tank; pumping the liquid phase into a feed section of an ultra-Filtration unit; filtering the liquid phase by passing it through a filter secured in the ultra-Filtration unit, where the filter has pores having pore sizes of 0.5-40 mu , and where a portion of lactose and a portion of minerals present in the liquid phase are filtered out to form a filtered liquid phase; cooling the filtered liquid phase to between about 5 degrees C and 7 degrees C; bubbling CO2 into the filtered liquid phase to reduce the pH to 4.5-5.0 to cause any lactose present to form into lactose crystals and to form a CO2 infused filtered liquid phase; returning the CO2 infused filtered liquid phase to the pre-filter; passing the CO2 infused filtered liquid phase through the pre-filter to remove any lactose crystals present to form a lactose-free, CO2 infused filtered liquid phase; recombining the solid phase with the lactose-free, CO2 infused filtered liquid phase in the stainless-steel tank to form lactose-free skim milk; heating or cooling the lactose-free skim milk to 65 degrees F; bubbling CO2 gas into the lactose-free skim milk until a pH of 4.5-4.6 is reached; coagulating a casein component in the lactose-free skim milk to form coagulated skim milk; filtering the coagulated skim milk by passing it through a filter having pores, where each pore has a size of 40 mu to separate the coagulated casein as retentate and liquid serum as permeate; adding calcium chloride to the permeate; adjusting the pH of the permeate to 6.5-7.0 with a base such as sodium hydroxide to form a white crystalline precipitate of calcium phosphate in the permeate; filtering the permeate in an ultra-filtration unit to separate the calcium phosphate from the permeate to form a phosphorus-reduced serum; and mixing the casein retentate with the phosphorus-reduced serum to form lactose-free, phosphorus-reduced skim milk.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The method involves initial steps of: providing raw milk comprising skim milk and cream; separating the skim milk and the cream by lowering the temperature of the raw milk to 2-7 degrees C; and removing the cream. Calcium chloride is added in an amount of 3-4 moles of calcium for every 2 moles of phosphorus in the skim milk. The method further involves adding calcium to the phosphorus-reduced skim milk in the form of calcium gluconate or calcium citrate, where calcium is added to achieve a final calcium concentration of 4.8-5.3 grams per gallon of skim milk. The method also involves pooling the cream in a jacketed stainless-steel tank; and chilling the separated cream to about 7 degrees C. It further involves: (a) storing the pasteurized skim milk at a temperature of about 4 degrees C before the pre-cooling step; (b) agitating the pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk while cooling it down before the ultra-centrifugation step; (c) agitating the solid phase when collecting and storing the solid phase in a jacketed stainless-steel tank to maintain solids in suspension; (d) passing the filtered liquid phase into a concentrate section of the ultra-filtration unit to remove and direct concentrates to a path selected from a downstream path for further processing, a drain path for elimination, a recycling path and its combinations. It also involves permitting the CO2 infused liquid phase to stand for approximately 6-10 hours before recycling the CO2 infused liquid phase through the pre-filter; and spray drying the lactose-free, phosphorus-reduced skim milk to product lactose-free, phosphorus-reduced skim milk powder.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

TI Mixed culture for mesenchymal stem cells of different tissue sources, by mixing mesenchymal stem cells isolated and purified from adipose tissue, bone marrow tissue, and umbilical cord tissue, culturing and observing cell growth.
AU WANG D
   WANG Y
AE YUNNAN YANLING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2019206252
AB    NOVELTY - Mixed culture method for mesenchymal stem cells of different tissue sources, involves isolating and purifying mesenchymal stem cells from mammalian adipose tissue, bone marrow tissue, and umbilical cord tissue, mixing mesenchymal stem cells isolated and purified from adipose tissue, bone marrow tissue, and umbilical cord tissue, culturing the cells, mixing first mesenchymal stem cells, the second mesenchymal stem cells, and the third mesenchymal stem cells to obtain P1 hybrid cell, inoculating P1 generation mixed cells into the culture flask, centrifuging the cells, storing light yellow supernatant, inoculating P2 generation mixed cells into the culture flask, adding complete medium, shaking, observing the cell growth every day, collecting the cells when the cells are covered with 80-90% of the flask, obtaining P5 mixed cells, collecting P5 mixed cells, detecting and identifying P5 generation mixed cells, and performing homeologous transplantation of qualified P5 hybrid cells.
   USE - The mixed culture method is useful for mesenchymal stem cells of different tissue sources (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables rapid proliferation, excellent cell consistency and resolves inconsistent proliferation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mixed culture method for mesenchymal stem cells of different tissue sources, involves isolating and purifying mesenchymal stem cells from mammalian adipose tissue, bone marrow tissue, and umbilical cord tissue, labeling mesenchymal stem cells isolated and purified from adipose tissue as first mesenchymal stem cells, which are isolated and purified from bone marrow tissue, labeling the mesenchymal stem cells as second mesenchymal stem cells, labeling the mesenchymal stem cells isolated and purified from the umbilical cord tissue as third mesenchymal stem cells, mixing mesenchymal stem cells isolated and purified from adipose tissue, bone marrow tissue, and umbilical cord tissue, culturing the cells, mixing first mesenchymal stem cells, the second mesenchymal stem cells, and the third mesenchymal stem cells in a ratio of 2:3:1 to obtain P1 hybrid cell, inoculating P1 generation mixed cells into the culture flask at 8000-10000/cm2, adding complete medium and 0.1% D solution to the total volume, shaking the mixture, placing the mixture in incubator at concentration of 5% carbon dioxide, culturing the cells in environment of 90% and a temperature of 37 degrees C, observing the cell growth every day, washing the cells for every 3 days, collecting the cells when the cells are covered with 80-90% of the culture bottles to obtain P2 mixed cells, where the complete medium is serum-free medium for mesenchymal stem cell culture, preparing the D liquid by placing the cord blood in centrifuge tube at 4-8 degrees C for 3 hours, centrifuging the cells, storing light yellow supernatant in temperature environment of 4 degrees C, marking the liquid as D liquid, inoculating P2 generation mixed cells into the culture flask at 8000-10000/cm2, adding complete medium and 0.1% D liquid to the total volume, shaking, culturing the cells in an environment of 90% and a temperature of 37 degrees C, observing growth of the cells for every day, washing the cells for every 3 days, collecting the cells while the cells are covered with 80-90% of the flasks to obtain P3 mixed cells, inoculating P3 generation mixed cells into the culture flask at 8000-10000/cm2, adding the cells to the culture medium, culturing the cells to obtain P4 mixed cells, inoculating P4 hybrid cells into the culture flask at 8000-10000/cm2, adding the cells to culture medium, placing the cells in incubator, culturing the medium in an environment having carbon dioxide concentration of 5%, humidity of 90%, and a temperature of 37 degrees C, observing the cell growth every day, washing the liquid for every 3 days, collecting the cells when the cells are covered with 80-90% of the flask, obtaining P5 mixed cells, collecting P5 mixed cells, detecting and identifying P5 generation mixed cells, and performing homeologous transplantation of qualified P5 hybrid cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves obtaining inguinal subcutaneous fat after anesthetizing the mammal, soaking the fat in alcohol for 3 minutes, removing the capillaries, washing with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) for 3-5 times, cutting the fat into 0.5-1 mm3 pellets, adding type I collagen into the culture dish, digesting the mixture at 37 degrees C for 60 minutes, placing the mixture in a centrifuge tube, centrifuging the mixture at 100 g for 10 minutes, removing the upper layer of fat and intermediate liquid, leaving the bottom sediment into 10 ml complete medium, shaking, centrifuging the medium for 10 minutes, removing the supernatant, shaking whole medium to prepare cell suspension, which is transferred into culture flask and placed in incubator at a concentration of 5% carbon dioxide, humidity of 90%, and temperature of 37 degrees C, observing the cell condition after 24 hours, waiting for long spindle cell to adhere to the culture bottle 20-30%, washing the liquid, observing the cell growth every day, washing the liquid once for every 3 days, and collecting P1 passage cells.

TI Catalytic combustion device based on gas phase organic thin two-dimensional zeolite catalyst comprises filter set to vacuum pump connected to tube and shell inlet of catalytic ceramic membrane module set to differential pressure controllers.
AU YU Z
   LI Y
   DAI M
   MA X
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201909203V
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic combustion device based on gas phase organic thin two-dimensional zeolite catalyst comprises inner and outer walls of the tube of a catalytic ceramic membrane module (8) coated with an ultra-thin two-dimensional zeolite catalyst layer, outlet of a filter (10) connected to a tube inlet and a shell inlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module through a pipeline set with a multiple valves, tube outlet and shell exit of the catalytic ceramic membrane module connected to the atmosphere through a pipeline set with a valve, inlet of a vacuum pump (9) connected to the tubular inlet and shell inlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module through a valve. The outlet of the vacuum pump connected to the inlet of the filter, and two differential pressure controllers (12) are respectively set to detect a pressure difference between the tube-side inlet and outlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module and the shell-side inlet and outlet.
   USE - Used as the catalytic combustion device based on gas phase organic thin two-dimensional zeolite catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has high combustion efficiency, low environmental protection energy consumption, no secondary pollution and can be repeatedly used to realize a catalytic combustion process of gas phase organic pollutants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for catalytic combustion of gas phase organic matter comprising opening a valve connecting to the tube inlet and the outlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module, then closing the remaining valves, adding the flue gas containing organic pollutants into the filter through the ventilation pipe, passing to the tube path of the catalytic ceramic membrane module along the ventilation duct, where there is the presence of oxygen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide and water in the flue gas permeate while completing the dust removal, leaving high concentration organic pollutants in contact with ultra-thin two-dimensional zeolite catalyst layer on the inner wall of the tube for catalytic combustion, discharging the produced product through tube outlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module, passing the tube of the catalytic ceramic membrane module through a vacuum pump when the differential pressure controller detects that the pressure difference between the tube inlet and the tube outlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module exceeds a set threshold for carrying out dredging operations, opening a valve connecting the shell-side inlet and shell-side outlet of catalytic ceramic membrane module, then passing the flue gas after the dust removal to the shell of the catalytic ceramic membrane module along the ventilation duct when there is presence of oxygen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide and water in the flue gas permeate, leaving high concentration organic pollutants in contact with the ultra-thin two-dimensional zeolite catalyst layer on the outer wall of the tube for catalytic combustion, then discharging the produced product through the shell-side outlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module, regenerating the catalyst of the inner and outer walls of the catalytic ceramic membrane module when the differential pressure controller detects that the pressure difference between the shell-side inlet and the shell-side outlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module exceeds a set threshold, opening the valve connecting the shell-side inlet and the shell-side outlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module after the catalyst is regenerated, passing the generated product through the catalytic ceramic membrane, discharging the shell side outlet of the module, unblocking the shell side of the catalytic ceramic membrane module by a vacuum pump when the differential pressure controller detects that the pressure difference between the shell-side inlet and the shell-side outlet of the catalytic ceramic membrane module exceeds a set threshold, then repeating the above steps by changing the direction of ventilation, and realizing the process of continuously treating gaseous organic pollutants by catalytic combustion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the catalytic combustion device based on gas phase organic thin two-dimensional zeolite catalyst.
   Catalytic ceramic membrane module (8)
   Vacuum pump (9)
   Filter (10)
   Differential pressure controllers (12)

PN CN109125349-A
TI Preparing gel useful for repairing trauma and            damaged epidermis, by taking umbilical cord, collecting            cells, centrifuging cells, storing in bottle, adding            medical benzalkonium bromide and tightening needle            cap.
AU SHEN J
   LU Z
   HONG J
   ZHANG B
AE TIANJIN XINPUSAIER BIOMEDICAL TECHNOLOGY (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201904986B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a gel involves taking                umbilical cord of healthy full-term newborn,                storing in the collection bottle containing                umbilical cord preservation solution and collecting                -5 ml maternal peripheral blood for five tests of                virus, sterilizing the outside of the collection                bottle with gauze soaked with 75% ethanol, opening                the cap in the biosafety cabinet, removing the                umbilical cord with a hemostat, placing in an                incubator at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide for                more than 4 hours, changing the liquid on the 7th                day, changing the medium by half-time change,                digesting the adherent cells, inoculating into T175                flask, subculturing 40 ml amplified phenol-free                complete medium and passing through the ratio                1:3-1:6, collecting the cells obtained in the each                passage into a 500 ml centrifuge cup, loading into                the prefilled syringe in accordance with the 3                ml/branch, unscrewing the needle cap with a finger,                pushing the push rod to remove the air in the tube                body and tightening the needle cap.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a gel which                is useful for repairing trauma and damaged                epidermis and providing anti-aging effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a gel involves (a1)                taking 8-12 cm umbilical cord of healthy full-term                newborn, storing in the collection bottle                containing umbilical cord preservation solution and                collecting -5 ml maternal peripheral blood for five                tests of virus, (a2) sterilizing the outside of the                collection bottle with gauze soaked with 75%                ethanol, opening the cap in the biosafety cabinet,                removing the umbilical cord with a hemostat,                placing in a 200 ml sterile cup and infiltrating                with 75% ethanol, removing after two seconds,                transfer to a new 200 ml sterile cup and then                washing the umbilical cord repeatedly with                physiological saline for 3-5 times, (a3) placing                the umbilical cord in a sterile plate filled with                physiological saline, squeezing the blood vessels                in the blood vessels with a hemostatic forceps,                continuously cleaning with normal saline, squeezing                the umbilical cord with a hemostat to remove                moisture, adding into 100 ml sterile beaker, using                a sterile scissors to cut the umbilical cord into a                uniform tissue block of about 1 mm3 and (a4) using                a sterile spatula to evenly inoculate the tissue                block in a T75 flask, adding 150-300 pieces per                bottle, raising the flask, adding 15 ml along the                wall of the uninoculated tissue block, screwing the                cap, turning on the bottle, placing the bottle wall                of the inoculated tissue block on top, placing in                an incubator at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide                for more than 4 hours, infiltrating the medium into                each piece of tissue, placing in an incubator for                cultivation, changing the liquid on the 7th day,                removing the tissue block, changing the medium by                half-time change, digesting the adherent cells with                TrypLE (RTM: Trypsin-like protease), inoculating                into T175 flask, subculturing 40 ml amplified                phenol-free complete medium and passing through the                ratio 1:3-1:6, (b) collecting the cells obtained in                the each passage into a 500 ml centrifuge cup,                centrifuging at 3000 revolutions per minute for 30                minutes to obtain umbilical cord mesenchymal stem                cell supernatant and transferring the supernatant                to a Nalgene 1000ml square bottle for storage, (c)                weighing 1 pt. wt. gel powder and 3 pts. wt.                propylene glycol, adding the sterilized gel powder                to the propylene glycol, adding 2 pts. wt. dimethyl                sulfoxide (DMSO) and 35 pts. wt. sterile                phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), refrigerating,                allowing to stand at 4 degrees C for 4 hours and                stirring for 48 hours using magnetic stirrer and                (d) adding medical benzalkonium bromide to the                umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell supernatant of                step (b), adding 3 ml/l 5% medical benzal bromide                in a ratio of 1:2, uniformly mixing the gel,                loading into the prefilled syringe in accordance                with the 3 ml/branch, unscrewing the needle cap                with a finger, pushing the push rod to remove the                air in the tube body and tightening the needle                cap.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The umbilical cord                  preservation solution comprises 50 ml HyClone                  (RTM: Not defined) Dulbecco's modified Eagle                  medium (DMEM)/F-12 medium and 100 U/ml                  gentamicin. The primary phenol-free complete                  medium comprises phenol-free red medium of                  HyClone (RTM: Not defined) DMEM/F-12 and 2 vol.%                  serum substitute. The amplified phenol-free                  complete medium comprises HyClone (RTM: Not                  defined) DMEM/F-12 phenol free red medium and 1%                  serum replacement. PN CN108690737-A
TI Preparing grass carp fish scale-based environmentally friendly dishwashing detergent comprises e.g. washing waste grass carp fish, freezing, drying, crushing, sieving, extracting, taking absolute ethyl alcohol as cosolvent, and extracting.
AU YE J
AE YE J (YEJJ-Individual)
GA 201885054S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing grass carp fish scale-based environmentally friendly dishwashing detergent comprises e.g. washing waste grass carp fish, freezing, drying, crushing, sieving, extracting, taking absolute ethyl alcohol as cosolvent, extracting, vacuum distilling the extract to separate anhydrous ethanol, vacuum drying, adding extract into beaker, heating, adding ethanol and potassium hydroxide solution, stirring for 60-80 minutes, sealing, incubating, curing, heating distilled water, adding obtained product, extract and alkyl glycoside, stirring to obtain mixed solution, mixing modified starch, maltodextrin and distilled water, heating and stirring, adding the mixed solution D, stirring, homogenizing, spray drying, adding distilled water, sodium gluconate, polyanionic cellulose, brown rice enzyme and ginger essential oil micro-sphere, continuously stirring to mix uniformly to obtain dishwashing detergent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing grass carp fish scale-based environmentally friendly dishwashing detergent.
   ADVANTAGE - The detergent has good stability, high removing rate, and good decontamination effect and will not cause property changes due to fluctuation of the temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing grass carp fish scale-based environmentally friendly dishwashing detergent comprises (1) collecting waste grass carp fish, washing with clean water, fast freezing to -40 degrees C at the cooling speed of 0.6-0.8 degrees C/minute, placing in vacuum chamber of vacuum drier to dry, where the absolute pressure in the vacuum chamber is 66-70 MPa, the temperature of heating plate is 35-37 degrees C, and the cold trap temperature is -60 degrees C, (2) crushing, and sieving with 60 mesh sieve, (3) filling the extraction kettle of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for extracting, checking air tightness of device, setting the extracting temperature to 45-55 degrees C, opening carbon dioxide and nitrogen air storage tank valve, setting the extracting pressure to 35-45 MPa, opening the outlet valve to collect the extract A, where extraction time is 2.5-3 hours, (4) taking absolute ethyl alcohol as cosolvent, setting the flow speed to 1.8-2 ml/minute, the extracting temperature to 50-60 degrees C, the extracting pressure to 30-40 MPa, continuously extracting for 2.5-3 hours, and collecting the extract, (5) vacuum distilling the extract to separate anhydrous ethanol, carrying out vacuum drying at 40-50 degrees C in vacuum drying box to obtain extract B, (6) adding 10-20 pts. wt. extract A into beaker, heating to 45-50 degrees C, adding 5-10 pts. wt. ethanol (95%) and volume extract A 1.2-1.3 times volume potassium hydroxide solution (10-20 wt.%), and stirring for 60-80 minutes, (7) sealing by preservative film, placing in incubator for 24-26 hours, taking out, placing in ventilation place and curing for 2 weeks to obtain product C, (8) taking 100-200 pts. wt. distilled water, heating to 45 degrees C, orderly adding 20-30 pts. wt. product C, 3-5 pts. wt. extract B and 9-11 pts. wt. alkyl glycoside, stirring to mix uniformly to obtain mixed solution, (9) mixing 10-20 pts. wt. modified starch, 12-25 pts. wt. maltodextrin and 60-100 pts. wt. distilled water, heating to 60-70 degrees C and stirring for 20-40 minutes, (10) adding the mixed solution D, stirring for 2-5 minutes, (11) placing in homogenizer, homogenizing at pressure of 30-35 MPa and at temperature of 60-65 degrees C for 2-3 times, each time for 5-10 minutes, (12) placing into spray dryer for spray drying, where the air inlet temperature is 180-190 degrees C and the air outlet temperature is 90-100 degrees C, feeding flow rate is 50-60 ml/minute, and (13) adding 100-200 pts. wt. distilled water, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium gluconate, 1.5-3 pts. wt. polyanionic cellulose, 1-2 pts. wt. brown rice enzyme and 0.5-1 pts. wt. ginger essential oil micro-sphere, continuously stirring to mix uniformly to obtain dishwashing detergent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ginger essential oil micro-sphere preparation comprises (a) taking 2-5 pts. wt. ethyl cellulose, adding 40-60 pts. wt. ethanol (80%), stirring and dissolving, (b) adding 5-10 pts. wt. ginger essential oil and 2% glycerol, stirring uniformly, (c) adding 1 wt.% talc powder, stirring uniformly, and (d) spray drying to obtain ginger essential oil micro-sphere, where the feeding speed is 3 ml/minute, the air inlet temperature is 100 degrees C, and the atomizing pressure is 100 kPa.
DC D25 (Detergents - other than soap and used for cleaning (C11D)); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
