PN CN108543050-A
TI Enhancing efficacy of Chinese herbal medicine useful for treating blood cancer, comprises e.g. placing and starting a crusher, placing medicine powder into a stainless steel container and adding phosphate buffered saline.
AU LIN J
AE FOSHAN CHANCHENG QIZHI INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201877238S
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing efficacy of Chinese herbal medicine, comprises e.g. placing and starting a crusher, taking the obtained medicine powder and placing into a stainless steel container; adding Chinese herbal medicine with phosphate buffered saline (PBS) three times into the stainless steel container, pouring dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) for dissolving the medicine, then allowing the medicine to stand at room temperature, filtering and packaging into sterile EP tube having patient's own information code and storing at -20 degrees C; preparing K562 cell culture medium by placing the prepared culture centering the container on the container with the patients information of the two-dimensional code; taking the blood of the patient's body containing K562 cells and placing blood in blood sample chamber; extracting blood K562 cell and adding K562 cell into a culture container using an optical tweezers device; and placing the culture container into an incubator at 34-40 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide and incubating.
   USE - The medicine is useful for treating blood cancer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medicine improves the treatment effect, has the same dose when compared with traditional Chinese medicine decoction and effectively enhanced efficacy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enhancing efficacy of Chinese herbal medicine, comprises (i) taking 10-20 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. figwort, 5-10 pts. wt. preferably 5 pts. wt. white ginseng, 15-20 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. rhubarb, 10-20 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. ginseng, 10-20 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. dendrobe, 15-25 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 5-15 pts. wt. preferably 5 pts. wt. bamboo shavings, 15-20 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. vanillin, 10-20 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. common peony, 1-5 pts. wt., preferably 1 pts. wt. radix isatidis, 1-3 pts. wt., preferably 1 pts. wt. rhizoma polygonati, 5-12 pts. wt. Ligusticum striatum, preferably 5 pts. wt. Ligusticum striatum, 10-15 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. honey-fried liquorice, 5-15 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. iris root, 2-5 pts. wt., preferably 2 pts. wt. dried ginger and 8-16 pts. wt., preferably 8 pts. wt. fructus amomi; (ii) placing and starting a crusher at a 1400-2000 revolutions/minute and rotating for 8-15 minutes; (iii) taking the obtained medicine powder and placing into a stainless steel container; (iv) adding Chinese herbal medicine with phosphate buffered saline (PBS) three times into the stainless steel container, pouring less than 1% dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) for dissolving the medicine, then allowing the medicine to stand at room temperature for 1-5 hours, filtering and packaging into sterile EP tube having patient's own information code and storing at -20 degrees C; (v) preparing K562 cell culture medium by placing the prepared culture centering the container on the container with the patients information of the two-dimensional code; (vi) taking the blood of the patient's body containing K562 cells and placing blood in the blood sample chamber; (vii) extracting blood K562 cell and adding K562 cell into a culture container using an optical tweezers device; (viii) placing the culture container into an incubator at 34-40 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide and incubating under saturated humidity for every 3 days; (ix) taking the EP tube mother liquid, placing in the culture box under normal temperature at 34-40 degrees C for 3-4hours to obtain active culture in Chinese medicine; and (x) taking the secondary cell of logarithmic growth for cell counting and Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's Medium (DMEM) for diluting the K562 cell, diluting the K562 cell into Chinese medicine EP tube for culturing the Chinese medicine of active phagocytosis or killing blood cancer cell along with culture Chinese medicine for K562 phagocytosis or killing of the cell thus increasing the effect after administration of cultured Chinese medicine to the patient to increase the Chinese medicine to patient body for K562 cell phagocytosis or killing or inhibiting effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (x), K562 cells are added to traditional Chinese medicines in small amounts to inhibit or phagocytosis or killing K562 cells in the traditional Chinese medicines. Preferred Components: In the step (v),the K562 cell culture medium is composed of 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) of DMEM (containing glutamine, 10 mM 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid (HEPES), 1 mM sodium pyruvate, 100 U/ml penicillin and 100 mu g/mL streptomycin). The DMEM is a high glucose type cell culture medium. In the step (vii), the aperture device includes a microscope and a microscope nano-optical conversion device disposed on the microscope.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PN CN108276331-A
TI Preparing norfloxacin comprises e. g. adding 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride and an acid binding agent into the reaction kettle, adding N,N-dimethylaminoacrylic acid ethyl ester, stirring and adding toluene and water.
AU WEN X
AE SHANXI YUNPENG PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201856747L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing norfloxacin comprises (i) adding 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride and an acid binding agent into the reaction kettle, adding N,N-dimethylaminoacrylic acid ethyl ester, and stirring , (ii) adding toluene and water into the reaction solution, adding hydrochloric acid to adjust the pH, stirring uniformly, allowing to stand, separating the layers, taking the organic layer, distilling off the residual water and transferring the organic phase to the autoclave, (iii) cooling the organic phase to 15 degrees C, adding triethylamine , introducing carbon dioxide gas to control the reaction pressure to 2 atm, reacting and recovering the N,N-dimethylamine complex by distillation to obtain mother liquor, (iv) adding toluene and sodium hydroxide into the reaction kettle and heating, (v) obtaining the solid and (viii) dissolving the solid obtained in step (vi) in dilute hydrochloric acid, adjusting the pH, allowing the crude product to stand, filtering and recrystallizing from ethanol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing norfloxacin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing norfloxacin comprises (i) adding 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride and an acid binding agent into the reaction kettle, adding 0.6-0.7 pts. wt. N,N-dimethylaminoacrylic acid ethyl ester with stirring, maintaining the reaction solution to 70 plus minus 5 degrees C and stirring for 3 hours, (ii) adding 4-5 pts. wt. toluene and 2-3 pts. wt. water into the reaction solution, adding hydrochloric acid to adjust the pH to 1-2, stirring uniformly, allowing to stand, separating the layers, taking the organic layer, washing to neutral, distilling off the residual water and transferring the organic phase to the autoclave, (iii) cooling the organic phase to 15 degrees C, adding 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. ethylamine, introducing carbon dioxide gas to control the reaction pressure to 2 atm, reacting for 3-5 hours and recovering the N,N-dimethylamine complex by distillation to obtain mother liquor, (iv) adding 4-5 pts. wt. toluene and 0.3-0.4 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide into the reaction kettle and heating to 100-130 degrees C, (v) stirring the reaction liquid in the reaction tank, dropping the mother liquor, distilling off the low-boiling substance produced by the reaction while dropping and carrying out heat preservation reaction for 3 hours, (vi) vacuum extracting the reaction solution and recovering the toluene to dryness to obtain an organic salt, (vii) adding 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. piperazine or ethyl piperazine into the organic salt, adding 0.05 pts. wt. Lewis acid and 4-5 pts. wt. n-butanol, maintaining the temperature at 100-130 degrees C, reflux reacting for 8 hours, cooling and filtering to obtain a solid and (viii) dissolving the solid obtained in step (vi) in dilute hydrochloric acid, adjusting the pH to 7.2-7.3 with alkali, allowing the crude product to stand, filtering and recrystallizing from ethanol. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for refining norfloxacin comprises (a) adding 4-5 pts. wt. norfloxacin crude product and 15-20 pts. wt. methanol into a reaction kettle and stirring, (b) heating to 50 degrees C, adding 0.8-1.0 pts. wt. glacial acetic acid and reflux heating for 2 hours, (c) cooling the cold brine to 10 degrees C and centrifuging to obtain norfloxacin acetate, (d) dropping 9-10 pts. wt. methanol and 3-5 pts. wt. pure water into the reaction kettle, placing the norfloxacin acetate into the water and heating to the dissolution of norfloxacin acetate, (e) adding aqueous ammonia into the reaction droplets until the pH of the reaction solution is 7-7.5 and heating until the pH of the reaction solution is constant and (f) cooling to less than 25 degrees C, washing with pure water, suction filtering and vacuum drying the product to less than 85 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The parts are all in parts by mole. The amount of the acid binding agent is 0.9-1.1 parts by mole when the acid binding agent is triethylamine and the adding amount of the acid binding agent is 0.5-0.7 parts by mole when the acid binding agent is potassium carbonate. The Lewis acid is an aluminum chloride solid or ferric chloride. The parts are all pts. wt. Preferred Method: In the step (b), the glacial acetic acid is added to control the temperature of the reaction liquid at 50-60 degrees C. In the step (ii), the insulation temperature is 60-65 degrees C. In the step (v), the reflux heating rate is 10-15 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN107589100-A; CN107589100-B
TI Estimating morpholine fuel sulfur content sniffer comprises adopting the exhaust gas collecting device of ship provided at monitoring point to collect tail gas from the passing ships on waterway in time sequence.
AU HU J
   ZHU J
   PENG S
   ZHAO H
   WANG X
AE TIANJIN RES INST WATER TRANSPORT ENG (TWTE-C)
GA 201807098N
AB    NOVELTY - Estimating morpholine fuel sulfur content sniffer comprises adopting the exhaust gas collecting device of ship provided at monitoring point to collect tail gas from the passing ships on waterway in time sequence; and the morpholine exhaust gas collecting device comprises a sulfur dioxide (SO2) analyzer and a carbon dioxide (CO2) analyzer; based on multiple SO2 concentration data and multiple CO2 concentration data continuously obtained by the SO2 analyzer and the CO2 analyzer, taking time as abscissa, gas concentration as ordinate and plotting SO2 monitoring curve and CO2 monitoring curve.
   USE - The method is useful for estimating morpholine fuel sulfur content sniffer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the purpose of identifying illegal use of high sulfur oil ships without boarding the case, avoids blind sampling caused by a huge waste, and provides an accurate sampling of ships in the ship-controlled area of China.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Estimating morpholine fuel sulfur content sniffer comprises (i) adopting the exhaust gas collecting device of the ship provided at the monitoring point to collect the tail gas from the passing ships on the waterway in time sequence, each exhaust gas collection interval is not greater than 10 seconds; and the morpholine exhaust gas collecting device comprises a sulfur dioxide (SO2) analyzer and a carbon dioxide (CO2) analyzer; (ii) based on the multiple SO2 concentration data and multiple CO2 concentration data continuously obtained by the SO2 analyzer and the CO2 analyzer, taking time as abscissa and gas concentration as ordinate, and plotting SO2 monitoring curve and CO2 monitoring curve; as each peak on the monitoring curve represents a ship upwind of the monitored point, thud SO2 concentration and CO2 concentration of the tested ship are obtained based on the peaks of SO2 and CO2 obtained under the same monitoring period, and the SO2 background concentration and the CO2 background concentration at the same monitoring time are obtained based on the concentration values corresponding to the peaks and valleys located on both sides of the SO2 peak and the CO2 peak; (iii) based on the material balance of the ship's fuel sulfur content (S%) (1) is S%=( Delta concentration SO2/ Delta concentration CO2x 0.232)x 100% calculating the sulfur content in the fuel of each ship tested in step (ii), the Delta concentration of SO2 is the difference between the SO2 concentration and the SO2 background concentration at the same monitoring time, and the concentration of CO2 is the difference between the CO2 concentration at the same monitoring time and the CO2 background concentration; (iv) comparing the value of sulfur content of each ship to be tested calculated in step (iii) with the standard value of the fuel sulfur content of the ship specified in the ship emission control zone, obtaining the corresponding data collection time t1 approximately t2 of the tested ship with sulfur content exceeding the standard; and (v) obtaining all ships automatic identification system (AIS) data within the monitoring range of the radius R of the peripheral position of the receiving antenna or by ship AIS data telecommunications network, and through real-time wind speed instrument obtaining real-time wind speed s and wind direction; all the suspected ships passing through the monitoring point within t0 minutes before the time point t1 are selected and their navigation path data are extracted, then, the time t3 and the longitude and latitude of the ship at the monitoring point upwind are extracted from the navigation track data of each ship to calculate the distance d of each ship from the monitoring point at that time, calculating the corresponding Delta d value of each ship, (2), Delta d=|(t3-t1)x s-d|, and then locking the ship corresponding to the minimum d value Delta and determining the standard ship for fuel sulfur content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The morpholine exhaust gas collecting device comprises an ultraviolet fluorescence SO2 analyzer and a non-infrared dispersion CO2 analyzer; the accuracy of the SO2 analyzer is less than or equal to 1 ppb, and the measuring range is greater than or equal to 1 ppm, the accuracy of the CO2 analyzer is less than or equal to 1 ppm and the measuring range is greater than or equal to 1000 ppm. The acquisition interval in step (i) is carried for 1-5 seconds. The peaks in the SO2 monitoring curve plotted in step (ii) with a concentration increment less than 3 ppb are eliminated. In step (ii), the SO2 concentration value and the CO2 concentration value are maximum value or the average value of the concentration of the corresponding peak. In step (v), R is 1 km and t0 is 5 minutes.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); W06 (Aviation, Marine and Radar Systems)
MC S03-E04A5; S03-E04D; W06-C01B7

PN CN114621812-A
TI Preparing transmission gear-box nano-diamond            machine oil comprises using the explosive detonation            method preparation the nanometer diamond, mixing the            high energy carbon-rich explosive TNT and black cable,            mixing with lubricating oil base oil by mixing the            modified surface-diamond,.
AU LI Q
   WEN X
   XU Q
   HU Z
   XU Z
AE HENAN HAILITE ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202289386K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing transmission gear-box nano-diamond                machine oil comprises (1) using the explosive                detonation method preparation the nanometer                diamond, mixing the high energy carbon-rich                explosive TNT and black cable according to weight                ratio of 50: 50 together, pressing into a cylinder,                the section diameter is 50 mm, the height is                200-300 mm, the cylinder is hung into the                detonation kettle, vacuumizing, the vacuum degree                is -150 Pa to -180Pa; then punching carbon dioxide                gas in the detonation kettle, the pressure value                reaches 100KPa to 120KPa, sealing the detonation                kettle, using initiator to synthesize nano-diamond;                (2) nano-diamond chemical treatment, remove the                organic matter and impurities, improving the purity                of the nano-diamond, the method is as follows:                taking 1 kg of nanometre diamond prepared by                detonation bombardment, putting into a container,                adding 20 l of hydrochloric acid with volume                concentration of 35 %, stirring uniformly and                heating boiling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                transmission gear-box nano-diamond machine                oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The machine oil has anti-wear effect, reduces                friction, saves fuel, reducing vibration and noise,                realizing friction pair dynamic automatic repairing                function automatically repairing mechanical                abrasion and processing, enhances motor vehicle                safety, shortens the vehicle grinding period, makes                the grinding effect reach the best, which is a                great innovation on the lubricating oil, and it has                great economic and social benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing transmission gear-box nano-diamond                machine oil comprises (1) using the explosive                detonation method preparation the nanometer                diamond, mixing the high energy carbon-rich                explosive TNT and black cable according to weight                ratio of 50: 50 together, pressing into a cylinder,                the section diameter is 50 mm, the height is                200-300 mm, the cylinder is hung into the                detonation kettle, vacuumizing, the vacuum degree                is -150 Pa to -180Pa; then punching carbon dioxide                gas in the detonation kettle, the pressure value                reaches 100KPa to 120KPa, sealing the detonation                kettle, using initiator to synthesize nano-diamond;                (2) nano-diamond chemical treatment, remove the                organic matter and impurities, improving the purity                of the nano-diamond, the method is as follows:                taking 1 kg of nanometre diamond prepared by                detonation bombardment, putting into a container,                adding 20 L of hydrochloric acid with volume                concentration of 35 %, stirring uniformly, heating                boiling, continuously boiling for 1 hours, using                distilled water to wash to neutral, removing                organic matter and metal impurities; then adding 7                l volume concentration of 70 wt.% perchloric acid                and 100 g of potassium dichromate in the container,                stirring uniformly, heating boiling, continuously                heating until the colour is converted to purple                red, removing other mineral impurities; then adding                3-4 l hydrofluoric acid with volume concentration                of 30 % in the container, heating to 80-80 degrees                centigrade, and stirring for 4 hours to remove dust                impurity, then using distilled water to wash to                neutral, drying, crushing, (3) processing the                chemically processed nano-diamond by plasma                hydrogen for 3 hours at 800C, modifying the                surface, making the nanodiamond not agglomerate, in                dispersed state, (4) mixing with lubricating oil                base oil by mixing the modified surface-diamond and                lubricating base oil according to weight ratio of                1: 1000-9000, using stirrer to stir for 4-6 hours,                soaking the gas-solid interface of the nanodiamond                particles surface liquid-solid interface, forming                mixed crude oil essence, the lubricating base oil                is mineral oil or synthetic oil, and (5)                homogenization treatment by high speed shearing for                1-3 hours in the mixed crude oil in high speed                shearing speed of 15-20 m/second, using colloid                mill to ultra-micro crushing the large particles in                the mixed crude oil fine for 1-2 hours, ultrasonic                processing of 28-40 KHz, ultrasonic cavitation for                30-90 minutes to obtain the finished product.

The section includes lubricants of non-petroleum origin eg silicone oils (C10M).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN113913461-A
TI Constructing Bovine viral diarrhea virus (BVDV) E0-E2 gene recombinant adenovirus vaccine by amplifying double-gene fusion expression by overlapping extension PCR, constructing novel recombinant adenovirus shuttle vector and co-transfecting and packing recombinant adenovirus.
AU WANG B
   ZENG Z
   TANG D
   REN J
   HUANG T
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 202215432J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for constructing Bovine viral diarrhea virus (BVDV) E0-E2 gene recombinant adenovirus vaccine involves amplifying the double-gene fusion expression by overlapping extension PCR, constructing novel recombinant adenovirus shuttle vector and co-transfecting and packing the recombinant adenovirus.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing BVDV E0-E2 gene recombinant adenovirus vaccine for preventing Bovine viral diarrhea.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for constructing Bovine viral diarrhea virus (BVDV) E0-E2 gene recombinant adenovirus vaccine involves amplifying the double-gene fusion expression by overlapping extension PCR, constructing novel recombinant adenovirus shuttle vector and co-transfecting and packing the recombinant adenovirus. The method involves (a) adding cloned and identified BVDV E0 and BVDV E2 genomes as templates and primers BVDV E0-F/R and BVDVE2-F/R to the reaction system in centrifuge tubes, then briefly centrifuging to remove air bubbles, pipetting and mixing, placing the tube in PCR apparatus to amplify the BVDV E0 and BVDV E2 genes, (b) taking the recovered E0 and E2 gene fragments as templates, using E0-F and E2-R synthetic primers to perform overlap extension PCR amplification, recovering the E0-E2 fusion gene, double digesting the fusion gene and pDC316-mCMV-EGFP with Hind III enzyme and SalI enzyme to recover the product, ligating the recovered EO-E2 fusion gene fragment and pDC316-mCMV-EGFP vector linearization fragment using T4 ligase, transferring into the competent cells Top 10, culturing, extracting the plasmids and verifying by double digestion and PCR, (c) uniformly mixing the validated shuttle plasmid pDC316-E0-E2-EGFP and the backbone plasmids pBHGlox(delta)E1 and 3re of the AdMax adenovirus system, co-transfecting into HEK293T cells, incubating overnight under 5% carbon dioxide at 37degreesC in cell incubator, observing the cell growth morphology until the cells appears to have cytopathogenic effect (CPE), transferring into a 2 ml cryopreservation tube when half of the cells are suspended and the rest are fluorescent, repeatedly freezing-thawing 3 times at -80degreesC/37degreesC in a water bath, vortexing for 10 minutes to release adenovirus particles, centrifuging at 12000 g and 4degreesC for 5 minutes, filtering using 0.22 mum pore filter to harvest the supernatant and verifying the adenovirus packaging to obtain named Ad5-E0-E2, (d) performing identification of recombinant adenovirus Ad5-E0-E2 expression using SDS-PAGE and Western-blot methods, infecting HEK293T cells with recombinant adenovirus, when the cells had numerous lesions and have not fallen off, collecting the cells to precipitate, lysing to release the adenovirus, subjecting the supernatant to 12% separating gel sodium dodecyl sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE) electrophoresis to separate proteins, transferring the membrane, blocking with 5% non-fat milk powder prepared using tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane (tris)-buffered saline containing Tween 20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) (TBST) at 37degreesC for 2 hours, washing the membrane 3 times with TBST for 10 minutes, using 1:1000 bovine BVDV positive serum as the primary antibody, incubating at 4degreesC overnight, diluting horseradish peroxidase-labeled goat anti-mouse immunoglobulin G (IgG) with TBST in a ratio of 1:1000 and incubating as secondary antibody at 37degreesC for 2 hours, using enhanced chemiluminescence (ECL) kit to develop color exposure and taking pictures and (e) transferring 100 mul HEK293T cells at a density of 1x105 to a 96-well cell culture plate, making 8 serial 10-fold dilutions of the amplified adenovirus in complete medium, discarding the old medium in the corresponding culture wells, adding 90 mul virus solution diluted in the gradient, adding 100 mul complete medium after 12 hours of infection, incubating at 37degreesC under 5% carbon dioxide in cell incubator, observing the cell growth morphology until cytopathic conditions appear, counting the number of fluorescent cells in the penultimate well and the number of fluorescent cells in the last well and calculating virus titers using specific equation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves designing specific PCR primers to amplify the target gene in accordance with the synthesized BVDV E2 and E0 gene fragments, where BVDV E0-F comprises a 31 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) fully defined in the specification, BVDV E0-R comprises a 38 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) fully defined in the specification, BVDV E0-R comprises a 38 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) fully defined in the specification and BVDV E2-R comprises base pair sequence of 5'-acaagcttctgaggccttctgttctg-3', using the recovered EO and E2 gene fragments are used as templates and EO-F, E2-R synthetic primers for overlapping extension PCR amplification and recovering the EO-E2 fusion gene.
   ACTIVITY - Antidiarrheic; Virucide.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Vaccine.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-E99; B04-E08; B04-E05; B14-S11A; B14-S11D3; B14-S12; B04-F11B; B11-C01E; B04-C03C; B04-F01; B04-F11A1E; B04-L03B; B04-G01; B04-B04D4; B04-G27G; C04-E99; C04-E08; C04-E05; C14-S11D3; C14-S12; C14-S11A; C11-C01E; C04-F01; C04-F11B; C04-F11A1E; C04-G01; C04-C03C; C04-L03B; C04-G27G; C04-B04D4; D05-H99

PN CN113480080-A; CN215403609-U
TI Zero-discharge treatment of high-salt organic wastewater, comprises e.g. pretreating, performing ultrafiltration and reverse osmosis, adsorbing calcium and magnesium hardness ions, performing photocatalytic oxidation, removing soluble silicon, selectively intercepting and processing.
AU TAN J
   LONG S
   JIA Y
   ZHU Q
   WANG Z
   LI Q
AE TIANJIN GEOENVIRON WATER TREATMENT TECHN (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B9967P
AB    NOVELTY - Zero-discharge treatment of high-salt organic wastewater, comprises (i) pretreating and removing of chemical oxygen demand (COD), biochemical oxygen demand (BOD), ammonia, total nitrogen (TN), total phosphorus (TP), calcium, magnesium, silicon dioxide pollutants, (ii) performing ultrafiltration and reverse osmosis, (iii) adsorbing calcium and magnesium hardness ions by ion exchange, (iv) adjusting pH value to be acidic and then stripping off carbon dioxide, (v) performing first photocatalytic oxidation reaction on organic matter, (vi) removing the suspended solids and catalyst particles by ultrafiltration, (vii) performing reverse osmosis and removing large amount of soluble silicon, (viii) removing suspended matter and (ix) selectively intercepting, processing to produce sodium chloride, then carrying out second photocatalytic oxidation reaction, ultrafiltration, sodium sulfate evaporation and crystallization of solution rich in sodium sulfate to produce sodium sulfate.
   USE - The method is useful for zero-discharge treatment of high-salt organic wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the high-efficiency removal of pollutants in high-salt organic wastewater, thus to significantly reduce the processing cost of the existing process and improve the operation stability of the organic wastewater discharge treatment system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Zero-discharge treatment of high-salt organic wastewater, comprises (i) pretreating and removing of chemical oxygen demand (COD), biochemical oxygen demand (BOD), ammonia, total nitrogen (TN), total phosphorus (TP), calcium, magnesium, silicon dioxide pollutants in high-salt organic wastewater to obtain the first wastewater, (ii) performing ultrafiltration and reverse osmosis to remove suspended matter, organic matter and inorganic salt in the first wastewater to obtain the second wastewater, (iii) adsorbing calcium and magnesium hardness ions in the second waste water by ion exchange, and releasing hydrogen ions by replacement to obtain the third waste water, (iv) adding hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid to the third waste water to adjust the pH value to be acidic and then stripping off carbon dioxide, reducing alkalinity in the third waste water to obtain the fourth waste water, (v) performing first photocatalytic oxidation reaction on the organic matter in the fourth waste water to obtain the fifth waste water, (vi) removing the suspended solids and catalyst particles in the fifth wastewater by ultrafiltration, neutralizing them to obtain the sixth wastewater, (vii) further performing reverse osmosis concentration treatment on the sixth wastewater, and removing large amount of soluble silicon in the concentrated sixth wastewater to obtain seventh wastewater, (viii) further removeing suspended matter in the seventh wastewater by ultrafiltration and (ix) selectively intercepting seventh wastewater after treatment by nanofiltration, processing the produced water rich in sodium chloride by reverse osmosis and sodium chloride evaporation and crystallizing to produce sodium chloride, then carrying out second photocatalytic oxidation reaction, ultrafiltration, and sodium sulfate evaporation and crystallization treatment of concentrated solution rich in sodium sulfate to produce sodium sulfate.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for treatment device based on the zero-discharge treatment of high-salt organic wastewater, comprising pretreatment system, the inlet of the pretreatment system inputs high-salt organic wastewater, the outlet end of the pretreatment system is in sequence with the first ultrafiltration membrane system, the first reverse osmosis membrane system, the ion exchange system, the decarburization tower, the first photocatalytic oxidation system, the second ultrafiltration membrane system, the neutralization system, the second reverse osmosis membrane system, silicon removal system, external pressure ultrafiltration membrane system and nanofiltration membrane system are connected; the water production port of the nanofiltration membrane system is sequentially connected with the sodium chloride reverse osmosis membrane system and the sodium chloride evaporation crystallization system, the sodium chloride evaporation crystallization system is used to produce sodium chloride; the concentrated liquid outlet of the nanofiltration membrane system is sequentially connected with the second photocatalytic oxidation system, the third ultrafiltration membrane system and the sodium sulfate evaporation crystallization system, the sodium sulfate evaporation crystallization system is used to produce sodium sulfate; both the first photocatalytic oxidation system and the second photocatalytic oxidation system are connected with a catalyst dissolving system and an hydrogen peroxide adding system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pretreatment includes grid filtration, coagulation sedimentation, softening and clarification, biochemical treatment and/or multi-media filtration. The ion exchange includes using an ion exchange resin to exchange and adsorb calcium and magnesium hardness ions in the second wastewater, and replace and release hydrogen ions. The neutralization treatment comprises adding sodium bisulfite and sodium sulfite reducing substances to the fifth waste water after ultrafiltration treatment to neutralize the residual hydroxy radicals in the fifth waste water, where the neutralization treatment time is not less than 15 minutes, and the OPR in the sixth wastewater is not more than 200 mv. The first photocatalytic oxidation reaction comprises sending the fourth wastewater to the first photocatalytic oxidation reactor; adding a ternary nano-doped nano-titanium(IV) oxide catalyst and hydrogen peroxide into the first photocatalytic oxidation reactor, and mixing and stirring the ternary nano-doped nano-titanium(IV) oxide catalyst, hydrogen peroxide, and the fourth wastewater to obtain a mixed solution, subjecting the obtained mixed solution to ultraviolet light oxidation treatment to obtain the fifth waste water. The second photocatalytic oxidation reaction comprises sending the concentrated solution rich in sodium sulfate to the second photocatalytic oxidation reactor; adding a ternary nano-doped nano-titanium(IV) oxide catalyst and hydrogen peroxide to the second photocatalytic oxidation reactor, and mixing and stirring the ternary nano-doped nano-titanium(IV) oxide catalyst, htdrogen peroxide and a concentrated solution rich in sodium sulfate to obtain a mixed solution; performing ultraviolet light oxidation treatment on the obtained mixed solution; subjecting the mixed solution to ultrafiltration and sodium sulfate evaporation and crystallization treatment to produce sodium sulfate. The method comprises dissolving ternary nano-doped nano-titanium(IV) oxide catalyst into an emulsion with a uniform solid content of 1-5% through an ultrasonic vibration emulsification solution system, adding to the first photocatalytic oxidation reactor and the second photocatalytic oxidation reactor; where the wavelength of the ultraviolet lamp is 150-450 nm, both the first photocatalytic oxidation reaction and the second photocatalytic oxidation reaction time are not less than 30 minutes; the concentration of the ternary nano-doped nano- titanium(IV) oxide catalyst is 200-30000 mg/liter, the diameter of the ternary nano-doped nano-titanium(IV) oxide catalyst is 10-500 nm. The method comprises, concentrated water of the fifth waste water after the ultrafiltration treatment entering the catalyst concentration pool, when the first photocatalytic oxidation time has not expired; the catalyst in the catalyst concentration tank is lifted by a pump and then re-enters into the first photocatalytic oxidation reactor to realize the reuse of the catalyst; when the first photocatalytic oxidation time is reached and the catalyst loses its activity, then catalyst in the catalyst concentration tank is lifted by a pump and then enters the sludge dewatering system, and the water content is reduced to below 70% through the sludge dewatering system, and then transported for treatment; the produced water after the ultrafiltration treatment is neutralized to obtain the sixth wastewater.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - The first photocatalytic oxidation system and the second photocatalytic oxidation system both include a reactor body, an ultraviolet light assembly, and a stirring device; the ultraviolet light assembly and the stirring device are both arranged in the reactor body, the catalyst dissolving system and the hydrogen peroxide adding system are both connected to the reactor body for providing catalyst and hydrogen peroxide inside the reactor body, the stirring device is used for mixing and stirring the liquid inside the reactor body.
DC A91 (Ion-exchange resins, polyelectrolytes.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC A12-W11F; A12-W11J; A12-M; D04-B07; D04-B06; D04-A01; E34-B; E34-D04; E31-K05; E32-A02; E32-A04; E31-B03D; E31-F05; E31-N05C; E35-K02; E31-U01; E33-C; E33-B; E31-P03

TI Use of aqueous polyurethane dispersion adhesive            for producing composite foils which are            biodisintegratable at home compost conditions, where            substrate is adhesive-bonded to another substrate with            use of polyurethane dispersion adhesive and substrate            is polymer foil which is biodisintegratable.
AU SCHUMACHER K
   LICHT U
   PIETSCH I
   HECKHOFF L
   HACKHOFF L
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 2021A3739C
AB    NOVELTY - Use of an aqueous polyurethane dispersion                adhesive for producing composite foils which are                biodisintegratable at home compost conditions (25                plus minus 5 degrees C), is claimed, where at least                one first substrate is adhesive-bonded to at least                one second substrate with use of the polyurethane                dispersion adhesive; at least one of the substrates                is a polymer foil which is biodisintegratable at                home compost conditions, and at least 60 wt.%                polyurethane is composed of (a) at least one                diisocyanate, (b) at least one polyesterdiol, and                (c) at least one bifunctional carboxylic acid                selected from dihydroxy carboxylic acids and                diamino carboxylic acids; the polyurethane has a                glass transition temperature below 20 degrees C,                characterized in that the polyurethane either has                no melting point above 20 degrees C or the                polyurethane has a melting point above 20 degrees C                with an enthalpy of fusion lower than 10 J/g; at                least 80 wt.% polyurethane is composed of the                component (b).
   USE - The aqueous polyurethane dispersion adhesive                is useful for producing composite foils which are                biodisintegratable at home compost conditions (25                plus minus 5 degrees C) and producing a home                compostable composite foil and a flexible packaging                made at least in part of the home compostable                composite foil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aqueous polyurethane dispersion adhesive                can provide biodegradable or home compostable                composite foils, preferably flexible packaging for                food packaging, where these can be easily produced                and have high quality of adhesive properties, and                also simultaneously have rapid                biodisintegratability under home composting                conditions, i.e. at 25 plus minus 5 degrees C and                provides excellent adhesive bonding of various                substrates to one another and the adhesive-bonded                composite high strength. The composite foils has                excellent biodegradability and home                compostability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of an aqueous polyurethane dispersion                adhesive for producing composite foils which are                biodisintegratable at home compost conditions (25                plus minus 5 degrees C), is claimed, where at least                one first substrate is adhesive-bonded to at least                one second substrate with use of the polyurethane                dispersion adhesive; at least one of the substrates                is a polymer foil which is biodisintegratable at                home compost conditions, and at least 60 wt.%                polyurethane is composed of (a) at least one                diisocyanate, (b) at least one polyesterdiol, and                (c) at least one bifunctional carboxylic acid                selected from dihydroxy carboxylic acids and                diamino carboxylic acids; the polyurethane has a                glass transition temperature below 20 degrees C,                characterized in that the polyurethane either has                no melting point above 20 degrees C or the                polyurethane has a melting point above 20 degrees C                with an enthalpy of fusion lower than 10 J/g; at                least 80 wt.% polyurethane is composed of the                component (b); a film of the polyurethane adhesive                decomposes at home compost conditions to more than                90 wt.% into carbon dioxide and water within 360                days; a material is biodisintegratable at home                compost conditions if at most 10% of the original                dry weight of the material is found to be present                after aerobic composting at 25 plus minus 5 degrees                C for a period of at most 180 days in a sieve                fraction greater than 2 mm. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a process for producing composite                foils which are biodisintegratable at home compost                conditions (25 plus minus 5 degrees C) involving                providing an aqueous polyurethane dispersion                adhesive with the polyurethane-based, and adhesive                bonding at least two substrates to one another with                use of the aqueous polyurethane dispersion                adhesive, where at least one of the substrates is a                foil which is biodisintegratable at home compost                conditions; and the polyurethane has a glass                transition temperature of below 20 degrees C,                characterized in that the polyurethane either has                no melting point above 20 degrees C or the                polyurethane has a melting point above 20 degrees C                with an enthalpy of fusion lower than 10 J/g; a                film of the polyurethane adhesive decomposes at                home compost conditions to more than 90 wt.% into                carbon dioxide and water within 360 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In use, at least 60                  wt.% polyurethane is composed of (a1) at least                  one aliphatic diisocyanate, (b1) at least one                  aliphatic polyesterdiol, (c1) at least one                  bifunctional carboxylic acid chosen from                  dihydroxycarboxylic acids and diaminocarboxylic                  acids and (d) at least one polyfunctional                  compound which differs from the monomers (a) to                  (c) and which has at least two reactive groups                  selected from primary and secondary amino groups.                  In the use, at least 80 wt.% component (b) is                  composed of at least one aliphatic dicarboxylic                  acid and at least one aliphatic diol. The                  polyurethane dispersion adhesive comprises at                  least one external crosslinking agent. The                  crosslinking agent has been chosen from                  isocyanurates formed from diisocyanates and                  having at least two isocyanate groups, compounds                  having at least one carbodiimide group,                  chemically capped isocyanates, encapsulated                  isocyanates, encapsulated uretdiones, biurets,                  allophanates, aziridines, oxazolines, epoxides,                  and mixtures of the substances mentioned. The                  material of the first substrate has been chosen                  from lignin, starch, cellulose materials,                  polylactic acid, polyglycolic acid, aliphatic                  polyesters, aliphatic-aromatic copolyesters,                  polyhydroxyalkanoates, polypropylene carbonate,                  and mixtures of the materials mentioned, and the                  material of the second substrate has been chosen                  from paper, lignin, starch, cellulose materials,                  polylactic acid, polyglycolic acid, aliphatic                  polyesters, aliphatic-aromatic copolyesters,                  polyhydroxyalkanoates, polypropylene carbonate,                  and mixtures of the materials mentioned. In the                  use, a film of the polyurethane adhesive and/or                  the first and second substrates and/or the                  composite foil is home compostable, where a                  material is home compostable if it is                  biodisintegratable at home compost conditions (25                  plus minus 5 degrees C) and if it decomposes at                  home compost conditions to more than 90 wt.% into                  carbon dioxide and water within 360 days; and a                  material is biodisintegratable at home compost                  conditions if at most 10% original dry weight of                  the material is found to be present after aerobic                  composting at 25 plus minus 5 degrees C for a                  period of at most 180 days in a sieve fraction                  greater than 2 mm. The composite foil is a part                  of a home compostable flexible packaging.                  Preferred Composition: In use, polyesterdiol is                  made of at least 10 mol%, preferably at least 20                  mol% based on the sum of diols used for making                  the polyesterdiol of branched aliphatic diols,                  preferably neopentyl glycol. The polyurethane is                  composed of the component (a), (b2) at least one                  diol, where, of these, (bi) 10-100 mol%, based on                  the total amount of the diols (b), are                  polyesterdiols with a molar mass of 500-5000                  g/mol, (bii) 0-90 mol%, based on the total amount                  of the diols (b), have a molar mass of from                  60-500 g/mol, (c) at least one bifunctional                  carboxylic acid chosen from dihydroxycarboxylic                  acids and diaminocarboxylic acids, (d) optionally                  other polyfunctional compounds which differ from                  the monomers (a) to (c) and which have reactive                  groups, where these are alcoholic hydroxy groups,                  primary or secondary amino groups, or isocyanate                  groups and (e) optionally monofunctional                  compounds which differ from the monomers (a) to                  (d) and which have a reactive group which is an                  alcoholic hydroxy group, a primary or secondary                  amino group, or an isocyanate group. Preferred                  Method: The process involves applying the first                  substrate, which is a home compostable polymer,                  via extrusion coating to the second substrate,                  and applying, as intermediate layer, an aqueous                  polyurethane dispersion adhesive with the                  polyurethane-based.

PT P
PN CN113355489-A; CN113355489-B
TI Method for purifying impurity of molten steel            comprises hermetically installing furnace substrate            part of intermediate frequency furnace with ventilating            layer whose bottom is installed with diffuser, and            performing decarburization slagging.
AU WANG Y
   CAO X
   QI Z
   ZHAO S
   QI L
   CHEN K
   MENG L
   QI G
AE SHANDONG TAISHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A7784Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for purifying impurity of molten steel                by intermediate frequency furnace comprises                hermetically installing furnace substrate part of                intermediate frequency furnace with ventilating                layer whose bottom is installed with diffuser in                the furnace lining. The diffuser is connected with                limestone powder injector. The limestone powder                injector is connected with an inert gas bottle.                Normal top oxygen blowing is performed.                Decarburization slagging is performed to fully                decomposes the limestone powder into calcium oxide                and carbon dioxide. Slag dragging device set on the                upper part drives a slag dragging plate to rotate                above the intermediate frequency furnace. Slag                suction column and slag filtering nets are                installed at the bottom of the slag removal plate                in contact with molten steel. After the impurities                are removed, the slag dragging plate is transferred                to the outside of the intermediate frequency                furnace under the drive of the rotating motor, the                lifting cylinder and bracket motor.
   USE - The method is used for purifying impurity of                molten steel in intermediate frequency                furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can improve the yield of the                product from 70% to more than 95%, effectively                solving the waste caused by the inclusion of                surface polishing, the bottom blowing argon and                limestone powder, the utilization rate of the                limestone powder is high, creating the low                temperature, high alkalinity, oxidizing the                dephosphorization environment, improving the                dephosphorization efficiency of the converter to a                certain extent, the dispersion distribution of CaO                promotes the desulfurization ability of the                converter. The upper part of the slag dragging                device realizes the stirring of the molten steel,                at the same time, realizing the adsorption of                impurities in the slept.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method comprises hermetically installing                furnace substrate part of intermediate frequency                furnace with ventilating layer whose bottom is                installed with diffuser in the furnace lining. The                diffuser is connected with limestone powder                injector. The limestone powder injector is                connected with an inert gas bottle. The inert gas                injected by the inert gas bottle is used as carrier                gas of the limestone powder, and enters the                intermediate frequency furnace from the diffuser                and the ventilating layer at the bottom of the                intermediate frequency furnace. Normal top oxygen                blowing is performed. Blowing oxygen on top of the                furnace is performed for 2-3 minutes. The inert gas                carrying the limestone powder is sprayed from the                bottom of the intermediate frequency furnace.                Decarburization slagging is performed to fully                decomposes the limestone powder into calcium oxide                and carbon dioxide. The inert gas is separated into                small bubbles by the diffuser and the ventilating                layer to rise in the molten steel. The impurity of                the molten steel is brought to the top of the                molten steel. Slag dragging device set on the upper                part drives a slag dragging plate to rotate above                the intermediate frequency furnace. Lifting                cylinder drives the slag dragging plate to fall to                the liquid level position of the molten steel.                Rotating motor drives the slag dragging plate to                rotate and stir the molten steel. The molten steel                makes the limestone powder fully decarburized and                reacted to remove phosphorus. The limestone powder                injector stops working after spraying the limestone                powder of 3-5 minutes, then the inert gas bottle                continuously works for 5-7 minutes. The impurity of                the molten steel is brought to the liquid surface                of the molten steel. Slag suction column and slag                filtering nets are installed at the bottom of the                slag removal plate in contact with molten steel,                The impurities on the surface of the molten steel                are absorbed and removed. After the impurities are                removed, the slag dragging plate is transferred to                the outside of the intermediate frequency furnace                under the drive of the rotating motor, the lifting                cylinder and the bracket motor. The molten steel                purification is completed, and the molten steel                meets other tapping requirements, and the steel is                discharged.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic view illustrating                the intermediate frequency furnace purifying molten                steel impurity structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The gas in the inert                  gas bottle is argon gas. The furnace lining is                  made of magnesite.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-A05A; M24-C01; M24-C06; M24-C07

PN CN112662189-A
TI Porous titanium dioxide modified asphalt comprises porous titanium dioxide, modifier, asphalt, calcium sulfate whiskers, microcrystalline cellulose and modified rubber composition.
AU DAI Y
AE DAI Y (DAIY-Individual)
GA 202151666H
AB    NOVELTY - Porous titanium dioxide modified asphalt comprises porous titanium dioxide, modifier, asphalt, calcium sulfate whiskers, microcrystalline cellulose and modified rubber composition with a mass ratio of 26:14-22:89-105:8-15:6-13:12-20. The porous titanium dioxide comprises tetrabutyl titanate, ethylacetate, ethyl carbonate, asphalt, lithium hydroxide, water, SE-10 emulsifier, melamine and carbontetrachloride with a mass ratio of 34:85-100:56-80:12-28:29-36:12-30:7-15:28-32:13-21. The modifier is polyether polyurethane resin solution, graphene solution, strontium emulsifier, acrylic acid, butyl acrylate, water and 819 photoinitiator with a mass ratio of 30:9-15:3-8:9-17:15-26:6-13:0.03-0.1-10. The modified rubber is composed of styrene-butadiene rubber, maleic anhydride, benzoyl peroxide, and trimellitium acid anhydride, bisphenol A epoxy resin and acrylic acid with a mass ratio of 35:5-11:0.02-0.3:7-16:18-35:5-12.
   USE - Used as porous titanium dioxide modified asphalt.
   ADVANTAGE - The asphalt excellent low-temperature flexibility and temperature resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing porous titanium dioxide modified asphalt (i) adding styrene butadiene rubber, maleic anhydride, benzoyl peroxide, bisphenol A epoxy resin and acrylic acid to the high-speed mixer at a mixing speed of 700 revolutions/minutes and maintaining the system reaction temperature at 30 degrees C, reacting for 5 minutes, heating to 120-180 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, cooling, transferring material to a crusher and crushing to micron level, transferring material to a high-speed mixer at a mixing speed of 700 revolutions/minutes to maintain the system reaction, reacting for 5 minutes at 30 degrees C, adding trimellitic anhydride to the high-speed mixer, and continuing to react for 5 minutes while maintaining the above reaction conditions to obtain a modified rubber; (ii) adding polyether polyurethane resin solution and the graphene solution to the reaction kettle, stirring at a speed of 200 revolutions/minute, maintaining reaction temperature of the system at 30 degrees C for 15 minutes, adding Sr-10 emulsifier, acrylic acid, and acrylic acid mixture of butyl ester, water and 819 photoinitiator to the reaction kettle at the addition rate of 0.2 pts. wt./minute, reacting for 30 minutes under the above reaction conditions, exposing product to 3000W LED ultraviolet light for 5-30 second, and exposing product to 90 degrees C. under -0.1MPa vacuum drying for 5 hours, crushing to obtain modifier; (iii) adding ethyl acetate, ethyl carbonate, pitch, SE-10 emulsifier, melamine and carbon tetrachloride to the reaction kettle at a stirring speed of 180 revolutions/minute and a reaction temperature of 50 degrees C to react for 20 minutes, adding tetrabutyl titanate to the reaction kettle for 15 minutes while maintaining the above reaction conditions, adding lithium hydroxide and water mixture to the reaction kettle at an addition rate of 0.03 pts. wt./minute, maintaining the above reaction conditions to continue the reaction for 30 minutes under the condition of carbon dioxide gas flow, maintaining the carbon dioxide gas flow rate at 0.2 pts. wt./minute, maintaining the stirring speed at 300 revolutions/minute, reacting at 55 degrees C for 7-10 hours under -0.1MPa vacuum drying for 5 hours, crushing, the product is heat treated at 100 degrees C for 1 hours, heat treating at 200 degrees C for 2 hours, 300 degrees C for 2 hours, 400 degrees C for 1 hours, 500 degrees C for 3 hours, 550 degrees C for 3 hours, crushing, washing with 3% hydrochloric acid aqueous solution 3 times, washing with water 3 times, washing with ethanol 3 times, and drying to obtain porous titanium dioxide; and (iv) adding porous titanium dioxide, modifier, asphalt, calcium sulfate whiskers, microcrystalline cellulose and modified rubber to the internal mixer, and banning it at 20-100 degrees C for 15-50 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of porous titanium dioxide, modifier, asphalt, calcium sulfate whisker, microcrystalline cellulose and modified rubber is 26:17.6:102:10.9: 9.9:16.5.

PT P
PN CN112624155-A
TI Preparing battery-grade anhydrous lithium chloride            by heating brine, separating calcium and magnesium            slag, acidifying, concentrating lithium chloride            solution, centrifuging, dehydrating wet lithium            chloride, cleaning, and packaging.
AU LI L
   LIU X
   WANG J
   WANG B
   YU X
   HU Z
   LIU F
   HU B
   ZHANG J
   PENG Y
   SONG Q
   FU L
   LUO M
   GAO J
AE JIANGXI GANFENG LITHIUM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202140533D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing battery-grade anhydrous lithium                chloride by purifying old halogen involves: (A)                heating brine, adding activated carbon, stirring,                and filtering; (B) separating calcium and magnesium                slag from the solution in step (A); (C) exchanging                the solution in step (B) to adsorb boron ions; (D)                separating calcium slag from the solution in step                (C); (E) adding a soda ash solution to a reactor,                heating, and adding old brine purification solution                in step (D); (F) separating the solid in step (E),                and washing; (G) adding the solid in step (F) to                deionized water or purified water and EDTA,                hydrogenating, and separating carbonized                supernatant from the insoluble matter; (H)                pyrolyzing the clear liquid in step (G), separating                solid, and washing; (I) acidifying the solid in                step (H); (J) evaporating and concentrating lithium                chloride solution, and centrifuging the slurry to                separate lithium chloride; and (K) drying and                dehydrating the wet lithium chloride material,                cleaning and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                battery-grade anhydrous lithium chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses inorganic chemical materials                that are safer than organic solvents as raw                materials; and enables simple, safe and economical                preparation of the battery-grade anhydrous lithium                chloride with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing battery-grade anhydrous lithium                chloride by purifying old halogen involves: (A)                heating purchased brine at a constant temperature                of 40-60 degrees C, adding an activated carbon,                stirring for 30-60 minutes, and filtering to remove                a small amount of organic matter, suspended matter                and solid sediment in the brine; (B) heating the                solution obtained in step (A) at 90-110 degrees C,                maintaining at constant temperature, adding 30-35%                liquid caustic soda, adjusting the pH to 6-9,                separating magnesium slag, adjusting the pH value                to 12-14, reacting for more than 20 minutes, and                again separating calcium and magnesium slag; (C)                exchanging the solution obtained in step (B) to                adsorb boron ions; (D) introducing the solution                obtained in step (C) into carbon dioxide to remove                calcium under stirring, and when the pH of the                solution reaches 6-7, and separating calcium slag;                (E) preparing 20% soda ash solution with steam                condensate, adjusting the pH to 12-14 by using                30-35% liquid caustic soda, adding EDTA to complex                the divalent metal ion, then adding the soda ash                solution to a reactor, heating at 90-100 degrees C,                increasing the stirring speed to 160-200 rpm, and                slowly adding a calculated amount of old brine                purification solution obtained in step (D) at                constant temperature for 0.5-2 hours; (F)                separating the solid obtained in step (E) by a                horizontal scraper centrifuge, and washing 2-3                times with steam condensed water; (G) adding the                solid obtained in step (F) to deionized water or                purified water and EDTA, then introducing carbon                dioxide under stirring for hydrogenation, when the                solid disappears, maintaining the pressure for 30                minutes, and separating the carbonized supernatant                from the insoluble matter through a filter press                with a filter paper; (H) increasing the temperature                of the clear liquid obtained in step (G) to 90-110                degrees C, then pyrolyzing at a constant                temperature for 30 minutes, separating the solid by                centrifuge, and washing 2-3 times with steam                condensed water; (I) slowly adding the solid                obtained in step (H) to synthetic hydrochloric acid                (non-industrial by-product hydrochloric acid) for                acidification transformation for 20 minutes, and                adjusting the pH to 7-9 by using lithium hydroxide                solid; (J) evaporating and concentrating pure                lithium chloride solution obtained in step (I) to                crystallize a solid, maintaining the solid content                in the evaporator at 5-30%, centrifuging to                separate lithium chloride solid, and reusing                according to the impurity content in the mother                liquor; and (K) continuously drying and dehydrating                the wet lithium chloride material obtained in step                (J) at 150-180 degrees C, maintaining the moisture                in the product at less than 0.4%, cleaning and                packaging. The concentration of boron ions in the                solution is less than 10 ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (A), the                  reaction temperature is 40-60 degrees C, and the                  stirring and adsorption time is 30-60 minutes.                  Preferred Components: The usage amount of                  activated carbon is 1-3% of the mass of brine. In                  step (I), the alkali source is battery-grade                  lithium hydroxide monohydrate with pH of 7-9.                  Preferred Property: In step (G), the deionized                  water or purified water has a resistivity of                  greater than or equal to 15 M Omega .cm at 15-25                  degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (C), the boron                  ion special resin is an amino resin, and has a                  saturated adsorption capacity of more than 4                  g/l.

TI Sewage treatment of combined dynamic membrane bioreactor in sewage treatment system comprises e.g. decomposing organic pollutants using facultative anaerobic microorganisms to obtain first sewage including organic low molecular pollutants.
AU ZOU Q
   ZOU H
   GAO H
   YANG Q
   ZHANG W
   YIN Y
AE GUANGXI GUOHONG ZHIHONG ENVIRONMENTAL (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020717336
AB    NOVELTY - Sewage treatment of combined dynamic membrane bioreactor in sewage treatment system including an anaerobic adjusting tank, a biological membrane reaction tank and a biological membrane separation tank comprises combining adjusting tank and anaerobic tank through anaerobic conditioning tank, decomposing organic high molecular pollutants in sewage flowing using facultative anaerobic microorganisms and anaerobic microorganisms through grid to obtain first sewage including organic low molecular pollutants, decomposing the first sewage containing organic low-molecular pollutants using aerobic microorganisms through the biological membrane reaction tank to obtain treated second sewage including carbon dioxide and water, intercepting treated second sewage using improved polyvinylidene fluoride membrane and self-generating dynamic membrane through the biological membrane separation tank to obtain treated third sewage, and returning intercepted sludge to anaerobic adjusting tank.
   USE - The device is useful for sewage treatment of combined dynamic membrane bioreactor in sewage treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The device maintains high-efficiency activated sludge, has high nitrification efficiency, and improved probability of removing pollutants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sewage treatment of combined dynamic membrane bioreactor in sewage treatment system including an anaerobic adjusting tank, a biological membrane reaction tank and a biological membrane separation tank comprises combining adjusting tank and anaerobic tank through anaerobic conditioning tank, decomposing organic high molecular pollutants in sewage flowing using facultative anaerobic microorganisms and anaerobic microorganisms through grid to obtain first sewage including organic low molecular pollutants, decomposing the first sewage containing organic low-molecular pollutants using aerobic microorganisms through the biological membrane reaction tank to obtain treated second sewage including carbon dioxide and water, cultivating multifunctional biofilm through biotechnology using bioreactor, intercepting treated second sewage using improved polyvinylidene fluoride membrane and self-generating dynamic membrane through the biological membrane separation tank to obtain treated third sewage, and returning intercepted sludge to anaerobic adjusting tank to remove nitrogen by denitrification, where the biological membrane reaction tank has built-in bioreactor combined with biological filler. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a sewage treatment device comprising a tank body (1) arranged horizontally. The two ends of the tank body are correspondingly set with a water inlet (2) and a water outlet (3). A pre-treatment area (4), a first biological reaction zone (6), a second biological reaction zone, and a membrane filter zone are orderly set in the tank body along the water inlet toward the water outlet. The first biological reaction zone, the second biological reaction zone, and the membrane filter zone are set above the pre-treatment area and are separated by a self-generating dynamic membrane. The first biological reaction zone and the second biological reaction zone are set with a biological membrane filler. The membrane filter zone is set on side of the water outlet and connected with the water outlet. The pre-treatment area is formed by a U-shaped pretreatment channel separated from two ends and bottom of the tank body by a bottom clapboard set in the tank body. One end of the pre-treatment area corresponds to the water inlet, and other end is connected to the water distribution pipe set above the biological reaction zone through a water pipe. The bottom clapboards are parallel to contours of two ends and bottom of the tank body respectively. The distance between the bottom clapboards and bottom of the tank body is 20 cm. A deposition filter tank 20 with a valve is set at bottom of the pre-treatment area. The deposition filter tank is set perpendicular to direction of the pre-treatment area and corresponds in parallel along bottom contour of the pre-treatment area. A sewage valve is set in middle of an opening bottom of the deposition filter tank. The deposition filter tank is set with a sieve plate on side of the shell close to the water outlet. The sieve plate separates the pre-treatment area and is inclined or bent towards side of the water inlet.

TI Process for co-producing methanol and ethylene            glycol involves contacting raw coal and gasifier,            hydrogenating obtained crude synthesis gas, cooling,            and combining separated carbon monoxide gas, flash            vapor and hydrogen-rich gas.
AU LIU Y
   WU X
   WANG H
   XIA J
   LIU W
   ZHANG G
   LIU X
   LI G
AE INNER MONGOLIA DATANG INT KESHIKETENG (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2019A75976
AB    NOVELTY - Process for co-producing methanol and ethylene                glycol from coal-based natural gas involves (i)                contacting raw coal and a gasifier in a crushed                coal pressurized gasification device, (ii)                subjecting obtained crude synthesis gas to a                high-temperature hydrogenation reaction, (iii)                cooling and purifying obtained desulfurized                synthesis gas by a low-temperature methanol washing                process to remove high-concentration acid gases,                (iv) deep-cooling purified synthesis gas using a                cryogenic separation method to separate a natural                gas product and a raw material gas comprising                carbon monoxide gas, hydrogen-rich gas and flash                steam for methanol and ethylene glycol synthesis,                (v) subjecting the obtained hydrogen-rich gas to                pressure swing adsorption to remove the impurity                components contained in the hydrogen-rich gas, (vi)                synthesizing carbon monoxide by oxidative coupling                method, and (vii) combining the separated carbon                monoxide gas, hydrogen-rich gas and flash vapor and                the remaining desorbed gas.
   USE - Process for co-producing methanol and ethylene                glycol from coal-based natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables co-production of methanol                and ethylene glycol with improved                energy-efficiency, and quality. The method is                capable of flexibly adjusting according to market                demand conditions, and achieving flexible operation                and easy adjustment of production mode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for co-producing methanol and ethylene                glycol from coal-based natural gas involves (i)                contacting raw coal and a gasifier in a crushed                coal pressurized gasification device and performing                gasification reaction under pressure of 3.8-4.2 MPa                to obtain crude syngas comprising 8-14 vol.%                methane and 2.5-2.8 vol.% hydrogen and carbon                monoxide, (ii) subjecting crude synthesis gas to a                high-temperature hydrogenation reaction to remove                residual oxygen and olefins in the crude synthesis                gas, and converting organic sulfur into hydrogen                sulfide, performing isothermal hydrolysis reaction                to obtain desulfurized synthesis gas, (iii) cooling                and purifying desulfurized synthesis gas by a                low-temperature methanol washing process to remove                high-concentration acid gases in the desulfurized                synthesis gas to obtain a purified synthesis gas                having a total sulfur content of 0.1 ppm, (iv)                deep-cooling purified synthesis gas using a                cryogenic separation method to separate a natural                gas product and a raw material gas comprising                carbon monoxide gas, hydrogen-rich gas and flash                steam for methanol and ethylene glycol synthesis,                carbon dioxide feeding separated carbon monoxide                gas to the methanol synthesis device and ethylene                glycol synthesis device, feeding a portion of the                separated hydrogen-rich gas for hydrogen                purification, (v) subjecting the obtained                hydrogen-rich gas to pressure swing adsorption to                remove the impurity components contained in the                hydrogen-rich gas, and feeding remaining desorbed                gas after purification of hydrogen to a methanol                synthesis device, (vi) synthesizing carbon monoxide                by oxidative coupling method, purifying dimethyl                oxalate by distillation and hydrogen to produce                crude ethylene glycol through a hydrogenation                reaction and performing crude glycol rectification                process to obtain finished rectified glycol, and                (vii) combining the separated carbon monoxide gas,                hydrogen-rich gas and flash vapor and the remaining                desorbed gas after purifying hydrogen to form a                methanol synthesis feed gas, extracting a portion                of carbon dioxide gas from the low-temperature                methanol washing reaction process so that the                hydrogen-carbon weight fraction ratio of the                methanol synthesis feed gas reaches 2.0-2.2,                feeding the methanol synthesis raw material gas                into a methanol synthesis device, under the                condition of a pressure of 7.5-8.5 MPa, using                carbon monoxide gas and hydrogen to react to                produce crude methanol under the action of a                methanol synthesis catalyst and subjecting the                obtained crude methanol to a rectification                treatment to remove dissolved gases and low-boiling                substances in the crude methanol to obtain a                finished refined methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (ii) involves                  heating desulfurization synthesis gas to 320-350                  degrees C through separation and heat exchange,                  removing traces of dust impurities and heavy oil                  in the desulfurized synthesis gas, and                  hydrogenating the synthesis gas from the                  impurities and heavy oil to remove residual                  oxygen and olefins in the crude synthesis gas,                  and converting organic sulfur into hydrogen                  sulfide.
