PN US10435637-B1; WO2020131427-A1; CN113454189-A
TI Generating methane-enriched product gas, product gas and ash product, involves reacting carbonaceous feedstock in hydromethanation reactor, feeding char product stream to catalyst recovery unit, and withdrawing oxidation reactor product gas.
AU SIRDESHPANDE A
   ROBINSON E T
   RAMAN P K
   KECKLER K
   KEKLER K
   SILDESPAND A
AE GREATPOINT ENERGY INC (GRAT-C)
   SURE CHAMPION INVESTMENT LTD (SURE-Non-standard)
GA 201983712E
AB    NOVELTY - Process for generating a fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream, an oxidation reactor product gas and an oxidation reactor ash product stream, involves preparing a carbonaceous feedstock from the non-gaseous carbonaceous material, and introducing the carbonaceous feedstock, the hydromethanation catalyst, steam and oxygen into a hydromethanation reactor comprising a fluidized bed, a disengagement zone above the fluidized bed, and a gas mixing zone below the fluidized bed. The carbonaceous feedstock is reacted in the hydromethanation reactor in presence of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, steam and hydromethanation catalyst, under pressure of 1825 kilopascal at 205-816 degrees C to produce a methane-enriched raw product gas, heat energy and a by-product char. A stream of methane-enriched raw product gas is withdrawn from the hydromethanation reactor as the methane-enriched raw product gas stream.
   USE - Process for generating a fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream, an oxidation reactor product gas and an oxidation reactor ash product stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the carbon utilization and thermal efficiency, co-produces electric power and steam from the by-product char in addition to the end-product pipeline quality substitute natural gas steam and electric power, and reduces the volume of char material processed in catalyst recovery (which allows the use of reduced equipment size), that results in lowering both operating and capital costs of the operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for generating a fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream, an oxidation reactor product gas and an oxidation reactor ash product stream, involves preparing a carbonaceous feedstock from the non-gaseous carbonaceous material, and introducing the carbonaceous feedstock, the hydromethanation catalyst, steam and oxygen into a hydromethanation reactor comprising a fluidized bed, a disengagement zone above the fluidized bed, and a gas mixing zone below the fluidized bed. The carbonaceous feedstock is reacted in the hydromethanation reactor in presence of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, steam and hydromethanation catalyst, under pressure of 1825 kilopascal at 205-816 degrees C to produce a methane-enriched raw product gas, heat energy and a by-product char. A stream of methane-enriched raw product gas is withdrawn from the hydromethanation reactor as the methane-enriched raw product gas stream, where the methane-enriched raw product gas stream comprises methane, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, steam, and entrained solids. A substantial portion of the entrained solids is withdrawn from the methane-enriched raw product gas stream to generate a solids-depleted, methane-enriched raw product gas stream and a recovered primary solids stream. A substantial portion of any fines is removed from the solids-depleted, methane-enriched raw product gas stream to generate the fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream and a recovered secondary fines stream. A stream of by-product char is withdrawn from the hydromethanation reactor as the hydromethanation reactor char product stream, where the hydromethanation reactor char product stream comprises a carbon content and entrained hydromethanation catalyst. A portion of the entrained catalyst is extracted from the hydromethanation reactor char product stream by feeding all or a portion of the hydromethanation reactor char product stream to a catalyst recovery unit, and withdrawing a stream of catalyst-depleted char from the catalyst recovery unit as the washed char product stream, the washed char product stream comprising a carbon content. A stream of liberated hydromethanation catalyst is withdrawn from the catalyst recovery unit as a recovered hydromethanation catalyst stream. All or a portion of the washed char product stream , and an oxygen-containing gas stream are fed into oxidation reactor. A portion of the carbon content of the washed char product stream with the oxygen-containing gas stream is fed in the oxidation reactor to produce an oxidation reactor ash product stream, and an oxidation reactor product gas comprising steam and carbon dioxide. The oxidation reactor product gas is withdrawn from the oxidation reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The hydromethanation catalyst comprises an alkali metal,e.g., potassium. Preferred Process: The process further comprises operating the oxidation reactor at a pressure greater than 791 kilopascal. When the oxidation reactor product gas further comprises carbon monoxide and hydrogen, all or a portion of the oxidation reactor product gas is fed into a gas turbine combined cycle system to generate power and steam. When carbon monoxide and hydrogen are not present in the oxidation reactor product gas, all or a portion of the oxidation reactor product gas is fed into a gas expander and a heat recovery steam generator to generate power and steam. The oxidation reactor ash product stream is further cooled or quenched. The oxidation reactor is operated at a pressure less than 400 kilopascal. When the oxidation reactor product gas further comprises carbon monoxide and hydrogen, all or a portion of the oxidation reactor product gas is fed to a heat recovery steam generator equipped with an auxiliary burner to generate steam. When carbon monoxide and hydrogen are not present in the oxidation reactor product gas, all or a portion of the oxidation reactor product gas are fed to a heat recovery steam generator to generate steam. All or a portion of the steam generatedis converted to power in a steam-turbine generator. The recovered primary solids stream are fed into the hydromethanation reactor, and all or a portion of the recovered secondary fines stream are fed into the catalyst recovery unit. The fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream is introduced into a heat exchanger unit to remove heat energy and a cooled methane-enriched raw product stream is generated. A portion of the carbon monoxide in the cooled methane-enriched raw product stream is subjected to steam shifting in a shift reactor system to generate a hydrogen-enriched raw product gas stream with a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of close to 3. The hydrogen-enriched raw product gas stream is dehydrated in a low-temperature gas cooling system, to generate a dry raw gas stream, the dry raw gas stream comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, hydrogen, carbon monoxide and methane. A substantial portion of the carbon dioxide and a substantial portion of the hydrogen sulfide is removed from the dry raw gas stream in an acid gas removal unit to produce a sweetened gas stream comprising a substantial portion of the hydrogen, carbon monoxide and methane from the dry raw gas stream. The carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the sweetened gas stream is reacted in a methanation system in the presence of a methanation catalyst to produce heat energy and a pipeline quality substitute natural gas stream. The heat energy from the catalytic methanation system is recovered. A portion of the recovered heat energy is utilized to generate and superheat a steam stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN CN109536268-A; CN109536268-B; WO2020125742-A1; US2020332220-A1
TI Preparing functional oil by mixed fungal staged            solid state fermentation useful for processing food            comprises e.g. crushing soybean, peeling, rolling,            puffing, cooling, peeling other oil seeds, crushing,            and high pressure sterilizing.
AU LIU Y
   LI J
   ZHAI Y
   DI Y
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
   KAIXIN GRAIN & OIL CO LTD (KAIX-Non-standard)
   UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
   UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 201931009E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing functional oil by mixed fungal                staged solid state fermentation comprises (i)                crushing soybean, peeling, rolling blank body,                puffing by a hydraulic conical plug bulking                machine, cooling to obtain soybean powder, shelling                or peeling other oil seeds, crushing, high pressure                sterilizing the crushed powder, and cooling to                obtain other oil seed powder, (ii) high pressure                sterilizing the puffed soybean powder and other                oilseed powders, mixing in fermentation tank                according to mass ratio of 6-9:1-4, adjusting the                moisture content of material in fermentation tank                to 20-30 wt.%, the glucose addition amount is 0.5-5                wt.%, and sodium chloride addition amount is                0.05-0.5 wt.%, adjusting pH, inoculating 10-20 wt.%                fungal strains, and performing sectional                fermentation, and (iii) transferring fermentation                product to extraction kettle for supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction, vacuum fractionating,                preparing nutrient-fortified oils with different                flavors and colors, and depositing nitrogen.
   USE - The functional oil is useful for processing                food (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the aftertaste of soybean                oil; produces different flavors; improves the                nutritional value of the oil; increases the oil                extraction rate; improves the fatty acid                composition of oils and fats; increases the content                of pyrazines, alcohols, and phenols; sensory                functions e.g. flavor and color are more in line                with consumer demand for innovative products;                further processed to produce by-products e.g.                functional peptides for sustainable green                development.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing functional oil by mixed fungal                staged solid state fermentation comprises (i)                crushing soybean, peeling, rolling blank body,                puffing by a hydraulic conical plug bulking machine                at 150-160&#176; C for 1-3 minutes, then                cooling to 25-35&#176; C to obtain soybean                powder, shelling or peeling other oil seeds,                crushing them to 50-100 meshes, high pressure                sterilizing the crushed powder at 115-121&#176;                C for 15-30 minutes, and cooling to 25-35&#176;                C, to obtain other oil seed powder, (ii) high                pressure sterilizing the puffed soybean powder and                other oilseed powders, mixing in fermentation tank                according to the mass ratio of 6-9:1-4, adjusting                the moisture content of material in fermentation                tank to 20-30 wt.%, the glucose addition amount is                0.5-5 wt.%, and sodium chloride addition amount is                0.05-0.5 wt.%, adjusting the pH to 3.5-9,                inoculating 10-20 wt.% fungal strains, and                performing sectional fermentation at                25-35&#176; C for is 3-7 days, (iii)                transferring fermentation product to extraction                kettle for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction,                vacuum fractionating, preparing nutrient-fortified                oils with different flavors and colors, depositing                nitrogen into oil and fat, where the fungus is                mold, or yeast, the mold comprises Aspergillus                oryzae, Aspergillus niger, and Mortierella                aurantium, the yeast comprises adhesive red yeast,                Candida albicans, and rhodospora Erythraea, the                section fermentation comprises when the fungus is                mold, adjusting pH to 3.5-9, the dissolved oxygen                amount is 30-40% of the total material volume,                firstly adding Aspergillus oryzaefor 1-3 days, then                adding 0.1-1 wt.% glucose, adjusting the pH to                3.5-9, and the dissolved oxygen amount is 30-40% of                the total material volume, adding Aspergillus                niger, and Mortierella aurantium, culturing for 2-6                days, or, when the fungus is yeast, adjusting the                pH to 5-9, the dissolved oxygen amount is 25-35% of                the total material volume, adding Candida albicans,                or Candida albicans, and rhodospora Erythraea,                fermenting for 2-5 days, then adding 1-3 wt.%                glucose, adjusting the pH to 5-9, where dissolved                oxygen amount is 10-20% of the total material                volume, and then adding with the adhesive red yeast                and rhodospora Erythraea, or red yeast, and                fermenting for 1-4 days. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are                also included for:
   functional oil prepared by the above mentioned                method; and
   food or cooking oil comprising the oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The other oil seeds                  are flaxseed, hemp seed, rapeseed, sesame,                  peanut, or corn germ. The mass ratio of soybean                  powder, and other oil seed powder is 6-9:1-4. The                  mass ratio of the Aspergillus oryzae, Aspergillus                  niger, and Mortierella aurantiumis 1-5:1-5:1-5.                  The mass ratio of adhesive red yeast, Candida                  albicans, and rhodospora Erythraeais 1-5:1-5:1-5.                  Preferred Method: The extraction pressure is                  30-40 MPa, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-20                  l/hour, and extracting at 40-50&#176; C for                  1.5-2 hours. The vacuum fractionation is divided                  into a first-stage pressure fractionation and                  second-stage pressure fractionation, where the                  pressure of first-stage decompression                  fractionation is 10-20 MPa, and temperature is                  40-50&#176; C, and the pressure of                  second-stage decompression fractionation is 8-10                  MPa, and temperature is 35-45&#176; C.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D03-H01T2B; D05-A04D; D05-C19; D10-A

PT P
PN CN109369082-A; CN109369082-B
TI Preparing a mortar useful for paving a floor tile, comprises e.g. adding sand, cement, hydroxyethyl carboxymethyl starch, fly ash loaded chitosan, porous nanofibers, nano titanium dioxide, modified sepiolite, and calcium sulfate whiskers.
AU ZHAO R
AE MIANYANG RUILONG BUILDING MATERIALS CO (MIAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI TUOKEN BUILDING MATERIALS CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201920939Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a mortar comprises e.g. adding sand, cement, hydroxyethyl carboxymethyl starch, fly ash loaded chitosan, porous nanofibers, nano titanium dioxide, modified sepiolite, calcium sulfate whiskers, and sodium polyacrylate, sending to a mixer and stirring uniformly, adding latex powder, antifoaming agent, and water reducing agent to the mixer, air entraining agent, thickening agent, grape sodium carbonate, stirring and uniformly mixing to obtain a mortar for paving tiles, where the fly ash is loaded with chitosan, where the preparation method comprises mixing fly ash with hydrofluoric acid, and activating, and filtering, washing the pH with water to obtain activated fly ash, adding activated fly ash to the supercritical reaction device, and adding chitosan solution having a and quaternary ammonium salt, and then sealing the system, reacting and stirring under the condition of carbon dioxide, relieving pressure, and drying to obtain fly ash-loaded chitosan.
   USE - The mortar is useful for paving a floor tile (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mortar: improves the adhesion and compactness of the mortar by adding components of fly ash-loaded chitosan and modified sepiolite, and improves mechanical properties of mortar by adding porous nanofibers, improves the tensile bond strength of the mortar, and has good weather resistance, stable performance and good durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a mortar comprises (i) adding 100-150 pts. wt. sand, 50-80 pts. wt. cement, 1-3 pts. wt. hydroxyethyl carboxymethyl starch, 5-10 pts. wt. fly ash loaded chitosan, 5-8 pts. wt. porous nanofibers, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide, 5-10 pts. wt. modified sepiolite, 1-3 pts. wt. calcium sulfate whiskers, and 1-3 pts. wt. sodium polyacrylate, sending to a mixer and stirring uniformly; (iii) adding 1-3 pts. wt. latex powder, 3-5 pts. wt. antifoaming agent, and 3-5 pts. wt. water reducing agent to the mixer, 0.5-1 pts. wt. air entraining agent, 0.5-1 pts. wt. thickening agent, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. grape sodium carbonate, stirring and uniformly mixing to obtain a mortar for paving tiles, where the fly ash is loaded with chitosan, where the preparation method comprises mixing 50 pts. wt. fly ash with 150-200 pts. wt. hydrofluoric acid having a concentration of 10-20 wt.%, and activating at 60-100 degrees C for 3-5 hours, and filtering, washing the pH to 5-6 with water to obtain activated fly ash, adding 20-30 pts. wt. activated fly ash to the supercritical reaction device, and adding 100-200 pts. wt. chitosan solution having a concentration of 2-3 wt.% and 3-5 pts. wt. quaternary ammonium salt, and then sealing the system, reacting and stirring for 60-120 minutes under the condition of carbon dioxide to 25-45 MPa and a temperature of 60-80 degrees C, relieving pressure, and drying to obtain fly ash-loaded chitosan, where the chitosan solution is prepared by adding chitosan to acetic acid at a concentration of 5-10 wt.%, where the modified sepiolite is prepared by adding 30-35 pts. wt. sepiolite powder and 180-250 pts. wt. mixture of acetone and water to a supercritical device at a temperature of 360-400 degrees C, and immersing in a supercritical acetone-water system with a pressure of 10-20 MPa for 60-120 minutes, filtering and drying to obtain pre-treated sepiolite and the volume ratio of acetone to water in the supercritical acetone-water system is 3:1, taking 20-25 pts. wt. pretreated sepiolite and add 100-120 pts. wt. water, then adding 5-10 pts. wt. sodium lignosulfonate and 3-5 pts. wt. sodium alkyl phenol oxylate, and heating to 100-120 degrees C, reacting and stirring for 3-5 hours, cooling to room temperature, and placing the reaction materials in an electron accelerator of 2.5 MeV and 40 mA for irradiation treatment, centrifugally separating, and drying to obtain modified sepiolite, the irradiation dose rate of the irradiation dose is 200-1000 kGy/h, and the irradiation dose is 400-1000 kGy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The porous nanofiber is prepared by preparing a polymer solution having a concentration of 5-10 wt.%, and injecting the polymer solution into the static electricity in the container with stainless steel nozzle of the spraying device, where the voltage is applied to the stainless steel nozzle by a high-voltage power source, and is sprayed onto the aluminum foil receiving device under high-voltage electrostatic spraying conditions, drying, obtaining polymer fibers, taking polymer fibers in a low-temperature plasma treatment apparatus for 30 to 60 minutes, and then immersing in a nano-inorganic enhancer solution, ultrasonicizing for 60-90 minutes to obtain porous nanofibers, introducing the process of ultrasound micro-nano bubbles into the mixed solution, where the aeration rate of the micro-nano bubbles is 100-120 ml/minute, and the frequency of the ultrasonic wave is 30-45 KHz, the atmosphere of low temperature plasma processing instrument is argon or nitrogen, the frequency of the low temperature plasma processing instrument is 40-80 KHz, the power is 40-100 W, and the pressure of the argon gas is 30-80 Pa. The injection condition of the electrospray device is: an ambient temperature of 50-60 degrees C, and a distance between the receiving device and the nozzle is 5 to 10 cm, the flow rate is 10-20 ml/hour, the voltage is 12-20 kV, and the inner diameter of the stainless steel nozzle is 0.8-1.6 mm. The method for preparing the polymer solution is: taking a polyacrylonitrile, polylactic acid, polystyrene and polyvinylpyrrolidone with a weight ratio of 3:1:1:1 and dissolving in an organic solvent. The nano inorganic reinforcing agent solution is prepared by taking 12-18 pts. wt. nano calcium carbonate, 8-12 pts. wt. nano hydroxyapatite, 5-8 pts. wt. nano-silica, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium tripolyphosphate are added to 150-200 pts. wt. water, and stirred for 60-120 minutes to obtain a nano-inorganic enhancer solution. Preferred Components: The organic solvent is dimethylformamide, toluene and chloroform in a volume ratio of 1:2:1. The quaternary ammonium salt is diallyldimethylammonium chloride, tetradecyltrimethylammonium chloride, or dodecyldimethylbenzylammonium chloride. The water reducing agent is naphthalene naphthalene-based superplasticizer, polycarboxylic acid superplasticizer, melamine superplasticizers, the air entraining agent is sodium rosinate, and sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate (SDBS), triterpenoid saponins, the latex powder is styrene-acrylic copolymer latex powder or ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer latex powder. The antifoaming agent is polydimethylsiloxane. The thickener is medical stone powder, diatomaceous earth powder and attapulgite powder at a weight ratio of 2:1:2. The alkylphenol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate is octylphenol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate, nonylphenol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate, and sodium dodecyl phenol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); Q45 (Roofing, stairs, floors (E04D,F))

PT P
PN CN108249497-A
TI Wastewater air distillation concentrated            purification system useful for concentrating the waste            water comprises an air intake unit, a wastewater inlet            unit, a distillation concentration tower and a heating            unit for heating air intake unit.
AU ZHANG J
AE ZHANG J (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201858340L
AB    NOVELTY - Wastewater air distillation concentrated                purification system comprises an air intake unit, a                wastewater inlet unit, a distillation concentration                tower, a heating unit for heating the air intake                unit or/and the wastewater inlet unit and system                further comprises collecting a preheater for                preheating the waste water into the liquid in the                humid air of the distillation concentration column.                The lower part of the distillation concentration                tower is provided with an air inlet, where the                bottom is provided with a concentrated liquid                collecting cone and the top is provided with a                humid air outlet. The air intake unit is provided                with an intake port of the distillation                concentration tower. The waste water inlet unit                comprises a waste water inlet pipe, where one end                of the pipe enters the tower body from the upper                part of the distillation concentration tower and is                opened downwards and the other end connected with                the waste water inlet port.
   USE - The system is useful for concentrating the                waste water (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Wastewater air distillation concentrated                purification system comprises an air intake unit, a                wastewater inlet unit, a distillation concentration                tower, a heating unit for heating the air intake                unit or/and the wastewater inlet unit and system                further comprises collecting a preheater for                preheating the waste water into the liquid in the                humid air of the distillation concentration column.                The lower part of the distillation concentration                tower is provided with an air inlet, where the                bottom is provided with a concentrated liquid                collecting cone and the top is provided with a                humid air outlet. The air intake unit is provided                with an intake port of the distillation                concentration tower. The waste water inlet unit                comprises a waste water inlet pipe, where one end                of the pipe enters the tower body from the upper                part of the distillation concentration tower and is                opened downwards and the other end connected with                the waste water inlet port. The air intake unit                or/and the wastewater inlet unit is provided with a                heating unit. The preheater is disposed on the                waste water inlet pipe and the preheater is further                connected with the humid air outlet of the                distillation concentration tower and the humid air                is condensed and dehumidified by the preheater and                then enters the air intake unit for natural                circulation or directly discharged. The air is a                carrier gas and the carrier gas includes flue gas,                helium gas, argon gas, nitrogen gas, carbon dioxide                or steam. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                concentrating the waste water by using the                wastewater air distillation concentrated                purification system comprising adding air as a                carrier gas from the inlet of the distillation                concentrating column, convecting in the                distillation concentration column with the                wastewater sprayed from the upper end of the                distillation concentration column, taking the part                of the water in the wastewater by the air as the                temperature rises to obtain wet saturated air and                wastewater concentrate respectively, where uses the                heat source from the gas side or from the liquid                side or both from the gas side and the liquid,                converting the humid saturated air into heat by the                preheater phase, condensing the water vapor to form                condensed water and dehumidified air, then directly                discharging the condensed water and passing the                dehumidified air into the air intake unit to be                naturally circulated or directly discharged, where                the concentrated liquid in the concentrate                collecting cone is directly discharged if it                reaches TDS greater than or equal to 250,000ppm. If                it does not reach TDS greater than or equal to                250,000ppm, it will be discharged through the                wastewater circulation outlet and the mixed mixing                of pipeline and wastewater to the water until the                TDS is greater than or equal to 250000 ppm and the                carrier gas may be a gas e.g. flue gas, helium gas,                argon gas, nitrogen gas, carbon dioxide or                steam.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation                of the wastewater air distillation concentrated                purification system
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The preheater is a                  multi-stage bubble column heat exchanger, and the                  multiple blister towers are provided with                  multiple condensers from bottom to top, which is                  provided with the multi-stage bubble. The bottom                  of the bonnet is provided with a cold flow inlet                  and the multiple heat flow inlets are                  corresponding to the respective condensers, which                  are provided from bottom to top. The cold flow                  inlet includes waste water inlets and the heat                  flow inlets are respectively connected to the                  distillation concentrating towers through pipes.                  The humid air of different temperatures is                  extracted at a height, and the multiple heat flow                  inlets are respectively correspond to the wet air                  extraction port, which is connected from the top                  to the bottom of the distillation concentration                  tower from bottom to top. The side of the                  multi-stage bubble tower is provided with a                  condensed product water outlet and the top is                  provided with a dehumidified air outlet and a hot                  waste water outlet which is preheated by                  saturated humid air. The system further comprises                  an air intake unit, a wastewater inlet unit, a                  distillation concentration tower, an air heating                  unit, and a preheating device for preheating the                  wastewater inlet unit. The lower part of the                  distillation concentration tower is provided with                  an air inlet, in which the bottom is provided                  with a concentrated liquid collecting cone and                  the top is provided with a humid air outlet. The                  air intake unit includes an air cleaner and a                  fan, and the air heating unit includes a heat                  exchanger, and the air, which is sequentially                  enters the tower body through an air cleaner, fan                  and heat exchanger from an air inlet of the                  distillation concentration tower. The heat                  exchanger is further connected to a heat source                  for heating the air. The waste water inlet unit                  comprises a waste water inlet pipe, where one end                  of the pipe enters the tower body from the upper                  part of the distillation concentration tower and                  is opened downward and the other end is provided                  with a waste water inlet port. The preheater is                  disposed on the waste water inlet pipe and the                  preheater is further connected with the humid air                  outlet of the distillation concentration tower                  and the humid air is directly condensed or                  dehumidified by the preheater and directly                  discharged into the natural circulation of the                  air intake unit. The concentrate collecting cone                  is provided with a discharge valve and a drain                  pump for discharging the concentrated liquid and                  the bottom of the cone is also provided with a                  waste water circulation outlet and a pipeline and                  the pipeline is connected to the waste water                  circulation outlet with the waste water inlet                  pipeline. The distillation concentration column                  further comprises a multiple packing layers,                  where the lower layer packing is a random                  packing, and the upper layer packing is a                  structured packing, and each unit volume specific                  surface area of the upper layer of the filler is                  1.25-2.5 times that of the next layer of the                  filler. The random packing is derived from a                  high-efficiency plastic filler of a washing tower                  and/or a Traleigh ring packing or a similar                  random packing with similar functional                  properties. The structured packing is derived                  from a pulse type structured packing or a similar                  structured packing having similar functional                  properties. The system further comprises an air                  intake modulating mechanism, which is provided at                  the air inlet of the tower body and an air outlet                  modulating device, which is disposed at the air                  outlet of the tower body mechanism. The air                  intake modulation mechanism includes an inlet gas                  distribution modulator and a multiple inlet                  baffles, which is disposed under the inlet gas                  distribution modulator for directing horizontal                  airflow into a vertical airflow. The air outlet                  modulating mechanism includes an outlet gas                  distribution modulator and multiple outlet                  baffles, which are disposed above the outlet gas                  distribution modulator for directing vertical                  airflow into a horizontal airflow. The inlet gas                  distribution modulator and the outlet gas                  distribution modulator is respectively comprises                  an intermediate modulation shaft body and a                  modulation blade, which is disposed around the                  modulation shaft body. The middle of the                  modulation shaft of the inlet gas distribution                  modulator is a cylinder, which is provided with a                  semi-spherical sphere or a cone at both ends. The                  modulation shaft of the outlet gas distribution                  modulator is a cylinder having a semicircular                  sphere or a cone at the lower end. The modulating                  blade is disposed at a lower end of the cylinder                  and includes multiple blades, which are arranged                  obliquely in a circumferential direction of the                  cylinder for modulating a vertical ascending                  airflow into a rotating ascending airflow. The                  outlet gas distribution modulator is located                  vertically above the inlet gas distribution                  modulator. The system further comprises an inlet                  deflector or an outlet deflector, which are                  respectively provided with a horizontal section,                  a vertical section, and an arc segment connecting                  the horizontal section and the vertical section,                  respectively. The modulating blade is                  circumferentially fixed by an inner stirrup and                  an outer stirrup at an equal interval and is                  located at a near lower end cone of the cylinder                  of the gas distribution modulator.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PT P
PN CN107573379-A
TI Preparing carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand involves reacting the vinyl(triphenylphosphine) derivative with Grignard reagent, reacting, adding glacial hydrochloric acid aqueous solution, extracting and decompressing.
AU JIANG W
   FAN L
   YUAN X
   ZHANG T
   WANG H
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201806142F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand (I) involves reacting the vinyl(triphenylphosphine) derivative (II) with Grignard reagent in molar ratio of 3-n:1 at 30-40 degrees C, reacting at 30-80 degrees C for 1-4 hours, adding 10% glacial hydrochloric acid aqueous solution, extracting orderly, decompressing and distilling, and recrystallizing to obtain carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand (III), where n is 0-2. The obtained carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand (III) is added in the solvent A at room temperature.
   USE - Method for preparing carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand improves the solubility of the phosphine ligand, realizes the free phosphine ligand and recycles the free phosphine ligand properties, where catalyst can be reused.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand of formula (I) involves reacting the vinyl(triphenylphosphine) derivative of formula (II) with Grignard reagent in molar ratio of 3-n:1 at 30-40 degrees C, reacting at 30-80 degrees C for 1-4 hours, adding 10% glacial hydrochloric acid aqueous solution, extracting orderly, decompressing and distilling, and recrystallizing to obtain carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand of formula (III), where n is 0-2. The obtained carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand of formula (III) is added in the solvent A at room temperature, added 30% hydrogen peroxide in mole ratio of 1:1.5-5.0 and subjected to obtain oxidation treatment to obtain carbonyl containing organic phosphine ligand. The carbonyl containing organic phosphine ligand is added to the 10% ferrous chloride solution and washed with de-ionized water till starch potassium iodide reaches blue, dried, filtered, evaporated to remove solvent and recrystallized to obtain phenyl containing organic phosphine ligand of formula (IV). The phenyl containing organic phosphine ligand of formula (IV) is dissolved in solvent B, added certain amount of sodium hydrogen carbonate solids and subjected to the epoxidation reagent, where epoxidation reagent is triphenyl phosphine oxide and reacted at 30-60 degrees C for 10-48 hours. The obtained mixed solution is washed with detergent to remove excess of peroxide, washed with detergent, dried, filtered, subjected to the rotary evaporation to remove solvent and separated by column chromatography. The obtained product is subjected to the epoxidation with reagent in ratio of 1:1.0/asterisk-3.0(3-n)/asterisk(3-n) to obtain epoxide phenyl containing organic phosphine ligand of formula (V), where n is 0-2. The molar ratio of phenyl containing organic phosphine ligand of formula (V) to soidum hydrogen carbonate is 1:1.0/asterisk1.8(3-n)/asterisk(3-n). The epoxide phenyl containing organic phosphine ligand (IV) is placed in the high pressure kettle filled with carbon dioxide at 30-80 degrees C, added 0.1-0.5 wt.% tetrabutyl ammonium bromide as phase transfer catalyst to react for 1-4 days, reduced pressure and dissolved in ethyl acetate. The obtained product is washed, separated, dried, filtered, decompressed to remove ethyl acetate, separated by column chromatography to obtain oxygen containing ring organic phosphine ligand of formula (VI). The initial pressure carbon dioxide in the high pressure kettle is 1-6 megaPascal. The oxygen containing ring organic phosphine ligand (VI) is dissolved in the toluene solution under ice water bath condition, added trichlorosilane and triethylamine in molar ratio of 1:1 under 100-130 degrees C, stirred and reacted for 2-5 hours under ice bath condition. The obtained product is added in the de-ionized water, neutralized the unreacted trichlorosilane and filtered to remove undissolved flocculent substance. The obtained product is transferred to the separating funnel with ethyl acetate, combined organic layer, dried, filtered under cold trap reducing pressure, distilled the organic layer by column chromatography to obtain carbonate-modified fluorine-free organic phosphine ligand of formula (I).
   R1 to R4=are H, (CH2)mCH3 or NO2, m is 0-4, and n is 0-2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phosphorus is phosphorus trichloride, diphenyl phosphorus or phenyl phosphorus dichloride. The extracting agent is ether or ethyl acetate. The epoxidizing agent is meta-chloroperoxybenzoic acid. The solvent A is dichloromethane or acetone. The solvent B is methylene dichloride, ethyl acetate or acetone. The detergent is saturated 7% sodium sulfite aqueous solution and saturated sodium chloride solution.
DC E11 (Containing P and/or Si.)

PT P
PN CN114621913-A
TI Establishing rainbow trout liver cell line            involves taking test fish after one day of temporary            culture, wiping fish body with alcohol, utilizing high            temperature sterilized surgical scissors, removing            liver tissue, and observing characteristic organelles            followed by detecting mycoplasma.
AU QUAN J
   ZHAO G
   LIU Z
AE UNIV GANSU AGRIC (UYGS-C)
GA 202282779E
AB    NOVELTY - Establishing rainbow trout liver cell line                involves taking the test fish after 1 day of                temporary culture, wiping the fish body with                alcohol for disinfection, utilizing high                temperature sterilized surgical scissors to cut the                abdominal cavity of the rainbow trout from cloaca,                carefully removing the liver tissue, and washing                3-4 times with Hanks solution without digallane and                magnesium hydroxide to wash off the blood stains on                the liver surface. The living cells are round and                transparent. The dead cells are round and                transparent. The cells are stained blue.A sodium                sulfite solution is added for 2 times and each time                for 2 minutes. The solution is rinsed with running                water for 2 minutes. A 0.5 mL of 5% glutaraldehyde                is added for fixation prepared as electron                microscope cell specimens. The characteristic                organelles are observed in the cytoplasm of                hepatocytes under transmission electron microscope                followed by detecting mycoplasma by Hoechst                staining method.
   USE - Method for establishing rainbow trout liver                cell line.
   ADVANTAGE - The method explores the primary culture of                rainbow trout liver cell line through different                separation methods and culture conditions to                establish a stable primary culture model, and                obtains a good and stable separation effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Establishing rainbow trout liver cell line                involves taking the test fish after 1 day of                temporary culture, wiping the fish body with                alcohol for disinfection, utilizing high                temperature sterilized surgical scissors to cut the                abdominal cavity of the rainbow trout from the                cloaca, carefully removing the liver tissue, and                washing 3-4 times with Hanks solution without                digallane and magnesium hydroxide to wash off the                blood stains on the liver surface. The tissue                blocks are cut to 1-3 mm and digested with 0.1%                collagenase II and 0.25% trypsin in a volume ratio                of 1:1. The digestion time is set to 5-40 minutes.                The tissue block is shaken continuously during the                digestion. An equal volume of culture medium is                added to terminate the digestion when the digestion                is completed. The DMEM, M199, L15 and MEM medium,                and set up 4 serum concentration gradients of                5-20%, 200 IU/mL penicillin and 200 &#956;g/mL                streptomycin are added to each medium. The                1.8&#215;105cells are inoculated into 4 kinds                of medium, and cultured in 20 &#176;C, 5%                carbon dioxide incubator for 12, 24, 36 and 48                hours. The cells are counted with a hemocytometer                after culturing for 12, 24, 36 and 48h and drawn                the growth curves of cells under different medium                conditions. The cell suspension with 0.4% trypan                blue is mixed in a ratio of 9:1 by volume, dropped                it on a hemocytometer for counting after 1 min, and                observed under a microscope. The living cells are                round and transparent. The dead cells are round and                transparent. The cells are stained blue. The cell                viability is expressed as the percentage of all                cells counted by viable cells. The 10 &#956;L                cell counting kit-8 solution is added to each well                in a 96-well hepatocyte culture plate, placed the                culture plate at 18 &#176;C, 5% carbon dioxide,                incubated in an incubator for 4h and utilized a                microplate reader to detect the absorbance value at                450 nm. A hepatocyte slides are fixed with periodic                acid-schiff fixative solution for 15min, washed 3                times with phosphate-buffered saline and air-dried.                A 5 g/L periodic acid oxidant are added, oxidized                at room temperature for 20min in the dark, rinsed                twice with tap water after oxidation, and dipped in                distilled water for 2 times. A Schiff reagent is                added and placed in a dark place at room                temperature for 20 minutes. A sodium sulfite                solution is added for 2 times and each time for 2                minutes. The solution is rinsed with running water                for 2 minutes. The Mayer`s hematoxylin staining                solution is added for 2 minutes. The cells are                digested with 0.25% trypsin after the cells                adhered. The digestion is stopped with DMEM medium                containing 15% fetal bovine serum, centrifuged at                1000 r/min for 5 min. The supernatant is discarded.                A 0.5 mL 5% glutaraldehyde is added for fixation                prepared as electron microscope cell specimens. The                characteristic organelles are observed in the                cytoplasm of hepatocytes under transmission                electron microscope followed by detecting                mycoplasma by Hoechst staining method.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN114160112-A
TI Preparing ethylbenzene oxidative dehydrogenation            catalyst useful in field of synthetic styrene catalyst            comprises e.g. adding aluminum isopropoxide to alcohol            solutions, obtaining catalyst carrier, mixing, grinding            catalyst carrier, diatomaceous earth, adding binder,            calcining, and granulating.
AU PAN Y
   LIU W
   GUO Q
   YANG C
   WANG B
AE TIANJIN DAGU CHEM IND CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202241473T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethylbenzene oxidative                dehydrogenation catalyst comprises e.g. adding                aluminum isopropoxide to mixed alcohol solutions,                stirring, adding distilled water, stirring to                obtain a gel, sealing, drying gel, reacting to                obtain the mesoporous alumina carrier, denoted as                MA, immersing mesoporous alumina carrier in the                isopropyl titanate suspension, drying, calcining,                obtaining catalyst carrier titanium                dioxide/aluminum oxide, which is denoted as xTMA,                adding catalyst carrier xTMA, ammonium                metavanadate, adding ammonium metavanadate into                distilled water, configuring into an ammonium                metavanadate suspension, immersing the xTMA                catalyst carrier in the ammonium metavanadate                suspension, drying, calcining to obtain catalyst                carrier, named as V2O5/TMA, mixing and grinding                catalyst carrier xTMA or yVTMA of catalyst carrier,                diatomaceous earth, adding binder and deionized                water, stirring and mixing, compacting and forming,                drying, calcining, granulating to obtain the                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                ethylbenzene oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst,                which is useful in oxidative dehydrogenation of                ethylbenzene (claimed), and in field of synthetic                styrene catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the reaction temperature;                improves the ethylbenzene conversion per pass; and                reduces carbon emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ethylbenzene oxidative                dehydrogenation catalyst comprises (1) adding 40-60                pts. wt. aluminum isopropoxide to 140-200 pts. wt.                mixed alcohol solutions under 25-60&#176;C,                stirring to make it completely dissolved, slowly                adding 7-10 pts. wt. distilled water dropwise,                stirring at room temperature for 5-10 hours to                obtain a gel, sealing the gel at room temperature                and allowing to stand for 8-12 hours, drying aged                gel at 65-75&#176;C for 20-30 hours, drying at                90-110&#176;C for 1.5-3.0 hours, reacting in                the reactor at 110-130&#176;C for 10-15 hours,                and finally according to the heating rate of                2-3&#176;C/minute, calcining at                500-600&#176;C for 4-6 hours to obtain the                mesoporous alumina carrier, denoted as MA, (2)                taking 40 pts. wt. the mesoporous alumina carrier,                35.5275-71.055 pts. wt. isopropyl titanate, adding                isopropyl titanate to distilled water twice the                volume of the mesoporous alumina carrier to form an                isopropyl titanate suspension dipping solution,                immersing mesoporous alumina carrier in the                isopropyl titanate suspension for 4-6 hours, drying                on a hot plate at 60-80&#176;C, drying at                95-105&#176;C for 10-15 hours, and drying at                500-600&#176;C for 10-15 hours, calcining for                4-6 hours, obtaining catalyst carrier titanium                dioxide/aluminum oxide, which is denoted as xTMA,                where x represents the mass ratio of titanium                dioxide and aluminum oxide in the catalyst carrier                titanium dioxide/aluminum oxide, the active                ingredient of the mesoporous alumina carrier is                aluminum oxide, and the active ingredient after                calcination of isopropyl titanate is titanium                dioxide, (3) adding 40 pts. wt. catalyst carrier                xTMA, 0.0515-7.3502 pts. wt. ammonium metavanadate,                adding ammonium metavanadate into distilled water                twice the volume of the catalyst carrier xTMA,                configuring into an ammonium metavanadate                suspension, and immersing the xTMA catalyst carrier                in the ammonium metavanadate suspension for 4-6                hours, drying on a hot plate at 60-80&#176;C,                drying at 90-110&#176;C for 10-15 hours, and                calcining at 500-600&#176;C for 4-6 hours to                obtain catalyst carrier V2O5 /TMA, recorded as                yVTMA, where y represents the vanadium oxide in                VTMA, (4) mixing and grinding 15-25 pts. wt.                catalyst carrier xTMA or yVTMA of catalyst carrier,                0.5-1.0 pts. wt. green powder, and 0.5-2.0 pts. wt.                diatomaceous earth for 0.5-2.0 hours, adding 20-30                ml binder and 0.1-20 ml deionized water, stirring                and mixing for 0.5-1.0 hours, compacting and                forming, drying, calcining, granulating to obtain                the ethylbenzene oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst                with particle size of 40-60 meshes.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   an ethylbenzene oxidative dehydrogenation                catalyst; and
   a method of using ethylbenzene oxidative                dehydrogenation catalyst comprising adding raw                materials ethylbenzene and carbon dioxide gas, 15                ml catalyst, reacting where the flow rate of carbon                dioxide is 500-1000 sccm, the reaction temperature                is 500-600&#176;C, and the flow rate of                ethylbenzene is 0.125-0.3 ml/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  titanium dioxide and aluminum oxide is 1: 2- 4.                  The binder is a mixture of A4 acid aluminum sol,                  acid silica alumina sol, alkaline silica alumina                  sol, special silica sol for CC type catalyst                  carrier, and silica zirconium sol.Preferred Method: In the process, mixing the                  mixed alcohol solution with 70- 100 pts. wt.                  isopropanol and 70-100 pts. wt. ethanol. The                  drying temperature is 80-120&#8451;, the                  drying time is 4-6 hours. The calcination                  temperature for molding is 450-600&#176;C,                  and the calcination time is 4-6 hours.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN113582892-A; CN113582892-B
TI Preparing metal organic cage compound useful for catalytic ethanol cracking, comprises e.g. reacting N-(4-(3,5-bis((4-((2,3-disulfanylbenzene)amido)phenyl))phenyl)phenyl)-2,3-disulfanylbenzamide ligand with 1,2-dichlorobenzene.
AU DUAN C
   LI Y
   HE C
   CAI J
   ZHAO L
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021C5684Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal organic cage compound comprises e.g. (i) adding 1,2-dichlorobenzene and isopropyl mercaptan into dimethylformamide, and rotating filtrate to obtain yellow oily product, (ii) adding yellow oily product into n-butyllithium and tetramethylethylenediamine to obtain light yellow slurry, drying carbon dioxide passed into light yellow slurry, (iii) adding off-white powder and thionyl chloride into 10-15 ml chloroform, refluxing mixed solution, removing solvent by rotary evaporation to obtain brown oily compound to obtain yellow solid, and (iv) adding yellow solid, sodium metal and naphthalene into dry tetrahydrofuran, acidifying obtained filtrate with concentrated hydrochloric acid to form white precipitate, and precipitating blue solid in solution to obtain final product, where ligand is N-(4-(3,5-bis((4-((2,3-disulfanylbenzene)amido)phenyl))phenyl)phenyl)-2,3-disulfanylbenzamide.
   USE - The metal organic cage compound is useful for catalytic ethanol cracking and in photocatalytic selective cracking of ethanol to produce hydrogen and acetaldehyde (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-organic cage compound has low raw material price, high yield, and the obtained compound has stable chemical properties and it can simultaneously catalyze the cracking of ethanol to produce hydrogen and acetaldehyde under mild light conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing metal organic cage compound comprises taking cobalt(II) ion as node in transition metal cobalt salt, L as ligand (H6TS) i.e. N-(4-(3,5-bis((4-((2,3-disulfanylbenzene)amido)phenyl))phenyl)phenyl)-2,3-disulfanylbenzamide, and transition metal cobalt salt is cobalt perchlorate, cobalt nitrate, cobalt tetrafluoroborate, cobalt chloride or cobalt trifluoromethanesulfonate, (i) adding 1,2-dichlorobenzene and isopropyl mercaptan into 200-500 ml dimethylformamide at a molar ratio of 1:5.0-5.5 under argon atmosphere, stirring at 95-100degrees Celsius for 3-4 days, then cooling the reaction solution and adding it to 500-800 ml ice water, stirring continuously for 1-2 hour, extracting with 500-800 ml of ether, drying the organic phase with 50-100 g anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering out the filtrate, and rotating the filtrate to obtain a yellow oily product, (ii) adding yellow oily product into n-butyllithium and tetramethylethylenediamine in a molar ratio of 1:1-1.1:1-1.1 into 200-400 ml hexane solution and mixing under argon atmosphere at 0-5degrees Celsius, stirring at 20-25degrees Celsius for 10-15 hours to obtain light yellow slurry, drying carbon dioxide passed into above-mentioned light yellow slurry, reacting for 1-2 hours, evaporating the solvent, dissolving residue in water and acidifying it with concentrated hydrochloric acid to a pH value of 1-2, extracting aqueous solution with 500-800 ml ether, drying the organic phase with 50-100 g anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering out the filtrate, and rotary evaporating the filtrate to obtain off-white powder, (iii) adding off-white powder and thionyl chloride into 10-15 ml chloroform at a molar ratio of 1:1-1.1 to mix, refluxing mixed solution at 70-80degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours after the reaction stops, removing solvent by rotary evaporation to obtain brown oily compound, mixing brown oily compound with benzenetriamine and triethylamine under the condition of 0-5degrees Celsius in a molar ratio of 1:0.6-0.7:1.0-1.1 in 20-40 ml tetrahydrofuran solution, stirring at room temperature for 10-15 hours, and then filtering to remove insoluble materials and removing the solvent in the filtrate by rotary evaporation to obtain yellow solid, and (iv) adding yellow solid, sodium metal and naphthalene into 20-50 ml dry tetrahydrofuran in a molar ratio of 1:20-25:5-10 under argon atmosphere, stirring at room temperature for 10-15 hours, then adding 5-10 ml methanol to remove unreacted sodium metal, and then removing all solvents by rotary evaporation, dissolving obtained solid in 2-5 ml deionized water, and washing with 50-100 ml ether, and removing insoluble matter by filtration, acidifying obtained filtrate with 1-2 ml concentrated hydrochloric acid to form a white precipitate, filtering, drying filter cake under vacuum to obtain a white powder which is the ligand H6TS, where molar ratio of ligand and transition metal cobalt salt is 1:1.5-1.6, adding 5-20 ml dimethylformamide solution, stirring at room temperature for 4-6 hours, stirring and filtering, allowing filtrate to stand at room temperature for 4 weeks, and precipitating blue solid in the solution to obtain final product.

DN 55-0-0-0-S K; 420-0-0-0-S K; 76-0-0-0-S K; 188508-0-0-0-S K; 91418-0-0-0-S K; 1550672-0-1-0-S K; 217690-0-0-0-S K; 4115-0-0-0-S K; 1442-0-0-0-S K; 699-0-0-0-S K; 6-0-0-0-S K; 107367-0-0-0-U V K; 9-0-0-0-U V K; 100-0-0-0-U V K; 97153-0-0-0-P K; 376-0-0-0-P K

PT P
PN CN113209836-A; CN113209836-B
TI Preparing composite separation membrane based on            fluorine-containing copolymer in-situ growing organic            framework structure used in gas separation, involves            adding first solvent to autoclave, and sealing through            nitrogen and exhaust oxygen.
AU YU D
   ZHENG W
   BAO Y
   CAI H
   WANG S
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   ZHEJIANG JUHUA TECHNOLOGY CENT CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202194068X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite separation membrane based                on fluorine-containing copolymer in-situ growing                organic framework structure involves adding 180-230                pts. wt. first solvent into autoclave, sealing                through nitrogen and exhaust oxygen until the                oxygen concentration is less than 5 parts per                million, mixing 19.5-36.6 pts. wt.                perfluoro-2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole and 0.14-4.01                pts. wt. amino-terminated olefin, and 1.0-5.0 pts.                wt. tetrafluoroethylene to the reactor, stirring                well, adding 0.10-0.18 pts. wt. diisopropyl                peroxydicarbonate, stirring, heating to 45-60                degrees C, polymerizing for 5-8 hours to obtain a                perfluoro-2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole-tetrafluoroethylene-amino-terminated                olefin ternary random copolymer containing reactive                functional group, and taking 0.01-0.30 pts. wt. the                obtained                perfluoro-2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole-tetrafluoroethylene-amino-terminated                olefin ternary random copolymer containing reactive                functional group.
   USE - Preparing composite separation membrane based                on fluorine-containing copolymer in-situ growing                organic framework structure used in gas separation                (claimed) e.g. oxygen and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite separation membrane has enhanced                bonding force between fluorine-containing copolymer                layer and organic frame structure layer through                amido bridging. The inner structure of pore canal                of organic frame structure layer is modified to                realize adjustable inner aperture size, it can aim                at organic gas and inorganic gas (oxygen, carbon                dioxide) to efficiently and selectively separate,                and has very high utility value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite separation membrane based                on fluorine-containing copolymer in-situ growing                organic framework structure involves adding 180-230                pts. wt. first solvent into autoclave, sealing                through nitrogen and exhaust oxygen until the                oxygen concentration is less than 5 parts per                million, mixing 19.5-36.6 pts. wt.                perfluoro-2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole and 0.14-4.01                pts. wt. amino-terminated olefin, and 1.0-5.0 pts.                wt. tetrafluoroethylene to the reactor, stirring                well, adding 0.10-0.18 pts. wt.                diisopropylperoxydicarbonate, stirring, heating to                45-60 degrees C, polymerizing for 5-8 hours to                obtain a                perfluoro-2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole-tetrafluoroethylene-amino-terminated                olefin ternary random copolymer containing reactive                functional group, taking 0.01-0.30 pts. wt. the                obtained                perfluoro-2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole-tetrafluoroethylene-amino-terminated                olefin ternary random copolymer containing reactive                functional group, 1.70-1.99 pts. wt.                perfluoro-2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole-co-tetrafluoroethylene                binary copolymer, dissolving in 10-100 pts. wt.                first solvent, subjecting the solution to                ultrasonic degassing treatment, standing, casting                the solution into a film, drying in vacuum at room                temperature to obtain a film with a diameter of                20-25 mm and a thickness of 30-40 micron, adding                0.09-0.32 pts. wt. aldehyde compound, 0.15-3.30                pts. wt. acid anhydride compound, dissolving in                10-50 pts. wt. anhydrous tetrahydrofuran containing                0-5 wt.% perfluoro(2-n-butyltetrahydrofuran),                performing at room temperature under the protection                of nitrogen, dropping the anhydrous tetrahydrofuran                solution containing aldehyde compound to the                solution containing acid anhydride to obtain a                mixed solution, refluxing the mixed solution at                30-80 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere for 12-36                hours after cooling to room temperature, adding                10-50 pts. wt. deionized water to the mixed                solution to stop the reaction to obtain a                carboxyl-containing compound, covering one side of                the obtained film, placing in the reactor,                dissolving all the obtained carboxyl-bearing                compound in 10-100 pts. wt. second solvent,                dissolve uniformly by ultrasonic, adding to the                reaction kettle, adding 1-30 pts. wt. acetic acid                solution, reacting at 100-200 degrees C for 1-5                hours, dissolving 0.09-0.41 pts. wt. the amine                compound in 10-50 pts. wt. second solvent, adding                to the obtained reaction solution after ultrasonic                treatment to dissolve uniformly, reacting                continuously at 100-200 degrees C for 36-72 hours                after finishing the reaction, taking out film,                washing to remove unreacted material, putting into                50-200 pts. wt. the aqueous solution of the                functional group compound, adjusting the pH of the                solution to 6.5-7.5 at 20-60 degrees C after                shaking for 10-72 hours, and taking out to obtain a                composite separation membrane based on the in-situ                growth organic framework of the fluorinated                copolymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The terminal amino                  olefin is selected from 4-penten-1-amine,                  5-hexen-1-amine, 3-butene-1-amine, and                  oleylamine. The first solvent is selected from                  perfluoroheptane,                  perfluoro(2-n-butyltetrahydrofuran),                  hexafluorobenzene, hexafluoroisopropanol, and                  dioxane. The aldehyde-based compound includes                  2,4,6-triformylphloroglucinol and                  2-hydroxy-1,3,5-benzenetricarbaldehyde. The                  second solvent is selected from perfluoro                  (2-n-butyltetrahydrofuran), mesitylene, dioxane,                  dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran, and ethanol.                  The amine compound includes p-phenylenediamine                  and 2,3,5,6-tetrafluoro-1,4-phenylenediamine. The                  functional group compound is selected from cobalt                  acetate, cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, cobalt                  chloride hexahydrate, 5-amino-1-pentanol,                  6-amino-1-hexanol, 4-amino-1-butanol, choline                  chloride, and                  1-(2-hydroxyethyl)-3-methylimidazole chloride.                  Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of aldehyde                  compound to acid anhydride compound is 1:3-10.                  The molar ratio of carboxylic acid-containing                  compound to amine compound is 1:1.5. The mass                  percent solubility of the aqueous solution of the                  functional group compound is 1-10 wt.%. The molar                  content ratio of the                  perfluoro-2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole in the                  copolymer is 65-98 mol.%. The total molar content                  ratio of tetrafluoroethylene and amino-terminated                  olefin in the copolymer is 2-35 mol.%. The molar                  content ratio of the copolymer of the                  amino-terminated olefin is 2-10 mol.%.

PT P
PN CN112048163-A
TI Preparation of impact-resistant street lamp cover material involves preparing catalyst, polycarbonate unsaturated polyester copolymer and mixed resin, adding curing agent, molding to shell, removing shell burr polishing, and smoothing.
AU TANG Z
AE MAANSHAN JIXIANG OPTOELECTRONIC TECHNOLO (MAAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4747J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of impact-resistant street lamp cover material involves preparing a catalyst, a polycarbonate unsaturated polyester copolymer and a mixed resin. The preparation of catalyst involves adding 58-63 parts mass ethylene glycol dimethyl ether to 4.7-5.1 parts mass 35-38% zinc chloride solution, adding 38-40 parts mass 8-9% potassium hexacyanocobaltate solution into the zinc chloride solution, continuously stirring for 20-30 minutes, filtering to separate the precipitate, vacuum drying at 50-60 degrees C. The preparation of copolymer involves using nitrogen to blow high pressure reaction kettle for 10 minutes under the protection of nitrogen atmosphere, adding 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. obtained catalyst, 27-31 pts. wt. propylene oxide, 15-24 pts. wt. phthalic anhydride, 3-6 pts. wt. maleic anhydride and 10-15 pts. wt. toluene, stirring at 25-30 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour, and filling carbon dioxide at 35 degrees C under pressure of 1.7-2 MPa with flow rate of 25-50 parts per 25 minutes.
   USE - Preparation of impact-resistant street lamp cover material.
   ADVANTAGE - Preparation of street lamp cover material provides the lamp cover with high light transmittance. The lamp cover does not fade easily, does not soften at high temperature and does not change its mechanical property in the bad environment. The material provides excellent shock resistance, scratch resistance, waterproof performance, impact resistance, electrical insulation, extensibility, dimensional stability, high strength, heat resistance, cold resistance, heat aging and electrical performance. The cover material can be colored and has excellent self-extinguishing property, flame retardance, non-toxic, corrosion resistance, wind resistance, explosion proof property and outdoor UV radiation proof property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of impact-resistant street lamp cover material involves preparing a catalyst, a polycarbonate unsaturated polyester copolymer and a mixed resin. The preparation of catalyst involves adding 58-63 parts mass ethylene glycol dimethyl ether to 4.7-5.1 parts mass 35-38% zinc chloride solution, adding 38-40 parts mass 8-9% potassium cobalt hexacyanocobaltate solution into the zinc chloride solution, continuously stirring for 20-30 minutes, filtering to separate the precipitate, vacuum drying at 50-60 degrees C. The preparation of copolymer involves using nitrogen to blow high pressure reaction kettle for 10 minutes under the protection of nitrogen atmosphere, adding 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. obtained catalyst, 27-31 pts. wt. propylene oxide, 15-24 pts. wt. phthalic anhydride, 3-6 pts. wt. maleic anhydride and 10-15 pts. wt. toluene, stirring at 25-30 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour, filling carbon dioxide at 35 degrees C under pressure of 1.7-2 MPa with flow rate of 25-50 parts per 25 minutes, stirring at 105-115 degrees C for 2-4 hours, cooling to room temperature, discharging the residual gas, washing the obtained precipitate with ethanol solution containing 2-3 %mass hydrochloric acid, washing three times by ethanol, and vacuum drying at 50-60 degrees C. The preparation of mixed resin involves mixing uniformly 5-10 pts. wt. methacrylate butadiene styrene (MBS) resin micro powder, 80-85 pts. wt. polycarbonate unsaturated polyester copolymer, 3-8 pts. wt. vinyl ester resin, 5-10 pts. wt. multifunctional astigmatism master batch and 4-9 pts. wt. diluent. The multifunctional astigmatism comprises 35.0-40.0 wt.% phosphorous flame retardant, 12.0-13.5 wt.% light scattering auxiliary agent, 12.0-18.0 wt.% antimony flame retardant, 4.5-5.0 wt.% compounded antioxidant, 12.0-13.5 wt.% dispersing lubricant and 15.0-20.0 wt.% polyvinylidene acetylene carrier resin. The process further involves adding high speed mixer to mix for 10-15 minutes, extruding and granulating using twin screw extruder at 160-180 degrees C, cooling, grinding and crushing to 800-1500 mesh. The processing of obtained mixed resin involves drying at 60-70 degrees C for 1-2 hours, adding 2-5% curing agent, uniformly stirring for 5-10 minutes at 160-180 degrees C, pressing and molding to a shell under the pressure of 200-300 MPa, removing the shell burr, polishing, and smoothing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The light scattering auxiliary agent comprises precipitated barium sulfate and/or precipitated zinc sulfate. The phosphorous flame retardant comprises at least one of cyclic alkyl phosphate, tetra(2-cyano-ethyl)phosphonium bromide, phosphoric acid tri(bromotoluene)ester, guanidine phosphate, N-hydroxymethyl-3-dimethyl phosphoryl propionamide, tetra(hydroxymethyl)phosphorous chloride and red phosphorus. The antimony flame retardant comprises at least one of bis(2,4,6-tribromophenoxy)triphenyl antimony, sodium antimonate, antimony trichloride and antimony pentachloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The compounded antioxidant comprises antioxidant 1010 (pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3',5'-di-tert-butyl-4'-hydroxyphenyl)propionate)), antioxidant 1076 (octadecyl 3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate) and antioxidant DSTP (distearyl thiodipropionate) in the weight ratio of 1:1:1. Preferred Components: The dispersing lubricant comprises one of morpholine fatty acid salt, diatomite and ethylene bisstearamide. The diluent comprises at least one of diallyl phthalate, methyl styrene and triallyl cyanurate. The curing agent comprises at least one or mixture of tert-butyl peroxy benzoate, di-tert-butyl peroxide, dicumyl peroxide and dibenzoyl peroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The vinyl ester resin comprises at least one of bisphenol A epoxy acrylate resin, phenolic epoxy vinyl ester, ethyl carbamate vinyl ester resin.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
