PT P
PN CN111454898-A
TI Preparing mesenchymal stem cell-conditioned medium used for preparing cosmetics, by preparing specific mediums, preparing lithium chloride mother solution, isolating and culturing cell, and preparing conditioned medium.
AU YANG J
   HAN L
   WANG X
   LI D
AE ZHONGKE SHENGHE STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 202075202U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) conditioned medium for cosmetics, involves (a) preparing No.1 medium by using high-sugar minimum essential medium (MEM), penicillin/streptomycin mixture, sodium pyruvate, non-essential amino acids, sodium glutamyl and fetal bovine serum, mixing, filtering and sterilizing, (b) preparing lithium chloride mother solution by using lithium chloride solid and high-sugar MEM, (c) preparing No.2 medium by using e.g. high-sugar MEM, (d) isolating and culturing MSC by cutting the umbilical cord tissue longitudinally along the umbilical cord, peeling off the blood vessels, separating the Wharton glue, cutting the Wharton glue, inoculating in a cell culture dish, adding No.1 culture medium, incubating, changing medium every three days, culturing, digesting with trypsin-EDTA solution, subculturing, passaging, taking a portion of cells to prepare the medium, and freezing the remaining cells, and (e) preparing MSC conditioned medium (MSC-CM).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing MSC conditioned medium, which is used for preparing cosmetics (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared MSC conditioned medium has good use effect, high utilization rate and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) conditioned medium for cosmetics, involves (a) preparing No.1 medium by taking 420 ml high-sugar minimum essential medium (MEM), adding into a container, adding 5 ml 100x penicillin/streptomycin mixture, 5 ml 100x sodium pyruvate, 5 ml 100x non-essential amino acids, 5 ml 100x sodium glutamyl and 50 ml fetal bovine serum, mixing, filtering and sterilizing, (b) preparing lithium chloride mother solution by weighing 100 mg lithium chloride solid, dissolving in 8 ml high-sugar MEM, and diluting to 10 ml after fully dissolving, where the concentration of the solution is 10 mg/ml, (c) preparing No.2 medium by taking 416 ml high-sugar MEM, adding into the container, adding 5 ml 100x penicillin/streptomycin mixture, 5 ml 100x sodium pyruvate, 5 ml 100x non-essential amino acids, 5 ml 100x sodium glutamyl and 50 ml fetal bovine serum, simultaneously adding 4 ml lithium chloride mother liquor, mixing, filtering and sterilizing, (d) isolating and culturing MSC by cutting the umbilical cord tissue longitudinally along the umbilical cord, peeling off the blood vessels, separating the Wharton glue inside the umbilical cord tissue with scissors, cutting the separated Wharton glue into small pieces of 1 mm3, then inoculating in a cell culture dish, adding an appropriate amount of No.1 culture medium, placing the Petri dish in an incubator with a temperature of 37 degrees C and a carbon dioxide concentration of 5% for 10-14 days, during this period, changing the No.1 culture medium every three days after proper liquid addition on the second day, culturing the cells to 80% confluency, digesting the adherent mesenchymal stem cells with 0.5% trypsin-EDTA solution, subculturing at a ratio of 1:3, passaging, taking a portion of the cells to prepare the MSC conditioned medium, and freezing the remaining cells at a density of 2x 106/ml, and (e) preparing MSC conditioned medium (MSC-CM) by taking the fourth passage of cells, inoculating the cells in No.1 medium and No.2 medium at a density of 1x 105/ml respectively, after the cells grow to 90% confluence in the two mediums, carefully discarding the culture, adding an appropriate amount of 1x Hanks' balanced salt solution (HBSS) solution (for a 10 cm cell culture dish, 10-15 ml HBSS solution can be added) to wash the cells twice, thoroughly sucking up the remaining HBSS solution, adding serum-free high-sugar Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) medium at 37 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 5% for 72 hours, collecting the two kinds of cell culture supernatants in a 50 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 1000 g for 10 minutes to remove cell debris, measuring the volume of the supernatant, adding an appropriate amount of 20% sterile sucrose solution to each supernatant to make the final concentration of sucrose reach 1 weight/volume%, dividing the above liquid into 10 ml/bottle (penicillin bottle), transferring to a vacuum freeze-drying bottle, after pre-frozen at -80 degrees C, lyophilizing in a vacuum freeze-dryer, and storing in a refrigerator at -80 degrees C. The two conditioned media are labeled MSC-CM1: conditioned media prepared by MSC cultured in medium No.1, and MSC-CM2: conditioned medium prepared from MSC cultured in medium No.2 (a medium containing lithium chloride).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The final concentration of the penicillin/streptomycin mixture is 100 ug/ml. The high-sugar DMEM in step (e) comprises 100 u/ml penicillin/100 ug/ml streptomycin, 1 mM sodium pyruvate, 10 uM non-essential amino acids, 2 mM L-glutamine, 55 uM mercaptoethanol, 10 ng/ml upper epidermal growth factor and 80 ug/ml lithium chloride mother liquor. The volume of the No.1 medium and No.2 medium is 500 ml.

TI Production of Q420 high-strength steel thick plate            without preheating submerged-arc welding method            involves welding the welding plate, setting angle of            front slope, cleaning to remove impurities, cooling,            cutting and extinguishing arc plate.
AU CHEN L
   ZHUO Z
   SHAO D
   ZHANG J
   TAN G
   ZENG F
AE GUANGZHOU HUANGCHUAN OCEANOGRAPHIC ENG (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202004112B
AB    NOVELTY - Production of Q420 high-strength steel thick                plate without preheating submerged-arc welding                method involves welding the welding plate by Q420                high-strength steel thick plate processing into                X-type welding slope port, setting angle of front                slope into 55-65 degrees , back groove angle into                65-75 degrees , depth of back groove plate                thickness into 1/3, truncated edge into 9-11 mm and                root gap into 0-1 mm, processing, and cleaning                X-type welding oxide layer and X-type welding                divided edge of groove face to remove impurities                into 20-30 mm range; welding to locate welding seam                into X-type welding divided edge through                semi-automatic carbon dioxide gas shielded welding,                and setting thickness of locating welding seam into                4-5 mm, locating weld seam length into 50-60 mm and                welding line distance into 400-500 mm; and cooling,                cutting welding joint two end arc striking and                extinguishing arc plate.
   USE - Method for producing Q420 high-strength steel                thick plate without preheating submerged-arc                welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good welding quality, reduced                welding number and improved welding                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of Q420 high-strength steel thick                plate without preheating submerged-arc welding                method comprises: (1) welding the welding plate by                Q420 high-strength steel thick plate processing                into X-type welding slope port, setting angle of                front slope into 55-65 degrees , back groove angle                into 65-75 degrees , depth of back groove plate                thickness into 1/3, truncated edge into 9-11 mm and                root gap into 0-1 mm, processing, and cleaning                X-type welding oxide layer and X-type welding                divided edge of groove face to remove impurities                into 20-30 mm range; (2) welding to locate welding                seam into X-type welding divided edge through                semi-automatic carbon dioxide gas shielded welding,                and setting thickness of locating welding seam into                4-5 mm, locating weld seam length into 50-60 mm and                welding line distance into 400-500 mm; (3)                connecting 2 ends of X-type welding divided edge                into arc initiation plate and arc blow-out plate,                centering front welding wire, adjusting welding gun                angle and distance, inclining front electrode into                0 degrees C, and setting front wire conductive                nozzle to workpiece distance into 35 mm, posterior                electrode tilting into 17-23 degrees and posterior                conductive nozzle to workpiece distance into 40 mm                and front electrode and back electrode distance                into 30-35 mm; (4) firstly welding front welding                seam and front groove, filling back side welding                seam, setting front backing welding current into                650-670 A, front welding voltage into 32-34 V,                front wire welding speed into 65-70 cm/minute,                posterior welding current into 600-620 A, posterior                welding voltage into 36-38 V and wire welding speed                into 65-70 cm/minute, front filling and cover                welding, setting front welding current into 670-680                A, front wire welding voltage into 31-33 V, front                wire welding speed into 65-70 cm/minute, welding                current into 560-580 A, posterior welding voltage                into 37-39 V and wire welding speed into 65-70                cm/minute, back welding, setting front welding                current into 950-970 A, front welding voltage into                32-34 V, front wire welding speed into 62-68                cm/minute, posterior welding current into 620-640                A, back welding voltage into 36-38 V and wire                welding speed into 62-68 cm/minute, back filling                and welding, setting front welding current into                650-670 A, front welding voltage into 32-34 V,                front welding speed into 70-75 cm/minute and                welding current into 560-580 A, posterior welding,                setting posterior welding voltage into 36-38 V and                wire welding speed into 70-75 cm/minute, back cover                surface welding, setting front welding current into                640-660 A, front wire welding voltage into 31-33 V,                front wire welding speed into 65-70 cm/minute and                welding current into 550-570 A, posterior welding,                and setting posterior welding voltage into 34-36 V                and wire welding speed into 65-70 cm/minute; and                (5) cooling, cutting welding joint two end arc                striking and extinguishing arc plate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  setting back wire electrode inclination into 20                  degrees C, front electrode and back electrode                  distance into 35 mm and welding wire conductive                  nozzle length into 35-45 mm, carrying out welding                  by using specified requirement of GB/TH08MnMoA                  model-12470, using double-power and                  double-electrode welding with front wire that                  adopts direct current power source welding and                  posterior welding by alternating current power,                  welding back side welding seam using carbon arc                  air gouging with mechanical polishing, cleaning                  divided edge root, root penetrating and removing                  un-melted slag defect, and welding channel at                  100-230 degrees C and staggering into 50 mm in                  welding each weld joint.

TI Three-dimensional network carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material, obtained by taking sodium dodecyl sulfate and furosemide into water, ultrasonically oscillating uniformly and adding carbon nanotubes.
AU LI H
   FU Y
   LIU H
   WEI D
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201962110H
AB    NOVELTY - Three-dimensional network carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material, where average specific surface area of the carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material is 600-608 m2/g, the nanoscale and microscale pores exist in both carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel materials. The three-dimensional network carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material is obtained by (i) taking 0.5-15 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate and 0.01-20 pts. wt. furosemide into 100 pts. wt. water, ultrasonically vibrating, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. carbon nanotubes, supersonically dispersing uniformly, vacuuming, maintaining vacuum, returning to normal pressure and repeating the vacuuming step to obtain the wet gel, (ii) obtaining the wet gel and (iii) obtaining the three-dimensional reticulated carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material by supercritical drying.
   USE - Used as three-dimensional network carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Three-dimensional network carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material, where average specific surface area of the carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material is 600-608 m2/g, the nanoscale and microscale pores exist in both carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel materials, the average of the nanoscale pores is 10-12 nm and the average of the microscale pores is 20-22 mu m. The three-dimensional network carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material is obtained by (i) taking 0.5-15 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate and 0.01-20 pts. wt. furosemide into 100 pts. wt. water, ultrasonically vibrating, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. carbon nanotubes, supersonically dispersing uniformly, vacuuming, maintaining vacuum, returning to normal pressure and repeating the vacuuming step three times to obtain the wet gel, (ii) drying 0.05-15 pts. wt. montmorillonite, using a solvent to obtain 0.05-25 wt.% montmorillonite dispersion, mechanically stirring the montmorillonite dispersion uniformly and ultrasonically obtaining the wet gel and (iii) mixing 0.5-35 pts. wt. wet gel of step (i), 0.5-30 pts. wt. wet gel of step (ii), 0.1-20 pts. wt. solution and 0.05-20 wt.% nifediquinone, stirring, ultrasonically centrifuging, obtaining the product, placing the product in a carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction unit, obtaining the three-dimensional reticulated carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material by supercritical drying with carbon dixoide as medium at 10-300 degrees C and a pressure of 1-20 MPa for at least 1 hour. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the three-dimensional network carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material, comprising, (i) taking 0.5-15 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS) and 0.01-20 pts. wt. furosemide into 100 pts. wt. water, ultrasonically oscillating uniformly, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. carbon nanotubes, carrying out ultrasonic dispersion, maintaining the vacuum, returning the pressure to normal pressure, and repeating the vacuuming three times to obtain the wet gel, (ii) drying 0.05-15 pts. wt. montmorillonite, dispersing 0.05-25% montmorillonite dispersion in a solvent, mechanically stirring the montmorillonite dispersion uniformly, ultrasonicating and obtaining gel, (iii) mixing 0.5-35 pts. wt. wet gel, 0.5-30 pts. wt. wet gel of step (ii) and 0.1-20 pts. wt. 0.05-20 wt.% nifediquinone, stirring, ultrasonically centrifuging, obtaining the product, placing the product in a CO2 supercritical high pressure extraction device, obtaining the three-dimensional reticulated carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material by supercritical drying at 10-300 degrees C and a pressure of 1-20 MPa for at least 1 hour using carbon dioxide as a medium, dispersing the furosemide into the hollow structure of the carbon nanotubes, where the hollow structure of the carbon nanotubes provides a microporous structure for the three-dimensional network of carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material, and the furosemide is supported in the carbon nanotubes, the three-dimensional network structure is formed between the carbon nanotubes, and the montmorillonite added in the step (ii) is successfully constructed into a network pore structure, the above three-dimensional network structure and carbon nanotubes are successfully constructed as a network pore structure to form a three-dimensional network pore structure and the three-dimensional network pore structure provides a mesoporous structure for the three-dimensional network carbon nanotube-montmorillonite complex aerogel material, and the nifediquinone is supported in the mesoporous structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises adding 1-10 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS) and 0.05-15 pts. wt. furosemide into water, ultrasonically vibrating for 2-48 hours, adding 1-10 pts. wt. carbon nanotubes, ultrasonically dispersing for 1-24 hours and vacuuming for 1 hour. The step (ii) comprises drying 0.1-10 pts. wt. montmorillonite at 60-180 degrees C for 5-20 hours, where the solvent is configured to be 0.1-20 wt.% of a montmorillonite dispersion, mechanically stirring the montmorillonite dispersion at 5-35 degrees C under 150-400 revolutions/minute for 5-10 hours and sonicating with a power of 50-300 W for 6-15 hours. The step (iii) comprises mixing 1-30 pts. wt. carbon nanotube drug-loaded wet gel, 1-30 pts. wt. montmorillonite wet gel and 0.5-15 pts. wt. nifedipine solution, stirring at 5-35 degrees C for 10-25 hours, sonicating for 6-15 hours and centrifuging under 1000-3500 revolutions/minute for 1-8 hours. In the step (iii), the supercritical drying time is 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours. In the step (i) comprises adding 1-10 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS) and 0.05-15 pts. wt. furosemide into the water, ultrasonically vibrating for 2-48 hours, adding 1-10 pts. wt. carbon nanotubes, ultrasonically dispersing for 1-24 hours and evacuating for 1 hour. The step (ii) comprises drying 0.1-10 pts. wt. montmorillonite at 60-180 degrees C for 5-20 hours, dissolving 0.1-20% montmorillonite dispersion with a solvent, mechanically stirring the montmorillonite dispersion at 5-35 degrees C under speed of 150-400 revolutions/minute for 4-10 hours and carrying out ultrasonic treatment with 50-300W power for 6-15 hours. The step (iii) comprises taking 1-30 pts. wt. carbon nanotube drug-loaded wet gel, 1-30 pts. wt. montmorillonite wet gel and 0.5-15 pts. wt. mixture of n-benzoquinone solutions, stirring at 10-35 degrees C for 10-25 hours, sonicating for 6-15 hours, and centrifuging at 1000-3500 revolutions/minute for 1-8 hours. In the step (iii), the supercritical drying time is 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours.

PN US10344231-B1; WO2020086258-A1; CN112930387-A
TI Generating fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream and oxidation reactor char product stream, comprises introducing carbonaceous feedstock, hydromethanation catalyst, steam and oxygen into reactor, and reacting feedstock.
AU ROBINSON E T
   SIRDESHPANDE A
   RAMAN P K
   KECKLER K
   KEKLER K
AE GREATPOINT ENERGY INC (GRAT-C)
   SURE CHAMPION INVESTMENT LTD (SURE-Non-standard)
GA 201960753U
AB    NOVELTY - Generating fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream and an oxidation reactor char product stream from a non-gaseous carbonaceous material and a hydromethanation catalyst, comprises e.g.: (a) preparing a carbonaceous feedstock from the non-gaseous carbonaceous material; (b) introducing the carbonaceous feedstock, a hydromethanation catalyst, steam and oxygen into a hydromethanation reactor, the hydromethanation reactor comprising a fluidized bed, an initial disengagement zone above the fluidized bed, and a gas mixing zone below the fluidized bed; and (c) reacting the carbonaceous feedstock in the hydromethanation reactor in the presence of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, steam and hydromethanation catalyst, and at an operating temperature of 400-1500 degrees F, and an operating pressure of at least 250 psig, to produce a methane-enriched raw product gas, heat energy and a by-product char.
   USE - The process is useful for generating fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream and an oxidation reactor char product stream from a non-gaseous carbonaceous material and a hydromethanation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The process present: improves carbon utilization in the process; improves the overall thermal efficiency; and reduces the volume of char material processed in catalyst recovery (which allows the use of reduced equipment size), thus lowering both operating and capital costs for the operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generating fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream and an oxidation reactor char product stream from a non-gaseous carbonaceous material and a hydromethanation catalyst, comprises: (a) preparing a carbonaceous feedstock from the non-gaseous carbonaceous material; (b) introducing the carbonaceous feedstock, a hydromethanation catalyst, steam and oxygen into a hydromethanation reactor, the hydromethanation reactor comprising a fluidized bed, an initial disengagement zone above the fluidized bed, and a gas mixing zone below the fluidized bed; (c) reacting the carbonaceous feedstock in the hydromethanation reactor in the presence of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, steam and hydromethanation catalyst, and at an operating temperature of 400-1500 degrees F, and an operating pressure of at least 250 psig, to produce a methane-enriched raw product gas, heat energy and a by-product char; (d) withdrawing a stream of methane-enriched raw product gas from the hydromethanation reactor as the methane-enriched raw product gas stream, where the methane-enriched raw product gas stream comprises methane, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, steam, and entrained solids; (e) removing a substantial portion of the entrained solids from the methane-enriched raw product gas stream to generate a solids-depleted, methane-enriched raw product gas stream and a recovered primary solids stream; (f) removing a substantial portion of any fines from the solids-depleted, methane-enriched raw product gas stream to generate a fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream and a recovered secondary fines stream; (g) withdrawing a stream of by-product char from the hydromethanation reactor as the by-product char stream, where the by-product char stream comprises a carbon content and entrained hydromethanation catalyst; (h) feeding all or a portion of the by-product char stream, oxygen and steam into an oxidation reactor integrated with the hydromethanation reactor or into a standalone oxidation reactor; (i) reacting at least a portion of the carbon content of the by-product char with oxygen and steam in the oxidation reactor at a temperature of less than or equal to 1500 degrees F, to produce an oxidation reactor char product stream comprising entrained hydromethanation catalyst, an oxidation gas comprising steam, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and heat energy; (j) withdrawing a stream of the oxidation gas from the standalone oxidation reactor, if present, as the oxidation gas stream and feeding all or a portion of the oxidation gas stream into the hydromethanation reactor through an oxidation gas distributor plate, combining all or a portion of the oxidation gas stream with the methane-enriched raw product gas stream, combining all or a portion of the oxidation gas stream with the solids-depleted methane-enriched raw product gas stream, and/or combining all or a portion of the oxidation gas stream with the fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream; and (k) withdrawing the oxidation reactor char product stream from the oxidation reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process has a steam demand and a power demand that are met by internal energy integration such that the process requires no net import of steam or power. The operating pressure in the hydromethanation reactor is at least 250-1200 psig, where the oxidation reactor is a fluidized-bed oxidation reactor, the hydromethanation catalyst comprises an alkali metal, and the alkali metal is potassium. The process further comprises: treating the oxidation reactor char product stream in a catalyst recovery unit to extract a portion of the entrained catalyst, to generate a washed char product and to generate liberated hydromethanation catalyst, withdrawing a stream of the washed char product from the catalyst recovery unit, and withdrawing a stream of the liberated hydromethanation catalyst from the catalyst recovery unit as a recovered hydromethanation catalyst stream; feeding the recovered primary solids stream into the hydromethanation reactor, and feeding all or a portion of the recovered secondary fines stream into a catalyst recovery unit or into the oxidation reactor, where the hydromethanation catalyst comprises at least a portion of the recovered hydromethanation catalyst stream; introducing the fines-cleaned methane-enriched raw product gas stream into a heat exchanger unit to remove heat energy and generate a cooled methane-enriched raw product stream, steam shifting a portion of the carbon monoxide in the cooled methane-enriched raw product stream as required in a shift reactor system to generate a hydrogen-enriched raw product gas stream with a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of close to 3, dehydrating the hydrogen-enriched raw product gas stream in a low-temperature gas cooling system, to generate a dry raw gas stream, and removing a substantial portion of the carbon dioxide and a substantial portion of the hydrogen sulfide from the dry raw gas stream in an acid gas removal unit to produce a sweetened gas stream comprising a substantial portion of the hydrogen, carbon monoxide and methane from the dry raw gas stream; and reacting the carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the sweetened gas stream in a methanation system in the presence of a methanation catalyst to produce heat energy and a pipeline quality substitute natural gas stream, recovering the heat energy from the catalytic methanation, and utilizing at least a portion of the recovered heat energy to generate and superheat a steam stream, where the process has a steam demand and a power demand that are met by internal energy integration so that the process requires no net import of steam or power.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109053177-A
TI Preparing highly thermally conductive ceramic substrate comprises e.g. preparing highly thermally conductive polyester resin, and then dissolving copper sulfate pentahydrate and sodium tartrate in normal temperature water.
AU SONG L
AE SONG L (SONG-Individual)
GA 201900918U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly thermally conductive ceramic substrate comprises e.g. (i) preparing highly thermally conductive polyester resin, (ii) dissolving copper sulfate pentahydrate and sodium tartrate in normal temperature water to obtain solution of divalent copper solution and sodium tartrate, and dissolving sodium hydroxide in water to obtain alkali solution, and mixing divalent copper solution and the sodium tartrate solution to obtain mixed solution, (iii) placing above mixture in three-necked flask equipped, and adding above-mentioned alkali solution to three-necked flask and heating, (iv) after solution in the above three flasks appeared blue, keeping the mixture and adding glucose solution to the three-necked flask until the blue color disappeared, performing filtration, then separating red particles were, washing and drying to obtain cuprous oxide particles and (v) adding sodium aluminate solution into beaker, passing carbon dioxide gas into beaker and ventilating.
   USE - The highly thermally conductive ceramic substrate is useful for electronic circuit (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing highly thermally conductive ceramic substrate comprises (i) adding 45-50 pts. wt. phthalic acid, 20-24 pts. wt. neopentyl glycol, 6-10 pts. wt. trimethylolpropane, 1-3 pts. wt. monobutyltin oxide into four-necked flask equipped with a condensing unit, starting a stirrer, stirring at a speed of 400-440 revolutions/minute, heating at 235-250 degrees C under the protection of nitrogen; insulating the reaction for 2-2.5 hours, adding 12-15 pts. wt. 1,4-cyclohexanedicarboxylic acid, then performing acid hydrolysis reaction for 2-2.5 hours, cooling to room temperature, adding 22-30 pts. wt. quartz sand with a particle size of 400 mesh, and then continuously reacting for 20-28 minutes and cooling to room temperature to obtain highly thermally conductive polyester resin, (ii) dissolving 52-60 g copper sulfate pentahydrate and 75-79 g sodium tartrate in 220-300 ml normal temperature water to obtain solution of divalent copper solution and sodium tartrate, and dissolving 75 g sodium hydroxide in 200 ml water to obtain alkali solution, and mixing divalent copper solution and the sodium tartrate solution in a volume ratio of 4:1 to obtain mixed solution, (iii) placing above mixture in a three-necked flask equipped with a dropping funnel, and adding above-mentioned alkali solution to a three-necked flask at a dropping acceleration rate of 4-5 ml/minute using a dropping funnel, and heating the three-necked flask at 92-100 degrees C, where the volume of the alkali solution added is 1/4-1/3 volume of mixture, (iv) after the solution in the above three flasks appeared blue, keeping the mixture for 10-14 minutes, and adding 10% glucose solution to the three-necked flask until the blue color disappeared, removing the filtrate by filtration, and then separating red particles were, and washing the red particles 3-4 times with water and ethanol and then drying in an oven at a setting temperature of 52-60 degrees C and a drying time of 7-7.5 hours to obtain cuprous oxide particles, (v) adding 720-800 ml 20% sodium aluminate solution into a beaker, passing carbon dioxide gas into the beaker, then ventilating at a ventilation rate of 20-28 ml/minute for 41-45 minutes, after the ventilation is completed, adding 150-175 g mullite powder and 8-10 g ammonium polyacrylate to the beaker, placing the beaker in a water bath, heating at 72-80 degrees C, stirring and mixing for 30-34 minutes to obtain mixed slurry, (vi) pouring the mixed slurry into a casting machine for casting, after the mixed slurry is cast, allowing to stand and drying for 2.5-3 hours to obtain dried raw material, heating at 200-280 degrees C to obtain hot melt coating, applying a hot melt coating to the surface of the cast green tape and heating at 1510-1550 degrees C, insulating and sintering for 45-49 minutes, heating in a carbon monoxide atmosphere to 520-600 degrees C and then insulating for 4-4.5 hours to obtain a highly thermally conductive ceramic substrate, where the mass ratio between cuprous oxide particles and high thermal conductivity polyester resin is 4:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (vi): the flow delay control blade height is 0.5-1.5 mm, and the casting base tape moving speed is 0.5-1.5 cm/seconds.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); ; L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); V04 (Printed Circuits and Connectors)
MC A04-D09; A05-E02A; A05-E05; A08-M09C; A08-R06A; A09-A01A; A10-D05; A10-E05B; A12-E07; L02-A02; L02-A03; L02-G01A; L02-G01B1; L02-G04; L02-G11; L02-G12E1; L02-G12X; L02-J02C; L02-K; L03-A02D; V04-R03L; V04-S02; V04-S15; V04-T03J; V04-T03P; V04-X01E

PT P
PN CN108726477-A; CN108726477-B
TI Heat balance compact fixed bed chemical looping hydrogen production integrated decarburization device comprises e.g. a fixed bottom plate, a reaction body A, a reaction body B, heat exchanger A, and heat exchanger B.
AU DOU B
   HE M
   ZHANG H
   CUI G
   RUAN C
AE UNIV SHANGHAI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USHS-C)
GA 201887266E
AB    NOVELTY - Heat balance compact fixed bed chemical looping hydrogen production integrated decarburization device comprises a fixed bottom plate (1), a reaction body A (2), a reaction body B (3), heat exchanger A (4), heat exchanger B (5), the top of the complex containing rotary combination valve, insulator and standard fastener, the fixed bottom plate with the reaction body A, reaction body B, the heat exchanger A and the heat exchanger B. The insulator and a standard fastener connected with the reaction body A, the body B, the heat exchanger A, the heat exchanger B connection is processed with four lug bosses, can be used for sealing and positioning. The four lug bosses is provided with two channels for communicating reaction body A and the heat exchanger B, reaction body B is connected with the heat exchanger A, the fixed bottom plate can be connected through standard fastening piece with the top body, the reaction body A.
   USE - Used as heat balance compact fixed bed chemical looping hydrogen production integrated decarburization device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has flexible arrangement, small floor area, economical and good security.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Heat balance compact fixed bed chemical looping hydrogen production integrated decarburization device comprises a fixed bottom plate (1), a reaction body A (2), a reaction body B (3), heat exchanger A (4), heat exchanger B (5), the top of the complex containing rotary combination valve, insulator and standard fastener, the fixed bottom plate with the reaction body A, reaction body B, the heat exchanger A and the heat exchanger B. The insulator and a standard fastener connected with the reaction body A, the body B, the heat exchanger A, the heat exchanger B connection is processed with four lug bosses, can be used for sealing and positioning. The four lug bosses is provided with two channels for communicating reaction body A and the heat exchanger B, reaction body B is connected with the heat exchanger A, the fixed bottom plate can be connected through standard fastening piece with the top body, the reaction body A, reaction body B have the same structure with chemical chain vapor reconstruction producing hydrogen catalysts, catalyst on the lower end are respectively provided with a porous gas flow distributor and filter cotton. The two reaction body and the fixed bottom plate and the top body connected with the heat exchanger A, the heat exchanger B have the same structure, heat exchanger adopts double-channel design, where a channel of a filled carbon dioxide absorbent, the inlet and outlet interface is located on the heat side channel of heat exchange body is U-shaped design, another channel b and outlet interface located at the two side of the heat exchanger, b channel where one interface connected with the passage of the fixing bottom plate, another interface with a channel interface connected with the top body, the top with the rotary combination valve complex whose one side with the reaction body A, reaction body B, the heat exchanger A, the heat exchanger B connection is processed with four lug bosses, can be used for sealing and locating the four lug bosses is provided with interface, where reaction body A, B each one of the heat exchanger A, B each three. The middle part of the other side of the top composite has locating groove capable of connected with the matched rotation control mechanism to drive the rotary combined valve core to rotate in the interior of top body, the slot is arranged beside the six interfaces are pyrolysis gas inlet, water in air inlet, an exhaust outlet, a carbon dioxide outlet, hydrogen the top of complex can be connected with the fixing bottom plate through the standard fastener. The whole reactor is set outside the insulator, the insulator outside the stainless steel sheet is fixed on the fixed bottom plate and the top body outer circle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the heat balance compact fixed bed chemical looping hydrogen production integrated decarburization device.
   Fixed bottom plate (1)
   Reaction body A (2)
   Reaction body B (3)
   Heat exchanger A (4)
   Heat exchanger B (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reaction body A, reaction body B is a fixed bed chemical looping reactor, the oxidation and reduction process by flowing through the switching of the medium is completed. The top body composed of inner containing rotary combination valve switching for each material in the device and each timing time length of the control is finished. The two reaction body and the two heat exchange cross symmetrically arranged, and are wrapped with heat insulating material. The device in the reaction body A, the body B, the heat exchanger A and the heat exchanger B are made of high temperature resistant iron-base alloy material, the fixed bottom plate made of high temperature ceramic material. The active component of the hydrogen-catalyzed oxygen carrier in the reaction body is nickel oxide, ferric oxide, manganese oxide or cobalt monoxide, the inactive carrier is aluminum oxide, magnesium aluminate or silicon dioxide, and the carbon dioxide absorber in the heat exchanger is calcium oxide. The molar ratio of the hydrogen-producing oxygen carrier in the reaction body to the carbon dioxide absorber in the heat exchanger is 0.2-2. The working temperature of the chemical chain steam reforming hydrogen production reaction device is 700-800 degrees C, the carbon dioxide absorbent to absorb working temperature is 500-600 degrees C, the decomposing carbon dioxide of working temperature is 800-900 degrees C. The device is the continuous circulation, in one cycle can be divided into eight steps, (i) the high temperature pyrolysis raw material gas enters from the top body, the heat exchanger A to exchange heat with the carbon dioxide absorber channel a channel b to be dropped in temperature, and through the fixed bottom plate inner channel into the reaction body A by steam reforming of the raw material gas in oxygen catalysis to obtain hydrogen and carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide absorbent inlet channel a is sent to the heat exchanger B, carbon dioxide is absorbed by the carbon dioxide absorbent. The hydrogen exhausting access external hydrogen storage tank through the top body from the outlet of the channel a, the steam enters from the top body after heat exchange to exchange heat with the    ZHOU J
   TANG H
   LI Z
   JI W
   QU G
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202292513G
AB    NOVELTY - Performing plasma excitation photoelectric                catalytic degradation VOCs involves introducing the                gas containing VOCs into the plasma excitation                photoelectric catalytic component, the plasma                excitation photoelectric catalytic component                completely catalytic degradation is carbon dioxide                and water, discharging after the catalytic                degradation process can prevent the catalyst carbon                deposition, where the plasma excitation                photoelectric catalytic component comprises an                insulating shell, a cylindrical negative electrode,                a honeycomb anode, a gas distribution plate, an                insulating ball, a gas collecting chamber, the                insulating shell is sleeved outside the cylindrical                negative electrode, the honeycomb anode is set at                the center of the insulating shell, the chamber                between the cylindrical negative electrode and the                honeycomb anode is filled with insulating balls,                one end of the insulating shell is provided with an                air inlet.
   USE - Method for performing plasma excitation                photoelectric catalytic degradation of VOCs.

PN CN114431458-A
TI Preparing honey fruit and vegetable ferment raw            material involves cleaning fruits, removing kernels,            chopping, sterilizing, adding honey of equal quality            into obtained fruit, stirring uniformly, and adding            purified water to obtain fruit-honey mixture.
AU WANG G
   SUN M
AE GUANGZHOU MUJIA HEALTH IND CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202266364P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing honey fruit and vegetable ferment                raw material involves cleaning fruits, removing                kernels, chopping, sterilizing, adding honey of                equal quality into obtained fruit, stirring                uniformly, adding purified water to obtain                fruit-honey mixture, adding biological selenium and                stirring uniformly, allowed to stand for 30-45 days                under the condition of passing carbon dioxide for                anaerobic fermentation to obtain fruit and                vegetable honey enzyme liquid one. The pH value of                fruit and vegetable honey ferment liquid I is                adjusted to 7.0, honey source bacteria is added,                and aerobic fermentation is carried out.
   USE - Method for preparing honey fruit and vegetable                ferment raw material for use in the field of                microorganism.
   ADVANTAGE - The honey fruit and vegetable fermented honey                is sensitive to Salmonella, Staphylococcus aureus                and Escherichia coli based on honey fruit and                vegetable fermented honey, which well inhibits the                growth of mixed bacteria, effectively improves the                honey preservation effect, reduces the sugar                content of the honey fruit and vegetable fermented                honey, improves the nutritional value of the honey                fruit and vegetable fermented honey, and is rich in                multiple probiotic enzyme substances, where                probiotic enzyme substances comprise SOD, protease,                lipase amino acid and the like. The preparation                method enables to obviously reduce the content of                methanol generated in the fermentation process and                greatly reduce the harm to human bodies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing honey fruit and vegetable ferment                raw material involves cleaning fruits, removing                kernels, chopping, sterilizing, adding honey of                equal quality into obtained fruit, stirring                uniformly, adding purified water to obtain                fruit-honey mixture, adding biological selenium and                stirring uniformly, allowed to stand for 30-45 days                under the condition of passing carbon dioxide for                anaerobic fermentation to obtain fruit and                vegetable honey enzyme liquid one. The pH value of                fruit and vegetable honey ferment liquid I is                adjusted to 7.0, honey source bacteria is added,                and aerobic fermentation is carried out. The                temperature is maintained at 27-29degrees Celsius,                pH is adjusted to 4.5-5.5, compound fermentation                bacteria powder is added, stirred continuously, and                anaerobic fermentation is carried out continuously                for 8-12 days to obtain fruit and vegetable honey                enzyme solution two. The total acid value of fruit,                vegetable and honey enzyme solution two is                measured, sealing state is carried out to maintain                the temperature 28-30degrees Celsius, cellar                storage is performed for 45-55 days, and filtered                to obtain fruit and vegetable honey ferment raw                liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The honey source                  bacteria is Bacillus licheniformis strain. The                  methylotrophic bacterium is Pichia pastoris                  strain.Preferred Composition: The amount of                  biological selenium added is 1-3 wt.% of the                  fruit and honey mixture. The amount of the honey                  source bacteria added is 0.5-2 wt.% of the fruit                  and vegetable honey enzyme solution one. The                  addition amount of the composite fermention                  powder is 10-15 wt.% of the fruit and vegetable                  honey enzyme solution one. The composite                  fermention powder is prepared from methylotrophic                  bacteria, soybean peptide powder and peach gum                  extract in a weight ratio of 1:18:6. The culture                  medium comprises 10.0 g casein peptone, 10.0 g                  beef extract, 5.0 g glucose, 5.0 g sodium                  acetate, 2.0 g diammonium citrate, 1.0 g Tween                  (RTM: polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate) 80,                  2.0 g dipotassium hydrogenphosphate, 0.2 g                  magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.05 g manganese                  sulfate monohydrate, 20.0 g calcium carbonate,                  0.5 g sodium selenite, 1.2 g vitamin E, 15.0 g                  agar and 1.0 L distilled water, where culture                  conditions comprises pH of 6.8, and 37degrees                  Celsius.Preferred Process: The aerobic fermentation                  process after the honey source bacteria are added                  involves performing aerobic fermentation at                  27-32degrees Celsius for 35-45 days while                  stirring every 3-5 hours. The biological selenium                  is obtained by inoculating Lactobacillus                  plantarum subspecies plantarum strain in a                  culture medium under aseptic conditions.

PN CN114053205-A
TI Preparation of facial skin anti-aging nutritional liquid freeze-dried powder involves culturing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell, growing cell, centrifuging cell lysis solution, mixing stem cell factor extract with nutritional solution and freeze-drying mixture.
AU ZHANG W
   CUI D
AE SHANGHAI NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
GA 202231789M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of facial skin anti-aging nutritional liquid freeze-dried powder involves culturing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell in vitro, selecting umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell from at least one of P3-P20 generation at 36.5-37.5degreesC and 5% carbon dioxide cell culture medium in adherent culture, growing the cell to 85-90%, digesting and collecting the cell, obtaining the collected umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell for cell lysis to obtain cell lysis solution, centrifuging the cell lysis solution to obtain supernatant, obtaining the supernatant as stem cell factor extract, mixing the stem cell factor extract with nutritional solution in a volume ratio of 2-3:2-3 to obtain raw liquid, mixing the raw liquid and the freeze-drying protective agent in a volume ratio of (0.8-1.2):(8.5-9.5), and freeze-drying the mixture. The nutritional solution comprises 10-50 microg/ml vitamin C, 1-5 mg/ml sodium hyaluronate and 1-10 microg/ml transferrin.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of facial skin anti-aging nutritional liquid freeze-dried powder and in cosmetics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves safety of the product under premise of ensuring the effectiveness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation method of facial skin anti-aging nutritional liquid freeze-dried powder involves culturing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell in vitro, selecting umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell from at least one of P3-P20 generation at 36.5-37.5degreesC and 5% carbon dioxide cell culture medium in adherent culture, growing the cell to 85-90%, digesting and collecting the cell, obtaining the collected umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell for cell lysis to obtain cell lysis solution, centrifuging the cell lysis solution to obtain supernatant, obtaining the supernatant as stem cell factor extract, mixing the stem cell factor extract with nutritional solution in a volume ratio of 2-3:2-3 to obtain raw liquid, mixing the raw liquid and the freeze-drying protective agent in a volume ratio of (0.8-1.2):(8.5-9.5), and freeze-drying the mixture. The nutritional solution comprises 10-50 microg/ml vitamin C, 1-5 mg/ml sodium hyaluronate and 1-10 microg/ml transferrin. The freeze-drying protective agent comprises 50-150 g/l mannitol, 1-10 g/l trehalose and 5-15 g/l dextran 40.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for facial skin anti-aging nutritional solution freeze-dried powder, which is prepared by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The cell culture medium comprises 1-100 microg/ml Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) culture medium containing recombinant human insulin, 1-50 microg/ml human transferrin and 1-80 microg/ml selenious acid.Preferred Method: The method involves collecting the cultured umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell, adding 0.1-1 ml physiological saline to the 1000 million IU cells, adding the mixture into centrifugal tube, placing in -60 to -80degreesC in refrigerator for repeatedly freeze-thawing, ultrasonically crushing the cell, filtering the obtained cell lysis solution by the screen, centrifuging the obtained filtrate at 4degreesC and 10000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 15 minutes, and collecting the supernatant. The ultrasonic crushing cell adopts the 400-650 W, frequency is 20-25 KHz, each time ultrasonic is 5-10 seconds, ultrasonic for 5 times, and/or the aperture of the screen is 20-40 microns. The freeze-drying temperature is -60degreesC to -80degreesC, pre-freezing time is 4-8 hours, and vacuum freezing time is 48-72 hours. The umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell is obtained by culturing for stem cell marker identification and detecting the cell factor in the stem cell factor extract. The preparation method involves inoculating umbilical cord-derived mesenchymal stem cells according to density of 5000-10000/cm2, performing conventional in vitro cell culture, where each subculture ratio is 1:10, selecting umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell from at least one of P3-P20 generation at 36.5-37.5degreesC and 5% carbon dioxide cell culture medium in adherent culture, until the cell grows to 85-90%, digesting and collecting the cell, performing morphological identification to the stem cell marker of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell, where the result shows that the shape of stem cell does not change substantially, adding 0.5 ml physiological saline to the 10 million IU cells to re-suspend, adding into 1.5 ml centrifugal tube, placing in -80degreesC in refrigerator for repeatedly freeze-thawing for 3 times, where each time is placed at 37degreesC water bath to thaw, processing cell for 5 times under ultrasonic condition, where each time ultrasonic time is 5-10 seconds, performing ultrasonic cracking for several times, where the ultrasonic power is 600 W and frequency is 20KHz, filtering the obtained solution using 0.22 m filter, collecting the sterile container to obtain the cell lysis solution, collecting the cell lysis solution in a high speed freezing centrifuge at 4degreesC and 10000 rpm for 15 minutes, collecting the supernatant, obtaining stem cell factor extract, as liquid A, storing in the environment of -20degreesC, detecting the component of the stem cell factor extract, thus the result shows that the extract is rich in epidermal growth factor (EGF), fibroblast growth factor (FGF), keratinocyte growth factor (KGF), insulin-like growth factor (IGF), vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) and transforming growth factor of growth factor-beta (TGF-beta), adding 4 mg vitamin C and 3 g sodium hyaluronate into 100 ml physiological saline and 0.8 mg human transferrin, obtaining nutritional liquid B, mixing the liquid A and the liquid B to obtain freeze-dried powder stock solution, mixing the raw liquid with the freeze-drying protective agent, freeze-drying at -60degreesC to -80degreesC for 4 hours, vacuum-freezing for 48 hours to obtain facial skin anti-aging nutrient liquid freeze-dried powder, storing at -20degreesC, adding freeze-dried powder raw liquid, and finally fixing the volume to 1 l. The freeze-dried powder is prepared by dissolving in 0.9% physiological saline, and preparing anti-aging nutritional solution. The nano-crystal or nano-needle is introduced into the skin.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

PN WO2021247210-A1
TI Epoxy-curable silicone release coating composition for preparing release liner has epoxy-functional polyorganosiloxane and inhibited catalyst selected from complex of fluorinated triaryl borane and amine, and Bridged Frustrated Lewis Pair.
AU WEI Y
   NIU Z
   FU P
AE DOW SILICONES CORP (DOWO-C)
GA 2021E0419Y
AB    NOVELTY - An epoxy-curable silicone release coating composition comprises (A) epoxy-functional polyorganosiloxane (I); and (B) inhibited catalyst selected from (B-1) complex comprising (i) fluorinated triaryl borane and (ii) amine, where nitrogen is not a member of an N=C-N linkage, and (B-2) Bridged Frustrated Lewis Pair comprising a complex of (i) fluorinated triaryl borane, (ii) Lewis base, and (iii) bridging molecule connecting the fluorinated triaryl borane and the Lewis base, where the bridging molecule is selected from carbon dioxide, hydrogen (H2), nitriles, alkenes, alkynes, ketones, esters, and aldehydes.
   USE - An epoxy-curable silicone release coating composition for preparing release liner (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The epoxy-curable silicone release coating composition is capable of curing to form a silicone release coating at relatively low temperatures with acceptable anchorage to substrates. Releasing the Lewis acid catalyst from the inhibitor allows the Lewis acid to catalyze cure of the epoxy-groups.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An epoxy-curable silicone release coating composition comprises (A) epoxy-functional polyorganosiloxane comprising unit of formula (R1R2R3SiO1/2)a(R1R2SiO2/2)b(R1SiO3/2)c(SiO4/2)d(ZO1/2)e (I); and (B) inhibited catalyst selected from (B-1) complex comprising (i) fluorinated triaryl borane and (ii) amine of formula R4R3R3N, where nitrogen is not a member of an N=C-N linkage, and (B-2) Bridged Frustrated Lewis Pair comprising a complex of (i) fluorinated triaryl borane, (ii) Lewis base selected from PR73, P(NR72)3, NR73, N(Si73)xR73-x, R7C(NR7)N, P(N-R7)R73, or formula (T), and (iii) bridging molecule connecting the fluorinated triaryl borane and the Lewis base, where the bridging molecule is selected from carbon dioxide, hydrogen (H2), nitriles, alkenes, alkynes, ketones, esters, and aldehydes.
   R1-R3=1-30C monovalent hydrocarbon or 1-30C monovalent hydrocarbon functionalized with a cyclic epoxy-functional group;
   Z=H or Me, provided that greater than or equal to 2 R1 groups per molecule are 1-30C monovalent hydrocarbon functionalized with cyclic epoxy-functional group;
   a= greater than or equal to 1;
   b, c, d, e= greater than or equal to 0;
   (a+b+c+d)=2-1000;
   R4-R6=H or optionally substituted alkyl, where each of R4-R6 is connected to N by a non-conjugated C; and
   R7=H, optionally alkyl, or optionally substituted aryl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of epoxy-curable silicone release coating composition which involves mixing, under ambient conditions, starting materials comprising (A) and (B).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Fluorinated triaryl borane is of formula (II). Amine is a trialkyl amine selected from triethylamine, tripropylamine, tributylamine, tripentylamine, and trihexylamine. Lewis base is PR73. Bridging molecule is carbon dioxide.R=H, F, or CF3, provided that at least one instance of R per molecule is F or CF3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Epoxy-functional polyorganosiloxane is linear and has unit of formula (R1'R2'R3'SiO1/2)2(R1'R2'SiO2/2)b'. Epoxy-functional polyorganosiloxane has unit of formula (R1'R2'R3'SiO1/2)a'(R1SiO3/2)c'(SiO4/2)d'(ZO1/2)e', where number weight average molecular weight is 2000-200000 g/mol.R1'=alkyl optionally functionalized with a cyclic epoxy functional group;R2', R3'=alkyl;b'=5-900;a', c', d'= greater than or equal to 1; ande'= greater than or equal to 0.Preferred Processes: The method further comprises coating the epoxy-curable silicone release coating composition on a substrate. The method further comprises heating the epoxy-curable silicone release coating composition at 80-95 degrees C for 10-50 s to cure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: Substrate comprises glassine paper. Substrate comprises thermal paper.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R7=alkyl.
   EXAMPLE - An epoxy-functional polyorganosiloxane and an inhibited catalyst were mixed in amounts sufficient to provide 500 ppm tris(pentafluorophenyl)borane based on the weight of the epoxy-curable silicone release coating composition.

PN CN113479897-A
TI Preparing two-dimensional nano-sheet silicate useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce methanol involves immersing attapulgite powder into hydrochloric acid solution, separating out solid, washing, dissolving transition metal nitrate in suspension, cooling and vacuum drying.
AU WU F
   LI Z
   YAO C
   ZUO S
   SHI A
   ZHONG M
   LI X
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 2021B8884Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing two-dimensional nano-sheet silicate by using attapulgite involves (i) immersing the attapulgite powder into hydrochloric acid solution, mixing and stirring, washing and drying to obtain the pre-processed attapulgite powder; (ii) dispersing the attapulgite powder pre-processed in the step (i) into the acid solution, hydrothermal stirring, separating out solid, washing and drying to obtain precursor; (iii) dispersing the precursor obtained in the step (ii) in water to form a suspension and ultrasonically dispersing, adjusting the pH value, dissolving the transition metal nitrate in the suspension, adding the ammonium nitrate into the suspension, adding the aqueous ammonia into the suspension and stirring to obtain uniform suspension; and (iv) transferring the uniform suspension obtained in the step (iii) into the polytetrafluoroethylene hydrothermal reaction kettle, microwave reacting, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging, separating the solid, washing and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method for peparing two-dimensional nano-sheet silicate is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low raw material cost, simple synthesis method and is good for large-scale popularization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing two-dimensional nano-sheet silicate by using attapulgite involves (i) immersing the attapulgite powder into 3-7 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, mixing and stirring for 0.5-4 hours, washing and drying to obtain the pre-processed attapulgite powder; (ii) dispersing the attapulgite powder pre-processed in the step (i) into the acid solution of less than 5 mol/l, hydrothermal stirring for 4-16 hours, separating out solid, washing and drying to obtain the six-ring-based silicone tetrahedral white precursor; (iii) dispersing the precursor obtained in the step (ii) in water to form a suspension and ultrasonically dispersing, adjusting the pH value between 6-10 in stirring, dissolving the transition metal nitrate in the suspension, adding the ammonium nitrate into the suspension, gradually adding the aqueous ammonia into the suspension, stirring uniformly to obtain uniform suspension, where the mol ratio of silicon, transition metal element and ammonium ion is 1-2:1-4:1-12 and the transition metal nitrate is iron, cobalt, copper and nitrate; and (iv) transferring the uniform suspension obtained in the step (iii) into the polytetrafluoroethylene hydrothermal reaction kettle, under the condition of 120-220degrees Celsius, microwave reacting for 30-120 minutes, naturally cooling to room temperature, centrifuging and separating the solid, washing and vacuum drying to obtain the two-dimensional nano-sheet silicate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solid-liquid ratio of the attapulgite powder and the hydrochloric acid solution in the step (i) is 1:1. The concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 5 mol/L. The solid-to-liquid ratio of the attapulgite powder and the hydrochloric acid solution in the step (ii) is 1: 10. The concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 2 mol/L. The mass concentration of aqueous ammonia water is 28%. The acid solution is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid or nitric acid.Preferred Method: In the step (i), pre-treating for 2 hours. In the step (ii), hydrothermal stirring at 80degrees Celsius for 6 hours. In the step (iii), adjusting pH by dropping 0.5 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution or 0.1 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, ultrasonic dispersing for 30 minutes and stirring for 10 minutes. In the step (iv), the vacuum drying at 60degrees Celsius for 2 hours. The method for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce methanol comprises dispersing the two-dimensional nano-sheet silicate in deionized water, adding photocatalytic the reaction device, and introducing carbon-dioxide into the reaction device at a set flow rate and light catalytic carbon dioxide reduction to obtain methanol.

PN CN113402765-A
TI Preparing polymer cross-linked aerogel material, involves mixing methanol, organosilicon precursor and aminosilane coupling agent, mixing methanol, aqueous ammonia and deionized water, adding to above mixed solution, stirring , adding aging liquid, performing solvent replacement, and drying.
AU WU X
   SHEN X
   CUI S
   DING J
   HUANG S
   MAN J
   TAI J
   LU J
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   JIANGSU RUIYING NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B09318
AB    NOVELTY - A preparation method of polymer cross-linked aerogel material, involves (1) uniformly mixing methanol, organosilicon precursor and aminosilane coupling agent, and uniformly stirring for 0.5-4 hours at 5-50 degrees C to obtain silica sol system (A), (2) uniformly mixing methanol, aqueous ammonia and deionized water, and uniformly stirring at 5-50 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours to obtain alkaline solution (B), (3) adding the alkaline solution (B) to the silica sol system (A) obtained in the step (1), uniformly stirring to gel at 5-40 degrees C, and leaving still for 15-30 hours, (4) adding aging liquid containing crosslinking agent to the gel formed in the step (3), leaving still, modifying in oven, and performing solvent replacement, and (5) performing carbon dioxide supercritical drying treatment to the wet gel obtained in the step (4) to obtain a final product.
   USE - Preparation method of polymer cross-linked aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of polymer cross-linked aerogel material with high strength, low thermal conductivity, and low energy consumption, by simple and convenient process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A preparation method of polymer cross-linked aerogel material, involves (1) uniformly mixing methanol, organosilicon precursor and aminosilane coupling agent, and uniformly stirring for 0.5-4 hours at 5-50 degrees C to obtain silica sol system (A), (2) uniformly mixing methanol, aqueous ammonia and deionized water, and uniformly stirring at 5-50 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours to obtain alkaline solution (B), (3) adding the alkaline solution (b) to the silica sol system (a) obtained in the step (1), uniformly stirring to gel at 5-40 degrees c, and leaving still for 15-30 hours, (4) adding aging liquid containing crosslinking agent to the gel formed in the step (3), leaving still, modifying in oven, and performing solvent replacement, and (5) performing carbon dioxide supercritical drying treatment to the wet gel obtained in the step (4) to obtain a final product, where the methanol, organosilicon precursor and aminosilane coupling agent in the step (1) are uniformly mixed in a molar ratio of (5-30):(0.33-3):1, the methanol, aqueous ammonia and deionized water are mixed according to the molar ratio of (200-500): 1:(200-500), and the methanol in the silica sol system (A) and the methanol in the alkaline solution (B) are mixed in the step (3) according to the molar ratio of 1:(0.33-3).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organosilicon precursor in step (1) is one or more of tetraethyl orthosilicate, tetramethyl orthosilicate, methyltrimethoxysilane, dimethyldimethoxysilane or ethyl trimethoxysilane. The aminosilane coupling agent in step (1) is one or more of ( gamma )-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, ( gamma )-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, phenylaminomethyltriethoxysilane, phenylaminomethyltrimethoxysilane or aminoethylaminoethylaminopropyl trimethoxysilane. The crosslinking agent in step (4) is one or more of toluene diisocyanate, dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate, hexamethylene diisocyanate or lysine diisocyanate. The solvent in the aging liquid is one or a mixture of methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, acetone, acetonitrile, or dimethyl ether. Preferred Composition: The amount of aqueous ammonia in step (2) is 5-25 %mass. The amount of the crosslinking agent in the aging liquid in step (4) is 5-50 vol.%. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the oven in step (4) is 20-60 degrees C, the static modification time is 24-72 hours, the number of solvent replacement 3-9 times, and each time interval is 8-24 hours. The drying method in step (5) is a carbon dioxide supercritical drying method, which is performed by using carbon dioxide as a drying medium, the reaction temperature is 40-80 degrees C, the pressure in the autoclave is 8-12 MPa, the gas release rate is 5-10 L/minute, and the drying time is 8-15 hours.

PN CN113336996-A
TI Preparing dual fiber co-reinforced recyclable            poly(butylene adipate-co-terephthalate) foam useful in            thermal insulation materials, comprises e.g. placing            bio-based long fiber in ethanol, grinding and preparing            particles into foam material.
AU OU X
   LEI C
   XU R
   DONG Z
   CHEN Z
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 2021A4431F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dual fiber co-reinforced recyclable                poly(butylene adipate-co-terephthalate) (PBAT) foam                comprises (i) placing bio-based long fiber in an                ethanol solution containing a coupling agent,                adding water to pH 4-5, stirring, removing                bio-based long fibers, placing removed bio-based                long fibers in an oven to dry and grinding; (ii)                placing bio-based short fiber in ethanol solution                containing a coupling agent, adding water to pH                4-5, stirring, removing bio-based short fibers,                placing removed bio-based short fibers into an oven                to dry and grinding; (iii) mixing fine powder and                second fine powder, placing in a stirring device,                stirring, and embedding bio-based long fibers in                the bio-based short fibers, mixing stirred mixture                and PBAT uniformly, preparing into pellets through                twin-screw extruder; and (iv) using supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid as a physical foaming agent                and preparing particles into a foam material by a                kettle pressure foaming method.
   USE - The material is useful in disposable                tableware, thermal insulation materials, sound                insulation materials, farmland compost and moisture                retention materials (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material is economical, non-toxic and                environment-friendly and has high melt strength,                high compressive strength, and strong                degradability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dual fiber co-reinforced recyclable                poly(butylene adipate-co-terephthalate) (PBAT) foam                comprises (i) placing the bio-based long fiber in                an ethanol solution containing a coupling agent,                adding water to pH 4-5, stirring, removing the                bio-based long fibers, placing the removed                bio-based long fibers in an oven to dry and                grinding into fine powder; (ii) placing the                bio-based short fiber in an ethanol solution                containing a coupling agent, adding water to pH                4-5, stirring, removing the bio-based short fibers,                placing the removed bio-based short fibers into an                oven to dry and grinding into fine powder; (iii)                mixing fine powder and the second fine powder,                placing in a stirring device for stirring, and                embedding the bio-based long fibers in the                bio-based short fibers during stirring, mixing the                stirred mixture and PBAT uniformly, preparing into                pellets through a twin-screw extruder; and (iv)                using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as a                physical foaming agent and preparing the particles                into a foam material by a kettle pressure foaming                method. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                dual fiber co-reinforced recyclable PBAT foam                prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  coupling agent is a silane coupling agent, the                  dosage of the coupling agent is 0.3-2%, and the                  ethanol solution is an ethanol aqueous solution                  with a volume concentration of 95%. In the step                  (ii), the coupling agent is a silane coupling                  agent, the amount of the coupling agent is                  0.3-2%, and the ethanol solution is an ethanol                  aqueous solution with a volume concentration of                  95%. In the step (ii), the bio-based short-fiber                  is processed bagasse, and the treatment process                  comprises drying bagasse, sieving through a                  50-mesh sieve, and drying the obtained powder,                  the average length of bio-based short fibers is                  35-50 microns, and the width of bio-based short                  fibers is 15-20 microns, the average length of                  bio-based long fibers is 50-500 microns, and the                  width of bio-based long fibers is 20-25 microns.                  Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises placing                  the bio-based long fiber in an ethanol solution                  containing a coupling agent, adding water to pH                  4-5, stirring for 3 hours, allowing to stand for                  30 minutes, removing the bio-based long fibers,                  placing the removed bio-based long fibers in an                  oven, drying at 100 degrees C for 1 hour and                  grinding into fine powder. In the step (i), the                  bio-based long fiber is processed bamboo powder                  and the process comprises drying the powder,                  sieving through a 50-mesh sieve, and drying the                  obtained powder. The step (ii) comprises placing                  the bio-based short fiber in an ethanol solution                  containing a coupling agent, adding water to pH                  4-5, stirring for 3 hours, allowing to stand for                  30 minutes, removing the bio-based short fibers,                  placing the removed bio-based short fibers into                  an oven, drying at 100 degrees C for 1 hour and                  grinding into fine powder. The step (iii) mixing                  fine powder and the second fine powder, placing                  in a stirring device for stirring, and embedding                  the bio-based long fibers in the bio-based short                  fibers during stirring for 1 hour, mixing the                  stirred mixture and PBAT uniformly, preparing                  into pellets through a twin-screw extruder with                  conveying section temperature of 150-160 degrees                  C, compression section temperature of 160-170                  degrees C, and homogenization section temperature                  of 180 degrees C, where the mass ratio of the                  mixture to the PBAT is 1:99-5:95. In the step                  (iv), the foaming is carried out at 80-100                  degrees C with pressure of 8-32 Mpa.

AU CHERUVU M K
   CHAGANTI V R
AE CHERUVU M K (CHER-Individual)
   CHAGANTI V R (CHAG-Individual)
GA 2021A32832
AB    NOVELTY - Performing biomass combustion (burning) or                thermodynamics fireball process on a daily basis                with a prescribed metrics of substances (e.g. cow                ghee, biomass and fuel): increases immunity,                decreases major chronic and acute health problems                in humans, reduces viruses/bacteria/mosquitoes                within the proximity, reduces indoor and ambient                (outdoor) air pollution e.g. particulate matters                (PM2.5, PM10), nitrogen oxides, carbon oxide,                volatile organic compounds, sulfur dioxide,                greenhouse gases-carbon dioxide, methane and ground                level ozone within the proximity; mitigates the                intensities of hurricanes, tornadoes and storms                within the proximity; and constitutes favorable                conditions for clean and abundant rainfall that has                less total dissolved solids and pH levels within                6-8, reversing drought conditions and restricting                wildfires within the proximity.
   USE - The method is useful for: performing biomass                combustion (burning) or thermodynamics fireball                process on a daily basis with a prescribed metrics                of substances (e.g. cow ghee, biomass and fuel) for                increasing immunity, decreasing major chronic and                acute health problems in humans, reducing                viruses/bacteria/mosquitoes within the proximity,                reducing indoor and ambient (outdoor) air pollution                e.g. particulate matters (PM2.5, PM10), nitrogen                oxides, carbon oxide, volatile organic compounds,                sulfur dioxide, greenhouse gases-carbon dioxide,                methane and ground level ozone within the                proximity; mitigating the intensities of                hurricanes, tornadoes and storms within the                proximity; and constituting favorable conditions                for clean and abundant rainfall that has less total                dissolved solids and pH levels within 6-8,                reversing drought conditions and restricting                wildfires within the proximity; and as the                combustion chamber (Brazier) with its paraphernalia                (all claimed).

PN KR2239939-B1
TI Plasma device for performing discharge at atmospheric pressure, has central nozzle which injects gas into center of swirl generator without swirling and high voltage direct current (DC) power supply applies high voltage.
AU UHM S H
   LEE Y S
AE EN2CORE TECHNOLOGY INC (ENTW-Non-standard)
GA 202140186A
AB    NOVELTY - The plasma device has a dielectric discharge tube (170) and a swirl generator (140). An induction coil (120) is mounted to surround the dielectric discharge tube. An initial discharge generator (150) is mounted around the dielectric discharge tube to provide an initial discharge. A central nozzle (145) injects gas into the center of the swirl generator without swirling. A high voltage direct current (DC) power supply applies a high voltage between the pair of initial discharge electrodes. The thermal plasma generates syngas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide at atmospheric pressure or above atmospheric pressure by receiving carbon dioxide and methane. The central nozzle discharges methane for reaction. The inner nozzle and the outer nozzle discharge carbon dioxide to provide swirl flow. The AC power supply unit is a variable frequency power supply, and the flow rate of the gas is from several tens of liters to several hundred liters per minute.
   USE - Plasma device for performing discharge at atmospheric pressure or above atmospheric pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The swirling flow is provided in order to minimize heat transfer between the inductively coupled plasma and the dielectric tube and maintain the stability of the plasma. The magnetic flux confinement portion increases the intensity of the induced electric field, Swirl generator is easily mounted on a cylindrical discharge tube that provides gas swirl flow, Swirl guide provides a stable flow pattern , an auxiliary dielectric tube structure provides gas that performs endothermic reaction to the plasma generation location to efficiently utilize the heat generated from the plasma, an initial discharge portion that efficiently generates an initial discharge, and provides a swirl flow. The auxiliary swirl generator that improves gas-plasma interaction and increases flow stability by providing a gas swirl in the same direction as the swirl generator, and an alternating current (AC) power supply to improve the plasma stability of the induction coil are applied. The antenna of a stacked structure that increases the intensity of an induction electric field, for reducing the impedance of the induction coil in order to improve the efficiency or stability of a conventional inductively coupled plasma. The amount of current required to transmit the same power decreases, and the efficiency is also improved. The methane gas is supplied through the auxiliary dielectric tube improves discharge stability and process capability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a plasma device.
   Induction coil (120)
   Swirl generator (140)
   Central nozzle (145)
   Initial discharge generator (150)
   Dielectric discharge tube (170)
