PN CN112011509-A
TI Isolating primary microglia from rats used for            obtaining high-purity microglia from spinal cord            injury, by preparing rat model of spinal cord injury,            isolating microglia, culturing microglia, and            identifying microglia.
AU GUO Y
   WU C
   MA Y
   PAN Y
   TU P
   WANG L
   LIU M
   SUN J
   YANG G
AE UNIV NANJING CHINESE MEDICINE (UNCM-C)
GA 2020C20597
AB    NOVELTY - Method for isolating primary microglia from                rats with spinal cord injury involves (a) preparing                rat model of spinal cord injury, (b) isolating and                culturing microglia by (i) placing the raised rat                model for 1-14 days to death, using sterile                instruments to remove the spinal cord from the                damaged area after disinfection, after rinsing,                placing in DMEM/F12(HAM) complete medium at 0                degrees C to strip and removing the meninges and                blood vessels, (ii) placing the spinal cord on the                filter, grinding, washing in a centrifuge tube with                DMEM/F12 (HAM) complete medium, discarding the                supernatant and resuspending after centrifugation,                and (iii) inoculating the resuspended cells,                incubating in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide                incubator and then changing the medium for culture,                and (c) identifying microglia by taking the                cultured cells and using CD11b immunofluorescence                staining to identify them as microglia.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating primary                microglia from spinal cord injury rats, which is                useful for obtaining high-purity microglia from                spinal cord injury (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains high-purity microglia from                spinal cord injury in a short time. The short time                is 2-3 days after spinal cord injury, and the high                purity is 98% or more.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for isolating primary microglia from                rats with spinal cord injury involves (a) preparing                rat model of spinal cord injury by (i) using SD                rats, measuring their body weight, performing                intraperitoneal anesthesia, shaving back rat hair,                spreading towels, disinfecting with iodophor, using                sterile instruments to cut the skin, separating the                muscle fascia, biting off the lamina and completely                exposing the spinal cord, and (ii) using a spinal                cord blower to hit the spinal cord, then suturing                the skin, disinfecting with iodophor after surgery,                urinating regularly and maintaining warm, (b)                isolating and culturing microglia by (i) placing                the raised rat model for 1-14 days to death, using                sterile instruments to remove the spinal cord from                the damaged area after disinfection, after rinsing,                placing in DMEM/F12(HAM) complete medium at 0                degrees C to strip and removing the meninges and                blood vessels, (ii) placing the spinal cord on the                filter, grinding, washing in a centrifuge tube with                DMEM/F12 (HAM) complete medium, discarding the                supernatant and resuspending after centrifugation,                and (iii) inoculating the resuspended cells,                incubating in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide                incubator and then changing the medium for culture,                and (c) identifying microglia by taking the                cultured cells and using CD11b immunofluorescence                staining to identify them as microglia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The T10 spinal cord                  segment is completely exposed in step (a). In                  step (b), a spinal cord striker is used to hit                  the T10 segment of the spinal cord. The weight                  and diameter of the falling object are 10 g and                  2.5 mm, respectively, and the falling height and                  depth are 10 cm and 2 mm, respectively. In step                  (c), the spinal cord injury rat model after 3                  days of feeding is sacrificed. The complete                  medium is 89% DMEM/F12 (HAM), 10% fetal bovine                  serum, and 1% 100 U/ml penicillin streptomycin.                  In step (d), the pore size of the filter is 40 mu                  m, and the spinal cord is ground using a syringe                  tail. In step (e), the cells are incubated for 4                  hours and then cultured by changing the medium.                  In step (f), the CD11b immunofluorescence                  staining method identified microglia with a                  purity of more than 98%.

AU TONG F

GA 2020B8187T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing microbial fertilizer by whisky distillation residual liquid comprises (i) removing ethanol in distillation residual liquid , carbon dioxide and little volatile substance, residual liquid sugar content is 4-4.5%, (ii) introducing whisky distillation residual liquid into culture tank, adding strain, ventilating and culturing, (iii) leading the residual liquid 180-220 l primary expanding culture tank, heating and sterilizing, expanding culture tank, oxygenizing device and jacket, filling jacket with ice water, starting stirring and cooling, stopping cooling, inoculating strain from the bacteria inoculating port in sterile operation mode, measuring pH every day, and detecting number of bacteria, (iv) adding distillation residual liquid into secondary amplification tank, cooling, pressing culture liquid in primary culture tank into secondary amplification tank, an d culturing first-level expansion tank, and (v) filling sterile air, placing into bottle, storing, and selling.
   USE - The method is useful in producing microbial fertilizer by whisky distillation residual liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing microbial fertilizer by whisky distillation residual liquid comprises (i) removing ethanol in distillation residual liquid , carbon dioxide and little volatile substance, residual liquid sugar content is 4-4.5 %, where further comprises rich N, P components after using beer distillation whisky, (ii) introducing whisky distillation residual liquid into culture tank, adding strain, ventilating and culturing, where the production process is divided into two-stage expanding culture, the first stage is 180-220 l, and the second stage is 900-1000 l, (iii) leading the residual liquid 180-220 l primary expanding culture tank, using sodium hydroxide solution to adjust the pH to 5.5, heating and sterilizing, expanding culture tank is provided with stirring, oxygenizing device and jacket, filling the jacket with steam for heating and sterilizing, filling the jacket with ice water, starting stirring and cooling to 30 degrees C, stopping cooling, inoculating strain from the bacteria inoculating port in sterile operation mode, measuring the pH every day until the pH value is reduced to 3.5 after 3-4 days, and detecting the number of the bacteria, (iv) adding 900-1000 l distillation residual liquid into secondary amplification tank when the thallus content in the step (iii) reaches to 10 billion/ml, adjusting pH to 5.5, cooling to 30 degrees C, pressing the culture liquid in the primary culture tank into secondary amplification tank, culturing first-level expansion tank for 3-4 days, and (v) filling sterile air for 30 minutes everyday, exposing for 7 days, placing into bottle, storing, and selling.

PT P
PN CN111534480-A; CN111534480-B
TI In vitro culturing of mammalian embryos involves            placing sheep oocytes from ewes in mature culture            medium, culturing to obtain mature sheep oocytes,            culturing with sperm, washing and placing in incubator            for in vitro embryo culture.
AU QIU H
   WU T
   XU Y
   DONG X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   GUANGZHOU HUAZHEN BIOSCI CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202082019S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for in vitro culturing of mammalian                embryos involves (a) obtaining sheep oocytes from                ewes, placing in mature culture medium, and                culturing in an incubator for 20-24 hours to obtain                mature sheep oocytes, (b) placing mature sheep                oocytes and sperm into the fertilization culture                solution and culturing in an incubator for 18-24                hours for fertilization to obtain early sheep                embryos, and (c) washing the fertilized sheep                embryos with tissue culture medium (TCM)199                repeatedly, then placing into the sheep embryo in                vitro culture medium, placing in an incubator for                embryo culture in vitro, and maintaining the                temperature in the incubator at 38-39&#176; C                and gas concentration in the incubator at 5% carbon                dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for in vitro culturing of                mammalian embryos.
   ADVANTAGE - The stability of the environment in the                incubator ensures the survival rate of                embryos.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for in vitro culturing of mammalian                embryos involves (a) obtaining sheep oocytes from                ewes, placing in mature culture medium, and                culturing in an incubator for 20-24 hours to obtain                mature sheep oocytes, (b) placing mature sheep                oocytes and sperm into the fertilization culture                solution and culturing in an incubator for 18-24                hours for fertilization to obtain early sheep                embryos, and (c) washing the fertilized sheep                embryos with tissue culture medium (TCM)199                repeatedly, then placing into the sheep embryo in                vitro culture medium, placing in an incubator for                embryo culture in vitro, and maintaining the                temperature in the incubator at 38-39&#176; C                and gas concentration in the incubator at 5% carbon                dioxide. The incubator has a culture chamber, a                door matched with the culture chamber and a gas                concentration adjustment chamber connected with the                culture chamber. The culture chamber has multiple                culturing bins respectively provided with a culture                medium placement rack and a partition device. The                culture medium placement rack is inserted into the                culture warehouse, and after insertion, the culture                medium placement rack is sealed and matched with                the entrance of the culture warehouse. When it is                necessary to observe the embryos at different                positions in the incubator during the                above-mentioned cultivation steps, the operator                takes the medium placement rack out of the                independent cultivation chamber. In addition,                during the process that the culture medium                placement rack is withdrawn, the partition device                cuts off the communication between the                corresponding culture chamber and the outside. The                fertilization culture medium is TCM199 culture                medium containing 3% goat serum.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN110846096-A
TI Catalytic conversion of biomass synthesis gas useful in preparing liquid petroleum gas comprises weighing iron(II, III) oxide, adding e.g. silica solution, heating, drying, switching biomass synthesis gas and collecting downstream product.
AU MA W
   LA T
   CHEN G
   ZHONG L
   YAN B
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202019940C
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic conversion of biomass synthesis gas comprises (a) weighing iron(II, III) oxide, adding silica solution, adding zeolite socony mobil-5 (ZSM-5) molecular sieve to the silicon dioxide solution, shaking vigorously until uniformly mixed, (b) adding the uniformly mixed solution in a vacuum drying box to dry, adding the dried solid in a muffle furnace to calcine and remove silicon dioxide and (c) placing iron(II, III) oxide doped ZSM-5 solid catalyst precursor in constant region of the fixed bed reactor, heating, performing reduction under reducing atmosphere, switching the biomass synthesis gas as raw material into the fixed bed reactor, collecting downstream product of the reaction tube, which is liquid petroleum gas, where the composition of the biomass synthesis gas raw material comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing liquid petroleum gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is simple to prepare and economical; and exhibits good catalytic activity; achieves a carbon monoxide conversion of 81.6% and a selectivity of 42% for liquid petroleum gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalytic conversion of biomass synthesis gas comprises (a) weighing iron(II, III) oxide through a 20-40 mesh, adding 40 wt.% silica solution for wetting, adding zeolite socony mobil-5 (ZSM-5) molecular sieve to the silicon dioxide solution, shaking vigorously until uniformly mixed, where the mass ratio of ZSM-5 molecular sieve and iron(II, III) oxide is 0.2-1 wt.% and the mass ratio of iron(II, III) oxide to silicon dioxide is 1:2-2:1, (b) adding the uniformly mixed solution in a vacuum drying box to dry to obtain a solid, adding the dried solid in a muffle furnace to calcine and remove silicon dioxide to obtain a iron(II, III) oxide doped ZSM-5 solid catalyst precursor and (c) placing 1 g iron(II, III) oxide doped ZSM-5 solid catalyst precursor in constant region of the fixed bed reactor, heating at 300 degrees C at a rate of 2 degrees C/minutes, performing reduction under reducing atmosphere for 10 hours at a flow rate of 50 ml/minute, switching the biomass synthesis gas as raw material into the fixed bed reactor having a reaction space velocity of 1500/hour, collecting downstream product of the reaction tube, which is liquid petroleum gas, where the composition of the biomass synthesis gas raw material comprises 60 wt.% hydrogen, 30 wt.% carbon monoxide, 5 wt.% nitrogen and 5 wt.% carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) comprises drying at 100 degrees C for 4 hours and calcining at 500 degrees C for 4 hours. Preferred Components: The reducing gas is hydrogen, carbon monoxide or a mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon monoxide in a ratio of 2:1.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC H06-A01; H06-A04; H06-P; H09-F03; J04-E01; J04-E04; J04-E06

PT P
PN CN110713969-A
TI Blue crab blood cell grouping culture method comprises mixing the blue crab blood with anticoagulant, performing density gradient centrifugation, inoculating cell suspension into different cell culture medium and culturing.
AU SHU M
   ZHOU Y
   LI B
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202009946M
AB    NOVELTY - Blue crab blood cell grouping culture method comprises: (i) preparing anticoagulant, cell separation fluid and cell culture fluid for blue crab blood; (ii) washing the crab with seawater and sterilizing the crab surface; (iii) collecting the blue crab blood and mixing with anticoagulant to obtain blood cell suspension, transferring the blood cell suspension into a centrifugal tube filled with cell separation fluid, and performing density gradient centrifugation to obtain layered solution of three cell subpopulations i.e. transparent cells, semi-granular cells and granulocytes from top to bottom, respectively; (iv) inoculating cell suspensions of three different cell subgroups into different cell culture medium and culturing primary cells at 28 degrees C without carbon dioxide, where the cell culture solution is prepared by adding 0.2 mM sodium chloride, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml streptomycin and 5-10 vol.% fetal bovine serum into L15 medium, and maintaining the pH to 7.4-7.6.
   USE - Used as blue crab blood cell grouping culture method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; achieves stable cell growth; and improves the separating effect of three different cell subsets.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Blue crab blood cell grouping culture method comprises: (i) preparing anticoagulant, cell separation fluid and cell culture fluid for blue crab blood, where the cell separation liquid is prepared by adding equal volumes of Percoll (RTM: Medium for density gradient centrifugation of cells, viruses, and subcellular particles) cell separation solution having mass fraction of 80 wt.%, 60 wt.% and 30 wt.% into the centrifuge tube, respectively; (ii) washing the crab with seawater and sterilizing the crab surface; (iii) collecting the blue crab blood and mixing with anticoagulant to obtain blood cell suspension, transferring the blood cell suspension into a centrifugal tube filled with cell separation fluid, and performing density gradient centrifugation to obtain layered solution of three cell subpopulations i.e. transparent cells, semi-granular cells and granulocytes from top to bottom, respectively, where the anticoagulant comprises 4.5-5 g/l citric acid, 13-13.5 g/l sodium citrate, 14.5-15 g/l glucose and 1-1.5 g/l sodium chloride; (iv) inoculating cell suspensions of three different cell subgroups into different cell culture medium and culturing primary cells at 28 degrees C without carbon dioxide; where the cell culture solution is prepared by adding 0.2 mM sodium chloride, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml streptomycin and 5-10 vol.% fetal bovine serum into L15 medium, and maintaining the pH to 7.4-7.6.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the seawater has a salinity of 25-35 ppt. after sterilization. In the step (iii), the volume ratio of blue crab blood and anticoagulant is 1:1. The blue crab is Scylla paramamosain. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the surface of the crab is sterilized using 70-80 vol.% ethanol solution. In the step (iii), the density gradient centrifugation is carried out at 1200-1500 g for 25-35 minutes. In the step (iv), changing half volume of cell culture medium for every two days, and observing the cell state of the cell culture solution under inverted phase contrast microscope for every 6 hours.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN110648421-A; CN110648421-B
TI Calculating thickness of decarburized layer on            decarburized spring steel surface comprises e. g.            determining equilibrium carbon concentration according            to the atmosphere in the furnace by ensuring the            content of hydrogen oxide.
AU CHEN Y
   WEI H
   HU L
   MA H
   MI Z
   CHEN H
   SU L
   SUN Z
   WU J
   SONG Y
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
   JIANGSU SHAGANG GROUP CO LTD (JSSG-C)
GA 2020045930
AB    NOVELTY - Calculating thickness of decarburized layer on                decarburized spring steel surface comprises e. g.                (i) determining the equilibrium carbon                concentration according to the atmosphere in the                furnace by ensuring the content of hydrogen oxide,                carbon dioxide, oxygen, and hydrogen in spring                steel in the decarburization atmosphere changes                with the decarburization reaction and setting the                equilibrium carbon concentration to 0-0.01; (ii)                determining the carbon content in the decarburized                layer and the carbon diffusion coefficient in                ferrite, where the calculation formula for the                carbon content C of the spring steel in the                decarburized layer is C is the carbon content in                the decarburized layer,%, C0 is the original                content of carbon in steel,%, t is the holding time                is s; and (iii) establishing measurement models for                decarburization of spring steel at different                holding temperatures and holding times to determine                the thickness of the surface decarburized                layer.
   USE - The method is useful for calculating thickness                of decarburized layer on decarburized spring steel                surface.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the purpose reducing the                decarburization layer thickness and improves the                surface quality of the spring steel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Calculating thickness of decarburized layer on                decarburized spring steel surface comprises (i)                determining the equilibrium carbon concentration                according to the atmosphere in the furnace by                ensuring the content of hydrogen oxide, carbon                dioxide, oxygen, and hydrogen in spring steel in                the decarburization atmosphere changes with the                decarburization reaction and setting the                equilibrium carbon concentration to 0-0.01; (ii)                determining the carbon content in the decarburized                layer and the carbon diffusion coefficient in                ferrite, where the calculation formula for the                carbon content C of the spring steel in the                decarburized layer is C is the carbon content in                the decarburized layer,%, C0 is the original                content of carbon in steel,%, t is the holding time                is s, D is the diffusion coefficient of carbon in                ferrite, m2/second, x is the thickness of the                decarburized layer, m, the formula for the                diffusion coefficient D of carbon in ferrite, where                D0 is the diffusion constant, m2/second, Q is the                diffusion activation energy, J/mol, R is the gas                constant, J/(mol/K), and T is the absolute                temperature K; and (iii) establishing measurement                models for decarburization of spring steel at                different holding temperatures and holding times to                determine the thickness of the surface decarburized                layer, where the decarburization model calculation                of spring steel at different heating temperatures,                different heating times, and different furnace                atmospheres and the formula is as specified and d                is the thickness of the decarburized layer in the                steel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  chemical reaction equation of decarburization is                  as specified, where ax + (C) = by + cz, a, b, and                  c are the factors of the chemical equation, x is                  the reaction gas, and y, z are the production                  gas, the equilibrium carbon concentration is                  related to the carbon activity on the surface of                  the spring steel, and the carbon activity                  calculation formula on the surface of the spring                  steel is as specified, where ac is the activity                  of carbon in austenitic steel, KP is the                  equilibrium constant, KP is constant when the                  temperature is constant, Px, Py, Pz respectively                  represent the partial pressure of each component                  of the furnace gas,%, Csat is the austenite                  saturated carbon concentration. When the                  temperature is constant, Csat is constant,%, Cs                  is the carbon content when the steel surface is                  in equilibrium with the furnace gas,% and                  determines the degree and speed of                  decarburization of the steel surface. In the step                  (iii), the formula for calculating the thickness                  of the decarburized layer is as specified under                  variable temperature condition, where Ci                  represents the carbon content in the decarburized                  layer at time i,%, Csi represents the carbon                  content when the steel surface is in equilibrium                  with the furnace gas at time i,%, Di represents                  the diffusion coefficient of carbon in ferrite at                  time i, cm2/s, ti represents the time step at                  time i, s, di represents the thickness increase                  of the decarburized layer under the conditions of                  time i and constant temperature Ti, mm, where i =                  1, 2, 3,... N, Ti represents the decarburization                  temperature at time i, K and C0 represents the                  original content of carbon in the steel.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); T01 (Digital Computers); T05 (Counting, Checking, Vending, ATM and POS Systems)

PN CN110563625-A; CN110563625-B
TI Separating and purifying zeaxanthin by mixing marigold oleoresin and ethyl acetate-ethanol solution, concentrating zeaxanthin-containing analytical solution, hydrolyzing, adding ethanol solution, filtering, grinding, filtering and drying.
AU LIU W
   LI Y
   DU M
   YIN D
   ZHAO C
AE SHANDONG TIANYIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A66642
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating and purifying zeaxanthin, involves (i) mixing and dissolving marigold oleoresin and ethyl acetate-ethanol solution, transferring to a analytical column filled with macroporous adsorption resin, using the ethyl acetate-ethanol solution for analysis and analyzing to obtain zeaxanthin-containing analytical solution, (ii) concentrating the zeaxanthin-containing analytical solution to obtain a zeaxanthin ester extract, (iii) hydrolyzing zeaxanthin ester extract with alcohol and alkali solution in a reaction kettle filled with carbon dioxide at 70-90 degrees C for 2-8 hours to obtain zeaxanthin saponification, (iv) adding zeaxanthin saponification to acid, adjusting pH value to 7-9, adding ethanol solution, leaving still for 1-5 hours, filtering to obtain crude zeaxanthin crystal, and (v) moistening crude zeaxanthin crystal with deionized water, grinding at 70-80 degrees C, dissolving, filtering and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and purifying zeaxanthin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separation and purification of zeaxanthin which is natural zeaxanthin and convenient for large-scale production, with more than 95% purity and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separating and purifying zeaxanthin, involves (i) mixing and dissolving marigold oleoresin and ethyl acetate-ethanol solution in a mass ratio of 1:(4-10), transferring to a analytical column filled with macroporous adsorption resin, using the ethyl acetate-ethanol solution for analysis and analyzing to obtain zeaxanthin-containing analytical solution, where the mass ratio of the marigold oleoresin and the ethyl acetate-ethanol analytical solution is 1:(2-10), the ethyl acetate-ethanol solution is a mixed solution of ethyl acetate and ethanol in a volume ratio of (5-10):1, the ethyl acetate-ethanol analytical solution is a mixed solution of ethyl acetate and ethanol in a volume ratio of (5-10):1, (ii) concentrating the zeaxanthin-containing analytical solution to obtain a zeaxanthin ester extract, where the residual volatile substance in the zeaxanthin extract is controlled at 5-10 %mass, (iii) hydrolyzing zeaxanthin ester extract with alcohol and alkali solution in a reaction kettle filled with carbon dioxide at 70-90 degrees C for 2-8 hours to obtain zeaxanthin saponification, (iv) adding zeaxanthin saponification to acid, adjusting pH value to 7-9, adding 30-50 %mass ethanol solution, where the mass ratio of ethanol solution and zeaxanthin ester extract in step (iii) is (1-10):1, leaving still for 1-5 hours, filtering to obtain crude zeaxanthin crystal, and (v) moistening crude zeaxanthin crystal with deionized water, grinding at 70-80 degrees C, dissolving, filtering and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (i), the mass ratio of marigold oleoresin and ethyl acetate-ethanol solution is 1:(7-9), the mass ratio of the marigold oleoresin to the ethyl acetate-ethanol analytical solution is 1:(6-8), the ethyl acetate-ethanol solution is a mixed solution of ethyl acetate and ethanol in a volume ratio of (6-7):1, and the ethyl acetate-ethanol analytical solution is a mixed solution of ethyl acetate and ethanol in a volume ratio of (6-7):1. In step (iii), the mass ratio of alcohol and zeaxanthin extract is (0.1-1):1, the alkali solution is 30-60 wt.% potassium hydroxide and/or sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, and the mass ratio of alkali solution and zeaxanthin extract is (0.3-1):1. In step (v), the mass ratio of crude zeaxanthin crystal and deionized water is 1:(2-5). Preferred Components: In step (i), the macroporous adsorption resin is AB-8 type macroporous adsorption resin. In step (iii), the alcohol is propylene glycol, glycerol or butanol. In step (iv), the acid is hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid, oxalic acid, acetic acid and/or citric acid.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)

PN CN110419676-A
TI Leisure noodle product comprises wheat flour, long-chain inulin, fingered citron flower oil plant extract, salt, monoglyceride, rapeseed oil, sesame oil, Potentilla fruticosa extract and water.
AU WANG X
   LENG Y
   ZHANG Y
   HU X
   FU Y
   WANG S
   LI L
   TANG M
AE UNIV WUHAN POLYTECHNIC (UWHP-C)
GA 201995631E
AB    NOVELTY - Leisure noodle product comprises wheat flour, long-chain inulin, fingered citron flower oil plant extract, salt, monoglyceride, rapeseed oil, sesame oil, Potentilla fruticosa extract and water, where the long-chain inulin is prepared by extracting Jerusalem artichoke with water, carrying out suction filtration, carrying out vacuum concentration, removing precipitates and evaporating the supernatant. The fingered citron flower oil plant extract is prepared by using fingered citron flower petals, carrying out ethanol solution extraction and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, mixing with a gamma -cyclodextrin saturated solution, carrying out suction filtration and drying to obtain the extract. The P.fruticosa extract is prepared by mixing and hydrolyzing P.fruticosa leaves and cellulase, carrying out suction filtration and carrying out vacuum concentration to obtain the extract.
   USE - Leisure noodle product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method slows down the hardening of the noodle product during long-term storage and enhances oil-fixing capacity. The product is natural and beneficial to human health.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Leisure noodle product comprises 1125-1325 pts. wt. wheat flour, 15-35 pts. wt. long-chain inulin, 14-18 pts. wt. fingered citron flower oil plant extract, 38-62 pts. wt. salt, 5-7 pts. wt. monoglyceride, 170-197.2 pts. wt. rapeseed oil, 30-34.8 pts. wt. sesame oil, 25-29 pts. wt. Potentilla fruticosa extract and 337.5-397.5 pts. wt. water, where the long-chain inulin is prepared by extracting Jerusalem artichoke with water, carrying out suction filtration, carrying out vacuum concentration, removing precipitates and evaporating the supernatant. The fingered citron flower oil plant extract is prepared by using fingered citron flower petals, carrying out ethanol solution extraction and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, mixing with a gamma -cyclodextrin saturated solution, carrying out suction filtration and drying to obtain the extract. The P.fruticosa extract is prepared by mixing and hydrolyzing P.fruticosa leaves and cellulase, carrying out suction filtration and carrying out vacuum concentration to obtain the extract. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the noodle product, involving (1) mixing fingered citron flower oil plant extract, long-chain inulin, wheat flour and brine to obtain a dough, carrying out extrusion and curing to obtain a semi-finished product and (2) mixing and stirring the semi-finished product with rapeseed oil, sesame oil and P.fruticosa extract to obtain the finished product, where the brine is a mixture of salt, monoglyceride and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The long-chain inulin is prepared by (a) extracting Jerusalem artichoke in a closed container with water and activated carbon at 70-90 degrees C and filtering and carrying out vacuum concentrating to obtain a crude inulin and (b) dissolving the crude inulin in water in a material to liquid ratio of 1 g:(6-10) ml, adding an excess of 25-35 ethanol, removing the precipitate and evaporating the supernatant to dryness. The fingered citron flower oil plant extract is prepared by (i) mixing and extracting the dried fingered citron flower petals, carrying out ethanol solution extraction using 55-65% ethanol at 60-70 degrees C and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 35-45 degrees C for 10-20 minutes at the pressure of 12-18 MPa to obtain a fingered citron flower oil and (ii) fully mixing the flower oil and excessive gamma -cyclodextrin saturated solution using a microencapsulation technology, cooling the mixture, carrying out suction filtration and drying to obtain the extract. The P.fruticosa extract is prepared by mixing P.fruticosa leaves with cellulase in a closed container in a weight ratio of 100:(0.08-0.12), hydrolyzing for 50-70 minutes, maintaining the temperature at 75-85 degrees C for 90-110 minutes, carrying out suction filtration and carrying out vacuum concentration to obtain the extract. Preferred Composition: The noodle product comprises 1175-1275 pts. wt. wheat flour, 20-30 pts. wt. long-chain inulin, 15-17 pts. wt. fingered citron flower oil plant extract, 44-56 pts. wt. salt, 5.5-6.5 pts. wt. monoglyceride, 176.8-190.4 pts. wt. rapeseed oil, 39.2-33.6 pts. wt. sesame oil, 26-28 pts. wt. P.fruticosa extract and 352.5-382.5 pts. wt. water. The material to liquid ratio of Jerusalem artichoke to water is 1 g:(8-12) ml. The volume ratio of Jerusalem artichoke to activated carbon is 1:(1-2). The ratio of the fingered citron flower petals to 55-65% ethanol is 1 g:(30-40) ml. Preferred Properties: The wheat flour has an attenuation value of 400-1100 RVU, a water solvent retention of 70-95% and a total protein content of 9.5-12%.

PN CN110320352-A
TI Detecting antibody titer based on antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity, by preparing target and effector cells, inoculating target cells into plate, adding e.g. antibody, incubating, and analyzing percentage of dead cells.
AU XIANG Y
   WANG J
AE SHANGHAI XUANFENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201988038L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for detecting antibody titer based on antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity, involves (a) preparing target cells by performing experimenting in the BSL-3 experimental facility, infecting raji cells with A/California/04/2009 (H1N1) influenza virus for 48 hours before starting the experiment, collecting target cells after 48 hours after infection, evaluating the ability of the target cells, counting target cells and washing, (b) preparing effector cells by collecting whole blood from the volunteer body, and obtaining peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMC), and (c) inoculating the target cells into the culture plate, adding 1640 medium, labeled target cells, and antibody, incubating, suspending unlabeled normal PBMC effector cells, and adding cell suspension, incubating, adding fluorescent dye 7-amino-actinomycin-D, incubating, obtaining cells, and performing analysis of the percentage of dead cells in the target cells reflects the titer of the antibody.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting antibody titer based on antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method detects the titer of different kinds of antibodies by modifying the target cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for detecting antibody titer based on antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity, involves (a) preparing target cells by performing experimenting in the BSL-3 experimental facility, infecting raji cells with A/California/04/2009 (H1N1) influenza virus for 48 hours before starting the experiment, where the multiplicity of infection (MOI) is 2, and the cell confluence is 80-95%, collecting target cells after 48 hours after infection, evaluating the ability of the target cells to infect cells to produce blood agglutination and bind to polyclonal antibodies, counting 2x 106 target cells and washing with phosphate buffer saline (PBS), after the ability assessment is passed, (b) preparing effector cells by collecting whole blood from the volunteer body, and then obtaining peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMC) by density gradient centrifugation, and (c) inoculating the target cells into the culture plate, adding 50ul 1640 medium per well, 5x C104 labeled target cells per well, and 50 ul antibody to each well, incubating for 15 minutes in a 37 degrees C carbon dioxide incubator, suspending 2.5x 107 cells/ml unlabeled normal PBMC effector cells, and adding 100 ul cell suspension to each well, incubating for 2 hours in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, adding 1 ul fluorescent dye 7-amino-actinomycin-D, incubating at 4 degrees Cf0r 20 minutes under dark condition, obtaining 5000 cells using a flow cytometry analysis, and performing analysis of the percentage of dead cells in the target cells reflects the titer of the antibody.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves using PBS having a pH of 7.2-7.4, which provides a stable ionic environment and pH buffering capacity, and is isotonic with human blood, where the PBS is used in biology for molecular cloning and cell culture, and comprises potassium dihydrogen phosphate, disodium phosphate, sodium chloride and potassium chloride. The MOI is the multiplicity of infection, i.e. the ratio of virus to cell number at the time of infection. Preferred Components: The culture plate in the third step is a 96-well round bottom culture plate.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN110123858-A
TI Use of Saussurea costus essential oil in preparation of medicines and health care product for treating or preventing brain functions and its disorder such as anxiety, depression, insomnia epilepsy, mania, schizophrenia.
AU XU M
   CHEN S
   HAN J
   LI H
   YANG C
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 201972845T
AB    NOVELTY - Use of Saussurea costus essential oil in preparation of medicines and health care product for treating or preventing brain functions and its disorder, is claimed.
   USE - The S.costus essential oil is useful in preparing medicines and health care product for treating or preventing brain functions and its disorder such as anxiety, depression, insomnia, epilepsy, mania, schizophrenia, spasm and biphase disorders, middle wind, hungton's disease, cerebral trauma, spinal injury and neurodegenerative diseases. The neurodegenerative diseases comprises Alzheimers disease, Parkinsons disease, Huntingtons disease, and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
   ADVANTAGE - The S.costus essential oil inhibits cholinesterase, adjusts mechanism of multi-molecule, exhibits synergistic effect, has excellent pharmacokinetic parametersm, is easy to penetrate through a blood brain barrier, has toxicology safety, metabolism stability, long half-life period and/or relatively small side effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing the S.costus essential oil, comprising (i) selecting dried root, stem, leaf or thin fruit, piling for 1-2 days, crushing into coarse particles having a size of 30-60 mesh, (ii) soaking the coarse particles in 7-9 times hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid aqueous solution having a pH of 5-6, and leaching overnight to obtain leaching mixture, (iii) adding cellulase and pectinase to the leaching mixture, where the mass ratio of cellulase, pectinase and leaching mixture is (0.5-1):(0.1-0.5):100, and performing enzyme hydrolysis at 35-37 degrees C for 2-3 hours, (iv) placing the mixed solution obtained in step (iii) in a steam distillation extraction apparatus, refluxing at 100-120 degrees C for 5-7 hours, collecting distillate, cooling to room temperature, placing in oil-water separator, separating, drying oil layer using anhydrous sodium sulfate, separating to obtain S.costus essential oil or placing the mixed solution obtained in step (iii) in carbon dioxide supercritical extraction apparatus, extracting at 45-48 degrees C and at pressure of 13.70-17.80 MPa for 50-100 minutes with flow rate of carbon dioxide is 25-35 kg/hour, collecting essential oil, refining in a molecular distillation apparatus, where the feeding temperature is 40-45 degrees C and at a speed of 480-530 ml/hour, evaporating at 110-115 degrees C, performing two-stage evaporation at 60-65 degrees C, and collecting the two-stage distillation portion to obtain the S.costus essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The S.costus root is Saussurea or Inula japonica flower, elecampane, Tibetan wood, Racemosa elecampane, or Chrysanthemum flower, leaves thick woody, vegetables, limbus woody and muli woody. The health care product comprises cosmetics, bathing agent and aromatherapy. The medicine comprises oral preparation and non-oral administration preparation. Preferred Composition: The S.costus essential oil is analyzed using gas chromatography mass spectrometer. The components comprises more than 20% S.costus root, more than 8% S.costus alcohol, more than 6% dehydrowood lactone, more than 5% alpha -osthole and more than 4% caryophyllene.
   ACTIVITY - Tranquilizer; Antidepressant; Hypnotic; Anticonvulsant; Antimanic; Neuroleptic; Muscular-Gen.; Vulnerary; Neuroprotective; Nootropic; Antiparkinsonian.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the oral preparation is in the form of tablets, pills, capsules, granules, suspension, dropping pill and oral liquid and non-oral administration preparation is in the form of inhalant, injection, aerosol, suppository and subcutaneous (claimed).

PN CN109988742-A
TI Culturing autologous fibroblast comprises e.g.            sequentially performing skin sample processing step            comprises providing skin from back of ear or eyelid and            obtaining fibroblasts by enzymatic digestion comprises            removing subcutaneous tissue.
AU LIU J
   HE X
   AI X
AE XIAN LUOWEITA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201962621K
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing autologous fibroblast comprises e.g.                sequentially performing skin sample processing                step, enzymatic digestion to obtain a fibroblast                step, and a fibroblast culture step, where the skin                specimen processing steps comprises providing the                skin from the back of ear or eyelid under sterile                conditions, the step of obtaining fibroblasts by                enzymatic digestion method comprises removing the                subcutaneous tissue and blood vessels of the                selected skin specimens in a sterile environment of                a clean bench, adding trypsin and EDTA, digesting                the skin specimen, adding type I collagenase                digestion to obtain fibroblasts, the fibroblast                culture step comprises adding cell growth factor                keratinocyte growth factor, platelet derived growth                factor and platelet-rich plasma to Dulbecco's                modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 medium                and culturing fibroblasts in carbon dioxide                incubator, and the platelet-rich plasma is a                platelet concentrate obtained by centrifugation of                autologous whole blood.
   USE - The culturing autologous fibroblast is useful                for removing wrinkles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high safety, does not undergo                rejection reaction, is easy to survive after                reinfusion, and can rapidly secrete collagen and                has no phenomenon of excessive collagen hyperplasia                due to the regulation of the body's metabolic                function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culturing autologous fibroblast comprises                sequentially performing a skin sample processing                step, enzymatic digestion to obtain a fibroblast                step, and a fibroblast culture step, where the skin                specimen processing steps comprises providing the                skin from the back of ear or eyelid under sterile                conditions, the step of obtaining fibroblasts by                enzymatic digestion method comprises removing the                subcutaneous tissue and blood vessels of the                selected skin specimens in a sterile environment of                a clean bench, adding 0.25-0.5% trypsin and                0.2-0.4% EDTA, digesting the skin specimen, adding                1-5 mg/ml type I collagenase digestion to obtain                fibroblasts, the fibroblast culture step comprises                adding cell growth factor keratinocyte growth                factor, platelet derived growth factor and                platelet-rich plasma to Dulbecco's modified eagle                medium/nutrient mixture F-12 medium, culturing                fibroblasts in a concentration of 1-5% carbon                dioxide incubator at 35-37 degrees C, where the                concentration of the cell growth factor is: the                keratinocyte growth factor activity unit is 50-200                U/ml, the platelet derived growth factor activity                unit is 100-500 U/ml, and the platelet-rich plasma                is a platelet concentrate obtained by                centrifugation of autologous whole blood, adding to                Dulbecco's modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture                F-12 medium, and the final plasma concentration is                5-20%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step of obtaining the                  fibroblast by the enzymatic digestion method                  comprises providing the skin from ear or eyelid                  under the aseptic condition, removing the                  subcutaneous tissue and blood vessels of the skin                  on GMP laboratory's 100-stage ultra-clean work                  table, adding 0.5% trypsin and 0.2% EDTA in                  volume ratio of 10:1, digesting at 37 degrees C                  for 2 hours, adding 1 mg/ml of type I collagenase                  for 1 hour at 37 degrees C, and digesting the                  above digested solution through 200 mesh and                  filtered by blowing to obtain fibroblasts. The                  fibroblast culture step comprises adding cell                  growth factor keratinocyte growth factor,                  platelet derived growth factor and platelet-rich                  plasma to Dulbecco's modified eagle                  medium/nutrient mixture F-12 medium, culturing                  fibroblasts in a concentration of 5% carbon                  dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 48 hours,                  where the concentration of the cell growth factor                  is: the keratinocyte growth factor activity unit                  is 50 U/ml, the platelet derived growth factor                  activity unit is 100 U/ml and the final plasma                  concentration is 10%.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

PN CN109988781-A
TI Co-digesting and recovering nitrogen, phosphorus            and purifying biogas by high carbonaceous substrate and            microalgae comprises mixing high carbonaceous substrate            with Chlorella sp., thermosonic treatment, and biogas            bio-desulfurization.
AU ZHOU J
   YOU X
   GONG L
   ZHOU Y
   WANG J
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201962329J
AB    NOVELTY - Co-digesting and recovering nitrogen,                phosphorus and purifying biogas by high                carbonaceous substrate and microalgae comprises                mixing high carbonaceous substrate with Chlorella                sp. for performing thermosonic treatment, then                adding nano zero-valent iron (NZVI), performing                solid-liquid separation to the fermentation liquid                after the anaerobic digestion to obtain biogas                residue and concentrated biogas slurry, adding                Chlorella sp., then removing the crude biogas from                the carbon dioxide by the microalgae photoreactor                to obtain fine biogas, then returning to the next                round of anaerobic digestion process after                desulfurization of the fine biogas                bio-desulfurization, subjecting the concentrated                algae liquid to solid-liquid separation to obtain                algae residue and algae solution, inoculating                Enterobacter sp. EMB19 in the bacterial                mineralization stage, adding magnesium oxide for                recovering nitrogen and phosphorus from the algae                liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for co-digesting and                recovering nitrogen, phosphorus and purifying                biogas by high carbonaceous substrate and                microalgae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can realize the resource recovery                of the high carbonaceous substrate and the                microalgae; and can improve the anaerobic digestion                performance and the methane conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-digesting and recovering nitrogen,                phosphorus and purifying biogas by high                carbonaceous substrate and microalgae comprises (a)                mixing high carbonaceous substrate with Chlorella                sp. for performing thermosonic treatment, then                adding nano zero-valent iron (NZVI) for                pretreatment after anaerobic digestion, where the                ratio of solid to liquid in anaerobic digestion is                10-15% to obtain coarse biogas during anaerobic                digestion, performing solid-liquid separation to                the fermentation liquid after the anaerobic                digestion to obtain biogas residue and concentrated                biogas slurry, (b) respectively passing the crude                biogas and concentrated biogas slurry to the                microalgae photoreactor, then adding Chlorella sp.                at the initial stage of the reaction, then removing                the crude biogas from the carbon dioxide by the                microalgae photoreactor to obtain fine biogas, and                passing the concentrated biogas slurry to the                microalgae photoreactor for promoting the growth of                Chlorella sp. to obtain concentrated algae liquid,                (c) then returning to the next round of anaerobic                digestion process after desulfurization of the fine                biogas bio-desulfurization, subjecting the                concentrated algae liquid to solid-liquid                separation to obtain algae residue and algae                solution, then returning the algae residue to the                next round of anaerobic digestion process, and then                passing the algae liquid to the bacterial                mineralization stage, and (d) inoculating                Enterobacter sp. EMB19 in the bacterial                mineralization stage, then adding magnesium oxide                for recovering nitrogen and phosphorus from the                algae liquid to obtain struvite, and continuing the                liquid phase for returning to the next round of                anaerobic digestion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The concentration of                  the nano zero-valent iron (NZVI) is 500-1000                  mg/l. The high carbonaceous substrate is sludge,                  kitchen waste, straw, animal waste and/or wood                  waste. Preferred Method: The thermosonic                  treatment is carried out at 120-150 degrees C for                  1-2 hours under an ultrasonic treatment frequency                  of 20-30 kHz. The anaerobic digestion is carried                  out at 35 degrees C for 20-30 days. The                  pretreated material is adjusted to pH of 7.2-7.6                  before passing to the anaerobic digestion tank.                  The microalgae photoreactor has a temperature of                  20-25 degrees C, adding water to concentrated                  biogas slurry of the microalgae photoreactor for                  diluting the concentration of the concentrated                  biogas slurry, and allowing to stand for 5-10                  days. The amount of magnesium oxide and the algae                  solution to be added is 0.5-1 g/l, then allowing                  to stand at 30 degrees C for 3-5 days. The                  struvite after precipitation is recovered and                  used as an agricultural fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

TI New grafted glycidyl citrate compound used as e.g.            plasticizer, chain extender, and crystallization            nucleating agent, for preparing polylactic acid forming            agent, is preferably ethylene bis-12-hydroxystearic            acid-grafted glycidyl citrate.
AU WANG X
   LI P
   CHEN H
   LU D
AE NINGBO HOMELINK ECO-ITECH CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
   NINGBO HOMELINK TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
   NINGBO HOMELINK ECO-ITECH CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
   NINGBO HOMELINK ECO-ITECH CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
   NINGBO HOMELINK ECO-ITECH CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201956750K
AB    NOVELTY - A grafted glycidyl citrate compound (I), is                new. The compound (I) is preferably ethylene                bis-12-hydroxystearic acid-grafted glycidyl citrate                (II).
   USE - New grafted glycidyl citrate compound used as                internal lubricant, mold release agent,                plasticizer, interfacial compatibilizer, chain                extender, and crystallization nucleating agent,                preferably chain extender and crystallization                nucleating agent, for preparing polylactic acid                foaming material (all claimed) used as replacement                of petroleum-based plastic disposable foaming                products e.g. polypropylene and polystyrene.
   ADVANTAGE - The new compound provides biodegradable                foaming product with excellent heat resistance,                uniform appearance, and relatively low density. The                foaming material can be prepared by easy method, on                industrially large-scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A grafted glycidyl citrate compound of formula                (I), is new. The compound (I) is preferably                ethylene bis-12-hydroxystearic acid-grafted                glycidyl citrate of formula (II).n = 1-9.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) grafted olefinic citrate compound of                formula (III), preferably ethylene                bis-12-hydroxystearamide-grafted olefinic citrate                of formula (IV);
   (2) N,N'-ethylene bis-12-hydroxystearamide                grafted citric acid of formula (V);
   (3) preparation of the compound (I), which                involves subjecting citric acid of formula (VI),                oxalyl chloride of formula (VII), and ethylene                bis-12-hydroxystearamide of formula (VIII) to                elimination reaction, obtaining compound (V),                subjecting the compound (V) and haloalkene of                formula (IX) to elimination reaction to obtain                compound (III), and subjecting the compound (III)                to an oxidation reaction;
   (4) polylactic acid foaming material, which                comprises 90-95 wt.% polylactic acid, 1-5 wt.%                polybutyrate adipate terephthalate (PBAT) resin,                0.1-3 wt.% cell nucleating agent, 0.1-3 wt.%                foaming agent, and 0.1-2.0 wt.% compound (II);                and
   (5) preparation of the polylactic acid foaming                material, which involves drying polylactic acid at                -100 degrees C to 110 degrees C for 20-40 minutes                using a high-speed mixer, adding other components                and mixing, adding the mixed material to a                twin-screw extruder, melt-blending at 180-200                degrees C, drawing, air-cooling, pelletizing to                obtain modified heat-resistant polylactic acid                material particles, adding the obtained particles                to a twin-screw material forming machine,                melt-blending and extruding using physical foaming                agent, with 10-20 times foaming rate. The screw                length-to-diameter ratio of twin-screw extruder is                36:1-48:1. The physical foaming agent is carbon                dioxide, nitrogen, pentane, butane, and/or Freon,                preferably carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen in a                volume ratio of 20%:80%.
   X = chloro or bromo.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The inert gas is                  nitrogen. The cell nucleating agent is micron                  talc, nano mica, and/or nano organic                  montmorillonite, preferably, nano organic                  montmorillonite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst (A) is                  potassium carbonate and/or sodium carbonate. The                  solvent (A) is chloroform, toluene, and/or                  tetrahydrofuran, preferably chloroform. The                  solvent (B) is dimethyl sulfoxide,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, toluene, and/or                  N,N-dimethylacetamide, preferably                  N,N-dimethylformamide. The compound (IX) is                  3-bromo-1 propylene, 4-bromo-1-butene,                  5-bromo-1-pentene, 6-bromo-1-hexene,                  7-bromo-1-heptene, 8-bromo-1-octene,                  9-bromo-1-nonene, 3-chloro-1-propene,                  4-chloro-1-butene, 5-chloro-1-pentene,                  6-chloro-1-hexene, 7-chloro-1-heptene,                  8-chloro-1-octene, or 9-chloro-1-nonene,                  preferably 3-bromo-1-propene or                  3-chloro-1-propene, more preferably                  3-bromo-1-propene. The catalyst (B) is                  m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid, peroxybenzoic acid,                  and/or p-nitroperoxybenzoic acid, preferably                  m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid. The solvent (C) is                  dichloromethane, chloroform, acetone, methyl                  ethyl ketone, and/or toluene, preferably                  chloroform. Preferred Composition: The molar                  ratio of compound (VI), compound (VII), compound                  (VIII), and catalyst (A) is                  2.2-2.5:2.2-2.5:1.0:3.0-5.5. The weight ratio of                  compound (VIII) and solvent (A) is 1:8-10. The                  molar ratio of compound (V), compound (IX), and                  catalyst (A) is 1.0:7.3-9.6:2-6. The molar ratio                  of compound (V) and solvent (B) is 1:10-15. The                  molar ratio of compound (IV) and catalyst (B) is                  1.0:6.6-8.5. The molar ratio of compound (IV) and                  solvent (C) is 1:8-13.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The compound (IA) is                  prepared by mixing compound (VI), compound (VII),                  compound (VIII), catalyst (A), and solvent (A),                  heating and reacting at 20-60 degrees C for 30-60                  hours under stirring conditions and inert gas                  protection, distilling under reduced pressure to                  obtain compound (V), mixing compound (V),                  compound (IX), catalyst (A), and solvent (B),                  heating and reacting at 40-60 degrees C for 25-50                  hours under stirring conditions and inert gas                  protection, washing, distilling under reduced                  pressure to obtain compound (IV), mixing compound                  (IV), catalyst (B), and solvent (C), heating and                  reacting at 40-60 degrees C for 20-50 hours under                  stirring conditions and inert gas protection,                  washing, and distilling under reduced pressure.                  Preferred Components: The polylactic acid is                  L-polylactic acid, D-polylactic acid, and/or LD                  mixed polylactic acid. The PBAT resin is a                  copolymer of butylene adipate and butylene                  terephthalate. The foaming agent is citric acid                  fatty acid glyceride, polyoxyethylene sorbitan                  fatty acid ester, sorbitan fatty acid, and/or                  castor oil polyoxyethylene ether, preferably                  citric acid fatty acid glyceride. Preferred                  Properties: The polylactic acid has a weight                  average molecular weight of 100000-300000 and a                  molecular weight distribution (Mw/Mn) of 1.3-1.8.                  The PBAT resin has a weight average molecular                  weight of 50000-80000 and a molecular weight                  distribution (Mw/Mn) of 1.2-1.6.

PT P
PN CN109626699-A
TI Treating wastewater comprises e.g. providing an acidification tank, adding a hydrolyzed autonomous bacteria, carrying out methanation treatment, introducing the waste liquid and carrying out subsequent discharge treatment.
AU 
AE FOSHAN JIEHAO ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201939128M
AB    NOVELTY - Treating wastewater comprises e.g. (i) providing an acidification tank and adding a hydrolyzed autonomous bacteria for producing a hydrolysis acidification reaction; (b) providing a fermentation tank and adding a methylated methane-producing bacteria for producing a decomposition reaction of acetic acid; (c) introducing the waste liquid after the oxidation reaction into the acidification tank and staying for a predetermined time to carry out hydrolysis and acidification treatment on the waste liquid; (d) introducing the waste liquid which has been subjected to hydrolysis and acidification treatment through the acidification tank and staying for a predetermined time to carry out methanation treatment; and (e) introducing the waste liquid which has been methanated in the fermentation tank, staying for a predetermined time to precipitate the sludge in the waste liquid and separating the treated waste liquid and the sludge and carrying out subsequent discharge treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for treating wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: degrades macromolecular organic substances e.g carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, and cellulose, which are secreted by hydrolyzed bacteria or fermenting bacteria to decompose extracellular enzymes into small molecular substances, so that organic substances are metabolized by bacteria; converts dissolved organic matter into an organic acid, ethanol, hydrogen and carbon dioxide by the acidified bacteria and organic acid and the alcohol into acetic acid and hydrogen; and decomposes acetic acid to form methane and carbon dioxide to degrade the chemical oxygen demand.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating wastewater comprises (i) providing an acidification tank and adding a hydrolyzed autonomous bacteria for producing a hydrolysis acidification reaction in the acidification tank; (b) providing a fermentation tank and adding a methylated methane-producing bacteria for producing a decomposition reaction of acetic acid into the fermentation tank; (c) introducing the waste liquid after the oxidation reaction by the branching method into the acidification tank and staying for a predetermined time to carry out hydrolysis and acidification treatment on the waste liquid; (d) introducing the waste liquid which has been subjected to hydrolysis and acidification treatment through the acidification tank into the fermentation tank and staying for a predetermined time to carry out methanation treatment on the waste liquid; and (e) introducing the waste liquid which has been methanated in the fermentation tank into a sedimentation tank, staying for a predetermined time to precipitate the sludge in the waste liquid respectively and separating the treated waste liquid and the sludge separately and carrying out subsequent discharge treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The operating temperature of the acidification tank is controlled at a normal temperature. The pH of the acidification tank is kept at 4-5 and carrying out hydraulic retention for 5 days. The operating temperature of the fermentation tank is controlled at 55 degrees C. The pH of the acidification tank is maintained at 7-8 and carrying out hydraulic retention time for 5 days.

PN CN109503375-A
TI Preparing homogeneously coupled heterogeneously catalyzed ethyl methyl carbonate comprises e.g. carrying out process in a pre-reactor, and reacting raw materials under homogeneous catalyst to obtain first mixture comprising e.g. methanol.
AU SHI L
   FAN J
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 201928658X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing homogeneously coupled heterogeneously catalyzed ethyl methyl carbonate comprises e.g. carrying out process in a pre-reactor, and reacting raw materials under a homogeneous catalyst to obtain a first mixture comprising methanol, ethanol, dimethyl carbonate, ethyl methyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate, first mixture enters the first reaction, distillation column is rectifying and flashing to obtain second mixture comprising dimethyl carbonate, ethyl methyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate, a very small amount of methanol and ethanol, filtering second mixture, allowing to stand, and introducing carbon dioxide gas, allowing to stand, and filtering to obtain a third mixture, including dimethyl carbonate, ethyl methyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate, heating third mixture by second rectification column, and top of the column is first producing with dimethyl carbonate, and then a qualified product of ethyl methyl carbonate, dimethyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate is obtained.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing homogeneously coupled heterogeneously catalyzed ethyl methyl carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces energy consumption, and improves an identical device to carbonate is yield of target product is not greater than 2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing homogeneously coupled heterogeneously catalyzed ethyl methyl carbonate comprises carrying out process in a pre-reactor, and reacting raw materials under a homogeneous catalyst to obtain a first mixture comprising methanol, ethanol, dimethyl carbonate, ethyl methyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate, the first mixture enters the first reaction the distillation column is rectifying and flashing to obtain a second mixture comprising dimethyl carbonate, ethyl methyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate, a very small amount of methanol and a very small amount of ethanol, filtering the second mixture, allowing to stand, and introducing carbon dioxide gas, allowing to stand, and filtering to obtain a third mixture, including dimethyl carbonate, ethyl methyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate, the third mixture is slowly heating by the second rectification column, and the top of the column is first producing with dimethyl carbonate, and then a qualified product of ethyl methyl carbonate, dimethyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate is obtained by adding additional dimethyl carbonate to obtain a fourth mixture, and the fourth mixture is dimethyl carbonate and carbonic acid in a molar ratio of about 1:1, diethyl ester, re-reacting fourth mixture through a fixed bed reactor equipping with a heterogeneous catalyst to obtain a second mixture back to the second rectification column, including dimethyl carbonate, ethyl methyl carbonate and diethyl carbonate, this is now recycling byproduct diethyl carbonate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic representation of the preparing homogeneously coupled heterogeneously catalyzed ethyl methyl carbonate. Drawing includes non-English language text.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the reaction temperature of pre-reactor is normal temperature, stirring speed is 500-2000 revolutions/minutes (rpm), and the absolute pressure in the reaction vessel is 0.01-5 MPa. The raw material is a mixture of dimethyl carbonate and ethanol, and the molar ratio of two is 0.5:1-2:1, and the liquid feed is pumping by a metering pump. The homogeneous catalyst is sodium methoxide, potassium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate and/or lithium carbonate, the amount of catalyst is from 0.1-5% of the total mass of the mixed raw materials. The filtration operation uses a 500-1000 mesh screen. The heterogeneous catalyst is a metal oxide, a supported metal oxide or a mixture of plurality of metal oxides. The fixed bed reaction temperature is 75-120 degrees C, the mass velocity of the mixed raw materials is 0.2-1 to 20-1 hours, and loading amount of the heterogeneous catalyst is 500-20000 kg. The absolute pressure in the first reactive distillation column is 0.01-1 MPa, the temperature at the top of the column is 30-80 degrees C, the temperature at the bottom of the column is 80-150 degrees C, the absolute pressure in the second distillation column, the temperature is from 0.01-1 MPa, the temperature at the top of the column is from 90-125 degrees C, and the temperature at the bottom of the column is from 100-140 degrees C. The reflux ratio of the first reactive distillation column and the second rectification column is 1-20.

PN CN109457287-A; CN109457287-B
TI Preparation of sulfur-nitrogen co-doped black titanium dioxide nanotube array used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves adding ammonia fluoride, ethylene glycol and deionized water to container, annealing and drying.
AU XUE J
   GAO J
   SHEN Q
   WU Y
   JIA S
   JIA H
   XU B
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201925355A
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of sulfur-nitrogen co-doped black titanium dioxide nanotube array involves (S1) adding ammonia fluoride, ethylene glycol and deionized water to container, placing the container in ultrasonic disperser and then ultrasonically dispersing to obtain electrolyte, (S2) placing in electrolytic cell, mounting anode with titanium piece using conductive hanging wire, mounting cathode with platinum piece using conductive hanging wire, adding electrolyte to electrolytic cell, electrolytically reacting, forming titanium dioxide nanotube array on the anode titanium sheet, taking titanium piece and then washing using deionized water, (S3) drying, placing in high-temperature resistant container and then drying using oven and (S4) annealing under vacuum, washing vacuum heat treatment furnace, spreading ammonium sulfate on the bottom of the high-temperature resistant container, introducing inert gas into the heat treatment furnace, cooling to 20-30 degrees C and washing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of sulfur-nitrogen co-doped black titanium dioxide nanotube array (claimed), which is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of sulfur-nitrogen co-doped black titanium dioxide nanotube array with high efficiency and accuracy and excellent chemical and physical property, structural characteristics, visible light response and absorption effect on UV light and visible light.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of sulfur-nitrogen co-doped black titanium dioxide nanotube array involves (S1) adding ammonia fluoride, ethylene glycol and deionized water to container, placing the container in ultrasonic disperser and then ultrasonically dispersing to obtain electrolyte, (S2) placing in electrolytic cell, mounting anode with titanium piece using conductive hanging wire, mounting cathode with platinum piece using conductive hanging wire, adding electrolyte to electrolytic cell, turning on the direct current power supply, electrolytically reacting, forming titanium dioxide nanotube array on the anode titanium sheet, turning off the direct current power supply, taking titanium piece and then washing using deionized water to obtain titanium sheet containing titanium dioxide nanotube array, (S3) drying, placing in high-temperature resistant container and then drying using oven to obtain dried titanium sheet containing titanium dioxide nanotube array and (S4) annealing under vacuum, washing vacuum heat treatment furnace, spreading ammonium sulfate on the bottom of the high-temperature resistant container, placing titanium sheet containing titanium dioxide nanotube array in ammonium sulfate, placing high-temperature resistant container in vacuum heat treatment furnace, sealing, extracting, introducing inert gas into the heat treatment furnace, adjusting constant pressure, turning on the heater, performing heat treatment annealing, finishing heat treatment, turning off the heater, stopping inert gas, cooling to 20-30 degrees C and washing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The bottom of the electrolytic cell is provided with magnetic agitator for stirring electrolyte.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (S2) involves washing titanium sheet and platinum sheet, placing in container, immersing in acetone solution, anhydrous ethanol solution and deionized water solution, washing and drying. The pressure in the heat treatment furnace is constant at 0.101 MPa. The heat treatment annealing step is carried out at 400-500 degrees C, preferably 450 degrees C for 200-300 minutes, preferably 240 minutes. The drying step is carried out at 50-70 degrees C for 10-30 minutes under vacuum degree of 4-8 Pa. The electrolytically reacting step is carried out at 20-30 degrees C, preferably 25 degrees C for 50-70 minutes, preferably 60 minutes under voltage of 50 V. Preferred Components: Ethylene glycol and deionized water are used in a volume ratio of (95-99):(1-5). Ammonium fluoride has concentration of 0.5-1 mol/l. Ammonium sulfate and titanium sheet containing titanium dioxide nanotube array are used in a mass ratio of (1.2-3):1, preferably 2:1. The inert gas is argon.
