TI Preparing homopolymers or block copolymers of            vinyl aromatic monomers by anionic polymerization            involves polymerising one vinyl aromatic monomer and            one conjugated diene in inert non-polar solvent in            presence of organometal initiator.
AU VERSWYVEL M
   NIESSNER N
   MOORS R
   KNOLL K
   VAN-DEN-BOSSCHE B
   CAMERLINCK C
AE INEOS STYROLUTION GROUP GMBH (INEO-C)
   INEOS STYROLUTION GROUP GMBH (INEO-C)
   INEOS STYROLUTION GROUP GMBH (INEO-C)
   INEOS STYROLUTION GROUP GMBH (INEO-C)
GA 2019230042
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing homopolymers or block copolymers of                vinyl aromatic monomers by anionic polymerization                involves polymerising one vinyl aromatic monomer                and optionally one conjugated diene in an inert                non-polar solvent in the presence of an organometal                initiator in particular organolithium initiator in                a reactor, preferably a batch reactor. The                subsequent deactivation of the obtained living                polymer chains with a terminating agent to obtain a                polymer solution. The polymer solution is passed                through a first filter. The polymer solution is fed                to a dispersing device to which water is added in a                continuous or in a discontinuous mode. The obtained                polymer solution is fed to a buffer vessel. The                continuously withdrawn polymer solution is fed from                the buffer vessel into a static mixer for                impregnation by addition of further water, carbon                dioxide and one or more stabilizers, no water is                present in the first filter.
   USE - Method for preparing homopolymers or block                copolymers of vinyl aromatic monomers by anionic                polymerization having shaped articles used as                plastic household articles (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The first filter, preferably a bagfilter, has                a mesh size of 200-1500 micrometer, the flow rate                of the polymer solution is 10-500 m3/hour at a                temperature of from 50-130 degrees C. The                dispersion device is a second filter, preferably a                bagfilter, having a mesh size of 200-1500                micrometer, a static mixer or a process tube in                which the characteristic length of the process                tube, the velocity, the density and the dynamic                viscosity of the polymer solution are cho- sen in                such a way that a transitional or turbulent flow                with a Reynolds number above 2300 occurs. The water                is added in amounts of 0.01-0.50 l/m3 polymer                solution, polymer solution. The flow rate of the                additional water is more than 0.05 l/m3 polymer                solution, preferably more than 0.10 l/m3 polymer                solution, and the flow rate of the carbon dioxide                is more than 5 l/m3 polymer solution, preferred                10-20 l/m3 polymer solution, more preferred                14.5-17.5 l/m3 polymer solution. The pH of the                water is in the range of from 5 -7, preferably                6.2-6.6. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a homo- or blockcopolymers of vinyl                aromatic monomers obtained by the process;
   (2) a polymer blend, which comprises one homo-                or block copolymer; and
   (3) a shaped articles, which comprises homo-                or block copolymers or a polymer blend.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reactor is a batch                  reactor. The dispersing device is a filter. The                  dispersing device is a process flow portion,                  specifically a tube or pipe, in which the polymer                  solution has a tran- sitional flow with a                  Reynolds number between 2300-4000. The dispersing                  device is a process tube having a characteristic                  length of from 0.05-1.0 m, preferably 0.05-5 m                  and the polymer solution has a velocity of from                  1.0-10.0 m/s, preferably 1.0- 10.0 m/s density of                  from 750-900 kg/m3, preferably 750-900 kg/m3, and                  a dynamic viscosity of from 0.01 -10 Ns/m2,                  preferably 0.01 -10 Ns/m2 at a temperature of                  from 60-80 degrees C, preferably 60-95 degrees C.                  The filter is a bagfilter. The mesh size of the                  filter is 500-1000 micrometer in particular 700-                  900 micrometer. The water is added in amounts of                  0.05- 0.20 l/m3 polymer solution, more preferably                  0.05 to 0.15 l/m3 polymer solution. The polymer                  solution is fed to a further buffer vessel. The                  polymer solution continuously withdrawn from the                  buffer vessel is filtered by a third filter. The                  third filter is a cartridge filter with a mesh                  size between 50-300 micrometer, preferably                  between 100-150 micrometer. The stabilizers are                  added as a solution with a flow rate of 1-8 l/m3                  polymer solution, in particular 3.5-4.5 l/m3                  polymer solution. The stabilizers are dissolved                  in a nonpolar solvent where the concentration of                  each of the one or more stabilizers is in the                  range of from 3.5-15 wt.%, preferably 5-12 wt.%.                  A plasticizer, preferably mineral oil is added                  with an injection flow of 0.1-30 l/m3 polymer                  solution, in particular 0.5-15 l/m3 polymer                  solution.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE GUIZHOU GUIFU ECOLOGICAL FERTILIZER IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
   GUIZHOU KEXIN CHEM & METALLURGY CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018A2644W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing potassium and calcium fertilizer, involves crushing the potassium-containing shale through 150-200 mesh sieve, mixing crushed content with phosphogypsum, additives and modifier to make raw material, adding additive and modifier, then transferring the raw material to a kiln for roasting to obtain clinker, grinding obtained clinker, dissolving and subjecting content to solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide to liquid, then filtering, drying and calcining aluminum hydroxide to obtain alumina, calcining sulfide, drying and crushing dried remaining residue of flotation to obtain potassium-calcium-based fertilizer, adding potassium diammonium phosphate, grass ash, organic matter, bagasse, chicken manure and antibacterial agent to the potassium-calcium base fertilizer and mixing the content.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing potassium and calcium fertilizer which is useful for nectarine and recyling aluminum by acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the added value of phosphogypsum and potassium-containing shale, and enables preparing potassium and calcium fertilizer for nectarine which has small effect on the acidity and alkalinity of the nectarine planting soil, and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing potassium and calcium fertilizer, involves crushing the potassium-containing shale through 150-200 mesh sieve, mixing crushed content with phosphogypsum, additives and modifier to make raw material, controlling the weight ratio of phosphogypsum and potassium-containing shale in the raw material is 1:0.5-1.5, adding the additive ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide and aluminum oxide with ferric oxide contained in the raw material, and the mixing ratio of the modifier is 10-25% of the total weight of the raw material, then transferring the raw material to a kiln for roasting to obtain clinker, grinding obtained clinker, dissolving and subjecting content to solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide to the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation to a white precipitate is no longer generated, and then filtering off the white precipitate, washing and drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide, and calcining the obtained aluminum hydroxide to obtain alumina, the filter residue obtained by solid-liquid separation is floated, the sulfide is floated, calcining sulfide, and the generated flue gas is sent to a sulfuric acid preparation system to prepare sulfuric acid, drying remaining residue of the flotation and pulverizing content to obtain potassium-calcium-base fertilizer, adding potassium diammonium phosphate, grass ash, organic matter, bagasse, chicken manure and antibacterial agent to the potassium-calcium base fertilizer and mixing the content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The potassium-containing shale comprises 6-10% potassium oxide, 17-20% aluminum oxide, 5-7% ferric oxide, and 40-55% silicon dioxide. The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln. Preferred Method: In step (a), the calcination time is 1-3 hours at a temperature of 1000-1200 degrees C. In step (b), the grinding is water milling, and is dissolved in water when dissolved. The liquid-solid ratio at the time of dissolution is (3-6):1. In step (c), the calcination is performed at a temperature of 800-1000 degrees C for 1-3 hours. In step (d), the floced sulfide is placed in a 35-50% oxygen-rich environment and calcined at 800-1000 degrees C for 1-3 hours. The flue gas generated by the calcination is catalyzed by vanadium pentoxide and then absorbed by concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid. In step (e), the fineness after drying and pulverization of the residue is 90-100 mesh sieve, and the residual amount of the sieve is less than 6%. The potassium and calcium fertilizer comprises 22-35 pts. wt. potassium-calcium based fertilizer, 5-10 pts. wt. diammonium phosphate, 2-6 pts. wt. plant ash, 10-15 pts. wt. organic matter, 5-10 pts. wt. bagasse, 5-10 pts. wt. chicken manure and 1-5 pts. wt. antibacterial agent. The organic matter is one of fermented soybean material or humic acid. The antibacterial agent is composed of vitamin C, vitamin E, sawn wood powder and distilled water in a mass ratio of 1:1:(1-3):(10-15).
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN CN108835300-A
TI Processing green tea with better color comprises picking the tea leaves, dehydrating, drying, cooling, frying, placing in a bamboo sieve, leveling, cooling, weighing the tea, bagging and sealing the bag mouth.
AU LIU Y
AE PINGSHAN SHUIZHONGYUN TEA IND CO LTD (PING-Non-standard)
GA 201894617D
AB    NOVELTY - Processing green tea with better color comprises: (i) picking the tea leaves, dehydrating, drying the tea leaves in a drum type machine and heating; (ii) cooling the tea leaves by placing tea on a bamboo sieve, placing the bamboo screen in a ventilated place, naturally cooling, blowing the flat tea leaves using a flattening fan, then speeding up to cool the tea leaves, rubbing the tea leaves once by tea machine or manually for 20-25 minutes, making the cell breakage rate of tea to more than 65%; (iii) drying the tea leaves using a tea dryer at 110-130 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, transferring into a frying machine, frying, passing carbon dioxide gas and completely drying the tea leaves; and (iv) placing the dried tea leaves in a bamboo sieve, leveling, cooling, weighing the tea, bagging under vacuum condition, discharging air inside the bag, and simultaneously sealing the bag mouth.
   USE - The method is useful for processing green tea with better color.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces oxidation of zeaxanthin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing green tea with better color comprises: (i) picking the tea leaves, dehydrating by placing in a cylindrical container with open ends, placing the container in a cool place, providing a filter screen for the barrier at both ends of the container to block tea leaves, blowing inside the container by a blower, drying the tea leaves, evaporating water inside the tea leaves, reducing the moisture content of tea cells, and simultaneously reducing its activity, taking out the dehydrated tea leaves, pouring into a drum type machine or iron pot, heating at 260-320 degrees C for 3-5 minutes for passivating enzymes in tea leaves; (ii) cooling the tea leaves by placing tea on a bamboo sieve, where the upper part is provided with a filter screen for blocking tea leaves, placing the bamboo screen in a ventilated place, naturally cooling, blowing the flat tea leaves using a flattening fan, then speeding up to cool the tea leaves, rubbing the tea leaves once by tea machine or manually for 20-25 minutes, making the cell breakage rate of tea to more than 65%; (iii) drying the tea leaves using a tea dryer at 110-130 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, transferring into a frying machine, frying, passing carbon dioxide gas and completely drying the tea leaves; and (iv) placing the dried tea leaves in a bamboo sieve, leveling, cooling, weighing the tea, bagging under vacuum condition, discharging air inside the bag, and simultaneously sealing the bag mouth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (i), blowing the tea leaves using the blower at 40-60 degrees C. In the step (ii), picking out broken tea leaves by manual picking when the tea is flattened on the bamboo sieve. In the step (iii), frying the tea leaves in the frying machine at 200-220 degrees C for 20-21 minutes. In the step (iv), sealing the bag mouth by hot melt sealing machine, and closing the mouth of the bag, packaging in package bag made of opaque plastic bag, placing the tea bag by blocking light from direct exposure.

PN CN108721592-A
TI Essential oil for treating neck and shoulder pain, raw materials comprises garden balsam stem, rhizoma ligustici wallichii, camphor oil, mint extract, Achyranthes, ginger, agrimony, rosin, saffron, seaweed, Aloe, chamomile and squalane.
AU LI T
AE HUNAN XINQIU TRADE CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201890102S
AB    NOVELTY - Essential oil raw materials comprises 7-14 pts. wt. garden balsam stem, 7-14 pts. wt. rhizoma ligustici wallichii, 6-12 pts. wt. camphor oil, 5-10 pts. wt. mint extract, 4-9 pts. wt. Achyranthes, 5-10 pts. wt. ginger, 3-8 pts. wt. agrimony, 3-8 pts. wt. rosin, 3-8 pts. wt. saffron, 2-6 pts. wt. seaweed, 2-6 pts. wt. Aloe, 2-6 pts. wt. chamomile, 1-3 pts. wt. squalane and 5-10 pts. wt. honey-fried licorice.
   USE - Essential oil for treating neck and shoulder pain.
   ADVANTAGE - The essential oil is pure traditional Chinese medicine preparation, selected materials are suitable, and the pharmaceutical preparation technology of modern advanced, can directly act on the affected area for a long time, has quick acting effect, it can quickly and effectively relieving the pain of the patient and is not easy to relapse, and it si safe without any toxic and side effect, and is suitable for treating neck and shoulder pain.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing essential oil, which involves:
   (A) taking the seaweed Aloe and chamomile;
   (B) cutting into sheets;
   (C) adding warm water to soak for 30-45 minutes;
   (D) extracting the volatile oil by extractor;
   (E) collecting volatile oil to obtain the first essential oil;
   (F) cleaning the balsam, ginger, agrimony and honey-fried licorice to remove impurities;
   (G) adding camphor oil to stir;
   (H) putting them in steamer for steaming;
   (I) taking out;
   (J) drying;
   (K) putting the dried peony root into a grinder for grinding;
   (L) sieving to obtain powder;
   (M) crushing the hemlock parsley medicinal material until the granularity is 30-60 meshes;
   (N) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for 2 hours at 48-52 degrees C under the pressure of 6 mega-Pascal to obtain Ligusticum wallichii oil;
   (O) mixing Achyranthes, rosin and saffron after removing impurity;
   (P) crushing the mixed medicinal material until the granularity is 40-60 meshes;
   (Q) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for 2.5-3 hours at 48-52 degrees C under the pressure of 6 mega-Pascal to obtain the second essential oil;
   (R) mixing the extracted first essential oil, second essential oil, prepared powder and peppermint extract;
   (S) adding squalane and camphor oil;
   (T) stirring completely;
   (U) standing for 30-60 minutes; and
   (V) taking the filtered liquid to obtain the essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The essential oil raw materials comprises 10 pts. wt. garden balsam stem, 10 pts. wt. rhizoma ligustici wallichii, 9 pts. wt. camphor oil, 7 pts. wt. mint extract, 7 pts. wt. Achyranthes, 7 pts. wt. ginger, 6 pts. wt. agrimony, 6 pts. wt. rosin, 6 pts. wt. saffron, 4 pts. wt. seaweed, 4 pts. wt. Aloe, 4 pts. wt. chamomile, 2 pts. wt. squalane and 7 pts. wt. honey-fried licorice. Preferred Conditions: The parameter of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the Ligusticum wallichii oil and second essential oil comprsies the extraction kettle pressure is 30-40 mega-Pascal, the extracting temperature is 40-50 degrees C and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 7-10 L/hour. Preferred Process: The camphor oil is prepared by selecting fresh camphor leaves, cleaning, soaking in 6-10% aqueous salt solution, then putting the soaked camphor incage drawer and steaming for 3-4 minutes under the temperature of 100-110 degrees C and pressure in the cage drawer is kept at 0.14-0.16 Mega-Pascal, placing the layered camphor on the rack, and introducing the wet air by the draft to obtain wet air with camphor essential oil, cooling the wet air with the camphor essential oil, removing the condensed water, using an adsorbent to adsorb the adsorbent into the supercritical extraction kettle, taking carbondioxide as extraction fluid for extraction for the first time, then putting the obtained extract of first time extraction into the supercritical extraction kettle again and using as entrainer to absolute ethylalcohol of carbon dioxide as stripping fluid extraction for the second time to obtain the second extract, and distilling the extract of the second time under reduced pressure to obtain the camphor oil. The peppermint extract is prepared by taking peppermint. taking a mixed solution of methanol, water and acetic acid in the mass ratio of 30-50:20-35:0.15-0.18 as an extracting agent, putting by mixing the extracting agent and mint according to the mass ratio of 16-20:1 into the container and extracting under the condition of 30-40 degrees C for 1-2 hours to obtain the crude extract, putting the crude extract into the sonicator and extracting for 1-2 hours at 60-65 degrees C constant temperature water bath to obtain a mixture, filtering the mixture through macroporous resin to adsorb and then eluting by ethanol, collecting the eluent, concentrating and drying at 40-50 degrees C until the solid content is not greater than 10%, then performing vacuum drying to obtain the peppermint extract.
   ACTIVITY - Analgesic.

TI Coproducing activated carbon by wood particle            gasification power generation comprises e.g. placing            sawdust or wood powder into the molding machine to            obtain wood particle and carrying out pyrolysis,            reduction, oxidation and micro-activation.

AB    NOVELTY - Coproducing activated carbon by wood particle                gasification power generation comprises e.g.                placing sawdust or wood powder into the molding                machine to obtain wood particles having 5-8 mm                diameter, 20-40 mm length and 1.0-1.2 g/cm3                density, placing wood particles into the                gasification device, carrying out pyrolysis,                reduction, oxidation, and micro-activation, where                in pyrolysis zone performing pyrolysis using air as                gasification agent, preheating wood particles at                230 degrees C under anoxic conditions, releasing                large amount of heat, hot gas, small molecular                organic matter and biomass charcoal, where hot gas                comprises carbon monoxide, methane, hydrogen,                gaseous tar, and gaseous wood vinegar, in reduction                zone reacting carbon dioxide and water with biochar                to produce carbon monoxide and hydrogen, in                oxidation oxidizing biochar to release heat,                providing heat for reduction, pyrolysis, and                micro-activation zones.
   USE - The method is useful for coproducing activated                carbon by wood particle gasification power                generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Coproducing activated carbon by wood particle                gasification power generation comprises (i) placing                sawdust or wood powder into the molding machine to                obtain wood particles having 5-8 mm diameter, 20-40                mm length and 1.0-1.2 g/cm3 density, (ii) placing                wood particles into the gasification device,                carrying out pyrolysis, reduction, oxidation, and                micro-activation, where in pyrolysis zone                performing pyrolysis using air as gasification                agent, preheating wood particles at 230 degrees C                under anoxic conditions, releasing large amount of                heat, hot gas, small molecular organic matter and                biomass charcoal, where hot gas comprises carbon                monoxide, methane, hydrogen, gaseous tar, and                gaseous wood vinegar, in reduction zone reacting                carbon dioxide and water with biochar to produce                carbon monoxide and hydrogen, in oxidation                oxidizing biochar to release heat, providing heat                for reduction, pyrolysis, and micro-activation                zones, in micro-activation zone activating biochar                by steam as activator which is obtained by                thermally decomposing three major elements with                air, and activating biochar at 850-900 degrees C to                obtain primary activated carbon, (iii) conveying                hot gas to the boiler for combustion to produce                medium-temperature medium-pressure steam, and                promoting steam turbine power generation or                industrial and civil heating, and (iv) conveying                primary activated carbon to the rotary activation                furnace, activating using steam from a boiler as an                active agent to obtain a wood activated carbon, and                sieving to obtain wood activated carbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow diagram of the                coproducing activated carbon by wood particle                gasification power generation (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN CN107917855-A
TI Detecting comprehensive high temperature metallurgy performance of iron-containing furnace charge comprises e.g. forming reducing gas, passing reducing gas to testing device and testing reductive properties of iron-containing furnace charge.
AU DI H
   HE Z
   GUO Q
   WU Y
   ZHAN W
   ZHANG J
   PANG Q
AE UNIV LIAONING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (ULST-C)
GA 2018313162
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting comprehensive high temperature metallurgy performance of iron-containing furnace charge comprises e.g. (i) using high temperature carbon dioxide gas and nitrogen gas to form reducing gas, and passing the reducing into the high temperature metallurgical performance comprehensive testing device, (ii) using the balance to test the reductive properties of iron-containing furnace charge, weighing the weight loss of iron, (iii) placing the iron on the rotating stage, placing metal pressure rods with pressure sensors above the rotating stage, and (iv) carrying out real-time observation.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting comprehensive high temperature metallurgy performance of iron-containing furnace charge.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting comprehensive high temperature metallurgy performance of iron-containing furnace charge comprises (i) using high temperature carbon dioxide gas and nitrogen gas in a ratio of 7:3 to form reducing gas, and passing the reducing into the high temperature metallurgical performance comprehensive testing device, (ii) using the balance to test the reductive properties of iron-containing furnace charge, weighing the weight loss of the iron-containing in a reduced state in real time through the balance, using the formula as mentioned in the specification to calculate the iron ore reduction performance, where RI: degree of reduction, m0: initial iron-containing quality, Ma: Instantaneous mass before reduction, Mb: quality of after reduction, w ferrous oxide: mass fraction of ferrous oxide in iron, and w tetrafluoroethylene: the mass fraction of iron in the iron, (iii) placing the iron on the rotating stage, placing metal pressure rods with pressure sensors above the rotating stage, where the metal rod is perpendicular to the rotating stage, and used to detect the hot compressive strength of the iron-containing at multiple points, where the calculation is as follows: sigma is hot compressive strength of iron-containing furnace charge, sigma 1... sigma n: multiple measurements of the hot compressive strength values of the corresponding points of the iron-containing furnace charge, and (iv) carrying out real-time observation of the internal conditions of the high-temperature metallurgical performance comprehensive inspection device through a camera monitoring device, where determining the thermal expansion performance of the iron-bearing charge through the formula as mentioned in the specification, where nu : thermal expansion coefficient of the iron-bearing charge, d1: monitoring the particle size of the charge in real time to capture the charge, and d0: monitoring real-time capture charge under initial conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The high temperature metallurgy performance comprehensive detection device comprises a metal shell, steel tube, heating resistance wire, compressive refractory filler, rotational stage, metal pressure rod, pressure sensor, reducing gas path, monitoring camera apparatus, the metal furnace shell wall is provided with a flame-resistant filler, the compressive refractory filler is provided with heating resistance wire, the steel tube is set inside of the heating resistance wire, the upper end of the metal furnace shell is provided with a hook below the steel tube provided with pressure-resisting refractory filler, the rotational stage is installed in the steel tube driven by the motor to rotate, the metal pressure rod is vertically arranged above the rotary object stage from a rotational position of the stage central shaft. The metal pressing rod is provided with a pressure sensor and provided with a monitoring camera device in the metal furnace shell connected to the reducing gas passage of external reducing gas pipeline. The testing device further comprises a gas reaction device, nitrogen bottle, gas reaction device and nitrogen bottle connected with gas flow controller through the pipeline connected with the reducing gas passage.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon disulfide removal method involves (i) calcining kapok fiber, polyacrylonitrile fiber/viscose fiber, carbonizing in nitrogen atmosphere, cooling to obtain activated carbon fiber precursor, (ii) calcining activated carbon fiber precursor in carbon dioxide-nitrogen mixed gas atmosphere, activating, cooling, grinding, sieving to obtain activated carbon fiber carrier (A), (iii) mixing activated carbon fiber precursor with activator potassium hydroxide particles, baking, activating in nitrogen atmosphere, cooling, washing, drying to obtain activated carbon fiber carrier (B).
   USE - Carbon disulfide removal method based on low-temperature plasma and activated carbon fiber catalyst, for use in industrial exhaust gas treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon disulfide removal method based on low-temperature plasma and activated carbon fiber catalyst, involves (i) calcining kapok fiber, polyacrylonitrile fiber or viscose fiber at a temperature of 700-1000 degrees C, and carbonizing in a nitrogen atmosphere for 60-150 minutes, cooling to room temperature to obtain activated carbon fiber precursor, (ii) placing activated carbon fiber precursor at a temperature of 600-900 degrees C, calcining in carbon dioxide-nitrogen mixed gas atmosphere and activating for 30-120 minutes, cooling down, grinding and sieving to obtain activated carbon fiber carrier (A), (iii) mixing activated carbon fiber precursor with the activator potassium hydroxide particles, then placing at a temperature of 600-900 degrees C, baking and activating in nitrogen atmosphere for 30-120 minutes, cooling to room temperature, washing to neutral with deionized water, and drying to obtain activated carbon fiber carrier (B), (iv) supporting transition metal salt on the activated carbon fiber carrier (A) or activated carbon fiber carrier (B) by an impregnation method or a sol-gel method, and then placing in a temperature of 300-600 degrees C, calcining in nitrogen atmosphere for 60-180 minutes to obtain activated carbon fiber catalyst where the transition metal salt is a nitrate of iron, copper, zinc, aluminum, nickel or cobalt, the weight of transition metal loaded on the activated carbon fiber catalyst is 1-10% of the mass of carrier, (v) placing activated carbon fiber catalyst in a discharge area of a low-temperature plasma reactor, setting output voltage and discharge gap, passing carbon disulfide-containing flue gas into a low-temperature plasma reactor to remove carbon disulfide where the gas exiting in low temperature plasma reactor is purified flue gas and flow rate of the gas containing carbon disulfide is 60-120 mL/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide-nitrogen mixed gas is 10-20%. The mass ratio of activated carbon fiber precursor to activator potassium hydroxide particles is 1:(1-4). Preferred Properties: The flow rate of carbon dioxide-nitrogen mixed gas is 20-60 mL/minute. Preferred Condition: In step (iv), the output voltage is set to 1-10 kV and the discharge gap is 3-9 mm.

PN CN107712783-A
TI Preparation of hot pot oil by heating vegetable oil, adding Ciba chili and beans, stir-frying, adding pickled pepper, ginger and garlic, stir-frying, adding rock sugar, glutinous rice, white wine and composite spices, and frying.
AU XIE Z
   QU M
   DONG Z
   YANG G
AE CHONGQING MEIXIANGYUAN IND GROUP CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2018168409
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hot pot oil includes pouring vegetable oil into pan, heating, adding Ciba chili and beans, stir-frying, adding pickled pepper, ginger and garlic, stir-frying, adding rock sugar, glutinous rice, white wine and composite spices, frying, adding red pepper, frying, adding monosodium glutamate, salt and chicken essence, stirring, turning off heat, filtering, and collecting filtrate and residue; adding filtrate with base material, frying, filtering, and collecting filtrate and residue; and carbon dioxide supercritical extracting step (A) residue, two-stage separating.
   USE - Method for preparation of hot pot oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully extracts the flavoring substance of raw materials, improves the utilization rate of raw materials, avoids traditional hot pot base material slow taste, and solves problem of complementary taste deficiency. The product has higher content of effective substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of hot pot oil comprises:
   (A) pouring 70-85 pts. wt. vegetable oil into pan, heating to 130-140 degrees C, adding 15-18 pts. wt. Ciba chili and 8-10 pts. wt. beans, stir-frying for 5-6 minutes, adding 10-12 pts. wt. pickled pepper, 6-8 pts. wt. ginger and 6-8 pts. wt. garlic, stir-frying for 3-4 minutes, adding 4-6 pts. wt. rock sugar, 4-6 pts. wt. glutinous rice, 5-7 pts. wt. white wine and 18-20 pts. wt. composite spices, frying for 5-6 minutes, adding 10-14 pts. wt. red pepper, frying to 100-110 degrees C until oil is shiny and fragrance overflowed, adding 4-6 pts. wt. monosodium glutamate, 2-4 pts. wt. salt and 2-4 pts. wt. chicken essence, stirring, turning off heat, filtering, and collecting filtrate and residue;
   (B) adding filtrate with base material, frying, filtering, and collecting filtrate and residue;
   (C) adding step (A) residue to extraction tank, carbon dioxide supercritical extracting at room temperature and at 20-28 MPa for 6-12 hours, two-stage separating at 12 MPa and 6 MPa respectively, and collecting residual mucus; and
   (D) adding 10 wt.% vegetable oil to step (B) residue, mixing, subjecting to high temperature and high pressure extraction at 130-140 degrees C and at 0.3-0.6 MPa for 25-30 minutes, filtering, combining all filtrates and step (C) product, and stirring.
   The base material comprises other ingredients, and does not contain vegetable oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The step (A) raw material comprises 77 pts. wt. vegetable oil, 16 pts. wt. Ciba chili, 8 pts. wt. beans, 12 pts. wt. pickled pepper, 6 pts. wt. ginger, 8 pts. wt. garlic, 5 pts. wt. rock sugar, 5 pts. wt. glutinous rice, 6 pts. wt. white wine, 18 pts. wt. composite spices, 12 pts. wt. red pepper, 2 pts. wt. monosodium glutamate, 4 pts. wt. salt and 2 pts. wt. chicken essence. The composite spices comprise 8-10 pts. wt. cinnamon sticks, 4-5 pts. wt. dried orange peel, 10-14 pts. wt. aniseed, 14-18 pts. wt. tsaoko amomum fruit, and 20-25 pts. wt. thyme.

The method further involves after the conversion                  is completed, filtering to obtain insoluble waste                  residue and primary clear liquid, adjusting the                  pH of the primary clear liquid to be acidic by                  adding sulfuric acid to the primary clear liquid,                  adjusting pH to 2-3 and maintaining until the                  oxidation reaction is over, then performing an                  oxidation reaction at 90-100 degrees C to obtain                  an oxidation liquid, filtering the oxidizing                  liquid to obtain elemental sulfur and a secondary                  clear liquid, adding fluorosilicic acid to the                  secondary clear liquid for reaction, obtaining                  sodium fluorosilicate and the tertiary clear                  liquid after filtration, adjusting the pH of the                  third clear liquid to be alkaline by adding                  sodium hydroxide to the tertiary clear liquid and                  adjusting the pH to 9-11, reducing the                  temperature to 80-90 degrees C, then filtering to                  obtain waste salt residue and the fourth clear                  liquid, adding barium hydroxide to the four clear                  liquids for reaction, adding activated carbon for                  decolorization, then filtering to obtain solid                  barium sulfate and five clear liquids, and after                  the five times the clear liquid is concentrated                  (to 700-800 g/l) and crystallized, dehydrating to                  obtain sodium thiocyanate. While crystallization                  treatment is performed on the five-time clear                  liquid after the concentration treatment,                  temperature reduction treatment is also                  performed. During the cooling process, the                  temperature of the five clear liquids is                  maintained at 50-60 degrees C.

PN CN110628236-A; CN110628236-B
TI Extracting medlar pigment red, comprises e.g. taking dried medlar fruits, crushing, sieving, adding palmitic acid, and stirring, performing microwave heating under stirring conditions, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU HU W
   GUO Z
   CHENG Y
   MA Y
AE NINGXIA BAOFENG ECOLOGICAL PASTURE CO (NING-Non-standard)
   NINGXIA BAOFENG ECOLOGICAL PASTURE CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202003760K
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting medlar pigment red, comprises (i) taking dried medlar fruits, crushing, and sieving by using 40-80 mesh sieve, (ii) adding palmitic acid to the medlar powder, and stirring, (iii) performing microwave heating under stirring conditions, (iv) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain a crude extract of lycopene of L. barbarum, and (v) sequentially washing the crude extract with hot alcohol, hot-filtering, collecting filter cake, and drying to obtain lycopene.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting medlar pigment red.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high product yield; and simultaneously ensures the stability and purity of the product during processing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the drying is carried out by performing vacuum drying at 50-65 degrees C with a vacuum degree of 70-100 KPa until the moisture content is 6 wt.% or less. In the step (ii), the method further comprises adding palmitic acid powder to medlar powder in a mass ratio of 1-3 wt.% under the condition of 0-30 degrees C, and uniformly stirring. In the step (iii), the microwave power used in heating is 400-700 W, where the heating method is intermittent heating, and the microwave is irradiated for 2 minutes and separated for 8 minutes, and the steps of first irradiating with microwaves are repeated, where the interval to control the total time of microwave heating treatment is 2-3 hours. In the step (iv), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out with a pressure of 20-25 MPa at 35-45 degrees C for 2-3 hours, and the separation is carried out at 30-40 degrees C. In the step (v), the crude extract is added to 75-95% ethanol solution or absolute ethanol at a volume ratio of 1:2-3, heated to 60-70 degrees C, uniformly stirred, hot-filtered at 60-70 degrees C, then the filter cake is collected, and dried at 55-65 degrees C.

   HAO P

GA 201952434V
AB    NOVELTY - A modified silica gel carrier is prepared by                weighing raw materials comprising silicate and                inorganic acid, taking silicate aqueous solution,                adding inorganic acid solution, adjusting pH value                to 10-12, adding 2-8 wt.% ethanol aqueous solution                with volume ratio of 1:1-1:10, passing carbon                dioxide gas, adding organic acid aqueous solution                with volume ratio of 1:5-1:30, adjusting pH value                to 9-10, carrying out hydrothermal reaction at                70-100 degrees C, acidifying, washing, drying, and                activating.
   USE - Method for preparing modified silica gel                carrier.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has improved pore volume and                distribution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The modified silica gel                  carrier is prepared by taking 30-60 mL of 1.0-1.5                  mol/L silicate aqueous solution, heating and                  stirring at 40-60 degrees C, adding 0.8-1.3 mol/L                  inorganic acid aqueous solution at flow rate of                  3-6 mL/minute, adjusting pH value to 10-12,                  reacting for 0.5-1.0 hour, adding 10-15 mL of 2-8                  wt.% ethanol aqueous solution with volume ratio                  of 1:5-1:9 at flow rate of 2-5 mL/minute,                  reacting at 0.5-1.0 hour, passing carbon dioxide                  gas under 0.01-0.05 MPa, adding 1.0-3.0 mol/L                  organic acid aqueous solution with volume ratio                  of 1:10-1:20 at flow rate of 4-9 mL/minute,                  adjusting pH value to 9-10, reacting for 1.0-2.5                  hours, carrying out hydrothermal reaction at                  70-100 degrees C for 3.0-5.0 hours, adding                  1.0-3.0 mol/L inorganic acid aqueous solution,                  adjusting pH value to 3-6, stopping carbon                  dioxide feed, cooling, washing with distilled                  water and/or distilled water and ethanol, drying                  at 300-335 degrees C, and activating under inert                  gas conditions at 400-600 degrees C for 3.0-6.0                  hours. Preferred Components: The silicate is                  sodium silicate, potassium silicate and water                  glass. The inorganic acid is sulfuric acid,                  hydrochloric acid and nitric acid. Preferred                  Components: The organic alcohol is n-butanol,                  isobutanol, 1,2-butanediol, 1,3-butanediol,                  1,4-butanediol, 1-pentanol, 2-pentanol,                  3-pentanol, 2-methyl-1-butanol,                  2-methyl-2-butanol, 3-methyl-2-butanol,                  3-methyl-1-butanol and 2,2-dimethyl-1-propanol,                  preferably n-butanol and 1,3-butanediol.

   CHOI Y H
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
GA 201870691L

   USE - Catalyst precursor for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Catalyst precursor has low temperature and high yield, and improves molecular weight of catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing catalyst precursor, which involves:
   (A) selecting iron raw material and copper raw material;
   (B) adding iron and copper raw materials to solvent to prepare solution;
   (C) introducing reducing agent into solution; and
   (D) synthesizing solution hydrothermally and adding reducing agent to prepare catalyst precursor, where iron source material is iron chloride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precursor comprises trigonal form. The copper raw material is copper nitrate and copper chloride. The reducing agent is propionaldehyde, and ethylene glycol. The catalyst is copper and iron. The product by reaction is olefin, paraffin and hydrocarbon. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of solution is 150-200 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours. The specific surface area of catalyst is 10 m2/g. The diameter of the catalyst is less than 800 nanometer. The porosity of catalyst is 0.12-0.17 cm3/g. The ratio of copper and iron is greater than 0.594. The distance of iron metal lattice plane of catalyst is greater than 0.2 nanometer. The spherical copper particle is positioned on surface of catalyst. The ratio of paraffin to olefin in catalyst is 9. The change of carbon dioxide per iron per unit time of catalyst is 1.7x 10-6 mole CO2 gfe-1s-1. The catalyst precursor is restored at 200-500 degrees C. The catalyst precursor is reduced under hydrogen gas atmosphere. The flow rate of hydrogen gas is 50-200 standard cubic centimeters per minute. Preferred Composition: The amount of raw material is 5-10 pts. wt. of 100 pts. wt. solvent. The amount of sodium hydroxide is 6-11 pts. wt. of total solution. The amount of reducing agent is 0-3 pts. wt. of total solution. The amount of hydrocarbon is 59 pts. wt. of total hydrocarbons in product which has 5C or more. Preferred Process: The raw material is injected in solvent and sodium hydroxide is further placed into solution.

AB    NOVELTY - A nano calcium carbonate is prepared by calcining limestone into calcium oxide, adding water to prepare 5-10 wt.% calcium hydroxide solution, spraying carbon dioxide and nitrogen mixed gas, filtering, collecting solid, returning filtrate to spray tower, adjusting pH value to 7-7.2, circulating, taking solid, adding stearic amide and 4-6 wt.% higher fatty alcohol, reacting at 80-100 degrees C for 5 hours to obtain nano-calcium carbonate mixture, carrying out solid-liquid separation, taking solid, and drying for 10 hours.

AU SU Z
AE QI J (QIJJ-Individual)
   SU Z (SUZZ-Individual)
GA 202271590V
AB    NOVELTY - Use of a nursing composition in preparing medicine composition for treating and controlling secretion vesicle number in nasal cavity cup-shaped cell. The composition is composed of sweet potato leaf, extract of stem and/or leaves of Pachyrhizus, and ion solution.
   USE - The nursing composition is used in preparing medicine composition for treating and controlling secretion vesicle number in nasal cavity cup-shaped cell, and regulating secretion of the secretory vesicle, decomposing the histamine, inhibiting the generation of leukotriene and prostaglandin (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for nursing composition, composed of sweet potato leaf, extract of stem and/or leaves of Pachyrhizus and yam, and ion solution, where the nursing composition is prepared by: (1) drying Pachyrhizus leaves and yam, sweet potato leaf in 70degrees Celsius box until the water content is 5-7%, preventing organic matter chelate metal ion; (2) taking dry Pachyrhizus leaf, placing into planetary ball mill, grinding the sweet potato leaf, and/or yam leaf into powder, the size is between 1 m to 0.1 m; (3) pressurizing the liquid carbon dioxide by high pressure pump to more than critical pressure (higher than 72.9 atm), and flowing through the heat exchanger to make the high pressure liquid carbon dioxide into supercritical state, after the supercritical carbon dioxide enters into the high pressure groove with the sweet potato leaf powder, extracting the fat-soluble component in the sweet potato leaf powder, and performing static extraction for 20 minutes and dynamically extracting for 4 minutes, and then reducing pressure to 1 atm, raising the temperature to room temperature, after gasification of carbon dioxide, entering into separating groove, (4) electrodialyzing the ionic solution into equal ionic solution, placing into beaker to boil, adding Pachyrhizus leaf, sweet potato and/or yam leaf powder, and covering the aluminum foil paper, stirring with magnet for 1 hour, maintaining constant temperature at 75 degrees Celsius, the where Pachyrhizus leaf, sweet potato and/or yam leaf powder containing metal ion, reaching magnesium ion content is more than 0.25% (w/w), and magnesium ion, potassium ion is 4-17: 1 (w/w); (5) after cooling the heated Pachyrhizus leaf, sweet potato and/or yam leaf solution, introducing enough carbon dioxide gas to form sodium bicarbonate with sodium ion in the solution, precipitating and separating out, making the sodium ion content in the ion concentration filtrate less than 0.3 % (w/w); and (6) pouring the Pachyrhizus leaves containing sodium bicarbonate precipitate, sweet potato and/or yam leaf solution into the filter cup (aperture 0.1 m), performing vacuum filtration, filtering the obtained filtrate to obtain the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The number of the secretory vesicle with reduced regulation is not greater than 45%. The amount of the secretory mucus secreted mucus in the cup-shaped cell is increased by less than 240 %. The ion solution comprises magnesium, potassium and sodium ion, where the concentration ratio of the magnesium ion to potassium ion is 4-17: 1 (w/w), and the content of magnesium ion is 0.3-1%, and the content of sodium ion is less than 0.3%, potassium content is 0.02-0.2 %, and the salinity is 0.9-4%. The nursing composition further comprises immunosuppressant, immunomodulator or antiinflammatory drug. The total salinity of the solution is 10. The sodium ion content is less than 0.3 % (w/w). The magnesium ion content is higher than 0.25 % (w/w). The content of magnesium ion is 0.3-1 wt.%. The magnesium and potassium ratio is 4-17:1 (w/w). The potassium ion content is 0.02-0.2 (w/w). The nursing composition further comprises iron, copper, zinc, iodine, chromium and/or molybdenum ion.

PT P
PN CN113964405-A
TI Performing online monitoring of high voltage lithium cobaltate surface residual alkali concentration for lithium ion battery material, involves setting temperature curve and air inlet and outlet amount of sintering kiln.
AU ZHANG J
   SONG Y
   WANG Y
   YANG K
   ZHANG Y
   XU K
AE GEM JIANGSU COBALT IND CO LTD (JMGL-C)
GA 202220123E
AB    NOVELTY - Performing online monitoring of high voltage lithium cobaltate surface residual alkali concentration involves setting temperature curve and air inlet and outlet amount of sintering kiln. The materials are placed into sintering kiln to continuously sinter the materials when sintering kiln reaches set parameters and is stable. The material is continuously input into sintering kiln to obtain sintering product, and carbon dioxide concentration of each temperature zone in sintering kiln is measured.
   USE - Method for performing online monitoring of high voltage lithium cobaltate surface residual alkali concentration for lithium ion battery material used in the fields of communication, consumer electronics and computer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce surface residual alkali content of high-voltage lithium cobalt oxide material, realize precise control of production process, and improve performance of product. The method is simple and practical for adjusting the sintering atmosphere by changing the bell mouth suction to direct air exhaust, which accelerates exhaust gas discharge, improves reaction rate of solid-phase reaction, improves performance of sintering equipment, and improves performance of product. The improvement provides a stable support, which significantly reduces residual alkali on the surface of charged material, improves stability of high-voltage lithium cobalt oxide material, and reduces gas expansion performance of lithium cobalt oxide battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing online monitoring of high voltage lithium cobaltate surface residual alkali concentration involves setting temperature curve and air inlet and outlet amount of sintering kiln. The materials are placed into sintering kiln to continuously sinter the materials when sintering kiln reaches set parameters and is stable. The material is continuously input into sintering kiln to obtain sintering product, and carbon dioxide concentration of each temperature zone in sintering kiln is measured. The gas flow rate of each exhaust pipe of sintering kiln sampling is measured and residual alkali content on surface of sintered product is tested repeatedly for multiple times as basic data. The carbon dioxide concentration data of each temperature zone in sintering kiln is analyzed, gas flow direction and carbon dioxide distribution state in the sintering kiln is determined, so as to determine area with highest solid-phase reaction activity. The gas flow data of each exhaust pipe of sintering kiln is analyzed to confirm exhaust status of region with highest solid-phase reaction activity. The oxygen intake in area with highest solid-phase reactivity is increased, exhaust pipe in area with highest solid-phase reactivity is wrapped with refractory cotton, and exhaust pipe in area with highest solid-phase reactivity is changed to direct exhaust. The carbon dioxide concentration in sintering kiln is tested, sintered product is sampled to test surface residual alkali content, and data is analyzed.

TI Droplet shape control device for carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, comprises excitation power supply device which includes excitation power supply and control system respectively connected to input end of excitation power supply.
AU CHANG Y
   ZOU W
   LI Y
   DONG Z
   MEI Q
   WU H
   LIU D
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 202058164Q
AB    NOVELTY - Droplet shape control device comprises excitation power supply device (5), magnetic head device, hall voltage sensor (6) and carbon dioxide (CO2) gas shielded welding machine. The excitation power supply device includes an excitation power supply and a control system, the control system is respectively connected to the input end of the excitation power supply and the output end of the Hall voltage sensor. The output end of the excitation power supply is connected to the magnetic induction coil (9) of the magnetic head device. The input terminal of the Hall voltage sensor is connected to the output terminal of the CO2 gas shielded welding machine, and is used to collect an arc voltage signal at the output terminal of the CO2 welding machine and transmit to the control system.
   USE - Droplet shape control device for carbon dioxide gas shielded welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The droplet shape control device improves weld formation and the stability of the welding process and suitable for high current low splash CO2 gas shielded welding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for controlling the droplet form of carbon dioxide gas-shielded welding, which involves;
   (A) collecting the arc voltage signal at the output of the CO2 gas shielded welding machine by hall voltage sensor and sending the arc voltage signal to the control system;
   (B) detecting the rising and falling edges of the arc voltage signal;
   (C) turning on the excitation power supply when the control system detects the rising edge of the arc voltage signal, and the excitation power supply provides an excitation pulse current for the magnetic induction coil;
   (D) generating Lorentz force when passing the excitation pulse current by magnetic induction coil, and generating by the excitation pulse current and the electromagnetic contraction force and spots generating by the welding current of the welding gun of the CO2 gas shielded welding machine, plasma flow force and the surface tension of the droplet at the end of the welding wire of the welding gun affects the shape of the droplet;
   (E) changing the shape of the droplet by adjusting the magnitude of the excitation pulse current and the welding current; and
   (F) disconnecting the excitation power supply when the control system detects the falling edge of the arc voltage signal, and the excitation power supply stops supplying the excitation pulse current to the magnetic induction coil.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of droplet shape control device for carbon dioxide gas shielded welding.

   Worktables (2)

TI Installation for membrane permeation treatment of feed gas stream e.g. biogas, consists of compressor for compressing feed gas flow, and membrane separation units, each having membrane(s) more permeable to carbon dioxide than to methane.
AU BARRAUD F
   CHAREYRE J
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2020534215
AB    NOVELTY - A membrane permeation treatment installation of a feed gas stream comprising at least methane and carbon dioxide consists of a compressor for compressing feed gas flow, a membrane separation unit (A) capable of receiving the gas flow coming from the compressor and providing a permeate (P1) and a retentate (R1), a membrane separation unit (B) capable of receiving the retentate (R1) and providing a permeate (P2) and a retentate (R2), a membrane separation unit (C) capable of receiving the permeate (P1) and providing a permeate (P3) and a retentate (R3), at least one unit for measuring the suction pressure of the permeate (P3) of the membrane unit (B), at least one unit for measuring the methane concentration in the retentate (R2) and at least one compressor allowing the suction of the permeate (P2) and the adjustment of the pressure of the permeate (P2). Each membrane separation unit comprises at least one membrane more permeable to carbon dioxide than to methane.
   USE - Installation for membrane permeation treatment of feed gas stream e.g. biogas (claimed), and producing biomethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The installation is environmentally-friendly, and enables effective membrane permeation treatment of biogas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A membrane permeation treatment installation of a feed gas stream comprising at least methane and carbon dioxide consists of a compressor for compressing feed gas flow, a membrane separation unit (A) capable of receiving the gas flow coming from the compressor and providing a permeate (P1) and a retentate (R1), a membrane separation unit (B) capable of receiving the retentate (R1) and providing a permeate (P2) and a retentate (R2), a membrane separation unit (C) capable of receiving the permeate (P1) and providing a permeate (P3) and a retentate (R3), at least one unit for measuring the suction pressure of the permeate (P3) of the membrane unit (B), at least one unit for measuring the methane concentration in the retentate (R2) and at least one compressor allowing the suction of the permeate (P2) and the adjustment of the pressure of the permeate (P2) as a function of the suction pressure measured and of the methane concentration measured before recycling the permeate (P2) in the gas flow supply downstream of compressor. Each membrane separation unit comprises at least one membrane more permeable to carbon dioxide than to methane. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for controlling installation, which involves measuring the pressure of the permeate (P2), measuring the methane concentration in the retentate (R2), comparing the measured pressure and concentration with set values, determining the deviation from set values, adjusting the pressure of the permeate (P2) using the compressor to obtain the correct concentration of methane in retentate (R2).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of installation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The installation comprises at least one unit for adjusting the pressure of the supply gas flow as a function of the measured methane concentration. The unit for adjusting the pressure of the supply gas flow is a compressor or a shut-off valve and progressive pressurization. The retentate (R) is recycled to the compressor for compressing the feed gas flow. The membranes used in the membrane separation units have the same selectivity. At least one membrane separation unit comprises two or more membranes of different selectivities. Preferred Method: The pressure of the supply gas stream is adjusted by increasing or decreasing the pressure. The compressor undergoes acceleration or a deceleration. The comparison and adjustment are carried out automatically by data transmission and data processing unit.

TI Preparing modified adhesive useful for manufacturing tire comprises e.g. preparing etherified product by adding hexamethylol melamine to methanol, carrying out the etherification reaction, and adding di-tert-butylbenzothiazole.
AU HU L
   LANG X
   ZONG H
   TAN J
AE JIANGSU GUOLI CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201996166A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified adhesive comprises e.g. (a) preparing etherified product by adding 1 pts. wt. hexamethylol melamine to 8-12 pts. wt. methanol, carrying out the etherification reaction at the pH of 3-4.5 and at a temperature of 75-80?C for 15-30 minutes, adding 0.35-0.6 pts. wt. di-tert-butylbenzothiazole, carrying out the etherification reaction at pH of 3-4.5 and the temperature of 80 degrees C for 30 minutes, obtaining the etherification product, and adjusting the pH by concentrated sulfuric acid, and (b) preparing modified preform by adding 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. filler, 0.1-0.12 pts. wt. fatty acid metal salt, 0.08-0.18 pts. wt. an organic crosslinking agent, and 0.003-0.02 pts. wt. curing agent to 1 pts. wt. the etherified product, stirring uniformly to obtain mixture, adding mixture and magnetic material to supercritical carbon dioxide reaction reactor, adding excess deionized water to the reaction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction vessel.
   USE - The modified adhesive is useful for manufacturing tire (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high etherification reaction; improves adhesive properties of the adhesive; and increases the binding of formaldehyde content due to di-tert-butylbenzothiazole.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing modified adhesive comprises (a) preparing etherified product by adding 1 pts. wt. hexamethylol melamine to 8-12 pts. wt. methanol, carrying out the etherification reaction at the pH of 3-4.5 and at a temperature of 75-80?C for 15-30 minutes, adding 0.35-0.6 pts. wt. di-tert-butylbenzothiazole, carrying out the etherification reaction at pH of 3-4.5 and the temperature of 80 degrees C for 30 minutes, obtaining the etherification product, and adjusting the pH by concentrated sulfuric acid, (b) preparing modified preform by adding 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. filler, 0.1-0.12 pts. wt. fatty acid metal salt, 0.08-0.18 pts. wt. an organic crosslinking agent, and 0.003-0.02 pts. wt. curing agent to 1 pts. wt. the etherified product, stirring uniformly to obtain mixture, adding mixture and magnetic material to a supercritical carbon dioxide reaction reactor at the mass ratio of 170-250:1, adding excess deionized water to the reaction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction vessel , where the reactor temperature of the vessel is 35-40 degrees C, the pressure is 10- 25 MPa, stirring for 45-50 minutes, releasing the carbon dioxide gas in the reactor, reducing the internal pressure of the reactor to normal pressure and obtaining modified preform, and (c) performing the surface treatment of modified preform with magnesium oxide to obtain modified adhesive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the preparation of the filler comprises mixing silica and carbon black in the mass ratio of 0.1-0.5:1. The step (c), specifically comprises mixing 17-26 pts. wt. modified preform and 1 pts. wt. magnesium oxide powder in high-speed mixer. In the step (c), performing the heat-treatment of modified preform under stirring in high-speed mixer at the heating temperature of 35-50 degrees C . The step (c) specifically comprises dissolving 17-26 pts. wt. the modified preform in a non-aqueous solvent, adding 1 pts. wt. magnesium oxide powder, stirring and removing the non-aqueous solvent by drying. The step (b) specifically comprises adding excess deionized water to the reactor before introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reactor. Preferred Components: The non-aqueous solvent is ethanol.

PN CN114045314-A; CN114045314-B
TI Preparing 2-hydroxytetrahydrofuran or 1,4-butyrolactone by enzyme catalytic oxidation of tetrahydrofuran, comprises e.g. carrying out enzyme catalytic oxidation of tetrahydrofuran in presence of phosphate or citric acid buffer solution as solvent, peroxidase as catalyst, and peroxide.
AU CHEN S
   HAN X
   SANG X
   ZHANG W
AE TIANJIN INST IND BIOTECHNOLOGY (TIIB-C)
GA 202230018G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2-hydroxytetrahydrofuran (I) or 1,4-butyrolactone (II) by enzyme catalytic oxidation of tetrahydrofuran, comprises taking tetrahydrofuran is as raw material, phosphate or citric acid buffer solution as solvent, carrying out enzyme catalysis by using peroxidase with heme activity as catalyst, adding peroxide for carrying out reaction to obtain final product.
   USE - (II) is useful in preparing gamma-hydroxybutyrate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environment-friendly with peroxide synthase as catalyst; has mild condition; reduces the carbon dioxide emission; is simple and environmentally-friendly; utilizes peroxide synthase has good stability; and expands the reaction for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing sodium gamma-hydroxybutyrate, comprising utilizing the preparation method of 1,4-butyrolactone (II), taking the organic phase after extraction, removing the organic solvent after drying, adding sodium hydroxide and anhydrous ethanol, and stirring to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: When the ratio between amount of peroxide and raw material is controlled within 1.5:1, the reaction is carried out to synthesize (I); or when the ratio between amount of peroxide and raw material is controlled to be 1.5-4.0: 1, the reaction is performed to synthesize (II). The peroxidase is haloperoxidase Lfu CPO derived from Leptoxyphium fumago, haloperoxidase Cf CPO of Caldariomyces fumago, peroxidase Aae UPO derived from Agrocybe aegerita, peroxidase Cgl UPO derived from Chaetomium globosum, peroxidase Mro UPO derived from Marasmius rotula, peroxidase Mwe UPO derived from Marasmius wettsteinii, peroxidase Cci UPO derived from Coprinopsis cinerea, peroxidase Mth UPO derived from Myceliophthora thermophila, peroxidase Tte UPO derived from Thielavia terrestris, peroxidase Cra UPO from Coprrinellus radians, and/or peroxidase Pab UPO from Psathyrella aberdarensis. The peroxidase is in the form of whole cells, crude enzyme powder, crude enzyme liquid, pure enzyme or immobilized enzyme. The concentration of tetrahydrofuran is 5-100 mM, the peroxidase concentration is 500-2000 nM, or the enzyme activity is 0.02-0.4 U/ml, and the peroxide concentration is 5-200 mM. The peroxide is hydrogen peroxide or organic peroxide. The organic peroxide is tert-butanol peroxide, tetrahydrofuran peroxide, benzoyl peroxide, ethylene glycol dimethyl ether peroxide, methyl ethyl ketone peroxide, peroxyacetyl nitrate, triacetone triperoxide, diethylene oxide and derivatives, diethyl ether peroxide, peracetic acid and/or cumene hydroperoxide. The peroxide is gradually added into reaction system through syringe pump, and the addition rate corresponding to 1 ml volume is 10-30 microl/hr for concentration of 5-200 mM peroxide. The organic solvent used in the extraction is ethyl acetate.Preferred Method: The reaction is carried out for 20-55degrees Celsius for 8-24 hours. The buffer is citrate or phosphate buffer, and the pH is 4-10. After the reaction is completed, the reaction solution is extracted, the organic phase is taken, and the organic solvent is removed after drying to obtain the final product.

   ADVANTAGE - The poly (benzoxazole - co - amide) hollow fiber membrane having thermotropic rearrangement property, has an asymmetric structure with compact skin layer. The hollow fiber membrane has improved flexibility, organic solvent resistance, acid and alkali resistance, thermal stability and compressive property.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - FIG. 1(a) is an electron micrograph of a cross section of a hollow fiber membrane
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The poly (benzoxazole-co-amide) copolymer of the hollow fiber gas separation membrane, comprises repeating units:M, n = mole fraction of the corresponding repeating unit, and satisfy 0 .1 less than or equal to m less than or equal to 0 .90 .1 less than or equal to n less than or equal to 0 .9 and m+n =1.The ortho hydroxyl-containing polyamide precursor comprises repeating unit:M, n = same as above.The polyamide raw material containing a hydroxyl group at the ortho position, has weight average molecular weight 10000 to 2000000, preferably 100000 to 1000000. The polyamide precursor solution containing hydroxyl at the ortho position, is prepared using 15 to 30 wt.% 2, 2-bis (3-amino-4-hydroxyphenyl) hexafluoropropane (APAF) and 4, 4' -diaminodiphenyl ether (ODA) as diamine monomers, terephthaloyl chloride ( TPC) as a diacyl chloride monomer and N-methylpyrrolidone (NMP) as a solvent, by a solution polycondensation method, and is defoamed for later use. The molar equivalent of APAF is 10 to 90 percent by mass and the molar equivalent of TPC diacid chloride is 1.0 to 1.1, preferably 1.01 to 1.05. The core liquid used in preparation of precursor solution, is alcohol, ketone, deionized water and/or N-methyl pyrrolidone aqueous solution. The gel bath used during the process, is water, alcohol (such as methanol and/or ethanol) solution of volume concentration 1 to 20 %, and/or ketone (such as acetone) solution, N-methylpyrrolidone aqueous solution, etc.

PN KR2021027922-A
TI Producing mesoporous hollow silica material comprises forming polystyrene by adding styrene and 2,2'-azobis(2-methylpropionamidine) dihydrochloride to distilled water, and adding distilled water, and cetyltrimethylammonium chloride.
AU BAE J Y
   KIM T H
   LIM S J
   JANG S
AE UNIV KEIMYUNG IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYKM-C)
GA 2021256235
AB    NOVELTY - Producing mesoporous hollow silica material comprises (a) forming polystyrene as core material by adding styrene as precursor and 2,2,-azobis(2-methylpropionamidine) dihydrochloride (AIBA) as thermal initiator to distilled water; and (b) adding the polystyrene to distilled water, and then adding cetyltrimethylammonium chloride as predetermined cationic surfactant and water glass as precursor and stirring for predetermined time, and then adding aqueous ammonia to the stirred solution through reaction at room temperature for predetermined time, followed by filtration, washing, and then firing process at predetermined time and predetermined temperature to form mesoporous hollow silica material.
   USE - The method is useful for producing mesoporous hollow silica material.
   ADVANTAGE - The mesoporous hollow silica material has excellent specific surface area value, and low carbon dioxide adsorption performance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for producing mesoporous hollow silica material using water glass (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), 1-20 ml styrene and AIBA added to 500-1000 ml distilled water. In the step (a), the AIBA and styrene is added in molar ratio of 1:0.1-0.3. The step (a) is carried out by drying at 90-100 degrees C and reacting for 15-24 hours to synthesize polystyrene as core material, after adding the styrene and AIBA. In the step (b), 10-50 ml cetyltrimethylammonium chloride is added to the distilled water, and then 10-50 ml water glass is added at concentration of 0.5-5 wt.%. In the step (b), the aqueous ammonia is added at pH of 8-10. In the step (b), the firing process is performed at 600 degrees C for predetermined time. The method further comprises (c) drying at 70-90 degrees C for 24 hours before the firing process. Preferred Components: The cetyltrimethylammonium chloride has concentration of 0.2-0.5 wt.%. The water glass has concentration of 0.6-0.8 wt.%. The polystyrene has concentration of 9-11 wt.%. The pore structure of the mesoporous hollow silica material is to form hexagonal structure.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2021000542-A1; CN112175226-A; JP2022515100-W; JP7165825-B2
TI Preparing cellulose sponge from bean dregs            comprises performing alkaline pre-cleaning of bean            dregs, subjecting to deep oxidation, separating            cellulose, regenerating cellulose, controlling pores            and drying.
AU CAO S
   SHEN J
   YANG Y
   SHI L
   YUAN H
   FENG X
   MA X
AE SHANGHAI QINGMEI GREEN FOOD GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI TRAMY GREEN FOOD GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
   UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 202104532B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cellulose sponge from bean dregs                comprises (i) performing alkaline pre-cleaning of                bean dregs, then subjecting to deep oxidation to                remove impurities and separating soybean dregs and                cellulose, and (ii) dissolving and regenerating the                extracted cellulose, regulating and controlling the                pores of the cellulose sponge, removing free ions                on the cellulose sponge after the adjustment and                shaping, then cooling and drying the cellulose                sponge.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cellulose                sponge from bean dregs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the production of toxic                gases e.g. hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide when                polyurethane and polystyrene are used as raw                materials for cellulose in normal process, improves                the utilization rate of bean dregs, and overcomes                the problems of high cost for dissolving cellulose                and difficulty in large-scale application.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for preparing cellulose sponge from bean                dregs (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bean dregs are                  leftover materials after making tofu from soy                  milk. Preferred Method: In the step (i): the bean                  dregs are alkaline pre-cleaned at 60-80 degrees C                  for 2-3 hours under stirring condition using                  10-15 wt.% alkaline pre-cleaning solvent                  including sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide,                  sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate or aqueous                  ammonia; the bean dregs are deeply oxidized by                  oxidizing at 60-80 degrees C by stirring for 2-3                  hours using 10-15 wt.% impurity removing solvent                  including sodium sulfate, potassium persulfate,                  ammonium persulfate, sodium percarbonate,                  potassium percarbonate, hydrogen peroxide, sodium                  peroxide or potassium peroxide. The separation of                  soybean dregs and cellulose is carried out by                  either probe ultrasonic treatment, water bath                  ultrasonic treatment, high-speed shearing                  treatment or high-pressure homogenization                  treatment. In the step (ii), pore controlling and                  molding treatment is carried out on cellulose                  sponge using anhydrous sodium sulfate, and                  removing free ions by lime method, hydroxide                  method or sulfide method.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112080461-A; CN112080461-B
TI Preparing animal primary stem cell sterilization culture solution involves taking sheep fetus in second trimester under aseptic conditions, cutting corresponding tissue, and keeping stand in culture medium II.
AU WANG F
   LI J
   LI X
   LUO X
   ZHANG Q
   SUN J
   YAN H
   HOU Y
AE INNER MONGOLIA ACAD AGRIC & ANIMAL HUSBA (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2020C80554
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing animal primary stem cell sterilization culture solution involves taking sheep fetus in second trimester under aseptic conditions, cutting corresponding tissue, and keeping stand in culture medium II for 30 minutes. The tissue mechanical separation method is used to separate, and used culture medium I to culture sheep fetal tissue primary cells under carbon dioxide (CO2) saturated humidity conditions. The cell clones are appeared, and washed with Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline (D-PBS). The cell clones in better condition are picked up, cultured in culture medium III, and cultured and frozen.
   USE - Method for preparing animal primary stem cell sterilization culture solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare animal primary stem cell sterilization culture solution that maintains characteristics of stem cells, and ensures that no bacterial or fungal contamination occurs during process of separating and cultivating primary stem cells from animal tissues.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Compositions: The medium liquid I comprises 1% L-glutamine, 1% non-essential amino acids, 1% sodium pyruvate, trace beta -mercaptoethanol, 5n954 g or 500 u/mL leukemia inhibitory factor (LIF), 5 nanogram/mL basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF), 15% FBS, 200 international unit penicillin, 200 international unit streptomycin, 100 microgram kanamycin, 15 microgram nystatin and 5 microgram amphotericin B. The culture medium III comprises 1% L-glutamine, 1% non-essential amino acids, 1% sodium pyruvate, trace beta -mercaptoethanol, 5 nanogram or 500 u/mL LIF, 5 nanogram/mL bFGF+15% FBS, 100 mL Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's medium (DMEM) low sugar. Preferred Process: The DMEM low sugar is diluted to 100 mL, filtered with 0.22 microliter filter, and stored at 4 degrees C. The primary cells of sheep fetal tissues are cultured with culture medium I at 37 degrees C under 5% CO2 saturated humidity for 48-96 hours, and cultured to 3-4 generations and cryopreserved.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A04B

TI Functionalization of surface of polymeric organic            matrices, involves contacting substrate including            labile hydrogen functions, and subjecting to            temperature sufficient to cause release of blocked            isocyanate functional group.
