   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
GA 2020A44546
AB    NOVELTY - Functionalization of surface of substrate in                supercritical fluid medium involves (i) providing a                substrate including a labile hydrogen functions on                the surface, (ii) contacting the substrate, in a                supercritical fluid, with at least one organic                molecule carrying at least one blocked isocyanate                functional group which can be activated by heating,                and (iii) subjecting the whole to a temperature (G)                sufficient to cause the release of the blocked                isocyanate functional group carried by the                molecule, and the covalent grafting of the molecule                by reaction of the isocyanate functional group with                a labile hydrogen function on the surface of the                substrate.
   USE - Functionalization of surface of substrate in                supercritical fluid such as carbon dioxide, ethane,                propane, butane, dimethyl ether and their mixtures,                excluding water used for functionalizing polymeric                organic matrices.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables functionalization of                surface of substrate by easily removing all the                by-products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The functions                  containing labile hydrogen, carried by the                  substrate, are chosen from hydroxyl, primary                  amine and secondary amine and carboxylic acid                  functions, preferably hydroxyl functions. The                  blocked isocyanate functional group is of                  formula: -NH-C(=0)-B' (i). The blocked isocyanate                  functional group is a urethane function, in                  particular of formula: -NH-C(=0)-B'' (ii). The                  molecule carries, in addition to a blocked                  isocyanate functional group, at least one                  radical-polymerizable ethylenically unsaturated                  group, is chosen from (meth)acrylate groups, in                  particular monomers with ethylenically                  unsaturation(s) such as di(meth)acrylate                  monomers. The compound comprises at least two                  isocyanate functional groups is chosen from                  diisocyanate and triisocyanate compounds, in                  particular from aliphatic diisocyanate such as                  hexamethylenediisocyanate,                  isophoronediisocyanate,                  bis(4-isocyanatocyclohexyl) methane, hydrogenated                  xylylene diisocyanate and m-tetramethylxylylene                  diisocyanate, aromatic diisocyanates such as                  toluene diisocyanate, diphenylmethane                  diisocyanate, xylylene diisocyanate and                  paraphenylene diisocyanate, aliphatic,                  cycloaliphatic and/or aromatic triisocyanate,                  such as isocyanatobiuret of formula (I). The                  compound carries at least one labile hydrogen                  function is water, capable of interacting with                  isocyanate functional groups to form amino                  functional group covalently grafted to the                  surface of the substrate.B' = radical derived from a BH blocking                  agent containing organic compounds comprising                  single labile hydrogen atom, or chosen from                  alcohol, phenol, amido, cyclic amido such as                  caprolactam, oxime such as methyl ethyl                  ketone-oxime or 2-butanone oxime , beta                  -dicarbonyl compounds, pyrazolyl, ester of                  hydroxycinnamic acid or 1-6C alcohols, triazolyl,                  imidazolyl, tetrahydropyrimidinyl, or                  imidazolyl;B'' = -OR radical;R = linear or branched 1-6C, preferably 2-4C                  alkyl;radical -O-N = CR1R2;R1, R2 = H, or linear or branched 1-6C                  alkyl, or radical -NR'R'';R'+R''+N = carries them cyclic lactam                  group;andR'a = linear, branched or cyclic 2-30C                  hydrocarbyl.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The substrate is                  chosen from organic polymeric matrices, in                  particular of cellulose fibers, such as cotton                  and paper, of wood, polymethylmethacrylate,                  ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, partially or                  fully hydrolyzed polyvinyl acetate, polyamide (s)                  such as polyamide 6, polyamide 6-6, polyamide 11                  and polyamide 12, and matrices optionally                  incorporating one or more inorganic fillers, such                  as glass fibers or beads, silica, clays, aluminum                  hydroxide, aluminum oxyhydroxide and talc and                  inorganic substrates, such as glass fiber or                  silica substrates.

PT P
PN CN111303035-A
TI Preparing 3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxylic acid, involves dissolving N-methyl-3-aminopyrazole, reacting with halogen, reacting with potassium difluoromethyl trifluoroborate, quenching and recrystallizing.
AU CHEN H
   YU W
AE XUZHOU SHENGYUAN CHEM CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202059349V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing 3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxylic acid, involves (i) dissolving N-methyl-3-aminopyrazole in water and reacting with halogen to obtain 4-halo-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-amine, (ii) reacting 4-halo-1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-amine with an aqueous solution of sodium nitrite to obtain a diazonium salt, and reacting with potassium difluoromethyl trifluoroborate in the presence of cuprous oxide to obtain 4-halo-3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole, and (iii) exchanging 4-halo-3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole with Grignard reagent, reacting with carbon dioxide after cooling down, quenching and recrystallizing to obtain the product.
   USE - Method for preparing 3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate, with a total three-step yield of up to 64% and a product purity of more than 99.5%, avoids the presence of isomers in traditional processes and has the potential for process amplification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The halogen is chosen from bromine and iodine, and when iodine is used, hydrogen peroxide is added to react. The Grignard reagent is chosen from isopropyl magnesium chloride, isopropyl magnesium chloride-lithium chloride or sec-butyl magnesium chloride-lithium chloride. Preferred Method: The reaction with carbon dioxide in step (iii) is carried out by passing in carbon dioxide gas or a saturated solution of carbon dioxide, and is carried out under the condition of catalytic amount 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene. In step (iii), a mixture of alcoholic solvent and water is used for crystallization. In step (iii), 40% ethanol aqueous solution is used for the recrystallization. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the molar ratio of N-methyl-3-aminopyrazole and halogen is 1:(0.5-1.05), where the iodine elemental substance adopts nearly 0.5 equivalent, and bromine is used as the equivalent reaction. In step (ii), the molar ratio of the added cuprous oxide to 4-halo-1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-amine is (0.05-0.1):1. In step (ii), the molar ratio of 4-halo-1-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-amine, potassium difluoromethyl trifluoroborate and sodium nitrite is 1:(0.98-1.3):(2-2.5). In step (iii), the molar ratio of 4-halo-3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole, Grignard reagent and carbon dioxide is 1:(1.01-1.15):(1-2.5).
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)

PN CN111196715-A; CN111196715-B
TI Preparation of inert alumina chemical filler            involves ball milling activated alumina ball industrial            solid waste hazardous waste, mixing with water and            graphite, adding zirconium sol, pressing, and adding            mineralizing agent for calcining.
AU WANG C
   QI Z
AE YUGAN JINSHI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUGA-Non-standard)
   YUGAN JINSHI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (YUGA-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG ZHONGHE JINSHI NEW MATERIAL TEC (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202048656K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of inert alumina chemical filler                involves (1) adding activated alumina ball                industrial solid waste hazardous waste raw                materials and other raw materials into ball mill,                wet ultrafine grinding, sieving to remove iron,                obtaining nano ceramic mud, (2) mixing the                nano-ceramic mud with water and graphite, stirring,                placing in a steam environment to keep warm,                keeping warm, continuously feeding carbon dioxide                gas and stirring at the same time, adding acid to                control pH, forming aluminum sol, (3) adding                zirconium sol to the aluminum sol obtained in step                (2), drying, obtaining xerogel, (4) granulating the                xerogel, pressing into blank, (5) drying the blank,                and adding mineralizing agent for calcining.
   USE - Preparation of inert alumina chemical                filler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out using easily                available raw materials and economical. The                preparation process is performed under low                sintering temperature, and provides product with                high purity, and improved performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The preparation of                  inert alumina chemical filler involves using raw                  materials in step (1) comprising 3-10 parts mass                  kaolin, 1-3 parts mass barium carbonate, 1-3                  parts mass dolomite, 3-10 parts mass calcium                  bicarbonate, 1-3 parts mass calcined talc, 10-40                  parts mass alpha -alumina powder, 3-20 parts mass                  clay. In step (2), the graphite is added in an                  amount of 5-10 %mass with respect to the mass of                  nano-ceramic mud, and the water is added in an                  amount of 300-500 %mass with respect to the mass                  of the nano-ceramic mud. In step (3), the                  zirconium sol is added in an amount of 5-10 %mass                  with respect to the mass of aluminum sol. In step                  (5), the mineralizing agent is added in an amount                  of 0.5-2 %mass with respect to the mass of the                  blank. Preferred Component: The alpha -alumina                  powder is derived from one or more solid waste                  hazardous waste activated alpha -alumina balls                  produced in the oil refining industry, paper                  industry, hydrogen peroxide treatment industry or                  adsorption industry.

PN CN111097475-A
TI Preparing hydrogen peroxide modified graphite phase carbon nitride nanosheet useful in water decomposition under visible light to produce e.g. hydrogen comprises adding urea graphitic carbon nitride powder, centrifuging and grinding.
AU JIANG H
   WANG W
   LUO Y
   SHENG J
   ZHOU P
AE CHANGZHOU INST TECHNOLOGY (UYCZ-C)
GA 202040429Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen peroxide modified graphite phase carbon nitride nanosheet comprises (a) adding urea graphitic carbon nitride powder to the mixed solvent of hydrogen peroxide and ethanol, stirring and performing ultrasonic treatment to make the solution evenly dispersed to obtain a mixed solution, (b) centrifuging the mixed solution and drying to obtain a solid and (c) grinding the solid into a fine powder, placing in a crucible and performing high-temperature calcination to obtain hydrogen peroxide modified graphite phase carbon nitride.
   USE - The nanosheet is useful in the field of water decomposition under visible light to produce hydrogen, carbon dioxide reduction or organic matter degradation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanosheet shows a significant increase in photocatalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a hydrogen peroxide modified graphite phase carbon nitride nanosheet obtained by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of hydrogen peroxide and ethanol is 2:1. The mass-volume ratio of urea graphitic carbon nitride powder and mixed solvent is 2.67:100. Preferred Method: The ultrasonic treatment after stirring in step (a) is ultrasonic treatment for 12 hours after stirring at 25 degrees C for 30 minutes and the ultrasonic power is 500W. The high-temperature calcination is calcining in a muffle furnace at 600 degrees C for 60 seconds. The heating rate of the high-temperature calcination is 8 degrees C/min. The preparation of urea graphitic carbon nitride comprises placing 25 g urea in a covered ceramic crucible, controlling the heating rate of the muffle furnace to 8 degrees C/min, heating the crucible to 550 degrees C for 3 hours, cooling to room temperature and grinding the solid material in the crucible to obtain a pale yellow solid, namely urea graphitic carbon nitride powder. The centrifugation in the step (b) is at 6000 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes.

TI Preparing single atoms by surface immobilization            used in various electrocatalytic reactions, supporting            the active metal complex on the organic base material,            and further carrying out carbon bond reconstruction            under heating condition.
AU CHEN Y
   HOU Q
   HUANG Y
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2020340761
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing single atoms by surface                immobilization involves supporting the active metal                complex on the organic base material. The carbon                bond reconstruction is further carried out under                the heating condition of 200-450 degrees C to fix                it on the surface of the base material and                achieving the controllable, simple and effective                preparation.
   USE - Method for preparing single atoms by surface                immobilization used in various electrocatalytic                reactions, such as hydrogen evolution, oxygen                evolution, oxygen reduction, carbon dioxide                reduction and nitrogen reduction, can also be                applied to various chemical catalytic reactions,                such as C-H, C-O, C-X bond activation, C-C bond                coupling, carbon chain rearrangement and                isomerization reactions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare single atoms                which shows excellent performance in                electrocatalysis and thermal catalysis, and has                broad and excellent industrial application                prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal                  ions (such as chromium, manganese, iron, cobalt,                  nickel, copper, zinc, palladium and platinum) and                  organic ligand coordination compounds. The                  substrate material contains organic groups, has a                  large accessible surface, such as lamellar or                  macroporous structure of MOF, COF, porous polymer                  or other organic materials. The active metal                  complex is dissolved in a certain solvent, when                  the MOF and COF are prepared by the solvothermal                  method, the complex is loaded on the generated                  metal organic frameworks (MOFs) and covalent                  organic frameworks (COFs)F in situ. The organic                  ligand is mainly composed of a polydentate fatty                  amine, aromatic amines, pyridines and other                  nitrogen heterocyclic ligands, polydentate                  nitrogen heterocyclic ligands, cyclophosphanes,                  crown ether ether ligands and organic phosphine                  ligands. The MOFs that can be used are mainly                  metal ions such as manganese, iron, cobalt,                  nickel, copper, zinc and carboxylic acid ligands                  (such as: 1,3,5-trimellitic acid, bivalent                  phthalic acid, 1,3,5-tris (4-carboxyphenyl)                  benzene, 1,4-phthalic acid, 3,3 ',                  5,5'-biphenyltetracarboxylic acid) and imidazole                  ligands. The COF that can be used is mainly the                  imine COF synthesized by condensation of amine                  and aldehyde. The MXn center of the metal complex                  remains unchanged during the pyrolysis process,                  and the organic group connected to the heteroatom                  restructures and bonds with the substrate to                  connect and fix, and achieved controllable                  preparation of the metal active site. Preferred                  Conditions: The ligand has 1-4 nitrogen, oxygen,                  sulfur and phosphorus under the heating condition                  of 200-450 degrees C, and carbon-carbon bonds or                  carbon-heterobonds can be reconstructed.                  Preferred Process: The complex is loaded on the                  surface of the material by the dipping method and                  controlling the loading concentration and                  preparation conditions of the metal complex,                  monodisperse metal complex sites are formed on                  the surface of the base material with a loading                  of 1-20 wt.%.

TI Activated rubber powder comprises waste rubber powder, swelling agent, deodorant, and reinforcing agent.
AU TAN S
   BAI L
   YANG D
AE YAAN HEXIN RUBBER & PLASTIC CO LTD (YAAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020345014
AB    NOVELTY - Activated rubber powder comprises waste rubber powder, swelling agent, deodorant, and reinforcing agent.
   USE - Activated rubber powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The activated rubber powder has modification effect on asphalt, and a small amount of carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and other gasses are discharged during the use process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing activated rubber powder, which involves:
   (A) using waste rubber as raw material to prepare 30-100 mesh rubber powder;
   (B) adding swelling agent, deodorant and reinforcing agent to the 30-100 mesh powder obtained, and mixing;
   (C) putting mixture into a desulfurization tank, and then passing a certain pressure of steam into the desulfurization tank, releasing pressure and exhausting to obtain modified rubber powder when the pressure in the desulfurization tank reaches 3.5 Mpa; and
   (D) cooling modified rubber powder, reducing the temperature to below 40 degrees C, using vibrating screen with a 20-mesh screen to remove the agglomerated rubber powder, and measuring and packaging to obtain the finished activated rubber powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of rubber powder and swelling agent is 8-15:1, the mass ratio of rubber powder and reinforcing agent is 185-190:1, and 0.017-0.03ml of deodorant is added per 1kg of rubber powder. The mass ratio of the rubber powder to the swelling agent is 12:1, and the mass ratio of the rubber powder to the reinforcing agent is 180:1, and 0.023 ml of deodorant is added per 1 kg of rubber powder. The particle size of the rubber powder is 30-100 mesh. The mass ratio of the rubber powder and the swelling agent is 8-15:1, the mass ratio of the rubber powder and the reinforcing agent is 185-190:1, and 0.017-0.03ml of deodorant is added per 1kg of rubber powder. The mass ratio of the rubber powder to the swelling agent is 12:1, and the mass ratio of the rubber powder to the reinforcing agent is 180:1, and 0.023 ml of deodorant is added per 1 kg of rubber powder. The reinforcing agent is a resin reinforcing material for rubber. The deodorant is a plant-based extraction fragrance. Preferred Process: The rubber powder ais prepared by crushing waste rubber and separate the impurities and rubber in the crushed material, pulverizing the rubber block in step a into powder and adding to a blender for mixing, using grinder to finish the mixed rubber powder to obtain fine powder. The fine powder is subjected to air separation, fiber removal, and magnetic separation and sieve out the required mesh powder.

TI Judging blast furnace operation furnace type            involves judging the height position of the soft            melting zone at the cooling wall in the furnace            according to the temperature range of each section of            the cooling wall in the furnace.
AU ZHANG Z
   YIN T
   ZHENG H
   ZHU Y
   LIN W
   HUANG P
   LI X
AE WUHAN STEEL CO LTD (WSGC-C)
GA 202018823M
AB    NOVELTY - Judging blast furnace operation furnace type                involves judging the height position of the soft                melting zone at the cooling wall in the furnace                according to the temperature range of each section                of the cooling wall in the furnace, determining the                shape of the soft melt zone detected by the                infrared temperature measurement on the top of the                blast furnace or the value of the carbon dioxide                content at each radial point in the furnace throat                gas to determine the shape of the soft melt zone                and judging the blast furnace operation furnace                based on the height position of the soft melt belt                in the furnace and the shape of the soft melt                belt.
   USE - Method for judging blast furnace operation                furnace type.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieve the goals of high output,                low consumption, high quality, and long life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The judgment is based                  on the temperature range of each section of the                  cooling wall in the furnace, specifically: the                  average temperature of the cooling wall is lower                  than 60 degrees C and the cooling wall                  temperature range is different If the temperature                  is less than 10 degrees C. The cooling wall is                  located in the drip zone. The average temperature                  of the cooling wall reaches 90-150 degrees C and                  the temperature of the cooling wall is in the                  range of 70-200 degrees C. The cooling wall is                  located at the root of the melt zone. The average                  temperature of the stave is high, the                  circumferential direction is uniform, and the                  area where the average temperature gradually                  decreases from about 150-70 degrees C C from                  bottom to top is a block zone.

TI LM- BPNN based IWF calculating method, involves training LM-BPNN model by correcting weight of LM-BPNN and threshold to reduce error function along gradient direction, and adjusting parameters and retrain.
AU PEI Z
   HAN G
   SHI T
   QIU R
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 202015304R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating atmospheric remote sensing training data by physical process, where the atmospheric remote sensing training data includes temperature, humidity, pressure and IWF value data. The training data is modified to unify a training data. The training data is inputted into a LM-BPNN model for training. The LM-BPNN model is trained by correcting a weight of the LM-BPNN and threshold to reduce an error function along a gradient direction. Parameters and retrain are adjusted when error satisfy accuracy requirement.
   USE - LM- BPNN based IWF calculating method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calculating IWF value and replacing traditional physical process of large so as to satisfy future carbon dioxide laser radar satellite data reverse in time requirements.

AB    NOVELTY - Preserving soft-seed pomegranates in modified                atmosphere library involves placing soft seed                pomegranates into warehouse, cleaning up the inside                of the modified atmosphere fresh-keeping storehouse                with alcohol, and ventilating and dryiing, placing                soft seed pomegranate in the modified atmosphere                store, adjusting the oxygen concentration in the                modified atmosphere storage to 3%, carbon dioxide                concentration to 3%, and nitrogen concentration to                94%. The pressure in the warehouse is adjusted to                10-70 millimeter mercury through the air pressure                controller and the temperature in the warehouse is                reduced to 2-3 degrees C through air conditioning.                The collection box containing soft-seed                pomegranates is reduced. The soft-seed pomegranates                are waked up one week after they are released and                sold in their entirety or transferred to a cold                storage and sold slowly.
   USE - Method for preserving soft-seed pomegranates                in modified atmosphere library.

The control room is equipped with a                  nitrogen generator, a gas pressure controller, a                  carbon dioxide remover, a temperature and                  humidity detector, a pressure detector, and a                  control panel. The portion of the nitrogen                  generator,air pressure controller, carbon dioxide                  remover, temperature and humidity detector, and                  air pressure detector is located in the storage                  room. There is a sealing ring, and the storage                  room and the control room are provided with an                  openable door body which is characterized in that                  a buffer device is installed on the balance pipe.                  The buffer device consists of two circles                  symmetrically arranged left and right.

PN CN110643433-A
TI Extracting Dendrobium flower essential oil, comprises e.g. washing fresh Dendrobium flower, mixing with distilled water, breaking or homogenizing, pre-freezing, separating, extracting using anhydrous ethanol, filtering, and evaporating.
AU ZHOU J
   WANG Y
   WANG B
   LI S
AE WUHAN YUNJU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
   UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 202004041J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Dendrobium flower essential oil, comprises (i) taking fresh Dendrobium flower, washing and removing impurities, then adding distilled water, fully stirring and allowing to stand, (ii) breaking or homogenizing obtained mixture of Dendrobium flower and distilled water using a high pressure homogenizer or wall-breaking machine to obtain material liquid, (iii) pre-freezing the obtained material liquid, collecting the liquid discharged from the condenser, and separating by oil-water separator, (iv) extracting the dry material using anhydrous ethanol or supercritical carbon dioxide, filtering, rotary evaporating the obtained filter liquid to remove ethanol, and collecting liquid containing Dendrobium flower essential oil, and (v) uniformly mixing the obtained essential oil to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting Dendrobium flower essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high accelerates extraction rate, simple extracting technique and high index material utilization; and improves extracting rate of essential oil. The essential oil has high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the material-liquid mass ratio between Dendrobium flower and distilled water is 1:4-10, and the time for allowing to stand is 0.5-1.5 hours. In step (iv), the mass ratio between drying material and anhydrous ethanol is 1:2-5. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the homogenizing pressure of the high-pressure homogenizer is 20-80 MPa, and the number of homogenization times is 1-4 times; the speed of the wall breaking machine is 20000-40000 revolutions/minute, and the number of times of wall breaking is 1-3 times. In step (iii), pre-freezing temperature is -50 to -30 degrees C and pre-freezing time is 1-3 hours. In step (iii), freezing and drying technique parameter: the vacuum degree of the drying chamber is 50-80 Pa, the cold trap temperature is -60 to -40 degrees C, freezing and drying time is 18-54 hours, freezing and drying the heating plate temperature is 40-60 degrees C. : In step (iv), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction has an extraction pressure of 15-40 MPa, extraction temperature of 25-50 degrees C, and a carbon dioxide extraction gas flow rate of 10-25 ml/minute. In step (iv), extracting time is 1-4 hours. In step (iv), the rotary evaporation temperature of the rotary evaporator is 50-70 degrees C, the rotary evaporation time is 60-120 minutes, the volatile ethanol during the rotary evaporation can be recycled after being condensed and recovered.

PT P
PN CN110564699-A; CN110564699-B
TI New Muscovy duck adenovirus strain useful in            Muscovy duck adenovirus inactivated vaccine for            preventing and treating Muscovy duck adenovirus            disease.
AU LUO J
   CHANG W
   WANG X
   LIU N
   GUO C
   LI C
AE SHANDONG SINDER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   QINGDAO XINDE PHARM CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6640D
AB    NOVELTY - Muscovy duck adenovirus strain (CCTCC NO:                V201952), is new.
   USE - The strain is useful in Muscovy duck                adenovirus inactivated vaccine (claimed) for                preventing and treating Muscovy duck adenovirus                disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The method overcomes the loss of the Muscovy                duck breeding industry due to reduced Muscovy                duck's adenovirus disease, thus it is                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) Muscovy duck adenovirus inactivated                vaccine, prepared by using Muscovy duck adenovirus                strain as an inactivated antigen;
   (2) preparing Muscovy duck adenovirus                inactivated vaccine, comprising inoculating the                pre-prepared Muscovy duck adenovirus strain into                cultured monolayer Leghorn male hepatoma (LMH)                cells, placing at 37 degrees C under 5%                carbon-dioxide for 72-96 hours, collecting virus                fluid when the cytopathy reaches more than 80%,                adding 10% formaldehyde solution into the collected                virus solution and mixing thoroughly and obtaining                inactivated antigen solution at 4 degrees C for 36                hours, mixing the inactivated antigen solution and                the vaccine adjuvant and emulsifying; and
   (3) preparing the strain.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  strain comprises inoculating LMH cells with the                  third generation embryonic allantoic fluid of                  Muscovy duck adenovirus, passing LMH cells for                  5-9 generations, until more than 80% of the cells                  appear diseased, measuring the virus content of                  the 5-9 generations of the strain and choosing                  the strain with the highest titer as the virus                  species. Preferred Method: The culture method of                  LMH cells used for inoculation of strain is:                  taking LMH cells growing to a dense monolayer and                  discarding the original culture solution, washing                  the cells with sterile phosphate-buffered saline                  (PBS), digesting with 0.25% trypsin, discarding                  the trypsin solution, adding 8% fetal calf serum                  in Dulbecco's modified eagle medium:nutrient                  mixture F-12 (DMEM/F-12) cell culture medium and                  mixing the cells, passing the cells at a ratio of                  1:4-1:5 and culturing to a cell monolayer at 37                  degrees C and 5% carbon-dioxide. The collected                  virus solution is introduced into an inactivation                  bottle, and 10% formaldehyde solution is measured                  in and mixed thoroughly, where the final                  concentration of formaldehyde is 0.1%, after                  adding the formaldehyde solution, introducing                  into another inactivated bottle, inactivating at                  4 degrees C for 36 hours to obtain an inactivated                  antigen solution. The method comprises performing                  sterility test and inactivation test after the                  preparation of inactivated antigen solution,                  where during the inactivation test, 10-fold                  dilution of the inactivated antigen solution is                  used, 4 wells of LMH cells with a single layer on                  the cell plate, 0.2 ml per well, supplement the                  maintenance solution to 2 ml, simultaneously                  setting the inactivated virus solution as a                  negative control, inoculating the inactivated                  virus solution as a positive control and                  incubating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon-dioxide                  incubator and culture is collected repeatedly                  frozen and thawed for 1 generation and then                  cultured for 120 hours to observe whether                  cytopathic effects occur. The vaccine adjuvant is                  SEPPIC 71R oil adjuvant, and the inactivated                  antigen solution and SEPPIC 71R oil adjuvant are                  emulsified at a ratio of 3:7. The emulsification                  process is as follows: first starting a motor and                  stirring SEPPIC 71R oil adjuvant at low speed for                  2 minutes, simultaneously slowly inactivating the                  antigen solution and emulsifying at 10000                  revolutions/minute for 15 minutes to obtain oil                  emulsion inactivated vaccine. Preferred                  Components: The strain has 76.6% nucleotide                  homology with the duck adenovirus type 2 GR                  strain Hexon gene published in the GenBank                  database.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant; Virucide.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Muscovy duck adenovirus inactivated                vaccine.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN110395769-A
TI Preparation of high purity 3R delafossite structure microcrystalline material used e.g. for degrading pollutants involves dissolving copper nitrate trihydrayte and iron nitrate nonahydrate, reacting with n-propionaldehyde, and drying.
AU LIU Q
   ZHAO Z
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 201992730D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high purity 3R delafossite structure microcrystalline material comprises taking copper nitrate trihydrayte and iron nitrate nonahydrate at molar ratio of 1:1, adding deionized water, magnetically stirring, and dissolving; dripping sodium hydroxide solution, sonicating, and magnetically stirring; adding n-propionaldehyde and continuously magnetic stirring; filling into reaction kettle, reacting at 100-180 degrees C for 6-24 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, taking out reaction product, washing, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing high purity 3R delafossite structure microcrystalline material for photocatalytic decomposition of water to produce hydrogen, degrading pollutants, photocatalytic reducing carbon dioxide, transparent conductive oxides, and ferromagnetic materials, and photoelectric conversion functional materials and devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and has easy control of process parameters, no pollution, and high yield. The microcrystalline material has complete grain development and uniform particle size distribution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of copper nitrate trihydrayte is 0.5-0.8 mol/L. The concentration of sodium hydroxide solution is 1.5-4 mol/L. Preferred Method: The washing is done by using deionized water, dilute nitric acid, and absolute ethanol. The drying is done at 60-80 degrees C for 10-12 hours.

   USE - The method is for comprehensive utilization of polygeneration chemicals by coke tail gas grading.
   ADVANTAGE - The process achieves high temperature conversion for convenient material separation and realizes energy-saving environmental protection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises using controllable multi-stage pressure swing adsorption process for separating and purifying gas to obtain mixed gas of H2/nitrogen (N2) and high purity H2 (with H2 volume content of greater than or equal to 90%), when nitrogen volume content of the gas after acid removal is as high as 40-50% and the component is complex, conducting controllable pressure swing adsorption using greater than or equal to 4 kinds of different selective multi-pore regenerated adsorbent, conducting layered filling and bed layer filling, using more than grade 2 fractionation removal, obtaining adjustable composition ratio of H2/N2 mixed gas, and obtaining H2 of different purity grades, where purity can be controlled at 80-99.99 vol.%. The oxygen quantity supplemented is controlled at 0.01-0.28 Nm3 oxygen/Nm3 tail gas. The water vapor amount supplemented is controlled at 0-500 kg steam/104 Nm3 tail gas. The temperature of high temperature conversion is controlled at 900-1300 degrees C.

PT P
PN US2019233954-A1; US11299811-B2
TI Electrochemical reactor for use with electrolyte that generates gaseous products, comprises liquid or solid electrolyte, electrolyte channel sandwiched between two gas channels, two electrode layers separating electrolyte channel.
AU TACCONI N S
   CHANMANEE W
   DENNIS B
   RAJESHWAR K
AE UNIV TEXAS SYSTEM (TEXA-C)
GA 201966608H
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical reactor comprises liquid or solid electrolyte, electrolyte channel sandwiched between two gas channels, two electrode layers separating electrolyte channel and two gas channels (102) respectively, where each of two electrode layers is hydrophilic on catalyst side and hydrophobic on gas side.
   USE - Electrochemical reactor for use with electrolyte that generates gaseous products.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical reactor improves performance of electrode layer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for producing ethylene from carbon dioxide, which comprises electrochemical reactor; and
   (2) a method for electrodepositing hybrid copper oxide copper bromide film on gas diffusion layer support material, which involves providing water-soluble copper solution, providing complexing agent, adjusting pH of solution to target simultaneous deposition of cubic nanostructures composed of copper, oxygen and halogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of electrochemical reactor.
   Gas channels (102)
   Electrolyte channel (108)
   Gas inlet (110)
   Electrolyte inlet (114)
   Electrolyte outlet (116)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrode layer comprises electrically conducting porous layer and catalyst on catalyst side. The electrically conducting porous layer is made from a hydrophobic material. The hydrophobic material is polytetrafluoroethylene. The catalyst is electrodeposited on catalyst side of electrically conducting porous layer. The catalyst can be platinum, ruthenium, copper, titanium, nickel, or copper bromine. The catalyst is formed from hybrid copper oxide copper bromide film. The solution comprises copper sulfate and sodium bromide. The complexing agent comprises lactic acid. The electrolyte is anion conducting electrolyte. The electrolyte is cation conducting electrolyte. The electrolyte is sodium hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate, potassium hydroxide, or sodium sulfate. Preferred Conditions: The adjusted pH of solution is 7-9. The ratio of molar lactic acid to cupric cation is about 10. The liquid electrolyte is pumped continuously into electrolyte channel of electrochemical reactor. Preferred Process: The method further involves electrodepositing film at about (-)0.4 volt for 15-20 minutes, adding sodium bromide-containing solution to cathode compartment and continuing electrode position for 5 minutes.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110028591-A
TI Preparing cellulose 3-(2-hydroxyphenyl)propionate comprises e.g. mixing cellulose, organic alkali and organic solvent, reacting and adding dihydrocoumarin to the cellulose solution, reacting and adding lower aliphatic alcohol or water.
AU XIE H
   GUO Y
   ZHENG Q
   GUO G
   ZHAO T
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201965129K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cellulose 3-(2-hydroxyphenyl)propionate comprises (i) mixing cellulose, organic alkali and organic solvent, and reacting the mixture in a reaction vessel filled with a carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-10 MPa and a temperature of 30-100 degrees C for 0.1-12 hours to obtain cellulose solution, (ii) adding dihydrocoumarin to the cellulose solution, and reacting at 30-150 degrees C for 0.1-48 hours to obtain solution of cellulose 3-(2-hydroxyphenyl)propionate, and (iii) adding 1-4C lower aliphatic alcohol or water into a solution of cellulose 3-(2-hydroxyphenyl)propionate, and filtering the mixed solution, purifying filtering the obtained solid mixture, washing with 1-4C lower aliphatic alcohol or water, then purifying and drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cellulose 3-(2-hydroxyphenyl)propionate.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has adjustable properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cellulose is microcrystalline cellulose, alpha -cellulose, cotton, wood pulp, bamboo pulp and/or plant cellulose separated from agricultural and forestry lignocellulosic waste, and the cellulose is 6-(hydroxymethyl)tetrahydro-2H-pyran-3,4-diol compound of formula (I). In step (i): the mass concentration of the cellulose in the mixture is 1-20 wt.%, the mass concentration of the organic alkali is 0.5-30 wt.% and the balance is organic solvent. The organic alkali is e.g. 2,3,4,6,7,8-hexahydropyrrolo(1,2-a)pyrimidine, 2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepine, 2,3,4,6,7,8-hexahydropyrrolo(1,2-a)pyrimidine, 8-thia-1,10-diazatricyclo(7.4.0.0(2,7))trideca-2,4,6,9-tetraene, (2'E,N'E)-1,1'-(naphthalene-1,8-diyl)bis(1,2',3,3-tetramethylguanidine), guanidine compound of formula (II), formimidamide compound of formula (IV), and imidazolidin-2-imine compound of formula (III). The organic solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methylpyrrolidone, tetramethyl hydrazine, N,N-dimethylimidazolidinone, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-diethylacetamide, 2-pyrrolidone, 2-azacyclohexanone, epsilon -caprolactam, N,N-dimethylpropenylurea, sulfolane and/or piperylene sulfone. The molar ratio of the dihydrocoumarin and hydroxy group on the glucose unit in the cellulose solution is 0.5-10:1. In step (iii): the volume ratio of 1-4C lower aliphatic alcohol or water to the solution of cellulose 3-(2-hydroxyphenyl)propionate is 0.5-10:1. The cellulose 3-(2-hydroxyphenyl)propionate is 4-(2-hydroxyphenyl)butan-2-one. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide pressure is 0.5-3 MPa, the temperature is 40-60 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-4 hours. In step (ii): the reaction temperature is 80-130 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-10 hours.x = less than 50-1000;R1 = 1-6C alkyl;R2, R3 and R4 = methyl groups;R5 = H, 1-6C alkyl, 3-10C ethoxy polyethylene glycol, methoxy polyethylene glycol, hydroxylated 1-6C alkyl or 4-8C polyethylene glycol;R6-R9 = hydrogen, methyl or ethyl;n = 1 or 2; andm = 1-6.

PN CN109908903-A
TI Nickel-based catalyst preparation involves mixing lignin with an aqueous solution of additive under heating and stirring to obtain pyrolytic carbon, and then removing unreacted additive in pyrolytic carbon.
AU LU X
   XU Y
   TU W
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   UNIV DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDU-C)
GA 201957351F
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel-based catalyst preparation involves mixing lignin with an aqueous solution of additive under heating and stirring to obtain pyrolytic carbon, and then removing the unreacted additive in the pyrolytic carbon to obtain high specific surface area activated carbon. The high specific surface area activated carbon is immersed in the nickel source solution, and then dried and vacuum calcined to obtain a nickel-based catalyst. The additive is potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, phosphoric acid, zinc chloride, sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate. The vacuum pyrolysis is performed at 600-900 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing nickel-based catalyst used as a support in carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction or carbon monoxide hydrogenation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the nickel-based catalyst in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with large specific surface area, high catalytic efficiency and low reduction temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The additive and the lignin are provided in a mass ratio of 0.5-3:1. The nickel source is nickel nitrate hexahydrate, nickel chloride hexahydrate or nickel acetate. The lignin is an alkali lignin. The impregnation is performed by mixing activated carbon with nickel source solution under heating and stirring to obtain slurry.

TI Processing waste germ into culture solution comprises disinfecting waste germ using aqueous disinfectant, adding water and heating, adding yeast, stirring, sealing, fermenting, adding brown sugar, sealing, filtering and sterilizing.
AU FANG Y
   WANG P
   ZHANG X
   RUI L
AE MAANSHAN QINMIAO AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MAAN-Non-standard)
GA 201902212B
AB    NOVELTY - Processing waste germ into culture solution comprises (i) adding waste germ to be processed into aqueous disinfectant, disinfecting, washing and placing in a steel tank, (ii) adding water into the steel tank and heating to 25 degrees C, (iii) adding yeast into the steel tank and stirring uniformly, (iv) sealing and fermenting the steel tank for 7 days, (v) opening the steel tank, adding brown sugar and sealing the steel tank for 2 days, (vi) passing the filtrate in the steel tank into a filter tank, filtering and adding nitric oxide into the resulting filtrate, and (vii) introducing carbon dioxide under high-pressure of 0.1 MPa and sterilizing.

TI Heavy metal coagulating treatment method using phosphogypsum and red mud acid involves mixing phosphogypsum and red mud, adding additive and modifier, mixing and grinding, sintering to obtain clinker, and performing water dissolution.
AU CHEN X
   WANG H
   YANG H
   WANG Q
   HUANG B
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201895524V
AB    NOVELTY - A heavy metal coagulating treatment method using phosphogypsum and red mud acid comprises mixing phosphogypsum and red mud, adding additive and modifier, mixing and grinding, sintering to obtain clinker, performing water dissolution, carrying out solid-liquid separation, taking precipitate, performing flotation, separating out sulfide, sintering, reacting flue gas with vanadium pentoxide, performing concentrated sulfuric acid absorption, obtaining sulfuric acid, taking liquid, adding carbon dioxide, precipitating, drying precipitate, obtaining aluminum hydroxide, adding sodium hydroxide and water, stirring, boiling, reacting, cooling, adding hydrochloric acid solution, regulating pH value to 2.3-2.6, stirring, and heating and aging to obtain heavy metal processing coagulant.
   USE - Heavy metal coagulating treatment method using phosphogypsum and red mud acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high large additional constituent utilization rate; prepared aluminum hydroxide has high purity; and coagulant has low production cost with good heavy metal removing effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The heavy metal coagulating treatment method using phosphogypsum and red mud acid comprises mixing 1-1.2:1-1.5 weight ratio of phosphogypsum and red mud, adding additive comprising 1:1 molar ratio of sodium oxide and alumina+iron(III) oxide, adding 14-18 wt.% modifier, mixing and grinding, sintering at 1000-1400 degrees C for 1-4 hours to obtain clinker, performing water dissolution, carrying out solid-liquid separation according to liquid-solid volume ratio of 2-6:1, taking precipitate, performing flotation, separating out sulfide, sintering in 35-45% oxygen-rich atmosphere at 900-1300 degrees C for 2-5 hours, reacting flue gas with vanadium pentoxide, performing concentrated sulfuric acid absorption, obtaining sulfuric acid, taking liquid, adding carbon dioxide, precipitating, drying precipitate at 90-120 degrees C to water content of 0.3%, obtaining aluminum hydroxide, adding 50-55 wt.% sodium hydroxide according to mass ratio of 1.5-1.8:1, adding and water, stirring, boiling, reacting, cooling, adding 20-30 wt.% hydrochloric acid solution, regulating pH value to 2.3-2.6, stirring, heating at 50-70 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours, and aging for 2-4 days to obtain heavy metal processing coagulant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The red mud is red mud produced by Bayer process for producing alumina; additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or sodium hydroxide; and modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue.

GA 201888589A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing activated carbon for super capacitor                comprises (i) subjecting the carbonized material to                a first gas activation to obtain a primary                activated material, activating the first gas at                800-950 degrees C for 30-120 minutes, (ii)                subjecting the primary activated material obtained                in the step (i) to a second gas activation, and                cooling to obtain secondary activated material,                activating the second gas at 800-1000 degrees C for                30-180 minutes, (iii) sequentially grinding,                washing and drying the secondary activated material                obtained in the step (ii) to obtain activated                carbon for a super-capacitor, where the first gas                activating activator is independent of the second                gas activating activator the ground includes at                least one of water vapor, carbon dioxide, air, and                ammonia, and the two gas activation activators are                different.

When the first gas activating                  activator is carbon dioxide, the second gas                  activating activator is water vapor. When the                  first gas activating activator is a mixture of                  air and water, the second gas activating                  activator is carbon dioxide. The step (i) is a                  method for preparing a carbonized material                  comprises carbonizing a biomass raw material                  and/or a coal raw material to obtain a carbonized                  material. The carbonization temperature is                  700-1000 degrees C, and the carbonization time is                  60-180 minutes. The temperature of the                  carbonization is reached by the temperature rise,                  and the rate of the temperature rise is less than                  or equal to 15 degrees C/minutes.    LI Z
   YAN L
   WANG Y
   LU P
   JIN J
   BIAN Z
   TANG G
AE NAT TOBACCO QUALITY SUPERVISION & INSPEC (CNTA-C)
GA 201874727C
AB    NOVELTY - A tobacco N-nitroso nornicotine optical                isomers separation and determination method                involves weighing 0.25-0.5 g smoke sample, adding                5-10 ml methanol solution containing 0.5% acetic                acid, ultrasonically extracting and filtering to                obtain a solution to be tested, The solution to be                tested is subjected to ultra-high performance                convergent chromatography tandem mass spectrometry                analysis, where the chromatographic conditions is                mobile phase A is carbon dioxide, mobile phase B is                methanol, and compensation solvent is methanol                solution containing 0.1% formic acid, and using the                external standard curve method to quantitatively                calculate the content of (S)-N-nitroso nornicotine                and (R)-N-nitroso nornicotine in the sample.
   USE - Method for separation and determination of                N-nitroso nornicotine optical isomers in                tobacco.

TI Semiconductor photoelectrode used in photocatalyst device, comprises semiconductor film which generates electron and hole from target substance by light irradiation, current collector layer which removes electron or hole, and catalyst layer.
AU ONO Y
   UZUMAKI Y
   KOMATSU T
AE NIPPON TELEGRAPH & TELEPHONE CORP (NITE-C)
GA 2018484012
AB    NOVELTY - A semiconductor photoelectrode (100) consists of a semiconductor film (2) which exhibits a catalytic function, triggers the chemical reaction of a target substance, and generates an electron and a hole by light irradiation, a current collector layer (3) which removes electron or hole, and is formed in a portion of the semiconductor film, and a catalyst layer (5) formed on the whole surface of the semiconductor film and the current collector layer.
   USE - Semiconductor photoelectrode used in photocatalyst device for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide, formic acid, methanol, or methane by light energy, preferably solar light energy, and generating hydrogen by photolysis of water.
   ADVANTAGE - The semiconductor photoelectrode has excellent durability even under light irradiation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the semiconductor photoelectrode.
   Semiconductor film (2)
   Current collector layer (3)
   Metal wiring (4)
   Catalyst layer (5)
   Semiconductor photoelectrode (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The semiconductor film is a laminate of n-type gallium nitride and aluminum gallium nitride. The current collector layer includes a structure in which circular or regular 3 or more polygons are arranged. The structure is a honeycomb structure of a regular hexagon. The catalyst layer contains a metal oxide chosen from oxide of nickel, cobalt, ruthenium, iridium, and iron. The current collector layer includes a titanium layer, an aluminum layer, an indium layer, a nickel layer, a laminate of titanium and aluminum, a laminate of titanium and nickel, and a laminate of titanium, aluminum, titanium and platinum, in order. The target substance is an oxidation target substance or a reduction target substance.

PN CN108142861-A; CN108142861-B
TI Preparation of flavored fish sausage by airflow crushing taro coarse powder, preparing fish granules, adding e.g. taro fine powder, mixing, adding to protein casing, kneading, radio frequency treating resistance heating, and cooling.
AU GUO Z
   WANG J
   ZHANG Y
   SUN S
   LIN X
   LIN H
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 2018474566
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of flavored fish sausage comprises adding 60 mesh taro coarse powder into airflow supermicron mill crusher, crushing, and sieving through 400-600 mesh sieve; pre-processing fresh fish, washing fish meat with neutral electrolytic water at 20 degrees C or less for 2-3 times, dehydrating, subjecting to high density carbon dioxide treatment, and crushing to obtain fish granules; and adding 1-1.5 wt.% salt, monosodium glutamate, pepper, vinegar, cooking wine, disodium 5'-ribonucleotide and taro fine powder for 3-5 minutes, continuously mixing for 5-8 minutes, adding to protein casing, kneading at 0-5 degrees C, subjecting to radio frequency treatment, performing resistance heating, cooling, and packaging.
   USE - Method for preparation of flavored fish sausage.
   ADVANTAGE - The flavored fish sausage has low salt content, and good gel strength, softness and flavor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The airflow supermicron mill crusher is done at airflow pressure of 0.5-1 MPa and feeding speed of 80-110 kg/hour. The fish pre-processing is done by washing fish with primary water, removing scales, head, tail and internal organs, washing with secondary water, and removing fish bone and skin. The washing with neutral electrolytic water is done through electrolysis water processor at current of 3 A and voltage of 4.8-5.2 V until chlorine ion concentration is 0.015 mol/L, and spiral filtering. The high density carbon dioxide treatment is done at 32-37 degrees C and at 20-30 MPa for 12-15 minutes. The radio frequency treatment is done at 70-80 degrees C and at 25-28 MHz. The resistance heating is done at 90 degrees C for 1-2 minutes, and applying voltage gradient of 4 V/cm for 5-8 minutes. Preferred Components: The flavored fish sausage raw material comprises fish granules, 1-1.5 wt.% salt, 0.5-1.5 wt.% monosodium glutamate, 0.1-0.6 wt.% pepper, 0.5-1.6 wt.% vinegar, 1-2.4 wt.% cooking wine, 0.04-0.10 wt.% disodium 5'-ribonucleotide and 25-30 wt.% taro fine powder.

PN CN108085271-A; CN108085271-B
TI Preparing excrement bacterial solution for removing mercury, thallium, and arsenic ions from human excrement, involves adding sterile sodium chloride solution to excrement sample, adding lithium-based sepiolite, and stirring uniformly.
AU CHEN T
   CAI X
   ZHANG Z
   MA D
AE GUANGDONG LONGSEE MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201845132Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing an excrement bacterial solution involves adding the sterile sodium chloride solution to the excrement sample, adding the lithium-based sepiolite, and stirring uniformly to obtain waste excrement slurry. The excrement slurry is filtered, the filtrate is taken, stirring is performed at intervals of 1800-2200 rpm for 3-6 minutes every 0.8-1.2 hours, under the protection of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, helium, neon, or argon gas, and the excrement bacteria slurry is obtained after 4-7 hours. The excrement bacteria slurry is filtered to obtain filtrate to give the crude excrement bacteria solution, the crude excrement bacteria solution is centrifuged, the precipitate is collected, and resuspended to obtain a colorless, odorless excrement bacterial solution of mercury-free, antimony, and arsenic ions.
   USE - Method for preparing an excrement bacterial solution for removing mercury, thallium, and arsenic ions from human excrement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The lithium-based sepiolite is prepared by adding water and lithium metaphosphate to sepiolite mineral, heating to 70-80 degrees C, adding sodium carbonate, stirring for 2.5-3.5 hours, allowing the mixture to stand for 20-30 hours, washing with deionized water, recovering the solid, and drying to obtain lithium-based sepiolite. The primary filtration is carried out by removing the large particulate matter by filtering the excrement slurry through a filter with a pore size of 4.5-5.5 mm. The filtration of the excrement slurry involves filtering through sieves having an aperture of 1.5-2.5 mm, 0.8-1.2 mm, 0.4-0.6 mm, and 0.2-0.3 mm in order. Preferred Conditions: The amount ratio of the sepiolite mineral, lithium metaphosphate, sodium carbonate, and water is 80-120g:8-12g:18-22g:1L. The lithium-based sepiolite has a cation exchange capacity of 20-40 mmol per 100 g. The dosage ratio of the excrement sample, sodium chloride solution and lithium-based sepiolite is 1g:3-7 mL:0.5-1.5 g. The concentration of the sodium chloride solution is 0.85-0.95% w/v. The stirring is carried out at a speed of 1800-2200 rotations per minute (rpm) for 2-4 minutes. The rotation speed of the centrifuge is 5500-6500 rpm and the time is 12-18 minutes.

PN CN107952489-A
TI Organic-inorganic hybrid photo-catalyst synthesis involves placing tin salt with or without crystal water, sulfur source and water in reaction vessel, and then adding amine-based organic surfactant, where mixture is fully stirred.
AU DAI K
   HUO Y
   WANG Z
   HU T
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV HUAIBEI NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018330771
AB    NOVELTY - Organic-inorganic hybrid photo-catalyst synthesis involves placing tin salt with or without crystal water, sulfur source and water in a reaction vessel, and then adding amine-based organic surfactant, where the mixture is fully stirred. The reaction mixture is uniformly reacted to obtain organic-inorganic hybrid tin sulfide photo-catalyst precipitation, where the precipitate is washed with alcohol and deionized water. The formed precipitate is freeze-dried and ground to prepare organic-inorganic hybrid tin sulfide photo-catalyst powder.
   USE - Method for synthesizing organic-inorganic hybrid photo-catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method synthesizes the organic-inorganic hybrid photo-catalyst in a simple and cost-effective manner with smaller grain size and large specific surface, which provides great convenience for separation of carriers, improves utilization of visible light and conversion of carbon dioxide to methane and methanol, and improves stability of the photo-catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The tin salt is selected from anhydrous tin tetrachloride, tin tetrachloride, tin dichloride, stannous oxalate, tin propane sulfonate, sodium stannate, tin hydroxide, stannous sulfate, stannous acetate, stannous acid tin, tin pyrophosphate, stannous pyrophosphate or tin sulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfur source is selected from thioacetamide, sulfur powder, thiourea amino acid, thiol, thiazole, tetramethyl disulfide, thiuram, thioether, thiosulfonium, ammonium tetrathiomolybdate, thiosalicylic acid, thio semicarbazide, thiobenzamide, sodium thiosulfate, phenothiazine, dithizone and l-cystine. The amine-based organic surfactant is selected from triethylenetetraamine, triethylenediamine, triethylamine, diglycolamine, ethanolamine, dimethylamine, hexamethyltriethylenetetraamine, tetraethylenepentamine, ethylenediamine or cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. Preferred Conditions: The water and the amine-based organic surfactant are provided in a ratio of 0.2-4:1. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 60-180 degrees C for 2-48 hours. The tin source and the sulfur source are provided in a ratio of 0.2-5:1.

PT P
PN CN107841332-A; CN107841332-B
TI Preparing aviation kerosene range alkanes, comprises e.g. carrying out aqueous phase reforming reaction of alcohol compounds, and hydrodeoxygenation reaction of 8-16C furan and/or oxygen compound containing aldehyde ketone.
AU LI N
   TANG H
   LI G
   ZHANG T
   WANG A
   WANG X
   CONG Y
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2018252658
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aviation kerosene range alkanes, comprises entering aqueous solution of 1-6C biomass alcohol compound and 8-16C furan, and/or oxygen compound containing aldehyde ketone into a fixed bed reactor, carrying out aqueous phase reforming reaction of alcohol compounds to produce hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide, and carrying out hydrodeoxygenation reaction of 8-16C furan and/or oxygen compound containing aldehyde ketone to obtain 8-16C biomass aviation kerosene range alkanes, where the method utilizes metal-solid acid bifunctional catalyst for catalyzing this coupling process.
   USE - The aviation kerosene useful as alternative of liquid fuel, and as additive for improving fuel cetane number and cold resistance capability.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids utilization of high pressure hydrogen gas; is safe and more reliable than the existing method. The aviation kerosene: reduce country's dependence on imported oil for liquid fuels.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of supported metal-solid acid bifunctional catalyst by impregnation method, comprises adding soluble salt solution containing active metal A into acidic carrier X, impregnating, allowing to stand at room temperature for more than 2 hours, then drying, and calcining at 300-600 degrees C for 2-5 hours. In the fixed bed reactor, the space velocity between aqueous solution of 1-6C biomass alcohol compound and 8-16C furan, and/or oxygen compound containing aldehyde ketone is 10:1-1:20 (preferably 5:1), the reaction temperature is 100-500 degrees C (preferably 200-300 degrees C), the system maintains certain pressure (preferably 2-7 MPa) under nitrogen gas atmosphere, the nitrogen gas pressure is 0.1-10 MPa, the space velocity of 1-6C biomass alcohol compound aqueous solution is 0.1-10 h(-1) (preferably 0.5-5 h(-1)), the reaction is carried out under liquid condition, and the 1-6C biomass alcohol compound aqueous solution has concentration of 0.5-90% (preferably 5-50%). In the method, the unreacted raw materials are removed from the reaction system by distillation or rectification and can be recycled. Preferred Components: The metal-solid acid catalyst comprises active metal A and acidic carrier X, where the active metal A is nickel, platinum, palladium, ruthenium, rhodium, iridium, iron, copper and/or cobalt; the acidic carrier X is molecular sieves of H- beta , H-Y, H-USY, H-ZSM-5, H-MOR, H-MCM-22, HSAPO, aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide, aluminum oxide-silicon dioxide, titanium dioxide, AC or iron oxide complex (FeOx), and heteropoly acid, zirconium phosphate, zirconium tungstate, silicon oxide or acid clay; the amount of active metal A in catalyst is 0.1-80%. The amount of active metal A in catalyst is 0.5-20%. The 1-6C biomass alcohol compound is methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol, ethylene glycol, glycerol, xylitol and/or sorbitol; and the 8-16C furan and/or oxygen compound containing aldehyde ketone is 5,5'-(furan-2-ylmethylene)bis(2-methylfuran), 5,5'-(butane-1,1-diyl)bis(2-methylfuran), 2,2-bis(5-methylfuran-2-yl)propan-1-ol, 5,5'-(propane-2,2-diyl)bis(2-methylfuran), 5,5'-(cyclopentane-1,1-diyl)bis(2-methylfuran), (E)-1-(furan-2-yl)-5-methylhex-1-en-3-one, bicyclopentyliden-2-one, (E)-1-(furan-2-yl)hex-1-en-3-one, (E)-1-(furan-2-yl)oct-1-en-3-one, (1E,4E)-1,5-di(furan-2-yl)penta-1,4-dien-3-one and/or 3,5,5-trimethylcyclohex-2-enone.x = not defined.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gas-permeable membrane comprises a partial structure i.e. substituted silicon containing units (I) of formula (I-a) or (I-b).
   R1, R2 = 1-6C alkyl (preferred), H, 2-6C alkenyl, 1-6C alkoxy, 2-6C alkenyloxy, aryl or aryloxy;
   M1-M3 = metal atom, preferably titanium, zirconium, hafnium, aluminum, niobium, silicon or boron;
   m1 = integer;
   a-c = 1-3; and
   a1 = bonding hand.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a composition for forming the gas-permeable membrane, comprising a polymer having a siloxane bond as a main chain and having at least two hydroxy groups per molecule, and a metal compound capable of generating at least two hydroxy groups per molecule by hydrolysis; and
   (2) producing the gas-permeable membrane, comprising heat-treating the composition to produce a polymer membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the membrane, a permeability to carbon dioxide gas and hydrogen gas is higher than a permeability to nitrogen gas. The metal compound is a metal compound having an alkoxy group, a metal compound having an isocyanate group, a metal compound having a halogen atom or a metal compound having a carbonyl group. The composition comprises 5-20 mass% of the metal compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer is: at least one of a linear polymer having a siloxane bond as a main chain and having at least two hydroxy groups per molecule, and a silsesquioxane derivative having at least two hydroxy groups per molecule; or a dimethylpolysiloxane having silanol groups at both ends.
   EXAMPLE - Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS-OH) having silanol group at the end was dissolved in isopropanol. Four types of PDMS-OH with four different viscosities were used. The viscosity of PDMS-OH with the lowest viscosity was 25 cSt and its molecular weight was 550. The viscosities of the other three types of PDMS-OH were 64, 750 and 1800 to 2200 respectively. In general, since the viscosity and the molecular weight are proportional to each other, the molecular weights of the remaining three types of PDMS-OH were calculated as 1800 (viscosity of 64 cSt), 18000 (viscosity of 750 cSt), 36000 (viscosity of 1800-2200 cSt), based on the fact that when the molecular weight is 550, the viscosity is 25 cSt. Titanium-n-butoxide was added to an isopropanol solution in which PDMS-OH was dissolved, and the obtained mixture was stirred for 30 minutes. Titanium-n-butoxide was added so that the weight percentage of titanium dioxide when the titanium-n-butoxide is completely converted to titanium oxide was 5%, 10%, 15% or 20% with respect to the final membrane material (total solid content). The mixed solution was heated at 70 degrees C for 1 hour. Next, 5-6 ml mixed solution was added dropwise into a Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethylene liner) petri dish, heated at 60 degrees C for 48 hours, and further heated at 150 degrees C for 72 hours. Thereafter, the membrane was allowed to cool to room temperature, and the formed membrane was peeled from the petri dish. The thickness of the produced membrane was 300-1000 mu m.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).)

PN JP2018009029-A; JP6437614-B2
TI Manufacture of hydrocarbon e.g. ethane used as chemical substance raw material, involves reacting gas comprising saturated hydrocarbon and unsaturated hydrocarbon in presence of catalyst, and adding hydrogen to unsaturated hydrocarbon.
AU NISHIMURA N
   ONISHI K
   TOJO M
AE ASAHI KASEI CORP (ASAH-C)
GA 201805351U
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of hydrocarbon involves reacting a mixed gas comprising 1-4C saturated hydrocarbon (a) at a molar fraction of 0.05-0.999 and 2-8C unsaturated hydrocarbon (x) at a molar fraction of 0.001-0.95 in the presence of a catalyst, condensing hydrocarbon (a) by dehydrogenation, adding dissociated hydrogen or hydrogen molecules dehydrogenated from the hydrocarbon (a) to the hydrocarbon (x), and manufacturing hydrocarbon (b) having carbon skeleton same as the carbon skeleton of hydrocarbon (x).
   USE - Manufacture of hydrocarbon e.g. ethane, butane, butene, ethylene, cyclohexane, cyclohexene, cyclohexadiene, methylcyclohexane, methylcyclohexene, and methylcyclohexadiene (all claimed) used as chemical substance raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of hydrocarbon with excellent reusability of mixture gas and reduced generation of byproducts such as carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, without requiring high temperature and controlling concentration of raw material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst is a heterogeneous catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reaction process is performed at 30-440 degrees C and atmospheric pressure to 50 MPa. Manufacture of hydrocarbon further involves introducing hydrogen during the reaction process, manufacturing unsaturated hydrocarbon (y) having a carbon number of 2m-8m, where m is the carbon number of the hydrocarbon (x), before or after the reaction process, manufacturing hydrocarbon (x) from the hydrocarbon (b) at 300-1300 degrees C, without using the catalyst, and using a portion(s) of obtained mixture gas or the hydrocarbon (x) as a raw material in the reaction process and using a portion(s) of generated hydrogen in the reaction process twice or more, without changing a composition of the component contained in the mixture gas. Preferred Composition: The unsaturation degree of the bonding between carbon atoms of the hydrocarbon (b) is lower than the hydrocarbon (x). The hydrocarbon (a) is methane or ethane. The hydrocarbon (x) is alkene, alkyne, or a hydrocarbon containing an aromatic ring. The number of unsaturated bonds between carbon atoms of alkene is 1 or more. The alkene is ethylene, butene, or butadiene. The alkyne is acetylene. The hydrocarbon containing aromatic ring is benzene or toluene. The molar fraction of hydrocarbon (a) is preferably 0.1-0.95. The molar fraction of hydrocarbon (x) is preferably 0.05-0.9. The ratio of hydrocarbon (x) with respect to hydrocarbon (a) is 0.001-10. The molar ratio of hydrogen with respect to total content of hydrocarbon (a) and hydrocarbon (x) is 100 or less, preferably 0.0001-0.5. The molar fraction of hydrogen contained in the raw material gas is less than 0.0001 or 0.0001 or more.

PN CN107556066-A
TI Treating straw particle in bio-reactor for protecting ground planting vegetable, involves constructing fermentation tank, piling up straw grain in fermentation tank, adding nitrogen source substance and adjusting carbon and nitrogen ratio.
AU DING H
   DING X
   JIANG P
   KE D
   LAN L
   LI M
   WANG Y
   WANG Z
AE WANG Y (WANG-Individual)
GA 201805023K
AB    NOVELTY - Treating straw particle in bio-reactor involves constructing a fermentation tank into the ground under the protection of greenhouse effect, piling up the straw grain in the fermentation tank, adding nitrogen source substance, adjusting the carbon and nitrogen ratio, inoculating seedling with water, covering the fermentation tank with film, blowing air to exhaust carbon dioxide and carrying out the aerobic fermentation with heat to make straw particles into organic matter for the growth and development of crops.
   USE - Method for treating straw particle in bio-reactor for protecting the ground planting vegetable, green plant and crops such as fruit tree and other soil cultivation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method for treating straw particle in bio-reactor enables to facilities culture of vegetable, fruit tree and other crops and other soil-less cultivation of green plant for their growth and development.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Conditions: The straw particle is the crop straw prepared by crushing and pressing into particles, having the moisture content is not greater than 15%. The ratio of carbon and nitrogen is 25-35:1. The fermentation tank provided with a ventilation pipe having carved shape with proper density of fine hole, one end of ventilation pipe is connected with the fan, and the other end is closed. The fermentation tank is installed with the corresponding blowing and exhaust device. The reactor pile is sealed inside the fermentation tank by using plastic film, airing the reactor pile by starting the fan every day during day time through the ventilation pipe, at the same time, starting the exhaust fan to generate discharge reactor through to the greenhouse gas with hot gas and carbon dioxide, increasing the indoor temperature, providing a carbon dioxide air fertilizer, stopping blowing and exhausting at night time and measuring the reactor temperature. Preferred Process: The treatment of straw particle in bio-reactor is performed by preparing straw particles, building the fermentation tank with laying pipe, placing the straw grain into the fermentation tank, adding the nitrogen source substance, mixing uniformly to obtain mixed material, carrying out the pre-treatment of strain by adding suitable amount of water or nitrogen source material to obtain processed strain, inoculating the required amount of processed strain by spraying or spreading uniformly on the mixed material, mixing uniformly, piling, covering with plastic film to seal the fermentation tank, blowing and exhausting the fermentation tank during the day, uncovering the film, turning the pile, adjusting the moisture content of the fermentation tank to 60% to maintain the fermentation condition, laminating to close the fermentation tank, blowing and exhausting the fermentation tank during the day to finish the fermentation, and re-turning, or processing the organic material directly in the fermentation tank into organic fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
