TI Natural stable isotope (13C) detection and            inversion method for foundation-wavelength differential            absorption laser radar, involves calculating            differential absorption optical thickness (DAOD) of            water, using emission signals, extracting absorption            cross-sectional area of gas to be tested.
AU ZHANG H
   GONG W
   HAN G
   SHI T
   XU J
   MA X
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2022C5488F
AB    NOVELTY - Natural stable isotope (13C) detection and                inversion method involves (i) selecting working                wavelength, and set n kinds of on wavelengths and 1                off wavelengths; where n is the default value,                according to the absorption rate of water (H2O),                12CO2 (carbon dioxide) and 13CO2 for different                laser wavelengths, (ii) according to the set n                kinds of on wavelengths and 1 kind of off                wavelengths, the multi-wavelength differential                absorption lidar transmits laser pulses of n+1                wavelengths, and receiving reflected signal to                obtain the wavelength of the transmitted pulse, the                transmission distance, the power of the transmitted                signal P0 &#955; and the power of the received                signal P &#955; R; (iii) calculating                differential absorption optical thickness DAOD of                H2O, 12CO2 and 13CO2 using the emission signals of                n kinds of on wavelengths.
   USE - Detection inversion method of                foundation-wavelength differential absorption laser                radar.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Natural stable isotope (13C) detection and                inversion method involves (i) selecting working                wavelength, and set n kinds of on wavelengths and 1                off wavelengths; where n is the default value,                according to the absorption rate of water (H2O),                12CO2 (carbon dioxide) and 13CO2 for different                laser wavelengths, (ii) according to the set n                kinds of on wavelengths and 1 kind of off                wavelengths, the multi-wavelength differential                absorption lidar transmits laser pulses of n+1                wavelengths, and receiving reflected signal to                obtain the wavelength of the transmitted pulse, the                transmission distance, the power of the transmitted                signal P0 &#955; and the power of the received                signal P &#955; R; (iii) calculating                differential absorption optical thickness DAOD of                H2O, 12CO2 and 13CO2 using the emission signals of                n kinds of on wavelengths; (iv) extracting                absorption cross-sectional area of the gas to be                tested under the conditions of test air                temperature, test air pressure, and emission signal                wavelength; (v) using the DAOD of H2O, 12CO2 and                13CO2 and the corresponding absorption                cross-sectional area, establishing the optimal                inversion model, and obtain 12CO2, 13 CO2 and H2O                concentrations C12CO2, C13CO2, CH2O; (vi) according                to the measured concentration of C12 CO2, C13 CO2                and the VPDB isotope ratio, calculating natural                stable isotope &#948;13 C, determining type of                natural substances, and realizing utilization of                multiple Wavelength differential absorption lidar                measures &#948;13C.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a 13C                detection inversion system of the foundation                wavelength differential absorption laser                radar.

TI Regenerated modified plastic based on waste            plastics comprises base resin, recycling waste plastic            particles, e.g. polyethylene and polypropylene,            modifier, lubricant, antioxidant, and            compatibilizer.

   JIANG J
   ZHANG Y
AE ANHUI CHAOXING NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2022708343
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerated modified plastic based on waste                plastics, comprises 50-85 pts. wt. matrix resin,                25-45 pts. wt. recycled waste plastic particles,                15-45 pts. wt. modifier, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt.                lubricant, 0.5-3.5 pts. wt. antioxidant and 1.5-3                pts. wt. compatibilizer. The recycled waste plastic                particles are polyethylene and polypropylene. The                modifier is prepared by adding hydrochloric acid                into a mixed solvent at room temperature to enable                the pH value of the mixed solvent to be 3.5-5.5,                then adding a KH560                ((3-glycidoxypropyl)trimethoxysilane) aqueous                solution, adding acidified single-layer graphene                under ultrasonic atmosphere and mechanical                stirring, reacting for 8-12 hours, performing                suction filtration, washing, and freeze drying to                obtain grafted graphene, where the mixed solvent is                formed by mixing deionized water and ethanol in a                volume ratio of 1:10. The grafted graphene and                silicon-containing monomer are added into a closed                container with magnetic stirring.
   USE - Regenerated modified plastic based on waste                plastics
   ADVANTAGE - The regeneration modified plastic improves                binding force of modifier and the regenerated                modified plastic, furtherimproves durability of the                modifier on the modification effect of the                regenerated modified plastic, and prolongs service                life of the regenerated modified plastic. The                modification enables to play a role in enhancing                the flame retardant property, the mechanical                property and the weather resistance of the                regenerated modified plastic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regenerated modified plastic based on waste                plastics, comprises 50-85 pts. wt. matrix resin,                25-45 pts. wt. recycled waste plastic particles,                15-45 pts. wt. modifier, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt.                lubricant, 0.5-3.5 pts. wt. antioxidant and 1.5-3                pts. wt. compatibilizer. The recycled waste plastic                particles are polyethylene and polypropylene. The                modifier is prepared by adding hydrochloric acid                into a mixed solvent at room temperature to enable                the pH value of the mixed solvent to be 3.5-5.5,                then adding a KH560 aqueous solution, adding                acidified single-layer graphene under ultrasonic                atmosphere and mechanical stirring, reacting for                8-12 hours, then performing suction filtration,                washing, and freeze drying to obtain grafted                graphene, where the mixed solvent is formed by                mixing deionized water and ethanol in a volume                ratio of 1:10. The grafted graphene and the                silicon-containing monomer are added into a closed                container accompanied with magnetic stirring,                injecting supercritical carbon dioxide, performed                ultrasonic stirring for 12 hours under the pressure                condition of 11 MPa. The carbon dioxide is removed                under reduced pressure, washed the obtained                reaction product with methanol, and dried in an                oven to obtain the modifier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The silicon-containing                  monomer is prepared by uniformly mixing                  pentaerythritol derivatives and DMF, adding                  potassium carbonate and potassium iodide,                  stirring and heating at 70-80degrees Celsius,                  then slowly dropwise adding                  dimethylvinylchlorosilane, continuously stirring                  and reacting for 6 hours, stopping reaction,                  reducing the temperature and pressure, carrying                  out rotary evaporation, and carrying out vacuum                  drying. The pentaerythritol derivative is                  prepared by uniformly mixing chlorinated                  benzophenone derivatives,                  3-chloropropyltrimethoxysilane, triethylamine and                  dichloromethane, slowly dropwise adding                  pentaerythritol under the conditions of ice water                  bath and stirring, heating to room temperature                  after complete dropwise adding, reacting for 2-3                  hours, stopping reaction, washing with water,                  separating liquid, and rotationally evaporating                  an organic phase to obtain the pentaerythritol                  derivatives. The chlorinated benzophenone                  derivative is prepared by mixing chloroacetyl                  chloride and dichloromethane, slowly dropwise                  adding a mixed solution containing 2, 4-dihydroxy                  benzophenone, triethylamine and dichloromethane                  under the conditions of ice water bath and                  stirring, heating to room temperature for                  reacting for 6 hours, stopping the reaction, and                  performing post-treatment. Preferred                  Compositions: The mass ratio of the KH560 and                  acidified single-layer graphene is 1-3:10. The                  dosage ratio of the grafted graphene, the                  silicon-containing monomer and the supercritical                  carbon dioxide is 10-14 g: 1.5-2.5 g: 60-80 mL.                  The dosage ratio of the pentaerythritol                  derivative, dimethylformamide (DMF), potassium                  carbonate, potassium iodide and                  dimethylvinylchlorosilane is 43-45 g: 60-100 mL:                  28-30 g, 0.1-0.3g, 12.1 g. The mass ratio of the                  chlorinated benzophenone derivative,                  3-chloropropyltrimethoxysilane and                  pentaerythritol is 29:20:15-18.

PN CN114468277-A
TI Method for preparing blueberry enzyme by biological enzyme method, involves placing blueberry pulp in a closed container and evacuating, and then dipping the slurry, placing impregnating slurry in an ultrasonic device for enzymatic hydrolysis, then inoculating the mixed strain into treated slurry.
AU SONG Z
   YE Z
   SHEN M
AE ANHUI CHANGRONG AGRIC COMPREHENSIVE DEV (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202271274V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing blueberry enzyme by biological enzyme method, involves i. placing above blueberry pulp in a closed container and evacuating, and then filling a sufficient amount of carbon dioxide into the container for full dipping for 10-15 hours, and then the dipping slurry is taken out for use; ii. taking the above-mentioned impregnating slurry out and placing in a wall breaker for ultra-fine crushing, then adding pectinase to mix and stirring evenly, and then placing in an ultrasonic device for ultrasonic combined enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain enzymatic hydrolysis slurry; iii. heating the above-mentioned enzymolysis slurry, then cooling to normal temperature; and iv. inoculating the mixed strain into the above-mentioned treated slurry, and after low-temperature fermentation for 10-12 hours, performing the fermentation at a variable temperature for 10-12 hours, and then closing anaerobic fermentation at normal temperature.
   USE - Method for preparing blueberry enzyme by biological enzyme method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for preparing blueberry enzyme by biological enzyme method effectively improves the leaching rate of the health-care components in the blueberries, greatly retains the content of anthocyanin, reduces the enzymolysis time, improves the oxidation resistance of the final enzyme, and enhances the health-care performance of the enzyme.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing blueberry enzyme by biological enzyme method, involves selecting whole and healthy blueberries, washing them, and making pulp to get blueberry pulp for use; ii. placing above blueberry pulp in a closed container and evacuating, and then filling a sufficient amount of carbon dioxide into the container for full dipping for 10-15 hours, and then the dipping slurry is taken out for use; iii. the above-mentioned impregnating slurry is taken out and placing in a wall breaker for ultra-fine crushing, then adding pectinase to mix and stirring evenly, and then placing in an ultrasonic device for ultrasonic combined enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain enzymatic hydrolysis slurry; iv. heating the above-mentioned enzymolysis slurry to 90 degreesC, continuously stirring to inactivate the enzyme for 5-8 minutes, then cooling to normal temperature, and then placing in an ultra-high pressure environment to maintain pressure and stand, and the slurry must be processed for use; and v. inoculating the mixed strain into the above-mentioned treated slurry, and after low-temperature fermentation for 10-12 hours, performing the fermentation at a variable temperature for 10-12 hours, and then closing anaerobic fermentation at normal temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preparing blueberry enzyme by biological enzyme method involves during the beating process of the blueberry in the step (1), the degree of pulp breakage is greater than 60 wt.%, evacuating the blueberry pulp and kept under pressure for 1-2 hours, and then filling carbon dioxide into it, the carbon dioxide impregnation temperature of blueberry pulp is 20-25 degreesC, adding the amount of pectinase in the step (3) is 0.1-1.5 wt.% of the total amount of the impregnated slurry. The temperature of the enzymolysis in the step (3) is 50 degreesC, the power of the ultrasonic is 420 Watt, the frequency of the ultrasonic is 28 kilohertz, and the time of the ultrasonic combined enzymolysis is 30 minutes. The rotating speed of continuous stirring to kill the enzyme in the step (4) is 25-30 revolutions per minute (r/min). In the step (4), the pressure of the ultra-high pressure environment for maintaining pressure and standing still is 350 megapascal, and the standing time is 30 minutes. In the step (5), the temperature of the low-temperature fermentation is 15 degreesC, and the temperature-variable fermentation method is to raise the temperature by 2 degreesC every 2 hours, until the temperature reaches a normal temperature of 25-30 degreesC, and then the temperature is stopped.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mixed bacterial species is Lactobacillus plantarum, Pediococcus lactis, Lactobacillus acidophilus, Lactobacillus paracasei, and Lactobacillus rhamnosus, which are mixed and prepared according to the inoculation ratio of 3:5:2:1:1, and the initial inoculation amount is 5.5x106 CFU/mL.

TI Method for preparing soluble aluminum hydroxide by using red mud as raw material, involves introducing certain amount of carbon dioxide into sodium meta-aluminate solution to separate out aluminum in form of aluminum hydroxide and filtering to obtain soluble aluminum hydroxide.
AU MA Y
   ZHANG R

   ZHU K
   DU J
AE KAILI XINTAI MELT CO LTD (KAIL-Non-standard)
GA 2022459042
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drying and grinding the red mud and sieving the ground red mud with a 300-mesh sieve to obtain red mud powder. The red mud is fully contacted with a certain amount of 32% sodium hydroxide solution, a certain amount of sodium hypochlorite is added to react at a certain temperature, filtered to obtain a sodium ferrate solution, and filtered and separated to reduce the iron content in the remaining red mud to below 1%. The sodium ferrate solution obtained by filtering is frozen and reacted with the potassium hydroxide saturated solution to obtain potassium ferrate and refined to obtain a potassium ferrate product. The filter residue obtained by filtering the prepared sodium ferrate is reacted with sodium hydroxide and filtered to obtain a sodium meta-aluminate solution. A certain amount of carbon dioxide is introduced into the sodium meta-aluminate solution to separate out aluminum in the form of aluminum hydroxide and filtered to obtain soluble aluminum hydroxide.
   USE - Method for preparing soluble aluminum hydroxide by using red mud as raw material for use in field of red mud recycling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare soluble aluminum hydroxide, which improves the flexibility and practicability of the device for use, improves the working efficiency of the red mud drying, avoids the red-mud moisture, and easy to dissolve the preparation of aluminum-hydroxide. The iron content in the residual red mud is reduced to less than 1 %, and the method has low performance requirement of device, without consuming a lot of energy, and will not generate waste gas, causing pollution, at the same time, it can only extract the aluminum in the process of refining, which is more convenient for people to use. The method has high energy consumption and secondary pollution in the extraction of aluminum in red mud in the existing technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for preparing soluble aluminum hydroxide by using red mud as a raw material.

   USE - The high-strength gypsum foaming agent is useful for preparing building material and uniform gypsum board.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-strength gypsum foaming agent promotes gypsum foaming by extracting alginic acid from kelp and utilizing the surface active effect of alginic acid, can be prepared by a simple and cost-effective method, and promotes the formation of bicarbonate by heating montmorillonite, albite, and celestite at high temperature to disintegrate under the action of microwaves to increase surface area, and then soaking in the solution and injecting carbon dioxide, so that the foam stability in gypsum molding is improved. The foam formed by using the foaming agent has high stability and flexibility, and is easy to transport.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the high-strength gypsum foaming agent, which involves: (1) preparing the natural polymer compound by washing and drying 10-13 pts. wt. kelp, pulverizing with an ultrafine pulverizer for 20-30 minutes, mixing the obtained kelp powder with 180-190 pts. wt. clean water, stirring for 20-30 minutes, adding 3-5 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, stirring for 40-50 minutes, filtering to obtain a filter residue and a filtrate, precipitating the filtrate with 5-8 %mass hydrochloric acid, decompressing and drying to obtain a dry substance, placing the filter residue in 80-90 pts. wt. 0.1-0.3 %mass dilute nitric acid solution, soaking for 3-5 hours, filtering, rinsing 1-3 times with clean water, and carbonizing in a vacuum furnace at 800-900degrees Celsius for 20-30 minutes, mixing the carbonized particles with the dry substance product in a mass ratio of 1:(5-9); (2) preparing the adsorbent by mixing montmorillonite, albite and celestite, processing to obtain powder, placing the powder in a heating dish, vacuumizing to 10-30 Pa, heating at 500-600degrees Celsius, microwave treating at 800-900 W for 20-30 minutes, cooling to room temperature, mixing the powder with 10-20 times its mass of water, pouring into a sealing tank, adding water-soluble polyurethane resin, ultrasonically treating at 1-3degrees Celsius, injecting carbon dioxide, stirring for 20-30 minutes, decompressing and drying; and (3) mixing sulfonate, natural polymer compound and water, adding to a stirrer, stirring at 1000-1300 rpm for 30-40 minutes, then adding adsorbent, and stirring at 500-600 rpm for 20-30 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent comprises 1-3 pts. wt. montmorillonite, 3-7 pts. wt. albite and 2-11 pts. wt. celestite.    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sulfonate is formed by mixing sodium alpha-alkenyl sulfonate and sodium isethionate in a mass ratio of 1:(3-5).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The natural polymer compound is alginic acid extract.Preferred Composition: The dosage of the water-soluble polyurethane resin is 1-3% of the mass of the powder.

TI Method for purifying octafluorocyclobutane            involves oxidizing octafluorocyclobutane raw material            gas through column having carrier loaded with oxidant,            pretreating carrier, and reacting with hydrofluoric            acid and hydrochloric acid.
AU JIN X
   SUN M
   JIA L
   SHANG Y
   PAN H
   ZHANG H
AE SUZHOU JINHONG GAS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021E4875N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for purifying octafluorocyclobutane                involves preforming adsorption and rectification to                octafluorocyclobutane feed gas, oxidizing raw                material gas, through an oxidation column including                a carrier loaded with an oxidant, where the loading                of oxidant on surface of the carrier is carried out                by spraying an aqueous solution, and the carrier is                formed with a passage for flow of the raw material                gas, pretreating the carrier by taking 4.5-5 kg of                grinded graphite micropowder and putting into the                reactor, adding 2-2.5% hydrofluoric acid, 0.2-0.32%                nitric acid and 0.2-0.24% hydrochloric acid, and                adding 5-10 volume times of pure water, raising                temperature of the reactor to 86-90degrees Celsius,                reacting for 3-4 hours, stirring for every 1 hour,                where the stirring time is 4-6 minutes each time,                and immersing the carrier in reactor, pressurizing                with carbon dioxide to 1-3 MPa and soaking for 2-3                hours under heat preservation conditions, and                taking out and drying.
   USE - Method for purifying                octafluorocyclobutane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the product with purity of                more than 99.999%, and has high safety, less                equipment investment, less energy consumption, low                cost and simple process operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for purifying octafluorocyclobutane                involves preforming adsorption and rectification to                octafluorocyclobutane feed gas, oxidizing the raw                material gas after adsorption, through an oxidation                column including a carrier loaded with an oxidant,                where the loading of oxidant on surface of the                carrier is carried out by spraying an aqueous                solution, the carrier is formed with a porous                structure, and the carrier is formed with a passage                for flow of the raw material gas in direction of                flow of the raw material gas, pretreating the                carrier by taking 4.5-5 kg of grinded graphite                micropowder with a particle size of 1-10 micron and                putting into the reactor, adding 2-2.5%                hydrofluoric acid, 0.2-0.32% nitric acid and                0.2-0.24% hydrochloric acid, and adding 5-10 volume                times of pure water after mixing and stirring,                raising temperature of the reactor to 86-90degrees                Celsius, carrying out the reaction for 3-4 hours,                stirring for every 1 hour, where the stirring time                is 4-6 minutes each time, and immersing the carrier                in reactor, pressurizing with carbon dioxide to 1-3                MPa and soaking for 2-3 hours under heat                preservation conditions, and taking out and                drying.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of purifying                method of octafluorocyclobutane. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidant includes                  potassium permanganate or potassium dichromate.                  The oxidant is supported on the surface of the                  porous structure of the carrier. The carrier is                  zeolite, porous alumina, porous magnesia or                  porous ceramic.

TI Hydrolyzing and decarboxylating aromatic dicarboxylic acid ester compound, comprises e.g. adding aromatic dicarboxylic acid ester, acid catalyst, water and alcohol solvent into autoclave with automatic pressure relief device, sealing, stirring and maintaining reaction system under constant pressure.
AU FENG S
   LIU J
   YANG H
   WANG Y
   LIU P
AE HUNAN SUBO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2021D1140X
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrolyzing and decarboxylating aromatic dicarboxylic acid ester compound (I), comprises adding aromatic dicarboxylic acid ester, acid catalyst, water and alcohol solvent into a autoclave with automatic pressure relief device, sealing, then stirring and dissolving and setting the pressure of the automatic pressure relief device to certain pressure value to raise the temperature for reaction and the pressure relief valve automatically vents when the pressure in kettle exceeds the set pressure thus the reaction system is maintained at constant pressure and carrying out heat preservation and reacting, taking sample for liquid chromatography analysis, stopping the reaction when the raw material is less than or equal to 0.5%, cooling and recycling the part of solvent from the reaction liquid under negative pressure, cooling, then precipitating out the product, centrifuging, filtering and drying to obtain product i.e. substituted aromatic compound (II) and carbon dioxide and ethanol as by-products.
   USE - The method is useful for hydrolyzing and decarboxylating aromatic dicarboxylic acid ester compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the problem of material decomposition and coking caused by traditional high-temperature hydrolysis and decarboxylation; has simple operation process, improved production efficiency, low production cost, and high yield; reduces the amount of waste salt, reduces the environmental protection treatment cost; and is suitable for large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrolyzing and decarboxylating aromatic dicarboxylic acid ester compound of formula (I), comprises adding aromatic dicarboxylic acid ester, acid catalyst, water and alcohol solvent into a autoclave with an automatic pressure relief device, sealing, then stirring and dissolving and setting the pressure of the automatic pressure relief device to certain pressure value to raise the temperature for reaction and the pressure relief valve automatically vents when the pressure in the kettle exceeds the set pressure thus the reaction system is maintained at constant pressure and carrying out heat preservation and reacting for 3-6 hours, taking sample for liquid chromatography analysis, stopping the reaction when the raw material is less than or equal to 0.5%, cooling to 70-80degrees Celsius and recycling the part of solvent from the reaction liquid under negative pressure, then cooling to 20-25degrees Celsius, precipitating out the product, centrifuging, filtering and drying to obtain product i.e. substituted aromatic compound of formula (II) and carbon dioxide and ethanol as by-products.
   Ar, R = not defined.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The decarboxylation temperature is 100-150degrees Celsius.Preferred Components: The alcohol solvent is isobutanol, n-butanol, sec-butanol or tert-butanol and the dosage of alcohol solvent is 4-6 times the mass of (I). The amount of water used is 1-3 times the mass of (I). The acid catalyst is sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, p-toluenesulfonic acid, methanesulfonic acid or trifluoromethanesulfonic acid and the amount of the catalyst is 0.5-5% of the mass of (I). The pressure of the automatic pressure relief valve is set to 0.1-0.5 MPa. The volume of the negative pressure recycling solvent is 25-75% of the volume of the reaction liquid. The aromatic dicarboxylic acid ester (I) is 2-(2-cyclopropyl-2-oxyethyl)-2-(pyridin-2-yl)malonate, diethyl 2-cinnamyl-2-(pyrimidin-4-yl)malonate, diethyl 2-allyl-2-(1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)malonate or 3-chloro-5-trifluoromethyl-2-pyridylethyl(diethyl ester)-2-trifluoromethylhydrolyzingbenzamide.

TI Preparing red blood cells from stem cells for clinical transfusions, by culturing stem cells in medium, renewing culture medium, maintaining culture, staining cells with Giemsa stain for morphologic analysis, and centrifuging cells for predetermined time and examining pellet under microscope.
AU SINGH V K
AE UNIV AMITY (UYAM-Non-standard)
GA 2021A1635L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing red blood cells (RBCs) from stem cells for clinical transfusions involves culturing the stem cells in a standard medium expanding the cells and a blocking peptide to enhance receptor derived signaling, providing a predetermined diluted volume of the cell culture on a predetermined period in a volume of fresh medium containing SCF, IL-3, and hydrocortisone and renewing the culture medium containing growth factors for a predetermined period, providing parallel to the above defined steps in another culture well the same number of the cells are seeded without any growth factors, maintaining the culture at a temperature in carbon dioxide in the air, staining the cells with Giemsa stain, modified solution (Sigma) for morphologic analysis, providing the cells i.e. ished in PBS and incubated with Giemsa in an Eppendorf tube, and centrifuging the cells for the predetermined time at a predetermined revolutions per minute and examining pellet under a microscope.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing RBCs from stem cells for clinical transfusions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing red blood cells (RBCs) from stem cells for clinical transfusions involves culturing the stem cells in a standard medium chosen from a group of Iscove's modified Dulbecco's medium (IMDM) (Sigma) supplemented with growth factors such as insulin, transferrin and human serum from male AB plasma (Sigma), expanding the cells after culturing in the presence of hydrocortisone (Sigma), stem cell factor (SCF) (Genedirex), interleukin (IL)-3 (Genedirex), and erythropoietin (EPO) (invitrogen) and a blocking peptide to enhance the EPO-EPO receptor (EPOR) derived signaling, providing a predetermined diluted volume of the cell culture on a predetermined period in a predetermined volume of fresh medium containing SCF, IL-3, Epo, and hydrocortisone and renewing the culture medium containing growth factors for a predetermined period, providing parallel to the above defined steps in another culture well the same number of the cells are seeded without any growth factors, maintaining the culture at a predetermined temperature in carbon dioxide in the air, staining the cells with Giemsa stain, modified solution (Sigma) for morphologic analysis, providing the cells i.e. ished in PBS and incubated with Giemsa in an Eppendorf tube for the predetermined period for Giemsa staining, and centrifuging the cells for the predetermined time at a predetermined revolutions per minute and examining pellet under a microscope.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The cells are isolated by using spectra optia apheresis system after a predetermined time of granulocyte colony-stimulating factor (G-CSF) administration. The cells are mobilized into peripheral blood after the isolation. The cells are further purified by density gradient cell separation to remove the traces of RBCs, platelets, and plasma. The morphologic analysis is performed by using a graphical user interface on a digital device.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of isolation of stem cells/cluster of differentiation (CD)34+ cells and erythropoietin mimetic designing that do not support the claims.

PN CN113244927-A
TI Purifying carbon disulfide and recovering sulfur with dielectric barrier discharge plasma synergistic catalyst comprises stirring e.g. copper salt and aluminum oxide powder, heating, converting carbon disulfide and recovering sulfur.
AU MA Y
   CHU X
   CHEN P
   NING P
   WANG X
   WANG L
   WANG M
   LI D
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202196872S
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying carbon disulfide and recovering sulfur with dielectric barrier discharge plasma synergistic catalyst comprises adding copper salt, iron salt and lanthanum salt in the deionized water, stirring, ultrasonically vibrating, adding nano gamma - aluminum oxide powder into mixed solution, stirring, ultrasonically shaking to obtain the precursor solution, placing in water bath, shaking, heating to obtain semi-solid precursor slurry, drying, heating, reacting to obtain the gamma - aluminum oxide loaded lanthanum-based double metal catalyst, adding to a dielectric barrier discharge low-temperature plasma reactor discharge region, introducing carbon disulfide tail gas under the synergistic effect of the DBD plasma and the catalyst, converting carbon disulfide into elemental sulfur, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide gas and recovering the elemental sulfur by heating the catalyst to realize the purification of carbon disulfide- tail gas and resource utilization of sulfur.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying carbon disulfide and recovering sulfur with dielectric barrier discharge plasma synergistic catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst inhibit the production of sulfur dioxide; promotes the conversion of carbon disulfide into elemental sulfur; avoids secondary pollution and realizes the resource utilization of sulfur.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying carbon disulfide and recovering sulfur with dielectric barrier discharge plasma synergistic catalyst comprises (1) (a) adding copper salt, iron salt and lanthanum salt in the deionized water, stirring, ultrasonically vibrating for 2-4 minutes, (b) adding nano gamma - aluminum oxide powder into the mixed solution of step (a), stirring uniformly, ultrasonically shaking for 25-30 minutes to obtain the precursor solution, (c) placing the precursor solution in step (b) in water bath, shaking, heating to obtain semi-solid precursor slurry,(d) drying the semi-solid precursor slurry of step (c) at 75-85 degrees C for 12 hours, heating to 250-270 degrees C at a rate of 1.5-2 degrees C /minute for 1 hour, reacting to 350-450 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain the gamma - aluminum oxide loaded lanthanum-based double metal catalyst and (2) adding the gamma - aluminum oxide loaded lanthanum-based double metal catalyst in step (1) to a dielectric barrier discharge low-temperature plasma reactor discharge region, introducing carbon disulfide tail gas under the synergistic effect of the DBD plasma and the catalyst, converting carbon disulfide into elemental sulfur, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide gas and recovering the elemental sulfur by heating the catalyst to realize the purification of the carbon disulfide- tail gas and resource utilization of sulfur.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper salt comprises copper nitrate, copper sulfate and/or copper carbonate. The iron salt comprises iron nitrate, iron sulfate and/or iron carbonate. The lanthanum salt is lanthanum nitrate. The ratio of copper salt and iron salt to lanthanum salt is 1-4:1. The amount of double metal elements is 4-8 wt.%. of the catalyst.

PN CN113219097-A; CN113219097-B
TI Separation and determination of carnitine enantiomers in infant formula milk powder involves performing ultra-high performance convergence chromatographic analysis on standard solution and sample solution, preparing standard curve, and calculating content and purity of L-carnitine and D-carnitine.
AU ZHANG W
   XIE W
   HOU J
   ZOU X
   WANG P
   ZHANG Y
   HU X
   XU D
AE HANGZHOU CUSTOMS DISTRICT TECH CENT (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202194064W
AB    NOVELTY - Separation and determination of carnitine enantiomers in infant formula milk powder involves extracting, purifying, performing derivatization process to obtain sample solution, preparing standard solution, performing ultra-high performance convergence chromatographic analysis on the standard solution and sample solution, preparing standard curve, and calculating the content and purity of L-carnitine and D-carnitine in the sample solution through the standard curve. The ultra-high performance convergence chromatographic analysis is carried out using carbon dioxide as mobile phase (A), 1 v/v% ammonia-methanol solution as mobile phase (B), flow rate of 1.0 mL/minute, system back pressure of 13.8 MPa, column temperature of 40 degrees C, injection volume of 5 mu L, detection wavelength of 244 nm, and gradient elution using 90 v/v% mobile phase (A) and 10 v/v% mobile phase (B) for 0-7 minutes.
   USE - Separation and determination of carnitine enantiomers in infant formula milk powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures excellent separation effect, fast analysis speed, and reduced consumption of organic solvents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation and determination of carnitine enantiomers in infant formula milk powder involves extracting, purifying, performing derivatization process to obtain sample solution, preparing standard solution, performing ultra-high performance convergence chromatographic analysis on the standard solution and sample solution, preparing standard curve, and calculating the content and purity of L-carnitine and D-carnitine in the sample solution through the standard curve. The ultra-high performance convergence chromatographic analysis is carried out using carbon dioxide as mobile phase (A), 1 v/v% ammonia-methanol solution as mobile phase (B), flow rate of 1.0 mL/minute, system back pressure of 13.8 MPa, column temperature of 40 degrees C, injection volume of 5 mu L, detection wavelength of 244 nm, and gradient elution using 90 v/v% mobile phase (A) and 10 v/v% mobile phase (B) for 0-7 minutes, 90-72 v/v% mobile phase (A) and 10-20 v/v% mobile phase (B) for 7-9 minutes, 72 v/v% mobile phase (A) and 78 v/v% mobile phase (B) for 9-12 minutes, 72-90 v/v% mobile phase (A) and 28-10 v/v% mobile phase (B) for 12-13 minutes and 90 v/v% mobile phase (A) or 10 v/v% mobile phase (B) for 13-14 minutes. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for kit for separating and determining carnitine enantiomers in infant formula milk powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sample is extracted by weighing 5 g milk powder sample in 50 mL volumetric flask, adding 20 mL of 0.1 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution, vortex mixing for 1 minute, ultrasonically-processing for 5 min to fully dissolve the sample, adding 5 mL of 1 mol/L potassium hydroxide solution, vortex mixing for 1 minute, diluting to 50 mL with 0.1 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution, taking 1 mL extract in a 15 mL centrifuge tube, adding 9 mL acetonitrile, vortex mixing for 1 minute, centrifuging at 15000 rpm for 5 minutes, and purifying the supernatant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The derivatization reagent used in derivatization process is 4.5 g/L L-alanine-( beta ) naphthylamine acetonitrile solution. Preferred Process: The derivatization process involves using 5.0 g/L triethylamine chloroform solution as catalyst (C1), 5.0 g /L butyl chloroformate chloroform solution as catalyst (C2), and 50 mmol/L sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution as reaction termination liquid.

PT P
PN CN112661158-A
TI Separation of light hydrocarbon and chlorosilane full temperature range-pressure swing adsorption in tail gas of chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition crystal and film growth involves using e.g. hydrogen as carrier gas.
AU WANG L
   ZHONG Y
   CHEN Y
   TANG J
   CAI Y
AE ZHEJIANG TIANCAI YUNJI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202151025X
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of light hydrocarbon and chlorosilane full temperature range-pressure swing adsorption in tail gas of chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition crystal and film growth involves using 2C hydrocarbon or higher light hydrocarbon components as raw material gas, using a chlorosilane or silane, preferably trichlorosilane or dichlorosilane or silicon tetrachloride as silicon source, adding hydrogen chloride and hydrogen as carrier gas for chemical vapor deposition preparation of silicon carbide crystals or substrate-based thin film epitaxial growth of chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition crystal and film growth process in tail gas. The method uses a large amount of hydrogen, and light hydrocarbon, chlorosilane and hydrogen chloride as main components, a small amount of methane, silane, chloroalkanes, chloroalkenes, and a small amount of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water and silicon dioxide, silicon/carbon fine particles.
   USE - Separation of light hydrocarbon and chlorosilane full temperature range-pressure swing adsorption in tail gas of chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition crystal and film growth.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the technical bottleneck of the most difficult and most critical separation and purification in the exhaust gas of the chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition process. The method recycles the exhaust gas or hydrogen extraction or full-component recovery of hydrogen, light hydrocarbon, hydrogen chloride and chlorosilane and returns to the silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition process for recycling, realizes the recovery and reuse of exhaust gas, and reduces exhaust emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of light hydrocarbon and chlorosilane full temperature range-pressure swing adsorption in tail gas of chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition crystal and film growth involves using 2C hydrocarbon or higher light hydrocarbon components, preferably ethylene or propylene as raw material gas, using a chlorosilane (SiHmCln) or silane, preferably trichlorosilane or dichlorosilane or silicon tetrachloride as silicon source, adding hydrogen chloride and hydrogen as carrier gas for chemical vapor deposition preparation of silicon carbide crystals or chlorine-based silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition crystals based on film epitaxial growth on the substrate under the carrier gas. The method uses a large amount of hydrogen, and light hydrocarbon, chlorosilane and hydrogen chloride as main components, a small amount of methane, silane, chloroalkanes (CHmCln), chloroalkenes (VCM), and a small amount of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water and silicon dioxide, silicon/carbon fine particles. The reaction is performed at normal pressure or low pressure, and normal temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The tail gas is pressurized and fed to a pretreatment unit composed of a dust collector, a particulate filter, and an oil mist trap under the operating conditions of 0.2-0.3 MPa pressure and normal temperature, the pretreatment unit successively removes dust, particles, oil mist, some perchlorosilane, perchloroalkane and high hydrocarbon impurities in the tail gas, and fed the formed purified raw material gas into chlorosilane spray absorption. The chlorosilane spray absorption involves pressurizing the purified raw material gas from the pretreatment process at 0.6-1.0MPa, heating to 80-160 degrees C, feeding from the bottom of the spray absorption tower, using chlorosilane and hydrogen chloride mixed liquid as absorbent, spraying from the top of the spray absorption tower to perform reverse mass transfer exchange with the purified raw gas, flowing out the absorption liquid enriched with chlorosilane and hydrogen chloride from the bottom of the chlorosilane spray absorption tower, feeding into subsequent multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process, discharging a small amount of residual particles, perchlorosilane, perchloroalkane and high hydrocarbon impurities from the bottom of the tower which are environmentally-friendly, flowing out non-condensable gas from the top of the spray absorption tower, and feeding directly to medium temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration. The medium temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration is performed by feeding the non-condensable gas from the chlorosilane spray absorption process into medium temperature pressure swing adsorption concentration process consisting of 4 or more adsorption towers, performing adsorption concentration at 80-160 degrees C under the operating pressure of 0.4-0.8 MPa, flowing out the hydrogen-rich adsorption waste gas from the top of the adsorption tower, washing, using as fuel gas or as raw material gas for hydrogen extraction, obtaining the concentrated gas from the bottom of the adsorption tower by vacuum, and feeding into shallow cold oil absorption after cold and heat exchange and pressurization. The shallow cold oil absorption is carried out by heating the concentrated gas to 5-15 degrees C and compressing at 2.5-3.5 MPa, feeding from the bottom of the absorption tower of the shallow cold oil absorption process, using 5-15 degrees C, 2.5-3.5 MPa 4C (n-butane, isobutane or mixed butane) liquid solvent as absorbent, performing top-down spray absorption, flowing out the non-condensable gas from the top of the absorption tower as fuel gas after cold and heat exchange, flowing out light hydrocarbon-rich liquid from the bottom of the absorption tower, feeding into desorption tower, flowing out light hydrocarbon gas from the top of the tower, refining by ethylene and propane distillation tower, producing ethylene or propane or other light hydrocarbon components, directly returning ethylene or propane to silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition process for recycling, flowing out the 4C absorbent from the bottom of the desorption tower, and returning to the absorption tower as an absorbent for recycling. The multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation is carried out by feeding the absorption liquid from the chlorosilane spray absorption process into multi-stage evaporation, feeding into condenser, obtaining crude hydrogen chloride gas in the gas phase, forming crude stage liquid and feeding into hydrogen chloride refining, flowing out the crude chlorosilane liquid from the condenser, and feeding into the subsequent chlorosilane medium shallow cold distillation. The hydrogen chloride refining process is carried out by crude hydrogen chloride liquid from multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process, feeding into hydrogen chloride refining process consisting of hydrogen chloride rectification tower and vacuum rectification tower, controlling the operating pressure of the distillation tower at 0.3-1.0 MPa, and the operating temperature at 60-120 degrees C, operating pressure of the vacuum tower at -0.08 MPa to -0.1 MPa and the operating temperature at 60-120 degrees C, flowing out hydrogen chloride product gas with a purity greater than 99.9% from the top of the rectification tower, returning portion of hydrogen chloride product gas to the silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition process for recycling, and recycling a portion of hydrogen chloride product gas as an absorbent in the chlorosilane spray absorption process, feeding the effluent from the bottom of the rectification tower into vacuum tower, flowing out overhead gas from the top of the tower, or feeding directly to the incinerator for incineration treatment and discharge, or feeding out to extract VCM and chloroalkanes, and recombining from the bottom of the vacuum tower, or returning to the multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process, or returning to chlorosilane medium-cold rectification process. The overhead gas contains VCM and chloroalkanes. The chlorosilane medium-cold rectification process involves mixing crude chlorosilane liquid from multi-stage evaporation/compression/condensation process and/or the heavy component fluid from the bottom of the vacuum tower from the hydrogen chloride refining process into the chlorosilane medium and shallow cold distillation process, controlling operating temperature at -35-10 degrees C and operating pressure at 0.6-2.0 MPa, flowing out the non-condensable gas from the top of the rectification tower, feeding to pressure swing absorbers concentrator after cold and heat exchange, recovering light hydrocarbon, flowing out the chlorosilane liquid from the bottom of the distillation tower, vaporizing and returning a portion of chlorosilane liquid to the silicon carbide-chemical vapor deposition process for recycling, and directly returning another portion of chlorosilane liquid to the chlorosilane spray absorption process as an absorbent for recycling.

PN CN111778422-A; CN111778422-B
TI High-efficiency method for degassing aluminum water, involves utilizing an outer furnace body and a furnace hearth, where degassing cavity for containing aluminum water is arranged inside furnace hearth.
AU LU Y
   PENG B
   QIAO M
   LI B
   LI D
   CAI J
AE JIANGSU SUMEC ALUMINUM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A62147
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency method involves utilizing an outer furnace body and a furnace hearth. A degassing cavity for containing aluminum water is arranged inside the furnace hearth. A heating assembly is arranged between the outer furnace body and the furnace hearth. Furnace cover, a rotatable shaft 1 is vertically arranged in the degassing chamber, and a shaft 2 and a shaft 3 are horizontally arranged in the degassing chamber. The shafts 2 and 3 are respectively located on the left and right sides of the shaft 1. 2. It is perpendicular to the first shaft, each shaft is a hollow shaft, each shaft is provided with a gas channel, the inlet of each gas channel is connected with the gas source, the lower end of the first shaft is provided with an extension rotating portion.
   USE - High-efficiency method for degassing aluminium water.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-efficiency method can improve the degassing efficiency and the quality of the manufactured wheel hub blanks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The gas source includes three gas supply assembly channels arranged in parallel. The outlet of each gas supply component channel is connected to the mixed gas chamber, and nitrogen, helium, and carbon dioxide are respectively introduced into the three-way gas supply component channels. The method involves passing in the aluminum water. The aluminum water source 1 obtained by smelting aluminum ingots and the aluminum water source 2 obtained by smelting aluminum scrap in the combustion furnace are respectively passed through the mass ratio (2-3):1, passing the nitrogen, helium and carbon dioxide in the channels of the three-way gas supply component into the mixing chamber according to the volume ratio of (4-5): (2-2.5):1 for mixing, heating the aluminum water to 830-845 degrees C, and then according to the flow rate of 10-14l/min of mixed gas per 300l of aluminum water in the degassing chamber, the mixed gas in the mixed gas chamber is divided into three routes to the shaft. The gas flow ratio in branch three is (1.5~2): 1:1; at the same time, shaft one, shaft two and shaft three rotate at 600-650r/min, 550-600r/min, 550-600r/min, addding slag remover to the degassing chamber at the ratio of 0.8-0.9kg of slag remover per 100l of aluminum water, and stopping the degassing after each rotating shaft rotates for 3-5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The orizontal distance between the second rotating shaft and the first rotating shaft is 1.6-1.8m. Preferred Components: The rotating shaft is located above the outer rotating portion and is provided with at least three groups of rotating components at intervals along the axial direction. The rotating assembly includes four groups of agitating portions distributed at equal intervals in the circumferential direction. Each agitating portion is provided with branch channels inside, and each branch channel is connected to the gas channel, and the edge of the agitating portion is provided with a gas outlet.

PN CN111632037-A
TI Sodium alginate-chitosan microspheres used for            preparing protective agent, are prepared by adding            sodium alginate to normal saline, adding chitosan to            acetic acid solution, soaking microspheres in sodium            alginate solution, and washing.
AU CAO Y
   TENG R
   HE W
   WANG Y
   ZHAO Y
   BAI Z
AE BEIJING TANGYI HUIKANG BIOMEDICAL TECHNO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020896385
AB    NOVELTY - Sodium alginate-chitosan microsphere is                prepared by (a) adding sodium alginate to normal                saline, and uniformly stirring to obtain 1-3 %mass                sodium alginate solution, (b) adding chitosan to                5-60% acetic acid solution, and uniformly                dissolving to obtain 1-3 %mass chitosan solution,                (c) adding chitosan solution to sodium alginate                solution, uniformly stirring and dissolving, where                the volume ratio of chitosan solution and sodium                alginate solution is 1:5-4:1 to obtain                chitosan/sodium alginate dispersion, (d) preparing                chitosan/sodium alginate dispersion to microspheres                using spray drying process to obtain microspheres,                and (e) soaking microspheres in 0.1% sodium                alginate solution for 2-12 hours, where the                concentration of microspheres in sodium alginate                solution is 0.1-0.3 g/ml, washing and drying                crosslinked microspheres to obtain sodium                alginate-chitosan microspheres, where the diameter                of sodium alginate-chitosan microspheres is 5-50 mu                m.
   USE - The sodium alginate-chitosan microspheres are                used for preparing protective agent for                encapsulating mesenchymal stem cells, preparing                microsphere carriers for stem cell preparations,                and useful in preparation of products for                maintaining viability and pluripotency of stem                cells (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sodium alginate-chitosan microspheres are                prepared by simple process, efficient and easy in                mass production, and have uniform and controllable                particle size and excellent dispersibility,                performance and biocompatibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a stem cell coated with sodium                alginate-chitosan microspheres, involving preparing                stem cells by adding 0.2-0.5 g sodium                alginate-chitosan microspheres to 10-30 ml                dulbecco's modified eagle's medium supplemented                with 10% fetal bovine serum, where the                concentration of sodium alginate-chitosan                microspheres in culture medium is 7-50 g/l,                inocultaing the stem cells to above-mentioned                microspheres at density of 1-3x 106/ml, culturing                at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide incubator,                changing the medium every other day, centrifuging                after digesting the cells on 5th day adding                dulbecco's modified eagle's medium supplemented                with 10% fetal bovine serum, placing in incubator,                and culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon                dioxide for 5-7 days; and
   (2) a spray system prepared using stem cells,                comprising connecting spray instrument with drug                delivery tracheal pipeline prepared by                polyurethene, where the diameter of medicine                tracheal pipeline is 2 mm, and the length is 20-25                cm, one end of drug delivery tracheal pipeline                comprising micropores, and the other end is                connected with sprayer, and the sprayer is filled                with solution containing stem cells coated with                sodium alginate-chitosan microspheres to obtain                spray system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  sodium alginate in sodium alginate solution is 2                  %mass. Preferred Conditions: The stirring speed                  when stirring and dissolving in step (c) is                  50-300 rpm. The spray drying in step (d) involves                  the inlet air temperature is carried out at                  120-180 degrees C, the outlet air temperature is                  carried out at 50-80 degrees C, and the feed rate                  is 5-10 ml/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  chitosan in chitosan solution in step (b) is 2                  %mass.

PN CN111606343-A
TI Preparing modified nano calcium carbonate, comprises e.g. preparing lime slurry using quicklime, mixing with hydrogel prepared from e.g. tannic acid and polyvinyl alcohol, carbonizing, and reacting with stearic acid, urea and starch.
AU WEI M
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202087248N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified nano calcium carbonate, comprises e.g. (i) mixing by stirring quicklime and water, adding EDTA, continuously stirring, sieving, filtering to remove slag to obtain lime slurry and adjusting solid content of lime slurry to 10-12 wt.%, (ii) mixing tannic acid and deionized water, ultrasonically oscillating to obtain solution A, adding polyvinyl alcohol into mixed solution A under stirring condition, heating to 85-90 degrees C, maintaining constant temperature for 3-5 hours, naturally cooling to 20-25 degrees C to obtain hydrogel, (iii) mixing lime slurry and hydrogel under stirring condition to obtain mixed solution B, adding carbon dioxide into mixed solution B, carbonizing, heating, adding stearic acid, urea and starch, and mixing by stirring to obtain modified nano calcium carbonate suspension, and (iv) filtering obtained modified nano calcium carbonate suspension and vacuum drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves and controls crystal form of nano calcium carbonate product; and produces nano calcium carbonate product having large specific surface area, and loose and porous appearance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing modified nano calcium carbonate, comprises (i) mixing quicklime and water in a mass ratio of 1:3-5, adding EDTA, continuously stirring for 15-30 minutes, sieving through 150-200 mesh sieve, filtering to remove slag to obtain lime slurry and adjusting solid content of lime slurry to 10-12 wt.%, where addition amount of EDTA is 10-15% of the mass of quicklime, (ii) taking tannic acid, polyvinyl alcohol and deionized water in a mass ratio of 2-3:4-6:100, mixing tannic acid and deionized water, ultrasonically oscillating for 10-15 minutes to obtain solution A, adding polyvinyl alcohol into mixed solution A under stirring condition, heating to 85-90 degrees C, maintaining constant temperature for 3-5 hours, naturally cooling to 20-25 degrees C to obtain hydrogel, (iii) mixing lime slurry and hydrogel in a mass ratio of 8-10:1 under stirring condition to obtain mixed solution B, adding carbon dioxide into mixed solution B, carbonizing for 10-30 minutes, heating to 50-60 degrees C, adding stearic acid, urea and starch, and mixing by stirring for 2-5 hours to obtain modified nano calcium carbonate suspension, where the mass ratio between lime slurry, stearic acid, urea and starch is 20:1-3:1-3:2-5, and (iv) filtering obtained modified nano calcium carbonate suspension and vacuum drying to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the quicklime is composed of more than 95 wt.% calcium oxide and less than 2 wt.% silicon dioxide. Preferred Method: In step (i), the stirring speed is 500-600 revolutions/minute. In step (ii): the ultrasonic frequency is 40 kHz and the ultrasonic power is 100 W; and the stirring speed is 100-150 revolutions/minute. In step (iv), the vacuum drying is carried out at 85-90 degrees C for 2-3 hours under a pressure of 0.05-0.08 MPa.

PN CN111537625-A; CN111537625-B
TI Detecting dimethyl fumarate in medical textile and            leather involves weighing dimethyl fumarate standard            product, diluting it to corresponding volume with the            extracting agent, and obtaining standard stock solution            of dimethyl fumarate.
AU BAO Q
   FU K
   WEI H
   LU J
   GUO H
   LIU J
AE NINGBO HEALTH SCI COLLEGE (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202082012J
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting dimethyl fumarate in medical textile                and leather involves weighing dimethyl fumarate                standard product, diluting it to corresponding                volume with the extracting agent, and obtaining                standard stock solution of dimethyl fumarate. The                extracting agent is used as solvent, standard stock                solution of dimethyl fumarate is diluted into                working standard solutions of various concentration                of dimethyl fumarate according to testing                requirement. The method involves weighing medical                textile and/or leather samples to be tested,                putting it into extraction tank, adding entrainer                to entrainer bottle and sealing it, adjusting                extraction pressure, extraction temperature, carbon                dioxide flow rate and entrainer content, and                preparing sample by supercritical CO2 extraction                method to obtain sample extraction liquid. The                constant volume of sample extraction liquid is                transferred to a pear-shaped bottle, which is                rotated in a water bath to evaporate and                concentrate sample extraction liquid.
   USE - Method for detecting dimethyl fumarate in                medical textile and leather.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to detect dimethyl fumarate                in medical textile and leather, which has simple                operation, fast speed, high accuracy and low                detection limit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting dimethyl fumarate in medical textile                and leather involves weighing dimethyl fumarate                standard product, diluting it to corresponding                volume with the extracting agent, and obtaining                standard stock solution of dimethyl fumarate. The                extracting agent is used as solvent, standard stock                solution of dimethyl fumarate is diluted into                working standard solutions of various concentration                of dimethyl fumarate according to testing                requirement. The method involves weighing medical                textile and/or leather samples to be tested,                putting it into extraction tank, adding entrainer                to entrainer bottle and sealing it, adjusting                extraction pressure, extraction temperature, carbon                dioxide flow rate and entrainer content, and                preparing sample by supercritical CO2 extraction                method to obtain sample extraction liquid. The                constant volume of sample extraction liquid is                transferred to a pear-shaped bottle, which is                rotated in a water bath to evaporate and                concentrate sample extraction liquid, adding                extraction agent to clean pear-shaped bottle,                extract lotion is centrifuged in a centrifuge, and                supernatant is taken to be analyzed. The                chromatographic separation condition is determined,                gas chromatographic mass spectrometry is performed                on supernatant, retention time of dimethyl fumarate                and total ion current diagram and mass spectrum are                obtained, strongest signal is determined by viewing                purity of characteristic ions of chromatographic                peak feature ions, selective ion detection is used                for qualitative, and peak area of characteristic                ion with strongest signal for quantification is                obtained.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The extracting agent                  is ethyl acetate. The entrainer is ethyl acetate.                  Preferred Conditions: The method involves                  adopting response surface method, taking                  extraction pressure, extraction temperature,                  extraction time and carbon dioxide flow rate as                  variables, taking extraction rate of dimethyl                  fumarate as response value, and adopting                  polynomial fitting regression method, and then                  constructing corresponding regression equation to                  determine the optimal extraction conditions. The                  response surface method is performed at                  extraction pressure of 100-500bar, extraction                  temperature of 20-60 degrees C, carbon dioxide                  flow rate of 10-50g/minute and extraction time of                  10-50 minutes. The selected extraction is                  performed at extraction pressure of 447.94 bar,                  extraction temperature of 60 degrees C,                  extraction time of 50 minutes and carbon dioxide                  flow rate of 50g/minute. The chromatographic                  separation is performed at inlet temperature of                  220 degrees C, chromatographic column temperature                  rise curve of 50 degrees C for 1 minute, at 150                  degrees C for 1 minute, at 260 degrees C for 1                  minute, and mass spectrometry and gas                  chromatography interface temperature is 280                  degrees C. The gas chromatography mass                  spectrometry is performed on prepared standard                  working solutions of dimethyl fumarate with                  various concentrations by adopting selective ion                  detection for qualitative, extracting peak area                  of characteristic ion with strongest signal for                  quantification, using chromatographic peak area                  as ordinate, and using concentration of dimethyl                  fumarate on abscissa. A standard curve is                  established to verify linear relationship of                  dimethyl fumarate in corresponding concentration                  range. The method involves taking two samples of                  medical textiles and leather that do not contain                  dimethyl fumarate, taking three samples of each,                  adding prepared working standard solutions of                  dimethyl fumarate with various concentrations,                  performing supercritical carbon dioxide                  extraction in accordance with determined                  chromatographic separation conditions, and                  determination is carried out for five times in                  same day and five consecutive days to calculate                  intra-day and inter-day precision and recovery                  rate of standard addition.

PN CN111302342-A; CN212440619-U
TI Manufacturing super filtration module by crushing            and grinding activated carbon fiber, then adding            asphalt and additive, mixing, pouring other additive            aqueous solution, mixing, extruding to obtain super            filter module blank, and drying.
AU YANG J
   YANG G
AE JIANGSU KEJING CARBON FIBER CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202058103X
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing super filtration module using                activated carbon fiber powder involves: (1)                crushing and grinding the activated carbon fiber,                and then sieving; (2) taking activated carbon fiber                powder, adding asphalt and No.1 additive, and                thoroughly mixing in a mixer; (3) dissolving No.2                additive in water; (4) pouring the No.2 additive                aqueous solution into the mixer for mixing until                the various components are evenly mixed; (5)                extruding the mixed raw materials into square,                round or fan shape in a mold to make a super filter                module blank; (6) air-drying the blank under                natural conditions; (7) placing the blank in an                oven and drying; (8) putting the dried blank into a                carbonization activation device, passing nitrogen                for protection, heating and then carrying out                constant temperature carbonization; (9) introducing                an activator carbon dioxide gas, heating and then                activating; and (10) when the temperature is                reduced to 45-55&#176; C, taking out the                finished product of the super filter module.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing super                filtration module using activated carbon fiber                powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The super filtration module has high specific                surface area and strength, and excellent absorbing                ability and molding property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing super filtration module using                activated carbon fiber powder involves: (1)                crushing and grinding the activated carbon fiber                into a 180-220 mesh activated carbon fiber powder,                and sieving; (2) taking activated carbon fiber                powder as a main raw material, adding asphalt and                No.1 additive, and thoroughly mixing in a mixer                until the color is uniform without binding                particles; (3) dissolving No.2 additive in water;                (4) pouring the No.2 additive aqueous solution into                the mixer for mixing until the various components                are evenly mixed; (5) extruding the mixed raw                materials into square, round or fan shape in a mold                to make a super filter module blank; (6) air-drying                the blank under natural conditions; (7) placing the                blank in an oven and drying at a constant                temperature of 110-130&#176; C; (8) putting the                dried blank into a carbonization activation device,                passing nitrogen for protection, heating and then                carrying out constant temperature carbonization;                (9) introducing an activator carbon dioxide gas,                heating and then activating at constant                temperature; and (10) when the temperature is                reduced to 45-55&#176; C, taking out the                finished product of the super filter module under                the protection of high-purity nitrogen. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the carbonization                activation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: In step (8), the                  temperature required for constant temperature                  carbonization is 300-400&#176; C, and the                  time is 40 minutes. In step (9), the temperature                  required for constant temperature activation is                  900-1000&#176; C, and the time is 20 minutes.                  Preferred Components: The No.1 additive is                  carboxymethyl cellulose. The No.2 additive is a                  water-soluble phenolic resin. The mass ratio of                  the activated carbon fiber powder, asphalt,                  carboxymethyl cellulose, water-soluble phenolic                  resin and water is 7.5:1:1.2:1.3:2.

TI Hydrogen production apparatus consists of modifier            having burner in which source gas and water vapor            containing hydrocarbon react in presence of catalyst,            and recovery control portion.
AU EGUCHI K
   KUSHI T
   IINUMA H
AE TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
   TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
GA 202054151H
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen production apparatus consists of a                modifier which has a burner for being a modifier                which makes the source gas and water vapor                containing a hydrocarbon react in presence of a                catalyst, and produces carbon monoxide and                hydrogen, and outputs reformed gas, and heating the                catalyst, carbon monoxide conversion device,                pressure-swing-adsorption apparatus which separates                the conversion gas into product hydrogen and the                off gas by repeating, off-gas tank which is                connected to the pressure-swing-adsorption                apparatus and the burner, and stores the off gas,                pressure sensor which measures the pressure in the                off-gas tank, acquisition portion which acquires                the pressure from the pressure sensor, and recovery                control portion. The carbon monoxide conversion                device makes the carbon monoxide and water vapor in                the reformed gas from the modifier react, and                produce a carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and produces                a conversion gas.
   USE - Apparatus for producing hydrogen used for                metal bright annealings such as steel plate and                glass manufacture.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen production apparatus provides by                operation method capable of suppressing decrease in                amount of refined product hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogen production apparatus consists of a                modifier which has a burner for being a modifier                which makes the source gas and water vapor                containing a hydrocarbon react in presence of a                catalyst, and produces carbon monoxide and                hydrogen, and outputs reformed gas, and heating the                catalyst, carbon monoxide conversion device,                pressure-swing-adsorption apparatus which separates                the conversion gas into product hydrogen and the                off gas by repeating, off-gas tank which is                connected to the pressure-swing-adsorption                apparatus and the burner, and stores the off gas,                pressure sensor which measures the pressure in the                off-gas tank, acquisition portion which acquires                the pressure from the pressure sensor, and recovery                control portion. The carbon monoxide conversion                device makes the carbon monoxide and water vapor in                the reformed gas from the modifier react, and                produce a carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and produces                a conversion gas. The impurity in the conversion                gas from the carbon monoxide conversion device is                adsorbed to an adsorption agent layer in a                pressurization environment, and the pressure is                reduced to preset pressure to remove the impurity                from the adsorption agent layer, and discharge the                impurity as an off gas. The recovery control                portion recovers the pressure more than the lower                limit when the pressure acquired in the acquisition                portion becomes lower than a lower limit. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for operation of                hydrogen production apparatus.
