TI Adipose stem cell composition for wound repair, comprises adipose stem cell culture product and medicinal adjuvant, where cell culture product is prepared by inoculating adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, culturing, and centrifuging.
AU CHEN X
   ZHANG J
   HAN H
   FENG C
AE UNICELL LIFE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UNIC-Non-standard)
GA 202055936C
AB    NOVELTY - Adipose stem cell composition comprises an adipose stem cell culture product of 1-2 wt.% as an active ingredient and at least one medicinal adjuvant that is used externally, where the adipose stem cell culture product is prepared by (a) inoculating adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells in 3-6x 104/ml medium which is the basic medium containing the inducing culture reagent, (b) culturing the petri dish inoculated with adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells under the conditions of 10-15% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C, and changing the medium every 3 days, co-culturing for 7-8 days, and (c) centrifuging to remove the medium and collecting the cells after the culture is completed, washing the cells with phosphate buffer solution 2-3 times, homogenizing the cells below 4 degrees C, and freezing, adding in warm water below 42 degrees C, thawing, taking the supernatant by centrifugation, dialyzing, and freezing.
   USE - The adipose stem cell composition is used for wound repair.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Adipose stem cell composition comprises an adipose stem cell culture product of 1-2 wt.% as an active ingredient and at least one medicinal adjuvant that is used externally, where the adipose stem cell culture product is prepared by (a) inoculating adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells in 3-6x 104/ml in the medium which is the basic medium containing the inducing culture reagent, and the basic medium contains 10 vol.% fraction low-sugar DMEM medium for fetal bovine serum, the induction culture reagents are dexamethasone, diammonium glycyrrhizinate, vitamin D3, and retinoic acid, the dosage of the induction culture reagents in the culture medium is 0.2-0.3 mu mol/L dexamethasone, 10-15 mu mol/L diammonium glycyrrhizinate, 6-10 mu mol/L vitamin D3, 1-3 mu mol/L retinoic acid, (b) culturing the petri dish inoculated with adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells under the conditions of 10-15% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C, and changing the medium every 3 days, co-culturing for 7-8 days, and (c) centrifuging to remove the medium and collecting the cells after the culture is completed, washing the cells with phosphate buffer solution 2-3 times, homogenizing the cells below 4 degrees C, and freezing the resulting homogenate until completely frozen, adding in warm water below 42 degrees C, thawing thoroughly, taking the supernatant by centrifugation, placing the supernatant in a dialysis bag, dialyzing at 4 degrees C phosphate buffer solution for 8-24 hours, freezing the solution in the dialysis bag as a product of adipose stem cell culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition is a liquid dressing, and the medicinal adjuvants for external use include chitosan, acetic acid, hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, pH adjusting agent and the remaining amount of water. Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 1-3 wt.%, preferably 1.5-2.5 wt.% chitosan, and 0.5-1 wt.% hydroxypropyl methylcellulose. Preferred Properties: The deacetylation degree of the chitosan is 85-95%. The pH adjusting agent adjusts the pH of the composition to 6.5-7.0.
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary.

TI Producing putty powder using industrial alkali residue white mud comprises introducing alkali residue white mud, collecting filtrate, adding dispersing and chelating agent, heating, drying, micro-crushing, adding e.g. cellulose and mixing.
AU LI B
   CHEN Z
AE GUANGDONG YUFENG POWDER MATERIAL CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020514471
AB    NOVELTY - Producing putty powder using industrial alkali residue white mud comprises (a) introducing the alkali residue white mud into high pressure kettle in the first supercritical state, maintaining the temperature and pressure, (b) introducing the materials in the kettle into the first purifying pond, adding flocculating agent, fully stirring, allowing to stand, separating and collecting the filtrate to obtain residue, (c) adding water to residue, adding dispersing agent and chelating agent, heating, filtering, using water, filtering and re-pulping, pulping, filtering, repeating the operation to obtain the fully pulped filter residue, (d) fully pulping and drying to obtain powder and (e) carrying out micro-crushing, adding glue, cellulose, wetting agent, thickening agent and white cement and uniformly mixing.
   USE - The method is useful for producing putty powder using industrial alkali residue white mud.
   ADVANTAGE - The putty powder: is uniform in the container without agglomeration, smooth in construction and coating and excellent in whiteness and texture, sanding and water resistance; and has good bonding strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing putty powder using industrial alkali residue white mud comprises (a) introducing the alkali residue white mud into high pressure kettle in the first supercritical state, maintaining the temperature at 32-35 degrees C and stirring for 2-3 hours, maintaining the pressure in the kettle at 7.5-7.8 MPa for 2-3 hours after introducing carbon dioxide, maintaining the pressure at second supercritical state at 40-45 degrees C for 1 hour after stirring, introducing carbon dioxide for maintaining the pressure in the kettle at 8-10 MPa for 1-2 hours, maintaining the pressure in the third supercritical state at 45-50 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour after stirring, (b) introducing the materials in the kettle into the first purifying pond, adding flocculating agent, fully stirring, allowing to stand, separating and collecting the filtrate to obtain residue, (c) adding 3-4 times of water to the residue obtained in the step (b), adding dispersing agent and chelating agent, heating to 50-60 degrees C heat preservation, filtering, using 0.5-1 times of water, filtering and re-pulping, pulping, filtering, repeating the operation for 3-5 times to obtain the fully pulped filter residue, (d) fully pulping and drying to obtain powder and (e) carrying out micro-crushing, adding glue, cellulose, wetting agent, thickening agent and white cement and uniformly mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The holding time in the step (c) is 2-3 hours. The temperature during beating in the step (c) is 40-45 degrees C. Preferred Components: The dispersant in the step (c) is disodium edetate. The chelating agent in the step (c) is an amino acid chelating agent. The thickener in the step (e) is methyl cellulose ether. The wetting agent in the step (e) is Span-80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80). The weight ratio of powder, glue, cellulose, wetting agent, thickener and white cement is 10:20-30:2-5:1-2:1-2:50-60.

AU LONG J
AE DONGGUAN AIHUXI ELECTRICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201985628S
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalyst air purifying powder for interior wall, comprises calcium hydroxide with purity requirement is 90-99%, nano zinc oxide with particle size requirement is 1-100 nanometer, plant cellulose which requires removal of lignin, pectin, resin, fat, etc. from plant cellulose, mixing 60-89% calcium hydroxide, 10-35% nano zinc oxide, 1-5% plant cellulose, mechanical stirring speed of 600-2000 revolution/minute, after preparation and storage, photocatalyst air purifying powder can not only meet people's requirements for esthetics and decorative effects, but also is non-toxic and harmless, does not harm environment and human health, and has antibacterial and photocatalytic effects, which can kill environment harmful bacteria that decompose harmful gasses in environment, formaldehyde, benzene, ammonia, carbon monoxide, sulfur dioxide.
   USE - Photocatalyst air purifying powder for interior wall.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst air purifying powder solves problem that material is easy to fall off after construction, non toxic and has low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Photocatalyst air purifying powder for interior interior wall, comprises calcium hydroxide with purity requirement is 90-99%, nano zinc oxide with particle size requirement is 1-100 nanometer, plant cellulose which requires removal of lignin, pectin, resin, fat, etc. from plant cellulose, mixing 60-89% calcium hydroxide, 10-35% nano zinc oxide, 1-5% plant cellulose, mechanical stirring speed of 600-2000 revolution/minute, after preparation and storage, photocatalyst air purifying powder can not only meet people's requirements for esthetics and decorative effects, but also is non-toxic and harmless, does not harm environment and human health, and has antibacterial and photocatalytic effects, which can kill environment harmful bacteria that decompose harmful gasses in environment, formaldehyde, benzene, ammonia, carbon monoxide, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxides can play role in purifying environment and maintaining health. The calcium hydroxide white powder commonly known as slaked lime or slaked lime is strong alkali substance, soluble in water, solution after water added is easy to dissolve nano zinc oxide, is commonly used building material, price incorruptible, chemically react with carbon dioxide in air to form calcium carbonate. The calcium hydroxide is reacted with carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate, where calcium carbonate is white solid which is chemically stable at normal temperature. The high-purity calcium hydroxide is used as core functional material nano-zinc oxide carrier, which can meet public's esthetic requirements for interior decoration materials and can be mixed with nano-zinc oxide to facilitate use of interior decoration and construction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The thickness of indoor wall is required to be 0.2-0.5mm. Preferred Process: The method involves using photocatalytic energy to activate catalyst, reaction can be carry out under normal temperature and pressure. The photocatalytic technique is organic combination of photochemistry and catalyst. The natural light or light is used as light source to excite generated active radicals contaminant reaction, contaminant + OH or O2-H2O, inorganic small molecule zinc oxide possesses photocatalytic function because when it is expose to light, electrons on zinc atom are excite, and its orbit changes to produce strong oxidizing power. The organic matter is decomposed into carbon dioxide and water molecules and can also degrade some inorganic substances. The general building materials, decorative materials and chemicals used will release variety of volatile organic and inorganic substances, such as formaldehyde, stupid, ammonia, acetone, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, carbon monoxide, most of these harmful gases, through photocatalytic action of nano-zinc oxide, are completely decomposed and destroyed, reached inorganicization, and finally leaving clean air, superior to nano-zinc oxide. The photocatalytic action and the light energy are applied at normal temperature and pressure. The nano zinc oxide is used as core functional material. The pectin, resin, fat and other impurities are removed by processing cellulose and hemicellulose are left. This is a major feature of this formula. Cellulose and hemicellulose are very chemically stable. The coating has different degrees of cracking and shedding, it is found that plant cellulose has very good crack resistance. It is widely used in the slurry concrete, and the plant cellulose is added to the formula, and same experimental observation is added. The coating of plant fiber does not fall off and crack. The plant cellulose can be decomposed well in water and lignin in plant cellulose is needed, glue, resin, fat and other impurities are removed. The wood pulp paper towels are ideal raw material, plant cellulose is processed from natural wood, straw, wheat straw, reed, bamboo, wood, hemp and cotton.

PN CN110204812-A
TI Preparing HDPE material having good bending strength comprises e.g. placing silicon carbide whiskers in glassware, adding 1,4-dichlorobutane to glassware, sealing glassware, heating, placing sealed glassware under 60Co irradiation source.
AU XU Y
AE XU Y (XUYY-Individual)
GA 2019785118
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high density polyethylene (HDPE) material having good bending strength comprises e.g. (1) placing the silicon carbide whiskers in glassware, adding 1,4-dichlorobutane to the glassware, sealing the glassware, heating, placing sealed glassware under 60Co irradiation source for gamma ray irradiation, adjusting glassware temperature after the irradiation treatment, maintaining for 30 minutes, removing the treated silicon carbide whiskers by filtration, immersing and washing the treated silicon carbide whiskers with ethanol, subjecting the treated silicon carbide whiskers to activation treatment in trifluoromethanesulfonic anhydride solution, filtering, washing with deionized water, and drying to constant weight to obtain modified silicon carbide whiskers; (2) separately adding the flax fibers to reaction kettle, adding ethanol, stirring evenly, adding 25 pts. wt. deionized water, heating, introducing carbon dioxide into reactor, removing air in reactor, sealing, and stirring.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing HDPE material having good bending strength (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The HDPE material: has excellent bending strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high density polyethylene (HDPE) material having good bending strength comprises (1) placing the silicon carbide whiskers in glassware, adding 1,4-dichlorobutane to the glassware, sealing the glassware, heating to 70-73 degrees C for 1 hour, placing sealed glassware under 60Co irradiation source for gamma ray irradiation, adjusting glassware temperature to 55-58 degrees C after the irradiation treatment, maintaining for 30 minutes, removing the treated silicon carbide whiskers by filtration, immersing and washing the treated silicon carbide whiskers for 10-15 minutes with ethanol, subjecting the treated silicon carbide whiskers to activation treatment in trifluoromethanesulfonic anhydride solution for 2 hours, filtering, washing with deionized water, and drying to constant weight to obtain modified silicon carbide whiskers; (2) separately adding the 20-25 flax fibers to reaction kettle, adding 200 pts. wt. ethanol, stirring evenly, adding 25 pts. wt. deionized water, heating to 60-62 degrees C for 30-35 minutes, introducing carbon dioxide into reactor, removing air in reactor, sealing, stirring at 1500 revolutions/minute, heating to 122-125 degrees C for 78-80 minutes, releasing the pressure, removing flax fibers by filtration, washing with deionized water, and drying under vacuum to constant weight to obtain modified hemp fibers; and (3) uniformly adding 1000 pts. wt. HDPE, 15-18 pts. wt. tributyl citrate, 20-24 pts. wt. modified flax fiber, and 5-6 pts. wt. modified silicon carbide whisker to high-speed mixer, mixing at high speed and adding to twin-screw extruder for extrusion molding, to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), completely immersing the silicon carbide whiskers in 1,4 -dichlorobutane in glassware. In step (1), the gamma ray irradiation treatment time is 12 minutes, and the irradiation dose is 550 kGY. In the step (1), the activation temperature of the trifluoromethanesulfonic anhydride solution is 60 degrees C. In the step (2), the pressure of the carbon dioxide in the reaction kettle is 1.2-1.4 MPa. In the step 3), the high speed mixer has rotation speed of 2500 revolutions/minute. In the step (3), the temperature control parameters of twin-screw extruder includes feed section temperature is 172 degrees C, the compression section temperature is 185 degrees C, head temperature is 198 degrees C, and mold temperature is 192 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (1), the concentration of the trifluoromethanesulfonic anhydride solution is 0.38 mol/l.

PN CN110182995-A; CN110182995-B
TI Advanced treatment method for coking wastewater comprises e.g. passing biochemically treated coking wastewater, adding flocculating agent, reacting, removing suspended colloidal particles, passing effluent, and separating mud, and water.
AU WANG L
   ZHANG L
   DUAN A
   XIAO J
   YAN B
   LIU P
   LIU X
AE WUHAN STEEL CO LTD (WSGC-C)
GA 2019760421
AB    NOVELTY - Advanced treatment method for coking wastewater comprises (i) passing biochemically treated coking wastewater into reaction tank, adding flocculating agent, reacting for removing suspended colloidal particles carried in wastewater, (ii) passing effluent from reaction tank into precipitating tank for separating mud, and water, (iii) passing effluent from precipitating tank into filter packed with oxidative reactive filter material, (iv) passing effluent of first reactive filtration into ozone catalytic oxidation device, oxidizing organic substances which are difficult to be degraded by biochemical effluent by hydroxy radicals through ozone, degrading into small molecules, or carbon dioxide, performing ozone oxidation, and (v) filtering effluent catalyzed by ozone into filter packed with reactive filter material, removing small amount of suspended substance, degrading organic matter in water oxidized out of ozone, and after filtering the effluent chemical oxygen demand is less than or equal to 40 mg/l.
   USE - The method is useful for advanced treatment method for coking wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: breaks through traditional inert filter material filtering conception; filtration reaction is integrated by using oxidizing reactive filter material; degrades organic matter while filtering; and has stable operation, processing effect, and low processing cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Advanced treatment method for coking wastewater comprises (i) passing biochemically treated coking wastewater into reaction tank, adding flocculating agent, reacting for removing suspended colloidal particles carried in wastewater, (ii) passing effluent from reaction tank into precipitating tank for separating mud, and water, and suspended solids in the precipitating tank are less than or equal to 30 mg/l, chemical oxygen demand less than or equal to 120 mg/l, (iii) passing effluent from precipitating tank into filter packed with oxidative reactive filter material, and effluent chemical oxygen demand after filtration is 80-100 mg/l, and suspended matter is 10-20 mg/l, (iv) passing effluent of first reactive filtration into ozone catalytic oxidation device, oxidizing organic substances which are difficult to be degraded by biochemical effluent by hydroxy radicals through ozone, degrading into small molecules, or carbon dioxide, performing ozone oxidation, the effluent chemical oxygen demand is 40-50 mg/l, and suspended substance is 5-10 mg/l, and (v) filtering effluent catalyzed by ozone into filter packed with reactive filter material, removing small amount of suspended substance carried in wastewater, further degrading organic matter in water oxidized out of ozone, and after filtering the effluent chemical oxygen demand is less than or equal to 40 mg/l, and suspended substance is less than or equal to 10 mg/l.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the flocculating agent is polyaluminum ferric chloride flocculant. In the steps (iii), and (v), the volume ratio of three raw materials coking coke powder, rolled steel oxide scale, and blast furnace dust ash is 1:1-2:1-3. The ratio of sum of volume of three raw materials, and volume of organic binder is 1:1-3. The coked coke powder has a particle diameter of 0.15-0.3 mm. The particle size of blast furnace dust ash is 0.15-0.3 mm. The rolled steel oxide scale has a particle size of 0.5-2 mm. In the step (E) the volume ratio of deionized water, starch, and calcium lignosulfonate to the volume of organic binder is 1:1-3. Preferred Method: The polyaluminum ferric chloride flocculant is obtained by rolling steel oxide scale, rolling steel waste acid, and power plant fly ash comprising (a) filtering, and removing waste acid from rolled steel, (b) dissolving rolled iron oxide scale in waste acid after filtering, and removing impurities, stirring and reacting for 30-60 minutes, where the liquid-solid ratio is 3-5:1, acid pickling, and taking supernatant, (c) dissolving power plant fly ash in waste acid after impurity removal in a liquid-solid ratio of 2-4:1, stirring and reacting for 30-60 minutes, acid pickling, and taking supernatant, and (d) mixing the supernatants obtained in steps (a) and (b), adjusting volume ratio of two for making make the mass content of aluminum oxide after mixing to 10-15%, total iron content to 8-12%, adding 0.1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution for controlling the alkalinity to 8-12%, carrying out hydrolysis reaction at 50-80 degrees C to obtain the finished product. In the step (i), stirring the reaction tank for 1-2 minutes, and speed gradient G value is 700-1000 S-1. In the step (ii), the precipitating tank uses flow-type precipitating tank with water outlet around center water inlet, and hydraulic retention time of precipitating tank is more than 30 minutes. In the step (iii), the filtering time of filter time tower is 0.2-0.4 mm/s, and hydraulic retention time is 30-60 minutes. In the step (v), the filtering time of filtration time tower is 0.3-0.5 mm/s, and hydraulic retention time is 20-40 minutes. In the steps (iii), and (v), the reactive filter material is prepared by coking coke powder, rolled steel oxide scale, and blast furnace dust ash, then adding organic binder, then mixing, granulating, and sintering into the prepared iron carbon filter material. The organic binder is prepared by mixing deionized water, starch, calcium lignosulfonate in a volume ratio of 1:0.1-0.2:0.1-0.2. The preparation method of the reactive filter material comprises (A) treating rolled iron oxide scale comprising sieving steel oxide scale having particle size of 0.5-2 mm, washing with water at least twice for removing water-soluble salts, and impurities in the scale, then using 1-5 mol/l sodium hydroxide alkali, stirring, soaking for not less than 12 hours, carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain solid, washing the solid, and removing impurities, (B) pretreating blast furnace dust ash comprising sieving blast furnace dust ash having particle size of 0.15-0.3 mm, washing with water for removing water-soluble salt substances and impurities in blast furnace dust ash, then using 1-5 mol/l sodium hydroxide alkali solution, stirring, soaking for not less than 12 hours, further carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain solid, washing the solid, and removing impurities, (C) pretreating coke powder comprising in the coking process, using coke powder having particle diameter of 0.15-0.30 mm for washing and removing impurities, (D) pretreating three raw materials in accordance with coking coke powder, rolled steel oxide scale, and blast furnace dust ash in a volume ratio of 1:1-2:1-3, and mixing uniformly, (E) uniformly mixing deionized water, starch and calcium lignosulfonate in a volume ratio of 1:0.1-0.2:0.1-0.2 to obtain organic binder, then adding organic binder to the step (D) to obtain mixture, and stirring uniformly, (F) granulating, and preparing spherical particles having particle diameter of 5-10 mm, (G) drying the spherical particles at normal temperature for not less than 24 hours, and (H) sintering under isolated air at 1000-1200 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and sintering atmosphere is nitrogen-protected reducing atmosphere. In the step (iv), catalytic oxidation of ozone by using iron oxide red waste to obtain catalytic material by surface modification of small molecular acid, dosage of ozone is 50-100 g/h, and wastewater retention time is between 10-20 minutes. The method further comprises desalting comprising passing second reactive filtration effluent into desalting unit, desalting by electrosorption method combined with membrane method, and then the effluent conductivity after desalting is 200-500 mu Sx cm-1, and effluent chemical oxygen demand is less than 20 mg/l.

PN CN110064345-A
TI Preparation of magnetic hydrogel, involves taking mixed solvent, acrylamide, sodium acrylate, adding polyacrylic acid and sodium acrylate, stirring, discharging material, and collecting discharged material.
AU LONG S
AE LONG S (LONG-Individual)
GA 201968168A
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of magnetic hydrogel involves (1) putting calcium oxide, water and additives into the reactor, mixing well, stirring and heating, and cooling, and passing carbon dioxide from the bottom of reactor, carrying out reaction, (2) adding auxiliary agent to reactor, and adjusting pH by adding aqueous ammonia, and stirring and mixing mixture, spraying the mixture, drying, ball milling, collecting ball milled powder, (3) milling ferric chloride and ferrous chloride, and collecting milled material, ultrasonically oscillating milled product and mixed solvent and adding a guiding agent with milled material, standing, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain magnetic particles, (4) taking mixed solvent, acrylamide, sodium acrylate and magnetic particles, adding initiator and auxiliary agent, raising temperature, and adding polyacrylic acid and sodium acrylate, continuing stirring, discharging material, and collecting discharged material to obtain magnetic hydrogel.
   USE - Preparation of magnetic hydrogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The void can be well formed to load the magnetic particles while the formation of nano-materials can effectively increase the mechanical properties and magnetic properties of hydrogels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of magnetic hydrogel involves (1) putting calcium oxide, water and additives into the reactor at a mass ratio of (4-7):20:(1-3), mixing well, stirring and heating, and cooling, and passing carbon dioxide from the bottom of reactor at 100-120 mL/minutes, carrying out the reaction at 20-23 degrees C, (2) adding auxiliary agent having a calcium oxide mass of 10-15% to the reactor after the reaction is completed, allowing to stand the mixture, and adjusting pH to 8.0-8.5 by adding aqueous ammonia, and stirring and mixing the mixture at 60-65 degrees C, and spraying the mixture, drying, ball milling, collecting ball milled powder, (3) milling ferric chloride and ferrous chloride at mass ratio of 1:(8-10):(3-5), and collecting milled material, ultrasonically oscillating the milled product and mixed solvent at a mass ratio of 4:(9-11) and adding a guiding agent with a mass of 1-3% of milled material, standing, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain magnetic particles, (4) taking 150-180 pts. wt. mixed solvent, 30-40 pts. wt. acrylamide, 20-30 pts. wt. sodium acrylate, 14-16 pts. wt. magnetic particles, 10-13 pts. wt. polyacrylic acid, 3-8 pts. wt. initiator, and 2-5 pts. wt. auxiliary agent, placing the mixed solvent, acrylamide, magnetic particles in reaction vessel, protecting nitrogen, adding initiator and auxiliary agent, raising temperature, and adding polyacrylic acid and sodium acrylate, continuing stirring, discharging material, and collecting discharged material to obtain magnetic hydrogel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation method of additive involves (A) ultrasonically oscillating composite titanium mixture and absolute ethanol at a mass ratio of 1:(3-5), and adding the inhibitor of composite titanium mixture in an amount of 2-4 %mass, stirring and mixing, and collecting stirred mixture, mixing the stirring mixture with the hydrochloric acid solution at a mass ratio of 3:(3-5), and standing still, (B) adding a composite polymer of 10-15 %mass composite titanium mixture having a mass of 3-5% of sodium lauryl sulfate, and uniformly mixing composite titanium mixture having a mass of 2% of ammonium hydrogen carbonate, stirring, spray drying, and collecting and drying, calcining dried product at 400-450 degrees C, collecting calcined product, crushing, sieving, and collecting sieved particles to obtain an additive. Preferred Composition: The arrangement of composite titanium mixture in the step A is a mixture of tetrabutyl titanate and titanium chloride in a mass ratio of 4:(1-3). The composite polymer is obtained by mixing polyethylene glycol and polystyrene microspheres at a mass ratio of (3-5):(4-9). The auxiliary agent is a mixture of gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane and polyvinylpyrrolidone in a mass ratio of (2-4):5. The guiding agent is sucrose and sodium hexametaphosphate mixed at a mass ratio of 1:(5-7). The mixed solvent is an ethanol solution and an aqueous ammonia solution mixed with a mass ratio of (9-12):(2-4). Preferred Components: The inhibitor is diethanolamine and methylethanolamine. The medium initiator is sodium persulfate and potassium persulfate. The medium additives is a mixture of diethylamine and N,N-methylene acrylamide in a mass ratio of 4:(8-11).

PN CN109717347-A
TI Modifying fresh-keeping Cordyceps sinensis by modified atmosphere packaging and ultraviolet radiation, comprises e.g. immersing fresh Cordyceps sinensis in fresh-keeping liquid, drying, soaking in coating liquid and filling mixed gas.
AU BI H
   WEI L
   XIAO Y
   DU Y
   YANG H
AE NORTHWEST INST PLATEAU BIOLOGY CHINESE A (CAPB-C)
GA 201942956B
AB    NOVELTY - Modifying fresh-keeping Cordyceps sinensis by modified atmosphere packaging and ultraviolet radiation, comprises e.g. immersing fresh Cordyceps sinensis in a fresh-keeping liquid, taking out and drying to obtain a soaked Cordyceps sinensis, where the fresh-keeping liquid composed of 1-3 mmol/l Sorbitol, 1-3 mmol/l vitamin C or isovitamin C, 6-9 mu mol/l phytic acid, 0.4-0.8 mg/kg L-cysteine; soaking the soaked Cordyceps sinensis in membrane liquid, taking out and drying to obtain a film-coated Cordyceps sinensis, where coating liquid includes 30-100 mg/ml ethanol extract fermentation liquid of Cordyceps sinensis, 5-30 mg/l chitosan oligosaccharide and 5-50 mg/l chitosan glucose; packaging filmed Cordyceps sinensis into a packaging bag, and filling with a mixed gas composed of oxygen and carbon dioxide for air-conditioning packaging to obtain a modified air-packaged Cordyceps sinensis; and ultraviolet-spraying.
   USE - The method is useful for modifying fresh-keeping Cordyceps sinensis by modified atmosphere packaging and ultraviolet radiation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can significantly inhibit the weight loss rate of Cordyceps sinensis, slows down the decrease of the soluble protein content and delay the growth of the polyphenol oxidase activity, fresh-keeping time can be at least 75 days, and the color and flavor of the fresh Cordyceps sinensis is better.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Modifying fresh-keeping Cordyceps sinensis by modified atmosphere packaging and ultraviolet radiation, comprises (a) immersing fresh Cordyceps sinensis in a fresh-keeping liquid, taking out and drying to obtain a soaked Cordyceps sinensis, where the fresh-keeping liquid composed of 1-3 mmol/l Sorbitol, 1-3 mmol/l vitamin C or isovitamin C, 6-9 mu mol/l phytic acid, 0.4-0.8 mg/kg L-cysteine; (b) soaking the soaked Cordyceps sinensis in membrane liquid, taking out and drying to obtain a film-coated Cordyceps sinensis, where coating liquid includes 30-100 mg/ml ethanol extract fermentation liquid of Cordyceps sinensis, 5-30 mg/l chitosan oligosaccharide and 5-50 mg/l chitosan glucose; (c) packaging filmed Cordyceps sinensis into a packaging bag, and filling with a mixed gas composed of oxygen and carbon dioxide for air-conditioning packaging to obtain a modified air-packaged Cordyceps sinensis; and (d) ultraviolet-spraying of the air-conditioned packaging of Cordyceps sinensis photographed, refrigerated, where irradiation dose is 2-4 KJ/m2 and irradiation time is 10-20 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the fresh Cordyceps sinensis is ultrasonically washed and then immersed in a preservative solution. The liquid fermentation broth in the step (i) comprises potato, soybean, sucrose, glucose, peptone, yeast extract, mineral salt or vitamin. Preferred Method: The ultrasonic cleaning power is 250-1200 W, the ultrasonic cleaning frequency is 40-120 kHz, and the ultrasonic cleaning is performed 2-5 times, and the time of each ultrasonic cleaning is independent and ground for 5-10 minutes. In the step (a), the method for preparing the ethanol extract of Cordyceps sinensis fermentation liquid comprises inoculation of Cordyceps sinensis in a liquid fermentation medium, and culturing 6-8 days, solid-liquid separation, to obtain fermentation liquid of Cordyceps sinensis, adding ethanol to ethanol to the fermentation broth of the Cordyceps sinensis to a volume percentage of 70-80%, centrifuge the supernatant, remove the ethanol and then dry. In the step (a), the preparation of chitosan glucose complex comprises0.5-2 vol.% chitosan and glucose are dissolved in 0.5-2 vol.% glacial acetic acid, where glacial acetic acid solution containing 0.5-2 vol.% chitosan and 0.5-2 vol.% glucose in 103.4 KPa reacted at 121.3 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, and lyophilized to obtain a chitosan glucose complex. The soaking time in the step (a) is 2-5 minutes. The soaking time in the step (b) is 30-200 seconds. In the step (c), the volume ratio of oxygen to carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 55-65:35-45. The refrigerating temperature in the step (d) is -4 to 4 degrees C.

PN CN109486752-A; CN109486752-B
TI Separating intramuscular fat cells of beef cattle, by isolating longest muscle tissue, adding tissue block, digesting tissue with type II collagenase, neutralizing, culturing, and using pre-adipocyte-specific marker Pref-1.
AU WANG X
   ZAN L
   LI A
   ZHANG Y
   YANG W
AE UNIV NORTHWEST A & F (UNAF-C)
   UNIV NORTHWEST A & F (UNAF-C)
GA 2019271353
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating intramuscular fat cells of beef cattle, involves isolating longest muscle tissue of the 4-day-old beef cattle under aseptic conditions, washing the muscle tissue with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), 2% penicillin and streptomycin, cutting into tissue pieces of 1-2 mm3, adding the tissue block to the centrifuge tube, digesting the tissue with type II collagenase, neutralizing tissue block digested with type II collagenase with Dulbecco's modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12) medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS), centrifuging the obtained filtrate, discarding the supernatant to obtain cells, resuspending obtained cells in serum-free DMEM/F12 medium, centrifuging the cells, washing the cells twice after centrifugation, resuspending washed cells in DMEM/F12 medium containing 10% FBS, seeding cells in dish, culturing the cells, changing the fresh DMEM/F12 medium containing 10% FBS and using the pre-adipocyte-specific marker Pref-1.
   USE - The method is useful for separating intramuscular fat cells of beef cattle (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves intramuscular fat deposition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separating intramuscular fat cells of beef cattle, involves isolating longest muscle tissue of the 4-day-old beef cattle under aseptic conditions, washing the muscle tissue with Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), 2% penicillin and streptomycin, cutting into tissue pieces of 1-2 mm3, adding the tissue block to the centrifuge tube, digesting the tissue with type II collagenase, neutralizing tissue block digested with type II collagenase with Dulbecco's modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12) medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS), centrifuging the obtained filtrate at 1500 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 10 minutes, discarding the supernatant to obtain cells, resuspending obtained cells in serum-free DMEM/F12 medium, centrifuging the cells at 1500 rpm for 10 minutes, washing the cells twice after centrifugation, resuspending washed cells in DMEM/F12 medium containing 10% FBS, seeding cells in dish at a density of 2.5x 105 cells, placing the culture dish at carbon dioxide concentration for 1.5 hours in incubator, washing the cells with sterile PBS to remove non-adherent cells, replacing the original medium in the culture dish with fresh DMEM/F12 medium containing 10% FBS, culturing the cells in incubator at 37 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 5%, changing the fresh DMEM/F12 medium containing 10% FBS for every 2 days until the cell confluence reaches 70-80%, and using the pre-adipocyte-specific marker Pref-1 for cell immunofluorescence labeling positive cell identification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The immunofluorescence labeling positive cells are prepared by seeding the cells in 6-well culture plate, culturing the cells in DMEM/F12 medium containing 10% FBS, when the cells reaches 70% confluence, discarding the DMEM/F12 medium containing 10% FBS, washing cells with PBS, mixing the cells with 4% paraformaldehyde at room temperature for 30 minutes, permeabilizing cells at room temperature for 60 minutes with a concentration of 0.1% bovine serum albumin and 0.5% nonionic surfactant, discarding the liquid, washing the with PBS, discarding the blocking solution, adding diluted murine Pref-1 antibody, incubating the medium at 4 degrees C for overnight, discarding the murine Pref-1 antibody, adding diluted anti-mouse fluorescent secondary antibody, incubating the medium at 37 degrees C for 80 minutes, removing the secondary antibody, adding 4',6-diamidino-2-phenylindole (DAPI) to incubate for 5 min in the dark, washing the cells for 5 minutes, and observing the image under fluorescence microscope.

PN CN109384254-A; CN109384254-B
TI Preparation of crystalline aluminum chloride and            white carbon black by preparing crude aluminum salt,            dissolving in hot water, cooling, standing, dissolving            in ammonia water, settling, washing aluminum hydroxide            and adding hydrochloric acid.
AU GUO Y
   ZHAO Z
   CHENG F
   GAO J
   XUE F
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 201921296N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of crystalline aluminum chloride                and white carbon black includes mixing pulverized                coal ash, coal gangue and potassium carbonate,                calcining, cooling to obtain sintered clinker,                adding sulfuric acid, heating, and filtering while                hot to obtain filtrate and silicon-containing                residue; naturally cooling filtrate, standing,                filtering to obtain crude aluminum salt and liquid,                recycling liquid, dissolving crude aluminum salt in                hot water, stirring, cooling, standing, dissolving                in ammonia water, settling, washing aluminum                hydroxide, and concentrating mother liquor; adding                aluminum hydroxide with hydrochloric acid, heating,                and washing with saturated aluminum chloride                solution to obtain crystalline aluminum chloride;                and adding step (A) silicon-containing residue with                odium hydroxide, reacting, adding carbon dioxide to                carry out carbonization reaction, separating                precipitated white carbon black solid and mother                liquid, and concentrating mother liquid to obtain                sodium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparation of crystalline aluminum                chloride and white carbon black.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and has low energy                consumption and material consumption, and high                utilization rate of alumina in pulverized coal ash                and coal gangue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of crystalline aluminum chloride                and white carbon black comprises:
   (A) mixing pulverized coal ash, coal gangue                and potassium carbonate at molar ratio of                0.5:1-2:1, calcining to 700-1000 degrees C, cooling                to obtain sintered clinker, adding 10-70 wt.%                sulfuric acid at mass ratio of 1:1-1:20, heating to                80-150 degrees C, and filtering while hot to obtain                filtrate and silicon-containing residue;
   (B) naturally cooling filtrate to below 20                degrees C, standing, filtering to obtain crude                aluminum salt and liquid, recycling liquid as                sulfuric acid solution for soaking sintered clinker                while dissolving crude aluminum salt in hot water                at 50-100 degrees C, stirring for 1-3 hours,                cooling to below 20 degrees C, standing, collecting                refined aluminum salt, dissolving in 4-20 wt.%                ammonia water, settling to separate, washing                aluminum hydroxide,and concentrating mother liquor                to obtain nitrogen-potassium mixed                fertilizer;
   (C) adding aluminum hydroxide with 20-35 wt.%                hydrochloric acid, heating to 60-100 degrees C to                obtain crystals, and washing with saturated                aluminum chloride solution to obtain crystalline                aluminum chloride; and
   (D) adding step (A) silicon-containing residue                with 20-60 wt.% sodium hydroxide, reacting at                60-110 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain sodium                silicate solution, adding carbon dioxide to carry                out carbonization reaction at normal temperature,                separating precipitated white carbon black solid                and mother liquid, and concentrating mother liquid                to obtain sodium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pulverized coal                  ash is from coal-fired boiler or coal                  gasification furnace bottom ash. The coal gangue                  is from mining shovel or washing shovel. The                  molar ratio of pulverized coal ash, coal gangue                  and potassium carbonate is 0.5:1-1.5:1. The                  sulfuric acid has concentration of 30-60%. The                  mass ratio of sintered clinker and sulfuric acid                  solution is 1:4-1:8. Preferred Method: The                  calcination is done at 800-950 degrees C. The                  acid soaking is done at 90-130 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN109259043-A
TI Extracting natural bacteriostatic agent from pepper leaves comprises e.g. cleaning pepper leaves, pulverizing dried pepper leaves, ultrasonicating, filtering, fermenting, vacuum-rotating, and freeze-drying.
AU ZHANG T
AE HANSHAN BEIQIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANS-Non-standard)
GA 2019115338
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting natural bacteriostatic agent from pepper leaves comprises e.g. (i) cleaning the pepper leaves, and freeze-drying, (ii) pulverizing dried pepper leaves, adding pure water, ultrasonicating and filtering, (iii) adding alcohol solution into filter residue-a, and ultrasonically filtering, (iv) adding the activated lactic acid bacteria into the secondary filter residue, mixing, and fermenting, (v) placing fermentation slag in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, and the pressure of the fixed separation kettle II is 9-10 MPa, and the dynamic cycle is 3-4 hour to obtain an extract, and (vi) mixing the primary filtrate, the secondary filtrate, the fermentation broth and the extract, vacuum-rotating to no alcohol, and freeze-drying to a moisture content of 0-6%.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting natural bacteriostatic agent from pepper leaves.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves utilization rate of raw material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting natural bacteriostatic agent from pepper leaves comprises (i) taking the pepper leaves from July to October, cleaning, and freeze-drying to a moisture content of 0-10% to obtain dried pepper leaves, (ii) pulverizing the dried pepper leaves to 1-3 mm, adding pure water 70-80 times of the dried pepper leaves, ultrasonicating at 45-50 degrees C and 32-34 kHz for 20-30 minutes, and filtering to obtain filtrate-a and filter residue-a, (iii) adding 77-79% alcohol solution 65-75 times of filter residue-a, and ultrasonically filtering at 6-10 degrees C, 30-32 kHz for 25-35 minutes, and filtering to obtain filtrate-b and filter residue-b, (iv) adding the 2-3% activated lactic acid bacteria into the secondary filter residue, mixing, and fermenting at 18-20 degrees C for 3-4 days to obtain fermentation broth and fermentation slag, (v) placing fermentation slag in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, and the pressure of the fixed separation kettle II is 9-10 MPa, the temperature is 31-32 degrees C, the flow rate is 23-24 kg/hour, and the dynamic cycle is 3-4 hour to obtain an extract, and (vi) mixing the primary filtrate, the secondary filtrate, the fermentation broth and the extract, vacuum-rotating to no alcohol, and freeze-drying to a moisture content of 0-6%. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) natural bacteriostatic agent is prepared by the above method; and
   (2) using natural bacteriostatic agent, adding natural bacteriostatic agent having a weight of 0.5-0.7% of the food, and 100-120 times water, mixing uniformly, adding into food raw materials, performing subsequent process or spraying directly on food surface or refrigerator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (iv), the lactic acid bacteria includes 21-23 pts. wt. Lactobacillus delbrueckii, 14-16 pts. wt. Bifidobacterium and 5-7 pts. wt. Lactobacillus bulgaricus. Preferred Method: In the step (vi), the freeze-drying temperature is -44 to -46 degrees C.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D03-H01H; D03-H01T2A; D03-H01T2C; D05-D

PT P
PN CN109182271-A
TI Constructing normal gastric cancer xenograft model, by adding fetal bovine serum into culture medium, culturing, mixing adjusted human gastric cancer tumor cell suspension with incubated microcarrier and constructing xenograft model.
AU HONG F
   BI Y
   PAN H
   WANG Q
   ZHANG K
   CHEN T
   ZHANG F
AE SHANGHAI MEIFENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019101924
AB    NOVELTY - Method for constructing normal gastric cancer xenograft model, involves adding 10% fetal bovine serum into Dulbecco's modified eagle's medium (DMEM) complete culture medium, after resuscitation of human gastric cancer cell SGC-7901/MKN-45, culturing the medium, changing the medium, passing the mixture, soaking the microcarriers in 75% alcohol, washing the mixture, incubating the medium, obtaining human gastric cancer tumor cells SGC-7901/MKN-45 with exponential growth phase after passage, mixing adjusted human gastric cancer tumor cell SGC-7901/MKN-45 suspension with incubated microcarrier to obtain cell-microcarrier complex, incubating cells-microcarrier complex, resuspending the cells with buffer, adjusting the cell concentration, obtaining cell-microcarrier suspension, placing the suspension in ice, subcutaneously injecting the cell-microcarrier suspension, implanting in vivo, and constructing human gastric cancer xenograft model of normal immunization mice.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing normal gastric cancer xenograft model which is used in preparing and screening gastric cancer drugs (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for constructing normal gastric cancer xenograft model, involves adding 10% fetal bovine serum into Dulbecco's modified eagle's medium (DMEM) complete culture medium, after resuscitation of human gastric cancer cell SGC-7901/MKN-45, culturing the medium at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide incubator, changing the medium after 24-48 hours, passing the mixture for 3-4 times, soaking the microcarriers in 75% alcohol for 48 hours, washing the mixture for 5 times with 1X phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) buffer, incubating the medium for 24-48 hours, obtaining human gastric cancer tumor cells SGC-7901/MKN-45 with exponential growth phase after passage, if the number of viable cells counted by trypan blue is 95%, adjusting concentration of human gastric cancer tumor cells SGC-7901/MKN-45 to 2x 107/ml, mixing adjusted human gastric cancer tumor cell SGC-7901/MKN-45 suspension with incubated microcarrier to obtain cell-microcarrier complex, incubating the mixture at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24-48 hours, washing the mixture with 1X PBS buffer, incubating cells-microcarrier complex for 4 times, resuspending the cells with 1X PBS buffer, adjusting the cell concentration to 2x 107/ml to adjust the microcarrier concentration to 200-350 mu g/ml, obtaining cell-microcarrier suspension, placing the suspension in ice, subcutaneously injecting the cell-microcarrier suspension into C57BL/6 mice for 6-8 weeks, implanting in vivo for 20-30 days, and constructing human gastric cancer xenograft model of normal immunization mice.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microcarrier comprises positively-charged organic composite polymer and has multi-hole shape strips, cross-linked and crimped to form maze-like irregular structure with sufficient space. The volume ratio of the human gastric cancer tumor cell SGC-7901/MKN-45 suspension and microcarriers is 1:3-5. Preferred Method: The method involves constructing normal gastric cancer mouse gastric cancer xenograft model until the cells are grown to density of 90% or more, and changing the liquid for every 2 days. The method involves constructing normal gastric cancer mouse gastric cancer xenograft model at 37 degrees C for 24-48 hours, and saturating the cells in microcarriers after the microscope. The method involves injecting cell-microcarrier suspension into mouse.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN108912556-A
TI Impact-resistant PVC wallboard comprises e.g. PVC resin, pentaerythritol, chlorinated polyethylene resin, oxidized polyethylene wax, dioctyl adipate, modified silicon carbide, modified wollastonite powder and carboxymethylcellulose.
AU MIAO Y
   MIAO N
AE BENGBU XINSHENG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 2018984016
AB    NOVELTY - Impact-resistant PVC wallboard comprises 40-50 pts. wt. PVC resin, 5-15 pts. wt. chlorinated polyethylene (CPE) resin, 6-10 pts. wt. acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene (ACR) resin, 2-8 pts. wt. rare earth composite stabilizer 1-5 pts. wt. epoxidized soybean oil, 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. pentaerythritol, 0.2-1 pts. wt. oxidized polyethylene wax, 2-7 pts. wt. dioctyl adipate, 5-10 pts. wt. modified silicon carbide, 5-10 pts. wt. modified wollastonite powder, 2-8 pts. wt. carboxymethyl cellulose, 1-5 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, and 1-5 pts. wt. antioxidant 1076 (octadecyl 3-(3 5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate).
   USE - Used as impact-resistant PVC wallboard.
   ADVANTAGE - The wallboard: has excellent impact resistance, mechanical property and stability; is environmentally friendly; can suitable for different environments; and has greatly improved comprehensive performance and market competitiveness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the PVC wallboard, comprising (1) weighing the above mentioned each raw material, (2) mixing PVC resin, CPE resin, ACR resin, rare earth stabilizer and epoxidized soybean oil in a high-speed mixer, covering with a cover, starting a motor at a low speed, and then mixing at high-speed, (3) adding dibutyl adipate and pentaerythritol at 60 degrees C to the material under high rotating speed, (4) adding modified silicon carbide, modified wollastonite powder, carboxymethyl cellulose, zinc oxide, titanium dioxide and antioxidant 1076 into the material at 80 degrees C under high rotating speed, (5) adding oxidized polyethylene wax to the material at 90 degrees C under high rotating speed, (6) stopping the material when the temperature is 90-100 degrees C and placing the mixed material to a freezing device for freezing, (7) preheating the frozen material obtained in the step (6) at 180-245 degrees C using s barrel of injection molding machine for melting and plasticizing, (8) injecting supercritical fluid carbon dioxide into the injection molding machine in the machine barrel of plastic melt under the condition of 60-150 MPa, dispersing and homogenizing the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide by screw cutting, fully dissolving with the polymer melt to form single-phase melt, maintaining the pressure to be saturated, injecting it into mold to mold filling, cooling and shaping, (9) taking out and molding, trimming and cutting the obtained PVC wallboard, and (10) subjecting the obtained PVC wallboard to inspection, and qualifying before leaving the factory.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The wallboard comprises 45 pts. wt. PVC resin, 10 pts. wt. CPE resin, 8 pts. wt. ACR resin, 5 pts. wt. rare earth composite stabilizer, 2 pts. wt. epoxidized soybean oil, 0.3 pts. wt. pentaerythritol, 0.5 pts. wt. oxidized polyethylene wax, 5 pts. wt. dioctyl adipate, 7 pts. wt. modified silicon carbide, 6 pts. wt. modified wollastonite powder, 4 pts. wt. carboxymethyl cellulose, 3 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 1 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, and 3 pts. wt. antioxidant 1076. Preferred Method: The rare earth composite stabilizer is prepared by placing rare earth chloride, mixed rare earth and nano calcium carbonate in a measuring flask, completely dissolving in deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing for 0.5-1 hour and then adding zinc sulfate, calcium stearate, barium stearate and sodium hydroxide, placing the measuring flask in a magnetic stirrer, heating to 80-100 degrees C while stirring, reacting at constant temperature for 0.5-1.5 hours, then adding stearic acid and glyceryl monostearate, continuously reacting for 0.5 hours, adding pentaerythritol ester, and stirring for 1-2.5 hours, drying at 105 degrees C, grinding and passing through 200 mesh sieve to obtain the rare earth composite stabilizer, where the composite stabilizer comprises 10-20 pts. wt. rare earth chloride, 10-20 pts. wt. mixed rare earth, 10-20 pts. wt. nano calcium carbonate, 5-15 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 5-15 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 5-15 pts. wt. barium stearate, 10-20 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. pentaerythritol ester, 2-7 pts. wt. stearic acid, 2-7 pts. wt. glyceryl monostearate. The modified silicon carbide is prepared by adding nano calcium carbonate, lauryl glucoside and zinc stearate to silicon carbide, uniformly mixing into globe mill, ball milled for 3-4 hours, then adding calcium stearate and talc, uniformly mixing, continuously ball milling for 8-10 hours, removing out and drying at 200-300 degrees C for 4-5 hours, cooling, sealing and storing, where the modified silicon carbide comprises 45-55 pts. wt. silicon carbide, 1-10 pts. wt. lauryl glucoside, 1-5 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 5-15 pts. wt. nano calcium carbonate, 10-20 pts. wt. talc. The wollastonite powder is prepared by, grinding the wollastonite, sieving with 200-300 mesh sieve, completely dissolving calcined kaolin, nano titanium dioxide and sodium pyrophosphate using deionized water, uniformly mixing and stirring hydrochloric acid at 50-80 degrees C for 1-2 hours, ultrasonically dispersing for 0.5-1.5 hours, then transferring to a reaction kettle, adding stearate and fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, reacting under constant pressure at 3-5 MPa for 2-4 hours, removing out, centrifuging at speed of 600-800 revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes, drying at 105 degrees C for 3-4 hours, cooling and grinding, sieving with 200-mesh sieve, sealing and storing, comprising 45-55 pts. wt. wollastonite powder, 5-15 pts. wt. calcined kaolin, 1-10 pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide, 0.2-1 pts. wt. sodium pyrophosphate, 1-5 pts. wt. butyl stearate, 2-8 pts. wt. fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. hydrochloric acid and 1-5 pts. wt. deionized water. In the step (6), the freezing process is carried out at -20 degrees C for 2-3 hours, then taking out the material, placing in an environment of -5 to 10 degrees C when the temperature is restored to minus -5 to 10 degrees C, and continuously freezing for 3-5 hours. In the step (8), the temperature of the mold is 30-80 degrees C. Preferred Components: The concentration of hydrochloric acid is 0.1-3 mol/l.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.);

PT P
PN CN108878064-A
TI Phosphogypsum and pulverized coal ash conductive silver paste material sulfuric acid co-production comprises involves mixing phosphogypsum, pulverized coal ash, additive and modifier, milling, and sintering to obtain clinker.
AU CHEN X
   WANG L
   ZHAO P
   ZHENG K
   LI M
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018958871
AB    NOVELTY - A phosphogypsum and pulverized coal ash conductive silver paste material sulfuric acid co-production involves mixing phosphogypsum, pulverized coal ash, additive and modifier, milling, sintering to obtain clinker, performing water dissolution according to liquid-solid volume ratio of 4-6:1, carrying out solid-liquid separation, taking solid residue, separating by flotation to obtain sulfide, sintering in 30-50% oxygen-enriched environment for 3-5 hours at 800-1200 degrees C, catalyzing flue gas with vanadium pentoxide, absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid to prepare sulfuric acid, taking separated solution, adding calcium oxide, stirring, filtering too sodium metaaluminate solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas, precipitating, filtering to obtain aluminum hydroxide, adding hydrochloric acid, stirring 3-4 times, water washing till filtrate is neutral, sintering to obtain aluminum oxide powder.
   USE - Phosphogypsum and pulverized coal ash conductive silver paste material sulfuric acid co-production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, low-cost and has high waste utilization rate. The conductive silver material has good conductivity, good adhesive force and has low cadmium content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A phosphogypsum and pulverized coal ash conductive silver paste material sulfuric acid co-production comprises mixing phosphogypsum, pulverized coal ash, additive and modifier, milling, sintering to obtain clinker, performing water dissolution according to liquid-solid volume ratio of 4-6:1, carrying out solid-liquid separation, taking solid residue, separating by flotation to obtain sulfide, sintering in 30-50% oxygen-enriched environment for 3-5 hours at 800-1200 degrees C, catalyzing flue gas with vanadium pentoxide, absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid to prepare sulfuric acid, taking separated solution, adding calcium oxide, stirring, filtering too sodium metaaluminate solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas, precipitating, filtering to obtain aluminum hydroxide, adding hydrochloric acid, stirring 3-4 times, water washing till filtrate is neutral, sintering to obtain aluminum oxide powder, adding water, grinding into 200-300 mesh, adding barium hydroxide and sodium sulfate, grinding for 3-4 hours, spray drying, drying, sintering for 2-3 hours at 380-410 degrees C, obtaining mixed powder, taking dimethylsilicone oil and vulcanized isobutene, heating at 55-75 degrees C, adding tributyl borate, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether and chlorinated paraffin, performing heat preservation for 30-50 minutes, cooling, adding lithium carbonate, grinding to obtain slurry, taking mixed powder, slurry, silver powder, bismuth nitrate, sodium sulfate, additive and water, mixing and heating at 100-120 degrees C for 1-2 hours, cooling, and grinding into 10-20 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodiumsulphate or sodium hydroxide; and modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The conductive paste material comprises 5-10 pts. wt. aluminum oxide powder, 30-40 pts. wt. silver powder, 2-3 pts. wt. barium hydroxide, 2-4 pts. wt. tributyl borate, 0.2-0.5 pt. wt. bismuth nitrate, 0.5-1 pt. wt. fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium sulfate, 2-4 pts. wt. dimethyl silicone oil, 1-2 pts. wt. lithium carbonate, 1.5-2.5 pts. wt. chlorinated paraffin, 3-5 pts. wt. vulcanized isobutene, 30-40 pts. wt. additive and 10-15 pts. wt. water. Preferred Method: The sulfuric acid from phosphogypsum and pulverized coal ash conductive silver paste material is prepared by mixing 1:1-1.8 weight ratio of phosphogypsum and pulverized coal ash, adding additive comprising 1:1 sodium oxide+alumina and ferric oxide, adding 10-25 wt.% modifier, milling, sintering at 1000-1350 degrees C for 1-2 hours to obtain clinker, performing water dissolution according to liquid-solid volume ratio of 4-6:1, carrying out solid-liquid separation, taking solid residue, separating by flotation to obtain sulfide, sintering in 30-50% oxygen-enriched environment for 3-5 hours at 800-1200 degrees C, catalyzing flue gas with vanadium pentoxide, absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid to prepare sulfuric acid, taking separated solution, adding 7-12 g/L calcium oxide, stirring at 80-90 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours, filtering too sodium metaaluminate solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas, precipitating, filtering to obtain aluminum hydroxide, adding 40-60 g/L hydrochloric acid, stirring 3-4 times at 40-60 degrees C, water washing till filtrate is neutral, sintering at 350-600 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain aluminum oxide powder, adding water, grinding into 200-300 mesh, adding barium hydroxide and sodium sulfate, grinding for 3-4 hours, spray drying, drying, sintering for 2-3 hours at 380-410 degrees C, obtaining mixed powder, taking dimethyl silicone oil and vulcanized isobutene, heating at 55-75 degrees C, adding tributyl borate, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether and chlorinated paraffin, performing heat preservation for 30-50 minutes, cooling, adding lithium carbonate, grinding to obtain slurry, taking mixed powder, slurry, silver powder, bismuth nitrate, sodium sulfate, additive and water, mixing and heating at 100-120 degrees C for 1-2 hours, cooling, and grinding into 10-20 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive comprises 2-3 pts. wt. bismuth oxide, 3-4 pts. wt. boron oxide, 0.7-1.5 pts. wt. ethyl cellulose, 0.2-0.5 pt. wt. sodium lauryl sulfate, 0.3-0.7 pt. wt. dimethyl phthalate, 0.2-0.5 pt. wt. lecithin, 0.2-0.5 pt. wt. polysiloxane and 20-25 pts. wt. terpineol. Preferred Method: The additive is prepared by taking bismuth oxide and boron oxide, calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 1-2 hours, taking out, cooling, adding ethyl cellulose, grinding for 30-40 minutes to obtain mixture, taking dimethyl phthalate and terpineol, stirring and mixing, reacting at 150-180 degrees C for 2-3 hours, cooling to 80-100 degrees C, adding remaining raw materials, stirring for 3-4 hours, adding mixture, and grinding for 2-4 hours into 300-400 mesh.

TI Hair follicle stem cell storage solution comprises DMEM/F12, sodium bicarbonate, epidermal growth factor, insulin growth factor-I, hydrocortisone, fetal calf serum, cephalosporin, adenine, and basic fibroblast growth factor.
AU XU Z
   ZHANG X
AE GUANGZHOU SHAAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201892696E
AB    NOVELTY - Hair follicle stem cell storage solution comprises DMEM/F12, sodium bicarbonate, epidermal growth factor (EGF), insulin growth factor-I (IGF-I), hydrocortisone, fetal calf serum (FBS), cephalosporin, adenine, and basic fibroblast growth factor. The storage solution is based on DMEM culture solution and F12 culture solution in the ratio of 3-6:1. The concentration of sodium bicarbonate is 3-10 mg/ml. The concentration of epidermal growth factor (EGF) is 5-20 ng/ml. The concentration of insulin growth factor-I (IGF-I) is 15-50 ng/ml. The concentration of hydrocortisone is 200-300 ng/l. The percentage of fetal bovine serum is 1-10%, The concentration of cephalosporin is 20-50 ng/ml. The concentration of adenine is 30-80 mu g/ml. The concentration of basic fibroblast growth factor is 8-20 ng/ml.
   USE - Used as hair follicle stem cell storage solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases cell free growth, and growth rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cultivating hair follicle stem cells using the stock solution, comprising (i) washing isolated hair follicles 3-5 times with phosphate buffered saline (PBS) in a sterile environment, uniformly applying to the culture dish, and slowly adding 0.2% proteinase K dropwise using a pipette to the hair follicles that had not been freed, and placing the culture dish in a shaker at 37 degrees C under 80 rpm, and incubating for 5-20 minutes, (ii) using a pipette to suck out the residual digestive juice, immediately add a small amount of the stock solution to the infiltrated state, placing in a shaker at 37 degrees C under 70 rpm, and incubating for 60-120 minutes to promote cell adherence (iii) attaching the cells to the cells, adding a small amount of the storage solution, and culturing cells at 37 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration is 5%, and the humidity is 60-80%, and observing and recording the cell separation and growth, (iv) After a large amount of epithelial cells migrated around the hair follicle, replacing with 3 ml working medium per well, and starting the primary culture of ORSC in an incubator at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide and humidity of 60-80%, and (v) under the inverted microscope, the outer root sheath cells (ORSC) of the released hair follicles are grown into a single layer, and digesting and transferring into a culture flask to continue the culture using the working culture solution, and replacing the working culture medium once every 2-3 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The concentration of sodium bicarbonate is 5 mg/ml. The concentration of epidermal growth factor (EGF) is 10 ng/ml. The concentration of cephalosporin is 30 ng/ml. The concentration of adenine is 50 mu g/ml. The concentration of basic fibroblast growth factor is 10 ng/ml.

TI Ionic liquid catalyst useful for preparing glycerol carbonate by coupling reaction of glycerol and carbon dioxide, comprises cation and anion.
AU WANG H
   ZHOU X
   XU X
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 201890102G
AB    NOVELTY - Ionic liquid catalyst comprises cation and anion, where cation of the ionic liquid is a functionalized quaternary ammonium salt ion, where functionalized quaternary ammonium salt ionic functional group is amino, carboxy or hydroxy; and anion is a halide ion or a hydroxide ion.
   USE - The ionic liquid catalyst is useful for preparing glycerol carbonate by coupling reaction of glycerol and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high catalytic activity, simple preparation process and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing ionic liquid catalyst comprising either, (i) mixing trialkylamine and alkylating agent halo ammonium salt in a molar ratio of 1-6:1, dissolving in absolute ethanol under nitrogen atmosphere, reacting for 12-36 hours to obtain solution A, (ii) cooling the solution A obtained in step (i) to room temperature, filtering to remove insoluble matter, removing ethanol and unreacted trialkylamine by distillation under reduced pressure to obtain solution B, (iii) dissolving solution B obtained in step (ii) in a solvent, adding an acid-base regulator, adjusting pH of the solution to 8-10, stirring at room temperature to obtain solution C, (iv) distilling solution C obtained in step (iii) under reduced pressure and vacuum drying to obtain solid-liquid mixture D, and (v) dissolving solid-liquid mixture D obtained in step (iv) in a solvent, filtering, removing insoluble matter, distilling filtrate under reduced pressure and then drying to obtain amino-functionalized quaternary ammonium salt ionic liquid, or (i) mixing trialkylamine and alkylating agent haloalkyl alcohol in a molar ratio of 1-6:1, dissolving in tetrahydrofuran and reacting for 12-36 hours under nitrogen atmosphere to obtain solution A, (ii) cooling the solution A obtained in step (i) to room temperature, filtering to retain insoluble matter and drying to obtain solid B, and (iii) recrystallizing the solid B obtained in step (ii) in an ethanol-tetrahydrofuran mixed solvent, washing, drying to obtain hydroxy-functionalized quaternary ammonium salt ionic liquid, or (i) mixing trialkylamine and alkylating agent methyl halocarboxylate in a molar ratio of 1-6:1, dissolving in tetrahydrofuran, reacting for 12-36 hours under nitrogen atmosphere to obtain solution A, (ii) cooling solution A obtained in step (i) to room temperature, filtering to retain insoluble matter and drying to obtain solid B, (iii) recrystallizing solid B obtained in step (ii) in an ethanol-tetrahydrofuran mixed solvent, washing and drying to obtain solid C, and (iv) mixing solid C obtained in step (iii) with hydrohalic acid solution of the halogenated carboxylic acid methyl ester, reacting for 1-3 hours and then distilling off solvent under reduced pressure to obtain carboxy-functionalized quaternary ammonium salt ionic liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is amino compounds of formula (I). In step (i), alkylating agent is a halogenated ammonium salt of formula (X-(CH2)n-NH2HX). The alkylating agent methyl halocarboxylate is methyl iodoacetate; and hydrohalic acid is hydriodic acid.R1-R3 = 1-12C hydrocarbon;FG = amino, carboxy or hydroxy;n = 1-12; andX- = halo ion or hydroxide ion, preferably I-.

PN RU2671538-C1
TI HYDROGEN WATER AND METHOD FOR PRODUCING HYDROGEN WATER used in engineering, medicine, including practical healthcare, food and cosmetic industries, and agriculture.
AU BOGDANOVA YU A
AE BOGDANOVA YU A (BOGD-Individual)
GA 201888426E
AB    NOVELTY - Group of inventions relates to the field of water production for household and drinking purposes and can be used in engineering, medicine, including practical healthcare, food and cosmetic industries, and agriculture. Method of obtaining household water for drinking purposes, enriched with hydrogen, provides for sanitary processing of process equipment before production, water preparation, preparation of household water for drinking, enriched with hydrogen, bottling, capping, scavenging, labeling, packaging, transferring products to warehouse, storage and transportation of finished products. Water preparation is performed on membrane water treatment system to produce process water with balanced microelement composition. Temperature of water entering the saturator should be no more than 15 degrees C. Water treatment system comprises multilayer filtration unit, iron removal unit, softening unit, reverse osmosis membrane unit with regeneration unit. Original artesian water from storage tank under pressure of 4 atm is fed to the inlet of the multilayer filter, passing from top to bottom through layers of granular load and drainage layer of gravel, freed from mechanical impurities. Deferrization filter consists of a fiberglass tank loaded with a layer of granular filter material and the accompanying filter layers. After the deferrization unit, water enters softening unit, consisting of two Na+-cationite filters mounted in parallel. Membrane installation designed to reduce overall water salinity by the method of reverse osmosis, comprises barrier filter, high-pressure pump, diaphragm circuit consisting of three modules, chemical washing unit, piping, shut-off and control valves, control panel and recording and instrumentation equipment. Stage of preparing drinking water enriched with hydrogen is to saturate the prepared water with molecular hydrogen or carbon dioxide in a saturator. At the same time, a source of hydrogen, cylinder or generator of hydrogen, is connected through reducer and pipeline to saturator, inlet gas valve of hydrogen source opens, working pressure of hydrogen in range of 0.3-0.4 MPa is set on gearbox. Then gas inlet valve on saturator opens and hydrogen displaces water that was previously poured into saturator column into drain. After work is completed, the shut-off valve of the hydrogen source is closed, the water supply to the saturator is turned off, and the excess pressure in the saturator is discharged through the drainage pipe. Isobaric bottling is performed on the filling machine in bottles that have been rinsed with ozonated water. Filling is carried out using carbon dioxide for backpressure, and when stored in boxes made of corrugated cardboard, the bottles are stacked and stored upside down bottom up.
   USE - Water treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is to increase organoleptic properties of water composition by improving quality of water.
3 cl, 1 dwg, 2 tbl

PN CN108143648-A; CN108143648-B
TI Preparing peony essential oil facial mask comprises e.g. mixing fresh peony petals with water, smashing, ultrasonicating and centrifugal separating peony juice, adding super absorbent resin into the peony juice and then stirring.
AU CHEN Z
   SHEN Z
AE ANHUI XINYUANKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
   ANHUI HANBANG TECHNOLOGY CONSULTING CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201847440K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing peony essential oil facial mask comprises e.g. mixing fresh peony petals with water, smashing, ultrasonicating and centrifugal separating peony juice, adding super absorbent resin into the peony juice, stirring for 10-20 minutes and allowing to stand for 0-30 minutes to obtain gelatinous peony extract, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of gelatinized peony extract to obtain peony essential oil, mixing 10-15 pts. wt. glycerol, 8-12 pts. wt. hyaluronic acid, 1-2 pts. wt. vitamin E, 5-8 pts. wt. orange essential oils, 2-4 pts. wt. Rhodiola rosea essential oil, 6-9 pts. wt. grape seed oil, 20-25 pts. wt. water, 7-10 pts. wt. peony essential oils, mixing glycerol, hyaluronic acid, water and stirring at 70-80 degrees C to obtain aqueous solution, adding vitamin E, orange oil, Rhodiola rosea essential oil, grape seed oil, peony oil and blending to obtain oil phase liquid, adding oil phase liquid into the aqueous phase liquid and then homogenizing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing peony essential oil facial mask.
   ADVANTAGE - The facial mask: has whitening, anti-wrinkling and anti-aging effect; and enhance the skin elasticity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing peony essential oil facial mask comprises (i) mixing fresh peony petals with water, smashing, ultrasonicating and centrifugal separating peony juice, (ii) adding super absorbent resin into the peony juice obtained in step (i), stirring for 10-20 minutes and allowing to stand for 0-30 minutes to obtain gelatinous peony extract, (iii) carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of gelatinized peony extract obtained in step (ii) to obtain peony essential oil, (iv) mixing 10-15 pts. wt. glycerol, 8-12 pts. wt. hyaluronic acid, 1-2 pts. wt. vitamin E, 5-8 pts. wt. orange essential oils, 2-4 pts. wt. Rhodiola rosea essential oil, 6-9 pts. wt. grape seed oil, 20-25 pts. wt. water, 7-10 pts. wt. peony essential oils obtained in step (iii), (v) mixing glycerol, hyaluronic acid, water and stirring at 70-80 degrees C to obtain aqueous solution, (vi) adding vitamin E, orange oil, Rhodiola rosea essential oil, grape seed oil, peony oil and blending at 70-80 degrees C to obtain oil phase liquid, and (vii) adding oil phase liquid obtained in step (vi) into the aqueous phase liquid obtained in step (v), homogenizing for 15-20 minutes to obtain basic liquid, immersing mask into the basic liquid for 20-30 minutes to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In step (ii), superabsorbent resin comprises 12-15 pts. wt. corn starch, 65-70 pts. wt. acrylic acid, 5-8 pts. wt. corn stalk powder, 0.5-0.7 pts. wt. potassium persulfate, 0.06-0.08 pts. wt. N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide, 85-95 pts. wt. 26-29% sodium hydroxide solution and 15-17 pts. wt. water. In step (i), weight ratio between fresh peony petals and water is 1:1-1. In step (ii), amount of superabsorbent resin is 0.02-0.04 times the weight of peony juice. In step (i), ultrasonic power is 200-300 w, frequency is 28-32 Hz, and ultrasound time is 15-20 minutes. In step (vii), homogenous stirring speed is 2800-3200 revolutions per minute. Preferred Method: In step (ii), superabsorbent resin prepared by (i) adding cornstarch into water at 70-80 degrees C and stirring at constant temperature for 40-50 minutes to obtain gelatinized starch solution A, (ii) adding acrylic acid into sodium hydroxide solution, stirring for 20-30 minutes, adding corn powder, potassium persulfate, N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide and stirring to obtain mixed solution B, and (iii) mixing starch solution A obtained in step (i) mixed solution B obtained in step (ii), placing in microwave reactor, microwave treating for 3-5 minutes, microwave power is 250-300 W, drying and then crushing to obtain super absorbent resin. In step (iii), extraction pressure is 23-35 Mpa, extraction temperature is 35-45 degrees C, extraction time is 50-90 minutes, and carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-18 l/hour. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction instrument used has two separation vessels, pressure of the first separating kettle is 8-10 Mpa, temperature is 40-45 degrees C, pressure of the second separating kettle is 3-5 Mpa, and temperature is 30-35 degrees C. In step (iii), extracting carbon dioxide using cyclohexane as cosolvent, where flow of cyclohexane is 6-8% the flow rate of carbon dioxide.
