PN CN107754562-A
TI Normal-temperature acidic gas concentrating process involves setting sulfur-rich absorbent temperature from the upstream, heating with lean absorbent from heat regenerating tower, and sending to the flash tower for decompressing.
AU WANG T
   ZHOU X
   GUO J
   SONG Y
   WANG L
   HU Y
   WU D
AE SINOPEC NINGBO ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NINGBO TECHNOLOGY INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC REFINING & CHEM ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2018200896
AB    NOVELTY - Normal-temperature acidic gas concentrating process involves setting sulfur-rich absorbent temperature from the upstream at - 20 degrees C - 60 degrees C, pressure at 0.6-1.5 MPaG, dihydrogen sulfide (H2S) mol content at 0.05-5%, heating to 25-90 degrees C with lean absorbent from heat regenerating tower (1), sending to the flash tower (2) for decompressing and flash evaporation, and controlling the pressure at 0.1-0.4 MPaG; and sending the flash gas containing carbon dioxide and some H2S to the downstream process from the top of the flash tower.
   USE - Method for normal-temperature acidic gas concentrating process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Normal-temperature acidic gas concentrating process comprises setting sulfur-rich absorbent temperature from the upstream at - 20 degrees C - 60 degrees C, pressure at 0.6-1.5 MPaG, dihydrogen sulfide (H2S) mol content at 0.05-5%, heating to 25-90 degrees C with lean absorbent from heat regenerating tower (1), sending to the flash tower (2) for decompressing and flash evaporation, and controlling the pressure at 0.1-0.4 MPaG; sending the flash gas containing carbon dioxide and some H2S to the downstream process from the top of the flash tower, and sending the sulfur-rich absorbent from the bottom of flash tower to the gas stripping tower (3) for gas-extracting operation; introducing nitrogen into the lower portion of gas stripping tower at 10-90 degrees C, and controlling pressure at 0.1-0.4 MPaG; continuously heating the sulfur-rich absorbent to exchange heat with lean absorbent from the heat regenerating tower to obtain sulfur-rich absorbent with 85-105 degrees C temperature, sending to heat regenerating tower for thermal regeneration at 80-105 degrees C and 0.20-0.30 MPa, where the acidic gas sulfide dissolved in rich absorbent is desorbed forming a Claus gas, and cooling to 30 degrees C, where the mol content of H2S in Claus gas after cooling is 25-80%; sending the Claus gas into the downstream sulfur recovery process, separating condensed liquid, and returning the reflux liquid back to the heat regenerating tower; sending the regenerated lean absorbent from the lower portion of the heat regenerating tower to the downstream acid gas absorption process, and sending the absorbent solution into the downstream from the bottom output of the heat regenerating tower.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the normal-temperature acidic gas concentrating processing device.
   Heat regenerating tower (1)
   Flash tower (2)
   Gas stripping device (3)
   Second heat exchanger (4)
   First heat exchanger (5)

PN CN107649159-A; CN107649159-B
TI Preparation of organic dye-modified carbonitride graphene composite material used for catalytic synthesis of methanol by preparing graphitic carbon nitride organic solution, adding graphene alcohol solution, heating, centrifuging and drying.
AU CHEN A
   CHEN Q
   PENG X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201811563R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of organic dye-modified carbonitride graphene composite material includes mixing graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4) powder and alkaline aqueous solution, ball milling, filtering, adding precipitate to acidic aqueous solution, stirring, centrifuging, washing, and freeze-drying; adding first organic alcohol, adding with graphene alcohol solution, sonicating, heating, centrifuging, and vacuum-drying precipitate to obtain composite material; and adding with organic dye and third organic alcohol, sonicating, centrifuging, and washing precipitate.
   USE - Method for preparation of organic dye-modified carbonitride graphene composite material used for catalytic synthesis of methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has mesoporous structure, higher specific surface, improved hole-charge separation rate and light absorption rate and utilization, water vapor and carbon dioxide reduction in visible light, and good photocatalytic performance of more than 8 times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of organic dye-modified carbonitride graphene composite material comprises:
   (A) mixing graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4) powder and alkaline aqueous solution, ball milling at speed of 100-600 revolutions/minute, filtering, adding precipitate to acidic aqueous solution, magnetically stirring at 25-90 degrees C to remove iron impurities, centrifuging, repeatedly washing with deionized water until washing pH is 6-7, and freeze-drying;
   (B) adding first organic alcohol to obtain first organic alcohol solution, mixing graphene with second organic alcohol to obtain graphene alcohol solution, adding to first organic alcohol solution, sonicating at speed of 1000-4000 Hz, heating to 100-240 degrees C for 8-24 hours, centrifuging, and vacuum-drying precipitate to obtain composite material; and
   (C) adding with organic dye and third organic alcohol, sonicating at speed of 1000-4000 Hz, centrifuging, and washing precipitate with third organic alcohol.
   The alkaline aqueous solution is sodium hydroxide aqueous solution or potassium hydroxide aqueous solution. The first, second and third organic alcohols are methanol, ethanol or propanol. The g-C3N4 and graphene have mass ratio of 1:0.01-0.4. The composite material and organic dye have mass ratio of 1:0.005~0.02. The third organic alcohol has dosage of 200-600 mL/g. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for catalytic synthesis of methanol comprising adding organic dye-modified carbonitride graphene composite material and deionized water into reaction kettle of photocatalytic reaction device, purging with carbon dioxide at 60-100 degrees C and at 1-6 MPa for 3-5 hours, and illuminating at 105 mW/cm2 for 4 hours using 500 W xenon lamp as light source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline aqueous solution has concentration of 0.5-3 mol/L and dosage of 50-300 mL/g. The acidic aqueous solution is 0.1-3 mol/L hydrochloric acid aqueous solution or 0.1-3 mol/L sulfuric acid solution; and has dosage of 50-300 mL/g. The g-C3N4 first alcohol solution has concentration of 1-5 mg/mL. The graphene alcohol solution has concentration of 0.05-2 mg/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic dye is rhodamine B, methyl orange, methylene blue, methyl violet, phthalocyanine blue or bromocresol green.

PN CN107619283-A
TI Preparing high-density nano-ceramic material involves mixing silicon dioxide, magnesium oxide, potassium titanate and aluminum oxide into furnace, and adding silicon carbide, niobium carbide and anti-static agent in furnace.
AU SHAO G
AE FOSHAN HUICHUANG ZHENGYUAN NEW MATERIALS (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201808840U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a high-density nano-ceramic material involves mixing 4-10 pts. wt. silicon dioxide, 4-8 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 2-6 pts. wt. potassium titanate and 5-10 pts. wt. aluminum oxide into a vacuum furnace, and adding 10-13 pts. wt. silicon carbide, 1-2 pts. wt. niobium carbide and 1-3 pts. wt. anti-static agent in furnace, and reacting in furnace at a temperature of 1200-1300 degrees C and vacuum pressure of 1.0x 10-3 to 1.0x 10-2Pa for 2 hours to obtain slurry. The obtained slurry is injected into a twin-screw extruder to obtain a master batch.
   USE - Method for preparing a high-density nano-ceramic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a high-density nano-ceramic material, which has good anti-static property, high hardness and better application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a high-density nano-ceramic material involves mixing 4-10 pts. wt. silicon dioxide, 4-8 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 2-6 pts. wt. potassium titanate and 5-10 pts. wt. aluminum oxide into a vacuum furnace, and adding 10-13 pts. wt. silicon carbide, 1-2 pts. wt. niobium carbide and 1-3 pts. wt. anti-static agent in furnace, and reacting in furnace at a temperature of 1200-1300 degrees C and vacuum pressure of 1.0x 10-3 to 1.0x 10-2Pa for 2 hours to obtain slurry. The obtained slurry is injected into a twin-screw extruder to obtain a master batch. The obtained master batch is subjected to vacuum dehydration and wet forming pressing to obtain plate-shaped master batch. The obtained plate-shaped master batch is placed in a sealed container and heated to 1000-1100 degrees C followed by mixing into high-purity titanium tetrachloride vapor, nitrogen and hydrogen gas in a molar ratio of 5:39:85 for 20-30 minutes, followed by mixing into the high-purity titanium tetrachloride vapor, methane and hydrogen in a molar ratio of 2:9:65 for 30-40 minutes, and followed by mixing into carbon dioxide gas and hydrogen gas in a molar ratio of 1:95 for 5-10 minutes, and then followed by mixing into highly pure aluminum trichloride vapor, carbon dioxide gas and hydrogen in molar ratio of 7:1:98 for 50-60 minutes to obtain mixed master batch. The obtained mixed master batch is dried to obtain finial product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The anti-static agent is selected from one or more of a group consist of polyethylene glycol methacrylate copolymer, polyether ester amide, polyether ester acetamide, or polyethylene oxide. Preferred Conditions: The temperature and screwing speed of twin-screw extruder are 220-260 degrees C and 1500 revolutions per minute. The vacuum dehydration is performed at a pressure of 2x 10-4Pa and temperature of 120-150 degrees C. The drying is performed at a temperature of 280-300 degrees C.

TI Preparing low-precipitation sub-gloss polyester slice useful for pressing flexible circuit board, comprises mixing ethylene glycol and catalyst, performing pre-esterification reaction, introducing nitrogen, carrying out esterification reaction, carrying out polycondensation reaction and purifying.
AU MU Q
   ZHAO S
   TANG B
   ZHAO Y
   LI J
   WANG J
   LIU B
   WANG S
   ZHAO F
   TIAN L
AE TIANJIN WANHUA CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202245873F
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (i) uniformly mixing the ethylene glycol and catalyst in the reactor and performing pre-esterification reaction; (ii) adding the material liquid after pre-esterification reaction with terephthalic acid stirrer, stirring in the reactor, introducing nitrogen, opening the circulating water and carrying out esterification reaction; (iii) vacuumizing and pre-polycondensing; (iv) adding the chain extender pyromellitic dianhydride to carry out the polycondensation reaction and discharging when the intrinsic viscosity of the polymer is 0.7-0.9, after the polymer melt is cooled and granulating to obtain a low-precipitation sub-gloss polyester slice mixture; and (v) purifying the mixture of (iv) medium and low precipitation matt polyester chips through a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction device, circulating the carbon dioxide extraction oligomer and reducing the oligomer content of the low-precipitation sub-gloss polyester slice to the target value.
   USE - The method for peparing low-precipitation sub-gloss polyester slice is useful for pressing flexible circuit board.
   ADVANTAGE - The low-precipitation sub polyester slice uses the chain extending tackifying way, which overcomes the problem that the normal sub-optical polyester film is separated out in the long-time high-temperature working environment of the flexible circuit board high temperature pressing, improves the purity of the raw material and reduces the oligomer and other impurities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing low-precipitation sub-gloss polyester slice comprises (i) uniformly mixing the ethylene glycol and catalyst in the reactor, performing pre-esterification reaction at 175-188degrees Celsius for 3-5 hours; (ii) adding the material liquid after pre-esterification reaction with terephthalic acid stirrer, stirring in the reactor, introducing nitrogen, opening the circulating water, carrying out esterification reaction, controlling the esterification at 265-280degrees Celsius for 5-7 hours; (iii) after finishing the esterification reaction, vacuumizing, pre-polycondensing with the pressure of 2500-3500 Pa at 275-285degrees Celsius for 1.5-3 hours; (iv) adding the chain extender pyromellitic dianhydride at the temperature of 280-320degrees Celsius and the vacuum pressure of 45-85 Pa to carry out the polycondensation reaction for 3-5 hours, and discharging when the intrinsic viscosity of the polymer is 0.7-0.9, after the polymer melt is cooled and granulating to obtain a low-precipitation sub-gloss polyester slice mixture; and (v) purifying the mixture of (iv) medium and low precipitation matt polyester chips through a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction device, where the temperature of the extraction tower in the initial section is 38-45degrees Celsius, and the pressure is 14-16 MPa, the temperature of the middle section is 50-55degrees Celsius, the pressure is 6.5-8.5 MPa and the final temperature is 38-45degrees Celsius and the pressure is 3.5-4.2 MPa, circulating the carbon dioxide extraction oligomer for 3 times, reducing the oligomer content of the low-precipitation sub-gloss polyester slice to the target value, where the target value of oligomer xylene extraction value is less than 0.2%, the oligomer chloroform an extraction value less than 0.5% and the mass ratio of the chain extender pyromellitic dianhydride, catalyst, glycol and terephthalic acid is (3-9):10:(200-600):(240-720).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a low-precipitation sub-gloss polyester slice, is prepared as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Tthe catalyst is ethylene glycol antimony or diantimony trioxide.

PN CN111943246-A
TI Preparing calcium carbonate comprises e.g. mixing chitosan, acrylic acid and itaconic acid in deionized water, stirring and heating, adding aqueous ammonia to adjust the pH, continuously stirring, and adding deionized water for dilution.
AU WEI M
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B7485P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate comprises (1) mixing chitosan, acrylic acid and itaconic acid in deionized water, stirring and heating, adding aqueous ammonia to adjust the pH to 7-8, continuously stirring, adding deionized water for dilution, obtaining modified chitosan solution; (2) stirring and mixing high-purity calcium hydroxide and deionized water to obtain slurry-A; stirring slurry-A, adding the modified chitosan solution obtained in step (1) to slurry-A to obtain slurry-B; (3) stirring and heating the slurry-B obtained in step (2), introducing carbon dioxide under the action of a magnetic field for carbonization reaction for 2-3 hours, the carbon dioxide gas flow rate is 100-150 l/hour, then turning off the magnetic field, adding calcium stearate, starch and cinnamic acid to slurry-B, stirring to obtain slurry-C; and (4) centrifuging the slurry C obtained in step (3) to obtain precipitate, drying the precipitate, and crushing.
   USE - The calcium carbonate is useful for preparing waterproof ventilating film (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate: has uniform granularity and good dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium carbonate comprises (1) mixing chitosan, acrylic acid and itaconic acid in a mass ratio of 15:(1-3):(2-5) in deionized water, stirring and heating to constant temperature of 40-50 degrees C, adding aqueous ammonia to adjust the pH to 7-8, continuously stirring for 2-5 hours, adding deionized water for dilution, obtaining modified chitosan solution, where the mass of the modified chitosan is 20-30 wt.%; (2) stirring and mixing high-purity calcium hydroxide and deionized water to obtain slurry-A, the concentration of nano calcium hydroxide in the slurry is 60-90 g/l; stirring slurry-A, adding the modified chitosan solution obtained in step (1) to slurry-A to obtain slurry-B, the mass ratio of slurry A to the modified chitosan solution is 100: (5-10); (3) stirring and heating the slurry-B obtained in step (2) to constant temperature of 55-65 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide under the action of a magnetic field for carbonization reaction for 2-3 hours, the carbon dioxide gas flow rate is 100-150 l/hour, then turning off the magnetic field, adding calcium stearate, starch and cinnamic acid to slurry-B, stirring for 1-2 hours at 75-80 degrees C to obtain slurry-C; the mass ratio of the slurry-B: calcium stearate: starch: cinnamic acid is 100: (2-5): (2-5): (1-3); and (4) centrifuging the slurry C obtained in step (3) to obtain precipitate, drying the precipitate at 60-70 degrees C for 8-12 hours, crushing, to obtain the modified nano calcium carbonate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the waterproof ventilating film comprises comprising using the modified nano-calcium carbonate, (i) taking 70-90 pts. wt. polypropylene, 15-25 pts. wt. modified nano calcium carbonate, 10-15 pts. wt. bentonite, 5-8 pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide, 2-5 pts. wt. maleic rosin glyceride, 3-5 pts. wt. silane coupling agent, 1-3 pts. wt. cellulose acetate, 3-5 pts. wt. polyvinylpyrrolidone, and 1-3 pts. wt. sodium polyacrylate; (ii) mixing and extruding the raw materials obtained in the step (i) to form pellets, adding the pellets to the double screw extruder, heating them to molten state at 200-220 degrees C, filtering obtained melt, extruding into film through single-layer die head; and (iii) cooling the film obtained in step (ii) to 120-130 degrees C at cooling rate of 10 degrees C/minute, stretching for 4-6 times, performing heat setting treatment at 115 degrees C, cooling, flattening and rolling the shaped film, obtaining waterproof ventilating film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The purity of the high-purity calcium hydroxide is greater than or equal to 99.5 wt.%. Preferred Method: In the step (1), stirring speed is 50-80 revolutions/minute. In the step (2), stirring speed is 100-200 revolutions/minute. In the step (3), the magnetic induction intensity of the magnetic field is 0.6-0.8T. In the step (4), the conditions for centrifugation is centrifuging at speed of 2000 revolutions/minute for 3-5 minutes. In the step (ii), filtering the obtained melt through 350 mesh filter.

PN CN109880799-A
TI Constructing hepatic encephalopathy cell model, by separating rat astrocytes and neuron cells, purifying, inoculating microporous membrane with neuron cells, adding different concentrations of ammonium chloride and incubating.
AU CHEN Y
   JIN X
   CHEN D
   WU F
   ZHANG L
   PAN T
AE UNIV WENZHOU MEDICAL FIRST AFFILIATED HO (WEZN-C)
GA 2019565867
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing hepatic encephalopathy cell model, involves (i) taking bilateral cerebral cortex tissue from Sprague-Dawley rats, digesting the cells with trypsin, and Dulbecco's modified eagle medium, washing, and adding the medium to initial cell suspension, (ii) incubating into a polylysine coated culture flask, and performing glial fibrillary acidic protein immunofluorescence to identify primary astrocytes, (iii) taking hippocampus tissues of embryos from 16-18 embryonic day female rats, digesting with trypsin, adding neurobasal medium/B27 to initial cell suspension, inoculating to the polylysine coated flask, inoculating in neurobasal medium/B27 and glutamine medium, carrying out microtubule associated protein 2 immunofluorescence identification of neuronal cells, (iv) inoculating astrocytes in DMEM medium, and incubating in neurobasal medium/B27 and glutamine medium, and (v) inoculating the microporous membrane of the plate with culture chamber with neuron cells.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing hepatic encephalopathy cell model used in medicament for hepatic encephalopathy (all claimed) and research of hepatic encephalopathy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the production cost and production time of hepatic encephalopathy cell model in simple internal environment and reduces deviations in experimental results.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for constructing hepatic encephalopathy cell model, involves (i) sterilizing Sprague-Dawley rats with 75% alcohol within 24 hours of birth, taking bilateral cerebral cortex tissue under clean bench, digesting the cells with 0.125% trypsin at 37 degrees C for 15 minutes, adding Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) containing 10% fetal bovine serum, washing twice, adding the medium to the initial cell suspension and filtering through a 200 mesh sieve, (ii) inoculating into a polylysine coated culture flask, incubating at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, changing the liquid, replacing once every three days after the liquid is cultured for 9-12 days, maintaining the culture flask in sterile conditions, shaking for 18 hours, discarding the exfoliated cell suspension, and performing glial fibrillary acidic protein immunofluorescence to identify the successful cells as primary astrocytes, (iii) sterilizing 16-18 embryonic day female rats at -20 degrees C for 15 minutes, disinfecting with 75% alcohol, taking hippocampus tissues of both embryos, digesting with 0.125% trypsin at 37 degrees C for 15 minutes, adding 10% fetal bovine serum DMEM, stopping digestion, washing twice, adding neurobasal medium/B27 into the initial cell suspension, filtering through a 200 mesh sieve, inoculating into a polylysine coated flask, inoculating in neurobasal medium/B27 and 0.5 mmol/l glutamine medium, incubating at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator, carrying out adherence for 4 hours, replacing the solution once in every three days, replacing the liquid once every time, carrying out microtubule associated protein 2 immunofluorescence identification, and identifying successful cells as neuronal cells, (iv) inoculating 1x 105/ml astrocytes on a lower plate with Transwell culture chamber (RTM: Polycarbonate membrane cell culture inserts) in DMEM medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum overnight under sterile conditions, transferring the mixture to neurobasal medium/B27 and 0.5 mmol/l glutamine medium having different concentrations of ammonium chloride, and incubating at 37 degrees C in the 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, and (v) inoculating the microporous membrane of the above-mentioned plate with Transwell culture chamber (RTM: Polycarbonate membrane cell culture inserts) culture chamber with 1x 105/ml neuron cells under sterile conditions, incubating at 37 degrees C in the 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24-48 hours to form neuronal cell and astrocyte co-culture system. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for hepatic encephalopathy cell model that is constructed by the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the constant temperature is 37 degrees C under a speed of 250 rpm. In step (iv), the concentration of ammonium chloride is 1-10 mM, and the increase in concentration indicates that the degree of hepatic encephalopathy in the model is increased to simulate different degrees of hepatic encephalopathy. In step (v), the neuronal cells are co-cultured with an astrocyte culture chamber in a high ammonia state. Preferred Components: The medium for culturing the cells in step (ii) is neurobasal medium/B27 and 0.5 mmol/l serum-free glutamine. The hepatic encephalopathy cell model comprises astrocytes, neuronal cells and various concentrations of ammonia.

   HE B
   CHE H
   LIU F

   MAO Y
AE TUS ENVIRONMENTAL SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV (TUSE-Non-standard)
GA 2022B5572A
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of biogas involves: using a                biogas direct-drive purification system comprising                a desulfurization device, a dehumidifier, a heat                exchanger, a filter, a direct-drive biogas                compressor, a membrane separator and an                absorption-type water chilling unit; desulfurizing                biogas, condensing and dehydrating; introducing the                dehydrated biogas into the heat exchanger and                re-heating; introducing the reheated biogas into                the filter to remove micron-sized fine particles,                then introducing into the compressor, supplying a                portion of the biogas to the biogas internal                combustion engine of the compressor, pressurizing                biogas and then introducing into the separator to                separate methane and carbon dioxide; and                introducing the high-temperature flue gas generated                by combustion of the biogas internal combustion                engine of the compressor into the unit, supplying                the effluent of the unit to the dehumidifier, and                returning heated water in the dehumidifier to the                unit for recycling.
   USE - Purification of biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces emission and                the operation cost of biogas purification, and                saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of biogas involves: using a                biogas direct-drive purification system comprising                a desulfurization device, a dehumidifier, a heat                exchanger, a filter, a direct-drive biogas                compressor, a membrane separator and an                absorption-type water chilling unit; desulfurizing                biogas raw material to be purified using the                desulfurization device, introducing into the                dehumidifier for condensation and dehydration, and                discharging supersaturated water in liquid form                after condensation; introducing the dehydrated                biogas into the heat exchanger, re-heating to room                temperature using low-temperature flue gas from the                absorption-type water chilling unit, discharging                the low-temperature flue gas after heat exchange                through the heat exchanger or purifying according                to the flue gas emission standard after reaching                the discharge standard; introducing the reheated                biogas into the filter to be filtered to remove                micron-sized fine particles, then introducing into                the direct-drive biogas compressor, supplying a                portion of the biogas to the biogas internal                combustion engine of the compressor, pressurizing                the rest of the biogas using the compressor, and                introducing the pressurized biogas into the                membrane separator to separate methane and carbon                dioxide to obtain bio-natural gas with high-purity                methane concentration; and introducing the                high-temperature flue gas generated by the internal                combustion of the biogas internal combustion engine                of the compressor into the chilling unit as a                driving heat source, supplying the effluent of the                unit to the dehumidifier, and returning the heated                water in the dehumidifier to the unit for                recycling. The front end of the desulfurization                device is provided with a biogas inlet, and the                rear end is provided with a biogas outlet, which is                connected to the dehumidifier, the heat exchanger,                the filter, the compressor and the membrane                separator. The compressor is provided with a                high-temperature flue gas outlet, which is                connected to the high-temperature flue gas inlet of                the absorption-type water chilling unit through a                pipeline. The water chilling unit is provided with                a water outlet, a water return outlet and a                low-temperature flue gas outlet. The water outlet                and the water return outlet are connected with the                dehumidifier. The low-temperature flue gas outlet                is connected with the heat exchanger provided with                a tail gas outlet. The rear end of the membrane                separator is provided with a bionatural gas outlet                and a carbon dioxide outlet.

GA 2021390798
AB    (CN212902108-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a three-dimensional distributed liquid atomizing heat exchanger and refrigerating system and air conditioner. The utility model claims a three-dimensional distributed liquid atomizing heat exchanger, comprising a shell, an air extracting device, a heat exchange device and a liquid atomizing device; the air pumping device is used for forming a negative pressure in the shell; the liquid atomizing device comprises a liquid supply pipe, an atomizing pipe, a atomizing head, the atomizing pipe is connected with the liquid supply pipe, the atomizing head is set on the atomizing pipe, the atomizing pipe is distributed in the shell body. The beneficial effects are as follows: the control centre can according to the set time; the set atomizing head opening proportion; randomly selecting the atomizing head to be opened or closed according to the random function; the opening or closing of each atomizing head is random; the atomizing liquid in the shell is uniform.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a control method used for the three-dimensionally distributed liquid atomization heat exchanger;
   (2) a refrigeration system; and
   (3) a carbon dioxide multi-split central air conditioner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the structure of the three-dimensionally distributed liquid atomization heat exchanger.
   Housing (1)
   Air extraction device (2)
   Heat exchange device (3)
   Atomization discharge pipes (4)
EA    NOVELTY - The heat exchanger has a housing (1), an air extraction device (2), a heat exchange device (3) and a liquid atomization device. The air extraction device is used to form negative pressure in the housing. The liquid atomization device includes a liquid supply pipe, atomization discharge pipes (4), and atomization heads. The atomization discharge pipes are connected to the liquid supply pipe. The atomization heads are arranged on the atomization discharge pipes which are three-dimensionally distributed in the housing. Control devices are arranged on the atomization heads to control the opening and closing of the atomization heads. The control devices are connected to a control center which can randomly select the atomization heads to be opened or closed according to a preset time, a preset percentage of the atomization heads which are open, and a randomization function. Each atomization head is opened or closed randomly such that atomized liquid is uniformly distributed in the housing.
   USE - Three-dimensionally distributed liquid atomization heat exchanger for use in a refrigerating system and a carbon dioxide multi-split central air conditioner (all claimed). Can be used for a high-rise building.
   ADVANTAGE - The three-dimensionally distributed liquid atomization heat exchanger provides an energy-saving effect, is environment-friendly, and has high heat exchange efficiency. The liquid atomizing liquid diffused around the heat exchange device, under the action of the negative pressure, the liquid dioxide in the micro-mass and the liquid device finishes the radiation heat exchange and then is pumped out of the shell by the air pumping device, so that the carbon dioxide refrigerant is condensed and liquefied. The water micro mass in the cavity absorbs radiation heat of carbon dioxide flowing in the heat-exchange device, dynamically and continuously decomposes the large micro mass into small micro mass to take away heat, when refrigerating.

PN CN111875455-A; CN111875455-B
TI Manufacturing 3-nitro-1,2,4-triazol-5-one (NTO) plastic bounded explosive (PBX) explosive modeling powder, by mixing crude NTO with deionized water, heating, filtering, adding binder solution and heating PBX explosive modeling coarse powder.
AU JIA L
   JIANG Z
   ZHANG L
   LIU W
   DU J
   CHEN Z
   YU S
   GU Y
   WANG F
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 2020B2148W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for manufacturing 3-nitro-1,2,4-triazol-5-one (NTO)-based plastic bounded explosive (PBX) explosive modeling powder, involves (i) mixing crude NTO with deionized water, heating, dripping sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution, introducing solution to pre-cooled ice bath, stirring solution, precipitating NTO solids, filtering and drying to obtain NTO recrystallized product, (ii) sieving NTO recrystallized product, preparing binder using fluororubber F2641 and polyvinyl acetate, adding binder to ethyl acetate to prepare binder solution, adding mixed particle size NTO recrystallized product and mixing, passing carbon dioxide gas, stirring suspension, partially opening upper valve of kettle, and completely opening the upper valve of kettle to obtain PBX explosive modeling coarse powder, and (iii) heating PBX explosive modeling coarse powder, heating, heat-preserving, cooling, heat-preserving, naturally cooling to room temperature, and stirring PBX explosive modeling coarse powder.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing NTO-based PBX explosive modeling powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The PBX explosive modeling powder ensures the safety of the subsequent press-fitting process and the tensile strength and compressive strength of the press-packed grains.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for manufacturing 3-nitro-1,2,4-triazol-5-one (NTO)-based plastic bounded explosive (PBX) explosive modeling powder, involves (i) mixing crude NTO with deionized water at a mass ratio of 10 g:80 g, heating to dissolve, dripping 1 %mass sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution, when the pH of the solution is 2.3, stopping dripping, introducing the solution to a pre-cooled ice bath to cool the solution, completing introduction process within 10 minutes, stirring the solution in a ice bath for a turbulent state, precipitating NTO solids, when the solution temperature reduced to 5 degrees C, continuously stirring for 2 hours, filtering and drying to obtain NTO recrystallized product, (ii) sieving the NTO recrystallized product obtained in step (i) to obtain the NTO recrystallized product with two particle sizes of 30-60 mesh and 60-80 mesh, mixing the NTO recrystallized products with particle sizes of 30-60 mesh and 60-80 mesh at a mass ratio of 3:1 to obtain a mixed particle size NTO recrystallized product, preparing binder using fluororubber F2641 and polyvinyl acetate according to mass ratio of 3:1, adding the binder to ethyl acetate to prepare 20% g/ml binder solution, adding the mixed particle size NTO recrystallized product to the binder solution and mixing at a mass ratio of 90 g:10 g to form a suspension, placing in an autoclave heated by a water bath temperature of 50-60 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide gas from the bottom of the kettle, stirring the suspension until the temperature and pressure in the kettle reach a supercritical state of TC of 31.1 degrees C and PC of 7.39 MPa, partially opening the upper valve of the kettle, continuously feeding carbon dioxide gas to the lower part, adjusting the upper valve to maintain the pressure in the kettle constant, after 1 hour, stopping the feeding of carbon dioxide and completely opening the upper valve of the kettle to obtain PBX explosive modeling coarse powder, and (iii) heating the PBX explosive modeling coarse powder obtained in step (ii) at 70 degrees C for 1.5 hours, heating at 120 degrees C at a heating rate of 20 degrees C/hour, heat-preserving for 2 hours, cooling to 70 degrees C at a cooling rate of 20 degrees C/hour, heat-preserving for 1.5 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, and stirring the PBX explosive modeling coarse powder for every 0.5 hour during the entire temperature treatment process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of nitric acid of the NTO recrystallized product is greater than 0.05 %mass, and the content of moisture of the NTO recrystallized product is less than 0.10 %mass. Preferred Property: The purity of the NTO recrystallized product is less than 99%.

PN CN108441642-A
TI Wet reclaiming and harmless treating            arsenic-alkali residue in antimony smelting by crushing            arsenic-alkali residue from different antimony smelting            company, leaching water with arsenic-alkali residue,            filtering and continuously leaching.
AU ZHANG Z
   LIN P
   LIU W
   YUAN F
AE CHENZHOU YANGTAO ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOG (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201868174H
AB    NOVELTY - Wet reclamation and harmless treatment of                arsenic-alkali residue in antimony smelting                involves crushing arsenic-alkali residue from                different antimony smelting company, primary                leaching water with arsenic-alkali residue to                ensure residue with good filtering performance,                filtering to obtain primary residue and                arsenic-containing sodium carbonate solution, and                continuously leaching primary residue; secondary                leaching water and primary residue, completely                recovering alkali and arsenic, leaching and                filtering to obtain secondary residue liquid and                filter residue, washing filter residue to obtain                secondary residue and washing water, harmless                treating secondary residue, returning secondary                residue liquid to primary leaching, and returning                washing water to secondary leaching; and adding                hydrogen peroxide into arsenic-containing sodium                carbonate solution, stirring and oxidizing,                precipitating from solution, and filtering to                obtain residue and arsenic-containing                solution.
   USE - Method for wet reclaiming and harmless                treating arsenic-alkali residue in antimony                smelting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has waste gas emission standard and                zero discharge of waste residue and waste water,                realizes separation of valuable elements, and                solves and eliminates piling environmental risk                caused by arsenic-alkali residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Wet reclamation and harmless treatment of                arsenic-alkali residue in antimony smelting                involves:
   (A) crushing arsenic-alkali residue from                different antimony smelting company to granularity                of less than 100 meshes, primary leaching water                with arsenic-alkali residue at liquid-solid ratio                of 24:1 at 50-100 degrees C for 3-6 hours to ensure                residue with good filtering performance, filtering                to obtain primary residue and arsenic-containing                sodium carbonate solution, and continuously                leaching primary residue;
   (B) secondary leaching water and primary                residue at liquid-solid ratio of 0.9-1.2:1 at                50-100 degrees C for 3-6 hours, completely                recovering alkali and arsenic, leaching and                filtering to obtain secondary residue liquid and                filter residue, washing filter residue to obtain                secondary residue and washing water, harmless                treating secondary residue, returning secondary                residue liquid to primary leaching, and returning                washing water to secondary leaching;
   (C) adding hydrogen peroxide into                arsenic-containing sodium carbonate solution,                stirring and oxidizing at 60-100 degrees C for 3-5                hours, precipitating from solution, and filtering                to obtain residue and arsenic-containing                solution;
   (D) passing carbon dioxide into                arsenic-containing sodium carbonate solution,                hydrolyzing sodium silicate to form precipitated                silicate, reacting aluminate with carbon dioxide to                form hydroxide sediment and sodium bicarbonate,                controlling final pH value of the reaction to                9-9.5, filtering to obtain purified residue and                purifying liquid, mixing purified residue with                secondary residue, and harmless treating;
   (E) putting purified solution into                carbonization tower, continuously introducing                carbon dioxide into purified solution, converting                sodium carbonate into sodium bicarbonate,                precipitating sodium bicarbonate crystal from                solution, controlling final pH to 8-9, cooling and                filtering to obtain sodium bicarbonate and liquid,                and calcining sodium bicarbonate to obtain sodium                carbonate and carbon dioxide;
   (F) returning liquid to leaching, enriching                arsenic, arsenic concentrating to 20-50 g/L, adding                reducing agent, reducing pentavalent arsenic into                trivalent arsenic, adjusting pH value of the                solution to less than 1, co-precipitating, and                filtering to obtain arsenic sulfide and arsenic                precipitation subsequent liquid;
   (G) evaporating and crystallizing arsenic                precipitation subsequent liquid, and filtering to                obtain sodium sulfate and crystallized mother                liquid; and
   (H) mixing treated secondary residue and                purified residue with water to form slurry, adding                harmless agent A, uniformly stirring, continuously                adding harmless agent B, reacting for 0.5-2 hours,                adding harmless agent C, adjusting pH to 8-9,                reacting for 2-4 hours, filtering to obtain                harmless residue, returning filtrate to harmless                processing and recycling use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is                  sodium bisulfite or sodium sulfite. The arsenic                  precipitation agent is sodium sulfide, sodium                  hydrosulfide or sulfureted hydrogen. The arsenic                  content in arsenic precipitation subsequent                  liquid is less than 100 mg/L. The harmless agent                  A is soluble ferrous salt and calcium salt,                  including ferrous chloride, ferrous sulfate or                  calcium chloride. The harmless agent B is                  oxidant, including hydrogen peroxide, air and                  hypochlorite. The harmless agent C is alkali,                  including sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate,                  calcium hydroxide and calcium oxide.

GA 202273143A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing road base slow setting cement                involves preparing cement retarder, modified                volcanic ash, primary slow setting cement, cement,                where the cement retarder preparation includes                primary mixing, primary coating, secondary coating,                the primary mixing includes adding zinc chloride,                citric acid, glucose, polyethylene glycol-1500,                polyethylene wax in the ball mill at 0.2-0.3                megaPascal, continuously supplying under the                nitrogen atmosphere for ball milling, ball milling                to obtain primary mixing. The mass ratio of zinc                chloride, citric acid, glucose, polyethylene                glycol-1500, polyethylene wax is                25-30:15-20:7-10:3-5:5-8. The primary coating                includes primary mixing into the fluidized bed, the                primary mixing in the fluidized state and heating                to 65-70degrees Celsius.
   USE - Method for preparation of road base slow                setting cement used for road base construction,                paving, and compacting and in building                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high strength, reduces the                cement slurry consistency, improves the high                temperature strength of the cement and the                sensitivity of the temperature, and reduces lowest                temperature construction of the retarded cement to                less than 5degrees Celsius.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing road base slow setting cement                involves preparing cement retarder, modified                volcanic ash, primary slow setting cement, cement,                where the cement retarder preparation includes                primary mixing, primary coating, secondary coating,                the primary mixing includes adding zinc chloride,                citric acid, glucose, polyethylene glycol-1500,                polyethylene wax in the ball mill at 0.2-0.3                megaPascal, continuously supplying under the                nitrogen atmosphere for ball milling, ball milling                to obtain primary mixing. The mass ratio of zinc                chloride, citric acid, glucose, polyethylene                glycol-1500, polyethylene wax is                25-30:15-20:7-10:3-5:5-8. The primary coating                includes primary mixing into the fluidized bed, the                primary mixing in the fluidized state and heating                to 65-70degrees Celsius, the primary coating liquid                is pressurized to 0.4-0.5 megaPascal by the nozzle                of the fluidized bed is sprayed to the primary                mixing surface. The solvent in the primary coating                liquid quickly volatilized to obtain primary                coating, where the mass ratio of primary mixing and                primary coating liquid in the primary coating is                10:5-7, the primary coating in the primary coating                liquid comprises 35-40 pts. wt. deionized water,                3-4 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, 1-2                pts. wt. xanthan gum and 1-2 pts. wt. secondary                coating. The primary package is placed in the cold                treatment device of -5-3?oC for cold treatment,                naturally recovered to the room temperature to                obtain primary coating, placed the primary coating                after cold treatment into the fluidized bed, make                the primary coating material after cold treatment                in the fluidized state heated to 60-65degrees                Celsius, pressurized the secondary coating liquid                to 0.5-0.6 megaPascal, sprayed to the primary                coating material surface cold treatment by the                nozzle of the fluidized bed, quickly volatilized                the solvent in the secondary coating liquid to                obtain cement retarder, where the mass ratio of the                primary coating material and the secondary coating                liquid after cold treatment in the secondary                coating is 10:3-5, the secondary coating liquid                comprises 30-35 pts. wt. deionized water, 4-5 pts.                wt. itaconic acid, 3-5 pts. wt. glucose, 2-4 pts.                wt. pre-gelatinized starch, 2-3 pts. wt. borax. The                modified volcanic ash is prepared by adding the                volcanic ash and modified agent into the powder                mixer to stir to obtain modified volcanic ash,                where the mass ratio of the volcanic ash to the                modifier in the preparation the volcanic ash is                10:2-3. The composition of modifier while preparing                modified volcanic ash comprises 10-12 pts. wt.                cationic polyacrylamide, 4-6 pts. wt. stearic acid,                2-3 pts. wt. maleic anhydride, 6-8 pts. wt.                non-ionic polyvinyl pyrrolidone. The primary slow                setting cement preparation includes stirring                silicate cement clinker, fly ash, bentonite,                modified volcanic ash, cement retarder, magnesium                carbonate, undecylenic acid zinc into powder mixing                machine, controlling the temperature of the powder                mixing machine is 40-45degrees Celsius, the                rotating speed of the mixed powder is 250-350rpm,                stirring for 30-40 seconds to obtain primary slow                setting cement. The preparation ratio of the                silicate cement clinker, fly ash, bentonite,                modified volcanic ash, cement retarder, magnesium                carbonate, zinc undecylenic acid zinc is                100-110:10-20:14-15:2-3:0.2-0.4:3-5:1-2. The slow                preparation cement includes placing primary slow                setting cement in a sealed reactor for heat                treatment, the sealed reactor vacuumizing until the                vacuum degree is 50-80 Pascal, introduced carbon                dioxide gas, controlled the gas pressure of the                carbon dioxide gas in the sealed reactor is                0.15-0.2 megaPascal, the temperature of the sealed                reactor is 45-50degrees Celsius, the time of the                heat treatment is 40-50 minutes to obtain                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The ball material                  ratio of the ball milling in the mixing is                  20-25:1, the rotating speed of the ball mill is                  300-400rpm, the ball milling temperature is                  30-40degrees Celsius, and the ball milling time                  is 30-40 seconds. The molecular weight of the                  cation polyacrylamide in the step of preparation                  the volcanic ash is 800-1000 million and the                  cation degree is 15-20%. The initial setting time                  of the road base layer slow setting cement at                  20degrees Celsius is 484-502 minutes and the                  final setting time is 739-752 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing (P1) methanol and olefins, comprises: e.g. (a) feeding a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture to a CPO reaction zone, where the CPO reactant mixture comprises oxygen, first hydrocarbons, and optionally steam, at least a portion of the CPO reactant mixture reacts, via an exothermic CPO reaction, in the CPO reaction zone to produce a CPO reaction zone effluent, the CPO reaction zone comprises a CPO catalyst, the CPO reaction zone effluent comprises hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), water, and unreacted first hydrocarbons and the CPO reaction zone effluent having a CPO effluent temperature.
   USE - The processes and system are useful for producing methanol and olefins (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The processes and system: enhance catalyst life-time and productivity; and do not integrate a CPO reaction zone with a dehydrogenation zone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing (P1) methanol and olefins, comprises: (a) feeding a catalytic partial oxidation (CPO) reactant mixture to a CPO reaction zone, where the CPO reactant mixture comprises oxygen, first hydrocarbons, and optionally steam, at least a portion of the CPO reactant mixture reacts, via an exothermic CPO reaction, in the CPO reaction zone to produce a CPO reaction zone effluent, the CPO reaction zone comprises a CPO catalyst, the CPO reaction zone effluent comprises hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), water, and unreacted first hydrocarbons and the CPO reaction zone effluent having a CPO effluent temperature; (b) feeding a dehydrogenation zone reactant mixture to a dehydrogenation zone, where the dehydrogenation zone reactant mixture comprises at least a portion of the CPO reaction zone effluent and second hydrocarbons, a portion of the dehydrogenation zone reactant mixture reacts, via an endothermic catalytic dehydrogenation reaction, in the dehydrogenation zone to produce a combined effluent, the dehydrogenation zone comprises a dehydrogenation catalyst, the dehydrogenation zone has a dehydrogenation zone temperature, the CPO effluent temperature is greater than the dehydrogenation zone temperature, the first hydrocarbons and the second hydrocarbons are the same or different, the combined effluent comprises H2, CO, CO2, water, olefins, unreacted first hydrocarbons and unreacted second hydrocarbons; (c) cooling the CPO reaction zone effluent, where cooling the CPO reaction zone effluent comprises heating the second hydrocarbons to the dehydrogenation zone temperature while cooling the CPO reaction zone effluent by heat transfer between the CPO reaction zone effluent and the second hydrocarbons; (d) removing at least a portion of the water from the combined effluent to produce a dehydrated combined effluent; (e) separating at least a portion of the dehydrated combined effluent into syngas, an olefins stream, and an alkanes stream, where the syngas comprises H2, CO, CO2, and methane (CH4), the olefins stream comprises at least a portion of the olefins in the dehydrated combined effluent and the alkanes stream comprises at least a portion of the C2+ alkanes in the dehydrated combined effluent; and (f) introducing at least a portion of the syngas to a methanol reactor to produce methanol. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing (P2) methanol and ethylene; and
   (2) a system (1000) comprising (a2) a CPO reaction zone (100) comprising a CPO catalyst, (b2) a dehydrogenation zone operable to convert at least a portion of a dehydrogenation zone reactant mixture comprising at least a portion of the CPO reaction zone effluent, second hydrocarbons, and optionally steam via an endothermic catalytic dehydrogenation reaction, to a combined effluent comprising H2, CO, CO2, water, olefins, unreacted first hydrocarbons, and unreacted second hydrocarbons, where the dehydrogenation zone has a dehydrogenation zone temperature less than the CPO effluent temperature, the first hydrocarbons and the second hydrocarbons are the same or different, (c2) a separation apparatus (300) operable to remove at least a portion of the water from the combined effluent to produce a dehydrated combined effluent, and separate at least a portion of the dehydrated combined effluent into a syngas stream comprising H2, CO, CO2, and CH4 and a C2+ hydrocarbons stream comprising C2+ olefins and C2+ alkanes, and (d2) a methanol reactor (200) configured for producing methanol from at least a portion of the syngas stream, where the CPO reaction zone and the dehydrogenation zone are configured so that direct heat transfer between the CPO reaction zone effluent and the second hydrocarbons provides for cooling of the CPO reaction zone effluent and heating of the second hydrocarbons to the dehydrogenation zone temperature.

In the process (P1): a common housing comprises both the CPO reaction zone and the dehydrogenation zone; the dehydrogenation zone reactant mixture comprises a quantity of second hydrocarbons effective to provide for cooling the CPO reaction zone effluent to the dehydrogenation zone temperature, while heating the second hydrocarbons to the dehydrogenation zone temperature; the combined effluent has a combined effluent temperature, and the dehydrogenation zone reactant mixture comprises a quantity of second hydrocarbons effective to provide for a combined effluent temperature variation within less than plus minus 10% of a target combined effluent temperature; heating the second hydrocarbons to the dehydrogenation zone temperature comprises (i) introducing liquid second hydrocarbons to the dehydrogenation zone, (ii) heating the liquid second hydrocarbons to vaporize at least a portion thereof and to form gaseous second hydrocarbons, and (iii) heating the gaseous second hydrocarbons to the dehydrogenation zone temperature; the syngas has an M ratio of greater than or equal to 1.8, where the M ratio is a molar ratio defined as (H2-CO2)/(CO+CO2), where the syngas has a hydrogen to carbon monoxide (H/CO) molar ratio of greater than 2, the dehydrogenation zone reactant mixture comprises an amount of second hydrocarbons effective to provide for a syngas characterized by a hydrogen to carbon monoxide (H2/CO) molar ratio of greater than 2 and/or the syngas had an M ratio and/or a H2/CO molar ratio that is greater than an M ratio and/or a H2/CO molar ratio, respectively of a syngas produced in an otherwise similar process without feeding the CPO reaction zone effluent to a dehydrogenation zone; the CPO effluent temperature is 400-1600 degrees C and the dehydrogenation zone temperature is 300-800 degrees C; the step (e) of separating at least a portion of the dehydrated combined effluent into syngas, an olefins stream, and an alkanes stream comprises (e1) introducing at least a portion of the dehydrated combined effluent and oil to an oil scrubber to yield a syngas-containing stream and a scrubbed oil stream, where the syngas-containing stream comprises H2, CO, CO2, CH4, and C2+ hydrocarbons, where the scrubbed oil stream comprises C2+ hydrocarbons and oil, the quantity of C2+ hydrocarbons in the scrubbed oil stream is greater than the quantity of C2+ hydrocarbons in the syngas-containing stream, (e2) introducing at least a portion of the scrubbed oil stream to an oil regenerator to yield a recovered oil stream and a first C2+ hydrocarbons stream, (e3) optionally recycling at least a portion of the recovered oil stream to the oil scrubber in step (e1), (e4) feeding at least a portion of the syngas-containing stream to a size-exclusion column to yield the syngas and a second C2+ hydrocarbons stream, (e5) separating at least a portion of the first C2+ hydrocarbons stream and/or at least a portion of the second C2+ hydrocarbons stream into the olefins stream and the alkanes stream, (e6) optionally recycling at least a portion of the alkanes stream to the dehydrogenation zone in step (b) and/or to the CPO reaction zone in step (a), and (e7) optionally introducing at least a portion of the syngas-containing stream to the methanol reactor in step (f); the step (f) of introducing at least a portion of the syngas to a methanol reactor to produce methanol comprises (f1) introducing at least a portion of the syngas to a methanol reactor to produce a methanol reactor effluent stream, where the methanol reactor effluent stream comprises methanol, water, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, CH4, and optionally C2 alcohols, (f2) separating at least a portion of the methanol reactor effluent stream into a crude methanol stream and a vapor stream, where the crude methanol stream comprises methanol and water, and where the vapor stream comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, CH4, and optionally 1-2C alcohols, (f3) separating at least a portion of the vapor stream into a hydrogen stream and a residual gas stream, where the hydrogen stream comprises at least a portion of the hydrogen of the vapor stream, and the residual gas stream comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, CH4, and optionally 1-2C alcohols, (f4) recycling at least a portion of the hydrogen stream to the methanol reactor, and (f5) optionally recycling at least a portion of the residual gas stream to the dehydrogenation zone in step (b) and/or to the CPO reaction zone in step (a); and the first hydrocarbons and/or the second hydrocarbons comprise methane, ethane, propane, butanes, naphtha, natural gas, natural gas liquids, associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas, paraffins, shale gas, shale liquids, fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) off gas, refinery process gases, stack gases and/or fuel gas from fuel gas header and/or the second hydrocarbons comprise ethane, and the olefins comprise ethylene. AB    NOVELTY - Preparation device comprises e.g. a reaction kettle, where the reaction kettle is orderly provided with the atom fountain device by production process flow of polyethylene terephthalate (PET)-graphene film coiled material, field-effect separator, atom disperser, flattening shaft, PET film, reformer, hall discharging device, weight-efficient fine device, Fermi-enhancing device, inspection device, steering shaft, the reaction kettle is further provided with the PET film chamber, and PET graphene coating bin, the atomic fountain apparatus is set in the interior of the reaction kettle, the field-effect separator is set on the lower part, the lower part of the atom fountain of the atom disperser is set on the field-effect separator, the flattening shaft is set in corresponding position of the reaction kettle of the PET film chamber, the PET film is set between the flattening shaft and the hall fine discharging device, and the recombination device is set beside the PET film.
   USE - The preparation device useful in polyethylene terephthalate graphene coated coil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation device: utilizes carbonaceous raw material as the carbon source i.e. acetylene; can consumes very little power and the daily power consumption is 24 degrees .
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation device comprises a reaction kettle, where the reaction kettle is orderly provided with the atom fountain device by production process flow of polyethylene terephthalate (PET)-graphene film coiled material, field-effect separator, atom disperser, flattening shaft, PET film, reformer, hall discharging device, weight-efficient fine device, Fermi-enhancing device, inspection device, steering shaft, the reaction kettle is further provided with the PET film chamber, and PET graphene coating bin, the atomic fountain apparatus is set in the interior of the reaction kettle, the field-effect separator is set on the lower part, the lower part of the atom fountain of the atom disperser is set on the field-effect separator, the flattening shaft is set in corresponding position of the reaction kettle of the PET film chamber, the PET film is set between the flattening shaft and the Hall fine discharging device, the recombination device is set beside the PET film is connected with the flattening shaft, the hall bar device is set beside the recombining device, the dose-effect group is set beside the hall fine discharging device, the Fermi-enhancer is set beside the dose-effect group, the inspection device is set at the Fermi-enhancer, the steering shaft is set beside the inspection corresponding to the position of the PET graphene coating bin, the PET film chamber is set at the outside of the reaction kettle, and the PET graphene film coating chamber is set at the outside the reaction kettle. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing a PET graphene-coated coil, comprising (i) adding reaction kettle upper cover, sealing; (ii) feeding the nitrogen into the reaction kettle to replace the air in the kettle; (iii) placing carbon dioxide into the reaction kettle to replace the nitrogen in the kettle; (iv) sending the inert protective gas into the reaction kettle to replace the carbon dioxide in the kettle; (v) removing impurities in the reactor through the circulation of inert gas to make the reactor into an ultra-clean kettle; (vi) feeding the oxygen into a reaction kettle; (vii) adding acetylene into the atom fountain device; (viii) using atom fountain, which makes the acetylene molecule loses a hydrogen atom, and atom fountain device releasing the mixed gas of carbon elements and impurities; (ix) using field-effect separator for separating and purifying the mixed gas carbon element; (x) using atom disperser to make carbon element atoms are not agglomerated in the specific time; (xi) using reformer the pure carbon element of the guide make carbon element including graphene of point structure onto a PET film, walking structure graphene island structure graphene fine under hall fine discharging device formed on the PET film of the dot structure graphene fine island-type graphene structure; (xiii) using weight-efficient fine formed on the PET film; (xiv) using fermion reinforcer to strengthen the stray structure graphene on the PET film into a layered growth stable graphene; (xv) inspecting the quality of the laminated structure of the finished PET graphene film using quality inspector; (xvi) using the steering shaft outputs of the finished PET graphene coating to the outside of the reactor; and (xvii) using the PET graphene coating compartment to winds up the PET graphene coating outside the reaction kettle.

PN US2019070598-A1; US10815549-B2
TI Purifying alumina contaminated with iron, titanium and/or magnesium-containing compounds comprises e.g. digesting contaminated alumina in sulfuric acid to produce first solution comprising aluminum, ferric, titanyl and/or magnesium sulfates.
AU MANSON I
AE ALUCHEM CO INC (ALUC-Non-standard)
GA 201921788P
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying (P1) alumina contaminated with iron-, titanium- and/or magnesium-containing compounds, comprises e.g. digesting contaminated alumina in sulfuric acid to produce first solution comprising aluminum sulfate, ferric sulfate, titanyl sulfate and/or magnesium sulfate, passing first solution over metallic iron to reduce any ferric sulfate to ferrous sulfate and produce second solution, adding ammonia to second solution to precipitate hydrated titania from any titanyl sulfate, filtering second solution to remove any hydrated titania and produce third solution, adding ammonium sulfate to third solution to form fourth solution comprising ferrous ammonium sulfate and ammonium aluminum sulfate, crystallizing ammonium aluminum sulfate from fourth solution, dissolving crystallized ammonium aluminum sulfate to form fifth solution, adding ammonium bicarbonate to fifth solution to precipitate basic ammonium aluminum carbonate and recovering basic ammonium aluminum carbonate.
   USE - The processes are useful for purifying alumina contaminated with iron-, titanium- and/or magnesium-containing compounds and recovering reusable catalyst and alumina from a hydroprocessing operation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The processes are economically viable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying (P1) alumina contaminated with iron-, titanium- and/or magnesium-containing compounds, comprises digesting the contaminated alumina in sulfuric acid to produce a first solution comprising aluminum sulfate, ferric sulfate, titanyl sulfate and/or magnesium sulfate, passing the first solution over metallic iron to reduce any ferric sulfate to ferrous sulfate and produce a second solution, adding ammonia to the second solution to precipitate hydrated titania from any titanyl sulfate, filtering the second solution to remove any hydrated titania and produce a third solution, adding ammonium sulfate to the third solution to form a fourth solution comprising ferrous ammonium sulfate and ammonium aluminum sulfate, crystallizing ammonium aluminum sulfate from the fourth solution, dissolving the crystallized ammonium aluminum sulfate to form a fifth solution, adding ammonium bicarbonate to the fifth solution to precipitate basic ammonium aluminum carbonate, recovering the basic ammonium aluminum carbonate, heating the recovered basic ammonium aluminum carbonate for a time and at a temperature sufficient to produce alumina, ammonia and carbon dioxide, adding ammonia or ammonium sulfate to the fourth solution to adjust to equimolar conditions to form a sixth solution, adding oxalic acid to the sixth solution to form insoluble ferrous oxalate, filtering the fifth solution to remove any ferrous oxalate and produce a seventh solution, adding oxalic acid to the seventh solution to precipitate magnesium oxalate, recovering any magnesium oxalate from the seventh solution, heating the magnesium oxalate to form magnesium oxide, heating the ferrous oxalate to produce ferrous oxide and carbon monoxide and reducing any ferrous oxide to metallic iron. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for recovering (P2) reusable catalyst and alumina from a hydroprocessing operation, comprising washing a granular, vanadium- and oil-contaminated, alumina-based catalyst with an oil-dissolving solvent sufficiently to render the catalyst free-flowing (non-sticky), separating the washed catalyst into a first, higher density portion and a second, lower density portion, returning the second, lower density portion to the hydroprocessing operation, purifying alumina contaminated with iron-, titanium- and/or magnesium-containing compounds in the first, higher density portion by digesting the contaminated alumina in sulfuric acid to produce a first solution comprising aluminum sulfate, ferric sulfate, titanyl sulfate and/or magnesium sulfate, passing the first solution over metallic iron to reduce any ferric sulfate to ferrous sulfate and produce a second solution, adding ammonia to the second solution to precipitate hydrated titania from any titanyl sulfate, filtering the second solution to remove any hydrated titania and produce a third solution, adding ammonium sulfate to the third solution to form a fourth solution comprising ferrous ammonium sulfate and ammonium aluminum sulfate, crystallizing ammonium aluminum sulfate from the fourth solution, dissolving the crystallized ammonium aluminum sulfate to form a fifth solution, adding ammonium bicarbonate to the fifth solution to precipitate basic ammonium aluminum carbonate, recovering the basic ammonium aluminum carbonate, heating the recovered basic ammonium aluminum carbonate for a time and at a temperature sufficient to produce alumina, ammonia and carbon dioxide, adding ammonia or ammonium sulfate to the fourth solution to adjust to equimolar conditions to form a sixth solution, adding oxalic acid to the sixth solution to form insoluble ferrous oxalate, filtering the fifth solution to remove any ferrous oxalate and produce a seventh solution, adding oxalic acid to the seventh solution to precipitate magnesium oxalate, recovering any magnesium oxalate from the seventh solution, heating the magnesium oxalate to form magnesium oxide, heating the ferrous oxalate to produce ferrous oxide and carbon monoxide and reducing any ferrous oxide to metallic iron.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (P1) further comprises: reacting the ammonia and carbon dioxide produced by heating the recovered basic ammonium aluminum carbonate with water to produce ammonium bicarbonate; recycling the produced ammonium bicarbonate to the process by adding it to the fifth solution to precipitate basic ammonium aluminum carbonate; filtering the first solution to remove silica; refining the ammonium aluminum sulfate using multiple crystallizations; providing heat pumps to transfer recovered cooling energy into the heat needed to re-dissolve the crystal crop between each crystallization; washing the crystals of ammonium aluminum sulfate with a solution of ammonium sulfate to reduce impurity levels at the crystal surfaces; washing, drying and calcining the hydrated titania to produce anatase titania; crystallizing ammonium sulfate produced when adding oxalic acid to the sixth solution to form insoluble ferrous oxalate; decomposing the ammonium sulfate crystals in hot, concentrated sulfuric acid to produce ammonia and additional sulfuric acid; and recycling the ammonia to the process. The filtering is performed with the temperature of the first solution at 90 degrees C. The multiple crystallizations are performed in chilled crystallization tanks. The ammonium sulfate is added at a concentration of 25-30% greater than the stoichiometric amount. Reducing the ferrous oxide to metallic iron comprises contacting the ferrous oxide with carbon monoxide. The oxalic acid is produced by the oxidation of glucose with nitric acid. The oxidation of glucose with nitric acid produces nitric oxide which is then oxidized to nitrogen dioxide which is then absorbed in water to produce nitric acid. Heating the magnesium oxalate to form magnesium oxide additionally forms carbon monoxide that is recycled to the process as a reducing agent for converting iron oxide to iron. Preferred Components: The carbon monoxide comprises the carbon monoxide produced by the thermal decomposition of the ferrous oxalate or the ferrous oxalate. The metallic iron over which the first solution is passed to reduce the ferric sulfate to ferrous sulfate comprises scrap iron.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109385394-A
TI Extracting, isolating and culturing homologous hepatic primary cells comprises e.g. anesthetizing the rodent, opening the abdomen, replacing the perfusate and digesting the digestive juice.
AU LUO Y
   DENG J
   GAO Z
   ZHAO W
   LIN B
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019212730
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting, isolating and culturing homologous hepatic primary cells comprises (i) anesthetizing the rodent, opening the abdomen using the indwelling needle and the hepatic portal vein cannula, (ii) replacing the perfusate, and digesting the digestive juice, (iii) taking out the digested liver in step (ii), placing in a vessel containing the separation solution, cutting the capsule on the surface of the liver with tweezers, bluntly separate, shaking to release a variety of livers and sieving the cell suspension of cells and the separation liquid is basal medium supplemented, (iv) obtaining parenchymal cells and resuspending the parenchymal cells prepared parenchymal culture medium, (v) obtaining upper and lower layers, (vi) centrifuging and culturing the four primary liver cells extracted and isolated in a cell culture incubator at 37 degrees C under a carbon dioxide content of 5% and a humidity of 95%.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting, isolating and culturing homologous hepatic primary cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting, isolating and culturing homologous hepatic primary cells comprises (i) anesthetizing the rodent, opening the abdomen, using the indwelling needle and the hepatic portal vein cannula, where the perfusate is perfused through the hepatic portal vein at 37 degrees C for 5-30 minutes and the perfusion rate is 5-25 mL/ Min and no bubbles should be generated during the perfusion process, where the perfusate is adding a buffer having a final concentration of 0.5-10 mM EGTA and 1-50 mM HEPES and the buffer is HBSS or D-Hanks or phosphate buffered saline without calcium ion and magnesium ion, (ii) replacing the perfusate, and digesting the digestive juice through the portal vein at 37 degrees C for 2-15 minutes, where the perfusion rate is 5-20 ml/minute and the digestive juice is consisting of basal medium supplemented with a final concentration of 50-300 mg/l calcium chloride, 1-50 mM HEPES, 10-100 mg/l collagenase, 0-100 U/ml penicillin and 0-100 U/ml streptomycin, the collagenase is one of type II or type IV collagenases and the base medium is DMEM-high sugar, DMEM-low sugar or 1640, (iii) taking out the digested liver in step (ii), placing in a vessel containing the separation solution, cutting the capsule on the surface of the liver with tweezers, bluntly separate, shaking to release a variety of livers and sieving the cell suspension of cells and the separation liquid is basal medium supplemented with 0.05-5 mu M dexamethasone and 2-10% FBS, the basal medium is DMEM-high sugar, DMEM-low sugar or 1640, which is screened from a tissue sieve or gauze having a pore size of 20-200 mu m, (iv) adding the cell suspension prepared in step (iii) to a centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 500-800 revolutions/minute for 1-5 minutes, obtaining the supernatant, resuspending the pellet in the cell separation solution, centrifuging at 500-800 revolutions/minute for 1-5 minutes, obtaining parenchymal cells, resuspending the parenchymal cells prepared parenchymal culture medium, where the parenchymal cell culture medium is includes 2%-10% FBS, 0-100 U/mL penicillin, 0-100 U/mL streptomycin, 1-10 mM L-glutamine, and 10- 100 nM dexamethasone Williams E medium, (v) adding 10-20 ml 20-50% percoll solution to a 50 ml centrifuge tube, adding 5-15 ml 10-20% percoll solution, centrifuging the supernatant at 1500-2000 revolutions/minute for 10-20 minutes to obtain upper and lower layers, (vi) centrifuging the two spare cell suspensions, resuspending in the buffer of magnetic bead sorting, and incubated with immunomagnetic beads connected with hepatic sinusoidal endothelial cells and Kupffer cells for 0.5-5 hours, slowly added to the sorting column, placing the sorting column, draining all the cell suspensions, taking out from the magnetic field, adsorbing the cells on the sorting column, washing with a special buffer for magnetic bead sorting, centrifuging at 100-1000 revolutions/minute for 1-10 minutes to obtain hepatic sinusoidal endothelial cells and Kupffer cells, immediately resuspending in the corresponding specially prepared medium, where the specially prepared hepatic sinusoidal endothelial cell medium includes 2%-10% FBS, 0-100 U/ml penicillin, 0-100 U/ml streptomycin, 1-10 mM L-glutamine, 10 -100 nM dexamethasone and 1-50 ng/ml VEGF in 1640 medium, the Kupffer cell medium includes 2-10% FBS, 0-100 U/ml penicillin, 0-100 U/ml streptomycin and 1-10 mM L-glutamine in 1640 medium, The hepatic sinusoidal endothelial cell-specific immunomagnetic beads IS commercially available Anti-CD146 or Anti-CD31 antibody-conjugated magnetic beads, The Kupffer cell-specific immunomagnetic beads is commercially available Anti-CD14, Anti-GFAP or Anti-F4/80 antibody-conjugated magnetic beads and culturing the four primary liver cells extracted and isolated in a cell culture incubator at 37 degrees C under a carbon dioxide content of 5% and a humidity of 95%.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
