PN US2018009662-A1; CA2935564-A1; WO2018007912-A2; WO2018007912-A3; TW201834741-A
TI Gas mixer used to mix hydrocarbon containing gas            with gaseous oxidant, comprises e.g. closed mixing            vessel, liquid supply nozzle, drain connection,            hydrocarbon containing gas supply nozzle, and gaseous            oxidant supply nozzle.
AU SIMANZHENKOV V
   GOODARZNIA S
   SERHAL K E
   OLAYIWOLA B
   CLAVELLE E
   SERHAL K
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM CORP (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 201803058K
AB    NOVELTY - Gas mixer (1) comprises e.g. closed mixing                vessel (10) having top end (9), bottom end (7), and                flooded with non-flammable liquid, a liquid supply                nozzle for introducing non-flammable liquid into                the closed mixing vessel in close proximity to the                top end, drain connection for removing                non-flammable liquid from the closed mixing vessel                located in close proximity to the lowest point of                the bottom end, at least one hydrocarbon containing                gas supply nozzle (4) for introducing hydrocarbon                containing gas into closed mixing vessel near the                bottom end, and at least one gaseous oxidant supply                nozzle.
   USE - The gas mixer is useful for mixing a                hydrocarbon containing gas with a gaseous oxidant,                preferably ODH process of ethane to ethylene (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas mixer: utilizes the impeller that                exhibits improved mixing; is inherently simple and                safe for mixing of a hydrocarbon containing gas                e.g. ethane with a gaseous oxidant that minimizes                the risk of an explosive event; utilizes the ODH                process for conversion of ethane to ethylene with                high conversion and selectivity rates, as the                resulting gaseous mixture comprising unreacted                ethane, ethylene and other hydrocarbons can be                recycled back to the closed mixing vessel, hence                efficient; and utilizes the exothermic oxidation                process that reduces the cost associated with                providing heat through an internal or external                heater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gas mixer (1) comprises a closed mixing vessel                (10) having a top end (9), a bottom end (7), and                flooded with a non-flammable liquid, a liquid                supply nozzle for introducing a non-flammable                liquid into the closed mixing vessel in close                proximity to the top end, a drain connection for                removing non-flammable liquid from the closed                mixing vessel located in close proximity to the                lowest point of the bottom end, at least one                hydrocarbon containing gas supply nozzle (4) for                introducing hydrocarbon containing gas into the                closed mixing vessel near the bottom end, at least                one gaseous oxidant supply nozzle for introducing                gaseous oxidant into the closed mixing vessel near                the bottom end, at least one means within the                closed mixing vessel for internal mixing of                introduced hydrocarbon containing gas with                introduced gaseous oxidant, and a mixed gas outlet                located in close proximity to the uppermost point                of the top end. The level of non-flammable liquid                within the closed mixing vessel is at a height                sufficient to allow mixing of the introduced                hydrocarbon containing gas and the gaseous oxidant                before reaching the top end so that bubbles of gas                exiting the non-flammable liquid comprise a mixture                of hydrocarbon containing gas and gaseous oxidant                that is outside the flammability limit. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) mixing a hydrocarbon containing gas and a                gaseous oxidant within a closed mixing vessel                flooded with a non-flammable liquid and having a                bottom end, a top end, and internal mixing means,                with the resulting gaseous mixture being homogenous                and comprising a ratio of hydrocarbon containing                gas to gaseous oxidant that is outside the                flammability envelope, comprising: introducing a                hydrocarbon containing gas into close proximity to                the bottom end of the closed mixing vessel,                introducing a gaseous oxidant, simultaneously with                the hydrocarbon containing gas, in close proximity                to the bottom end of the closed mixing vessel, and                recovering a homogenous gaseous mixture of                hydrocarbon containing gas and gaseous oxidant in                close proximity to the top end of the closed mixing                vessel, where the level of non-flammable liquid                together with the internal mixing means provide                sufficient residence time and promote interactions                between bubbles of hydrocarbon containing gas and                gaseous oxidant so that the gas exiting the                non-flammable liquid comprises a mixture of                hydrocarbon containing gas and gaseous oxidant that                is outside the flammability limit; and
   (2) oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) of ethane                to ethylene, comprising (i) mixing an ethane                containing gas and a gaseous oxidant in a closed                mixing vessel, (ii) passing the mixture from (i)                through a heat exchanger to raise the temperature                to at least 250 degrees C, (iii) introducing the                mixture from (ii) into an ODH reactor containing an                ODH catalyst, giving a product stream containing                unreacted ethane, carbon monoxide, ethane, carbon                dioxide, water, acetic acid, minimal hydrocarbons                and possibly oxygen, (iv) directing product stream                from (iii) through a quench tower to remove water                and acetic acid, (v) directing product stream from                (iv) through at least one amine wash to remove                carbon dioxide, and (vi) directing product stream                from (v) through a demethanizer (distillation                tower) to separate from the product stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic view of the gas                mixer.
   Gas mixer (1)
   Hydrocarbon containing gas supply nozzle                (4)
   Bottom end (7)
   Top end (9)
   Closed mixing vessel (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The non-flammable                  liquid is water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The means for internal                  mixing comprises a static mixer, a packed bed, a                  structured bed and an impeller. The hydrocarbon                  containing gas supply nozzle and the gaseous                  oxidant supply nozzle is connected to a sparger.                  The hydrocarbon gas containing supply nozzle and                  the gaseous oxidant supply nozzle comprises an                  aerator nozzle. The gas mixer further comprises:                  a condenser down-stream of the mixed gas outlet                  for condensing gaseous contaminants, including                  gaseous non-flammable liquid, present in the                  gaseous mixture; a recycle line for conducting                  non-flammable liquid from the condenser back to                  the closed mixing vessel where the non-flammable                  liquid is reintroduced in close proximity to the                  top end; at least one secondary hydrocarbon                  supply nozzle for introducing into the closed                  mixing vessel a hydrocarbon containing gas, a                  product stream via a product recycle line from an                  oxidation type reaction process, or both at a                  location distant from the bottom end, where the                  hydrocarbon containing gas is ethane and the                  oxidative type reaction process is an ODH                  reaction process; means for controlling the                  temperature of the non-flammable liquid in the                  closed mixing vessel comprising heat from the                  product recycle line, an external heater, and an                  internal heater; an inert material inlet located                  near the top end for introducing an inert                  material into the gas mixer above the level of                  the non-flammable liquid; and a suppression                  outlet located near the top end and leading to an                  explosion suppression system for removing gaseous                  mixture from above the level of the non-flammable                  liquid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon                  containing gas comprises ethane or methane.                  Preferred Method: The resulting gaseous mixture                  is passed through a condenser to remove gaseous                  non-flammable liquid, which can be recycled back                  to the closed mixing vessel, and other gaseous                  contaminants which can be discarded. The product                  stream from an ODH process and/or ethane is                  introduced into the closed mixing vessel at a                  location distant from the bottom end. The gaseous                  oxidant is diluted with water prior to                  introduction into the closed mixing vessel.                  Preferred Process: In the process, the product                  stream from any steps of (iii), (iv), (v) or (vi)                  is recycled back to the closed mixing                  vessel.

AU SONG W
   YANG H
   TIAN L
   YANG P
AE SICHUAN TIANREN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D6209K
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling solid waste containing elemental sulfur and silicon dioxide calcium sulfate hydrate comprises (i) taking solid waste comprising 4-20 wt.% elemental sulfur, 4-20 wt.% silicon dioxide, and balance calcium sulfate, and adding 5-10 wt.% carbon powder to the solid waste, (ii) placing the solid waste into a sealed container, circulating 4-12 times excess oxygen (or air or oxygen-enriched air), heating to 250-280degrees Celsius for 20-180 minutes, (iii) reacting sulfur dioxide gas, carbon and oxygen to produce carbon dioxide, and (iv) sending the carbon dioxide and the oxygen to gas utilization process to synthesize sulfur trioxide, and at the same time, obtaining a dehydrated microcrystalline solid of calcium sulfate and silicon dioxide with loose structure without elementalthe microcrystalline anhydrous sulfur. The microcrystalline solid has better strength than anhydrous gypsum after adding water to form.
   USE - Method for recycling solid waste containing elemental sulfur silicon dioxide and calcium sulfate hydrate.

PT P
PN CN113620334-A; CN113620334-B
TI Preparing dendritic ordered mesoporous copper            oxide nanomaterials useful in electrocatalytic carbon            dioxide reduction and other fields comprises e.g.            synthesizing mesoporous silicon dioxide template with            rich silicon hydroxy groups on surface, stirring,            carrying out reaction, drying, and calcining.
AU SONG S
   GU D
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2021C9799N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dendritic ordered mesoporous copper                oxide nanomaterials comprises (i) using                hydrothermal method for synthesizing mesoporous                silicon dioxide template with rich silicon hydroxy                groups on the surface, (ii) preparing the alcohol                solution of copper salt, (iii) adding tetraethyl                orthosilicate to the alcohol solution of copper                salt, adding the hydrochloric acid alcohol                solution, and completely stirring to obtain                solution A, (iv) adding the mesoporous silicon                dioxide template with rich silicon hydroxy groups                to the solution A, stirring and carrying out the                reaction, and drying to obtain the copper                salt-silicon dioxide complex, and (v) transferring                copper salt-silicon dioxide complex, calcining, and                removing the silicon dioxide template to obtain                dendritic ordered mesoporous copper oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dendritic                ordered mesoporous copper oxide nanomaterials,                which is useful in electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction and other fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is safe, reliable, relatively                simple and controllable; overcomes the serious                crystallization of mesoporous copper oxide in the                traditional preparation process, and the structure                is easy to be destroyed; and provides a feasible                method for the synthesis of other mesoporous metal                oxides. The nanomaterial: has high specific surface                area about (80-150 m2/g) and ordered mesoporous                structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                dendritic ordered mesoporous copper oxide                nanomaterial prepared by above mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  mesoporous silicon dioxide template with rich                  silicon hydroxy groups comprises Santa Barbara                  amorphous-15, mesostructured cellular foam, and                  Korea advanced institute of science and                  technology-6. In the step (ii), the copper salt                  comprises copper nitrate and copper sulfate. The                  alcohol comprises ethanol. The ratio of copper                  salt to ethanol is 1 g:5-20 ml. In the step (ii),                  the hydrochloric acid alcohol solution comprises                  an ethanol solution of hydrochloric acid with a                  concentration of 0.2-0.3 M. The dosage ratio of                  copper salt, tetraethyl orthosilicate and                  hydrochloric acid alcohol solution is 1-2 g:1 g:1                  ml. In the step (iv), the dosage ratio of the                  mesoporous silicon dioxide template with rich                  silicon hydroxy groups on the surface and the                  solution A is 1 g:20 ml. The filling ratio of the                  tetraethyl orthosilicate and the metal copper                  salt in the pore volume of the template is                  20-100%. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the                  stirring reaction is carried out at                  30-80&#176;C under sealed conditions for 2-4                  hours. The drying is carried out at                  50-80&#176;C. In the step (v), the                  calcination is carried out at 300-500&#176;C.                  The removing of the silicon dioxide template is                  carried out by using 0.5-2 mol/l sodium hydroxide                  at 40-80&#176;C.
DC E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E31-U01; E35-A; E12-A10; E31-N05C; E11-Q02C; J03-B01; J04-F02; J04-E04D; J04-E04A

PN CN214571769-U
TI Supercritical extraction device for edible oil,            has gap that is fixedly connected with high elastic            rubber block, rubber block and gap are sealed, and            pressure reducing block presses rubber block into            device main portion.
AU YU J
   LIN X
   XIE J
   HUANG C
   LIN S
AE GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C7438P
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical                field of edible oil, especially a supercritical                extraction device for edible oil, comprising a                device main body; the top surface end of the device                main body is welded with a supporting frame; the                middle part of the top surface of the supporting                frame is fixedly installed with a pushing cylinder;                the push rod end of the pushing cylinder is welded                with a connecting plate; two ends of the bottom                surface of the connecting plate are welded with a                decompressing block through the connecting strip;                the utility model firstly injects carbon dioxide                gas to the device main body through the carbon                dioxide pressurizing pump and the high pressure gas                pipe; when the pressure sensor detects that the gas                in the device main body reaches the supercritical                state; injecting the extract into the device main                body through the oil pump and the oil conveying                pipe; at this time, starting the pushing cylinder;                lifting the pressure reducing block; reducing the                pressure in the device main body; making the                supercritical CO2 fluid become the common gas;                making the CO2 and the extract quickly become two                phases; the extract is completely or substantially                separated out; the separating effect is good, so as                to ensure the production benefit and production                quality of the enterprise.

PN CN214336430-U
TI Low-smoke halogen-free B-level flame-retardant fire-resistant cable for use in high-rise and ultra-high buildings, has bag belt whose inner part is filled with inert gas, where outer side of bag belt is regularly provided with wrinkles.
AU FAN J
   FAN X
AE HARBIN JIAOLIAN POWER CABLE MFG CO LTD (HARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021B7259A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of cable, especially a low-smoke halogen-free B-level flame-retardant fireproof cable, comprising a cable core, the outer side of the cable core is orderly sleeved with an insulating layer, a flame retardant layer; an oxygen barrier layer and an outer sheath; the inner wall of the outer sheath is provided with multiple groups of containing cavity at intervals; the inner part of the containing cavity is filled with sodium bicarbonate powder; the utility model generates carbon dioxide and water principle by heating sodium bicarbonate powder set at the inner side of the outer sheath; when the outer sheath is damaged by heating; sodium bicarbonate powder will be decomposed immediately, through the inert gas expansion of the inner part of the bag belt to open the through hole of the middle part of the bag belt; when the skin of the outer sheath is damaged, carbon dioxide pushes the water to flow out to realize the first step control of the external fire; through the set of the oxygen barrier decomposition to control the fire for the second time; so as to greatly enhance the flame retardant effect of the cable; the design is reasonable.

PN CN113457671-A
TI Preparing low temperature catalytic oxidation volatile organic compounds catalyst useful in oxidation of volatile organic compounds into carbon dioxide and water, comprises e.g. introducing metal salt of manganese in citric acid and water solution, adding ethylene glycol, evaporating and calcining.
AU SONG C
   LIU Q
   WU X
   HAN R
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2021B7519V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low temperature catalytic oxidation volatile organic compounds catalyst comprises (i) introducing metal salt of manganese in first mixed solution of citric acid and water, adding ethylene glycol solution under stirring state, continuously stirring until metal salt of manganese is completely dissolved to form second mixed solution, using aqueous ammonia solution to adjust second mixed solution with PH of 7, and evaporating the second solution in the oil bath pot to obtain sol, where the molar ratio of citric acid and metal salt in the first mixed solution is 800: 1-3200: 1, the mass ratio of citric acid and water in the first mixed solution is 0.4: 1-1.8: 1, and the volume ratio of water to ethylene glycol in the second mixed solution is: 1:1-2:1; and (ii) primary calcining the sol at 500-600degrees Celsius under a nitrogen atmosphere and reacting for 10-50 minutes, and secondary calcining sol at 300-400degrees Celsius for 2-5 hours in an air atmosphere.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in the oxidation of volatile organic compounds into carbon dioxide and water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has large specific surface area, high volatile organic compounds removal rate and high stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt of manganese is nitrate, chloride or acetate.Preferred Method: The oil bath process of the second solution comprises heating at 70-100degrees Celsius for 3-8 hours, and continuously heating at 100-150degrees Celsius for 5-10 hours in the oil bath.

PN CN113385891-A; CN113385891-B
TI Method for manufacturing climbing mold guide rail            used in engineering construction field, involves            welding subsection of web plate and corresponding            portion of cover surface in carbon dioxide arc welding            mode, and outputting formed creeping formwork guide            rail.
AU LIU D
   HAN G
   ZHOU L
   LEI F
   LI D
   JIANG Y
   ZHANG Y
AE HUAXI ENG TECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN CO LTD (HUAX-Non-standard)
GA 2021B1981D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cutting climbing holes in                a web plate in an order jumping mode. The web                plates are cut in a sectional manner by adopting a                symmetrical cutting mode from inside to outside.                The two webs are placed in parallel, and bridged                and lapped. The web cutting position in a cold                grinding mode is processed to form a V-shaped                broken opening. The web and the panel are spliced                to form a creeping formwork guide rail, and a first                set distance between the web and the panel is                reserved. The spliced creeping formwork guide rail                is integrally placed into an anti-twisting                deformation tool, and a gap is reserved with a                second set interval between the creeping formwork                guide rail and the tool. The subsection of the web                plate and the corresponding portion of the cover                surface in a carbon dioxide arc welding mode is                welded. The formed creeping formwork guide rail is                outputted after performing performance processing                on the deformed guide rail.
   USE - Method for manufacturing climbing mold guide                rail used in engineering construction field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts various different                procedures, avoids the guide rail deformation                caused by heating and cutting during the processing                process, increases the guide residual rate, and                improves the processing precision and product                quality.
DC P56 (Machine tools (B23P,Q).)
MC P56-U17; P56-U40; P56-X

PN CN113337497-A
TI Preparing Pickering microcapsules based on            metal-organic framework materials useful for embedding            carbonic anhydrase, comprises embedding carbonic            anhydrase enzyme in situ, modifying, adjusting volume            ratio of water and oil, and shaking.
AU SHI J
   CHEN Y
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2021A4547M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Pickering microcapsules based on                metal-organic framework materials for embedding                carbonic anhydrase, comprises (i) embedding                carbonic anhydrase (CA) enzyme in situ by                biomimetic mineralization, adjusting the ratio of                zinc ion and organic ligand to prepare CA-ZIF-L                particles, modifying the surface of solid CA by                electrostatic attraction using PLGA and DNA as                modulators with the carboxy group in the PLGA and                DNA competing for the coordination of zinc ion,                resulting in a defective structure in the                morphological evolution of the composite material,                and forming PLGA-CA-ZIF-8 particles and                DNA-CA-ZIF-8 particles; (ii) using Tris-HCl buffer                as aqueous phase and hexadecane as oil phase,                adjusting the volume ratio of water and oil placed                in multiple glass reactors, adding the CA-ZIF-L                particles, PLGA-CA-ZIF-8 particles and DNA-CA-ZIF-8                particles into the glass reactor, shaking                vigorously for 1-2 minutes to mix the particles                with oil and water and allowing to stand.
   USE - The Pickering microcapsules based on                metal-organic framework materials is useful for                embedding carbonic anhydrase (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method hinders the contact between the gas                phase and the liquid phase due to the low                solubility of carbon dioxide molecules in the                aqueous phase, and improves the contact between gas                and liquid phase and increases the conversion                efficiency of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The process of                  preparing CA-ZIF-L particles utilizes zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate, and 2-methylimidazole                  organic ligand aqueous solution. The method                  utilizes zinc acetate. The Pickering                  microcapsules obtained by the preparation method                  utilizes calcium carbonate precipitation reaction                  performance test respectively.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

The desulfurized waste water after processing is introduced into the subsequent processing device or ash system (7) for recycling. The granular softening sludge is discharged into a softening sludge collecting tank (5). AU YAN X
   CUI Q
   FAN Y
   LI S
   WANG J
AE NANJING IRON & STEEL CO LTD (FUXN-C)
GA 202191362D
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (i) adopting 690MPa grade weathering steel welding plate, and (ii) using welding wire comprising 0.065-0.067 wt.% carbon, 1.35-1.50 wt.% manganese, 0.35-0.37 wt.% silicon, 0.33-0.36 wt.% chromium, 0.08-0.10 wt.% copper, 2.71-2.95 wt.% nickel, 0.001-0.002 wt.% sulfur, 0.010-0.012 wt.% phosphorus, and balance iron and unavoidable impurities. The welding parameter groove form includes symmetrical X type groove, slope angle of 60 degrees , blunt edge size of 1-2 mm, pre-heating temperature before welding of 100-120 degrees C, and interlayer temperature of 120-150 degrees C. Reverse side welding is carried out root-cleaning treatment. Gas shielded welding is used with welding current of 250-270A, welding voltage of 25-26 V, welding speed of 25-27 cm/minute, and heat input of 14.17 KJ/cm. The protection gas of gas shielded welding is 20-80% carbon dioxide and 80% argon.
   USE - The method is useful for gas shielded welding of weathering steel with yield strength of 690MPa.
   ADVANTAGE - The welding joint has good mechanical property. The welding joint corrosion resistance is good. The method is suitable for the welding of the weather-resistant steel with yield strength of 690 MPa.

PN CN112321405-A
TI Purifying coenzyme Q10, comprises e.g. preparing coenzyme Q10 bacterial residue, subjecting to preliminary extraction treatment, then adding solvent, heating and stirring, adding silica gel powder, spreading sample, and extracting.
AU CHENG Z
AE SHANDONG TAIHE WATER TREATMENT CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202115418J
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying coenzyme Q10 i.e. 2-((2E,6E,10E,14E,18E,22E,26E,30E,34E)-3,7,11,15,19,23,27,31,35,39-decamethyltetraconta-2,6,10,14,18,22,26,30,34,38-decaenyl)-5,6-dimethoxy-3-methylcyclohexa-2,5-diene-1,4-dione, comprises (1) preparing coenzyme Q10 bacterial residue by microbial fermentation method, and subjecting to preliminary extraction treatment to obtain crude coenzyme Q10 extract, (2) adding solvent to the crude coenzyme Q10 extract, and heating and stirring to make homogeneous liquid mixed system, (3) continuing to maintain the system temperature, adding silica gel powder to the liquid mixed system, and stirring completely to make the material uniformly adsorb on the silica gel to form sample preparation, and (4) spreading the sample on lower end of the extraction kettle, then adding blank unloaded silica gel powder to the top 2-3 cm of the extraction kettle, eluting with supercritical carbon dioxide for extraction, and obtaining pure yellow coenzyme Q10 through two-stage separation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing purifying coenzyme Q10.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has excellent separation effect and high carbon dioxide utilization rate; utilizes low volume organic solvents; is safe, environmentally friendly and suitable for industrial production; reaches purity of product more than 99%; and ensures reuse of silica gel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (1): initial extraction treatment method is supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and separation; pressure of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 30-40 MPa; extraction and separation temperature is 40-45 degrees C; control flow rate of carbon dioxide is 30-50 l/hour; cycle extraction time is 1-4 hours; and content of coenzyme Q10 in the crude extract obtained by preliminary extraction process is 45-60 wt.%. In step (2): solvent for dissolving coenzyme Q10 is acetone, n-hexane, ethyl acetate, chloroform and/or ethanol; and mass ratio between solvent and crude extract is 1:0.1-10. In step (3) and step (4): particle size of silica gel powder is 40-300 mesh. The mass of silica gel powder added in the step (3) is 1-3 times the mass of the liquid mixture prepared in the step (2). The silica gel powder can be recycled and reused after repeated extraction and treatment of supercritical carbon dioxide. Preferred Method: In step (2) and step (3): heating and maintaining temperature is 40-55 degrees C. In step (4): mass ratio between sample preparation and blank silica gel powder is 1: 4-13; and pressure of medium-supercritical extraction is 30-35 MPa, extraction temperature range is 40-45 degrees C, two-stage separation temperature is 45-52 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate is 10-30 l/hour, and cycle extraction time is 3-10 hours.

PN CN111647227-A
TI Preparing compatibilizer masterbatch used in alloy material, involves mixing polypropylene, compatibilizer and nucleating agent, adding to extruder, heating, injecting supercritical carbon dioxide and extruding through die of extruder.
AU YU L
   DING Y
   WANG L
   REN D
   LI R
AE ORINKO ADVANCED PLASTIC CO LTD (ORIN-Non-standard)
GA 202091492Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method (A) for preparing compatibilizer masterbatch, involves (i) uniformly mixing polypropylene, compatibilizer and nucleating agent to obtain a mixture, and (ii) adding the mixture to an extruder, heating to melt to form a melt, injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into the compression section of the extruder to mix the melt uniformly, and extruding through the die of the extruder to obtain the product.
   USE - The method (A) is used for preparing compatibilizer masterbatch used in the alloy material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method (A) provides the compatibilizer masterbatch, reduces the problems of weak interfacial bonding with polypropylene/polymethyl methacrylate materials of the current compatibilizer, difficulty in dispersing uniformly in the matrix and difficulty in mixing, improves the compatibility of polypropylene/polymethyl methacrylate, and obtains the alloy material with excellent mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a compatibilizer masterbatch prepared by the above method;
   (2) a polypropylene/polymethyl methacrylate alloy material comprising 44-80 parts mass polypropylene, 10-45 parts mass polymethyl methacrylate, 0.1-1 part mass antioxidant, 0.1-1 part mass lubricant and 12-30 parts mass compatibilizer masterbatch; and
   (3) a method (B) for preparing the alloy material, involving weighing the above-mentioned raw materials, uniformly mixing polypropylene, polymethyl methacrylate, compatibilizer masterbatch, antioxidant and lubricant, adding to twin-screw extruder, melting, extruding, water cooling, air cooling, pelletizing and drying to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nucleating agent comprises at least one of calcium carbonate, talc powder, kaolin and silica. The lubricant comprises at least one of zinc stearate, calcium stearate, and aluminum distearate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The antioxidant comprises at least one of N,N'-1,6-hexylene-bis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionamide), beta -(4-hydroxy-3,5-di-tert-butylphenyl)n-octadecyl propionate and tris(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)phosphite. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of polypropylene, compatibilizer and nucleating agent is (40-50):(40-50):(1-3). The injection rate of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 10-50 g/minute. Preferred Property: The melt index of polymethyl methacrylate at 220 degrees C/10 kg is 3-30 g/10 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The compatibilizer comprises at least one of ethylene-glycidyl methacrylate copolymer, ethylene methacrylate-glycidyl methacrylate copolymer, ethylene-glycidyl methacrylate-maleic anhydride terpolymer. Preferred Device: The extruder in method (A) is a single-screw extruder, a twin-screw extruder, a tandem double-stage single-screw extruder, or a twin-screw-single-screw tandem double-stage extruder. The twin-screw extruder in method (B) sequentially comprises eight heating working areas from the feed port to the discharge port, where the temperature of each area includes zone (i) of 75-110 degrees C, zone (ii) of 110-225 degrees C, zone (iii) of 210-260 degrees C, zone (iv) of 220-260 degrees C, zone (v) of 220-260 degrees C, zone (vi) of 220-260 degrees C, zone (vii) of 220-260 degrees C, and zone (viii) of 220-260 degrees C, and the temperature of the head of the twin-screw extruder is 220-260 degrees C, and the screw speed is 180-300 rpm.

   PRESTON J
AE ELIXSYS INC (ELIX-Non-standard)
GA 202071055N

PT P
PN CN111454145-A
TI Preparing 2-(4-(bromomethyl)phenyl)propanoic acid,            comprises e.g. reacting styrene with hydrogen chloride            gas, performing Grignard reaction, carboxylation            reaction, hydrolyzing and performing bromomethylation            reaction with hydrobromic acid.
AU GONG H
   LUO H
AE ZHANG M (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2020752041
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2-(4-(bromomethyl)phenyl)propanoic                acid (I), comprises (i) taking styrene (II) as raw                material, reacting with hydrogen chloride gas in                organic solvent to obtain (1-chloro-ethyl)-benzene                (III), (ii) performing Grignard reaction of (III)                to obtain Grignard solution i.e.                (1-phenylethyl)magnesium chloride (IV), performing                carboxylation reaction of (IV) and carbon dioxide                gas to obtain carboxylated solution i.e. magnesium                2-phenylpropanoate chloride (V), and hydrolyzing                (V) to obtain 2-phenylpropionic acid (VI), and                (iii) performing bromomethylation reaction of (VI)                with hydrobromic acid and polyformaldehyde to                obtain (I).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                2-(4-(bromomethyl)phenyl)propanoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes cheap and easily                available raw materials; and is simple and suitable                for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 2-(4-(bromomethyl)phenyl)propanoic                acid of formula (I), comprises (i) taking styrene                of formula (II) as raw material, reacting with                hydrogen chloride gas in organic solvent to obtain                (1-chloro-ethyl)-benzene of formula (III), (ii)                performing Grignard reaction of (III) to obtain                Grignard solution i.e. (1-phenylethyl)magnesium                chloride of formula (IV), performing carboxylation                reaction of (IV) and carbon dioxide gas to obtain                carboxylated solution i.e. magnesium                2-phenylpropanoate chloride of formula (V), and                hydrolyzing (V) to obtain 2-phenylpropionic acid of                formula (VI), and (iii) performing bromomethylation                reaction of (VI) with hydrobromic acid and                polyformaldehyde to obtain (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the organic                  solvent is dichloromethane, the mass ratio                  between dichloromethane and (II) is 2-4:1, the                  reaction temperature is -5 degrees C to 20                  degrees C and the reaction time is 5-15 hours. In                  step (ii): the Grignard reaction is carried out                  with metal magnesium in 2-methyltetrahydrofuran                  as solvent, the mass ratio between                  2-methyltetrahydrofuran and (III) is 4-6:1, the                  molar ratio between metal magnesium and (III) is                  1.8-2.2:1, the Grignard reaction temperature is                  0-30 degrees C and the Grignard reaction time is                  3-10 hours; and the carboxylation reaction                  temperature is -15 degrees C to 30 degrees C and                  the carboxylation reaction time is 3-5 hours. The                  step (iii) comprises mixing hydrobromic acid,                  polyformaldehyde and (VI), dropwise adding                  sulfuric acid at controlled temperature, heating                  and maintaining constant temperature. The                  controlled temperature is 20-50 degrees C and the                  heating temperature is 60-120 degrees C. In step                  (iii), the bromomethylation reaction time is 3-20                  hours. The step (iii) further comprises cooling                  to 25-35 degrees C after the reaction is                  completed, separating lower acid water, adding                  dichloromethane, dissolving, washing the material                  until the aqueous phase is neutral, distilling                  off dichloromethane under normal pressure to                  obtain crude product, adding acetonitrile,                  repeatedly crystallizing and purifying for 1-2                  times, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain                  (I). Preferred Components: In step (ii), the                  hydrolysis solution for hydrolysis reaction is                  5-10% hydrogen chloride aqueous solution. In step                  (iii), the mass ratio between (VI), hydrobromic                  acid, polyformaldehyde and sulfuric acid is                  1:1.1-2.2:0.2-0.5:1.1-2.2.

PN CN110627028-A
TI Highly stable two-dimensional black phosphine useful as catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
AU XU H
   ZHOU W
   LI Q
   YANG J
   ZHOU G
   LI H
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202002591V
AB    NOVELTY - Highly stable two-dimensional black phosphine is claimed. The phosphine has an ultra-thin thickness of not greater than 2 nm, and is stable in the air and not easily oxidized.
   USE - The phosphine is useful as catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: modifies the material itself by using organic solvents; introduces hydroxy functional groups on the surface of black phosphorus to make it stable in the air; utilizes organic solvents to introduce organic functional groups to modify the ultra-thin black phosphorus surface; strips the bulk black phosphorus to not greater than 2 nm; and increases stability and catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the highly stable two-dimensional black phosphine, comprising (i) taking tin powder, iodine and red phosphorus, mixing in a quartz container, sealing under the protection of nitrogen, placing the quartz container in a muffle furnace, and reacting at a specific temperature for a specific period of time to obtain a bulk black phosphorus, (ii) freezing the bulk black phosphorus in liquid nitrogen for a specific period of time, dispersing the frozen solid in an organic solvent, and introducing hydroxy groups on the surface to protect the surface from excessive contact with air or water, (iii) treating the dispersion in an ultrasonic reactor for a specific period of time, and centrifuging the dispersion with a water bath to obtain a supernatant containing two-dimensional ultra-thin black phosphorus, and (iv) freezing and drying the supernatant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of the tin powder, red phosphorus and iodine is 10:5:1. In the step (ii), the mass of the body black phosphorus is 50-150 mg, Preferred Method: In the step (i), the muffle furnace is heated from room temperature to 600 degrees C in 7 hours, and then reacted at 600 degrees C for 5-10 hours. In the step (ii), the freezing is carried out for 30-60 minutes. In the step (iii), the ultrasonic reaction is carried out with a power of 100-200 W in a water bath of 0-10 degrees C for 3-5 hours, and the centrifugation is carried out at 500-5000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 3-5 minutes. In the step (iv), the freezing and drying are carried out at -60 to -80 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the length of the quartz container is 100 mm, the inner diameter is 8 mm, and the wall thickness is 1 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the organic solvent is isopropyl alcohol (IPA), ethanol (EtOH) or methanol (CH3OH), preferably an organic solvent diluted with water at a volume ratio of 1:0.5-1, where the amount of the organic solvent is 30-50 ml.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

The                infrared detection device is connected to the air                pump and detects the concentrations of nitrogen                oxides, hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide in the                automobile exhaust. A carbon monoxide sensor (5)                detects a carbon monoxide concentration in the                automobile exhaust. A signal processing circuit                analyzes and outputs detection results of infrared                sensor, oxygen sensor (4), and carbon monoxide                sensor. The control end of the air pump is                connected to a first relay. A first driving circuit                is connected to a signal processing circuit. A                display screen is connected to the signal                processing circuit and outputs a detection                result.
   USE - Device for detecting exhaust gas of                automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The device enables simple structure, wide                application prospect, and convenient usage. The                accuracy of the exhaust gas detection can be                improved.

AU LI B
AE LI B (LIBB-Individual)
GA 201996929L
AB    NOVELTY - A large-position hindered gas separation membrane comprises non-coplanar polyimide (I).
   USE - Large-position hindered gas separation membrane for separating and recovering hydrogen in hydrogen-containing gas, carbon dioxide in carbon-containing gas, and hydrogen sulfide in sulfur-containing gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas separation membrane has high permeability and selectivity, and excellent film-forming properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A large-position hindered gas separation membrane comprises non-coplanar polyimide having structural formula (I).
   Ar' = aryl;and
   n = positive number.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of gas separation membrane, which involves dissolving 4,4'-diamino-3,3'-dimethylbiphenyl in chlorobenzene, adding N-bromosuccinimide and azobisisobutyronitrile, reacting at 100-150 degrees C for 10-20 hours, precipitating by adding methanol, filtering, washing, drying to obtain 4,4'-diamino-3,3'-dibromomethylbiphenyl, dissolving 1-hydroxyadamantane in tetrahydrofuran, adding sodium hydride and 4,4'-diamino-3,3'-dibromomethylbiphenyl, reacting at 30-100 degrees C for 3-12 hours, precipitating by adding methanol, filtering, washing, drying to produce to obtain non-coplanar macro-hindered diamine monomer, dissolving the monomer in a polar solvent, stirring, adding aromatic dianhydride, reacting at 50-100 degrees C, stirring for 2-12 hours, applying resulting polyamic acid on a cleaned glass plate, placed in an oven to perform thermal imidization reaction at 80-220 degrees C for 5-12 hours, and naturally cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polar solvent is N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide or sulfolane. Aromatic dianhydride is pyromellitic anhydride, biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, diphenyl ether tetracarboxylic dianhydride, benzophenone tetracarboxylic dianhydride, hexafluoropropane dianhydride or dimethyltetracarboxylic dianhydride. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of 4,4'-diamino-3,3'-dimethylbiphenyl, N-bromosuccinimide, and azobisisobutyronitrile is 1:(2-4):(1-2). The molar ratio of 1-hydroxyadamantane, sodium hydride and 4,4'-diamino-3,3'-dibromomethylbiphenyl is 1:1:(0.3-1). The molar ratio of the diamine monomer and the aromatic dianhydride is 1:(0.5-2). The usage amount of 4,4'-diamino-3,3'-dimethylbiphenyl is 1-20 g. The usage amount of N-bromosuccinimide is 2-50 g. The usage amount of azobisisobutyronitrile is 0.05-2 g. The usage amount of 1-hydroxyadamantane is 1-15 g. The usage amount of sodium hydride is 0.1-3 g. The usage amount of 4,4'-diamino-3,3'-dibromomethylbiphenyl is 1-20 g. The usage amount of diamine monomer is 1-20 g. The usage amount of aromatic dianhydride is 0.5-25 g. The usage amount of chlorobenzene is 20-500 ml. The usage amount of methanol is 200-5000 ml. The usage amount of tetrahydrofuran is 20-500 ml. The usage amount of the polar solvent is 10-200 ml.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The gas separation membrane has hydrogen permeability coefficient of 20-150 barrer, oxygen permeability coefficient of 1-20 barrer, nitrogen permeability coefficient of 0.2-5 barrer, carbon dioxide permeability coefficient of 5-60 barrer, ratio of selectivity of hydrogen and nitrogen of 60-150, ratio of selectivity of oxygen and nitrogen of 5-10, and ratio of selectivity of carbon dioxide and nitrogen of 10-50.
   Preferred Definitions:
   Ar' = group chosen from groups of formulae (i)-(vi);and
   n = 30-200.

TI Big data based environment monitoring and verifying method, involves measuring concentration of calibration environment at different atmospheric pressures, and obtaining correction factor for different temperature and air pressure.
AU LI S
AE JIANGXI MINXUAN INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201995471B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing an environmental monitoring device in calibration environment (S2). Standard gas with concentration of carbon monoxide is injected. Standard gas evenly distributed in the calibration environment is ensured. The calibration environment is ventilated (S3) for 1 hour. The standard gas with carbon monoxide is re-injected (S4). Gas concentration of the calibration environment under different humidity is measured. The calibration environment is ventilated (S5) adequately for 1 hour. Gas concentration of the calibration environment at different atmospheric pressures is measured (S6). Correction factor for the different temperature, the humidity and the air pressure of the environmental monitoring device is obtained (S7), where the environment monitoring device is Carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide or nitric Oxide gas sensor.
   USE - Big data based environment monitoring and verifying method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively reducing the problem of environmental monitoring error caused by environmental factors interference and improving accuracy of environmental monitoring.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a big data based environment monitoring and verifying method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Step for placing environmental monitoring device in calibration environment (S2)
   Step for ventilating calibration environment (S3)
   Step for re-injecting standard gas with carbon dioxide (S4)
   Step for ventilating calibration environment (S5)
   Step for measuring gas concentration of calibration environment at different atmospheric pressures (S6)
   Step for obtaining correction factor for different temperature (S7)

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal nickel and manganese metal complex (A) of formulae C12H6N6(Ni)2O16)(sic) and (C12H12MnN4O10) is claimed. (A) is prepared by using 2-methyl-4,5 imidazole dicarboxylic acid as ligand, synthesizing with hexahydrate nickel chloride and manganese sulfate under solvothermal conditions to obtain product An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing metal nickel and metal manganese complex, comprising (i) dissolving 2-methyl-4,5-imidazoledicarboxylic acid with nickel chloride hexahydrate and manganese sulfate in a solvent to obtain mixed solution, (ii) sealing the mixture obtained in the step (i) and performing solvothermal reaction, slowly cooling to room temperature to obtain light green block crystals and pink block crystals, (iii) taking crystal obtained in the step (ii), separately washing, and naturally drying to obtain single crystal sample of the metal nickel complex and the metal manganese complex.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The form of the metal nickel complex and the metal manganese complex are solid crystals. The crystals belong to the monoclinic system, and the space groups are P21/m and P21/c, respectively. The unit cell parameters of the metal nickel complex are a is 13.868 (5) Angstrom , b is 6.839 (3), c is 16.578 (7) Angstrom , alpha , beta and gamma is 90 degrees , and V is 1572.44 (11) Angstrom 3. The unit cell parameters of the metal manganese complex are a is 12.256(6) Angstrom , b is 9.155 (5), c is 7.422 (3) Angstrom , alpha , gamma = 90 degrees , beta = 101.824 Angstrom (4), V = 815.17(7) Angstrom 3. In the step (i), the molar ratio of 2-propyl-4,5-imidazole dicarboxylic acid and nickel chloride hexahydrate is 1:2. The molar ratio of 2-propyl-4,5-imidazole dicarboxylic acid and manganese sulfate is 1:2. The solvent is mixed solvent of distilled water and ethanol. Preferred Method: The metal nickel complex has decomposition temperature of 210 degrees C and metal manganese complex decomposition temperature of 143 degrees C. In the step (ii), the solvothermal reaction is carried out at 120-130 degrees C for 48-72 hours. The cooling rate is 10 degrees C per hour. In the step (iii), the crystal obtained in the step (ii) is washed with ethanol.

PN DE202019000347-U1
TI Reclamation material used as soil replacement material to improve load-bearing capacity of subsoil in unsustainable seabed, comprises buoyant concrete having closed air pores and buoyancy-enhancing properties.
AU 
AE ULBRICH R (ULBR-Individual)
GA 201983103V
AB    NOVELTY - Land reclamation material having combined function of self-regulating height adjustment to the sea game angel, comprises buoyant concrete having closed air pores and buoyancy-enhancing properties.
   USE - The material is useful as soil replacement material for improving load-bearing capacity of subsoil in unsustainable seabed and relieving seabed by lower weight compared to sand bedding, and as substructure for traditional method of land reclamation by dumping, protection for buildings in earthquake-affected areas, protection against shear waves, land reclamation and for land, and liquid-tight to prevent contamination to substrate, liquid permeable can be produced as drain concrete, and filling tunnel, borehole, shaft and canal (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has less carbon-dioxide emissions than traditional landfill; reduces excavated soil and carbon-dioxide exchange; has compressive strength, bending strength and tensile strength; saves time and cost; prevents sound transmission while driving over or sound transmission to other structures; eliminates horizontal seal for subsequent constructions; promotes protection from cold and heat to sea water due to its good insulation and insulation value; and quickly placed back on site due to mobility to create it as in-situ concrete (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of material as a soil replacement material for improving the load-bearing capacity of the subsoil in unsustainable seabed and relieving seabed by lower weight compared to the sand bedding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material in various densities of 400-1000 kg/m3 can be produced, and thus has very high buoyancy. The material combine two previous systems, namely buoyant land reclamation and self-regulating sea level adaptation. The buoyancy characteristics depend on the dry space weight of the concrete. A landfill to the seabed is eliminated. The material is used for regions with very large sea depths and embankments surf is not destroyed by waves and water friction or can be removing. The mixture and the density can be adjusted so that it meets the requirements of building physics. The concrete can be produced as in-situ concrete or ready-mixed concrete. The concrete is highly fluid and thus self-leveling. The material has no gravel bearing layer, capillary-breaking layer and clean-up layer is required for support structure as specified. The material is a load-bearing material. The density of the concrete is determined by the pore size and the number of pores. The land reclamation by lightweight concrete/foam concrete is steam and gas diffusion tight. The material dampens vibration in sound waves (longitudinal waves) as well as waves generated by vibration (shear waves) and only a coarse planum has to be created for the foundation sole, since due to the high flow-ability of the concrete which lies frictionally on the coarse planum. The material under difficult subsoil conditions with low bearing capacity measures e.g. substitution of gravel, limestone, cohesive soil or pile foundations can be dispensed with by the weight saving and the specific gravity on the seabed. The foam concrete is manufactured with special air-entraining agents as in-situ concrete and ready-mix concrete and can also be adapted to the different weather conditions and with special air entrainment agents can be pumped over distances of more than 1 km, as well as being cast on site.

TI Use of extract of industrial hemp stalk for preparing activated carbon used as additive in cosmetics.
AU ZHANG W
   XIE J
   ZHAN Z
   LI J
AE YUNNAN FENGSHENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 201985289P
AB    NOVELTY - A use of extract of industrial hemp stalk for preparing activated carbon is claimed.
   USE - Use of extract of industrial hemp stalk for preparing activated carbon used as additive in cosmetics.
   ADVANTAGE - The activity of the active substance in the industrial hemp stalk is retained to ensure the quality of the activated carbon, and form activated carbon with specific functional activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation activated carbon using industrial hemp stalk extract which involves chopping fresh industrial hemp stalks, placing in the air for 12-24 hours to obtain material (A), adjusting the water content of the material (A) to 8-10% to obtain the material (B), placing material (B) in an extraction vessel of a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device at an extraction temperature of 50-55 degrees C and an extraction pressure of 30-35 MPa, extracting for 100-150 minutes under the condition of carbon dioxide flow rate of 30-50 kg/hour to obtain liquid (C) and residue (D), pulverizing residue (D), sieving to obtain the material (E), mixing material (E) and the activator solution, immersing at room temperature for 2-4 hours, drying to obtain a material (F), heating material (F) to 700-900 degrees C in a muffle furnace, keeping for 1-3 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain a material (G), adding liquid (C) to the material (G), mixing, heating to 400-600 degrees C, keeping for 0.5-1 hour and naturally cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The activator solution is a 0.2-1 %mass potassium permanganate solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The size of chopped industrial hemp stalk is (1-3)x (1-3) cm. The pulverized residue (D) is pulverized through a 50 mesh sieve. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the material (E) and the activator solution is 1:(50-100), preferably 1:60. Preferred Condition: The regulation is humidification or drying. The regulation is to place the material (A) in a closed constant temperature and humidity environment for humidity balance regulation.

TI Liquid supply system for preparing and dispensing water-based liquids at a desired temperature, has water supply that is provided with water tank and connections to a water main line, and water boiler heats water from the water supply.
AU DAHAN Y
   DAHAN A
AE UNITO SMART TECHNOLOGIES LTD (UNIT-Non-standard)
   UNITO SMART TECHNOLOGIES LTD (UNIT-Non-standard)
GA 2019753545
AB    NOVELTY - The liquid supply system (100) comprises a water supply that is provided with a water tank (102) and multiple connections (130,132) to a water main line. A faucet (112) has an outlet for dispensing the prepared water-based liquid. A water filter is adapted to filter the water from the water supply. A water boiler heats the water from the water supply to a predetermined temperature. A water chiller is adapted to cool the water from the water supply to a predetermined temperature. A soda machine carbonizes the water from the water supply to a predetermined carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - Liquid supply system for a faucet of a soda machine (all claimed) for preparing and dispensing water-based liquids at a desired temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The soda machine carbonizes the water from the water supply to a predetermined carbon dioxide concentration, and hence enhances the efficiency of gas mixing, and water quality, and reduces the water hardness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a liquid level sensor;
   (2) an ultra violet sterilizing device; and
   (3) a control and communication system for the liquid supply system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the liquid supply system.
   Liquid supply system (100)
   Water tank (102)
   Manifold (106)
   Pipes (108)
   Faucet (112)
   Connections (130,132)

PN FR3077958-A1; WO2019162579-A1; EP3755152-A1; US2021219560-A1; FR3077958-B1
TI Method for producing e.g. pastries, in bakery,            involves kneading dough composition in closed chamber,            and injecting carbon-dioxide into chamber so as to            obtain low-gluten/gluten-free cooking dough with            porosity superior than 17 percentage.
AU HENRY J
   JOSEPH H
   LE BAIL P
   LE BAIL A
   ALAIN L B
   PATRICIA L B
AE PHYTOBOKAZ SARL (PHYT-Non-standard)
   PHYTOBOKAZ SARL (PHYT-Non-standard)
   PHYTOBOKAZ SARL (PHYT-Non-standard)
   PHYTOBOKAZ SARL (PHYT-Non-standard)
GA 2019723810
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves kneading a dough                composition in a closed chamber, and injecting                carbon-dioxide into chamber simultaneously to the                kneading, so as to obtain a low-gluten or                gluten-free cooking dough with a porosity superior                than 17%, where the dough composition comprises                between 40 and 65% of weight of water and between                29 and 52% of weight of flour without gluten or                poor in gluten. The kneading of the dough                composition is stopped when pH of the dough is                stabilized between 5 and 5.4 and when the porosity                of the cooking dough ranges between 28 and 32                percentage.
   USE - Method for producing a low-gluten or                gluten-free cooking dough e.g. pasta dough such as                gluten-free bread dough, gluten-free bread, or                pastries, cakes, and pizzas, in bakery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables producing the gluten-free                or low-gluten dough with long shelf life, whose                physical properties remain comparable to the bread                pasta, without the addition of CMC improvers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a cooking dough
   (2) a system for producing a cooking dough                from a dough composition
   (3) a method for producing a product without                or low in gluten.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graph illustrating                evolution of sample.

PT P
PN KR2019011608-A
TI Converter refining method, involves absorbing heat of first oxidation refining step through endothermic reaction of second oxidation refining step to uniformly maintain temperature of metal melt in converter.
AU IL S
   CHUNG S H
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 201914309D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves charging (S10) a metal melt into a converter having an upturned lance and a deodorant nozzle. The oxygen is blown (S20) into the inside of the converter through uploss lance to perform a first oxidation refining of the metal melt. The carbon dioxide and the water vapor is injected by replacing oxygen into the inside of the converter through the low-loss nozzle to perform a second oxidative refining of the metal melt. The heat of the first oxidation refining step is absorbed (S30) through an endothermic reaction of the second oxidation refining step to uniformly maintain the temperature of the metal melt in the converter.
   USE - Converter refining method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the efficiency of the decarburization reaction. The amount of oxygen used for pyrolysis refining is reduced. The environmental contribution due to carbon dioxide emission is reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart illustrating the converter refining method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for charging a metal melt into a converter having an upturned lance and a deodorant nozzle (S10)
   Step for blowing oxygen into the inside of the converter through uploss lance to perform a first oxidation refining of the metal melt (S20)
   Step for absorbing heat of the first oxidation refining step through an endothermic reaction of the second oxidation refining step to uniformly maintain the temperature of the metal melt in the converter (S30)
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

GA 2018A0740T
AB    NOVELTY - Accelerating the moxa softening involves selecting a blade with good growth and removing unqualified blades, drying blades in the shade or the sun in a good ventilation manner, crushing the blades into a velvet shape by using a crusher or other tools for crushing, where the carbon source is used for uniformly mixing and stirring the crushed moxa and the dextrin, scattering the yeast seeds on the surface of the carbon source uniformly, stirring uniformly and placing in a container, sealing and solidifying and fermenting, where under the anaerobic condition, the yeast utilizes saccharides to produce ethanol and carbon dioxide (CO2), the total reaction formula is glucose (C6H12O6) ethanol (2C2H5OH) + carbon dioxide (2CO2), dissolving the volatile oil in ethanol, performing cellulose decomposition, carrying out semi-processing on the fermented moxa through an extrusion die, drying and heating the formed moxa sticks in a microwave oven.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Drying and heating the formed moxa sticks in a microwave oven, where the interaction in the radiation is extremely complicated, and used for sterilizing and drying water, cooling the dried moxa sticks and carrying out vacuum packaging.

The fermented moxa is formed by an extrusion die. The moxa sticks are heated in a microwave oven, where the interaction in the radiation is extremely complicated, and is mainly used for sterilizing and drying water. The dried moxa is cooled and vacuum-packed.

PN CN108117922-A; CN108117922-B
TI Extraction of fish oil by high-voltage pulse electric field by cleaning tuna leftovers, crushing, performing enzymolysis by pepsin, ultrasonically treating, introducing carbon dioxide gas and processing by high-voltage pulse electric field.
AU LU Z
   WEI C
   HUANG Y
AE GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   ZHONGGANG FUJIAN SEA FOOD CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 201845489L
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of fish oil by high-voltage pulse electric field comprises cleaning tuna leftovers with clean water, crushing, adding 0.5-2 times water to form slurry, uniformly stirring, adding 2-2.5 wt.% pepsin, performing primary enzymolysis at 30-35 degrees C at pH of 2-3 for 9-10 hours, ultrasonically treating at 800-950 kHz, placing in vessel, introducing carbon dioxide gas at flow rate of 4-5 m/second for 1-2 hours and at flow rate of 1-2 m/second, processing by high-voltage pulse electric field at electric field strength of 55-60 kV/cm, frequency of 100-150 Hz and pulse width of 20-25 mu second for 5-10 seconds, adding 2-3 wt.% neutral protease comprising trypsin, papain and subtilisin, trypsin, papain and subtilisin at weight ratio of 4-5: 1-2: 0.5-1, performing secondary enzymolysis at 40-45 degrees C at pH of 8-9 for 3-4 hours, ultrasonically treating at 650-700 kHz, adding 2-3 wt.% hydrochloric acid to regulate pH to 4-5, adding 65-70 vol.% anhydrous ethanol and standing for 20-23 hours.
   USE - Method for extracting fish oil by high-voltage pulse electric field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves extraction rate of fish oil, and increases content of eicosapentaenoic acid and docosahexaenoic acid in fish oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrochloric acid has concentration of 2 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anhydrous ethanol is 65 vol.% ethanol. Preferred Method: Standing is done for 20 minutes. The method further comprises centrifugally separating liquid at rotating speed of 8000-9000 revolutions/minute for 26-33 minutes, adding 3-4% activated carbon and centrifugally separating the activated carbon. The pulse electric field has strength of 55 kV/cm, frequency of 100 Hz and pulse width of 20 mu second. The carbon dioxide gas has flow rate of 4 m/second for 1 hour, and 1 m/second.

   USE - The pin1 inhibitor ATRA biodegradable                controlled release composition is useful in                preparing medicine for treating Pin1-related                diseases including cancer, inflammation, arthritis,                lupus erythematosus and asthma (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high entrapment rate and                high yield composition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Pin1 inhibitor ATRA biodegradable                controlled release composition, comprises: (i)                adding ATRA and encapsulating material into                dichloromethane organic solvent, then stirring to                obtain uniform oil phase; where the encapsulating                material is insoluble or slightly soluble in                supercritical carbon dioxide, and co-precipitate to                form micro or nano-particle with ATRA; and (ii)                pumping the oil phase into a reactor, reacting                under supercritical gas condition to obtain                paclitaxel loaded poly(L-lactic acid) (PLLA)                microspheres loaded with ATRA, drying the                microspheres with supercritical gas for 30 minutes,                removing dichloromethane sufficiently. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for pin1                inhibitor ATRA biodegradable controlled release                composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The encapsulating                  material comprises PLLA and polylactic acid-                  polyethylene glycol block copolymer. The                  mass-volume concentration of PLLA in oil phase is                  0.5-5%. The molecular weight of PLLA is 10-100                  kDa. The mass ratio of ATRA to PLLA is 1-5%. The                  supercritical gas includes carbon dioxide gas.                  Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  supercritical gas has supercritical carbon                  dioxide pressure of 7.4-15 MPa, reactor                  temperature of 35-55 degrees C, carbon dioxide                  flow rate of 20-40 g/minute, and oil phase is                  pumped into the reactor at a rate of 0.5-4                  ml/minute.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Antiinflammatory; Antiarthritic;                Dermatological; Immunosuppressive;                Antiasthmatic.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for quickly starting a nitrosation                process of ammonia-containing wastewater involves                adding the mud-water mixture from the secondary                sedimentation tank of the sewage treatment plant                into the reactor, and then introducing carbon                dioxide gas for smoldering treatment until the                bacteria micelles are broken. The oxygen aeration                treatment is performed on the mixture, and added a                carrier and a carbon source to the system during                the treatment. The chemical oxygen demand (COD)                concentration is introduced in the system is                reduced to below 50 mg/L, preferably 30-50 mg/L,                stop oxygen aeration, and then carried out                sedimentation and drainage. The ammonia-containing                wastewater is introduced into the system to start                the nitrosation process, and adopt the operation                method of changing the drainage in batches and                gradually increased the concentration of ammonia                nitrogen in the system to carry out enrichment                culture of nitrous bacteria.
   USE - Method for quickly starting a nitrosation                process of ammonia-containing wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize the ammonia nitrogen                and total nitrogen at the same time and high                remove, and can ensure the long-term stable                operation of the reactor. GA 202256666E
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing a semiconductor device, comprises forming a silicon oxide film on a silicon carbide layer (10), performing first heat treatment at greater than or equal to1200degrees Celsius under atmosphere of first atmosphere containing ammonia gas, second atmosphere containing nitrogen gas and hydrogen gas, and third atmosphere containing nitrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas after the forming the silicon oxide film, and performing second heat treatment at greater than or equal to750degrees Celsius and less than or equal to1050degrees Celsius under atmosphere containing nitrogen oxide gas after the performing the first heat treatment. The silicon oxide film is formed by thermal oxidation, in an atmosphere including oxygen gas and carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing a semiconductor device for use in a vehicle and a lift.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing a semiconductor device with low loss and high-temperature operation by using characteristics of silicon carbide with superior physical properties. The method enables improving carrier mobility or threshold voltage. The method enables preventing threshold voltage of a metal oxide semiconductor FET from being changed by carbon defects or nitrogen defects existing in a gate insulating layer.

PN CN114146700-A
TI Preparing noble metal catalyst loaded with 3D            printing gas gel involves mixing the inorganic silicon            source, ultraviolet curing resin, whisker and metal            nano material, stirring to obtain the pre-mixed            liquid.
AU ZANG L
AE FUJIAN LANYAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202240035N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing noble metal catalyst loaded with 3D                printing gas gel involves mixing the inorganic                silicon source, ultraviolet curing resin, whisker                and metal nano material, stirring to obtain the                pre-mixed liquid, firstly the pre-mixed liquid is                added into three-dimensional photo-curing moulding                of the resin tank of the printer, the STL file of                the needed 3D structure is loaded into the slicing                software, filling the printing module with carbon                dioxide, printing according to the set model, aging                the printed 3D structure hydrogel, and then                performing solvent replacement for the aged                hydrogel, performing supercritical carbon dioxide                drying on the displaced gel finally roasting the                product after supercritical drying, finally                obtaining the silicon dioxide gas gel supported                noble metal catalyst of the 3D printing.
   USE - Method for preparing noble metal catalyst                loaded with 3D printing gas gel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare noble metal                catalyst loaded with 3D printing gas gel in a                simple manner, with high catalytic activity, it                does not need mould, it is integrally formed, it                can be applied to multiple scenes, it has important                meaning.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                preparation of method of noble metal catalyst                loaded with 3 D printing gas gel wherein it                comprises the following steps. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of                  the inorganic silicon source is 0.1 to 0.8M. The                  content of the inorganic silicon source is not                  higher than 20 %, the content of the curing resin                  is 70-80%, the content of the crystal whisker is                  not higher than 20 %, the content of the metal                  nano material is not higher than 10 %. The                  pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.8 to 4MPa.                  The temperature of the solvent replacement is                  10-60&#8451;, the mass ratio of the solvent                  and the gel is 1: 1-20: 1, the replacement time                  is 1-5 times. Preferred Composition: The ratio of LLDPE to LDPE is selected to be 20:80-10:90.
