PN CN111453754-A
TI Preparation of high-purity and high-activity nano calcium carbonate involves heating, melamine in deionized water, adding stearate, mixing with lime slurry, adding carbon dioxide, carbonizing, adding starch, heating, filtering and drying.
AU WEI M
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202072732U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-purity and high-activity nano calcium carbonate comprises mixing quicklime and water at mass ratio of 1:(3-5), adding 10-15 wt.% diethylenetriaminepentaacetic acid, stirring for 15-30 minutes, passing lime slurry through a 150-200 mesh sieve, and adjusting solid content of the lime slurry to 10-12%; dissolving melamine in deionized water at ratio of 2-3 g per 100 mL deionized water, heating to 85-95 degrees C, adding folic acid for 30-50 minutes under stirring, naturally cooling to 20-25 degrees C, adding stearate, stirring, and mixing to obtain first mixture, where molar ratio of melamine and folic acid is (4-8):1 and mass ratio of stearate and melamine is 1:(2 3); mixing lime slurry and first mixture at ratio of (10-15):1, stirring, adding carbon dioxide, carbonizing for 10-30 minutes, adding starch, stirring, heating to 50-60 degrees C, and stirring for 2-5 hours, where mass ratio of lime slurry to starch is 20:(2-5); and filtering and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing high-purity and high-activity nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is convenient to improve and control the crystal form of the nano calcium carbonate product and obtains nano calcium carbonate product with a large specific surface area and porous appearance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stearate is sodium stearate, zinc stearate, or calcium stearate.

TI Switchboard for use in electric room, has central control unit operating rotating unit when internal temperature exceeds predetermined temperature, and automatic fire extinguishing device rupturing fire to cause carbon dioxide or argon gas.
AU IM P C

GA 2020235853
AB    NOVELTY - The switchboard has a temperature sensing module installed inside the switchboard to measure internal temperature of the switchboard in real time. A central control unit rotates a screw rod (15) in different directions by operating a rotating unit (19) if internal temperature of the switchboard measured through the temperature sensing module exceeds a predetermined temperature. The temperature sensing module measures internal temperature of the switchboard and transmits the internal temperature to a central control unit. The central control unit operates the rotating unit when the internal temperature exceeds a predetermined temperature to, which the screw rod is rotated in single direction. An automatic fire extinguishing device ruptures a fire to cause carbon dioxide gas or argon gas to extinguish an internal fire.
   USE - Switchboard for use in an electric room. Uses include but are not limited to a power plant, a substation, a factory and a building.

PT P
PN WO2020028253-A1; US2021292637-A1
TI Composition used to enhance oil recovery from            oil-bearing subterranean formation, comprises chelating            agent, biosurfactant, carbonate salt and water.
AU FARMER S
   ALIBEK K
   LEFKOWITZ A R
   KARATHUR K N
   SHUMWAY M R
   MOLDAKOZHAYEV A
AE LOCUS OIL IP CO LLC (LOCU-Non-standard)
   LOCUS OIL IP CO LLC (LOCU-Non-standard)
GA 202011025D
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises a chelating agent, a                biosurfactant, a carbonate salt, and water.
   USE - The composition is useful for enhancing oil                recovery from an oil-bearing subterranean formation                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: is environmentally-friendly;                enhances oil recovery from low permeability                subterranean formation using repressurization,                reduction of interfacial tension and increased                permeability; and allows controlled release of                carbon dioxide within the formation without                complicated machinery or gas-producing                microorganisms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing composition for enhanced oil                recovery, comprising mixing a solution comprising a                chelating agent, a biosurfactant, a carbonate salt,                and water, where the mixing is performed under                basic conditions;
   (2) the composition prepared by (1) dissolving                the chelating agent in the water to form a                solution, (2) adding the biosurfactant to the                solution, (3) adding the carbonate salt to the                solution, (4) mixing the solution for 40 minutes                and (5) letting the solution rest for 20 minutes,                where after step (1) but before step (3), an                appropriate amount of a pH adjuster is added to the                solution to reach a pH of at least 9.5 and until                the chelating agent is completely dissolved;                and
   (3) enhancing oil recovery from an oil-bearing                subterranean formation, comprising injecting the                composition and injecting an acid solution into the                formation, where the acid solution lowers the pH of                the composition and causes the controlled release                of carbon dioxide gas inside the formation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises a pH adjuster. The composition                  comprises 5-50 g/l ethylenediaminetetraacetic                  acid (EDTA), 1-30 g/l sophorolipid and 20-200 g/l                  sodium bicarbonate. The composition is prepared                  by: (1a) dissolving 20 g/l of the chelating agent                  in the water to form a solution; (2a) adding 10                  g/l of the biosurfactant to the solution; (3a)                  adding 100 g/l of the carbonate salt todhe                  solution; (4a) mixing the solution for 40                  minutes; and (5a) letting the solution rest for                  20 minutes, where sometime after step (1a) but                  before step (3a), an appropriate amount of a pH                  adjuster is added to the solution to reach a pH                  of at least 9.5 and until the chelating agent is                  completely dissolved, and the chelating agent is                  EDTA, the biosurfactant is acidic form                  sophorolipid, the carbonate salt is sodium                  bicarbonate, and the pH adjuster is sodium                  hydroxide. Preferred Components: The pH adjuster                  is sodium hydroxide. The chelating agent is EDTA.                  The biosurfactant is a sophorolipid. The                  sophorolipid is an acidic form sophorolipid. The                  carbonate salt is sodium bicarbonate. The acid                  solution comprises 30% citric acid. Preferred                  Process: The process comprises: adding EDTA as                  the chelating agent to the water to form a                  solution; adding an acidic sophorolipid                  composition as the biosurfactant to the solution;                  adding sodium bicarbonate as the carbonate salt                  to the solution; and mixing the solution under                  basic conditions. The mixing the solution under                  basic conditions comprises adding a pH adjusting                  substance to the solution of EDTA and water in an                  amount that allows for complete dissolution of                  the EDTA in the water. The pH adjusting agent is                  sodium hydroxide in an amount that brings the pH                  of the solution to 9, 10, 11 or greater than or                  equal to 12. Preferred Method: The acid solution                  is injected prior to injection and after                  injection of the composition. The quantity of                  acid solution is added so that the pH of the                  composition is reduced to less than or equal to                  7. The method further comprises: injecting water                  or brine to push the composition and acid                  solution deep into the formation; a plugging                  step, where the plugging step comprises injecting                  a plugging composition comprising 1-1.5% sodium                  alginate dissolved in water into the formation;                  and shutting the formation for 3-30 days. The                  water or brine has a pH of less than or equal to                  3, thus obviating the need for the acid solution.                  The plugging composition further comprises at                  least one biopolymer comprising xanthan gum or                  guar gum. The once carbon dioxide release has                  stopped inside the formation, pumping of oil from                  the formation can begin after 1-2 days. The                  formation is a fair or low permeability                  formation. The formation has a permeability of                  less than or equal to 1 OmD.
   EXAMPLE - 20 g/l Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid was                added to water. 2.1 g/l Acidic sophorolipid was                added to the water. Sodium hydroxide was added                until all remaining ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid                was dissolved. 100 g/l Sodium bicarbonate was added                to the water. The solution was mixed for 40                minutes. The solution was left to rest for 20                minutes to form that composition.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); Q49 (Mining (E21))

AU AHLERS J
   JOPP D
   REIL F
   GUTMANN P
   LEHR F
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 202009080K
AB    NOVELTY - Foam particles comprise 85-100 wt.%                copolyamide obtained by polymerizing 15-84 wt.%                lactam, and 16-85 wt.% monomer mixture comprising                45-55 mol% 32-40C dimer acid and 45-55 mol% 4-12C                diamine, and have a bulk density of 300 kg/m3 based                on polyamide.
   USE - Foam particles are used for forming molded                article in automobile industry, wind turbine                industry, construction industry, packaging                industry, transport and construction applications,                and sport article. Uses include but are not limited                to tennis racket, core material for skis and                snowboard, water sport equipment, golf club, and                bicycle frame.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam particles have excellent solvent                resistance and low density, and enable formation of                molded article having excellent chemical                resistance, high-temperature resistance, and                stress-crack resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of foam particles, which involves                providing a polymer or a polymer mixture comprising                copolyamide, impregnating the polymer or the                polymer mixture with carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen                as a blowing agent, and foaming the reaction                mixture, or providing a polymer or a polymer                mixture comprising copolyamide, melting the polymer                or the polymer mixture with 0-1 wt.% nucleating                agent, impregnating with 1-3.5 wt.% carbon dioxide,                extruding the reaction mixture using a pelletizing                machine at 5-90 degrees C and 0.1-20 bar, and                discharging the expanded foam particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lactam is chosen                  from 3-aminopropanoic acid lactam,                  4-aminobutanoic acid lactam, and 5-aminopentanoic                  acid lactam. The diamine is chosen from                  tetramethylenediamine, pentamethylenediamine,                  hexamethylenediamine, decamethylenediamine, and                  dodecamethylenediamine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The foam particles                  further comprise additive. The foam particles                  comprise 85-100 wt.% copolyamide and 0-15 wt.%                  additive. The copolyamide comprises 0.05-1 wt.%                  water measured based on DIN EN ISO 15512:2017-03.                  Preferred Condition: The impregnation is                  performed at 18-25 MPa and 180-250 degrees C for                  1-5 hours. Preferred Properties: The copolyamide                  has a glass transition temperature of 20-50                  degrees C based on ISO 113572:2014, and a melting                  point based on ISO 11357-2:2014 of 150-210                  degrees C.

TI Preparing 2-fluoro-3-hydroxypyridine-4-carboxylic acid involves dissolving 2-fluoropyridin-3-ol and N, N-diisopropylethylamine in dichloromethane, cooling, and reacting with 2-(trimethylsilyl) ethoxymethyl chloride.
AU SHI J
   LIU C
   XU Y
   DAI H
   JIANG Y
AE ALI BIOLOGICAL NEW MATERIALS CHANGZHOU (ALIB-Non-standard)
GA 202006185X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2-fluoro-3-hydroxypyridine-4-carboxylic acid represented by (I) involves dissolving 2-fluoropyridin-3-ol and N,N-diisopropylethylamine in dichloromethane, cooling, and reacting with 2-(trimethylsilyl) ethoxymethyl chloride to obtain 2-((2-fluoro-3-pyridyl)oxymethoxy)ethyl-trimethyl-silane. The diisopropylamine is dissolved in tetrahydrofuran and cooled under the protection of nitrogen. After adding n-butyl lithium to stirred, cooedl again, added 2-((2-fluoro-3-pyridyl)oxymethoxy)ethyl-trimethyl-silane, and keep stirred at the cooling temperature, and reacted with carbon dioxide and heats up to give 2-fluoro-3-hydroxypyridine-4-carboxylic acid represented by (I).
   USE - Method for preparing 2-fluoro-3-hydroxypyridine-4-carboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare 2-fluoro-3-hydroxypyridine-4-carboxylic acid, that has short reaction steps, simple synthesis operation and easy implementation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 2-fluoro-3-hydroxypyridine-4-carboxylic acid represented by formula (I) involves dissolving 2-fluoropyridin-3-ol and N N-diisopropylethylamine in dichloromethane, cooled, and reacting with 2-(trimethylsilyl) ethoxymethyl chloride to obtain 2-((2-fluoro-3-pyridyl)oxymethoxy)ethyl-trimethyl-silane. The diisopropylamine is dissolved in tetrahydrofuran and cooled under the protection of nitrogen. After adding n-butyl lithium to stirred, cooedl again, added 2-((2-fluoro-3-pyridyl)oxymethoxy)ethyl-trimethyl-silane, and keep stirred at the cooling temperature, and reacted with carbon dioxide and heats up to give 2-fluoro-3-hydroxypyridine-4-carboxylic acid represented by formula (I)..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is 25 degrees C, and the reaction time is 5-40 hours. The molar ratio of the 2-fluoropyridin-3-ol, N, N-diisopropylethylamine and 2-(trimethylsilyl) ethoxymethyl chloride is 1:1.5:1.2. The cooling temperature in step (2) is -78 degrees C, and it reacts with carbon dioxide, the temperature is raised to 10-35 degrees C, and the reaction time is 5-40 hours. The concentration of n-butyllithium is 2.5 mol/L, and the molar ratio of 2-((2-fluoro-3-pyridyl)oxymethoxy)ethyl-trimethyl-silane, diisopropylamine, and n-butyllithium is 1:1.2:1.1.

   W' = ligand;
   L'j = arbitrary ligand;
   n = 2 or more;
   m = 1 to (n-1);
   i = 1-n;
   j = 1-m;and
   k = electric charge of complex compound catalyst, and is positive integer, 0 or negative integer.

PT P
PN CN110357224-A; CN110357224-B
TI Preparation of free radical electrode in treatment of organic pollutants, by roughening spherical hollow titanium matrix, followed by chemically plating to form tin antimony oxide bottom layer on surface of matrix and then alkaline plating.
AU QU G
   LI H
   LI J
   LI Z
   YANG C
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2019888451
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a free radical electrode involves roughening a spherical hollow titanium matrix, carrying out chemical plating on the roughened titanium matrix to form a tin antimony oxide bottom layer on the surface of the titanium substrate, then placing the chemically plated titanium substrate in an alkaline plating solution for alkaline plating to form an alpha -lead(IV) oxide layer on the titanium substrate, then performing an acidic composite plating to form a rare earth metal and a beta -lead(IV) oxide active layer on the alpha -lead(IV) oxide layer, and forming through holes with a diameter of 10-100 mu m on the surface of the spherical hollow titanium substrate by utilizing a laser drilling device with carbon dioxide as a laser source.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a free radical electrode, which is useful in the treatment of organic pollutants (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables rapid preparation of the free radical electrode with high concentration and less energy consumption. The electrode has large specific surface area, high porosity, and fast radical generation rate; and can quickly and effectively degrade pollutants into small molecules and harmless carbon dioxide and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the free radical electrode prepared by the above preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Properties: The spherical hollow free radical electrode has a diameter of 50-100 mm and a porosity of 30-50%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The roughening treatment involves immersing the spherical hollow titanium matrix in 10 %mass sodium hydroxide solution, heating in a water bath to 60-80 degrees C for 2 hours, taking out the spherical hollow titanium matrix, again immersing in 10 %mass oxalic acid solution, treating at 70-85 degrees C for 2 hours, removing the matrix and rinsing with double distilled water until the pH is neutral. The chemical plating step involves uniformly coating a tin antimony oxide sol on the surface of the roughened titanium matrix, then placing in a muffle furnace, drying at 130 degrees C for 20 minutes, then heating at 450-520 degrees C for 15 minutes, cooling to complete the treatment, repeating the above process for 9 times, again coating tin antimony oxide sol, followed by thermally decomposing at 450-520 degrees C for 60 minutes, and cooling, where the tin antimony oxide sol is comprises 5-10 g antimony trichloride, 90-110 g tin(IV) chloride pentahydrate, 251 ml ethylene glycol solution, and 200 g citric acid. The alkaline plating involves taking the titanium matrix with tin antimony oxide bottom layer as an anode and a titanium sheet as a cathode, plating the alpha -lead(IV) oxide layer in the alkaline plating solution, and carrying out alkaline plating at 50-60 degrees C and a current density of 0.3-0.5 A/dm-2 for 50-70 minutes, where the alkaline plating solution is prepared by adding lead(II) oxide to 4-5 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, where the concentration of lead(II) oxide in the alkaline plating solution is 0.1-0.2 mol/l. An acidic plating solution containing a rare earth metal nitrate is prepared by adding a rare earth metal nitrate to an acidic plating solution. Preferred Components: The acid plating solution contains 0.3 mol/l lead(II) nitrate, 0.025-0.04 mol/l potassium fluoride, and 6-7 mol/l aqueous solution of PTFE. The molar ratio of lead to rare earth metal elements in the plating solution is 100:1-200:1. The rare earth metal nitrate is a nitrate of lanthanum, yttrium, cerium, praseodymium, neodymium, samarium or europium.
DC M11 (Electroplating; electrolytic treatment of or with metals - including electro-deposition of metals, electro-plating apparatus, electro-forming, electro-erosion, spark erosion, anodising (electrophoretically) coating metals and electrolytic cleaning and polishing (C25).); M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

TI Preparing diindium trioxide/ceric oxide/hydrochloric acid-treated attapulgite composite photo-catalyst comprises e.g. dissolving cerous nitrate hexahydrate and hydrochloric acid-treated attapulgite in water, and adding sodium hydroxide.

   WANG H
   LI J
   LIU C
   HUO P
   YAN Y
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 201935076U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing diindium trioxide/ceric oxide/hydrochloric acid-treated attapulgite (HATP) composite photo-catalyst comprises (i) preparing HATP using attapulgite, (ii) dissolving cerous nitrate hexahydrate and HATP in deionized water, stirring and dissolving cerous nitrate, adding sodium hydroxide and continuing to stir, placing suspension into a reaction kettle, performing hydro-thermal treatment, taking out, naturally cooling to room temperature, using de-ionized water and ethanol to wash to be neutral, and drying to obtain ceric oxide/HATP composite photocatalyst, and (iii) weighing ceric oxide/HATP composite photocatalyst, taking indium nitrate trihydrate, adding de-ionized water and stirring, adding sodium hydroxide and continuing to stir to dissolve indium nitrate trihydrate completely, placing suspension into a reaction kettle, performing hydro-thermal treatment, taking out and naturally cooling to room temperature, using de-ionized water and ethanol to wash to be neutral and drying.
   USE - The composite photo-catalyst is useful in reducing carbon dioxide under ultraviolet light (claimed), and converting carbon dioxide to methane and carbon monoxide under ultraviolet light.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, does not cause resource waste and formation of secondary pollution, and relieves energy crisis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for diindium trioxide/ceric oxide/hydrochloric acid-treated attapulgite (HATP) composite photo-catalyst, where the morphology is ceric oxide nanocube and the random spherical diindium trioxide were distributed on the rod-shaped HATP.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), mass ratio of cerous nitrate hexahydrate, HATP and sodium hydroxide is 1:(0.01981-0.7927):7.9271. In the step (iii), indium nitrate trihydrate, sodium hydroxide and ceric oxide /HATP composite photo-catalyst mass ratio is (0.1375-1.3750):1:(5-50). Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the hydro-thermal treatment temperature is 160 degrees C; the hydrothermal treatment time is 10-48 hours. In the step (iii) the hydro-thermal treatment temperature is 120-200 degrees C; the hydrothermal treatment time is 6-20 hours. In step (ii) and (iii), the drying temperature is 70 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing succulent granule involves using Ampelopsis grossedentata powder, Chrysanthemum powder and ginger powder as raw materials, and mixing the three raw materials in supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain extract. The binder is mixed with the extract, dried, extracted for repeated 3 times, at 20-30 megaPacals and 40-50 degrees C, and again extracted at 12-20 megaPascals, and 35-45 degrees C, extracted at 4 to 12 megaPascals, and 25 to 35 degrees C.
   USE - Preparing succulent granule for preparing brown sugar ginger tea (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare succulent granule in which product has good taste, rich mouth feel, odor and a comprehensive application effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a brown sugar ginger tea further comprises 60 to 70 pts. wt. brown sugar and 1 to 5 pts. wt. xylooligo powder; and
   (2) a method for preparing brown sugar ginger tea, which involves pulverizing brown sugar and xylooligosaccharide powder separately, and then sieving, then sieving the brown sugar and low The xylose powder is added to the extract obtained by extracting ginger powder, chrysanthemum powder and rattan tea powder by supercritical carbon dioxide, stirring in the reverse direction, spraying the binder to obtain a mixture; the obtained mixture is dried and sieved to obtain granules and sterilizing obtained granules by high temperature, and qualifying water content to obtain brown sugar ginger tea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Condition: The three extractions have a carbon dioxide flow rate of 20 to 40 L/hour for each extraction time of 30 to 60 minutes. The Ampelopsis grossedentata is produced from Xiangxi, the Chrysanthemum powder is Chrysanthemum powder, and the ginger powder is small turmeric produced near 27 degrees north latitude. Preferred Composition: 0.1-3 pts. wt. Ampelopsis grossedentata, preferably 0.5-2 pts. wt., 2-10 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum powder, preferably 3-7 pts. wt. and 2-10 pts. wt. ginger powder, preferably 4-8 pts. wt. are mixed. Preferred Process: The sucrose granule is prepared by weighing ginger powder, chrysanthemum powder, and rattan tea powder, respectively pulverizing and sieving, extracting by mixing ginger powder, Chrysanthemum powder and rattan tea powder into a supercritical carbon dioxide extractor, adding an entraining agent, setting an extraction pressure and an extraction temperature, and extracting to obtain an extract. The extract obtained is added to the mixer, and the mixture is sprayed in the reverse direction, and the binder is sprayed to obtain a mixture. The obtained mixture is dried and sieved to obtain the succulent granules. Preferred Components: The entraining agent is one of a mixture of water, ethanol, ethanol and water, and the binder is water.

PN CN109181079-A; CN109181079-B
TI Preparation of calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide composite material for polyethylene plastic, involves mixing calcium oxide with water, digesting, regulating concentration, heating, carbonizing, adding titanium dioxide, drying and grinding.
AU WANG P
   XU H
   ZHAO Z
   XU K
   ZHANG M
   HAN X
   DONG L
AE HEBEI CHEM & PHARM VOCATIONAL & TECH COL (HEBE-Non-standard)
   HEBEI LIXIN CHEM CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 201909323P
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide composite material involves (a) mixing calcium oxide with water for digestion reaction, regulating the concentration after finishing reaction to obtain calcium hydroxide emulsion with concentration of 10-12 %mass, (b) adding calcium hydroxide emulsion to a reactor, heating up to 34-36 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide gas for carbonization reaction under the condition of stirring, adding titanium dioxide to the reactor when the pH value of the reaction system is 6.8-7.2, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas, finishing the reaction when the pH value of the reaction system is 6.5-6.6, and aging to obtain calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide slurry, and (c) filtering the calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide slurry, washing, drying and grinding.
   USE - Preparation method of calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide composite material used for polyethylene plastic (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide composite material with improved tensile strength by more than 22% and sterilizing rate by 90.2% when applied to plastics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation method of modified calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide composite material, which involves, spraying aluminate to calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide composite material at 70-100 degrees C and under the rotating speed of 600-800 rpm, modifying, and grinding; and
   (2) use of calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide composite material in polyethylene plastic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (b), the titanium dioxide is added in an amount of 28-55 %mass of the calcium oxide dry base. In step (b), the amount of carbon dioxide gas introduced is 16-20 L/h.L calcium hydroxide emulsion. The modifier is added in an amount of 0.5-2 %mass based on 100 %mass of the calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide composite material. Preferred Process: The calcium hydroxide emulsion is prepared by digesting the calcium oxide with water at 55-65 degrees C, aging at 72-85 degrees C for 22-26 hours after the reaction is finished to obtain digested slurry, and adjusting the concentration of the digested slurry. Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the stirring speed is 300-500 rpm, and the aging time is 15-25 minutes. In the step (c), the drying temperature is 110-130 degrees C and the time is 3-4 hours. The aluminate is heated into liquid, and is atomized by a sprayer to add the aluminate to the calcium carbonate/titanium dioxide composite material by spraying, and the flow rate of aluminate atomization is 1-2 cc/minute. The modification time is 10-40 minutes.

AU XU M
   YE Y
   HE X
AE GUANGZHOU MARITIME INST (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A5422L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using unmanned ship and diesel engine to generate high temperature tail gas after being processed into vehicle cabin portion. The high temperature tail gas is purified by contaminant purifier using metal honeycomb carrier coated with noble metal catalyst. The pollutant is chemically reacted and oxygen under effect of catalyst and high temperature into nontoxic water and carbon dioxide. The portion of tail gas treated by pollutant purifier is discharged to atmosphere through first exhaust valve and the other portion of tail gas is passed through second exhaust valve to enter condensing heat exchanger. A third automatic valve is cooled after ten minutes and discharge gas of upper portion of second automatic valve and bottom exhaust carbon dioxide is opened and the heat in the cabin is taken, the cabin is cooled and the stability of device cabin is ensured.
   USE - Fireproofing and temperature reducing method of small unmanned aerial vehicle cabin portion.
   ADVANTAGE - The fireproofing and temperature reducing method of small unmanned aerial vehicle cabin portion effectively prevents cabin equipment hazard caused by contact with oxygen by simply exhaust valve control, changes inert gas flow rate in cabin and reduces cabin temperature.

TI Manufacture of copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate-assembled nano-metal catalyst used in manufacture of N-carboxamide compound, involves ultrasonically treating copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate dispersion and metal precursor solution.
AU JI S
   LIU J
   MU J
   SUN R
   XU Y
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201862972X
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate-assembled nano-metal catalyst involves dissolving copper nitrate or copper acetate in deionized water to obtain copper nitrate/copper acetate solution, dissolving trimesic acid in N,N-dimethylformamide-ethanol mixture to form a trimesic acid solution, adding the copper-based solution to the trimesic acid solution in ratio of 3:2, stirring, ultrasonically treating at 100-150 W for 30-50 minutes, centrifuging, washing with deionized water and ethanol, vacuum drying to obtain copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate, dispersing copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate in ethanol to form copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate dispersion (A), mixing cesium chloride, chloroauric acid and palladium chloride in ethanol-water mixture to form metal precursor solution (B), mixing the solution (A) and the solution (B), stirring, ultrasonically treating at 50-100 W for 50-60 minutes, filtering, washing with deionized water and ethanol, and vacuum drying.
   USE - Manufacture of copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate-assembled nano-metal catalyst used in catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide with amine/hydrogen into N-carboxamide compound (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient manufacture of copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate-assembled nano-metal catalyst with excellent catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for usage of the catalyst in catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide with amine/hydrogen into N-carboxamide compound at 100-160 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the copper nitrate or copper acetate solution is 8-15 %mass. The concentration of the metal precursor solution is 1-2 %mass. The catalyst contains 3.13-5.87 %mass gold and 3.61-5.17 %mass palladium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol is 1:1. The concentration of the trimesic acid solution is 8-15 %mass. The concentration of the copper benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylate dispersion is 20-25 %mass.

TI Rice local estimation method for estimating place of production of rice based on isotope ratio analysis, involves estimating place of production of rice based on obtained stable-isotope ratio measurement result.
AU YASUI T
   YOSHIMURA R
   SAKAI Y
   SAKAI A
   SUZUKI K
AE NIPPON TELEGRAPH & TELEPHONE CORP (NITE-C)
   NTT TECHNOCROSS CORP (NITE-C)
GA 201852155X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pulverizing three or more rice grains selected from the rice, and uniformly mixing to produce a powder sample (S). The stable isotope ratio of the rice is measured based on a gas obtained by burning the powder sample. The place of production of the rice is estimated based on the obtained stable-isotope ratio measurement result. The carbon dioxide and water vapor as gas is generated by burning powder sample. A stable isotopic ratio of hydrogen contained in the sample is measured by laser irradiation with laser beam on generated carbon dioxide and water vapor. The quantitatively analyzing elemental weight of trace elements comprised of silicon, calcium, zinc, molybdenum, and barium contained in rice.
   USE - Rice local estimation method for estimating place of production of rice based on an isotope ratio analysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The variations in stable isotopic ratios made from rice grains itself are suppressed. The rice production place is estimated with higher accuracy. The production centers for the top-grade rice are determined easily and scientifically with high precision.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a rice local estimation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the rice local estimation system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Powder device (10)
   Laser light source (22)
   Gas cell (23)
   Powder sample (S)

PN WO2018074675-A1; KR2018042056-A; KR1855297-B1
TI Zinc-containing organic metal framework compound used as catalyst for manufacturing five-membered cyclic carbonate compound, is obtained using zinc fluoride tetrahydrate and titanium isopropoxide, and 1,2,4-triazole as organic compound.
AU PARK D
   CHANG J
   DAE W P
   JONG S C
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
   UNIV PUSAN NAT IND COOP FOUND (UYPU-C)
GA 2018332274
AB    NOVELTY - A zinc-containing organic metal framework compound is obtained using zinc fluoride tetrahydrate and titanium isopropoxide as a metal source forming skeleton, and 1,2,4-triazole as an organic compound. The zinc-containing organic metal framework compound is a porous coordination compound.
   USE - Zinc-containing organic metal framework compound is used as catalyst for manufacturing five-membered cyclic carbonate compound (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The zinc-containing organic metal framework compound as catalyst enables easy manufacture of five-membered cyclic carbonate compound with high yield under relatively low-pressure and low-temperature conditions. Since the zinc-containing organic metal framework compound is a safe porous catalyst having stable structure and large surface area, zinc atoms and titanium atoms can easily interact with oxygen atoms of epoxy compound as Lewis acid, and nitrogen atoms of triazole facilitates adsorption of carbon dioxide, and the catalyst has excellent reactivity, when used in manufacture of five-membered cyclic carbonate compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of zinc-containing organic metal framework compound, which involves performing hydrothermal reaction by mixing zinc fluoride tetrahydrate and titanium isopropoxide dissolved in distilled water, with 1,2,4-triazole; and
   (2) manufacture of five-membered cyclic carbonate compound, which involves performing carbonylation reaction of carbon dioxide and an epoxy compound using the zinc-containing organic metal framework compound as a catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The zinc-containing organic metal framework compound is porous coordination compound having a three-dimensional network structure. The zinc-containing organic metal framework compound comprises triazole group-containing structural units of formula: (Zn4(TiO6)x(TiF6)y(1,2,4-triazole)z). When the addition amount (mol) of the zinc-containing organic metal framework compound used in manufacture of five-membered cyclic carbonate compound is 100, the molar ratio of epoxy compound with respect to the metal source forming skeleton is 0.5-5. The epoxy compound is chosen from allyl glycidyl ether, butyl glycidyl ether, phenyl glycidyl ether, propylene oxide and vinylcyclohexene oxide.x = 0.4-1.2;y = 0.1-0.3;andz = 30-38.Preferred Process: The carbonylation reaction is performed at 80-150 degrees C and 0.8-1.6 MPa for 4-20 hours.

TI Preparation of aluminum alloy for stove holder, involves melting manganese-aluminum alloy and aluminum-titanium-silicon alloy, adding magnesium block, stirring, adding refining agent wrapped with foil, refining, removing slag, and casting.
AU LI Y
AE QINGDAO TIANDI CASTING CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2018214474
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aluminum alloy involves adding predetermine amount of manganese-aluminum alloy and aluminum-titanium-silicon alloy to a furnace, heating, adding aluminum-titanium-silicon alloy and aluminum-copper alloy, melting, cooling, adding magnesium block to resultant aluminum alloy fluid, stirring, wrapping refining agent with foil, pressing into aluminum alloy fluid, refining, adding magnesium in protective atmosphere comprising carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride in a volume ratio of 18-33, removing slag, and casting.
   USE - Preparation of aluminum alloy used for stove holder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical preparation of aluminum alloy having excellent mechanical properties, molding properties, uniform structure and component stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of aluminum alloy involves adding predetermine amount of manganese-aluminum alloy and aluminum-titanium-silicon alloy to a furnace, heating, adjusting furnace temperature to 780-1080 degrees C, melting, adding remaining aluminum-titanium-silicon alloy and aluminum-copper alloy, melting into aluminum alloy fluid, cooling to 720-800 degrees C, adding magnesium block into resultant aluminum alloy fluid, stirring, wrapping refining agent with an aluminum foil, pressing the into resultant aluminum solution with a bell jar, refining for 6-12 minutes, adding magnesium in protective atmosphere comprising carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride in a volume ratio of 18-33, maintaining the temperature of the resultant solution at 820-990 degrees C for 25-35 minutes, removing slag, and casting.

The difluorinating reagent is ethyl bromodifluoroacetate. Preferred Method: When carrying out the reaction, the reaction is carried out in atmosphere of carbon dioxide at atmospheric pressure; and/or when the blue light emitting diode is irradiated, blue light emitting diode of 30 W is used; and/or the reaction is carried out at room temperature; and/or the reaction time is 10 hours.

TI Plant growing system for producing plants such as            vegetables, has first air conditioning unit and second            air conditioning unit which discharge heat and moisture            into closed space of external environment management            room from air in internal space of first and second            plant growing facilities.
AU YIJUN X
   KAZUYA F
   YUTA T
AE NITTO DENKO CORP (NITL-C)
GA 2022982133
AB    NOVELTY - The system (20) has a first plant growing                facility (22) and a second plant growing facility                (23) that are provided with a cultivation unit (31)                for cultivating plants, and are in a closed space                together with store. The external environment                management room (21) is provided with the upper                environment control unit (26,27). The first air                conditioning unit (32-1) discharges the heat and                the moisture into the closed space of the external                environment management room from the air in the                internal space of the first plant growing facility.                The second air conditioning unit (32-2) discharges                the heat and the water content into the closed                space of the external environment management room                from the air in the internal space of the second                plant growing facility.
   USE - Plant growing system for producing plants such                as vegetables by artificially controlling internal                environments such as light, temperature, humidity,                and carbon dioxide (CO2) concentrations in                half-closed cultivation room or semi-closed                cultivation room in plant factory.
   ADVANTAGE - The environment necessary for efficiently                air-conditioning each of the inner space of the                first plant growing facility and the internal space                of the second plant growing facility can be                collectively controlled in the closed space of the                external environment management room. The                environment of the internal space of the first and                second plant growing facility can be adjusted in                the first and second air conditioning units                respectively such that the excess heat and the                moisture can be discharged. The first and second                plant-growing facilities has no need to install                air-conditioners that require large energy                consumption such that the recovery and reuse of                water and heat discharged from plants as a result                of cultivation can be facilitated. The system for                growing plants with little energy consumption can                be realized. The cultivation unit is not directly                brought into contact with the outside air, and is                possible to avoid the generation of condensation                associated with air conditioning in the vicinity of                each cultivation unit, and to prevent the                propagation of bacteria and microorganisms                adversely affecting the cultivation of plants.                Since the ventilation fan of the first and second                air conditioning units is or into the closed space,                the air in the internal space of the first or                second plant growing facility is discharged into                the closed space and the excess heat and moisture                in the internal space can be recovered with low                energy consumption, and the reuse is possible. The                heat exchanger can liquefy the steam contained in                the air flow discharged from the closed space so                that the temperature can be efficiently lowered by                removing the moisture from the air flow. The                temperature in the closed space and the humidity                can be lowered with low energy consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                plant-growing system. (Drawing includes non-English                language text)

   External environment management room                (21)
   First plant growing facility (22)
   Second plant growing facility (23)
   Upper environment control units                (26,27)
   Cultivation unit (31)
   First air conditioning unit (32-1)
   Second air conditioning unit (32-2)

PN CN114751817-A
TI Evaporative crystallization process for calcium            formate production comprises e.g. pressurizing purified            synthetic liquid by pump into preheater, heating by            steam, entering separation chamber, and entering fourth            effect and fifth effect for evaporation and            concentration.
AU DU G
   XU F
   ZHANG M
   ZHANG W
AE YANGCHENG ZHENGDA NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 202294575K
AB    NOVELTY - Evaporative crystallization process for                calcium formate production comprises (1) drawing                the gas from the gas generator to the purification                device, (2) passing the purified gas into the gas                compressor, (3) taking the calcium oxide and                passing the gas liquid into the tubular heater, (4)                feeding the tubular reaction to steam into the                tubular heater to heat up the gas liquid, water and                powdered calcium oxide, (5) removing impurities and                discharging the synthetic liquid into the                purification kettle, (6) preparing five-effect                evaporator, (7) discharging the calcium formate                suspension into the crystallization tank, and (8)                in the packaging process, passing the crystals into                the cooler for cooling, and then transporting to a                cooling cyclone to reduce the crystal temperature                to 40&#176;C and uniform product particles for                packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for evaporative                crystallization process for calcium formate                production.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium formate product produced has                content of more than 99%, high purity and pure                white color.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Evaporative crystallization process for                calcium formate production comprises (1) drawing                the gas from the gas generator to the purification                device, and treating the gas with a purification                device to increase the content of carbon monoxide                and reduce the content of carbon dioxide and other                impurities, (2) passing the purified gas into the                gas compressor, and using the gas compressor to                compress the gas to obtain gas liquid, (3) taking                the calcium oxide and passing the gas liquid into                the tubular heater, at the same time, using the                batching kettle and the metering pump to weigh the                quantitative powdery calcium oxide and input it to                the tubular heater, and feeding with water, (4)                feeding the tubular reaction to steam into the                tubular heater to heat up the gas liquid, water and                powdered calcium oxide, and then discharging into                the pulse synthesis tower for synthesis reaction to                obtain synthesis liquid, (5) removing impurities                and discharging the synthetic liquid into the                purification kettle, heating to 900&#176;C                under 101.325 kPa to remove calcium carbonate in                the synthetic liquid, then after the synthetic                liquid is cooled down, preparing heavy metal                capture microspheres, and removing heavy metals in                the synthetic liquid by precipitation, (6)                preparing five-effect evaporator, pressurizing the                purified synthetic liquid by a pump into the                preheater, heating by the steam in the first                effect, then entering the separation chamber, under                the action of the exhaust fan, providing a vacuum,                and flashing one-tenth of the liquid and                discharging into the atmosphere, then directly                discharging the synthetic liquid into the third                effect for evaporation and concentration, and then                entering the fourth effect and the fifth effect for                evaporation and concentration to obtain a calcium                formate suspension containing crystals, (7)                discharging the calcium formate suspension into the                crystallization tank, crystallizing and                centrifuging the calcium formate suspension,                obtaining crystals, transporting the crystal screw                feeder to the airflow dryer, the airflow contacts                the crystals and evaporates the moisture, and                entering the packaging process, and (8) in the                packaging process, passing the crystals into the                cooler for cooling, and then transporting to a                cooling cyclone to reduce the crystal temperature                to 40&#176;C and uniform product particles for                packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  content of the carbon monoxide in the gas is                  increased to more than 90 vol.%, reaching 90-98                  vol.%, preferably 95-98 vol.%, and the content of                  carbon dioxide is reduced to below 2 vol.%.Preferred Method: In the step (4), the                  heated gas liquid, water and powdered calcium                  oxide are discharged into the pulse synthesis                  tower, and then synthesized and reacted at a                  pressure of 1.3-2 Mpa and a temperature of                  70-90&#176;C. In the step (5), the calcium                  carbonate in the synthesis solution is removed by                  heating, the calcium carbonate is decomposed into                  calcium oxide and carbon dioxide, the calcium                  oxide remains in the synthesis solution and                  continues to participate in the reaction, and the                  carbon dioxide escapes. In the step (5), the                  process of preparing the heavy metal capture                  microspheres is as follows: dissolving sodium                  polyacrylate in distilled water to obtain a                  sodium polyacrylate solution, then adding sodium                  alginate and stirring until completely dissolved                  to obtain a mixed solution, then distilling the                  sodium polyacrylate solution to form a viscous                  liquid, which is added into the mold to be                  slightly heated and solidified into a spherical                  shape to obtain sodium polyacrylate balls, then                  spraying the mixed solution on the surface of the                  sodium polyacrylate balls, drying at a constant                  temperature to form a microsphere structure, then                  injecting the mixture of carbon disulfide,                  dimethylamine and absolute ethanol into the                  microsphere structure to obtain semi-finished                  microspheres, dissolving calcium chloride in                  ethanol water to form a dilute solution, spraying                  the dilute solution on the surface of the                  microspheres, and allowing to stand still to                  obtain the heavy metal capture microspheres. In                  the step (6), in the five-effect evaporator, the                  raw steam enters the first effect, the flow rate                  of the raw steam is 1.5 m3/hour, the secondary                  steam generated by the first effect evaporation                  enters the first effect demister at a speed of 20                  m/second, after removing most of the droplets, it                  enters the second-effect evaporator as a heat                  source, the steam generated by the second-effect                  evaporation enters the third-effect, the steam                  generated by the three-effect evaporation enters                  the four-effect, the steam generated by the                  four-effect evaporation enters the five-effect                  evaporator as a heat source, the second effect                  and the first effect use a natural circulation                  evaporator, the third, fourth, and fifth effect                  use a falling film evaporator, and the steam                  generated by the fifth effect evaporation enters                  the direct spray condenser for condensation. In                  the step (6), the temperature is raised to                  30&#176;C after heated by the steam in the                  first effect, then enters the separation chamber                  to provide a degree of vacuum, the degree of                  vacuum is -0.7 MPa, in the third effect for                  evaporation and concentration, the temperature                  rises to 40&#176;C, then enters the fourth                  effect, the temperature rises to 50&#176;C,                  then enter the fifth effect for evaporation and                  concentration, and the temperature during the                  fifth effect concentration is 65&#176;C. In                  the step (8), when the gas phase medium from the                  cooler and the cooling cyclone is cooled and                  heat-exchanged, when the temperature reaches                  60&#176;C, sending to the five-effect                  evaporator as hot air to recover the cooled                  heat.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Method for managing health of multiple devices in internet-of-things environment based on inter-device correlation for exchanging information with e.g. electronic device, involves detecting anomaly in device unit if determined change in ambience of device unit does not satisfy threshold criteria.
AU P I
   UDESHI A J
   CHIDDARWAR A S
   BISWAS R
   GOEL S
   MOULIK S
   INBARAJAN P
   ANERI J U
AE SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
   SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 202268765S
AB    NOVELTY - The method (900) involves detecting (902) an occurrence of an event on a device unit of a set of devices by an electronic device. A change in an ambience of the device unit corresponding to the occurrence of the event on the device unit is determined (904) by the electronic device, where the ambience of the device unit includes a co-used device that operate simultaneously with the device unit. An anomaly in the device unit is detected (906) by the electronic device if the determined change in the ambience of the device unit does not satisfy threshold criteria determined for the event. A root cause is determined (908) for the anomaly in the device unit by the electronic device.
   USE - Method for managing health of multiple devices in internet-of-things (IoT) environment based on inter-device correlation for exchanging information with an IoT cloud server and an electronic device (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to a smartphone, a mobile phone, a video phone, a computer, a tablet personal computer (PC), a netbook computer, a laptop, a wearable device, a vehicle infotainment system, a workstation, a server, a personal digital assistant (PDA), a portable multimedia player (PMP), a MPEG-1 Audio Layer 3 (MP3) layer, a mobile medical device, a light, a voice assistant device, a camera, a home appliance such as TV, DVD player, audio device, refrigerator, air conditioner, air purifier, chimney, cooktop, vacuum cleaner, microwave oven, washing machine, dryer, set-top box, home automation control panel, security control panel, game console, electronic key, camcorder, electronic picture frame, coffee maker, toaster oven, rice cooker and pressure cooker, and sensors such as temperature sensor, humidity sensor, infrared sensor, gyroscope sensor, atmospheric sensor, proximity sensor, red-green-blue (RGB) sensor, luminance sensor, photo-sensor, thermostat, UV light sensor, dust sensor, fire detection sensor, carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor, smoke sensor or equivalent sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively detecting the anomaly in the device unit based on the determined correlation between the events of the device unit and the change in the ambience. The method enable making a cloud to provide information about the health of the devices in a form of visual representations, so that the representations can be intuitive and full of technical jargons, thus allowing a user/layman to understand the representations corresponding to the device health in an efficient manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for managing health of multiple devices in IoT environment based on inter-device correlation.
   Managing health of multiple devices in IoT environment based on inter-device correlation (900)
   Detecting an occurrence of an event on a device unit of a set of devices by an electronic device (902)
   Determining a change in an ambience of the device unit corresponding to the occurrence of the event on the device unit by the electronic device (904)
   Detecting an anomaly in the device unit by the electronic device if the determined change in the ambience of the device unit does not satisfy threshold criteria determined for the event (906)

PN CN114149879-A
TI Natural antibacterial whitening soap containing plant extract prepared by e.g. preparing mulberry crude extract, soaking crude mulberry extract residue in distilled water, obtaining crude mulberry extract, soaking tribute cabbage in distilled water, and preparing crude extract of sticktight.
AU MAN Z
   MAN Y
   MAN H
AE HUNAN HANQING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2022409913
AB    NOVELTY - Natural antibacterial whitening soap containing plant extract prepared by (i) grinding and crushing dried mulberries, soaking in methanol solvent, extracting mulberries, and separating extract and residue to obtain crude mulberry extract, (ii) soaking residue of crude mulberry extract in distilled water, separating extract, combining with crude mulberry extract, and obtaining crude mulberry extract, (iii) soaking cut tribute cabbage in distilled water, extracting tribute cabbage, separating extract, and obtaining tribute cabbage extract, (iv) soaking sticktight in ethyl acetate solvent, extracting sticktight, and obtaining sticktight crude extract, (v) diluting crude extract of sticktight with distilled water, and obtaining sticktight extract, and (vi) adding coconut oil, olive oil and sodium hydroxide to distilled water, adding anti-crystallization agent rock sugar, adding mulberry crude extract, tribute cabbage extract, sticktight extract and aromatizing agent fragrance, is claimed.
   USE - Used as natural antibacterial whitening soap containing plant extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The soap: has better whitening and antibacterial effects; utilizes natural plant extracts instead of chemically synthesized surfactants; reduces the irritating behavior of soaps to the skin; and utilizes plant extract which contains a large amount of active substances e.g. flavonoids and polyacetylene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Natural antibacterial whitening soap containing plant extract prepared by (i) preparing mulberry crude extract by grinding and crushing dried mulberries, then soaking in methanol solvent for 3-8 hours, placing in ultrasonic device, extracting mulberries under assistance of ultrasonic waves, and separating the extract and residue to obtain crude mulberry extract, (ii) preparing mulberry extract by soaking residue of crude mulberry extract in distilled water for 18-30 hours, then boiling in boiling water for 1-3 hours, extracting under reflux, separating the extract, combining with the crude mulberry extract, and rotating the solvent to evaporate the solvent to obtain the crude mulberry extract, (iii) cutting tribute cabbage, placing in distilled water to soak for 12-24 hours, then transferring mixed solution to the extraction kettle of supercritical extraction device, starting the device, making the carbon dioxide liquefaction and circulate, extracting tribute cabbage, separating extract, and concentrating to obtain tribute cabbage extract, (iv) preparing crude extract of sticktight by cutting sticktight, then soaking in ethyl acetate solvent for 2-6 hours, placing in a microwave extraction device, starting the device, extracting sticktight under microwave, and separating extract, evaporating solvent to obtain sticktight crude extract, (v) using distilled water to dilute crude extract of sticktight, then introducing diluted solution into pretreated macroporous adsorption resin column, firstly using distilled water to wash until colorless, then using ethanol for continuously washing, and rotating to dryness to obtain the sticktight extract, and (vi) adding coconut oil, olive oil and sodium hydroxide to distilled water, heating to 60-80degrees Celsius, uniformly stirring, allowing to stand for 2-4 hours, adding anti-crystallization agent rock sugar, safely dissolving, adding mulberry crude extract, tribute cabbage extract, sticktight extract and aromatizing agent fragrance, uniformly stirring, then adding pH regulator citric acid to adjust the pH to 6.5-7, then introducing into mold for cooling and solidifying, and placing for 12-36 hours to obtain the finished product, is claimed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of mulberry and methanol is 10:180-600. In the step (ii), the mass ratio of residue of crude mulberry extract and distilled water is 10:150-500. In the step (iv), the mass ratio of sticktight and ethyl acetate is 10:80-200. In the step (v), the mass ratio of distilled water and crude extract of sticktight is 15-40:10. In the step (vi), the mass ratio of distilled water, coconut oil, olive oil, sodium hydroxide, rock sugar, mulberry crude extract, tribute cabbage extract, sticktight extract, and fragrance is 100:250-600:150-400:45-55:15-60:40-100:35-80:20-45:12-30.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the ultrasonic frequency is 10-30 KHz, and the extraction time is 4-10 hours. In the step (ii), extracting at 80-100degrees Celsius for 2-6 hours. In the step (iii), extracting at 25-60degrees Celsius for 3-8 hours at the pressure of 10-20 MPa, and the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 12-30 l/hour. In the step (iv), the microwave power is 150-400 W, and the extraction time is 30-80 seconds.

PN CN113974087-A
TI Producing biological, bacteriostatic and fresh-keeping packaged stewed chicken product, comprises placing chicken into pickling soup material and pickling, placing pickled chicken into cooking soup material to boil, stewing, cooling, draining surface floating oil, cutting, vacuumizing and packaging.
AU YANG X
AE TAICANG FEIFENG FOODS CO LTD (TAIC-Non-standard)
GA 202223324P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biological, bacteriostatic and fresh-keeping packaged stewed chicken product, comprises (i) selecting a healthy chicken and processing, (ii) preparing soup material, where the soup material comprises pickling soup material and cooking soup material, the pickling soup material comprises component A and component B, the cooking soup material comprises component C, component D and component E, (iii) adding the component A into the component B and mixing to obtain pickling soup material, placing 100 kg chicken into the pickling soup material, soaking the chicken completely into the pickling soup material, and pickling for 6-8 hours at 10degreesC, (iv) boiling the component C and the component D to obtain the cooking soup material, taking out the pickled chicken from the pickling soup material and placing into the cooking soup material to boil, and stewing, and (v) cooling to room temperature, draining the surface floating oil, cutting, vacuumizing and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for producing biological, bacteriostatic and fresh-keeping packaged stewed chicken product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for large-scale industrial production. The product has fresh-keeping period for 12-15 days 4degreesC.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing biological, bacteriostatic and fresh-keeping packaged stewed chicken product, comprises (i) selecting a healthy chicken of 10 months and weight of the 1.7-2.1 kg and processing, (ii) preparing soup material, where the soup material comprises pickling soup material and cooking soup material, the pickling soup material comprises component A and component B, the cooking soup material comprises component C, component D and component E, the component A (100 g of chicken as standard) comprises 20 g anise, 5 g fennel, 20 g cinnamon, 5 g clove and 10-20 kg water, the component B comprises 3 kg salt, 0.3-0.7 kg monosodium glutamate and 95-105 kg water, the component C comprises 100 g anise, 50 g fennel, 30 g cinnamon, 30 g clove, 60 g rhizoma kaempferiae, 20 g meat, 25 g Angelica dahurica, 10 g Murraya paniculata, 10 g aniseed, 10 g Myrcia, 10 g tsaoko amomum fruit and 15-25 kg water, and the component D comprises 3-4 kg salt, 1-2 kg soy sauce, 1-2 kg dark soy sauce, 0.3-0.5 kg chicken essence and 95-105 kg water, (iii) adding the component A into the component B and mixing to obtain pickling soup material, placing 100 kg chicken into the pickling soup material, soaking the chicken completely into the pickling soup material, and pickling for 6-8 hours at 10degreesC, (iv) boiling the component C and the component D to obtain the cooking soup material, taking out the pickled chicken from the pickling soup material and placing into the cooking soup material to boil, stewing with under low heat for 1-1.5 hours, turning off the flame and continuously soak the chicken in the cooking soup material for 3 hours, and (v) cooling boiled chicken and cooking soup material to room temperature, draining the surface floating oil, taking the chicken, cutting to the needed weight, adding 8-12% boiling soup material, vacuumizing and filling with nitrogen and carbon dioxide, coating and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the preparation of component A comprises wrapping anise, fennel, cinnamon and clove using gauze to obtain yarn bag, placing the yarn bag into 10-20 kg of water to boil, boiling with low flame for 1.5-2.5 hours, taking out the yarn bag, and airing to obtain component A. In the step (ii), the preparation of component B comprises adding salt and monosodium glutamate into water to boil and cooling to obtain component B. In the step (ii), the component C further comprises bacteriostatic component, where the bacteriostatic component comprises 100 g fresh garlic, 10 g cinnamon, 100 g ginger, 5 g turmeric, 15 g liquorice and 15 g thyme. In the step (ii), the preparation of component C comprises wrapping raw materials by gauze to obtain yarn bag and wrapping bacteriostatic component by gauze to obtain yarn bag, placing two yarn cloth bags into 15-25 kg water to boil, boiling with low flame for 1.5-2.5 hours, and taking out two gauzes to obtain component C. In the step (ii), the preparation of component D comprises adding salt into water, adding soy sauce and dark soy sauce, boiling and adding chicken essence to obtain component D. In the step (v), the ratio of nitrogen and carbon dioxide is 80%:20%.

A                susceptor is disposed on a majority of an interior                surface of the flexible film pouch without                contacting the first end seal, second end seal, or                fin seal 0.75-2.00 oz pasteurized or partially                roasted shelled nuts disposed within the interior                cavity of the flexible film pouch. The shelled nuts                are pasteurized or partially roasted prior of being                disposed within the flexible film pouch.
   USE - Packaged food product such as shelled nuts,                mission nuts, Carmel nuts, Non-Pareil nuts, Brazil                nuts, cashews, chestnuts, granola, hazelnuts,                macadamia nuts, peanuts including Jumbo Runner                peanuts and Virginia extra large peanuts, pecans,                pistachios, pine nuts, pumpkin seeds, sunflower                seeds, and walnuts for retail sale.
   ADVANTAGE - The packaged food product has sufficient                headspace to allow for aromas to develop, and                increased capacity to accommodate expansion of                nitrogen, carbon dioxide, water vapor, and/or other                gases during heating, and enables enhancement or                optimization of organoleptic properties of the food                product contained during microwave heating, and                provides desirable aroma characteristics while                reducing or eliminating undesirable aroma                components. The packaging also enables enhancement                or optimization of organoleptic properties of the                food product contained in the package during                microwave heating. The food product has a                well-defined configuration, such as solid,                one-piece farinaceous food products, which can help                to provide consistency and repeatability in                response to exposure to microwave energy for a                particular period of time at a particular power                setting in a particular type of oven.

The shelled nuts                are pasteurized or partially roasted prior of being                disposed within the flexible film pouch. A coating                on the shelled nuts is a sweet glaze on the shelled                nuts, where the sweet glaze and the shelled nuts                have a combined moisture level within the range of                1.0-1.6 wt.%, or a savory coating on the shelled                nuts. The savory coating and shelled nuts have a                combined moisture level within the range of 1.5-2.6                wt.%. The packaged food product is configured and                arranged to be heated in a microwave oven in the                closed configuration to roast the shelled nuts to a                temperature of 120-260degreesF, where the coating                and the susceptor are configured and arranged to                moderate heating of the shelled nuts by absorbing                and/or reflecting microwave energy thereby reducing                an amount of microwave energy absorbed by the                shelled nuts and increasing homogeneity of heating                the shelled nuts.
