AU FU H
AE UNIV QUFU NORMAL (UNQN-C)
GA 2021A42965
AB    NOVELTY - Binuclear rhodium-porphyrin derivative organic                supramolecular gel system comprises porphyrin                derivative i.e.                3-(3-(3-carboxyphenyl)-5-(7,12,17-tris(3,5-bis(3-carboxyphenyl)phenyl)-21,22,23,24-tetraazapentacyclo(16.2.1.1(3,6).1(8,11).1(13,16))tetracosa-1,3,5,7,9,11(23),12,14,16,18(21),19-undecaen-2-yl)phenyl)benzoic                acid (I) as a ligand for constructing metal                organogel and rhodium(II) acetate (II) as central                metal node and catalytic reaction site. The pores                of the constructed supramolecular gel can adsorb                organic dyes and catalyze the carbene insertion                reaction by using the vacant catalytic sites.
   USE - The binuclear rhodium-porphyrin derivative                organic supramolecular gel system is useful in                aerogel to adsorb organic dyes and catalyze the                silicon-hydrogen insertion reaction (claimed); in                aerogel to adsorb organic dye pollutants to clean                waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The binuclear rhodium-porphyrin derivative                organic supramolecular gel system: produces metal                organic aerogel which has a better pore structure                and a larger adsorption load capacity; utilizes (I)                as ligand which has a carboxy group at the meta                position, has great flexibility and is convenient                for controlling the pore structure; achieves                selective catalysis of different substrates by                degree of mutual fit between substrate and                pores.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Binuclear rhodium-porphyrin derivative organic                supramolecular gel system comprises porphyrin                derivative i.e.                3-(3-(3-carboxyphenyl)-5-(7,12,17-tris(3,5-bis(3-carboxyphenyl)phenyl)-21,22,23,24-tetraazapentacyclo(16.2.1.1(3,6).1(8,11).1(13,16))tetracosa-1,3,5,7,9,11(23),12,14,16,18(21),19-undecaen-2-yl)phenyl)benzoic                acid of formula (I) as a ligand for constructing                metal organogel and rhodium(II) acetate of formula                (II) as central metal node and catalytic reaction                site. The pores of the constructed supramolecular                gel can adsorb organic dyes and catalyze the                carbene insertion reaction by using the vacant                catalytic sites. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also                included for:
   (1) preparing binuclear rhodium-porphyrin                derivative organic supramolecular gel system,                comprising (i) preparing (I), (ii) preparing                binuclear rhodium-porphyrin derivative organic                supramolecular gel system and (iii) the gel                obtained in step (ii) is prepared by supercritical                carbon dioxide technology to prepare corresponding                metal organic supramolecular aerogel;
   (2) use of aerogel to adsorb organic dyes and                catalyze the silicon-hydrogen insertion reaction;                and
   (3) method for dye adsorption and catalysis of                silicon-hydrogen insertion reaction, comprising                taking 8 mg of supramolecular gel into three                centrifuge tubes, adding 8 ml Coomassie Brilliant                Blue, Methylene Blue and Orange G respectively, and                stirring and absorbing at room temperature for 24                hours, adding 5 eq of triethylsilane and 1 ml DCM                into three reaction tubes respectively, 2% eq of                gel, dissolving 1 eq of ethyl acetate diazonium                (EDA) in 1 ml dichloromethane, slowly adding them                into reaction tube, detecting the reaction process                by TLC until the EDA consumption was complete,                separating the supernatant by centrifugation,                spin-drying the solvent, dissolving it in a nuclear                magnetic field using deuterotrichloromethane                (CDCl3), obtaining the corresponding conversion                rate by analyzing the nuclear magnetic field.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises                  comprises (a) adding 1eq 3,5-dibromobenzaldehyde,                  2.5eq m-methoxycarbonylphenylboronic acid, 0.01eq                  sodium carbonate, 0.04eq                  tetra(triphenylphosphine)palladium, 400 ml                  methanol into a 1000 ml round bottom flask,                  quickly evacuating and passing nitrogen five                  times, refluxing for 18 hours under a nitrogen                  atmosphere; cooling to room temperature under                  nitrogen atmosphere, carrying out suction                  filtration under reduced pressure, recovering the                  filter cake; dissolving the filter cake with                  dichloromethane; recovering the filtrate by                  suction filtration; spin-drying the filtrate;                  passing through the column with the eluent of                  petroleum ether and dichloromethane in ratio 2:1                  to obtain 4.3 g white solid product with yield of                  86%, (b) dissolving 2eq of the aldehyde obtained                  in (i) in 300 ml dichloromethane, adding it to a                  round-bottomed flask, wrapping the round-bottomed                  flask with black paper to avoid light reaction,                  adding 2eq of pyrrole, stirring at room                  temperature for 0.5 hours; slowly adding 1 eq of                  boron trifluoride ether and dissolving it in 50                  mL dichloromethane; after the addition,                  maintaining reaction at room temperature for 8                  hours; adding 1.5eq                  2,3-dichloro-5,6-dicyano-1,4-benzoquinone (DDQ),                  continuously reacting for 12 hours; adding silica                  gel into the reaction flask and mixing sample;                  spin drying dichloromethane; rinsing with                  methanol stirring the sample of silica gel until                  it is colorless; drying the silica gel and                  passing the column with the eluent of                  dichloromethane/EA in ratio of 25:1 to obtain the                  corresponding porphyrin carboxylate with yield of                  20%, adding                  tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium, the                  solution turns pink color, after adding DDQ, the                  solution becomes black due to the oxidizing                  property of DDQ and (c) adding 1 eq of porphyrin                  carboxylate into a 250 ml round bottom flask;                  dissolving 200 eq potassium hydroxide in 120 ml                  mixed solvent of water/tetrahydrofuran/methanol                  in ratio1:1:1, refluxing at 80 degrees for 3                  hours; spin drying the tetrahydrofuran and                  methanol in the flask; adjusting pH to 2 with                  concentrated hydrochloric acid, filtering to                  obtain the product with the yield of 100%. The                  step (ii) comprises dissolving (I) and (II) in                  the corresponding solvent until they are                  completely dissolved, placing them in an oven,                  adjusting the temperature of the oven to 85                  degrees C and forming a gel after 48 hours, then                  aging for 24 hours at 85 degrees C, taking the                  gel out of the oven and cooling to room                  temperature, exchanging with ethanol repeatedly                  until the uncoordinated metal and ligand inside                  were completely replaced; the method for                  preparing the corresponding metal-organic aerogel                  from the obtained gel using supercritical carbon                  dioxide technology comprises a supercritical                  drying device and 270 g of supercritical carbon                  dioxide were used to dry for 24 hours to obtain                  the corresponding aerogel.
